title: Auto Diesel Principles
description: Explains the operating principles of the four-stroke diesel engine used in the automobile, including the construction, service, and diagnosis of the various engine systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Computer Keyboarding
description: Teaches touch keyboarding skills. Covers the alpha, number, and symbol keys; terminology; and basic formatting. Emphasizes accuracy and speed as the semester progresses.

title: Stroll Hamtramck
description: Rain or shine, enjoy a spring Saturday strolling Jos Campau Avenue, the main street of this colorful ethnic city, and visiting its stores and landmarks. As you walk, tour guide Don Samull will talk about Hamtramck's fascinating history as well as it's current situation. You'll have plenty of time to make purchases before you stop at a local Polish restaurant for lunch. Wear comfortable shoes. Lunch included in course fee.

title: Computer Technology Test
description: A credit for prior college-level learning test on the fundamentals of computer technology and information management. Successfully passing CLT-101 provides credit for CIS-100 and satisfies HFC's General Education Computer Technology requirement. Note: Before deciding to take CLT-101, a student is responsible for checking their program to see what computer-related course(s) must be taken to meet specific program requirements.

title: Intro Info Tech
description: Survey the field of computer technology and information management. Covers computer hardware, the use of the Internet for communication, e-commerce, information retrieval, the social impact of technology, computer security, networking, and industry-related careers. Also introduces students to the Windows operating system, Internet browsers, e-mail, word processing, spreadsheets, and presentation software using computer laboratory sessions. This course satisfies the HFC Computer Literacy Requirement.

title: Quick Spanish for Travelers
description: Tune up your artistic signing abilities. Join Anne Bolander, an author and member of the deaf community, for this unique opportunity to learn how to incorporate facial expression, body movement and rhythm into your signing. Whatever your reason, join this fun, lively class and learn to sign a variety of song lyrics.

title: Jazz Improvisation 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of jazz improvisation in which skills are developed through the study of jazz theory and the performances of a variety of jazz styles in a small group, jazz combo setting.

title: Glass Beadmaking 2
description: Embellish your glass beads using more advanced applications and tools. Professional glass beader Kathleen Robinson-Young will teach you how to encase simple floral beads, make a hollow bead, and construct a fish, turtle and heart bead to take home. $35 supply fee due to instructor. Please bring a notebook, pen or pencil and drinking water (to prevent dehydration from the torch) to class. For directions to the studio go to "Supplies and Maps". Class limited to 9.

title: Recession-Proof Your Wallet
description: Are you tired of living check to check every month? Did you know that up to 40% of all Americans spend more money than they actually earn? Learn the secrets of wealth management and how the rich are able to create money-making opportunities with little or none of their own money. Additionally, you will learn how to work less, increase earnings, pay less in taxes and much more. Don't miss out on this opportunity. Financial freedom is a choice. Make yours today. Join us!

title: Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance and alignment in this twelve week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing and relaxation techniques learned in Hatha Yoga 1, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Industrial Appl of Basic Math
description: Utilizes shop problems to help students relate math to various job situations. Topics include addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of whole and mixed numbers and common and decimal fractions; percents, averages, and estimates; graphs, tables, and statistical measure; powers and roots; linear, angular, and circular measure; surface area, volume, and cubic measure; ratios and proportion; and metrics and metric conversion.

title: Tastes of Italy
description: Prepare for a treat in this introductory cooking series that will teach you not only the basics of preparing authentic Italian regional cuisine but also the almost mythic approach to dining that makes Italy famous. With an emphasis on fresh seasonal foods, each week Robert Cascardo and Cristina Cotronei will teach you how to prepare a three-course meal, pair it with an appropriate wine and eat it the way they do in Italy. Cristina was born near Milan, and Robert has lived in various Italian cities including Florence, Rome and Turin. Naturally, they both love to cook. Join them for fun, educational evenings of preparing, eating and, of course, cleaning up after memorable meals. This class sells out, so register early.

title: Electronic Auto Chassis
description: This course is a study of electronic braking and chassis systems. Examines controller networks as they apply to steering, suspension, and braking systems. Explores computerized vehicle stability systems and components.

title: Process Technology Equipment
description: Introduces students to the purpose, components, operation, and troubleshooting of the equipment used within the process industry.

title: Organizing Your Digital Life
description: Getting overwhelmed by how many digital files you have? This course provides tips and techniques for getting a handle on your photos, videos, videos and documents. Explore different ways to convert original documents, photographs and video to a computer file or take what is already on your computer and organize it. Share your work with others using online services, printed media, CDs or DVDs.

title: Celebrate the Russian New Year
description: According to the old Julian calendar, Russian New Year is observed in mid-January. On the first evening of class, without flying half-way around the world, visit Moscow and some of the cities of the Golden Ring via a slide presentation. That evening view Russian artificats as you sample a Russian treat. Then on the following Sat. the class will gather for a short tour of a local orthodox monastery and a Slavic meal.

title: Residential Wiring
description: Explores the fundamentals of residential wiring, especially the aspects of the National Electrical Code specifically applying to residential construction and repairs. Covers electrical supplies and hardware appropriate for residential applications, and examines residential wiring techniques.

title: Basic Health Assessment
description: Focuses on theory and techniques involved in basic patient assessment using vital signs. Addresses the areas of medical asepsis, standard precautions, proper body mechanics, and patient transportation. Laboratory activities.

title: Lebanese Cooking IV
description: Lamb shawarma, Lebanese fattoush salad, potatoes kibbee with burgul vegetables.

title: Specialties of Kenya
description: Enjoy popular dishes, cooking methods and food culture with Chef Marilyn Suter just returning from a recent visit to Kenya. We will make ugali, a Kenyan staple, goat, curried bananas, a great vegetarian bean dish, and more. Bring an apron and a sense of adventure!

title: Mid East People and Cultures
description: Introduces the various ethnic, linguistic, and religious groups as well as cultures of the Middle East with an emphasis on both the Arabs and Islam as two of the most powerful social forces to have shaped the history and culture of the region. Explores Orientalism and western perceptions of the Middle East, colonialism. globalization and identity, nationalism, and other popular ideological currents that continue to influence the region and its peoples. Also examines the history and experiences of Arabs in the United States with a special focus on the large Arab-American community in Dearborn, Michigan.

title: Health PE for Elem Teachers
description: This course provides the theoretical framework of elementary and middle school health, physical activity, and safety curriculum. Students will gain knowledge and skills for the elementary classroom teacher on methods used to integrate health, nutrition, safety, and physical education into their daily curriculum. The course includes teaching strategies, projects, movement exercises, and games designed to engage the Pre-Elementary Education major. Developmental sequences and the health needs of children from elementary through middle school is included. Course emphasis is on providing learning experiences for children's development of lifelong positive health and fitness behaviors. Particular attention is given to the national health standards as well as the Michigan Model Modules.

title: ELL TOEFL Preparation
description: Prepares English Language Learners (ELL) to take the TOEFL (Test of English as a Foreign Language). Focuses on reading, listening, speaking, writing, vocabulary and test-taking strategies. Does not transfer nor count towards degree graduation requirements.

title: AC Theory & Equipment
description: Examines alternating current theory. Includes right triangle trigonometry and complex numbers to facilitate the AC calculations. Discusses concepts of reactance, impedance, and power factor. Compares single phase and three phase power. Introduces single phase and three phase alternators, motors, transformers, and both heating and lighting loads.

title: Applied Statics
description: Provides the necessary mechanics background for engineering technology programs. Main topics are forces, moments, equilibrium, free body diagrams, trusses, frames, internal forces, friction, and area moment of inertia.

title: Genealogy II
description: Are you encountering a seemingly insurmountable research obstacle? Do you want to know more about secondary genealogy sources and techniques? Patricia Gee, past president of the Dearborn Genealogical Society, will combine the latest internet technology with tried-and-true research strategies to help you find your ancestors. An optional research trip to the Library of Allen County in Fort Wayne (2nd largest genealogical library in the U.S.) will be discussed.

title: Say It With Sign 2
description: Increase your signing skills and understanding of the deaf culture in this unique intermediate level class. Working with Anne Bolander, author and hearing impaired instructor, you will become fully immersed in communicating with the deaf community. If you attended "Say it with Sign" or have a basic knowledge of signing, you won't want to miss this opportunity!

title: Turn Recipes Into a Gold Mine
description: Famous Amos converted his cookie recipe into millions of dollars. Learn from professional caterer Andrea Taylor how you can make money using your kitchen and family recipes. She will cover calculating prices, finding the right audience, marketing specialty items, meeting sanitation guidelines, getting financing, and creating a hit. Take this class as your first step to more cash.

title: Gluten Free Baking
description: A gluten free diet is the only medically accepted treatment for celiac disease and wheat allergy. Join Chef Marilyn Suter in making some tasty gluten free treats. Create a great pizza, muffins, white bread, and brownies. These products all store well in the freezer so you can plan ahead. Enhance your food choices with these special recipes.

title: Gentle Yoga
description: Start your day by experiencing the health benefits includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance and alignment; and you will achieve mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Comp Assisted N/C Programming
description: An introductory course in computer-assisted part programming. The student will use CAD/CAM software (Mastercam, Catia, and UG NX) running on a personal computer (PC). Programming for both the CNC Mill and CNC Lathe will be done in this class.

title: Intro to Energy Technology
description: Introduces traditional sources of energy and provides an overview of the various forms of energy and their applications. Covers alternative, renewable, and traditional non-renewable sources of energy with consideration for energy conservation and use and energy transmission systems.

title: EV Battery Technician Cert
description: Participants in this certificate course will acquire the necessary skills for designing, operating, testing, maintaining, and replacing battery cells and packs for various applications. In addition, the certificate allows participants to choose the right technology for the right challenge and evaluate battery performance. Moreover, to be able to work out a solid total solution, the battery management systems, the charging and maintenance of batteries, and their recycling process are also discussed in more detail. Finally, we give sufficient attention to safely handling these electrical applications, ensuring the well-being of both individuals and the environment.This certification empowers learners to: . Evaluate what battery technology is best suited for which application based on its characteristics. . Examine the advantages and disadvantages of the most commonly used battery technologies. . Design and implement the best-fit battery solution for each application. . Safely handle, test, repair, and dispose of battery systems according to legal obligations.

title: Dress Up Your Work
description: Documents with a little formatting are more interesting to look at and thus more likely to be read. What are formats? Changing the type face, the type size, adding spacing, using bolding, underline, and text color are all part of formatting. Learn to use tabs as well as the bullets and numbering feature. Sort out those pesky indents that seem to have a mind of their own. See how to use the format painter to make formatting so easy.

title: Spec Topics Indust Draf Techno
description: Designed to explore selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current drafting technology trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Students can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Digital Theory
description: Covers digital theory. Introduces basic gates and logic circuits, counters, registers, timers, LED and LCD displays, arithmetic circuits, and number systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Fundamentals Gd&t
description: This is a non-credit course that is running onsite at Advancing Engineering location. This course focuses on the fundamental subject matter that relates to Geometric Dimensioning & Tolerancing. In order to enhance the understanding of technical information being communicated on a part drawing for manufacturing, students will be exposed to terminologies, symbols, and definitions. They will also know when it is appropriate to apply GD&T symbols and how to interpret the terminologies.

title: Perennials: Perpetual Joy!
description: Plant perennials once and enjoy their beauty year after year! Discover how these plants can add excitement to any garden, offering dramatic and interesting ways to landscape your outdoor living room. Learn from a Master Gardener how to pick the right sun and shade perennials, amend and prepare the soil, and fertilize, divide, and prune perennial gardens. You'll also see how easy it is to grow them in containers for years of successful blooms.

title: Instruments
description: Introduces electrical measurement instruments, including digital and analog multimeters, clamp-on ammeters, megohmeters, and the oscilloscope. Requires hands-on lab time spent with each device type. Emphasizes safe measuring techniques. Covers additional devices such as pressure gauges, chart recorders, heat sensors and chain stretch monitor.

title: Earth Science for Educators
description: An inquiry-based course covering topics in the earth sciences - geology, meteorology, and astronomy. Course content is designed to give students a thorough familiarity with concepts critical to teaching earth science at the K-6 level and how the topics covered in the course fulfill National Science Education Standards. Learning is primarily through student inquiry and discovery of core concepts, with guidance and facilitation provided by the instructor. Four hours of integrated lecture and lab per week.

title: Kinesiology for PTA's
description: Reviews surface anatomy and functional anatomy, muscles and muscle function, proper posture and analysis of posture, and gait analysis and deviations. Medical Terminology is reinforced. A lab component familiarizes the student with functional aspects of human motion.

title: Ghost Hunting
description: Join a real-life local paranormal investigator to get a realistic view of what ghost hunting is all about--not the exaggerated TV version! Content includes the most common types of haunts and causes, basic equipment, cell phone apps, haunted MI locations, and how to get started. Our investigator will bring photos and recordings. Bring photos if you have them! Believers and non-believers welcome! Subject matter appropriate for mature children twelve or older accompanied by parents.

title: The Weekend Navigator
description: The U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary's Weekend Navigator course is a comprehensive course designed for both experienced and novice powerboat and sailboat operators. The course is designed to educate the boating enthusiast in skills required for a safe voyage on a variety of waters and boating conditions. Topics will include: fundamentals of waypoint navigation, how to read and use nautical charts and digital charts, cruise and chart planning software, chart plotters and navigating with radar and much more. Class includes The Weekend Navigator course book, charts, and handouts and a course completion certificate. The student needs to bring a parallel ruler or rolling chart plotter, dividers and a nautical slide rule for speed and distance calculations. There is no final examination. Visit the Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for a supply list, where to purchase supplies, and cost.

title: Therap Activities for Dementia
description: Sharing fun-filled activities can ease the distress of both persons with dementia and their caregivers. Amira Tame, a certified activities consultant, will teach word games that enhance memory recall, show videos and hands-on demonstrations of activities that promote healthy relationships with caregivers, and offer strategies for overcoming anger, anxiety, resistance, and passivity. Included is the presenter's book Healing the Wounds of Alzheimer's Disease. 4 Activity Professional CEUs.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 4
description: Develops low-intermediate-level college-preparatory reading and listening skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELI-105R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Simply Chakras & Auras- Part 2
description: Delve deeper into your inner energy! This class will expand on the Part 1 class and teach you how to extend your understanding of your chakra system and the discerning of auras. It will enhance your ability to see and understand your life, relationships and make a positive difference in the world!

title: Spec Topics Indust Draf Techno
description: Designed to explore selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current drafting technology trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Students can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Feed the Brain
description: Discover from biochemist Sandy Baumann easy, research-based strategies to fuel the brian with healthy foods and beverages that dramatically improve concentration, learning, memory and achievement, while decreasing ADD and distracting behaviors. In this fun interactive class educators will gain nutritional and lifestyle tools to improve performance on lessons, homework and tests. Ideal for teachers, counselors, Student Success Network members, food service personnel, administrators, and school nurses. Sign up today to help students get their brains in high gear for learning and remembering. Ask your principal about funds to attend this seminar. Includes lunch and the presenter's book Feed Your Brain for Learning ($15 value). 0.5 SBCEU or 5 nursing contact hours.

title: Ophthalmic Surgical Assisting
description: Introduces ophthalmic surgical procedures, including the indications, goals, and steps of select ophthalmic procedures. Emphasizes principles of asepsis, and care and maintenance of ophthalmic surgical instruments.

title: Chinese Med: Waking the Dragon
description: Combat modern stress and burnout, gather and conserve your vital energy through traditional Chinese medicine practices. Explore and practice the ancient energy techniques of Chi Gung. Also practice stimulating acuponts to experience optimal personal energy and restoration of stamina.

title: Excel for Beginners
description: Excel is a computer program for keeping track of numbers and lists. How much did I spend? How much will it cost? What if the interest rate goes up? Calculations are easy in Excel when you know how to create the formulas (you don't need to be a math whiz!) and use the built in functions that allow you find a sum, an average, or count entries, and much more, with just a few clicks of the mouse. Whether you are a keeping watch of the family finances, involved in business, or keeping track of your club's finances or the stats for the golf league, Exel can be a useful tool for you. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge required. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge required. Text provided.

title: Intro to American Cultural Stu
description: This course is a basic introduction to the study of America and its culture. The course focuses on the ideals, fears, conflicts and realities of American culture over the past centuries. This course will include a wide number of humanities disciplines (visual arts, music, drama, religion, philosophy, literature, and architecture) to explore these topics. The use of popular academic cultural theories will also be presented. This course will explore dominant cultures as well as subcultures that are prevalent in America and the way these cultures shape who we think we are as a nation.

title: Dining Room Service & Operati
description: Applies basic principles of table service in the production dining room. Emphasizes effective serving procedures and techniques, including cordial and prompt attention to customers, proper dress and grooming practices, and in-depth knowledge of menu items.

title: Apple Support
description: An introductory-level course focusing on using and supporting Apple products with primary emphasis on the Apple Operating Systems (OS), including installing, configuring, using, maintaining, troubleshooting, and securing. Working with applications, file management, network connections, peripherals, mobile products, and cross-platform compatibility are also covered. Suitable for end-users seeking an in-depth knowledge of Apple products or help desk specialists/computer technicians who will be supporting Apple products within an organization.

title: Conversational Spanish 1
description: Are you tired of language tapes that have you endlessly repeating phrases? Are you ready to learn Spanish for business or pleasure? This workshop covers the fundamental grammar points and functional vocabulary that will enable you to engage in basic conversations. Through interactive activities and exercises, gain a basic understanding of Spanish and its cultures. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $30).

title: Discover Your Passion
description: Are you interested in discovering your "passion" or "mission" in life? Do you need help figuring out the best occupation for you? If so, join this highly experiential, participative workshop and begin a journey of discovery tht could change your life. By exploring clues found in your personality talents, and skills and by using a variety of tools, you will develop a working mission state- ment. Appropriate for people in all life phases. $5 supply fee payable to instructor first night of class.

title: Introduction to Birding
description: This course introduces novices with an interest in birds to the basics of bird identification, observation, recording their sightings and selection of proper equipment. The course will begin with a classroom session to discuss some birding basics before taking to the field (locally in Dearborn) for the next session to begin putting into practice birding skills. (The Sibley Guide to Birds, required for class, can be purchased during the first class session. Binoculars required for second class session).

title: Interm. Clinical Therapies
description: Development of the clinical skills related to airway management, bronchial hygiene, resuscitation procedures, and oxygenation. Students evaluate, modify, and monitor patients' responses to respiratory care regimens. A seven-week directed clinical experience in selected clinical sites.

title: Special Topic Indus Draft Tech
description: Designed to explore selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current drafting technology trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Students can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Stage Costuming
description: Introduces contemporary aesthetic and creative design principles for theatrical costuming, with an emphasis on line, form, meeting production requirements, budgeting, and thematic unity.

title: Lebanese Cooking
description: Baba ghanouj, Lebanese tabouli salad, tomatoes kibee.

title: Desktop Publish with InDesign
description: Use the brand new Adobe InDesign CS3 software to create your catalogs, brochures, flyers and other items. Lay out your text and graphics like the pros and avoid having to send these items out to an expensive designer. Easily coordinate with professional printers, publish in your own office, or output to multiple media. Create rich full featured documents with easy integration with Photoshop and other programs. The instructor will get you started and help to demystify the many features incorporated in this professional page layout program. Prerequisite: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!
prerequisite_text: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!

title: Polish Churches of Hamtramck
description: Visit the beautiful historic churches of Hamtramck, Detroit's Polish enclave, and then enjoy lunch at a restaurant in the area. Join your instructor, Don Samull, on a guided, self-driving and walking tour of the three major Roman Catholic churches of that city, as well as the quaint Polish National Catholic church. Maps showing where to meet will be sent to registered students prior to the tour. The cost of the meal is included.

title: Weave an Amulet Bag
description: Just in time for Easter, design and weave your own amulet bag using the brick stitch (off loom). In Session 1, you'll chart your own neckpiece design, select your colors, and start weaving the body of the bag. Session 2, you'll attach the neckpiece and design the fringe components. In Session 3, you'll close the bottom of the bag and begin attaching fringe. Between sessions you'll need time to work on your bag. $15 supply fee payable to instructor.

title: Soup Cooking 101
description: Chef Peter Veach will be preparing four types of simple and easy soups.

title: Rehab Neuro Conditions Lab
description: Applies techniques learned in PTA-262 with an emphasis on the functional approach to patient treatment. Includes the use of Neurodevelopmental Treatment (NDT) and Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF) for adults with neurological conditions such as traumatic brain injury (TBI) and cerebrovascular accidents (CVAs). Guided laboratory setting.

title: Scenic Painting & Construction
description: This course explores beginning and intermediate techniques in traditional scenic contruction and painting, with direct application to live production, in a hands on environment.

title: Intro to Windows 7 - Part 1
description: If you can operate a car, you can operate a computer. Don't let confusing computer jargon stop you! Attend this hands-on workshop designed for the person with little or no experience, and see how easy it is to use the basic features of Windows Version 7 (Vista users welcome, too). You'll learn how to work with the mouse, keyboard, desktop, and start menu and become familiar with the components of a working window. By the end of the class, you'll also understand the difference between a hard drive, a disk, and a CD-ROM.

title: Hearty Vegetarian Dishes
description: Even avid meat-eaters are adding vegetarian meals to their diets more frequently. Come learn about the value of meatless eating, and experience the pleasure of preparing tasty vegetarian and vegan meals. Instructor Joy Callan will once again surprise you with a variety of new recipes. You're in for a treat! Bring an apron. Class limited to 14. Enroll early.

title: MIOSHA Recordkeeping
description: This full-day MIOSHA seminar will further understanding of MIOSHA Log 300 recordkeeping requirements; the importance of conducting a comprehensive investigation following near-miss incidents and accidents; and how to determine your company's insurance MOD factors how to reduce workers' compensation costs by obtaining scheduled and premium credits, and how to reduce medical-only claims by up to 70%.

title: Plumbing Fixture Installation
description: Covers the operation and availability of common plumbing fixtures and appliances. Focuses on the selection and installation of fixtures in residential, commercial, and industrial applications. The control and accessories used in fuel burning appliances, the differences in vent capacity and vent type, and the steps necessary to place an appliance in service are covered. Explores how electricity is incorporated into a piping system including concepts of energy savings and safety.

title: HFV- Internet for Beginners
description: Around the world in 80 seconds! The internet helps you communicate instantly with family and friends, find travel bargains, shop for the best deals, and research any topic, including health questions. In four 2-hour sessions you will learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, surf the internet to find information, bookmark favorite sites, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. You will have time to practice on the internet in class, so bring your questions. THIS CLASS IS RESTRICTED TO HENRY FORD VILLAGE RESIDENTS AND IS BILLED BY SECTION.

title: Advanced Rigging
description: Presents safe rigging practices and equipment used by mechanical trades people. Covers fiber and wire rope, block and tackle, lift and rigging chain, proof test, safe working load, design factor, sling geometry, fittings, and lifting and moving equipment.

title: Digital Image Acquisition Mgt.
description: This course addresses all technical aspects of digital image acquisition, manipulation, and storage. Course includes digital imaging characteristics, types of imaging systems and system components, digital image acquisition and manipulation, and digital image archiving systems. Also included are principles of imaging system quality assurance and maintenance.

title: Intermediate HipHop Dance III
description: This is the third of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of Hip Hop dance technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in Hip Hop dance history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and the principles of time, space, and energy. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of Hip Hop dance. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of Hip Hop dance.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, awareness of the body in space, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized

title: Heal Brain After Accidents
description: Did you know that simple falls and low-speed car accidents can produce hearing and vision changes, insomnia, panic attacks, persistent muscle pain, joint stiffness and depression? Let brain-stem chiropractor Dr. Barry Hobbs show you how these symptoms relate to certain blockages in nerve energy and can be treated with specific manipulation therapies. Then hear biochemist Sandy Baumann describe the foods, supplements and lifestyle strategies she used after a card accident to heal her brain from anxiety, memory loss and insomnia. Come find hope for your unresolved health problems. $12 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Predict Maint-Vibration Analys
description: Provides specialized instruction in diagnosing and resolving machinery vibration in rotating equipment, the most cost-effective methods to reduce maintenance costs, and how to extend machinery life.

title: Elementary ASL II
description: Further builds American Sign Language (ASL) skills, focusing on communication in a cultural context. Expands students' knowledge of fingerspelling, vocabulary and grammatical structures, and the development of visual receptive and gestural expressive skills in order to comprehend and express everyday ideas in ASL.

title: Psychic Detectives
description: Join Kristy Robinett, a professional Psychic Medium, as she shares stories of her work with law enforcement. Hear about times when the tables were turned, and Kristy became the one chased - by the unlawful party! [There were even some cases where Kristy required police protection as a result of her involvement! ] Kristy will explain how psychics can help, and hurt, police cases. You won't want to miss this fascinating presentation.

title: Protect Yourself-Scams, Frauds
description: Celebrate Older Americans Month with a special presentation by Wayne County Sheriff's Deputy Ki Sobol about protecting yourself from crime. discover how criminals pick their targets for identity theft, home burglary, purse snatching, mugging and carjacking. Learn red flags that signal danger when you are walking, driving, or traveling, including special precautions for evening activities. Persons who are not seniors are welcome to attend. Refreshments are included. Sign up today to keep yourself and your loved ones safe in your home, at stores, in your vehicle, and in your neighborhood.

title: Basic Hydraulics
description: Introduces basic hydraulic principles, laws, components, and symbols. Topics include: safety; the use of tools; and procedures in the design, building, testing, troubleshooting, and repair of industrial hydraulic systems. Includes demonstrations and extensive laboratory activities.

title: Breakthroughs for Health
description: A conference for the community and nurses. Includes lunch and breaks. 5 hours instruction time.

title: Mutual Fund Overview
description: What is a mutual fund? What types of mutual funds are there? How can they help you reach your financial goals? A licensed representative will answer these and other questions. He will explain the different types of mutual funds, and the risks and rewards of investing in them. Please bring paper and pen to class.

title: Intro to Novel
description: Focuses on reading, discussion, and written analysis of novels in order to develop 1) skill in literary analysis and interpretation and 2) familiarity with the conventions of the novel. Students will read major novelists primarily from the nineteenth, twentieth, and twenty-first centuries. The titles chosen exemplify important developments and themes in prose fiction, each discussed as a statement of a particular author, a reflection of the times in which the work was written, and an enduring expression of human experience.

title: Glass Ornament Workshop
description: Work with hot glass to blow, shape and hook your own holiday glass ornament! Watch master glassblowers demonstrate color, form and creating the loop for hanging. You will sketch your own design and then work with the hot glass to create it. Your ornament will be available for pickup after cooling (approx. 24 hrs). Please wear loose fitting cotton clothing. A materials fee of $28 is payable to the instructor in class. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: AK Steel Hydraulics Training
description: Hydraulics training for incumbent workers for AK Steel.

title: Self Defense for Women
description: Join other mothers, daughters, sisters, aunts and grandmothers in learning how to prevent victimization. Increase your awareness of self-protection strategies under the instruction of Jaye Spiro, a fifth-degree black belt. This fun and informative seminar is designed to increase confidence and reduce fear for you and your female loved ones. Come examine and practice simple, effective techniques--some physical, some not--which can be adapted into your life experience. Women of all ages, sizes, and fitness levels are welcome.

title: Energy Industry Fundamentals
description: Covers basic emerging principles and concepts that affect the energy industry, compliance with safety and health procedures, how electric power and natural gas generation, transmission, and distribution work, and entry-level energy careers. Students will gain an understanding of the energy industry as a prerequisite to occupation-specific knowledge and an understanding of the careers available in the energy industry.

title: Two Dimensional Design
description: Introduces the basic visual elements and principles of design, along with a wide range of visual problem solving strategies, and theoretical guidelines regarding two-dimensional composition. Covers a variety of art media and digital processes, using formal art and design vocabulary. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Automotive Fundamentals
description: Provides an introduction to the major automotive systems covered in greater detail in other automotive technology specific system classes. Explores how to perform preventive maintenance routines, such as oil changes, tire rotations on standard systems as well as on vehicles equipped with TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System); brake and suspension system inspections; and how to service the cooling system. This is the only course allowed for Secondary Partnership transfer credit (4 credit hours) from approved high school programs.

title: Paramedic Clinical II
description: Provides a structural clinical experience in the hospital and field environments where designated tasks in specific topic areas must be completed. This set of clinical rotations emphasizes airway management skills in the in the field; global cardiac patient management in the cardiac cath. lab; and neurological assessments and trauma patient management in a variety of clinical settings.NOTE: Due to the nature of this course, a student must have completed the Health Careers medical packet, drug screen, and criminal background clearance as required, and provide current ongoing proof of health insurance prior to attending advanced-level clinical.

title: Introd to Machine Tool Probing
description: Covers the elementary functions and application of the electronic probe on a Vertical Machining Center. Topics include set-up and calibration of the probe and use and application of the following cycles - Bore/Boss measuring cycle, Web/Pocket measuring cycle, and internal and external cycles. Laboratory activities.

title: Ballet for Pre-Teens
description: Give your child an opportunity to develop grace, coordination, and flexibility that will be a part of his or her life forever. In this class, Stephanie Fullerton will teach the terminology, technique, and precision associated with ballet.

title: Insurance Coding & Reimb
description: Further develops the methods and skills necessary for optimal reimbursement for services rendered in a healthcare setting. Explores billing and reimbursement cycle, HIPAA, diagnostic coding and procedural coding, coding compliance, and claims processing as well as physician and hospital coding reimbursement. Also provides an overview of the key financial circumstances impacting the healthcare delivery system.

title: Electrical Journeyman Prep
description: Course Description: This course provides an overview of electrical theory, the essentials of 2017 the Michigan Electrical Code Rules Part 8 and the 2017 National Electrical Code (NEC) to help prepare the student to take the electrical journeyman examination. Course content will be disseminated by lecture utilizing PowerPoint presentations. The method of instruction for the course is either classroom lecture or Zoom lecture utilizing PowerPoint. At the instructor's discretion, printed copies of the PowerPoint presentations will be handed out to the students, and/or they will be posted on the Henry Ford College Learning Management System (LMS).

title: Plumbing, Pipefitting Material
description: Introduces plumbing and pipe-fitting materials joining. Topics included are mechanical joints/pipe-cutting, brazing with turbo torches and oxyacetylene, soldering with LP and MAPP gases, plastic PVC pipe-joining, flared fittings, plastic fusion welding, and dielectric fittings. Students are required to study the theory behind these methods and perform hands-on labs.

title: Selling Home in Today's Market
description: Learn how to receive the most for your house and sell quickly in this hard buyer's market. Don't let the market beat you up. Learn how you can still come ahead selling your house. $15 material packet available from instructor.

title: Radiographic Procedures III
description: This lecture/laboratory course is the third in a four-course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This 8-week course reinforces fundamental radiology positioning terminology and provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for skull and headwork. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Salad & Sandwich Entertaining
description: Tired of serving the same old deli tray to guests? Get some unique salad and sandwich ideas that will upscale your next casual entertaining event. Chef Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will share her recipes for mango chutney cheese, tuna papaya and creamy pesto turkey sandwiches. She'll also whip up three salads: wilted bacom, roasted root vegetable and a not-so-orindary ambrosia. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Making Sense of Your Retiremen
description: If you don't prepare for your retirement, no one else will. That's why it's important to take time now to discuss your retirement goals. An experienced financial advisor will help you learn more about the five basic questions you should ask yourself to prepare for retirement. Discover options you can use now to save for retirement, and how time can help or hurt your retirement goals. Take the first steps toward a successful retirement. Call today to reserve space for yourself and a spouse or friend.

title: Savor the Flavors of Hungary
description: According to Elizabeth Krajcz, who was born in Hungary and has cooked at the Hungarian American Culture Center for 20 years, hearty Dinebean goulash is a delicious favorite. In this class, learn how to make it the authentic Hungarian way. Then discover her secrets for making a torte topped with mocha frosting. Yum! Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: ELL American Culture 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level knowledge of American culture, history, and geography for non-native English speakers. Focuses on both content (American culture) and format (academic lecture and note-taking). This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Civil Technology Studio
description: Students will explore the materials and methods used in the development of both civil and commercial construction projects. Students will analyze existing civil and commercial projects with a focus on the development of project specific details centered on material use, performance, and adherence to industry specific design guidelines. Projects will include: bridges, roadways, commercial site development, and civil construction details.

title: Camp Entrepreneurship
description: Curious about learning how to save, invest, build wealth, or start a business? Camp G.R.O.W. teaches students how to gain resources, and obtain wealth in a fun, exciting, and informative manner. Campers are immersed in the essentials of financial literacy, networking, goal setting, business planning and entrepreneurship. Using skill building games, visual aids and hands on activities, students will learn how to make, manage, and grow their money wisely. Participants will get experience making financial decisions they will face as adults, and finish the course with stronger business, financial leteracy, and communication skills.

title: Aerobics
description: Get in shape quick with this high energy class! Aerobics is a fast-paced, dance based class which will slim you down, tone you up and better your cardio vascular system. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Wear comfortable clothing. Please bring water and an exercise mat.

title: Get Comfortable With Internet
description: Here's your chance to have a knowledgeable instructor walk you through the internet world. Topics include choosing how to gain access to the internet, using search tools with confidence, preventing pop-ups, guarding against identity theft and scams, and setting up bookmarks to your favorite sites. Learn about "cookies", Map Quest, Google, Mozilla, Microsoft Internet Explorer, Abode Reader and more.

title: Indust Appl of Algebraic Princ
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas.

title: Knee, Ankle & Feet Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols to relieve pain and stiffness in knees, ankles and feet. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve stiffness and pain - without taking drugs.

title: Internet-Beginners & Tinkerers
description: Find out how easy it is to search the internet for a potpourri of websites in this non-threatening atmosphere. See how to shop online and order airline reservations. Students will explore search engines such as Yahoo and Google to obtain information related to their interest. Also, learn toolbar features such as history, refresh, favorites, home and others.

title: Lightouses Great Lakes
description: Did you know the Great Lakes are home to 350 lighthouses--the highest concentration in North America? To see some of these landmarks, join Wayne Sapulski, author of Lighthouses of Lake Michigan, Past and Present, for an armchair slide show. Wyane has personally visited and photographed all 350 lighthouses. He will discuss the crucial role of lighthouses in maritime history and describe preservation efforts that are currently underway. You will learn where to find these historic treasures and how to get involved in preserving them. Price includes a locator map of Great Lakes lighthouses, so you can personally visit them.

title: Adobe Acrobat: Intermediate
description: This class is designed for people who know how to create PDF documents and are interested in enhancing existing PDF files using Acrobat's more advanced features. Learn to insert one PDF file into another PDF file, add digital signatures, passwords, security settings and restrictions. You will create and modify PDF forms; add radio buttons, check boxes, drop down lists; preview and export forms. Enhance document navigation and create bookmarks. Learn to use Acrobat in the document review cycle, create comment markups, cloud callouts and more.

title: ELL Reading & Study Skills 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college-preparatory reading and study skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELL-182. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Clinical Externship II
description: A full-time supervised clinical practicum at affiliated physical therapy departments. Students participate in patient treatment under the delegation and supervision of a licensed physical therapist. Students integrate classroom knowledge and laboratory experience to achieve the competencies of an entry-level physical therapist assistant as outlined by the American Physical Therapy Association (APTA).

title: ALP: Reading and Writing
description: This course is intended for students whose placement review indicates the need for additional instructional support in order for them to be successful in ENG-131. ENG-094 is a co-requisite of ENG-131A as part of the Accelerated Learning Program and provides writing, reading, study skills, and non-cognitive instructional support of the ENG-131 curriculum. Course work will help students develop as college writers and readers through discussion, workshop, homework, and occasional reading and writing assignments that complement ENG-131 work.

title: Adult Tap Dance 2
description: If you have some experience with tap, join Emily Goward for this intermediate level class. This is a great opportunity to review the basic techniques and add another session of exercise to your life. Bring tap shoes.

title: Solid State Devices
description: Introduces the basic concepts of solid state devices and applications: semiconductor theory, the operational characteristics of devices such as the diode, bipolar junction transistors (BJT) and field effect transistors (FET), polarity, biasing, rectification and amplification. Introduces the basic DC power supply. Includes a discussion of camera-type vision systems, barcode readers and laser etchers as common Solid State devices found in industry. Pre-requisite: CIMEL 102 or Instructor Permission.

title: Ease Pain & Chronic Disease
description: If you are tired of living with pain and swelling, join this class with naturopath Mary Born to find new solutions that don't involve prescription drugs. You will learn that often the root cause of pain and chronic disease is inflammation. Discover how many people have reduced pain and inflammation using food choices, supplements, antioxidants and lifestyle changes. See how reversing inflammation helps alleviate gum disease, arthritis pain, constipation, fatigue, constant hunger, breathing problems, risk for heart disease and more.

title: Piano Class 2
description: This course builds upon skills developed in MUS 117 (Piano Class 1). Instruction takes place in the HFC Music Technology Lab.

title: Plumbing Pipefitting Code
description: Explores how to utilize the State of Michigan Plumbing Code to solve real-life problems (International Plumbing Code). Covers study habits and processes necessary to interpret and apply the code. Also helps prepare students for entry- level positions in the plumbing, multi-skilled maintenance, repair, and apprenticeship fields.

title: The Art of Bead Stringing
description: Tired of buying jewelry that doesn't quite fit? Frustrated with jewelry breaking after wearing it only a few times? Make your own quality jewelry in less than three hours! In this class you will learn the basics of using tools, stringing materials and selecting beads. Then make a fabulous pair of crystal earrings and matching crystal bracelet. $38 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Auto Alignment Clinic
description: Designed to cover the types of front and rear suspension systems used on today¿s vehicles. In addition, there is in-depth coverage of alignment factors. The majority of the class is devoted to inspection, repair, and alignment of a variety of suspension systems.

title: Downsizing 101
description: Do you have family members that moved out but still have items in the home? Do you want to downsize and reclaim the space or move? Let professional organizer Theresa Brune teach you pain-free techniques for sorting and sifting through years of "Stuff" and offer suggestions on what to do with unwanted items. Once you get the space back from the family member that moved out, you can start the re-decorating!

title: Intro to Sociology
description: Introduces the sociological approach to understanding culture and society. Examines human behavior in a group context, focusing on the prominent influence of culture and social structure on individuals. Connections between individuals and a variety of social institutions, such as the economy, government, and mass media, are examined and compared in U.S. society and on a global scale. Focuses on cultural diversity and social inequalities, such as those related to class, race, ethnicity and gender.

title: History of Modern Philosophy
description: A continuation of the history of Western philosophy focusing on the major philosophers and major developments through the twentieth century from Descartes through Wittgenstein. Examines influential currents such as rationalism, empiricism, idealism, romanticism, Marxism, Darwinism, positivism, pragmatism, phenomenology, existentialism and analysis.

title: Spice & Herb for Better Health
description: Beyond their flavorful aromas and delicate flavors, did you know that spices and herbs have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, anticancer and other health properties? If you haven't tried cooking with them, now's the time to start. Bring your apron and together we will create a scrumptious meal: Curry - Honey Chicken, Escabeche (fish with ginger), rosemary and garlic potatoes, and cucumber salad.

title: Topics in Film Study
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-235 to register for this course. Offers special study in an area of film, organized by theme, genre, historical period, or other criteria. Specific topics are listed in the semester class schedule; see individual course syllabi in the Communications division office.

title: Cardio Kickboxing
description: a fast, fun and energizing way to get or stay in shape while decreasing stress and fat! Using a combination of kickboxing techniques put to music, muscle toning and abdominal conditioning, you will burn calories and create a lean, sculpted appearance. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Project Management
description: An advanced-level course which studies project management techniques that provide a foundation for the CompTIA IT Project+ certification and Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK). Also covers project integration, scope, time, cost, quality, human resources, communications, risk, and procurement management. Utilizes Microsoft Project software to manage a project.

title: Steam and Hot Water Systems
description: For technical construction apprentices and those interested in seeking basic pipefitting -plumbing skills. Introduces principles of steam and hydronic systems, converter trap sizing, steam traps, and skimming boilers. Also examines the proper selection, sizing, and installation of pipe and fittings related to hydronic and steam systems. Application exercises allow students the opportunity to design and lay out typical systems. Laboratory activities.

title: ELL Communication 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-102C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Co-op in CIS
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5

title: Get the Red Out
description: Holding anger and grudges can have physiological effects on the mind and body. Biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you these effects and discuss the many emotions often vented as anger. Learn to identify the steps of escalating anger in another person and receive a wealth of helpful tips, including nutrition, to help you deal with anger. discover how to let go of grudges and resentment and why it is important. $5 material fee due to instructor the first session.

title: Auto Chassis Units
description: A study of the construction, operation, and maintenance of the various chassis units. Steering, suspension systems, and alignment are included as well as disc and drum braking systems.

title: Exploring Popular Music
description: This class explores the history of popular music from the 1950's through today, with emphasis on the relationship between music and sociopolitical issues.

title: Surveying & Site Design
description: This course focuses on the principles of basic land surveying and site development. Topics include basic surveying methods, common hardware, surveying tools, site analysis and site design. Course work will be a combination of hands on data collection, digital modeling and construction documentation. Students will learn the principles of linear measurement, angle and elevation measurement, topography, building layout, and site analysis and design.

title: The Fall Garden
description: Autumn is more than a garden clean-up season; it is also the beginning of your spring garden and an opportunity to create a winter landscape. Maintenance tasks such as pruning, transplanting, mulching and preparing the soil for spring plants will be covered. In addition, find out how you can enjoy fall blooms and a beautiful winter scene in your own backyard. Jane Geisler, Advanced Master Gardener, will take you through the steps to a healthy, well-maintained seasonal landscape.

title: Help for Seniors & Caregivers
description: Trying to assist aging parents and ailing loved exhausting. Find networks of support, available locally and in other states, by joining Michelle Hamilton from the Information Center (funded by the Michigan Office of Services to the Aging). She will share strategies for locating community resources and housing options, hiring in-home services, assessing safety risks, gathering necessary medical, legal and financial documents, etc. Enroll today to ease your mind and stretch your family's assets. Includes Caregiver Help Book ($20 value). Register by 2-10-05.

title: Fiesta Con't - Corn Tortillas
description: For those who love to cook and entertain we offer this class to help you develop your repertoire of elegant hors d'oeuvres. These will be made from scratch, using a variety of techniques. Whether preparing a prelude to a small dinner party or welcoming fifty for a holiday open house, you will benefit from increasing your repertoire of starters.

title: Creating Web Pages II
description: Are you using HTML to its fullest potential? In this hands-on course, you'll become adept at using color, tables and frames to give your Web pages a more polished and professional look, improved navigation and more organization. Beef up the fun factor with audio and interactive forms, and learn how to save hours of time and effort with Cascading Style sheets.

title: Introductory Psychology
description: Introduces elementary concepts and principles related to the scientific study of behavior and of the mental processes of cognition and affective states. Variables examined include the history of psychology, the scientific method, theory, biological foundations, psychological processes related to cognition and affective states, developmental changes over time, and applications related to healthy and unhealthy personalities.

title: Complete Stretch & Tone
description: Low impact exercise designed to improve flexibility and core strength while stretching and lengthening your muscles. Common side effects could include increased ability to concentrate, improved posture and reduced stress! Get out of your head and into your body in this yoga-inspired stretching and core conditioning class. Designed for adults of all fitness levels. Wear comfortable clothing and bring a mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Rental Real Est & Prop Managmt
description: Before you buy either residential or commercial rental property get the latest facts about actual profits and potential pitfalls in this illuminating class with successful property manager and investor Peter Pace. You will learn how to find and finance appropriate properties, set a market-driven rent, attract and screen tenants and generate cash flow. Get proven strategies for property management, timely maintenance, and tenant retention to maximize your income. Optional package of customized rental documents available from instructor after class for $25.

title: Heart Health
description: Here's a class for those with high cholesterol, heart conditions, high blood pressure or those seeking understanding and application of nutrition advice for a heart-healthy lifestyle. Dietician Lourdes Lore will help you find out about the diet that can help lower your blood pressure that does double duty to minimize PMS symptoms too.

title: Pen & Ink Drawing
description: Increase your basic drawing skills and expand your knowledge of using pen and ink as a drawing medium. Join artist Saundra Weed for this four-week class and discover how to create artwork suitable for framing, journaling, illustrating books or making cards. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Supply list will be provided in class. Appropriate for all levels.

title: Success With Less Stress - New
description: Do you dream of a day where you are well focused, complete your work or projects, and still have energy left for fun or family? Live your dream by joining Sandy Baumann, popular author of Feed Your Brain for Success, for an inspiring and lighthearted class. Find secrets for improving your productivity and mood with food, beverages, recreation, lifestyle choices, and simple supplements. Enjoy some laughter and a smorgasbord of easy ideas to fuel your brain and put the brakes on your stress! $12 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Programmable Controllers
description: Begins with a thorough discussion of the Allen Bradley Contrologix/Compactlogix programmable controller system characteristics, followed by a detailed presentation on how to use the programmable controller to solve the automated control problem.

title: Cooking With Fresh Herbs
description: Executive Chef Peter Veach of the University of Michigan will show you what to do with all those abundant herbs from your garden. What and how to save and preserve those wonderful flavors of fresh herbs from your garden.

title: Intro to Business
description: Presents an integrated examination of the American business system and the operations of business organizations. Covers theories of management and their application, production, operations, marketing, and financial management of firms.

title: EV Battery Technician Cert
description: Participants in this certificate course will acquire the necessary skills for designing, operating, testing, maintaining, and replacing battery cells and packs for various applications. In addition, the certificate allows participants to choose the right technology for the right challenge and evaluate battery performance. Moreover, to be able to work out a solid total solution, the battery management systems, the charging and maintenance of batteries, and their recycling process are also discussed in more detail. Finally, we give sufficient attention to safely handling these electrical applications, ensuring the well-being of both individuals and the environment.This certification empowers learners to: . Evaluate what battery technology is best suited for which application based on its characteristics. . Examine the advantages and disadvantages of the most commonly used battery technologies. . Design and implement the best-fit battery solution for each application. . Safely handle, test, repair, and dispose of battery systems according to legal obligations.

title: Intermediate Modern Dance III
description: This is the third of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of modern dance technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in modern dance history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and the principles of time, space, and energy. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of modern dance. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of modern dance.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, awareness of the body in space, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Earn & Learn Read & Math Refre
description: Program is a partnership between HFCC and Southwest Solutions designed to aid Earn and Learn Program participants who wish to improve their reading and math skills in order to pursue manufacturing-based employment and/or pre-apprenticeship training.

title: Buying a Computer for Those
description: Have you been thinking about buying your first computer? Well, in this relaxed environment, come learn the difference between a notebook and desktop computer. Get acquainted with terms like hard drive,memory, operating system, and others. Instructor will explain (in beginner's terms) how to be informed when faced with difficult issues of computer buying.

title: Industrial Computer Applicatio
description: Introduces computer systems and associated components typically found in the industrial environment. Presents basic skills in word processing, database, spreadsheet, email, specialized vendor software, and communication software. Also covers Internet and Intranet environments, including searching for industry manuals and parts, downloading software and drivers, and other related tasks. Utilizes the Windows operating system in conjunction with proprietary software. Discusses learning management systems including AMTEC curriculum and Moodle Rooms. Laboratory activities.

title: Weld Metallurgy-Ferrous Metal
description: Covers the mechanical and physical properties and the crystalline structures of ferrous metals. Also explores heat treating of ferrous metals.

title: Auto Engine Dynamometer
description: An advanced-level course in the purpose, construction, and operation of the automotive engine dynamometer. Presents how to conduct engine tests of horsepower and torque, and covers test cell setup of various sensor types and adaptation.

title: Diesel Eng Perf & Diagnosis
description: Offers a comprehensive overview of the operating principles of the diesel engine, including the construction, service, and diagnosis of the various engine systems and sub-systems. Covers the latest technological advancements in electronic fuel delivery systems, their diagnosis, and service along with the most recent advances in diesel fuel and Bio-diesel technology. Extensive laboratory activities.

title: N American Tech Excellence Cer
description: Explores various types of basic work knowledge of the Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning and Refrigeration (HVACR) systems that are installed in the HVACR field. Topics covered include safety, trade tools, heat and matter, electrical fundamentals, transformers and motors, electrical diagrams, and proper use of required test instruments. As a result of this class, students will learn about the structure and HVACR communication skills expected of them in the technology and skilled trade industry. This course helps to prepare students to take the NATE Core Certification Exam based on ACCA, NATE, and RSES information.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Introduces matrix and linear algebra. Covers systems of linear equations, matrix operations and properties of matrices, determinants, the n-dimensional real vector space, general vector spaces, inner product spaces, linear transformations, and eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Presents various applications. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Mouth-Watering Mexican Suppers
description: Under the expert guidance of Chef Tim Dare, discover how easy it is to put together arroz con pollo (chicken and rice), beef enchiladas, quesadillas, and taco salad. Get his secret recipe for Mexican shrimp that will tantalize your taste buds. Match spices to your choice of mild, moderate or HOT! Then sample what you cook. Bring an apron and your favorite hot sauce. Get recipes you can make in 30 minutes!

title: Winter Eve in Historic Beijing
description: Celebrate the Chinese New Year by spending an evening "visiting" China's capital and its surrounding areas. On Tuesday you will enjoy a Chinese dessert, view a display of Chinese souvenirs and tour historic Beijing via a slide presentation of Don Samull's wintertime visit to China. On Saturday you will rejoin Don for lunch at a well-known Chinese Restaurant. Includes meal cost. For directions to the restaurant, go to www.hfcc.edu/CL2 and click on "Supplies & Maps."

title: Adv. Clinical Therapeutic Prac
description: Directed clinical experience that further develops the clinical skills and techniques used to manage patients requiring mechanical ventilator support in the hospital, sub-acute and home care settings. Twenty-four hours per week.

title: Intermediate Ballet II
description: This is the second of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of ballet technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in ballet history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and classical dance principles. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of this traditional and foundational dance art. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of ballet.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, placement, and turn-out, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Pneumatic Power & Control
description: Explores the principles of fluid power pneumatics as applied to industrial systems. Examines various pneumatic components with respect to their functions within pneumatic power and control systems.

title: Adult Tap Dance 1
description: Get in shape and learn to tap! Its fun and it will improve your memory and coordination too! Learn basic steps skills such as shuffles, flaps, slaps, ball changes and cramp rolls. By the end of this session you should be able to perform short combinations with these basic steps. You can take this class over and over until you feel you can perform longer and faster combinations. Tap shoes mandatory or you will not be able to participate.

title: Accounting Fundamentals
description: Develop financial awareness and increase the odds of your business succeeding. You'll learn to do double-entry bookkeeping, analyze and record financial transactions, prepare various financial reports, and more.

title: Polish Special
description: This is one of our series of special Saturday workshops in Advanced Home Cooking. These classes are designed for students who know how to cook but wish to learn new techniques and to make specialty dishes. In this class students will create a range of Polish specialties from stuffed cabbage to pierogi (small dumplings with a variety of fillings) to city chicken. Students will cook their own lunch and divide up the day's creations. Your instructor, Marilyn Szymanski, is the Executive Chef at Eastern Michigan University. Bring an apron. This is a hands-on class.

title: Introduction to Acting
description: Explore the art of theater and discover whether the acting bug is a friendly one for you. Through the use of theater games, physical and vocal warm-ups, role playing and short scenes, you will learn the basics of being alive on stage. Donald Amerson, a graduate of U of M and the Youth Arts Professionals Institute in Los Angeles, is a dynamic instructor who spent the last two years in California practicing his craft. This is a great class for anyone curious about the acting field, as well as for business and professional presentations. Wear loose, comfortable clothing and be prepared to have fun in a safe, supportive environment.

title: Small Bus Bookkeeping
description: Continue learning the vast features of Quickbooks to learn how Quickbooks can be practically applied to your small business. Topics include inventory tracking, dealing with sales tax, creating purchase orders, receiving items, using QB classes, creating estimates, job costing and time tracking, working with customer & vendor profile lists, processing sales discounts & electronic payments, using Quickbooks for payroll, working with balance sheets and more. Prerequisite: Small Business Bookkeeping Using Quickbooks I.
prerequisite_text: Small Business Bookkeeping Using Quickbooks I.

title: Volleyball I
description: Covers the fundamental skills, strategy, history, rules, and values of volleyball. Discusses concept of team play and how to develop an appreciation for the sport as both player and spectator. Course also explores nutrition and health related fitness as it pertains to disease and weight prevention.

title: Creating a Classroom Web Site
description: Learn how to create a classroom Web site in this fun, easy-to-understand course for teachers. In no time, you'll build a site with text, images, animations, tables, links, and more. Then you'll create a WebQuest and a blog to add to your site. You'll do all this and more as you discover how simple creating a classroom Web site can be and how having one can make you a more effective and dynamic teacher.

title: The Art of Magic
description: Amaze your friends, delight the grandkids, and even liven up business meetings with easy-to-learn, easy-to-do effects. By the end of class, you will be able to entertain a small group of people with a 20-30 minute presentation using common, inexpensive household items. You will also learn some popular coin and card tricks that require ittle or no sleight-of-hand skills. Your instructor, Allan Geisler, is a long-time amateur magician and experienced teacher. Bring a used deck of cards.

title: Continuing Guitar
description: Continue developing your guitar playing skills as you learn more advanced techniques. Bring your own guitar and strum your favorite pop, rock, and folk music under the instruction of an experienced guitarist.

title: Polish Soups
description: Poles love their soups and Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suter can really make them. Get ready for the cold. The class will make a Golden Polish Mushroom Soup, Dill Pickle Soup, Borssht (beet) and Chicken soup. Wear an apron and bring a container.

title: Birding Field Trips
description: After attending the Introduction to Bird Watching class (or equivalent experience), participants may choose from a variety of one-day field trips designed to focus on specific types of birds present. February - Feeder Birds; March - Gullds; April - Ducks; May - Warblers. These field trips will provide participants with an opportunity to practice their identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are planned from late winter through spring. All field trips will meet at the HFCC parking lot where we will carpool to the sites, generally within 50 miles, to look for our target birds.

title: Children's Theatre Production
description: Offers lecture and practice in the organization of specific skills necessary for the public performance of children's plays such as acting, assistant directing, stage management, publicity, and house management. Requires students to participate in HFC's annual Children's Theatre presentation.

title: Business Law & Legal Environme
description: Examines a variety of legal topics including the American Legal System, the laws of contracts, business organizations, personal injury and product liability. Focuses on understanding the legal environment in which businesses function.

title: Web Server Administration
description: Introduces the installation, configuration, and management of web server software. Offers hands-on experience in installing and administering server software platforms such as Apache Web Server. Covers web server security, performance monitoring and tuning, virtual hosting, and supporting database and dynamic content. Intended for network, server, or web administration; web development; or computer information system students.

title: Revealing Your Dharma
description: Dharma is the law of our existence, the path of our destiny. Some people know exactly who they are and why they are here, some have a partial understanding, and some don't have a clue, but they would like to learn more about it. Through lectures, discussions, group activities, videos, and written exercises this class will assist participants in a quest for Dharma. The aim of this class is to let the group work together to increase each person's understanding of his own Dharma and then suggest ways to enhance your ongoing pursuit of it. The group will also explore ways to create a legacy.

title: Adv Grant Proposal Writing
description: Do you know the best way to present information in a grant proposal? In this course, an experienced grant writer will show you how to research and write winning proposals. Learn what to do--and, more importantly what not to do--on every part of your proposal. Understand the criteria funders use to determine whether your grant proposal receives money.
