You'll become expert at ferreting out corporate, foundation, and government grant makers, and you'll know how to tailor your responses to information found in the peer review criteria. You'll also discover a number of significant finishing touches that can give your project the edge over others. You'll know the best type of paper to use, which buzzwords to include, which fonts work best, and which types of graphics and formatting techniques will make your proposal more competitive.

title: Acrylic Painting 1
description: Among the most versatile media ever developed, acrylic colors offer an extraordinary variety of painting methods. In this 8-week class professional artist Saundra Weed will provide a wealth of useful suggestions and practical information about acrylic painting methods and materials. You'll learn about colors and their characterics, painting surfaces and their preparation, painting tools and much more. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. For supply list, got to "Supplies & Maps".

title: ABS Brakes
description: Covers the theory and operation of Anti-lock Brake Systems (ABS) and traction control systems. Includes the design and construction of ABS braking systems, as well as traction control system operating principles. Diagnostic techniques, troubleshooting, and repair of ABS/TC, along with service techniques and hands-on experiences are an integral component of the coursework.

title: Advanced Modern Dance
description: Explores the finer points of Modern Dance. Primary emphasis is on performing advanced movement phrases along with the study and practice of specific Modern Dance techniques. Course also supports the development of choreographic skill and stage presence.

title: Greek for Travelers
description: Whether you are planning a trip to Greece or simply want to learn conversational Greek skills, this course will introduce you to the appropriate use of Greek in various social situations and will provide you cultural information about everyday life in modern Greece. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Thessaloniki, Greece, will teach you basic expressions that will help you when making accommodations, eating out, traveling, sightseeing, shopping and more. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $15).

title: Solidworks Level I- Intro
description: Introduces SolidWorks and its functionality including the creation of a rough sketch of geometry and how to correctly define it. Explores how to set up drawing units, modify dimensions on a sketch, and complete a 3-dimensional (3D) solid model. Also covers features of SolidWorks including orthographic views and basic file management procedures.

title: Dining Room Captain
description: Covers advanced principles of table service and managing the production dining room. Emphasizes effective management procedures and techniques including scheduling, table assignments, side work, reservations, expediting, and training of the HOSP-150 students.

title: Chicken Around the World - New
description: Save your budget and your heart by joining Chef Tim Dare to tour the world using fast, easy-to-prepare chicken recipes. Take plain chicken and pair it with wonderful sauces to create chicken picatta (Italian), lemon & orange chicken (Chinese), Malibu chicken with honey mustard (American) and chicken Marsala (Italian). He will match entrees with an appropriate vegetable and starch to round out your menus. Bring an apron and your appetite. Take home wonderful recipes.

title: Workout With Pilates
description: Flatten abs, strengthen and tone muscles, and improve your flexibility, endurance and posture using the Pilates method, one of today's hottest trends. Developed over 90 years ago, Pilates is a tried-and-true, low-impact exercise system. It is popular with athletes, dancers, and anyone who wants to develop a more streamlined shape using an all-encompassing mind, body and spirt exercise. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Shielded Metal Arc Welding
description: Examines the theory and practical operation of shielded metal arc welding in both a flat and horizontal welding position. Emphasizes safety protocols, machine settings, and filler metals.

title: Fabulous Food Garnishes
description: Entertaining thoughts of throwing a sensational soiree, a fabulous fete, or a perfect party? Learn easy ways to dress up your dishes for most any occasion with stunning garnishes. You'll sculpt a strawberry fan, apply bird, tomato rose, melon swan, veggie centerpiece and more. Wear an apron and bring a cutting board and paring knife.

title: Literacy Foundations
description: Explores the principles, methods, and materials for teaching literacy through a research-based, integrated approach with children PreK-5. Emphasis is placed on the influence of a child's linguistic, cultural, and social background, fostering engaging literacy development in diverse and multicultural communities. Instructional methods include lectures, discussions, videos, small group work, hands-on experiences, and individual and group projects. The primary components of reading - phonemic awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, and comprehension - will be studied through assessment strategies and applied to teaching methods. The NAEYC (student learning outcomes) and MDE Quality Standards are addressed in this course.

title: Introduction to Public Health
description: Provides a broad overview of the various aspects of public health in the United States. Presents applications, stakeholders, and methods of public health in a way that allows for the identification of strengths and limitations of the current model of care delivery. Also offers an overview of careers available in the area of public health.

title: Introductory Psychology
description: Introduces elementary concepts and principles related to the scientific study of behavior and of the mental processes of cognition and affective states. Variables examined include the history of psychology, the scientific method, theory, biological foundations, psychological processes related to cognition and affective states, developmental changes over time, and applications related to healthy and unhealthy personalities.

title: Lebanese Cooking V
description: Arayes Lebanese kabab. Crush lintel soup. Lebanese cabbage salad.

title: ELL American Culture I
description: Introduces low-beginning-level knowledge of American culture for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-102A. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: EV Battery Technician Cert
description: Participants in this certificate course will acquire the necessary skills for designing, operating, testing, maintaining, and replacing battery cells and packs for various applications. In addition, the certificate allows participants to choose the right technology for the right challenge and evaluate battery performance. Moreover, to be able to work out a solid total solution, the battery management systems, the charging and maintenance of batteries, and their recycling process are also discussed in more detail. Finally, we give sufficient attention to safely handling these electrical applications, ensuring the well-being of both individuals and the environment.This certification empowers learners to: . Evaluate what battery technology is best suited for which application based on its characteristics. . Examine the advantages and disadvantages of the most commonly used battery technologies. . Design and implement the best-fit battery solution for each application. . Safely handle, test, repair, and dispose of battery systems according to legal obligations.

title: Intro to Healthcare Profession
description: This course is designed to introduce students to various health care professions. It includes skills and topics common to all healthcare providers such as ethical and legal issues, professionalism, cultural considerations, vital signs, body mechanics, communication, infection control, and evidence based practice. This course will also introduce key elements in the formation and modification of medical terms. This course has a lab hands on component.

title: Calculus III
description: Covers topics such as the calculus of vector-valued functions, the differential calculus of functions of more than one variable, directional derivatives, gradients, partial derivatives, multiple integration, vector fields, and line integrals. Various applications are covered throughout the course. Utilizes numerical, graphical, and algebraic approaches whenever possible. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Microbiology for AHS
description: For health service personnel not needing the extensive laboratory experience and breadth of material of BIO 251. Emphasizes the biology of medically important microbes, epidemiology and disease transmission, sterile technique, basics of immunity, the microbiology of wounds, and current regulations regarding blood-borne pathogens and bio-hazardous wastes. Three hours of lecture and two hours of lab per week.

title: International Cooking
description: Presents a comprehensive overview of cuisines throughout the world. Explores how demographic changes and the accessibility of travel have altered America's cultural and culinary perspectives of the world at large.

title: Windows Version 7 for Upgrader
description: This course is designed for persons who are familiar with and comfortable working in Windows XP and want to upgrade to the new and enhanced features of Windows 7. This course is not for students who are first-time Windows users.

title: Fall Seasonal Vegetables
description: Not sure what to do with all those fall vegetables? Chef Marilyn Suter will give you some healthy alternatives to meat and potatoes. We will make a butternut squash soup, enjoy a root vegetable salad that could be eaten hot or cold, a cooked celery root side dish and more. Come prepared to cook and have a good time.

title: Charm Bracelet
description: Design and make a stunning charm bracelet using a technique that will virtually guarantee that your charms will never fall off. You'll use a combination of semi-precious stones, crystal glass, sterling silver and other fun items to embellish the bracelet. Additional charms can be added later to enhance its beauty. Such a bracelet might sell for over $100 in a fine jewelry store. $35 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Cool Stuff for Sizzling Days
description: Forget the "same ole same ole." This class will help you to add some pizzazz to your summer entertaining. The salads and side dishes that will be prepared will be perfect for summer: quick and easy and able to be made ahead to leave you free to enjoy your company. Chef Marilyn Szymanski-Suter is an executive chef. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Unique Gifts Made With Money
description: Long stem roses with petals made from real money? An adorable mini men's shirt made almost entirely from real money? In this hands-on class you'll learn both of these very popular crafts! Great for party favors, gift giving, centerpieces, etc. The money is not damages in any way and can be easily removed for spending. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $16-$18).

title: Fall Birding in Michigan
description: If you are at least 16 years old, join Allen Chartier, co-author of Birds of Detroit and a very active birder, for a unique fall birding adventure. Through classroom instruction and field work you will improve your observation skills, develop ways to record your sightings and learn to use field marks to identify birds. After an initial classroom session, plan to meet your instructor at local sites to practice identifying and observing a variety of birds in their native habitats. Directions to the sites will be provided in the first class. Bring binoculars to enrich your experience. The required textbook, The Sibley Guide to Birds, will be available in class for approximately $25.

title: Jigs, Fixtures, and Tools
description: Covers advanced techniques in design, layout, and detail of production tooling. Consideration is given to locating, clamping, and tolerancing a jig and fixture for the manufacture of an industrial part. Standard parts catalogs and library reference materials are used to provide guidance in solving design problems. Drawing assignments are done extensively on CAD.

title: Exercise Physiology
description: Explores the physiological principles of exercise with emphasis on the circulatory, respiratory, and muscular systems. Discusses what changes occur to the body during exercise and as a result of regular exercise. Establishes a scientific basis for how to prescribe exercise appropriately. Laboratory activities include measurement of oxygen uptake, fat and muscle composition, anaerobic ability, and muscular strength.

title: Auto Chassis Units
description: A study of the construction, operation, and maintenance of the various chassis units. Examines steering, suspension systems, and alignment as well as disc and drum braking systems.

title: Buffet Entertaining
description: Whether you're planning a graduation party, a shower or another gathering of 50-100 people, make it a day to remember. Professional caterer Joy Callan will teach you how to save time, money and effort with hassle-free buffet entertaining. You will learn everything from menu planning to grocery shopping, food preparation and serving the meal. Bring your appetite, an apron and entertaining questions. Joy's a pro! Enroll early! Class limited to 14.

title: Advanced Powerpoint
description: Are you familiar with PowerPoint's tools and now want to give your slides presentations and printed materials a more professional look? In this course, Althea Lockridge, an award-winning editor & writer, will share techniques for balancing space, text, graphics, photos, animation, and color. PowerPoint: Basics and Beyond or strong basic knowledge of PowerPoint recommended.

title: Energy Systems Management
description: Presents the technical, economic, and regulatory aspects of energy efficient building energy management. Explores the latest energy and cost-reduction strategies. Focuses on laboratory exercises designed to simulate residential and commercial energy systems.

title: Stagecraft
description: Offers a basic survey of the major techniques and theories of scenography used in the modern theatre.

title: Ceramics III
description: An intermediate level course stressing refinement of ceramic construction techniques and design, primarily through utilitarian objects. This class also offers the opportunity for individual exploration of ceramic glaze and surface possibilities. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Theatre Experience II
description: This is the second of three experience-based one-credit courses where students work onstage, backstage, or in front-of-house operations for theatre productions during the semester. The course includes instructor presentation of specific elements of application though lecture, lecture/demonstration and/or laboratory experience.The production work for Theatre Experience II builds on the production work completed in Theatre Experience I. In this course, the student will practice and refine techniques learned in the previous Theatre Experience course in addition to learning new parts, backstage practices, etc.

title: Advanced Clinical Education
description: This is fifth in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) competencies. Course emphasizes headword including skull, facial bones, paranasal sinuses, and increasing technical proficiency in all previously covered radiographic procedures. Students will continue to develop trauma radiography skills and advanced mobile radiography skills.

title: Get Started With Computer (xp)
description: If you can operate a car, you can operate a computer. Don't let confusing computer jargon stop you! Attend this hands-on workshop designed for the person with little or no experience, and see how easy it is to use the basic features of Windows XP. You'll learn how to start and shut down a computer safely, use a computer mouse, navigate the keyboard, create simple files and more! By the end of the class you will finally understand the lingo of your computer savvy friends and relatives. Open to persons of any age.

title: Power Market for Photographers
description: Are you a wedding, portrait or senior photographer or a photography studio manager who would like to increase your cash flow? Are you having too many slow days and not enough cash? Well then, join photographer Andreas as he shares his simple 10-5-1 steps of completing the sale and increasing your cash flow. This three session class will focus on re-activating old clients, using the telephone to sell, and other useful topics.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 5
description: Develops intermediate-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-106W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Explore Japan
description: Whether you're an armchair traveler, first-time tourist, or experienced Japanophile, you'll gain insights into one of the world's most intriguing cultures from former Tokyo resident, Rick Gartland. He will share practical travel information for navigating the country. He'll give a behind-the-scenes look at the Japanese people and their customs and discuss "must-see" sights, including strategies for climbing Mt. Fuji!

title: Monitor Child's Internet Use
description: Do you know about MySpace, Facebook and Friendster? Chances are they may be an essential part of your teens social networking. Learn what MySpace and its contemporaries are all about, how to navigate them, create profiles, find and monitor your child's activities, and more. Students must have a working knowledge in computers and the internet.

title: Con't Conversational Italian
description: This class is for those who completed Conversational Italian 1 or for persons with a basic knowledge of the language. Books available for purchase the first night of class.

title: Organic Chemistry I
description: Introduces organic chemistry. Emphasizes structural theory, stereochemistry, physical properties, reaction mechanisms, and functional group chemistry. This course covers the first semester of a full year course. It is a lecture course designed to meet the requirements for science majors, pre-professional biology, pre-professional chemistry, as well as premedical, pre-dental, pre-pharmacy, pre-veterinary, and chemical engineering students. Students should check requirements for organic chemistry at their intended transfer institution. Four hours of lecture per week without lab.

title: Bldg Analyst Cert Written Test
description: Take the Building Performance Institute (BPI) written test for Building Analyst certification. Admission restricted. Contact Diane McCarthy at 313-845-9798 for details.

title: Hot Glass
description: Enjoy the roar of the 2100 degree furnace as you dip your pipe into the molten glass and try to make it stay attached to the 5 foot steel pole in your hands. Glassblowing is not for the faint of heart, but in this class you can say you actually worked with the molten glass. Instructor will give several short demonstrations, explain the process, history and answer any questions before the students work the hot glass themselves. Please bring sunglasses and wear loose fitting cotton clothing, especially a long sleeve cotton shirt. Your project will be available to take home after cooling (approx. 24 hrs). A materials fee of $40 is payable to the instructor in class. class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Comp Control Ignit Sys & Engin
description: An intermediate-level course on computer-controlled ignition systems and engine performance. Studies systems currently in use as well as newer systems available in the future.

title: Nature Painting Wth Pen & Wash
description: Explore your interest in nature and landscape through pen and ink wash! This is an introductory level course. In each class students will learn about the tools and techniques used in pen and ink wash paintings and spend time creating a landscape of their own. A supply list will be discussed at the first class meeting.

title: Printmaking Without a Press
description: Create a one-of-a-kind print as well as multiple images that can be used as frameable artwork or in combination with other projects such as collages, journal covers, covered treasure boxes, photo albums, scrapbooking, cards and more. A rolling pin, plus easy-to-use tools found around your home, will be used to create printing plates. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Supply list will be provided in the first class.

title: Plumbing Pipefitting Code
description: Explores how to utilize the State of Michigan Plumbing Code to solve real-life problems (International Plumbing Code). Covers study habits and processes necessary to interpret and apply the code. Also helps prepare students for entry- level positions in the plumbing, multi-skilled maintenance, repair, and apprenticeship fields.

title: Inside the Music Biz
description: Whether you're starting, thinking about starting, or strengthening a career in the music industry, enhance your chances for success in just eight weeks. Learn from an experienced production company founder what it takes to make it as a performer, technician or executive in this fascinating field. You'll become familiar with the basics of songwriting, publishing, promoting, licensing, producing, merchandising, copyrighting and more.

title: Work Wth Learn Disable Student
description: Learn how to successfully meet the diverse needs of the learning disabled students in your classroom.

title: Nutrition for the Professional
description: Introduces health care professionals to the basics of nutrition and its application to disease, growth, and development.

title: Indust App of Phys Science
description: Offers the apprentice lecture exposure to the applications of physics and chemistry found in today's industrial workplace. Covers the six elemental machines; applications of forces, motion, and work; and the chemistry of industrial materials and chemical interactions in the environment.

title: Screenwriting Act 1
description: Learn to write visually in this writing-intensive course. Through assignments, class critiques, and screenings of films and music videos which demonstrate storytelling techniques, effective characters, and convincing dialogue, instructor Frank Jamal will guide you in composing your first act. You will leave with your own complete Act 1 for a music video, commercial or 5-minute movie.

title: Preserve Family History
description: Join Michael Neill, a noted genealogist who has presented workshops throughout the Midwest, and discover effective ways to use the Internet to promote your research and contact family members and fellow researchers. You will also learn methods of publishing and preserving your genealogical compilations on the computer, as well as the pros and cons of various digital and print publication methods.

title: Adult Hip Hop (mini-Session)
description: If you want to continue your high-energy dance routine, this mini - session is for you.

title: Supply Chain Logistics
description: This 75 hour course, when successfully completed, will make the student an attractive candidate for the many entry and mid-level positions available in the supply chain logistics field. The training will provide the student with the broad knowledge to understand the Transportation, Distribution and Logistics field. Entry-level areas studied will include the global supply chain, material handling, safety, quality control, workplace communications, the importance of teamwork, problem solving and using the computer. More advanced areas studied will include product receiving, storing, order processing, packaging, shipping, tracking, handling hazardous materials, and inventory control. While mastering these measurements and metric conversions. After completion of the program, students will be eligible to test for the nationally recognized certification through MSSC as a Certified Logistics Associate and Certified Logistics Technician.

title: Intro to Industrial Hydraulics
description: Introduces the principles of fluid power as applied to industrial systems. Presents various hydraulic components and their functions within fluid power systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Introduction to CNC
description: An entry-level course covering the basic concepts of computer numerical control (CNC). Also explores set-up, operation, and programming of a CNC mill and CNC lathe. Laboratory activities.

title: Second-Year French IV
description: Follows FRE-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level French courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in French, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written French. Presents francophone culture.

title: Wall Makeovers With Paint
description: Give your home a fresh new look with a simple coat of paint. Instead of hiring someone, attend this workshop and learn to do it yourself like a "pro." A professional painter will explain how to properly plan and organize a project; discuss different paint types, finishes, tools and supplies; demonstrate proper wall preparation (including drywall repairs); and share some "tricks of the trade" that will simplify your life. Bring your questions. $10 material fee payable to instructor.

title: Complete Retirement Planning
description: Whether you're 10 days, 10 months or 10 years from retiring, you need to explore many issues before making that move. In this class you will examine the financial issues of retirement as well as such psychological aspects as personal and spousal adjustment, changes in family dynamics, and how you will spend your time. A licensed clinical psychologist, an estate-planning attorney, and a CPA will discuss how to make this much-anticipated transition successfully.

title: Lightarian Reiki Levels 1 & 2
description: Lightarian Reiki is the next higher band of energy Lightarian Reiki is a very masculine, yang energy, designed to really get your life moving! Included is the Lightarian Levels 1 and 2 with the Buddhic Boost. Manuals will be available for ordering after your attunement. Master Level required.

title: Exploration of Auto Tech
description: Introduces basic automotive technology principles, skills, and terminology with the assistance of an online course element (blended learning environment). Topics include career exploration in automotive technology, safety, tools and equipment, shop procedures, basic automotive maintenance, vehicle testing, and competitive automobiles.

title: Overhead Distribution
description: Designed as an introduction to operation and maintenance of overhead electrical distribution systems. Electrical theory application for various distribution line construction and overhead line configurations, energizing and de-energizing techniques, troubleshooting, and grounding of overhead circuits are explored. Single and three phase distribution transformer applications are discussed. Students will be able to identify and demonstrate personal and electrical safety procedures utilized in overhead distribution switching, isolating, testing and diagnostic applications. Overhead service and power quality issues are analyzed in relation to balanced loading and overall customer satisfaction.

title: Fall Birding
description: For a unique fall birding adventure join Arthur Chartier, coauthor of Birds of Detroit and a very active birder. Through classroom instruction and field work you will improve your observation skills, develop ways to record your sightings and learn to use field marks to identify birds. After an initial classroom session, plan to meet your instructor at local sites to practice identifying and observing a variety of birds in their native habitats. Directions to the sites will be provided the first day of class. Bring binoculars to enrich your experience.

title: Creat Link Necklace & Bracelet
description: Create your own multi-colored link necklace and bracelet set. You will learn to fasten these lush multi-colored links together to create a whimsical and vibrant set to take home with you the same evening. Keep it for your own enjoyment or great for gifting. No experience is necessary.

title: Earth Science for Educators
description: An inquiry-based course covering topics in the earth sciences - geology, meteorology, and astronomy. Course content is designed to give students a thorough familiarity with concepts critical to teaching earth science at the K-6 level and how the topics covered in the course fulfill National Science Education Standards. Learning is primarily through student inquiry and discovery of core concepts, with guidance and facilitation provided by the instructor. Four hours of integrated lecture and lab per week.

title: Yoga - Hatha Yoga 1
description: Hatha Yoga is an ancient system of exercise that includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through yoga, your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment. You will achieve mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Typical Everyday German Meal
description: If you love German cuisine, you are going to love this class. If you are not sure and just want to try it, you are also in the right place. Join Ursula Markovits, who was born and raised in Germany, for a bit of the country's food and culture. Learn how to whip up a traditional German dinner of rouladen (stuffed beef rolls), noodles, and cherry bread pudding. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Earn & Learn Read & Math Refre
description: Program is a partnership between HFCC and Southwest Solutions designed to aid Earn and Learn Program participants who wish to improve their reading and math skills in order to pursue manufacturing-based employment and/or pre-apprenticeship training.

title: EMS Instructor Coordinator
description: This course is designed for currently licensed EMS professionals who would like to become qualified to instruct in EMS educational programs. It also adresses al of the course objectives in the EMS National curricla and the National Dept. of Transportation curricula. Participants must have at least 3 years EMS experience and two letters of recommendation from currently licensed I/Cs. Successful completers are eligible to take the state exam for Instructor Coordinators. Note: All participants must complete 30 hours of student teaching prior to taking the state exam.

title: Chinese Medicine in Your Life
description: Gain additional insight into traditional Chinese medicine as you explore the pathways and relationships of the organ meridian system. Practice simple effective methods to address everyday ailments using the subtle energetics of acupressure therapeutics. Wear loose comfortable clothing.

title: Intuition Development
description: In this workshop Professional Intuitive Kristy Robinett will provide proven ways to tap into your own innate intuitive ability, which will help increase your effectiveness in all areas of your life. Participants will be able to: 1) use proven methods of centering to gain better clarity of the issues impacting their clients or themselves; 2) trust their intuition by removing their fear of tapping into this keen insight; and 3) be able to identify which of the three main types of intuition is their strongest. Gather up your friends and make a night of it!

title: Exercise Techniques I
description: Provides instruction in therapeutic exercise procedures utilized by physical therapist assistants. Topics include assessment of joint motion and muscle strength, implications of range of motion (ROM) and muscle testing for exercise prescription, types of exercise, basic exercise programs, and the use of exercise equipment. Theory only-no lab activities.

title: Co-op in CIS
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5

title: Easy Movement With Feldenkrais
description: Discover the way that small, gentle movements can help you to think about how you move and force your brain to focus on these movements through the Feldenkrais method. Marj Levine, who taught our Effortless Gardening class featured in a recent article in the Detroit Free Press, is a certified Feldenkrais instructor who can use simple movements (or sometimes just imagining the movements) to ease tension, boost coordination and relieve muscles. "With Feldenkrais, we don't ever try and fix anything," she says. "We attempt to improve the whole system." The Feldenkrais Method is about learning, not about doing exercises. Bring a floor mat and an open mind.

title: Alignment Training
description: This course covers alignment basics. It was intended for all personnel who will be involved with the mechanical maintenance and functions of laser alignment.

title: Maps of Our Karmic Destiny
description: The ancients determined that there are various methods of divination or karmic road maps to understand our life lessons and destinies. Robert will discuss such maps as astrology, palmistry and various mystic oracles, with personal demonstrations for you.

title: Spring Flowering Bulbs
description: Fall is the time to plant tulips, daffodils, crocus, hyacinth, and other hardy bulbs. Join Nancy Fedon, Advanced Certified Master Gardener, and learn how to select bulbs - including some uncommon hardy ones - for your specific garden conditions. You'll also discuss fertilization, division, storage, and effective planting combinations, as well as the perfect soil recipe for long-lasting blooms. You'll even find out how easy it is to force spring bulbs for a refreshing winter display.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories as you work your upper body, lower body and abdominals. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Engineering Physics I
description: Designed to meet the requirements of engineering students and physics majors. Covers kinematics, dynamics, energy, rotational motion, gravitation, simple harmonic motion, and thermodynamics. Four hours of lecture and three hours of laboratory activities per week. NOTE: Recommended co-requisite is MATH-183.

title: Instruction Tech for Educators
description: Covers basic computer literacy, including Microsoft Office, popular alternate productivity suites (e.g. Google Docs), use of the world-wide-web, and security, privacy, responsibility and ethics in a technological and online world. Also introduces future teachers to educational software, apps, devices, resources, and the pedagogy of technology integration. Includes the development of an electronic portfolio which demonstrate various ways of incorporating technology in the classroom environment. This course is designed as the first course in Instructional Technology for the early childhood and pre-education student and fulfills the HFC Institutional Outcome in Computer Technology.

title: Electronics Theory
description: Stresses the fundamental theories of electronic components and elementary semi-conductor circuit applications. Covers power supplies, amplifiers, oscillators, and semi-conductor controls. Includes use of test equipment, including oscilloscopes and meters.

title: Independent Student Industria
description: The Industrial Sewing Certificate Program instructs students in entry level skills to operate sewing machines and tools associated in the sewing industry. Safety, Ergonomics, various sewing machines, stitches and finishes, set-up, and quality are some of the topics that will be taught. Students will be provided the opportunity to practice on a variety of materials such as woven, knits, expanded vinyl, fine fabrics, and leather. Basic maintenance and troubleshooting skills will also be covered.

title: Sensors and Photoeyes
description: This course presents common circuits and sensors to perform the measurement of process flow variables such as pressure, level, temperature, flow and analytic characteristics. Positional sensors are also developed. Pre-requisite: CIMEL 104 or Instructor Permission.

title: Math for the Modern World
description: For students pursuing a liberal arts curriculum or a program without a specified mathematics requirement. Topics include numerical reasoning; personal finance; probability; statistics; geometry; and voting methods. Emphasizes techniques of problem-solving and application of modern mathematics to understanding quantitative information in the everyday world.

title: RT Clinical Sciences
description: This course focuses on the clinical application of science to the practice of respiratory care. Topics include oxygenation, acid-base balance, ventilation in health and disease, basic microbiology, common microbial pathogens seen with pulmonary infection and the physical principles of spontaneous and mechanical ventilation. Emphasis is placed on blood gas interpretation, capnography, arterial puncture techniques, and disinfection and sterilization of respiratory care equipment.

title: Dementia Caregiving
description: Are you caring for someone with dementia and find yourself at a loss as to how to keep them active and involved? Learn how one family has cared for their mother/wife at home for 16 years. In this workshop, Pricilla Davis, MAE, will share a variety of ways she has found successful in working with her own mother. Meet other care- givers and find new methods of creating a better environment for all.

title: Arabic Calligraphy
description: Introduces students to the history and development of different styles of Arabic Calligraphy- both traditional and modern. Students will learn the use of the traditional letters writing in the Kufic, Thuluth, Ruq`ah, Diwani, Naskh and modern styles. By the end of the course, students should be able to produce calligraphic compositions of their own by utilizing the rules of measurement and creativity. They will also be able to distinguish between various styles in historical calligraphic monuments. Basic knowledge of the Arabic language is helpful, but not necessary. As a part of this course, students will visit the Detroit Institute of Art.

title: Chandelier Earrings
description: Tired by buying jewelry that doesn't quite fit? Frustrated with jewelry that breaks after you wear it a few times? Then make your own quality jewelry in less than three hours! In this class you will learn the basics of using tools, stringing materials and selecting beads. Then make a fabulous pair of crystal-and-pearl chandelier earrings. $28 supply fee due to insructor in class.

title: Introduction to Social Work
description: The course takes a generalist practitioner approach to introduce social work philosophy, values, ethics, and the social work processes embedded within the field. This course is intended to provide students with a basic knowledge of the different elements of the social systems approach and how change can affect an individual's and family's ability to meet human needs.

title: Selling Home in Today's Market
description: Learn how to receive the most for your house and sell quickly in this hard buyer's market. Don't let the market beat you up. Learn how you can still come ahead selling your house. $15 material packet available from instructor.

title: MS Word: Tips Beyond Basics
description: Class will focus on how to use many less-known time saving feasures in Microsoft Word. Learn to use format styles, drop caps and auto text. Give new meaning to words and phrases by using the thesaurus, synonym and translation features. Find out how to use auto correct and password protect your document. Learn to convert text to tables, tables to text and so much more!

title: How to Write a Federal Grant
description: A successful federal grant application is one that is well-prepared, carefully and timely planned, and concisely packaged. Designed for the intermediate/advanced grant writer, you will learn how to read and understand the program announcement, answer the criteria, and present a high scoring application. You will learn how to complete a logic model, create evaluation tools, and create collaborative agreements and support letters. You will gain firsthand knowledge on where to go and how to research grant opportunities. This workshop is your opportunity to learn how to win government and foundation funding! Gain a better understanding of the grant writing process and increase your chances of writing and submitting successful grants.

title: Surgical Procedures I
description: Designed to continue to develop the concepts from SRG-104. It features general, gynecology, genitourinary, orthopedic, otorhinolaryngology, and endoscopy surgical procedures. The course also emphasizes the scheme or steps as well as the instrumentation of these surgical interventions. One or two half day operating room (OR) observations are required.

title: Intro Law Enf & Crim Just
description: An overview of the criminal justice system in theU.S. Topics surveyed are:the history of law enforcement: the political, sociological, and philosophic, and background of police functions; and the criminal courts. Constitutional problems as they relate to the police function are surveyed, and the use of recent technology in criminal justice is discussed.

title: Heating Systems
description: Introduces the principles of steam and hydronic heating systems. Covers proper sizing and selection of converters, traps, and boilers.

title: Programming PLCs
description: Introduces various elements of programming PLCs. Addresses the basic elements of PLC programming and routines. Requires student to program using ladder logic, structured text, sequential function chart, and function block languages. Pre-requisites: CIMEL 109 or Instructor Permission.

title: Making Sense of Your Retiremen
description: If you don't prepare for your retirement, no one else will. That's why it's important to take time now to discuss your retirement goals. An experienced financial advisor will help you learn more about the five basic questions you should ask yourself to prepare for retirement. Discover options you can use now to save for retirement, and how time can help or hurt your retirement goals. Take the first steps toward a successful retirement. Call today to reserve space for yourself and a spouse or friend.

title: Aerobics
description: Roll your way into shape! Get an aerobic workout while using skills you learned in beginner skating. Have fun on your skates and learn how to create your own exercise program all at the same time. An International Inline Skating Association (IISA) certified instructor will guide you through a circuit of moves that will get your heart rate going, and help to improve your skating skills. Students must provide skates equipped with a heel brake, helmet, wrist guards, elbow and kneepads.

title: Painting I
description: A beginning level college painting class that explores traditional and contemporary approaches in observational painting. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Purchasing & Supply Chain Mgmt
description: Introduces the critical function in Supply Chain Management (SCM) known as purchasing. Covers purchasing decisions, strategies, procedures, supplier selections, and negotiations. Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able to demonstrate competency in the following areas of SCM: purchasing strategies, legal aspects of purchasing, material management, just-in-time (lean) purchasing, procurement, strategic outsourcing, global sourcing, total quality management, cost and price determination, and bargaining and negotiations.

title: Return Rider Basic Rider Crse
description: A one day course designed for the experienced but unlicensed rider to become licensed and legal. Many experienced, licensed riders use this course as a refresher and skills tune up. Topics include: braking, counter steering, turning, speed judgment, and other techniques necessary for the experienced rider to operate a motorcycle skillfully and safely. To participate in this class, you must be at least 18 years old. If you are 16 or 17, you may take this class if you have a valid license and a current motorcycle endorsement. Each student must provide their own motorcycle. Sharing is not allowed. The cycle must be titled, properly registered, and display a valid license plate. Each student must provide proof of insurance and the bike must pass a safety inspection. Successful completion allows a student to waive the Secretary of State riding skills test.

title: Internet Marketing
description: Maximize the effectiveness of your website by creative Internet marketing. This course will cover the importance of web content, usability and "stickness." Stay in touch with your customers using email and other tools. Learn about search engine placement and the importance of links.

title: Mechanical Power Transmission
description: Presents installation and maintenance of mechanical power transmission systems. Covers bearings, couplings, belts, chains, shafts, pulleys, and speed reducers used in the modern factory by skilled trades.

title: ELL Communication 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course, along with satisfactory completion of ELI-106R and 106W, qualifies students for ELL-130. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Start Wedding/Portrait Bus
description: Are you an aspiring photographer? Do you enjoy taking pictures of others and wish you could make money doing it? If you have been thinking of starting a wedding/portrait photography business but are not sure where to start, photographer Andreas Browne will teach you. This two session workshop will give you all the information needed to get started the right way. The workshop consists of 12 comprehensive steps covering every aspect of starting and running a part-time or full-time based wedding/portrait business.

title: Civil War Series, Class 2
description: Did you know that soldiers of the American Civil War were stationed at the arsenal in Dearborn? Ron Cleveland has studied Battery M of the First Michigan Light Artillery and has the only surviving battle flag of this unit to show you. Through slides and stories he will describe the life and times of this unit through the eyes of nelson Cheney, one of the last surviving members of Battery M. Ron has written for major Civil War publications, loves to hunt relics, and belongs to the Civil War Preservationist Trust and the Michigan Regimental Round Table and other organizations. Feel the soldiers come alive through Ron's informative and entertaining presentation.

title: Jazz Dancing
description: Express yourself in the syncopated beat of jazz. Some basic moves include: ball change, twist, pivot step, touch step, Pique' passe', stag leap, jete', pencil spin, barrel turn, fan kick, jazz walk, jazz run, hip walk, chasses, cat walk and the catch step. Jazz dancing incorporates a wide variety of basic ballet based steps and modern dance. Join the fun with dance master Emily

title: Ocular Measurements
description: Discusses basic ophthalmic equipment and measurement protocol, instrument calibration, maintenance, and infection control. Students apply concepts to specific clinical situations related to vision care.

title: Property Law
description: Explores different types of real property ownership and the rights and liabilities associated with each type. Examines the anatomy of a real estate closing, real property leases, and the laws surrounding foreclosure and eviction. Covers the drafting of documents relevant to both the acquisition and loss of real property.

title: Second-Year German IV
description: Follows GER-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level German courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written German. Presents the cultures of German-speaking countries.

title: Win the Weight Game
description: Don't give up the foods you love! Instead, give up dieting and find out how to lose weight and keep it off permanently. Through this class, you will find out why just willpower or deprivation never work. Instead change your thinking about food and learn how to develop a healthy approach to eating and exercise. You will acquire skills to stay motivated for a lifetime.

title: Ear Wires for Beginners
description: Make your own pierced ear wires for a fraction of what you'd pay for them in retail stores. Bring your tools to class and learn several techniques for making ear wires. $4 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Applied Music (LSR&R)
description: Half-hour private lessons with a professional private teacher, intended to learn techniques for sound reinforcement and recording of live music performances. Students should contact the Applied Music Director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignment.

title: Beginning Choreography
description: This course provides opportunities to use individual creativity through the composition of dance movement. Discover and practice manipulating movement using the dance elements of time, space, and energy to create and build a dance. Movement will mainly be composed for solo pieces.

title: Tai Chi Chu'an (3-Wk Session)
description: Tai Chi, sometimes referred to as "meditation in motion," is an ancient Chinese martial art consisting of a sequence of slow, graceful motions that relax and strengthen the mind, body, and spirit. An excellent form of stress management suitable for people of all ages, Tai Chi has been shown to reduce blood pressure and strengthen bones. Join an experienced Tai Chi instructor and discover the benefits yourself. No experience needed. Wear loose, comfortable, modest clothing and shoes with no or low heels. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Basic Tech Mathematics
description: For those in technology programs who have not completed one year of algebra and one year of geometry or who need to review beginning algebra and geometry. Covers a review of arithmetic; signed numbers; scientific notation; measurement conversions; and an introduction to beginning algebra, geometry, and statistics. Emphasizes practical technical applications and requires the use of a non-graphing scientific calculator.

title: Neck & Shoulder Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols are used to relieve pain in neck and shoulders. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve aches and pains in your neck and shoulder without taking drugs.

title: Technical Mathematics
description: For students in technology programs who have had MATH 100 or one year of Beginning Algebra. Covers functions and graphs, properties of lines, the trigonometric functions, systems of linear equations, rational expressions, quadratic equations, solution of right triangles, vectors, integer exponents, linear regression, complex numbers, and an introduction to the non-graphing scientific calculator. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments.

title: Polish Christmas Trad
description: Here is your chance to learn about the culture and culinary traditions of Poland's Christmas celebrations. During the first session you will sample a delicious Polish poppy seed cake as you enjoy a slide show presentation and a display of Christmas artifacts from Poland. Your instructor, Don Samull, is an educator who has traveled to Poland at least a dozen times, publishes a Polish-American Calendar, and has an infectious love of his subject. The following Saturday everyone will meet in Hamtramck to eat and enjoy a traditional Christmas dinner (wigilia), served family style, at one of the most noted Polish restaurants in the Detroit area. The cost of lunch is included.

title: Process Technology Systems
description: Studies the interrelation of process equipment and process systems. Examines how to arrange process equipment into basic systems; describes the purpose and function of specific process systems; explains how factors affecting process systems are controlled under normal conditions; introduces the concept of system and plant economics; and explores how to recognize abnormal process conditions.

title: Strings - Violin, Viola, Cello
description: Get your string instrument out and review, refresh, or learn all over again -- or maybe for the first time! Instructor will evaluate your skill level the first session, and you will work in groups according to skill level in subsequent sessions. Essential Element Book 1 and Suzuki Book 1 will be needed for all beginners. All other students, please bring any music you currently have to class.

title: Calculus II
description: Topics include applications of the integral, techniques of integration, numerical integration, improper integrals, solutions of separable differential equations, infinite series, polynomial approximations of functions, power series, Taylor and MacLaurin series, and parametric and polar equations. Utilizes numerical, graphical, and algebraic approaches whenever possible. Includes applications throughout the course. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Create a Website-Dreamweaver
description: This course will cover advanced Adobe Dreamweaver CS3 topics including cascading style sheets, forms, navigation bars, spry widgets, flash buttons and AP elements. Upon course completion, you will have the skills necessary to create a working website with mnay advanced features. There will be lots of hands-on practice at your own workstation. Prerequisite: Create a Great Website with Dreamweaver CS3 or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Create a Great Website with Dreamweaver CS3 or equivalent knowledge.

title: Medieval-Early Modern World
description: Presents world civilizations during the transition from the Ancient classical times to the Modern Period, i.e., the Medieval and Early Modern periods (c. 500 CE - c. 1650 CE). The world civilizations include China from the Sui to the early Qing dynasties; Japan from its origins to the early Tokugawa Shogunate; early Arabia, the rise of Islam, and the Umayyad, Abbasid, Seljuk, and Ottoman empires; the Mongol Empire; the Aztec and Incan civilizations; and the European Middle Ages, Renaissance, and Reformation.

title: The Art of Couponing
description: Looking for a way to save money on your monthly budget? Couponer Elvira Rivera will teach you how to utilize your weekly sale flyers and coupons to your advantage. Learn how to find coupons, pitfalls to avoid, how to organize, how to use websites and how to track your savings. Things have changed in the world of coupons--learn the newest methods to save your hard-earned dollars!

title: Salad & Sandwich Entertaining
description: Tired of serving the same old deli tray to guests? Get some unique salad and sandwich ideas that will upscale your next casual entertaining event. Chef Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will share her recipes for mango chutney cheese, tuna papaya and creamy pesto turkey sandwiches. She'll also whip up three salads: wilted bacom, roasted root vegetable and a not-so-orindary ambrosia. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Interior Design Mat & Comp
description: Investigates the characteristics, properties, and uses of materials, components and construction methods as specified by interior designers. Emphasizes performance, use, maintenance, and sustainability of materials and finishes. Also explores quantity calculations, installation methods, workroom practices, and resource development.

title: Creating Web Pages II
description: Are you using HTML to its fullest potential? In this hands-on course, you'll become adept at using color, tables and frames to give your Web pages a more polished and professional look, improved navigation and more organization. Beef up the fun factor with audio and interactive forms, and learn how to save hours of time and effort with Cascading Style sheets.

title: Feed Your Face for Young Look
description: Treat your face to advanced facelift exercises and techniques in this fascinating class with acupressure specialist Esther McCormick. Learn what wrinkles say about your emotions and everyday reactions to stress. Then use gentle acupressure massage to relax your neck, shoulder and facial muscles into a more youthful appearance. Also create safe natural cleaners, masks, toners and moisturizers based on skin type to nourish your own face or use for holiday gifts. Bring a standup mirror, washcloth and a 1-cup plastic bowl to class. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Beginning Latin & Ballrm Dance
description: Provides the basics of ballroom in the American and International styles in a group setting. Students will be able to recognize and execute basics of American Smooth and International Standard styles of Waltz, Foxtrot, Cha Cha, Rumba and East Coast Swing. Technique, rhythms and leading and following are studied and practiced.

title: Social Psychology
description: This course focuses on how individual thought and behavior are impacted by the presence of others. Major evidence-based topics in the psychological and sociological approach to social psychology are covered. Examples include conformity, group processes, interpersonal attraction, prosocial behavior, aggression and prejudice.

title: Calculus 1
description: For students planning to pursue a course of study involving a concentration in mathematics. Topics include limits, continuity, the derivative, differentiation of algebraic, exponential, logarithmic, trigonometric and inverse trigonometric functions, applications of the derivative, antidifferentiation, and the definite integral. Numerical, graphical and algebraic approaches are used whenever possible. Credit cannot be earned for both MATH 153 and 180. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Birding Field Trips
description: After attending the Introduction to Bird Watching class (or equivalent experience), participants may choose from a variety of one-day field trips designed to focus on specific types of birds present. February - Feeder Birds; March - Gullds; April - Ducks; May - Warblers. These field trips will provide participants with an opportunity to practice their identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are planned from late winter through spring. All field trips will meet at the HFCC parking lot where we will carpool to the sites, generally within 50 miles, to look for our target birds.

title: Creating Collage
description: Take your artwork and creative expression into a world without limits in this one-of-a-kind workshop. Under the guidance of Saundra Weed you will learn to cut, paste, paint, layer paper and other objects to make original collage art. In addition, you'll be able to add stamping and writing to your collage. Personalize your projects by bringing personal photos and memorabilia, newspaper clippings, buttons, jewelry, beads, etc. $10 material fee due to instructor in class. Bring a bag lunch.

title: eBay - Essentials of Selling
description: Are you preparing to sell an item on eBay? Learn the basics of listing an item to sell including researching the best price, tips on photos, crafting a description, packing and more. Bring one or two items to class that you may want to sell. Basic computer and internet skills are required.

title: Tastes of Italy
description: Prepare for a treat in this introductory cooking series that will teach you not only the basics of preparing authentic Italian regional cuisine but also the almost mythic approach to dining that makes Italy famous. With an emphasis on fresh seasonal foods, each week Robert Cascardo and Cristina Cotronei will teach you how to prepare a three-course meal, pair it with an appropriate wine and eat it the way they do in Italy. Cristina was born near Milan, and Robert has lived in various Italian cities including Florence, Rome and Turin. Naturally, they both love to cook. Join them for fun, educational evenings of preparing, eating and, of course, cleaning up after memorable meals. This class sells out, so register early.

title: Emergency Preparedness
description: Are you personally prepared to deal with emergencies posed by a fire, tornado, power blackout or workplace accident? Let an American Red Cross instructor teach you five Emergency Action Steps, as well as first aid for choking, shock, bleeding, burns, injuries and allergic reactions. Includes Quick Reference Guide and a Safety Tube of supplies. Ideal for all who desire emergency response skills for home or work.

title: Relax Tech for Stress Mgmt.
description: Covers stress and its effects on the body. Emphasizes ways to reduce stress such as Yogic breathing, meditation, and other relaxation techniques. Discusses time and money management; conflict resolution and effective communication; resolving anxiety; a healthy diet, and how to incorporate these into one's lifestyle.

title: Birding: Focus on Hawks
description: We will visit a world-class hawk migration site to practice identification of hawk species in flight overhead. If weather conditions are favorable, we could see hundreds, or even thousands of hawks.

title: Get Started Wth Computers (xp)
description: If you can operate a car, you can operate a computer. Don't let confusing computer jargon stop you! Attend this hands-on workshop designed for the person with little or no experience and see how easy it is to use the basic features of Windows XP. You'll learn how to start and shut down a computer safely, use a computer mouse, navigate the keyboard, create simple files and more! By the end of the class you will finally understand the lingo of your computer-savvy friends and relatives. Open to persons of any age.

title: Intro Amer Gov't & Pol Sci
description: Examines principles and problems of American political institutions, including the role of government and politics in society, the balancing of liberty with authority, and theories of the state. Also explores mechanisms of popular control (public opinion, pressure groups, political parties, elections) and formal structure. Additional components include foreign policy, public policy, and contemporary events.

title: Applied Music (one Credit) 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of weekly half-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester to confirm teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Software Engineering
description: A capstone course involving the study and application of software engineering principles to design and develop software. Emphasizes written and oral communication skills necessary for the information technology professional through participation in a software project designed to reinforce course material.

title: Fall Flower Arranging
description: Fresh flower arrangements can have a positive effect on the mood in your home. This is your chance to create stunning fall floral arrangements. Learn basic floral handling techniques, design elements and principles, and creative tips from an internationally known floral designer and educator. Using his extensive background in floral artistry, horticulture, and retail/wholesale floristry. Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will demonstrate and guide you as you create a unique fall arrangement each week to take home. Bring a notepaid, pen/pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No previous experience necessary.

title: Industrial Apps Algebraic Prin
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas.

title: Adult Hip Hop
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? This high-energy, introductory class features the best of both! Work your body and soul to the hottest music craze this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Music Theory 2
description: Covers triads in inversion, phrase and cadences, harmonic progressions, and four-part harmonization including voice leading. Recommended to be taken in conjunction with MUS 142, Aural Music Skills 2.

title: RACH Light Commercial Systems
description: An advanced course continuing the study of light commercial heating, air conditioning/cooling systems. The focus is on the lash up, installation, and troubleshooting of all associated controls and control packages. Through lectures and laboratory experiences, students gain knowledge of the functions, operations and components in relation to pneumatic, electronic and DDC (Direct Digital Control), building control systems. Comparable to ENT 219.

title: ELL American Culture 2
description: Teaches beginning-level knowledge of American culture to non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-103A. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Math for Elem Teachers I
description: For students who are involved in a curriculum for elementary teachers. Includes problem solving, set theory, number theory, numeration systems, whole numbers, and fractions. Addresses two aspects of teaching children mathematics - content and pedagogy - focused on Michigan Department of Education Standards for the Preparation of Teachers of Lower Elementary (Pre-K - 3) and Upper Elementary (Grades 3 - 6). Also addresses concept development, algorithms, children's mathematical work, and communication skills--both oral and written--important for teaching children.

title: BBQ Ovenstyle
description: Discover how to prepare barbecue dishes without the backyard grill. Chef Sania shares her 37 years of "Soul Food" expertise with you while cooking barbeque in the oven and on top of the stove. Learn her cooking techniques, seasoning tips and top of the stove grill method. The menu includes BBQ ribs, BBQ chicken, BBQ fish and grilled vegetables.

title: Facilities Management
description: Studies the critical roles of housekeeping, maintenance, and design and development in hotel, restaurant, and non-commercial facilities. Explores the impact of these roles on operating budgets, guest perception, and guest service.

title: Substation Operation & Mainten
description: Introduces the operation and maintenance of substations and switchyards, and discusses commonly used equipment and and the roles of various field personnel. Topics include safety practices, power transformers, circuit breakers, system performance, and substation batteries.

title: Hatha Yoga for Beginners
description: Hatha Yoga is an ancient system of exercise that includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance and alignment; and you will achieve mental con- centration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Reiki Level 3 Certification
description: Deepen your understanding of Reiki by learning more advanced techniques, the last 2 symbols, and how to chart and assess. Homework is required. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. Prerequisite: Reiki Level 2 Certification.
prerequisite_text: Reiki Level 2 Certification.

title: DIMS Excel 2010 Lvl 1
description: Teaches the fundamental skills needed to work with Excel 2010, starting with basics like the Excel environment, entering and editing numbers and text, then moving on to writing formulas and using Excel's built-in functions. Students will also learn about formatting spreadsheets, sorting, filtering, and charts.

title: Found of Educ With Pract Exp
description: An educational foundations and policy studies approach to the field of education. Course topics will include but not be limited to the history of U.S. education, philosophies of education from Plato to the current testing philosophy, multi-culturalism/diversity, and historical records of educational reform movements. Other material covered within the course includes social issues such as socioeconomic status (SES), race, gender, and culture affecting educational achievement, teacher certification process, professional organizations (such as unions) and their purpose, differentiated teaching methods from a diversity perspective, classroom management, and historical changes of school financing. Students will be introduced to lesson planning, instructional techniques, and classroom instruction through their placement in a classroom setting at the grade level/special education area of interest at which the student wishes to teach. They will incorporate integrated essays into their e-portfolio.This course integrates the social foundations approach to the field of education with a 40-hour pre-student teaching practicum. Course reflective topics will include but will not be limited to the history of U.S. education, philosophies of education, multi-culturalism/diversity, educational reform movements, social issues affecting educational achievement, teacher certification process, professional organizations (such as unions) and their purpose, differentiated teaching methods, classroom management, and school financing. Standardized state standards and high-stakes testing will be addressed as well. Students will be introduced to lesson planning, instructional techniques, and classroom instruction during this practicum experience.This course requires a 40-hour off-campus field placement.

title: Bldg/Construction Apprentice
description: This nine week, 315 hour course prepares participants to qualify for entry into the building trades apprenticeships. The course covers introduction to the trades, blueprint reading, First Aid/CPR, OSHA 30 certification, building science principles, power actuated tool certification, construction math, reading and locating information. This program is a collaborative program offering of the Michigan State AFL-CIO Human Resources Development (Michigan HRDI), Access for All and the Workforce Development Division of Henry Ford College.

title: Ultimate German Feast
description: Anyone who loves a good substantial meal will be impressed by German cooking. Join Ursula Markovits, who was born and raised in Germany, for a bit of German culture. She will demonstrate dishes representing three regions of the country: wiener schnitzel from the Austrian region, Bavarian potato salad from the southern region, and a black forest torte from mid Germany. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Precalculus
description: Topics include algebraic, graphical and numerical representations of functions, including composition and inverses of functions. Focuses on the study of rational functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, and trigonometric functions of real numbers and angles. Also includes analytic trigonometry and solutions of triangles. Techniques of problem solving and applications are included throughout the course requiring the frequent usage of graphing utilities. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments and access to an online homework system.

title: Clinical Therapeutics Practic.
description: Directed clinical experience that develops therapeutic clinical skills for critical care areas and diagnostic labs. Emphasis is on assessing needs, designing care plans, and implementing and evaluating the procedures especially for mechanical ventilator support and systemic oxygenation. Fifteen hours per week.

title: Relieve Arthritis
description: If you have been diagnosed with arthritis, you not only aren't alone--you're in very good company. Arthritis is the most common joint disorder affecting millions of Americans. The good news, however, is that techniques are available to help you relieve and control your symptoms. Sandy Baumann, a biochemist, will present lifestyle and mental imagery techniques that can enhance relaxation, decrease stress, and ultimately reduce pain. In addition, she will share information about nutritional and herbal approaches to pain.

title: Intermediate Feng Shui
description: Experience immediate positive changes in your life as you apply the principles of feng shui more specifically to your personal environment. Susan Haigfleigh, the instructor, will combine her practical, logical training as an Architect and Interior Designer and her intuitive/metaphysical training as she assists you in creating a balance of the best of Eastern and Western thinking, practice, and philosophy. Prerequisite: CLNC 7031
prerequisite_text: CLNC 7031

title: Abc's of White Wine
description: Do you know the difference between a Chardonnay, Pinot Gringo and a Sauvignon Blanc? Are you intimidated by the vast number of white wines from which to choose? If you enjoy the taste of white wine, join Bruce Bell for a fun, educational evening designed to educate amateurs, as well as experts, about the parietals and the pleasures of drinking them. Participants must be at least 21 years old.

title: College Algebra
description: Covers analytic geometry, functions and their graphs, algebraic and graphical solutions of equations and inequalities, graphs and zeros of polynomial functions, conic sections, linear and polynomial modeling, systems of equations and inequalities, sequences and series, and the Binomial Theorem. Techniques of problem-solving and applications are included throughout the course requiring the frequent usage of graphing utilities. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments and access to an online homework system.

title: Polish Baking Class
description: Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suter shares more of her Polish heritage with this baking class. Paczki and Easter Babka are two of the dishes covered this day. Her authentic paczki truly convey the tradition of a decadent treat before the rigors of Lent. This is a hands-on class. Bring an apron and a lunch.

title: Die Design
description: Focuses on advanced drawing techniques in the layout and design of production press work dies. Typical dies covered are blank, cam pierce, form, cutoff, draw, and progressive. Press computations and accessories are put in perspective as they relate to design problems. Drawing assignments are done extensively on CAD.

title: Symphony Band 3
description: Third of four semesters of wind ensemble (symphony band) including preparation and performance of standard concert band music and transcriptions of all styles. This group performs for college as well as community functions. These courses are open to college students, adult members of the community, and advanced placement high school students who play woodwind, brass, and percussion instruments.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: As you watch the prices for produce soar at the market do you wish you knew how to grow your own vegetables? Let Laurie Jeffery, certified Master Gardener, help get you started! Learn how to pick the site for your garden, what to plant, how to plant, how to care for your garden and how to harvest your crop.

title: Energy Systems Design
description: Examines the design principles of heating, cooling, and hydronic systems. Topics include both residential and commercial heat loss and gain, equipment selection, duct design, piping design, and air terminal selection.

title: Jazz Improvisation 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of jazz improvisation in which skills are developed through the study of jazz theory and the performances of a variety of jazz styles in a small group, jazz combo setting.

title: Computer Keyboarding
description: Teaches touch keyboarding skills. Covers the alpha, number, and symbol keys; terminology; and basic formatting. Emphasizes accuracy and speed as the semester progresses.

title: ELL Reading & Study Skills 5
description: Develops intermediate-level college-preparatory reading and study skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELI-106R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Theatre Capstone
description: Provides the required assessment of knowledge and skills for students in their last semester of course work for the Theatre Pathway of the Associates of Arts Degree in Liberal Arts.

title: Coordinated Edu Exp Auto Svc 2
description: Designed to offer practical work experience within the Automotive Technology and Service field through participation in a supervised coordinated education program. This course integrates work experience with classroom and lab instruction.

title: World Regional Geography
description: An overview examining the spatial distribution of people and places around the world, with emphasis on the cultural, political, social, and economic aspects of regions.
