Topics are explored by examining ways that humans interact with the environment and one another. Emphasis is placed on the spatial variation of human activities at all scales from local to global.

title: Adobe Illustrator Cs5
description: Adobe Illustrator CS5 is a sophisticated graphics application that helps you to create complex and attractive designs with type effects. Create logos, advertisements, and other illustrations with ease using this software. Learn to use Adobe Illustrator CS5 by drawing and manipulating simple shapes to create logos. You will also combine text and graphics to create advertisements. Illustrator is a great companion for Flash and integrates throughout the Adobe Suite. Text provided. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.

title: Own a Successful Wedding Busin
description: Have you always loved attending weddings or watching Whose Wedding Is It Anyway? Think you might have what it takes to own your own business? If so, this class is the perfect way to turn an idea into a moneymaking business! Certified Wedding Planner Lindsey Nartker will teach you how to build a successful wedding planning business, how to create an inspiring event design and a few tips to managing the wedding day.

title: Practical Accounting
description: Offers practical knowledge of bookkeeping principles, including small-business accounting practices. Introduces the accounting cycle, the specialized journals employed by merchandising firms, and payroll accounting.

title: Intro Surgical Technology
description: The course introduces the fundamentals of operating room techniques, emphasizing principles and practices of asepsis and sterilization. Physical and psychosocial aspects of the patient are explored. Included are the roles and responsibilities of the surgical technologist and other surgical team members; accountability and legal aspects; identification of common equipment, instruments, and supplies; students practice scrubbing, gowning, and gloving. Selected laboratory practice required.

title: Clinical Education II
description: This is second in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and ASRT competencies. Course offers experiences in chart recognition, basic patient assessment, patient communication, and basic departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, abdomen, upper extremity, lower extremity and shoulder girdle radiographic positioning are emphasized.

title: Gage, Cam Layout, and Fixture
description: Focuses on the mastery of gage design principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Discusses gage design theory, computer-aided manufacturing, cam layout procedures, and fixturing. Emphasizes the team approach to projects.

title: 6+YOU: Mking 6+1 Trait Wrtng
description: makes student writing good writing, whether the student is five or thirteen or anywhere in between. You will take charge of the traits, developing writing prompts and classroom activities to teach and reinforce good writing practices. Youll assess your own writing samples to discover your strengths as a writer, and then translate those strengths to your teaching. Some time will also be dedicated to aligning the 6+1 Traits with the Common Core State Standards.

title: Continuing Watercolor
description: If you have some knowledge of watercolor and would like to move beyond the structured classroom experience to focus on developing your own personal style and creativity, join artist Saundra Weed in this continuing watercolor painting class. The instructor will guide and support you as your classmates cheer you on. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-104C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Coordinate Jewelry Like a Pro
description: Design jewelry in "living color", by selecting the perfect mix of colors and shades. In this class you will learn how to complement and contrast with colors. You will receive a color wheel and swatches to that you may take home for future reference. $15 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Fun & Easy Microsoft Office 07
description: This class is an easy way for beginners to learn to use the Microsoft Office 2007 Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Access). Learn the basics of word processing with MS Word, spreadsheet basics with MS Excel, and presentation techniques with MS Powerpoint. A demonstration of database concepts will also be shown using MS Access. The instructor has used these applications for 20+ years in the business world. Real world examples at a pace beginners can manage is the goal. Basic PC skills are a prerequisite. Text is included. A USB flash memory stick is recommended.

title: Gearing
description: Discusses the calculation of standard American National, United States V, Metric, Acme, and Worm screw threads. Covers standard notations and formulas for spur gears, bevel gears, worm and worm wheels, and helical gears. Also explains replacement of spur gears with helical gears, the use of idler gears, and discusses calculations for plain and differential indexing.

title: Lactation Consult Adv Pract
description: Focuses on the clinical application of lactation concepts to breastfeeding mothers. Select experiences in health care facilities enable students to assess, develop, implement and evaluate the care of the breastfeeding dyad. The course requires 200 hours in the health care setting. This course is the second part of the practicum, following AH-190, consistent with Pathway 2 according to International Board of Lactation Consultant Examiners (IBLCE).

title: Cont Piano Basics for Adults
description: For those who have already taken Piano Basics for Adults, this class offers an opportunity to go on with the piano lessons with Leslie Melville. Books available for approx. $20 if you don't have the book from the Piano Basics class.

title: Singapore Math Strategies
description: In this professional development course for teachers, you'll get the training you need to start teaching model drawing, the powerful Singapore Math strategy that gives word problems a visual context. As a teacher, you know that many students groan when it's time to solve word problems. Why is that? Are the problems too difficult? Do students get lost trying to decipher the wording or figure out the computation? Do they simply not know which strategy to use? Actually, it's a combination of all these issues. Luckily, model drawing, a Singapore Math strategy for working word problems, will help your students start to enjoy math in a way they may never have before.

title: Cont Cake Decorating
description: Step ahead with your decorating skills as you continue your work in developing more complex designs with our professional cake designer. Learn to dabble in gum paste and fondant to create more complex flowers and other decorative items.

title: PowerPoint Basics & Beyond
description: Why limit yourself to dull text-based slide presentations if you can enhance them with graphics? Discover the tools for designing exciting slides with drawings, clipart and photographs. Learn creative techniques to reposition, group and align content to give your slides that extra pizzazz. Identify the value of using the master slide feature. Create a slideshow. The instructor is a Microsoft certified and known as a PowerPoint "guru." Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.

title: Counseling & Interviewing Skil
description: Introduces the basic techniques of interviewing, counseling, and treatment therapies used in the mental health and social work fields. The course will expand on the historical and political aspects of the field of social work.

title: Golden Age of Toys
description: Explore the world of antique toys from the mid-1800's to pre WWI with our favorite historian, Mary MacDonald. See how toys mirror the times as Mary talks about cast iron, tin and other materials used to manufacture these childhood treasures. See a range of mechanical toys from the simple gravity and pull toys to wind-up, steam-operated, and early electrical toys. See beautiful dolls produced in Europe. It is a delightful evening for the child in each of us.

title: Exploration of Welding
description: Introduces basic welding principles, skills, and terminology. Uses welding simulator to demonstrate hands-on methods through a project-based approach. The purpose of this course is to explore various basic welding techniques that are similar to industry method of operations.

title: Art Appreciation
description: A global survey of the development of visual arts through studying major historic civilizations and cultures from the stone age to the present. Explains technical information on the materials, tools, and techniques used to create visual arts in different branches. Discusses how to interpret works of art by examining the contextual characteristics, the content, functionality and the culture(s) that produced it. Field work at a major museum/art gallery is required to strengthen student's knowledge.

title: Aerobic Dance
description: This is an exercise class which covers all of the health related components of physical fitness, with an emphasis on cardiovascular endurance. The benefits are similar to those experience in jogging, cycling, swimming and other endurance activities. The course also covers how to make choices for a healthy diet; weight and stress management; and disease prevention. No dance background is required.

title: Sheet Metal Radial & Triangula
description: Covers the development of geometrical elements of structures, their intersections by the radial line, triangulation methods of sheet metal layout, the drawing of development layouts, and the forming of cardboard or sheet metal models.

title: Create Your Own Spa Experience
description: Don't pay high prices at fancy department stores for luxurious sugar scrubs, skin cleansers, toners, bath salts, and facial masques - create them in our class for a fraction of the price! Fragrant, moisturizing, natural beauty products that will replenish your body and relax your mind - a true spa experience. Make wonderful gifts for your family and friends (these items are hot sellers online too - sell them and earn money!) Call us or visit the Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-$18.)

title: Clinical Education I
description: This is the introductory course in a five-course sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses designed in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologists (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologist (ARRT) competencies. The course offers exposure to chart recognition, basic patient assessment, patient communication, and basic departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, bony thorax, abdomen, and upper extremity radiographic positioning are emphasized.

title: Introduction to CATIA V5
description: Introduces the basics of CATIA V5 and its functionality. Utilizes the part design, sketcher, drafting, and assembly design workbenches to make models that are properly constructed and constrained. Coursework explores how CATIA's tools and toolbars are used in these workbenches.

title: Design Entrepreneur
description: This course is designed to help the individual navigate the business of design. Explores the fundamentals of design management, creative problem-solving techniques, and interpersonal skills for a beginning design entrepreneur who is building a client base.

title: Color Coaching Workshop
description: Discover color solutions for your home that excite you. Interior designer Darlene Rothman demonstrates how, using color, you can incorporate your existing furnishings into a new look. Discover ways to accentuate the positive architectural features in your home. Understand how to balance your favorite colors in each room. Explore current trends in color and how to make them work for you. Please bring a small item you want to work with (photos, pillow, fabric, artwork). If there is time at the end of the workshop we can discuss them.

title: Savory Soulfully Baked Chicken
description: Grab your apron and join Chef Saniyyah to prepare savory baked chicken, smothered cabbage, red beans/rice and cornbread for some "down home" good eatin'.

title: Smart Sellers Get Better Price
description: Get the expert advice you need to sell your home quickly at the best price in this buyer's market. Join an eye-opening class with realtor Jim Kaylor - whether you plan to sell by owner or list with a realtor. Discover how to competitively price and prepare your home for sale, what selling seasons bring the best prices, how to advertise and promote your home for little money, and how to attract qualified buyers. Get sample purchase agreements, disclosure forms, and a free booklet "Tips to Getting Top Dollar." Get your home ready quickly for savvy buyers.

title: Finan Strategies Retire
description: Learn the answers to your 10 most pressing financial questions and get the facts you need to prepare for a financially secure retirement. This seminar will show you a conservative approach to wise money management and will identify lifestyle issues facing retirees. A 'hear it, see it, read it, write it' teaching method will make it easy for you to understand and retain information on retirement planning, equity investments, retirement income, fixed-income securities, risk management, and estate planning. Walk through real-life examples that apply to your situation in this enlightening workshop.

title: Competency Evaluated NA(CENA)
description: Prepares individuals to become a Nurse Aide. This course serves as the educational foundation for the Nursing Care Skills certificate. Aides render care to clients in hospitals, long-term care facilities, and in home-care. This course, and the Nursing Care Skills certificate, consists of 35 hours of course work in theory, 45 hours of laboratory activities, and 20 hours in a clinical agency. The course requires consistent attendance from 9:00 a.m. - 2:30 p.m. four days a week.

title: Marriage & Family
description: This course aims to provide an overview of the historical and contemporary contexts that shape our understanding of marriages and families. Topics of discussion include definitions of marriage and family, diverse family structures, gender roles, partner selection, relationship formation, parenting, the intersection of family and work, family violence, divorce, and remarriage. Additionally, the course incorporates valuable insights from cross-cultural and historical comparisons.

title: Introduction to Social Media
description: Introduces students to different social media platforms and how they are used by people and organizations. Students will critically examine various social media forms in the context of society. Students will be exposed to the rules of ethical communication, create a basic social media communication plan, and gain basic understanding of social media analytics.

title: The Modern Middle East
description: Proceeding from the traditional civilizations in the Middle East, this course examines the impact of the industrialized powers, nationalism, industrialization, and religious and cultural change on the lands of the Middle East.

title: Industrial Computer Applicatio
description: Introduces computer systems and associated components typically found in the industrial environment. Presents basic skills in word processing, database, spreadsheet, email, specialized vendor software, and communication software. Also covers Internet and Intranet environments, including searching for industry manuals and parts, downloading software and drivers, and other related tasks. Utilizes the Windows operating system in conjunction with proprietary software. Discusses learning management systems including AMTEC curriculum and Moodle Rooms. Laboratory activities.

title: Spec Topics in Electrical Tech
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current electrical technology trends. The specific special topics and the prerequisites will be announced each term. Course may be repeated when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Youth Exploring Trades
description: For our Machining camp we will learn about manufacturing and how to cut metal. Using a machine tool, we will mill and turn metal into small project that you will be able to keep after you create. Safety and proper machine shop etiquette will be covered.

title: Fa Flowering Ctnr Garden Deck
description: Learn to effectively assemble an attractive flowering planter using colorful, cold hardy annuals and accent plants. You will see what plants will bloom and survive through Michigan's Autumn (through Thanksgiving). Includes the recipe for successful soil mixture, choosing the right plants for your conditions, care and maintenance.

title: Advanced Java
description: An advanced-level course which examines advanced Java features including advanced graphical user interfaces (GUI), data structures, file I/O, database connectivity, multithreading, generics, recursion, efficiency of algorithms, Java Server Pages (JSP), and Java Frameworks.

title: Basic Code: Theory & Practice
description: An intermediate-level coding class that incorporates hands-on practice in the assignment of insurance codes using case studies and medical record simulations. Presents theory and practice of coding diseases and procedures using International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) for inpatient facilities. Explores the Prospective Payment System (PPS) and the significance of Diagnosis Related Groupings (DRG). This course utilizes electronic and paper-based medical documentation to serve as resources for coding for inpatient reimbursement, and the application of all federal laws related to patient health information in the process of medical business practices.

title: Heal Digestive Orders Natural
description: Does eating cause you pain? Have you been diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome, ulcers, heartburn, bloating, or esophageal burning? Learn how others have stopped pain and received relief during this workshop by popular naturopath and herbalist Mary Born. Discover the basis for digestive problems and learn methods to encourage good digestion and the importance of enzymes. Handouts will discuss specific nutrients matched to symptoms and foods that speed healing of the GI tract.

title: Cooking for One
description: Anyone who has ever cooked for just one person knows how challenging it can be. If you find yourself regularly buying frozen dinners or fast food, you need to learn some quick and easy ways to cook a variety of healthful meals for one. Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will share shopping tips and ideas for preparing ahead as she shows you how to marinate meats and make a simple stir fry, pasta dishes, and items you can freeze for later. Bring an apron, and come ready to cook and eat. Class limited to 14. Enroll early.

title: Medieval Art
description: A comprehensive overview of medieval art and architecture from the late antiquity to the late Gothic period in Europe and the Mediterranean region. Discusses the visual art and cultures of about 1000 years (around 400-1400 CE) including Late Roman, Early Christian, Byzantine, Romanesque, Islamic, and Gothic art. Offers diverse perspectives on the development, exchange, interaction, and influence between all these cultures through their visual arts. A field trip to a major museum is required.

title: IRC Ford-Trade Related Prep
description: Preparation for a career in the trades is assessed through an overview of the industrial processes. A review of math, blueprint reading and measuring tools relative to shop application are major components of the course. Student receive diagnostic evaluation through testing in these major areas. This class is only for UAW-Ford employees who will be participating in Industrial Readiness Certificate Program.

title: Intermediate Spanish II
description: Follows SPN-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level Spanish courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Spanish, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written Spanish. Presents Hispanic culture.

title: Ethnic & Racial Diversity
description: Introduces students to the sociological study of ethnic and racial groups. Students explore key concepts and issues in connection with definitions of race and ethnicity, immigration, patterns of group inequality and domination, discrimination and prejudice. Focuses on the diversity ofU.S.society and explores the experiences of Native Americans and immigrant groups, including those from Africa, Asia, andEurope. Emphasis on current patterns and consequences of immigration will be studied. Ethnic and racial diversity in other societies will also be explored.

title: Auto Chassis Units
description: A study of the construction, operation, and maintenance of the various chassis units. Examines steering, suspension systems, and alignment as well as disc and drum braking systems.

title: Design Entrepreneur
description: This course is designed to help the individual navigate the business of design. Explores the fundamentals of design management, creative problem-solving techniques, and interpersonal skills for a beginning design entrepreneur who is building a client base.

title: Therapeutic Procedures II
description: This course provides instruction in therapeutic procedures utilized by the respiratory therapist. Course content includes airway care, emergency life support, bronchial hygiene therapy, and lung expansion therapy. Students practice these procedures in a guided laboratory setting. Course includes 3 hours of lecture and 2 hours of lab each week.

title: Pastels & Other Color Media
description: Whether you are beginning your creative journey or returning to it, learn to draw or paint with pastels to create richly colored, textured works of art. In this eight-week course, Kathy Boettcher, an experienced art instructor, will help you "see like an artist" and understand color, light effects, composition and more. Bring charcoal paper in various values (especially mid-value grey) and pastels. Individuals using other color media are also welcome.

title: Industrial Apps Algebraic Prin
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas.

title: Wall Makeovers With Paint
description: Give your home a fresh new look with a simple coat of paint. Instead of hiring someone, attend this workshop and learn to do it yourself like a "pro." A professional painter will explain how to properly plan and organize a project; discuss different paint types, finishes, tools and supplies; demonstrate proper wall preparation (including drywall repairs); and share some "tricks of the trade" that will simplify your life. Bring your questions. $10 material fee payable to instructor.

title: Freestyle Skating
description: If you have taken beginner and intermediate inline skating and want to learn more...freestyle is what you are looking for. Learn how to do tricks, skate through cones and skate with a partner. Learn how to combine a series of steps, using basic calls to perform synchronized skating with a group. In addition, learn some of the fancy moves like seat drops, pull throughs and spins. Students must provide skates equipped with a heel brake, helmet, wrist guards, elbow and kneepads.

title: Hear Rick Bloom
description: Because developing nations are gobbling resources and commodities, successful investors think globally. For a crash course about opportunities in precious metals, natural resources like oil and gas, mutual funds that feature commodities, and real estate investment trusts, come to hear Rick Bloom, host of a popular radio show on investing. Selected as one of the top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine, Rick will give you timely investment strategies, a list of his latest "Rick's Picks," and a wealth of handouts.

title: Piano Basics for Adults
description: Don't let that keyboard sit in the corner collecting dust anymore! Have some fun this winter learning how to play well-known classic and contemporary pieces. Under the direction of pianist Lesley Melville, you will become familiar with the keyboard, basic notes, hand positions, and music theory concepts. By the end of the course, you will be able to play popular songs using your left hand for chords and your right hand for melody. $16 textbook fee due to instructor in class. Please bring correct change.

title: Stunning Desserts, Dairy Free
description: Learn to create three delicious desserts that you won't believe are prepared dairy free. Chocolate lovers will want to be sure and attend this class. In this interactive class, Chefs Sherry Duquet and Sandy Boulton will prepare a Dark Chocolate Peanut Butter and Banana Pie, Choco-nut Truffles and Zesty Key Lime Pie. Nuts, wheat and soy products are used during cooking. Basic understanding of using cooking utensils and prepping food is helpful. Come ready to learn, cook and taste these delicious desserts.

title: Explor Ener Tech:HVAC & RE
description: Introduces the student to the various areas and applications in Energy Technology programs, such as Heating and Cooling (HVAC) and Alternative Energies (basics), and explores career choices, tools, and equipment associated with each area/program. Students will be introduced to various types of energy sources and how these apply to electrical wiring, furnaces, air conditioner/refrigeration systems. Students are given an exploratory experience in the early stage of Multi-Skilled Facility Maintenance. The purpose of this course is to explore a sampling of technical and skilled trades as related to Energy Technology and provide students with hands-on experience as related to industry.

title: Ready, Set, Read!
description: Learn what the newest research says about how children really learn to read and write and become a powerful guide to literacy development when you work with young children. Gain confidence in your ability to guide a child's literacy development, and take pleasure in seeing how even the littlest events can be really big steps in reading and writing success.

title: Fair Housing & Civil Rights
description: Approved by the Board of Real Estate Brokers for broker licensure education requirements, this six-hour course overviews the federal and state fair housing and civil rights requirements for rental properties. Topics include advertising, HUD regulations, the Fair Housing Act, case law, and much more. This is also an excellent class for landlords.

title: Wealth Wth Rental Real Estate
description: Is rental property your ticket to financial security? Get the latest facts about actual profits and potential pitfalls with rental real estate from successful property manager/investor Peter Pace. First, learn how to find property, evaluate its worth, negotiate price, and obtain financing. Then get Peter's advice to set a market-driven rent, write leases, attract and screen tenants, and generate cash flow. Discover his proven strategies for property management, timely maintenance, and tenant retention to maximize your income. Optional $25 package of rental documents available after class.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-104C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Shakespeare
description: Focuses on reading, discussion, and written analysis of six to eight of William Shakespeare's comedies, histories, and tragedies. Readings can also include Shakespeare's non-dramatic poetry. Students also have the opportunity to observe, analyze, and evaluate his works in performance, either live or recorded.

title: Should You Become Distributor?
description: Many cosmetic, home product and vitamin companies invite you to make money by becoming their distributor. This process is called multi-level (MLM) or network marketing. Being part of a well-run MLM can be a rewarding experience. Unfortunately, not every opportunity is legitimate. Before you sign up, learn from an experienced distributor what your responsibilities will be, how to evaluate an MLM, and what questions to ask to protect yourself from scams.

title: Chicken Shawarma
description: Chef Kamal Hachem will help you prepare one of the most popular Mediterranean dishes, Chicken Shawarma - deboned chicken breast, cut into finger size slices, with white pepper, paprika and other ingredients, served with garlic sauce. It will be served with Tabbouleh, a salad made with bulgur wheat and finely chopped scallions, tomatoes, mint, and parsley. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Intermediate ASL I
description: Follows ASL-132 and is the first of two intermediate-level American Sign Language (ASL) courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops knowledge of fingerspelling, vocabulary and grammatical structures, and expands visual receptive and gestural expressive skills in order to comprehend and express essential ideas in ASL.

title: Graphic Design Studio 2
description: Builds on skills developed in ART-114. Covers intermediate design solutions for a variety of media including print and digital using the Adobe Creative Software Suite. Focuses on creating materials for brand identity and provides an introduction into User Experience (UX) design. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.Students are expected to have access to the Adobe Creative Cloud applications including Photoshop, Indesign, Illustrator, Acrobat, AfterEffects and XD.

title: Be Safe From Crime
description: Can you become a crime victim because you talk on a cell phone outside a store? Learn from Wayne County Sheriff's Deptuy Ki Sobol how criminals pick their targets for identity theft, home burglary, purse snatching and carjacking. Discover red flags that signal danger for walkers, joggers and travelers, including special precautions for rape prevention. Sign up today to keep yourself and your children safe at work, at school, in your home and in your vehicle.

title: Quickbooks II - Bookkeeping
description: Continue learning the vast features of QuickBooks to learn how QuickBooks can be practically applied to your small business. Topics include inventory tracking, dealing with sales tax, creating purchase orders, receiving items, using QB classes, creating estimates, job costing and time tracking, working with customer & vendor profile lists, processing sales discounts & electronic payments, using QuickBooks for payroll, working with balance sheets and more. Prerequisite: QuickBooks 1 - Software for Small Business Bookkeeping or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: QuickBooks 1 - Software for Small Business Bookkeeping or equivalent knowledge.

title: Office Ready!
description: Office ready is a non-credit certificate program designed to train students in Microsoft Office Word 2010 and customer service skills that will help students handle specific customer service chanllenges. Students will be trained in on day workshops in Microsoft Office Word 2010 Levels 1, 2, and 3; Microsoft Office Excel 2010 Levels 1, 2, and 3, and Microsoft Office PowerPoint Levels 1 &2. the customer service portion included in this program is a two day workshop that will focus on learning timps and techniques that help best meet the customer's needs. The skills incluce deternining what to do and how to manage the customer interchange. The use of practical self-management skills, excercises and simulations will help each participant learn to solve their customer service chanllenges.15 May 2014 2:33 PM Arlicia Summerville

title: Inter Bead Making: Gardens
description: Embellish your glass beads with florals, leaves and vines as you learn about design, color and stringer control. Learn the floral and cased cane techniques for making leaves and petals, surface decoration and clear casing.

title: Weld Metallurgy-Non-Ferrous
description: Examines the mechanical and physical properties, and the crystalline structures of non ferrous metals. Also covers alloying and phase diagrams associated with non-ferrous metals like aluminum and copper.

title: Dream Vacation- Discount Price
description: If you've been bitten by the travel bug but think you can't afford to venture out of Michigan, attend this eye-opening seminar. Over and over again, world traveler Doug Denard has tourned extraordinary places at unheard of low prices. He'll share ideas on how to determine what kind of adventure is best for you, how to get the most for your dollar, and how to handle today's political ramifications when travelling abroad. Bring your questions.

title: Bldg Science Special Topic
description: This course provides students with an opportunity to explore emerging or specialized topics within the field of Building Science. Each semester, the focus of the course will vary based on current trends, faculty expertise, and industry developments-such as sustainable design practices, digital fabrication, adaptive reuse, building performance analysis, or community-based design. Students will engage in research, discussion, and hands-on projects that deepen their understanding of the built environment and expand their creative and technical skills. This course is designed to be flexible, encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration and critical thinking as students apply classroom knowledge to unique design and construction challenges.

title: Airbrushing
description: Explore the world of airbrushing and discover how versatile and fun this art form can be for pro- fessional artists as well as beginners. In just 6 weeks you will learn the basic techniques to crete great effects on surfaces such as t-shirts, motorcycles, hockey masks, ceramics, fingernails, paper, cars, and many more. Students supply their own airbrush and materials. A supply list will be mailed to registrants. Limited to 15.

title: When MIOSHA Visits
description: Is your organization ready to pass a Michigan Occupation Safety and Health Act (MIOSHA) inspection? If your company is not ready for inspection, you may not only run the risk of fines and penalties, but also may be paying too much in workers' compensation costs and may be fostering an environment that risks workplace accidents and lowers employee morale, efficiency and productivity.

title: Intro Digital Photo-Slr Users
description: Have a digital SLR cmera and are ready to start getting the most out of it? In this course learn about the exposure triange: ISO, Aperture and Shutterspeed. Learn how to control your camera's settings and get amazing photographs. Learn the digital camera terminology and learn how to navigate your DSL camera! Class includes hands-on exercises and photo critiques. Required: Digital SLR camera or a camera with program modes such as Av (Apperture Priority), Tv (shutterspeed Priority) and M (full manual modes).

title: Introduction to Robotics
description: Introduces industrial robotic safety standards, historic timeline of industrial robots, industrial classification of robots, common industrial applications of robots, basic system components found in industrial robot applications, robotic motion concepts and common robot terms and definitions used in computer integrated manufacturing (CIM). Pre-requisites: CIMEL 111 or Instructor Permission.

title: Creating Env, Labels, Mailmerg
description: Learn how to layout and input recipients' names & address information for envelopes and labels. You will make sender's multiple return address labels. Create multiple letters and labels to mail to friends, using Microsoft Word's mail merge database feature. You will also create badges, DVD labels, stickers, tent cards, shipping labels, etc. Prequisite: Basic computer skills.

title: Media Performance
description: This course offers experience in media performance, focusing on techniques for effective communication in front of the camera and microphone. Students will develop skills in announcing, interviewing, voiceover, and on-camera performance, building confidence in vocal delivery, body language, and character portrayal within a professional media studio environment. Emphasis is placed on industry standards, studio protocol, and adaptability across a variety of audio and visual media platforms, including independent and social media performance roles. Additionally, this class offers insights for those students interested in directing or producing to collaborate effectively with talent, and for media writers to explore the performance side of character development.

title: Poetry & Prose: Call the Muse
description: Let the muse guide you as you write wonderful poetry inspired by your life and that of other writers. Cheryl Vatcher-Martin, a columnist, editor, award-winning poet and professor, will teach you how to turn an everyday experience or emotion into a powerful poem. Explore writing techniques that will help fill your notebook with poetry and prose. In this workshop you will practice your poetry and get feedback from your classmates. $5 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Construction Blueprint Reading
description: Explores mechanical (HVAC), architectural, and electrical drawings required for energy technology, plumbing and pipefitting, electrical, and construction related trades. Covers print reading, interpreting common symbols, working notes, views, and title blocks. Introduces project management and estimating. Note: Basic measurement and math skills required for print reading section.

title: Intro Auto Industry Auto Maint
description: Provides an introduction to the major automotive systems covered in greater detail in other automotive technology specific system classes. Explores how to perform preventive maintenance routines, such as oil changes, tire rotations on standard systems as well as on vehicles equipped with TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System); brake and suspension system inspections; and how to service the cooling system.

title: Make Trad Pierogis
description: If you are not of Polish descent, you may never have tasted authentic, homemade Pierogis. Now is your chance to join instructor Marilyn Suter to make and eat the real thing. You will mix and handle the dough, use a variety of fillings, and stuff your pierogis. Bring an apron. You will eat what you create, and take home any leftovers. Class limited to 14. Enroll early!

title: Start Ebay Drop Off Shore
description: Turn other people's unwanted items into a lucrative source of income by starting an eBay drop off store. Master the art of setting up a business, writing a marketing plan, taking super photos, creating dynamite listings, and offering great customer service. Thousands of people have transformed their eBay knowledge into profitable second income sources or even full time jobs. Take your skills to the next level and avoid the common pitfalls that keep amateur sellers from reaching their full potential.

title: Continuing Digital Photography
description: This 4 week course is based on student interaction inside and outside the classroom. Students will be required to complete assignments to be discussed in detail in class. Learn how to shoot on manual mode, basic differences of lenses, more detailed composition, how to use your on-camera flash, photo critique, and other details not covered in the first class. Please bring your manual, fully charged camera, memory card, and a small collection of photographs taken since the first completed course for review, critique and questions! Prerequisite: Basic Digital Photography for SLR or permission of instructor.
prerequisite_text: Basic Digital Photography for SLR or permission of instructor.

title: Clown School Camp
description: 03 Aug 2012 11:09 AM Pauline Barbas Fun, play, and foolery can be serious business when it comes to learning values, seeing things in a different light, and discovering the inner you. The Clown is where the true self can come out and play. Join master clown Joe Zsigray in morning discovery to bring out the clown within you and have lots of fun. The 5 sessions will concentrate on finding your clown and giving it expression, through make up, costuming, communication, & movement. In addition, time will be spent in building a clown's tool box of magic, balloon sculptures, props, juggling, and harmonica music. Participants will need to bring some clothing and ordinary house hold items from home for costuming and props. Balloons, Harmonica, make-up, juggling bean bags, and magic equipment will be provided. The experience will end with a Celebration of Joy and Delight. Ages 10 - 14.

title: Chw Training W/ Indep. Study
description: Designed to train front-line public health workers to provide culturally responsive services in Michigan communities. The core competencies of the CHW are presented. They include: role, advocacy, and outreach; organization and resources; teaching and capacity building; legal and ethical responsibilities; coordination, documentation, and reporting; communication and cultural competence; healthy lifestyles; and metal health; 126 hours of training and 40 hours of intership hours. Internship consist of field experience in local agencies for those who are not employed as a CHW.

title: Moral Issues in Biotechnology
description: Introduces moral dilemmas that arise in the context of biotechnology and the theories and skills required to engage in high quality dialogue concerning those dilemmas. Includes study of the basic nature of biotechnology and the most influential theories of ethics and justice as relates to biotechnological research and products.

title: Access 3
description: The Level 3 course is for the individual whose job responsibilities include working with heavily related tables; creating advanced queries, forms, and reports; writing macros to automate common tasks; and performing general database maintenance. Prerequisite: Access 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text included.
prerequisite_text: Access 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text included.

title: Adult Tap Dance
description: Get in shape and learn to tap! Its fun and it will improve your memory and coordination too! Learn basic steps skills such as shuffles, flaps, slaps, ball changes and cramp rolls. By the end of this session you should be able to perform short combinations with these basic steps. You can take this class over and over until you feel you can perform longer and faster combinations. Tap shoes mandatory or you will not be able to participate.

title: Fit for Life
description: Experience the value of fitness and the benefits of activity by developing strength, endurance and flexibility at a comfortable pace. This class, designed for the needs of the older body, is for those who believe you are never too old to exercise.

title: Story Boarding for Design
description: This time based graphic course will provide experience with linear sequencing as well as exploring the users experience with a digital product. This course will develop skills used in 2D animation, motion and interactive experience design. This class meets for three (3) hours a week.

title: Contemporary Guitar 1
description: Learn the basics of playing guitar. While strumming your favorite pop, rock, or folk music, you will acquire tools to use for any style of guitar playing. No experience with guitar is necessary. Bring your own guitar.

title: Garden Fertilizers
description: Standing in a garden center looking at fertilizer packages can be confusing. What to buy? When to apply it? Organic or synthetic? Attend this class and get the answers to these and other questions from an Advanced Certified Master Gardner. By the time you leave, you'll be able to select the best products for your specific landscaping needs.

title: Symphony Band 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of wind ensemble (symphony band) including preparation and performance of standard concert band music and transcriptions of all styles. This group performs for college as well as community functions. These courses are open to college students, adult members of the community, and advanced placement high school students who play woodwind, brass, and percussion instruments.

title: Basic Macro Programmi for CNC
description: Introduces macro programming as applied to CNC machine tools. Basic elements of this type of programming include defining a macro, defined variables vs. undefined variables, and the use and application of arithmetic, logical and Boolean operators. Includes both classroom and application-based activities in the CNC laboratory.

title: Intro to Community Health
description: This is an introduction to community health. Providing awareness of biological, psychosocial, cultural, economic, philosophical, political, ethical, and environmental impact of the health of individuals and the community. It covers factors and determinants of health and individual wellness, history, and predictions for the future.

title: Jazz Band 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of jazz band, including preparation and performance of Big Band music in a variety of jazz styles from swing to contemporary. This group, which performs several concerts each semester, is open to college students and adult members of the community who play saxophone, trumpet, trombone, piano, guitar, bass, or drums.

title: Jewelry Knotting
description: Learn the secret of putting that knot just where you want it. Under the direction of a professional beader, you'll create a knotted necklace using semi-precious stones. You'll no doubt enjoy the compliments you receive every time you wear it! $10 supply fee payable to instructor.

title: Earn Money - Licensed Day Care
description: Want to turn your love of children into a daycare business? Let Doris Dickhudt, who ran a successful licensed daycare home for over 20 years, give you an overview before you start. Get her expert coaching about marketing, insurance, business planning, licensing requirements, educational resources, and other issues related to zoning, taxes and liability. Learn to plan daily routines, set hours conductive for parents, and still have a life for yourself. Turn a small initial investment into more income with this high-demand service to parents. Bring your questions!

title: Shielded Mtl Arc Overhead Wld
description: Discusses theory and operation of shielded metal arc welding. Emphasizes safety protocols and working specifically in the overhead welding position.

title: Create a Free E-Mail Account
description: You will create free e-mail accounts and start e-mailing during the first session! Continue to use your free e-mail address at any library, at home, or anywhere that has Internet access. Also learn how to create folders and move e-mails into them; send pictures and attachments; reply to, and forward e-mails; search for and find lost e-mails; and more as time permits.

title: Pathophysiology for Nurses
description: Enhances basic knowledge and understanding of human diseases. Reviews the etiology and the pathogeneses of diseases using the system approach. Also reviews etiology, risk factors, long term effects, and prognosis of commonly occurring health issues.

title: Extremity Orthopedics
description: Presents the etiology, course, prognosis, medical intervention, and treatment of orthopedic conditions of the extremities. Includes physical therapy intervention. Guided lab setting.

title: Hispanic Christmas Traditions
description: Bring the holiday customs and traditions of our Hispanic-American neighbors to life this year. On Thursday, Don Samull will present an educational, delightful slide tour, display Hispanic Christmas artifacts and share an ethenic holiday treat. On Saturday you will enjoy a Hispanic holiday meal at a local restaurant. Cost of meal included.

title: Con't Conversational Italian
description: This class is for those who completed Conversational Italian 1 or for persons with a basic knowledge of the language. Books available for purchase the first night of class.

title: Beginning Drawing
description: The basic skill required for all creative art is drawing. Everyone can be taught to draw using a few simple techniques and exercises. Drawing is a way of expressing ourselves and what we see in the world around us. You can tell a story or just have fun learning to use pencil and paper. Minimal materials are required for this class: A pad of drawing paper (11x14 or larger), a #2 pencil, a kneaded rubber eraser, and a vinyl eraser. Other materials will be discussed in class.

title: Computer Basics
description: Familiarizes students with the fundamentals of operating a computer and using software. Provides interactive, hands-on experience to help students become comfortable using computers. Topics include use of a mouse, familiarizing students with the keyboard, proper keyboarding technique, familiarizing students with features of Windows, introduction to files and folders, email basics, and use internet browsers and search engines.

title: Traditional Greek Spring Dish
description: Experience a culinary and cultural delight with instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and rasied in Greece's second largest city and is passionate about sharing her knowledge of food. In this class Zoe will thrill you with the delights of some of the traditional dishes of spring from the countryside of Greece. Start with triangles of cheese or spinach wrapped in a phylo dough and a colorful Greek salad. The main course will be Moussaka (a combination of eggplant, meat sauce and Bechamel sauce). A light dessert will complete the meal. Bring your apron and your appetite.

title: A to Z Grant Writing
description: Designed for individuals who want to get a 'bird's eye' view of the grant writing process, this course will teach you how to research and develop relationships with potential funding sources, complete proposal packages, dissolve crisis management, organize a grant writing campaign, and track funding sources, requests, and donations.

title: Adult Tap Dance 2 (4-Week)
description: If you want to continue your tap dance routine through the semester break, this short course is for you. Continue to work on your skills. Bring tap shoes. A four-week class.

title: Content Management Systems
description: Covers the installation, configuration and publishing of a several industry leading Content Management Systems (CMS). Additional topics include: templates, information architecture concepts, content management, widgets, use of HTML and CSS, user management, logins, usability, page design, backend database administration, modules/plug-ins, security, and web project management.

title: Intro Digital Photo Output!
description: Do you love your digital camera, but can't seem to get the photos to display or print correctly? In this course, you'll learn how to edit and crop unwanted portions of your photos, resize them, adjust their colors, add special effects, print at the highest possible quality, e-mail digital photos to friends and family, and more.

title: Vocal Jazz Ensemble 2
description: Second of four sequential semesters of vocal jazz ensemble, covering preparation and performance of a variety of jazz and popular music in an ensemble of 8-20 voices. Group performs for various HFC and community functions.

title: Life Drawing I
description: An introductory college level class on how to draw the human figure. Discusses proportion and anatomy through direct observation, and explores a variety of drawing media. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Musical Production 1
description: This course combines the study of musical skills and techniques involved in a staged musical production with performance activity in a major production and/or the classroom.

title: Sterling Byzantine Bracelet
description: Discover the secrets of putting sterling silver links together to make a nifty Byzantine bracelet. You'll leave with a ready-to-wear bracelet that could be worn by both men and women. You might later use it as a charm bracelet. $28 materials fees due to instructor in class.

title: RT Clinical Sciences
description: This course focuses on the clinical application of science to the practice of respiratory care. Topics include oxygenation, acid-base balance, ventilation in health and disease, basic microbiology, common microbial pathogens seen with pulmonary infection and the physical principles of spontaneous and mechanical ventilation. Emphasis is placed on blood gas interpretation, capnography, arterial puncture techniques, and disinfection and sterilization of respiratory care equipment.

title: Family Internet Safety
description: Get tips and tools for keeping both children and adults safe on the internet. Topics include: Protecting your kids from online risks and bullies, protecting your computer, protecting yourself from scams, protecting your personal information and creating stronger passwords. Prerequisite: Basic computer skills.
prerequisite_text: Basic computer skills.

title: Basic Tech Mathematics
description: For those in technology programs who have not completed one year of algebra and one year of geometry or who need to review beginning algebra and geometry. Covers a review of arithmetic; signed numbers; scientific notation; measurement conversions; and an introduction to beginning algebra, geometry, and statistics. Emphasizes practical technical applications and requires the use of a non-graphing scientific calculator.

title: Stage Your Home for Sale
description: If you are planning to put your home on the market this spring and it has the "lived-in" look, resembles the television set for "That '70s Show," or needs a general sprucing up, attend this timely seminar. Darlene Rothman, a professional home stager, will show you how to enhance the salability of your home using inexpensive tips from a new trend called "home staging." She'll discuss furniture arrangement, decluttering, paint colors, traffic flow and more. Feel free to bring pictures of your home.

title: Spec Topics in Auto Technology
description: This course explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current automotive technology trends. Specific special topics are announced together with the prerequisites each term. The student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic. This course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree or certificate.

title: Surgical Pharmacology
description: A study of medications used in the operating room with an emphasis on the common drugs used in the surgical area. This course examines the administration, actions, interactions, side effects, and terminology of perioperative medications. Included are legal responsibilities of the surgical technologist and common calculations performed in a sterile field. The student must present two reports as well as complete a term paper.

title: Creative Writing Workshop
description: If you are eager to express yourself through writing, this workshop is for you. As you practice a variety of writing strategies, you will develop confidence, enhance observation skills and gain fluency. Collaborate with other participants as you exchange ideas and respond to each other's work. You will also learn to shape your writing in imaginative and inventive ways. Through expressive, well-developed ideas, you will discover the meaning of your life even as you learn to hone language into a lively and precise style that is yours alone.

title: Intro to MS Word: the Basics
description: Learn to create attractive documents for business or personal use with this powerful word processing program. Designed especially for the novice, this course will teach you how to create, edit, save, and print documents in Word 2000; move and copy text; use Word 2000's AutoText and AutoComplete features; format text, paragraphs, and documents; and use the Find and Replace and spelling and grammar checking features. Microsoft Office User Specialist approved courseware (MOUS) materials included. Intro to PCs with Windows or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: Small Business Marketing
description: This class will focus on the various aspects of marketing and promotion for small and mid-sized businesses. Discussion with Kim Madeline, from Bloom Asset Management, will include determining your target market and your business' unique selling proposition; overview of various marketing tactics; the benefits of building and maximizing a customer database; the pros and cons of various advertising and promotion methods; and the importance of Public Relations in small-business marketing.

title: Learn to Knit Socks
description: Come and learn techniques necessary for basic sock needles, picking up stitches, grafting, working short rows - all useful in making other projects as well. Toddler size socks will be completed during this session. Students will need to bring a skein of worsted weight yarn, size 5 double point needles (bamboo recommended), a tape measure and one stitch marker. Some previous basic knitting experience required. Knitting homework will be necessary to advance and complete your socks.

title: Gentle Exercise Wth Zumba Gold
description: Have fun and get moving! Every exercise is low impact and may be adapted to fit your particular challenges. You can even exercise while sitting in a chair! Certified Zumba Gold fitness instructor Emily Goward will lead you through a Zumba Gold mix of safe and effective aerobic exercises designed to improve your overall strength, endurance and mobility. Adults of all ages and fitness levels welcome.

title: ELL Communication 2
description: Teaches beginning-level college communication skills and interactions to non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-103C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Power of Access and Excel
description: Learn how Access and Excel can work together to improve your reporting and data analysis. Features of the class include utilization of the built-in features of Access and Excel to access data; learning to build connection strings using ADO and DAO; discovering how to automate Excel reports; including formatting, functions and page setup; using Excel charts and pivot tables with Access data. Prerequisites: basic knowledge of Access & Excel. You may want to bring a flash drive to take home the same exercises and cases you will work on in class.
prerequisite_text: basic knowledge of Access & Excel. You may want to bring a flash drive to take home the same exercises and cases you will work on in class.

title: Commercial Wiring
description: Covers the fundamentals of commercial wiring, especially the aspects of the National Electrical Code that specifically apply to commercial construction and repairs. Discusses electrical supplies, hardware appropriate for commercial applications, and commercial wiring techniques. The method of instruction is classroom lecture utilizing PowerPoint presentations.

title: Line Dancing (mini-Session)
description: Step away from your daily routine and join us as we learn some popular line dances and get a great workout at the same time. This class is designed for beginners, but all levels are welcome. And, one of the best features of this type of dance is partners are optional. Wear comfortable shoes, come with energy, and be ready for fun!

title: Introduction to Cartooning
description: Have you always wanted to draw cartoons, either for fun or profit? Attend this class and enter the wonderful world of 'tooning" Using a variety of methods, professional artist Jamie Golob will teach you the basic terms and techniques of the cartoon trade. You'll learn how to sketch and compose, use color, draw caricatures, create your own cartoon characters, and make a comic strip from scratch.

title: Capstone
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-281 to register for this course. Provides required assessment of knowledge and skill for students in their last semester of course work before completing their Associate of Arts degree in Telecommunication. Should be taken during the student's last term of required TCM classes.

title: Basic Economics
description: Offers an overview of macroeconomics and microeconomics. Discusses markets, supply and demand equilibrium, firm decision making, aggregate output, monetary and fiscal policy, and the principles of international trade.

title: Start Licensed Day Care Home
description: Would you love to be home with your children and yet earn money providing services to other parents? Then meet Doris Dickhudt, who ran a licensed home day care for over 20 years. Gain expert coaching and find out how to apply for a home day care license. Learn about daily routines, liability issues, marketing, insurance, business planning and educational resources. Discover the rewards of teaching and training the next generation. Bring your questions!

title: Get the Most Frm Family Search
description: Learn the ins and outs of the Family History Library, the world's largest family history library, in this all-day workshop. Drawing on over 20 years experience, Michael Neill will teach you how to effectively search its various databases, including the Card Catalog, Ancestral File, Pedigree Resource File, International Genealogical Index, and the 1880 census transcripts. You'll also learn how to use the Library's website to plan a trip to Salt Lake City or to your local family history library, as well as use the Library's many research aids and guides. Each student will have a computer to use. Class limited to 14.

title: Equalizer Software Training
description: Equalizer software training (BS&A) provided through Wayne County Association of Assessing Office.

title: Desktop Publishing Indesign, 2
description: Explore the power of using styles, learn to develop complex paths (Bezier, compound,clipping) and use type outlines. Discover many ways graphics can be manipulated and incorporated in text layouts. See how a library and Adobe bridge can streamline your tasks. Learn the tricks to merging data and how to export pdf and XHTML files. See how to manage long documents with hyperlinks, footnotes, text variables, table of contents and indexes. Create a document in sections and easily combine it into a book. Text provided. Prerequisite: Desktop Publishing with InDesign or equivalent experience.
prerequisite_text: Desktop Publishing with InDesign or equivalent experience.

title: Business Grammar & Punctuation
description: Emphasizes punctuation that is commonly applied to documents constructed or edited in the business environment. Covers business grammar and parts of speech in proper context.

title: Senior Fitness
description: Experience the value of fitness and the benefits of activity by developing strength, endurance and flexibility at a comfortable pace. This class, designed for the needs of the older body, is for those who believe you are never too old to exercise. Bring a floor exercise mat.

title: Special Topics in Law
description: Explores selected legal topics with emphasis on current legal trends. Specific special topic will be announced each term the course is offered. Course may be repeated when different topics are offered and a student may earn credit for each different topic. Course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree.

title: Landlord Bookkeeping & Taxes
description: Proper bookkeeping can make or break a business. Too many landlords treat their business like a hobby. Learn how to streamline your booking to make your business more profitable. Bonus: IRS tips to decrease your income taxes. One-session class. There is an informational packet available from the instructor for $5.

title: Clinical Education IV
description: This is fourth in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologist (ARRT) competencies. Course emphasizes increasing technical proficiency in all previously covered radiographic procedures and introduces vertebral column, skull, facial bones, nasal bones, and sinuses. Experience in pediatric radiography is offered.

title: Simple Curtain
description: Dress up a living room, bedroom, kitchen or bathroom with a custom-made curtain that fits your decor! In this class you'll make a simple rod-pocket window treatment, lined or unlined, that can be used alone or over blinds or shades. Please bring your sewing machine and manual to each class. Sewing 101 or equivalent knowledge necessary. Supply list will be provided in first class.

title: Paramedic Procedures Lab II
description: This laboratory course develops overall patient management skills. Covers equipment, assessment, evaluation, treatment, documentation, communication and more. Lab activities focus on the management of the cardiac patient, mega-code scenarios, spinal immobilization, traumatic injuries, and traumatic resuscitation.

title: Create Your Own Spa Experience
description: Don't pay high prices at fancy department stores for luxurious sugar scrubs, skin cleansers, toners, bath salts, and facial masques - create them in our class for a fraction of the price! Fragrant, moisturizing, natural beauty products that will replenish your body and relax your mind - a true spa experience. Make wonderful gifts for your family and friends (these items are hot sellers online too - sell them and earn money!) Call us or visit the Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-$18.)

title: Interactive Experience Design
description: To provide students with the knowledge and skills needed for work in the field of experience design. Introduces User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) Design developing an understanding of basic design principles used in the creation of web and application-based communications. Through assigned projects, students will understand the nature of user interface design while the role of the user is introduced. To provide students with the ability to evaluate and apply design thinking process and systems to user experience design. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Advanced Feng Shui
description: Take your study and life to a whole new level by joining Susan Haigfleigh for this fascinating continuatin of the practice of Feng Shui. You will study some deeper aspects of the practice includ- ing earth energy, advanced placement, chi adjustment, and the use of all five senses. Introduction and Intermediate Feng Shui classes (or for those with prior experience, a personal phone interview with the instructor) required.

title: Souper Meals for Winter - New
description: On cold days, soup is marvelous comfort food. Savor wonderful aromas in this hands-on class with Chef Tim Dare as you discover his shortcuts and pantry lists for making basic homemade cream, broth and pureed soups. Under Tim's watchful eye use these techniques to prepare mushroom barley soup, Maryland cream of crab soup, hearty chicken vegetable soup, and more. From these basic techniques you can create so many variations that you will never want to open a can of soup again! Taste the results and take home Tim's quick-cooking, delicious recipes. Bring an apron.

title: Stress Mgmnt-Personal Approach
description: Offers a supportive group setting for students to examine their own personal sources of stress, how these stress factors are affecting them and what they can do to develop more effective coping strategies.

title: Environmental Science Lab
description: Investigation of current environmental concerns. Particular focus will be given to the Rouge River watershed. Field trips to local sites during the lab period will be included. This course meets once a week for three hours of lab work.

title: Easy Party Foods
description: Nothing facilitates holiday entertaining more than advance preparation of a variety of tasty morsels that can be served with ease once the guests have arrived. This class will focus on presentation and simple hors d'oeuvres for those who want to entertain without fussing for days over the food. The instructor is a professional caterer.

title: Camp RN
description: At Camp RN students are engaged in anatomy and physiology and nutritional science, while learning nursing fundamentals including CPR and first aid. Participants also tour the HFC M-TEC Center, interact with nurses in a hospital setting (shadowing), and utilize the physical assessment laboratory to learn about the human body.

title: Patient Care in Radiography
description: This course is designed to introduce the basic concepts of patient care including patient interactions, body mechanics, patient transfer techniques, vital signs, infection control, emergency situations, age specific care, oxygen administration, basic pharmacology and venipuncture.

title: Create Quick & Easy Slideshows
description: Acquire basic universal camera features and transfer photos from your camera to the computer. Get free on-line photo-editing software and learn to edit photos using highlight, contrast, red-eye reduction, special effects and more! Also, learn to create awesome professional slideshows and home presentations with ease, using pictures from your computer, as well as digital camera pictures & video clips. See how easy it is to create animated effects, use titles on your slides and play background music from a CD. The presentation can then be viewed on a home computer, projected on a large screen, emailed to friends, or put on a DVD for them to watch. Both programs for image editing and creating home movies are free.

title: Elementary German I
description: Introduces the basic skills of reading, writing, speaking, and listening in German within communicative contexts. Covers the elementary pronunciation and grammatical principles necessary for comprehending and expressing simple ideas in both spoken and written German. Presents the cultures of German-speaking countries.

title: Intro to the Study of Religion
description: WR-130 is an introduction to the academic study of religion. In this context, "academic" refers both to an objective approach to course material and to the emphasis on scholars whose writings have helped shape the field of religious studies. Through the works of Kant, Otto, Feuerbach, Marx and others, students examine various ways that "religion," as a category of academic inquiry, has been defined. In addition, through the lens of critical theory, they explore the thought of contemporary scholars as it pertains to definitions of religion and the role of religion in promoting or reducing the power of given social groups.

title: Pcs for Seniors & Beginners- 1
description: If you have little or no experience with computers and want to learn the basics of operating within the Windows environment, this hands-on workshop is for you. This course will provide an overview of Windows 95/98 features and benefits, including hardware and software needs and capabilities, PC concepts and terminology, starting and shutting down a PC, using the keyboard and mouse, inserting and extracting a CD-ROM and diskette, launching and closing a program, creating and saving a WordPad file, and locating and opening a WordPad file saved on a diskette. Textbook included.

title: Advanced Graphic Interpretatio
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of oblique surfaced objects, sectioning, processes used in the manufacturing environment, and geometric and traditional tolerancing. Requires knowledge of traditional and computer-based drafting techniques.

title: Free Health Info on Internet
description: If you seek to lose weight, find the latest reports about your health concerns, or research prescription prices and drug side effects, come to this class. With Internet pro Anita Denard, you will use various websites to check out weight-loss strategies, discover up-to-date, reliable health information, or learn about alternative therapies, vitamins, minerals, and other supplements. Search for answers in class to your questions about heart disease, arthritis, high blood pressure, osteoporosis, men's health concerns, etc. Designed for beginners and seniors with basic Internet and computer skills

title: Co-op in Electrical Technolog
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Drawing II
description: This is and intermediate college level drawing class that stresses drawing fundamentals through working with a variety of subject matter observed firsthand. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Dance Performance - Need Perm
description: This is a dance repertory class that provides opportunities to perform with the HFC Dance Company. Full dances will be taught by guest artists, student choreographers and teachers for performance. A dance technique background is required in any genre along with an audition and interview. Please call Diane Mancinelli at (313) 845-6314 for further information.

title: Ice Carving for Professional
description: Demonstrates how to carve ice sculptures in single and multi-block designs, custom-colored logo designs, and functional ice carvings used on culinary food buffets. Offers advanced techniques in the use of hand and power tools.

title: Pastel Drawing
description: Whether you are beginning your creative journey or returning to it, learn to draw with pastels to create richly colored, textured works of art. In this eight-week course an experienced art instructor will help you 'see like an artist' and understand color, light effects, composition, and more. Call or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $30 depending on what you want!)

title: Polish Baking Class
description: Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suter shares more of her Polish heritage with this baking class. Paczki and Easter Babka are two of the dishes covered this day. Her authentic paczki truly convey the tradition of a decadent treat before the rigors of Lent. This is a hands-on class. Bring an apron and a lunch.

title: Senior Fitness
description: Take care of yourself, make new friends, and experience the benefits of fitness and activity by developing strength, endurance and flexibility in this 10-week course. Exercises are designed with older adults in mind and are adaptable to individual fitness level.

title: Baseball Prospect Camp
description: This camp is designed for high school juniors and seniors to be evaluated by Henry Ford College coaches to be considered to join the Hawks. Eight-player form the last year's Prospect Camp were offered a roster spot and/or an athletic scholarship. Henry Ford College based players have been drafted by Major League Baseball teams and are often recruited to play at 4 year institutions around the country. Coach Tim Leighton host the Henry Ford Prospect Camp. Coach Leighton has completed three years as the Head Coach of the baseball team, and also serves as Associate Scout with Major League Baseball's San Diego Padres.

title: Metallic Jewelry - Mommy & Me
description: Make and take home several unique and colorful earrings, pins and rings, all handmade using "Friendly Plastic" jewelry-making sticks. Lots of fun and easy projects to do, an adult and child will create beautiful looking designs for themselves and each other too! Children age six or older. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list. Registration fee for adult - register children separately at discount rate - see below. Instructor will collect $5 per person materials fee first day of class.

title: Commercial Wiring
description: Covers the fundamentals of commercial wiring, especially the aspects of the National Electrical Code that specifically apply to commercial construction and repairs. Discusses electrical supplies, hardware appropriate for commercial applications, and commercial wiring techniques.

title: Continuing Hatha Yoga
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment in this eight-week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing and relaxation techniques learned in Beginning Yoga, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Camp Nursing Exploration
description: Explore the field of nursing in this one day experience. Learn about the history of nursing, the many nursing specialties (ex. emergency room, operating room), find out how tests are used to determine patient treatment, learn how to use a stethoscope, blood pressure cuff, thermometer, ace bandage or dressing, and take a pulse. One of the following must be completed in advance in order to earn the Nursing Exploration Badge: 1. Do a service project for a healthcare facility. Contact the facility to see what they need done. Organize a group of your peers to help with the project if necessary. 2. Volunteer in a healthcare facility, nursing home or hospital.

title: Begin Comp- Seniors & Newbies
description: Instructor Mike Wilson is on the ground floor. He will show you how to properly turn your computer on and off, use the mouse, and understand print features. Step up to creating a letter, saving a file, and learn how to use your computer's calculator and address book. Explore and use your computer's free software programs. How about a quick game of Solitaire, and then from the Internet, let's play "Who Wants to be a Millionaire." Find out how to download software onto your computer.
