Let's look at your creative side by using the free and fun software "Paint." Class will explore other computer basic features as time permits.

title: Let's Act
description: Learn how to act by doing! At this acting summer camps for teens, we aim to connect that excitement and energy for the stage. Whether a student has theatre acting experience, performed in a few short films with friends, or simply is taken by the dream of becoming an actor, our acting summer camps offers many possibilities to deepen your training and ultimately, to help guide you towards your dreams! Campers will display their new skills in a performance for family and friends on the last day of camp.

title: Beautiful Brush Strokes
description: Explore the art of black ink painting (sometimes called Sumi-e or Oriental Brush Painting) in this one-day workshop. Artist Saundra Weed will teach you the four basic brush strokes for painting flowers, fish, butterflies, bamboo and more. Use these easy-to-learn techniques, rice paper, and bamboo brushes to create note cards and small paintings. You will also learn mounting techniques. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: State&local Govern
description: Examines political institutions and processes in the fifty states, including their cultures and constitutions, as well as the public policies of state governments in areas such as education, social welfare, and the environment. Uses Michigan and Metropolitan Detroit as a basis for comparison to study state and local governments. This course is appropriate as a lead-in for those interested in the fields of political science, public administration, and criminal justice.

title: Acting II
description: Explores various acting styles that may include Greek, Medieval, Renaissance, French Classical, American Melodrama, Farce, Musical Theater, Artaudian Theatre, and other appropriate styles.

title: Basic Lactation Concepts
description: The first course in a two-semester sequence that examines basic lactation concepts including the history of breastfeeding: Breastfeeding Then and Now; Breastfeeding data; Breast Embryology and Anatomy; Relevant Infant Anatomy; Components of Human Milk; and Lactogenesis and Milk Production.

title: UG NX Level II - Intermediate
description: Builds on UG NX concepts introduced in DRAF-131. Explores different task environments. Topics include part families, expressions, assembly simulation, animation, and functional application of dimensions in drafting techniques for manufacturing.

title: Introduction to Archaeology
description: Introduces the branch of anthropology that relates to the study of past societies, cultures, and civilizations. Explores the role of excavation, survey, material culture analysis, dating methods, and interpretation of artifacts, features, inscriptions, and monumental architecture in the ancient world. Examines developments and archaeological discoveries related to various historical periods ranging from the era of pre-human history to the emergence of state-level societies in places such as ancient Mesopotamia, Egypt, and China. Part of the course is also dedicated to familiarizing students with studies and local projects related to the archaeology of Detroit.

title: Surgical Procedures I
description: Designed to continue to develop the concepts from SRG-104. It features general, gynecology, genitourinary, orthopedic, otorhinolaryngology, and endoscopy surgical procedures. The course also emphasizes the scheme or steps as well as the instrumentation of these surgical interventions. One or two half day operating room (OR) observations are required.

title: German Christmas Traditions
description: Experience Christmas the German way as you take a slide trip to Christmas sights in southern Germany and Austria, including some of their famous Christmas fairs and the town of Silent Night fame. As part of the trip you will sample German holiday treats and view a display of German holiday objects. On Saturday you'll enjoy an ethnic holiday meal at a local German restaurant. "Froeliche Weihnachten"!

title: Intro to Engineering
description: Introduces the engineering profession, problem solving fundamentals, applications, fields of study, career paths, teamwork skills, and ethics necessary in the engineering profession. Course work includes projects that deal with real world application of these aspects of engineering as related to engineering problems.

title: Stress Makes Me Gain Weight
description: As we draw nearer to the holidays, do you find yourself reaching for cookies, ice cream, chips or a cocktail? Is the scale creeping up? Then this class is for you. Biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you how such simple measures as getting more light, drinking more water, turning off the TV, and increaisng pleasure can help weight loss. After a self-assessment to determine if you are a fast-burner or a slow-burner, you will discover what foods give you energy, which mineral controls chocolate cravings, and which minerals stop sugar binges. Give yourself this class as a gift to gain control of your food intake, your weight, your brain chemistry and your mood.

title: Mach Guard, Jsa & Oper Train
description: This MIOSHA program involves a full day of safety training to address one of MIOSHA's Strategic Plan Goals: Reduce Amputations.

title: Network Security
description: Introduces network security. Emphasizes security terminology and concepts, network security design, security threats and vulnerabilities, security assessment tools and techniques, application, host, and data security, access control concepts and implementation, risk management and business continuity, and basic cryptography through laboratory exercises and detailed lectures. Course topics typically help students prepare for the CompTIA Security+ certification exam. NOTE: This exam is not included in this course. Industry certification exams are administered by an independent testing agency.

title: Sign for Fun 2
description: Increase your signing skills and understanding of the deaf culture in this unique intermediate-level class. Working with Anne Bolander, author and hearing-impaired instructor, you will become fully immersed in communicating with the deaf community. If you attended Sign for Fun or have a basic knowledge of signing, you won't want to miss this opportunity!

title: Programming/Editing Robots
description: Introduces robotic systems and programming. Reviews robotic system application, automated system safety, robotic system composition, robotic motion control, fundamental programming commands, and program editing. Emphasizes the fundamentals of robot control. Aids students in electronics, welding, computer technology, and general sciences. Pre-requisites: CIMEL 112 or Instructor Permission.

title: Fluid/Pneumatic Ps Cert
description: This class will allow a student to acquire a credential in both hydraulics and pneumatics. Explores the principles of basic hydraulic principles and fluid power pneumatics as they apply to industrial systems. Examines various pneumatic/hydraulic components with respect to their functions within pneumatic/hydraulic power and control systems. This includes laws, components, and symbols. Topics include: safety; the use of tools; and procedures in the design, building, testing, troubleshooting, and repair of industrial pneumatic/hydraulic systems. Includes demonstrations and extensive laboratory activities.

title: Windows Vista: Part 1
description: If you can operate a car you can operate a computer. Don't let confusing computer jargon stop you! Attend this hands-on workshop designed for the person with little or no experience; and see how easy it is to use the basic features of the latest operating system, Windows Vista. You'll learn how to work with the mouse, keyboard, desktop, start menu and become familiar with the components of a working window. By the end of the class you'll also understand the difference between a hard drive, a disk and a CD-ROM.

title: Modern Dance
description: Modern dance is a language of the body in motion. Learn movements designed to develop grace, increase flexibility and coordination, enhance rhythmic sensitivity, and develop body awareness to allow for freedom of expression. In this fun, unique class,dancer Stephanie Fullerton, will teach the innovative Horton technique, which combines diverse elements from ballet, jazz, syncopation, and even Native American dances. Wear comfortable clothing. Dance shoes are optional.

title: Yoga - Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment in this twelve-week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing, and relaxation techniques learned in Hatha Yoga 1, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation, and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Develop Wills & Estate Plans
description: Even if you have a will it may not help you avoid transferred directly to your heirs. Jonathan Goldberg will discuss common mistakes many people make when developing a will or an estate plan and how to avoid these pitfalls.

title: Differential Equations
description: Introduces ordinary differential equations by means of algebraic, numerical, and graphical analysis (including phase-plane analysis). Examines first order differential equations, second and higher order linear equations, methods for nonhomogeneous second order equations, series solutions, Laplace transforms, linear systems, and linearization of nonlinear systems. Covers various applications throughout the course. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Secrets Puff Pastr & Fi Do
description: Delight your friends and family at your next event with elegant homemade puffed pastry and fillo dough appetizers. Learn the secrets to baking spinach pie, strudels with pastry dough, and other yummy pastry appetizers perfect to have on hand for the holidays or that special party. Instructor Marilyn Suter will have you turning out these tasty nibbles like a pro! Come prepared to bake and sample some fantastic appetizers! Bring an apron. Class limited to 14.

title: History of Western Music 1
description: An intensive course providing a practical background in Western music from Greek Antiquity to 1750. Musical forms, styles, composers, compositions, and important influences on the development of Western music are covered through text readings, listening examples, and class discussion.

title: Indust Maintenance Externship
description: Provides field experience in an advanced manufacturing mechatronics environment with students working a minimum of four hours per week throughout the 15 week semester. Offers first-hand the activities, work-practices, and procedures of mechatronics maintenance personnel. Intended for those who have never worked in, or been exposed to, mechatronic manufacturing maintenance in an industrial production facility. Students will work with the divisional faculty/staff to identify field placements.

title: Ophthal Surg Asst & Curr Issue
description: Introduces ophthalmic surgical procedures, including the indications, goals, and steps of select ophthalmic procedures. Emphasizes principles of asepsis, and care and maintenance of ophthalmic surgical instruments. Also, explores issues and trends in healthcare delivery, including certification requirements and systematic review for the national certification examination for ophthalmic technicians.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories as you work your upper body, lowe body, and abdominals. Come for a more challenging class - moderate to advanced fitness levels. Beginners welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Residential Studio
description: This studio course continues the study of the built environment. Students learn design fundamentals as they relate to design challenges within a residential structure including vertical circulation, detailing, and the space planning. Topics covered include design, layout, material selection, detailing, and documentation. Using three-dimensional design software, students will use modeling to solve design problems and create plan, elevation, and section views as part of the architectural design and documentation process. Emphasis will be placed on the use of digital and physical modeling to blend functionality, aesthetics, and sustainability.

title: Residential Wiring
description: Course explores the history and fundamentals of residential wiring including the inspectors perspective, and the aspects of the National Electrical Code (NEC) that apply to residential construction and repairs. Electrical supplies, hardware and their associated wiring techniques for residential applications are explained. The method of instruction is classroom lecture utilizing PowerPoint.

title: Organic Gardening
description: Say "NO" to chemicals in your garden. Learn how to garden naturally to produce healthy plants and harvest in harmony with nature. Master Composter and Advanced Master Gardener Jane Geisler will discuss compost utilization, soil preparation plant selection, and insect and pest control.

title: National Electrical Code
description: This course covers the pertinent aspects of Chapters 1 through 4 of the 2017 National Electrical Code (NEC) as they apply to electrical examinations and installations in all types of buildings and structures covered by the Code. Also covered are essential rules of the Skilled Trades Regulation Act 407 of 2016 and the 2017 Michigan Electrical Code Rules Part 8. The course content is disseminated by lecture utilizing PowerPoint presentations.

title: Wartime Va Aid & Att Pension
description: Are you a veteran or the surviving spouse of a veteran who needs financial help with healthcare expenses? Learn the qualifications for eligibility and how to apply for the veteran's benefits to which you're entitled. Veteran volunteer Kathleen Bolan will explain this often overlooked program that could provide a maximum for a single veteran of up to $1,703 monthly, and help you to apply for it.

title: Exercise Techniques I
description: Provides instruction in therapeutic exercise procedures utilized by physical therapist assistants. Topics include assessment of joint motion and muscle strength, implications of range of motion (ROM) and muscle testing for exercise prescription, types of exercise, basic exercise programs, and the use of exercise equipment. Theory only-no lab activities.

title: English Language Prep
description: Get the necessary language skills to work in U.S. society and to prepare for further education at the college level. In this intermediate-level class you will improve your ability to speak, listen, read and write English, and gain practice for "real life" American situations. Ability to speak,read and write in English required. An interpreter will not be available. Required textbooks may be purchased in class for approximately $30.

title: Adv Acupressure Face Lift
description: Treat your face to advanced facelift exercises and techniques in this fascinating class with acupressure specialist Esther McCormick. Learn what wrinkles say about your emotions and everyday reactions to stress. Then use gentle acupressure massage to relax your neck, shoulder and facial muscles into a more youthful appearance. Also create safe natural cleaners, masks, toners and moisturizers based on skin type to nourish your own face or use for holiday gifts. Bring a stand-up mirror, washcloth and 1-cup plastic bowl to class. $3 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Paramedic Clinical III
description: This competency-based capstone course provides a field internship in order to develop team leadership. Students must complete designated tasks in specific topic areas and in team lead positions with EMS transports. Students who need extended rotations to complete tasks from earlier in the year (who received permission to continue on) can do so after the requisite skills for this rotation have been met. Each individual's program portfolio is completed and assessed this semester.NOTE: Due to the nature of this course, all students must have completed the Health Careers medical packet, drug screen, and criminal background clearance as required, and provide current ongoing proof of health insurance prior to attending advanced level clinical.

title: Intro to CAD
description: Introduces Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) techniques. Topics include: general computer operations, basic file management procedures, basic three-dimensional (3-D) solid modeling techniques, preparation of sketches for modeling, creating orthographic drawings, auxiliaries, sectioning, application of dimensions techniques, and assembly.

title: Is Home Based Business Right
description: Do you dream of earning money from the comfort of your own home? Is the hot, new trend of home businesses the solution to your income and lifestyle needs? Business owner Jayne Burch will discuss the pros and cons of home-business ownership and help you discover whether you have what it takes to succeed as an entrepreneur. She will also outline the steps necessary to get started in a business you can afford. Ideal for moms, retirees, and downsized employees.

title: Help for ADHD
description: If you are a parent, teacher, or counselor who works with children and teens challenged by ADHD, come to learn practical strategies from an expert. Educator Geri Markel, Ph.D., will discuss self-management approaches for use at home and at school, ways to enhance reading, tips for improving educational planning for ADHD, and concerns about medication. Optional book available at class: Helping Adolescents with ADHD & Learning Disabilities.

title: Pilates
description: Flatten abs, strengthen and tone muscles and improve your flexibility, endurance and posture using the Pilates method, one of today's hottest fitness trends. Developed over 90 years ago, Pilates is a tried-and-true, low-impact exercise system. It is popular with athletes, dancers and anyone who wants to develop a more streamlined shape using an all-encompassing mind, body and spirit exercise. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Genline.Com - Swedish Research
description: Explore the wealth of Swedish genealogical information available online at genline.com. Genealogist Michael Neill will teach you how to use the FamilyFinder software, navigate records, interpret online records, and make the most of a Genline subscription. Each student will have a computer and use of Genline during the workshop. For more information about Genline log onto www.genline.com.

title: Contracts
description: Explores the fundamental principles of contract law. Covers the elements of contract formation and breach under common law and Article II of the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC). Presents fundamentals of contract drafting. Discusses the laws of secured transactions under Article IX of the UCC, Bankruptcy Laws, and the laws and practices of collecting debt upon default.

title: Fluid Pow Sys: Circuit Design
description: Explores the principles of fluid power design and troubleshooting as applied to industrial systems. Presents various hydraulic circuits and discusses functions, efficiencies, and troubleshooting guidelines within fluid power systems.

title: Ac Theory & Equipment
description: Examines alternating current theory. Includes right triangle trigonometry and complex numbers to facilitate the AC calculations. Discusses concepts of reactance, impedance, and power factor. Compares single phase and three phase power. Introduces single phase and three phase alternators, motors, transformers, and both heating and lighting loads.

title: Line Dancing
description: Step away from your daily routine and join us as we learn some popular line dances and get a great workout at the same time. This class is designed for beginners, but all levels are welcome. And, one of the best features of this type of dance is partners are optional. Wear comfortable shoes, come with energy, and be ready for fun!

title: Thanksgiving Sides
description: Executive Chef Peter Veach of the University of Michigan will demonstrate some of Thanksgiving's great traditional side dishes and ways to prepare ahead of time to help make your Thanksgiving less stressful. His recipes will be based from the current November issues of "Food and Wine" and "Bon Appetit" magazine so bring your copy and learn some tricks to make your Thanksgiving one to remember.

title: Conversational Chinese
description: Whether for business or for pleasure, your trip to

title: Intro Info Tech
description: Survey the field of computer technology and information management. Covers computer hardware, the use of the Internet for communication, e-commerce, information retrieval, the social impact of technology, computer security, networking, and industry-related careers. Also introduces students to the Windows operating system, Internet browsers, e-mail, word processing, spreadsheets, and presentation software using computer laboratory sessions. This course satisfies the HFC's General Education Computer Literacy requirement.

title: Auto Air Conditioning
description: Studies automotive air conditioning systems, including components, functions, and types of service equipment. Also discusses safety protocols and environmental regulations. Course is for ASSET students.

title: PMP Certification Prep 2
description: Prepare to take--and pass--the Project Management Institute's PMP certification exam. Master chapters seven through twelve of A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge, the most essential resource for the PMP certification exam. Learn all about the nine Project Management Knowledge Areas, five Process Groups, and 39 project management processes. Raise your project management IQ by discovering tips and techniques related to the questions you'll encounter on the PMP exam. Use proven learning techniques to help you absorb key terminology, concepts, and formulas.

title: Guided Read-Differentiat Class
description: Now more than ever, today's teachers are grappling with the question of how to reach struggling readers. While there are no quick fixes, thousands of teachers are combining the principles of differentiated instruction and guided reading with unparalleled success. Mixed in the right proportion, these popular strategies will help you build a balanced literary framework that gets results with even the most challenged learners.

title: Art Appreciation
description: A global survey of the development of visual arts through studying major historic civilizations and cultures from the stone age to the present. Explains technical information on the materials, tools, and techniques used to create visual arts in different branches. Discusses how to interpret works of art by examining the contextual characteristics, the content, functionality and the culture(s) that produced it. Field work at a major museum/art gallery is required to strengthen student's knowledge.

title: Photoshop 1
description: Photoshop is the premier software package for image processing and photo retouching. Become acquainted with this powerful and versatile program as you learn to work with the selection tools, filters, layers, scanning, and more. This class is recommended before taking some of the other graphics design classes. There will be a computer for every student in this class.

title: Organizational Behavior
description: Introduces psychological theory, research, and practice in the business environment. Examines selection criteria and predictors, training and development, performance appraisal, employee motivation and satisfaction, and occupational health. Analyzes and evaluates effective teamwork strategies, leadership theories, and union-management relationships.

title: Lebanese Cooking IV
description: Fried kibbe balls stuffed with lamb. Lebanese green beans. Lebanese rice.

title: Academic Reading for the ELL
description: Emphasizes reading strategies and skills that will prepare English Language Learners for success in college level content-area and English composition courses. Students will learn how to make connections with a variety of authentic texts, practice strategies needed for critical reading, and expand their academic English vocabulary. Students must earn a grade of C or higher before enrolling in ENG-131. This course is non-transferable.

title: One Pot Meals
description: One-pot meals are a time saver, and there's nothing quite like the mouth-watering aromas given off by the simmering pot of food. Simplify your life by learning techniques for making some simple but delicious meals. Instructor Marilyn Szymanski, executive chef at Eastern Michigan University, will teach you four of her favorites: curry chicken, summer vegetable ragout, fried pilaf using leftovers, and an old-fashioned pot roast meal. Bring an apron and your appetite. Class limited to 14. Enroll early.

title: Advanced Mobile App Dev (iOS)
description: An advanced-level course expanding on mobile application development on the iOS platform. Covers data handling and connectivity to back-end services including those hosted in a cloud environment. Design approaches to efficiently reach a large segment in the mobile market will be covered.

title: Get Comfortable With Internet
description: Here's your chance to have a knowledgeable instructor walk you through the internet world. Topics include choosing how to gain access to the internet, using search tools with confidence, preventing pop-ups, guarding against identity theft and scams, and setting up bookmarks to your favorite sites. Learn about "cookies," mapquest, google, mozilla, microsoft internet explorer, adobe reader and more.

title: Graphic Design Studio 1
description: An introduction to visual communication using the Adobe Creative Software Suite. Provides a working knowledge of InDesign and Illustrator. Introduction to the fundamentals and principles of design. Explores the design and development of a variety of media, including multi-page print documents and digital output. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.Students are expected to have access to the Adobe Creative Cloud applications including Photoshop, Indesign, Illustrator, Acrobat, AfterEffects and XD.

title: Auto Tech Serv Exp Lab I
description: Provides real world work experiences and develops entry-level skills in diagnosis and repair of basic engine service, basic electrical, brake systems, suspension systems, and steering systems. Also covers general preventative maintenance procedures such as oil changes and tire rotation. Laboratory activities.

title: Energy Systems Management
description: Presents the technical, economic, and regulatory aspects of energy efficient building energy management. Explores the latest energy and cost-reduction strategies. Focuses on laboratory exercises designed to simulate residential and commercial energy systems.

title: Reduce Small Business Taxes
description: Would you like to increase income from your small business by decreasing taxes? Learn how from taxation specialist Ken Bloom. He will discuss ways to structure health care benefits, contributions, employee and owner fringe benefits, retirement plans, and pensions to lower taxes for small business owners. You will receive guidelines regarding independent contractors, home-based businesses and home office expenses. This class is designed for small businesses ranging from sole proprietors and LLCs to partnerships and corporations.

title: Smart Sellers Get Better Price
description: Get the expert advice you need to sell your home quickly at the best price in this buyer's market. Join an eye-opening class with realtor Jim Kaylor - whether you plan to sell by owner or list with a realtor. Discover how to competitively price and prepare your home for sale, what selling seasons bring the best prices, how to advertise and promote your home for little money, and how to attract qualified buyers. Get sample purchase agreements, disclosure forms, and a free booklet "Tips to Getting Top Dollar." Get your home ready quickly for savvy buyers.

title: Music Theory 3
description: Covers secondary dominants and borrowed chords, the use of the Neapolitan sixth, advanced modulation techniques, and how to identify non-chord tones.

title: Treat Your Feet to Reflexology
description: Under the expert direction of massage therapist Deborah Hanneman, discover the healing zones of your feet, hands and ears. Learn exactly where to press to ease the discomfort of back pain, headaches, arthritis, digestive problems, sinus congestion, sports injuries, insomnia and more. From this ancient healing art, find out what tender spots on your feet may teach you about potential health problems. Invite a friend to join you. Wear clean socks and bring a pillow and towel. Includes 3 reflexology charts.

title: Evolution and Behavior
description: Introduces principles of evolution and animal behavior (including human). Considers genetic, physiological, and evolutionary explanations of behaviors. Topics include evolution and natural selection, genetic inheritance, DNA structure and function, basic cell structure and function, innate behaviors, learning, motivation, communication, aggression, sexual behavior, territoriality, play, vestigial behaviors, selfishness, and altruism.

title: Intro Law Enf & Crim Just
description: An overview of the criminal justice system in the U.S. Topics covered are: the history of law enforcement: the political, sociological, and philosophic, and background of police functions; and the criminal courts. Constitutional problems as they relate to the police function are surveyed, and the use of recent technology in criminal justice is discussed.

title: Music Appreciation
description: This basic appreciation course includes a study of the elements of music and emphasizes listening skills, music vocabulary, and an open-mindedness toward all music. Fulfills humanities requirements.

title: Get Comfortable With Internet
description: Here's your chance to have a knowledgeable instructor walk you through the interenet world. Topics include choosing how to gain access to the internet, using search tools with confidence, preventing pop-ups, guarding against identity theft and scams, and setting up bookmarks to your favorite sites. Learn about "cookies", MapQuest, Google, Mozilla, Microsoft Internet Explorer, Adobe Reader and more.

title: Respiratory Care Neo & Pediatr
description: Introduces the principles of respiratory care for the neonatal or pediatric patient with cardiopulmonary disease or abnormalities. Covers anatomy, physiology, pathophysiology, and assessment of the neonatal and pediatric patient with cardiopulmonary abnormalities and diseases. Offered as an eight-week course.

title: Conversational Spanish Interm
description: If you have completed Conversational Spanish 1 or have a basic knowledge of the language, continue developing your skills in this fun and informative intermediate-level class. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $30).

title: Volleyball II
description: Covers both fundamental and advanced skills as well as strategy, history, rules, and values of volleyball. Student gains valuable social experience and develops a sense of team play along with an appreciation for the sport as a player and a spectator. Nutrition and health related fitness information is included as it pertains to disease prevention and weight control.

title: Achieve Search Engine Position
description: With nearly 90% of web traffic coming from search engines, the most important thing you can do to increase your web site's traffic is to increase your search engine ranking. Learn proven, step-by-step strategies to achieve the highest position with the major search engines.

title: Organize Kids & Their Clutter
description: Tired of chaotic mornings and your kid's clothes and toys strewn everywhere? Don't run away from home! Sign up for this class with professional organizer Theresa Brune. Learn to make organizing an enjoyable joint venture, use kid power to tame toy piles, conquer closet chaos, and put bedrooms in order quickly. Discover the best bins and tools, color-coding schemes, daily routines and school paperwork strategies to assist you. Then take the time you and your kids save by being organized and spend it having fun together!

title: Hammered Silver Bracelet
description: Take a piece of raw silver wire, shape it, hammer it and link it. It's as easy as it sounds when Kathleen Robinson walks you through the process. the result - an eye-catching sterling silver bracelet. The class is great for both men and women. $29 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Latin Hip Hop Groove
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? With a splash of salsa, merengue, mambo and cha-cha, followed by straight up, off the hook hip-hop moves, it's the best of both! Under the guidance of Dena Raptis-Evans, former owner of a Boston fitness center, you'll work your body and soul to the hottest music this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Co-Op in Graphic Design
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible. To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5. Contact the HFC Career Services Office for more information at careers@hfcc.edu.

title: Polish Special
description: This is one of our series of special Saturday workshops in Advanced Home Cooking. These classes are designed for students who know how to cook but wish to learn new techniques and to make specialty dishes. In this class students will create a range of Polish specialties from stuffed cabbage to pierogi (small dumplings with a variety of fillings) to city chicken. Students will cook their own lunch and divide up the day's creations. Your instructor, Marilyn Szymanski, is the Executive Chef at Eastern Michigan University. Bring an apron. This is a hands-on class.

title: Refrig Recov Cert
description: Review the subject matter for the various portions of the EPA Refrigerant Recovery Certification Exam and receive answers to your questions about operation, maintenance, and service of refrigeration systems. The EPA Refrigerant Recovery Exam will be administered at the end of the session. Textbooks available at Main Campus Bookstore. Please purchase and review prior to class.

title: Manage Rental Property
description: Managing your rental properties can be a headache if you don't know your way around the issues. Peter Pace, a successful manager of rental properties, will teach you interview techniques and give other tips to help you find the right client. Discover how to set the correct rental rate for your property. Learn how to property complete lease forms, perform maintenance repairs and generate cash flow for your real estate investments. Bring your questions.

title: Energy Auditor
description: This training will prepare participants to take Certification Field and Written Exam. This is a nationally recognied Home Energy Professional Certification.

title: Surgical Procedures II
description: Designed to further develop the student's knowledge of advanced surgical procedures. It features cardiovascular, thoracic, neurologic, oral, ophthalmic, plastic and reconstruction, pediatric, and geriatric surgical procedures.

title: System Technology
description: Noncredit version of CIMTA 112. The enrollee will be eligible for articulated credit upon their official admission to the college. The course is an overview of the technology found in the integrated systems of today's manufacturing facilities. The course examines the symbols, functions and circuits associated with Fluid Power, Electrical, Mechanical, Control and Communications found in modern integrated systems.

title: Hey! You! Get Off My Cloud!
description: Cloud Computing (the Cloud) promises to change the way we produce and share what we create on our computers. It's also a free and useful way to expand your storage without updating your computer. Have you ever been unable to email someone a document or photo because your email service says " this file is too large to send via our email system"? The Cloud fixes all that! Learn to store (upload), organize, share and download your files and photos and access them from any computer with an Internet connection. Then you can share photos and files with your friends or collaborate on documents with anyone you choose. Sign up for a couple of free user IDs prior to class and we can spend all our time learning. We'll be using skydrive.com, evernote.com, dropbox.com and photobucket.com as we put our heads in the Clouds.

title: Radiation Physics
description: This lecture course focuses upon fundamental physics for radiologic technology including electrostatics, electrodynamics, electromagnetic induction, fundamentals of x-ray generating equipment, x-ray production, and beam characteristics. X-ray photon interactions with matter will be reviewed.

title: History of Rock & Roll
description: Who doesn't know the sounds of Muddy Waters, Chuck Berry, Elvis, Jimi Hendrix, Stevie Wonder and many other legends of rock & roll. Trace the history of this musical phenomenon from its roots in the 40's to its evolution into popular music. Frank Jamal, founder of a production company, will discuss how rhythm and blues, rock, funk, techno, disco, metal, punk grunge and rap have evolved and tansformed the social, political and cultural landscape of the world.

title: Birding: Focus on Warblers
description: These colorful little birds with their musical songs usually arrive here just as the trees and foliage are leafing out, and are popular with the birdwatching community. Did you know they play great importance to the eco system of our North American forests? How many different types can you identify? Join Allen for an early morning walk to learn how and where they live.

title: ELL Communication 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course, along with satisfactory completion of ELI-106R and 106W, qualifies students for ELL-130. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Jump Start Your Job Search
description: Want a new job or promotion? Have you sent dozens - maybe hundreds - of resumes to employers and gotten no response? Take advantage of this informative presentation. See techniques for preparing a resume that will get the attention of recruiters and employers. Get tips for posting your resume to online job search sites and strategies for successful job interviewing. After attending this comprehensive overview, look for these follow-up career training sessions coming in the fall: Get Your Resume Noticed by Employers; What Do I Say; Effective Job Interview Techniques; Salary Negotiation Strategies to Ease Your Fears; Exploring Career Options.

title: Start Emailing Family & Friend
description: Find out how easy it is to email family and friends. Learn to use email features to enhance your email. Instructor and students will also send, reply, and forward email right in the class! Understand how to attach a file or picture to your email. Get rid of annoying multiple names in the header of your email before forwarding your message.

title: Women's Hlth & Maternity Care
description: Introduces the role of the professional nurse as the provider of care for women and their families throughout childbearing and beyond. Addresses health issues within the context of the family environment while recognizing female maturational milestones and discusses how to utilize critical thinking skills, in a systematic, problem solving process, as a framework for providing safe and effective care. Focuses on relationship-based, family-centered nursing that promotes health and manages illness when caring for the childbearing woman and her family. Coursework also discusses how to research best practices and integrate theory in the care of women from adolescence beyond menopause, with an emphasis on maternity care. This course is comprised of a combination of theory and a clinical/lab experience per week.

title: Ukulele
description: Enjoy a musical retreat to the tropical Pacific Islands as you discover the joy of ukulele playing in this two-hour class. By the time you leave, you'll be able to strum properly, read a chord chart, play basic songs, and tune this small, but beautiful-sounding, instrument. Included is your own ukulele. No musical training or experience necessary. All ages welcome.

title: Exp Theatrical Prod - Perm
description: This course is designed as a continuation of performance activities for theater students who have completed performance classes for college credit. Admission by administrative permission only. Contact Mr. Rick Goward at (313)845-6470 for details.

title: Environmental Science Lecture
description: Explores environmental science, an interdisciplinary study which combines ideas and information from the natural sciences (such as biology, chemistry, and geology) and the social sciences (such as economics, politics, and ethics) to present a general idea of how nature works and how everything in nature is interconnected. Emphasizes human modification of natural systems and strategies for promoting environmental health. Also discusses current environmental concerns.

title: Acad Writing & Reading for Ell
description: Intended for English Language Learners (ELLs) whose placement scores indicate the need for instruction or review in order for them to write acceptable college compositions in ENG-131, or for ELLs who have successfully completed ELI-106 R, W, and C courses. Various methods such as lectures, group discussions, textbook exercises, peer review and conferences will be used to help students improve their expository writing skills. This course also emphasizes reading strategies and skills that will prepare English Language Learners for success in college level content-area and English composition courses. Students will learn how to make connections with a variety of authentic texts, practice strategies needed for critical reading, and expand their academic English vocabulary. Students must earn a grade of C or higher in ELL-130 before enrolling in ENG-131. This course is non-transferable.

title: Film History and Criticism
description: This course explores the medium of film as an art form, an industry, and a source of entertainment, examining its historical, cultural, and technical evolution. Students will analyze cinematic techniques, genres, and influential movements to assess films' visual and narrative elements, understand cinema's personal, social, and global impact, and appreciate its role in shaping our world-from early movies to contemporary films.

title: Perennials: Perpetual Joy!
description: Plant perennials once, and enjoy their beauty year after year! Discover how these plants can add excitement to any garden, offering dramatic and interesting ways to landscape your outdoor living room. Learn from a Master Gardener how to pick the right sun and shade perennials, amend and prepare the soil, and fertilize, divide, and prune perennial gardens. You'll also see how easy it is to grow them in containers for years of successful blooms.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-102W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Intro to Physical Science
description: Topics explored include physics, chemistry, astronomy, meteorolgy, and geology. Non-science majors have an opportunity to better understand and appreciate the interaction between energy and matter in nature. Laboratory experiences are designed to improve scientific interest and to develop confidence in dealing with science.

title: Advd. Graphic Interpretation
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of oblique surfaced objects, sectioning, processes used in the manufacturing environment, and geometric and traditional tolerancing. Requires knowledge of traditional and computer-based drafting techniques. Non- credit equivilent to TAGD 120

title: Fund of Plumbing & Pipefitting
description: Explains the development of the proper procedures for the sizing, selection, and installation of pipe and fittings. Includes the development of pipe welding templates and bending of tubing and pipe.

title: Soils and Foundations
description: This course examines the principles and behaviors of soils in construction. Topics include the study of physical and mechanical properties of soils, design considerations, and construction applications. Laboratory and field tests are performed, along with an introduction to design principles of concrete and foundations.

title: Environmental Geography
description: An overview examining human environment interaction at all scales from local to global. Includes an overview of the physical environment and major earth systems with emphasis on the ways humans utilize and alter the earth's surface.

title: Descrip Geometry: Lines/Planes
description: Covers basic descriptive geometry theory, and explores how to use orthographic principles to find true views of lines and planes.

title: Music Appreciation
description: Introduces the basic elements and organizational forms of music, and emphasizes listening skills, music vocabulary, and being open-minded toward all types of music.

title: Applied Music (two Credits) 3
description: Third of four semesters of weekly one-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Ballroom Boot Camp!
description: Let's get ready to Rumba.and more! Always wanted to see what ballroom and Latin dancing was all about? Now is your chance to experience many different dances all in one class. Get an introduction to Foxtrot, Waltz, Rumba, Salsa, Swing, and more! After taking this class, you should feel comfortable enough to do the basics at weddings, clubs, dance parties or other ballroom dance venues. Singles or couples may join, though many feel more comfortable bringing a partner with them. See you on the dance floor!

title: Co-Op in Paralegal Studies
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Zigzag Jewelry
description: Are you looking for something different for your next jewelry project? A lightweight, elegant, zigzag necklace and matching earrings might be the perfect jewelry to wear to a social event or a business meeting. Choose your own colors. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Mexican Fiesta: Cinco De Mayo
description: It's the real deal - authentic Mexican good as Alicia Bonilla-Arias learned from her mother and grandmother. She has owned several Mexican restaurants and now wants to share her heritage with you. The class will focus on white flour tortillas. Learn to make burritos, chimichangas, quesadillas and sweet bunuelos while Alicia introduces you to the various salsas that are key to each dish. Alicia will also give tips on how to make the food more amenable to modern health concerns. Bring an apron!

title: Issues in Surgical Technology
description: Students will identify and discuss advanced surgical procedures focusing upon trauma, transplants, major orthopedic procedures, and the basic principles in the application of robotics in surgery. Electrical elements/concerns in the operating room will be discussed. All-Hazard Preparation will be discussed. The course will also emphasize the elements of professional development including professional organizations, leadership, teamwork, certification and continuing education. Students will discuss the role of the AST and its impact upon careers in surgical technology. Employability skills will be reviewed and applied. Students will be introduced to techniques and exercises in preparation for the national Certified Surgical Technologist (CST) exam. Written and oral presentations by the students are key elements of this course. NOTE: All students will be required to sit for the NBSTSA - CST exam as part of this course.

title: World Regional Geography
description: An overview examining the spatial distribution of people and places around the world, with emphasis on the cultural, political, social, and economic aspects of regions. Topics are explored by examining ways that humans interact with the environment and one another. Emphasis is placed on the spatial variation of human activities at all scales from local to global.

title: Multimedia Design Certificate
description: The Multimedia Certificate focuses on providing the conceptual, technical and visual design skills required to create multimedia applications and environments. Students learn professional applications for Adobe Photoshop, Macromedia Flash, Apple Final Cut Pro, and Adobe After Effects, or equivalent digital imaging, animation, digital video editing and motion graphics tools. Course projects include critique, storyboarding, retouching, compositing, Flash animation and site design, basic Action-Scripting, video and sound editing principles, motion graphics production, and interface design.

title: Beginning Algebra
description: A developmental course for students who need to develop skills in beginning algebra topics. Topics include solutions of linear equations and inequalities, an introduction to graphing linear equations, polynomial operations, factoring, properties of integer exponents, and solutions to quadratic equations by factoring. Utilizes techniques of problem solving and applications throughout the course. Requires a calculator and access to an online homework management system.

title: Polish Paczki
description: You don't have to be Polish to enjoy our paczki party! Come see Don Samull present slides of Warsaw, Poland; and enjoy Polish hors d'oeuvres and, of course, delicious jelly-filled paczkis. In addition, to complete the evening, the noted men's choir from Detroit's St. Hyacinth Polish Church will perform a mini concert.

title: Fingerspelling and Numbering
description: Provides an overview of American Sign Language (ASL) fingerspelling techniques and numbering skills, focusing on improving students' receptive and expressive skills so that students will be able to communicate with speed, dexterity and clarity.

title: Clinical Externship III
description: Continues structured clinical experiences with emphasis on gaining autonomy in performing basic procedures, while adding new experiences in digital imaging and surgical assisting. Students must document 320 clinical hours.

title: Bldg of the White House
description: The White House is the official home and principal workplace of the President of the United States of America. Built between 1792 and 1800 of white-painted Aquia sandstone in the late Georgian style, it has been the executive residence of every U.S. President since John Adams. Our resident historian, Mary McDonald, will lead you through the fascinating story of this building's many changes and reconstructions.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-102W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Personal Finance
description: Examines common financial concerns such as budgeting, obtaining and managing credit, buying insurance, planning for taxes, purchasing a car and/or home, and saving for retirement. Discusses stocks, bonds, and mutual funds. Also provides information on career planning and employee benefits.

title: Parent Care
description: If your kids have attention difficulties, you need a break. Take two hours a week to be gentle with yourself and nurture yourself the way you nurture your children. Make some time for yourself, so you come back to them refreshed and with more to give. Each week you will learn to give and receive a Serenity Ear Acupressure Treatment to help you relax and center. By the end of the series you will learn many different ways to nurture and treat yourself and your loved ones safely, without drugs. You will network with parents, educators and therapists. Bring tennis balls and socks. Wear comfortable soft clothes.

title: Essentials Anatomy&Physiology
description: A comprehensive study of all eleven body systems in a one-semester course. Lecture emphasizes how anatomy, chemistry, and cell biology permit the specific functioning of organs and systems. Lab reinforces understanding of the associated anatomic structures. Please note: BIO-134 is a 5 credit, content-heavy course, which requires a substantial time commitment and focused study beyond the conventional expectations in a standard 100 level science course. Four hours of lecture and two hours of lab per week.BIO-134 does not substitute for the BIO-233/BIO-234 sequence required by HFC's Nursing and Physical Therapist Assistant programs in the School of Health and Human Services (HHS). BIO-134 may serve as refresher/remedial course for the 233/234 sequence if taken within 5 years of original courses and permission is granted from the School of HHS.

title: Fibromyalgia
description: Imagine living with pain every hour of every day! Millions of people do. With symptoms such as achiness, low energy, sleep disturbance, head- aches, allergies, muscle spasms, or depression, many have no idea they may have Fibromyalgia/ Chronic Fatigue Syndrome. Learn about the causes, risk factors, symptoms, and prevention. Get information on breakthrough treatments, including dietary changes, massage, exercise, and other therapies.

title: Lariat Necklace
description: Create a super simple wire project that mixes Swarovski crystals and sterling silver. Using a gallery wrap technique, you'll produce a trendy, versatile lariat necklace that might well become your favorite one this session. $15 supply fee payable to instructor.

title: Spring Floral Arrangements
description: Fresh flower arrangements can have a positive effect on the mood in your home. This is your chance to create stunning spring floral arrangements. Learn basic floral handling techniques, design elements and principles, and creative tips from an internationally known floral designer and educator. Using his extensive background in floral artistry, horticulture, and retail/wholesale floristry, Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will demonstrate and guide you as you create a floral arrangement each week to take home. Bring a notepad, pen/pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No previous experience necessary.

title: Fusion Fitness Workout
description: Sculpt your muscles, boost your energy level and burn calories in this class designed for fun and fitness. You'll start the class with an invigorating warm-up, followed by simple low impact footwork for the hips and bootie, abdominal exercises to strengthen core muscle and light hand weights training for additional sculpting. All will be infused with yoga to add stretch and flexibility. Bring a mat, water and towel to class. All levels welcome.

title: Professional Strategies Culina
description: Covers careers in the culinary field across multiple interrelated segments including lodging, restaurants, managed / contract services, and travel and tourism. Examines how to perform an analysis of trends and current opportunities for entry-level management positions. Also discusses strategic career planning including use of the DISC profile questionnaire.

title: Basic Photo
description: Greatly improve the quality of your photos! Whether you are a beginner or an experienced photographer, you will gain a better understanding of your camera and its functions, photographic tools and materials, and basic photographic principles. This class is designed for users of 35 MM SLR cameras. An optional working field trip on Saturday, February 10, is included.

title: Be Your Own Bodyguard
description: Feel safer in any environment. Using techniques from a combination of several martial art forms, quickly develop survival skills and strategies that will help you defend yourself as well as stay in shape. Javarri Hill, a master in Chinese kenpo, a black belt in tae kwon do and an experienced instructor, will teach you how to use the martial arts for self defense. Appropriate for women and men of all ages. No prior experience required. Uniform included in cost and is yours to keep.

title: Intro to Homeland Security
description: Provides students with a comprehensive overview of Homeland Security policies, practices, and programs. Also discusses local, state, and federal initiatives, as well as the restructuring of the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Co-op in Communications
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Collecting Antique Dolls
description: Whether it's grandma's doll from the attic or a garage sale find, that special doll may become the centerpiece of a fascinating collection of various types of dolls made of wood, wax, cloth, porcelain, tin, plastic or perhaps celluloid. Learn of the great doll makers of Germany and France and our own American artists.

title: Fall Flower Arranging
description: Fresh flower arrangements can have a positive effect on the mood in your home. This is your chance to create stunning fall floral arrangements. Learn basic floral handling techniques, design elements and principles, and creative tips from an internationally known floral designer and educator. Using his extensive background in floral artistry, horticulture, and retail/wholesale floristry. Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will demonstrate and guide you as you create a unique fall arrangement each week to take home. Bring a notepaid, pen/pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No previous experience necessary.

title: Create Wealth Globally
description: Because of increased US outsourcing, rapid industrialization in China and India, and rising energy prices, allocating some of your portfolio to international investments can help you grow wealth. Let financial planner Ken Bloom review for you the opportunities and risks in global investing. Get inside information about why you need an emerging market fund in your account and how to use international funds to lower your investment risk. Take home many investment strategies and a packet of handouts.

title: Elementary Italian II
description: Further builds reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Italian, focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops knowledge of vocabulary, pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express everyday ideas in both spoken and written Italian. Presents Italian culture.

title: Special Topics Mach Tool Techn
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current machine tool technology. Specific special topics are announced together with the prerequisites each term. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic. Course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree or certificate.

title: Probation and Parole
description: Covers treatment of convicted law violators by correctional field services before and after prison, analysis of the role of probation and parole officers, and evaluation of community resources applied to the correctional task.

title: Photoshop CS5, Level 2
description: Move on in your exploration of Photoshop features as you learn how to use color fills and overlays as well as gradients. Explore various ways to extract an object from an image using a mask. Experiment with vector paths by creating vector masks and converting type into editable shapes. Learn to apply custom brushes, warping, and Smart Filters along with other creative image effects. After producing images for videos end the course by automating tasks and customizing Photoshop. Text provided. Prerequiste: Photoshop CS5 Level 1 or similar experience.

title: Residential Wiring Multifamily
description: This course provides an in-depth study of electrical systems and code requirements for multifamily dwellings. Students will explore wiring methods, switching layouts, and safety systems unique to multifamily residential structures. Emphasis is placed on electrical load calculations, firestopping standards, and compliance with the National Electrical Code (NEC) for installations, permits, and inspections. Students will also look at how to use special equipment and where to correctly install smoke alarms, carbon monoxide detectors, and safety devices like GFCI and AFCI circuits to make sure the electrical design and operation are safe and effective.

title: Jazz Dancing
description: Express yourself in the syncopated beat of jazz. Some basic moves include: ball change, twist, pivot step, touch step, Pique' passe', stag leap, jete', pencil spin, barrel turn, fan kick, jazz walk, jazz run, hip walk, chasses, cat walk and the catch step. Jazz dancing incorporates a wide variety of basic ballet based steps and modern dance. Join the fun with dance master Emily

title: Audio Mastering 1
description: Provides hands-on experience and guided practice in digital audio mixing and mastering. Explains how to prepare complex digital audio files for duplication and audio for mixing. Also covers basic techniques in mixing room acoustics, software plug-ins, how to troubleshoot various audio problems, equalization, stereo imagery, noise limitation, and bit conversion. Uses professional audio production software.

title: Topics in Skilled Trades
description: Provides specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics offered is identified by the Skilled Trades Apprenticeship Division faculty, STAD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the STAD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Course may be repeated for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Fall Birding
description: For a unique fall birding adventure join Arthur Chartier, coauthor of Birds of Detroit and a very active birder. Through classroom instruction and field work you will improve your observation skills, develop ways to record your sightings and learn to use field marks to identify birds. After an initial classroom session, plan to meet your instructor at local sites to practice identifying and observing a variety of birds in their native habitats. Directions to the sites will be provided the first day of class. Bring binoculars to enrich your experience.

title: Co-op in Social Science
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible. To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Psychiatric Mental Health Nurs
description: Introduces the second semester nursing student to the role of the nurse as a provider of care for clients with mental health disorders with emphasis on therapeutic communication techniques. Course presents the mental-health/mental-illness continuum as a framework for differentiating between healthy and unhealthy mental health behaviors. Discusses how to utilizes critical thinking skills, in a systematic, problem solving process, to provide safe and effective care to clients in the psychiatric mental health setting. This course includes supervised clinical agency/laboratory practicums. Course comprised of two hours of lecture and six hours of lab/clinical per week.

title: Maintain/Protect Your Computer
description: Your home or small business computer is a popular target for intruders. Why? Because unfortunately some people want what you have stored there. Just as you routinely do things to keep your home secure, you need to perform certain tasks to safeguard and maintain your computer. In this class, a computer expert will teach you to secure any computer by installing anti-virus and firewall programs, patching your system, checking e-mail attachments, making backups, creating passwords and more. This course is especially important for persons who have DSL or cable modems.

title: Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, bala includes stretching, breathing, posturing and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga, your body will evelop flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment; and you will achieve mental concentration relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Adv Cardiac Life Support Recer
description: Update your ACLS skills with the most current standards in Emergency Cardiac Care. A current ACLS card and a current BLS health care provider card are required and must be presented at class. No grace period. Upon successful completion, an ACLS card will be issued. Textbooks may be purchased from the instructor.

title: JB - Computer Keyboarding
description: This course is designed to teach students "touch" keyboarding skills on the personal computer. Terminology, the alpha keyboard, figure keys, symbol keys, the ten-key numeric pad, and basic formatting are introduced. As the semester progresses, accuracy and speed building are emphasized. Comparable to BCA 101.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 4
description: Develops low-intermediate-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-105W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: CPR, AED & First Aid
description: Gain the knowledge and skills needed to effectively identify and respond to emergencies caused by injury or sudden illness. Basic first aid, cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) and the automatic external defibrillator (AED) will be covered. Students will be expected to participate in all class activities. Upon successful completion you will receive an American Red Cross certificate. Course is appropriate for lay rescuers, adult or child caregivers, and anyone wanting to know how to help someone in an emergency. Wear comfortable clothing and bring a notebook and pen. Materials fee of $35 is payable to the instructor in class.

title: Presidential Ladies
description: Oh what fun to look at inaugural gowns and hear fascinating stories of the women who wore them. Mary MacDonald has slides of the china patterns and gowns of these women as they are featured in the Smithsonian Museum. Over the years Mary has researched these sometimes powerful and often overlooked women who stood behind the Presidents. What was life in the White House like for these ladies? How did they manage children and family in the eye of public scrutiny? This evening promises to be an interesting look into the lives and times of our first ladies.

title: Solving Classroom Discipline 2
description: In this professional development course, you'll get the teacher training you need to deal effectively with serious discipline problems and help even the most challenging students you're teaching make more responsible choices. You'll learn how to use a new research-based six-step approach to solve severe and chronic discipline problems such as bullying, fighting, using abusive language, stealing, and refusing to work. We'll look at numerous real-life examples set in elementary, middle, and high school classrooms so you can see how to put the ideas into action in your own teaching situation.

title: Ancient World History
description: Covers the rise of civilization to the decline of major world civilizations in the early Common Era (c. 200 CE to c. 500 CE). Studies the ancient civilizations of Mesopotamia, Egypt, India, China, and the Greco-Roman world.

title: Earn and Learn Bridge to Tech
description: The Earn and Learn Bridge program is a partnership between Henry Ford Community College and Southwest Solutions designed to aid Earn and Learn Program participants who wish to improve their reading and math skills in order to pursue higher education or career focused opportunities.

title: Clinical Optical Procedures
description: Focuses on the physiology of image formation and refractive errors, optical calculations involving use of the retinoscope, phoropter, trial lenses and trial frame, accommodation rulers, and instruments used in evaluating contact lenses.

title: C++ Programming
description: An advanced-level course expanding upon arrays, pointers, structures, templates, and file I/O. Test Driven Development (TDD), refactoring, and UML diagrams concepts are applied. Covers theory and application in areas such as the standard template libraries including vectors, lists, stacks, queues, sets, and maps. Object oriented principles of classes, data, inheritance and dynamic binding are covered.

title: Louisiana Style Cooking
description: Learn how to cook Gumbo, Jambalaya, Shrimp Etouffe' and Catfish with hushpuppies. Chef Sania will show you how.

title: The Story of Food
description: What was the first thing man ate? You'd be surprised! (Hint: this food was prevalent in the 1860's in Dearborn at special parties). Come find out if your guess is correct and learn more as Mary MacDonald, recently retired curator of the Dearborn Historical Museum, guides you through the edible evolutions of populations past and present. This is one presentation you won't want to miss.

title: Real Est Broker Prep
description: This 30-hour course will prepare the real estate agent to take the Michigan Broker Examination. It will include a review of basic real estate fundamentals, broker responsibilities, fair housing (3 hours), and much more. The course is approved toward broker licensure requirements. Cost includes all books and materials.

title: System Technology
description: This course is an overview of the technology found in the integrated systems of today's manufacturing facilities. The course examines the symbols, functions and circuits associated with Fluid Power, Electrical, Mechanical, Control and Communications found in modern integrated systems. Pre-requisites: None.

title: Media Writing
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-241 to register for this course. Offers an analysis of and practice with the forms and formats of mass media script writing: commercial, promotional, public service, instructional, and dramatic.

title: Wedding Flower Demo Class
description: Professional, award-winning designer Susan Muldowney brings wedding decorations to life in this demonstration class. She will show you how to make a bridal bouquet, centerpiece and other wedding crafts. Please bring a notepad and pencil to this class.

title: Live Well Be Well- Memca
description: Student lead conference surounding health, wellness, & community leadership.

title: 2 Day Mech Power Press
description: This course, offered by the Michigan Bureau of Safety&Regulatin, provides information about Power Press Safeguards; Control Reliability and Brake Monitors; Die Setting and Fastening; Injury Statistics; Most Frequently Cited Safety Rules - Part 24; Press Room Health Issues; Lockout Program and Procedures; and Job Safety Analysis & Operator Safety Training.

title: Piano Basics for Adults
description: Don't let that keyboard sit in the corner collecting dust anymore! Have some fun learning how to play well-known classic and contemporary pieces. Under the direction of our instructor, you will become familiar with the keyboard, basic notes, hand positions, and music theory concepts. By the end of the course, you will be able to play popular songs using your left hand for chords and your right hand for melody. Text book included.

title: Michigan Roses
description: Every Michigan gardener can enjoy the magnificent beauty and perfume of roses! Nancy Lindley, author of Roses for Michigan and owner of a popular rose store, will share her tips for planting, pruning, cultivating and winterizing roses. She'll also highlight the best roses for Michigan gardens, including hybrid teas, shrub, climbing and antique roses. You may buy an autographed copy of Roses for Michigan after class for $20.

title: Piano Basics for Adults
description: Don't let that keyboard sit in the corner collecting dust anymore! Have some fun this winter learning how to play well-known classic and contemporary pieces. Under the direction of pianist Lesley Melville, you will become familiar with the keyboard, basic notes, hand positions, and music theory concepts. By the end of the course, you will be able to play popular songs using your left hand for chords and your right hand for melody. $16 textbook fee due to instructor in class. Please bring correct change.

title: Arduino Gadgets
description: Do you like to build computers? Do you like turning your ideas into real devices: Then Ardunio Gadgets Camp is for you! Mini-computers, micro-controllers, control systems, sensors.electrical components, programming, and mechanical set-up of a prototype.it's all part of the fun! Learn to create a small autonomous car that can collect information from its environment via sensors and avoid obstacles using a control system that YOU program. Join us for this fun, engaging and educational three-day experience that will introduce you to the exciting world of engineering!

title: Co-op in Industrial Technolog
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.

title: Basic Sheet Metal & Fabricat
description: Covers layout, forming, and fabrication of basic sheet metal ductwork fittings and use of hand/power tools and equipment needed to accomplish various tasks. Explains how to fabricate square/round sheet metal ductwork, tapers, transitions, offsets, and how to fasten ductwork together.

title: Therapeutic Procedures II
description: This course provides instruction in therapeutic procedures utilized by the respiratory therapist. Course content includes airway care, emergency life support, bronchial hygiene therapy, and lung expansion therapy. Students practice these procedures in a guided laboratory setting. Course includes 3 hours of lecture and 2 hours of lab each week.

title: ELL American Culture 5
description: Develops intermediate-level knowledge of American culture, history, and geography for non-native English speakers. Focuses on both content (American culture) and format (academic lecture and note-taking). Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-106A. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Industrial Apps Algebraic Prin
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas.

title: Carve Ice!
description: This lecture and hands-on ice carving class is certified by the American Culinary Federation Educational Institute and is designed to benefit both beginning and advanced students. Learn how to carve single-block and multi-block ice sculp- tures under the direction of Chef Richard Teeple, an HFCC instructor whose ice carving teams are consistent local and national prize winners. Additional ice-carving experience will be available with the Ice Carving Club. All necessary tools will be provided.

title: Chicken Shawarma
description: Chef Kamal Hachem will help you prepare one of the most popular Mediterranean dishes, Chicken Shawarma - deboned chicken breast, cut into finger size slices, with white pepper, paprika and other ingredients, served with garlic sauce. It will be served with Tabbouleh, a salad made with bulgur wheat and finely chopped scallions, tomatoes, mint, and parsley. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Solidworks Level III - Applica
description: An advanced industrial applications course utilizing 3-dimensional (3D) advanced construction tools. Focuses on how to apply key concepts from prior SolidWorks courses to real-world industrial projects. Also examines how to create, edit, and export bills of materials; apply assembly modeling procedures; create exploded views; edit components in an assembly; add and constrain mates; utilize simulation Xpress tool; and perform basic motion analysis.

title: Ther Act for Dementia (nurses)
description: Sharing fun-filled activities can ease the distress of both persons with dementia and their caregivers.
