Amira Tame, a certified activities consultant, will teach word games that enhance memory recall, show videos and hands-on demonstrations of activities that promote healthy relationships with caregivers and offer strategies for overcoming anger, anxiety, resistance and passivity. Included is the presenter's book, Healing the Wounds of Alzheimer's Disease. 4 Activity Professional CEUs. 4 nursing contact hours.

title: Plumbing Design
description: Topics include health and safety, water supply, water wastes, piping materials, and building plans and drawings. The proper use of plumbing tools and equipment selection emphasizing overall job safety is integrated into the course material. Also covers the components of plumbing systems and installations.

title: Customer Service
description: Examines the principal functions of a customer service representative, the skills needed to succeed in the workplace, and the knowledge to advance into a leadership role within the service industry. Emphasizes effective communication skills, positive attitudes, problem solving, strategies for coping with challenging and dissatisfied customers, retention of customers, methods of measuring customer satisfaction, technology and customer service, and motivational techniques. NOTE: Upon successful completion of this course, students may choose to take the exam for National Professional Certification in Customer Service from the National Retail Federation (NRF) Foundation. This exam is not included in this course.

title: Party Like the Stars
description: Average budget? Celebrity taste? Personalize your party for that special occasion - birthdays, anniversaries, or maybe just a girl's night out - and have fun doing it! Learn to plan a party that is stress free, create a "theme" party for children or adults for any occasion, and be able to give it "high style" on a reasonable budget. For over 10 years, instructor Tanya Hughes has been a successful event planner, decorating for many corporate and private events. She will share her secrets with you and challenge you to get creative. Come on, let's party!

title: Applied Music (2 Credits) 2
description: Second of four semesters of weekly one-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Fair Housing & Civil Rights
description: Approved by the Board of Real Estate Brokers for broker licensure education requirements, this six-hour course overviews the federal and state fair housing and civil rights requirements for rental properties. Topics include advertising, HUD regulations, the Fair Housing Act, case law, and much more. This is also an excellent class for landlords.

title: Landscape Ser: Front/Back Yard
description: Register for this session to take both the front yard and the back yard classes at a 10% discount over the combined individual class rates.

title: Beat the Blues (for Nurses)
description: If those gray Michigan days of winter find you or a loved one feeling a bit blue, come hear biochemist Sandy Baumann talk about ways you can use bright lighting, healthy food choices, fun activities, vitamins, minerals, herbs, fish oil, and amino acids to help regain a positive outlook. You'll also learn about the physical and emotional origins of depression, and discover ways to help someone dealing with it. 6 nursing contact hours.

title: UG NX Level IV - Applications
description: An advanced-level industrial applications course that examines how to create 3-dimensional (3D) wireframe geometry, develop 3D models using surfacing functions, construct complex surfaces, create and operate kinematic simulations, and operate assembly simulations.

title: Practice Kung Fu
description: If you've had some experience with Kung Fu and are looking for an opportunity to practice, join expert Howard Scrivens.

title: Police Academy
description: Covers the proper techniques of investigation, crime scene process, patrol procedures, operations and techniques. Emphasizes conflict mediation, report writing, and detention and prosecution of prisoners. First aid, investigations, evidence collection, disaster control, civil disorders and tactical operations will also be covered.

title: Generate Income From Portfolio
description: Investigate ways to increase current income from your portfolio using mutual funds, dividend-producing stocks, fixed annuities, and other investments in order to balance periodic dips in the stock market. Investment advisor Ken Bloom will give specific recommendations to help you preserve your principal while generating more cash flow.

title: Photoshop Elements
description: Learn to use Photoshop Elements 4, the home-user version of the popular Photoshop photo-editing software. Get step-by-step instructions about how to share, organize, edit, crop and size your digital images. Correct red-eye, adjust skin tones, adjust skin tones and remove scars, wrinkles and other blemishes like a pro! Use new tools to lift images and place them into other photos for amazing collages and scrapbook effects. Discover techniques for combining photos into panoramic views and for restoring old photos. Includes a CD with practice files for home use. If you prefer to enroll in Photoshop CS, see CL2 schedule.

title: Photoshop Cs: Web Production
description: Optimize images for display on the internet. Set photoshop unit preferences to measure web image resolution; learn how to balance image quality with file size reduction; calibrate your system's color display to create web pages; use photoshop as a part of creating finished web pages; export individual slices and create an HTML web page that displays a table containing the sliced section. Have fun using ImageReady to create animation for your web pages. Textbook included. Photoshop 1 & 2 required.

title: Digital Audio Editing
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-151 to register for this course. Introduces digital audio editing using an industry standard software editing program on computers in the Telecommunication audio production labs. Covers both hardware and software issues, and explores how to perform short editing exercises.

title: Graphic Design Studio 2
description: Builds on skills developed in ART-114. Covers intermediate design solutions for a variety of media including print and digital using the Adobe Creative Software Suite. Focuses on creating materials for brand identity and provides an introduction into User Experience (UX) design. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.Students are expected to have access to the Adobe Creative Cloud applications including Photoshop, Indesign, Illustrator, Acrobat, AfterEffects and XD.

title: Chakras & Auras - Part 2
description: Delve deeper into your "inner-energy"! This class will expand on the Part 1 class and teach you how to expand your understanding of your chakra system and discerning auras. It will enhance your ability to "see" and understand your life, relationships and how you can make a positive difference in the world!

title: Clown School Camp
description: 03 Aug 2012 11:09 AM Pauline Barbas Fun, play, and foolery can be serious business when it comes to learning values, seeing things in a different light, and discovering the inner you. The Clown is where the true self can come out and play. Join master clown Joe Zsigray in morning discovery to bring out the clown within you and have lots of fun. The 5 sessions will concentrate on finding your clown and giving it expression, through make up, costuming, communication, & movement. In addition, time will be spent in building a clown's tool box of magic, balloon sculptures, props, juggling, and harmonica music. Participants will need to bring some clothing and ordinary house hold items from home for costuming and props. Balloons, Harmonica, make-up, juggling bean bags, and magic equipment will be provided. The experience will end with a Celebration of Joy and Delight. Ages 10 - 14.

title: Explore Watercolor - 2 Weeks
description: Rediscover the childhood joys of painting while composition, and a variety of simple watercolor painting techniques. Come enjoy ways you can become creatively self-expressive using this exciting medium. This class is suitable for all levels of skills: beginners to repeaters. Supply list available online before class.

title: Start Ebay Drop Off Shore
description: Turn other people's unwanted items into a lucrative source of income by starting an eBay drop off store. Master the art of setting up a business, writing a marketing plan, taking super photos, creating dynamite listings, and offering great customer service. Thousands of people have transformed their eBay knowledge into profitable second income sources or even full time jobs. Take your skills to the next level and avoid the common pitfalls that keep amateur sellers from reaching their full potential.

title: Product Drawing
description: Examines the demands of product drafting. The design process is applied in the development of a product with consideration given to its function, fix, aesthetics, ergonomics, and its ability to be economically manufactured. Drawing assignments are done extensively on CAD.

title: Theatrical Production
description: This course is designed as a continuation of performance activities for theater students who have completed performance classes for college credit. Admission by administrative permission only. contact Mr. Gerard Dzuiblinski at 313-845-9634 for details.

title: Honors Directed Study
description: In this sophomore-level directed study, students are challenged to apply the knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Students present summaries of their research before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty held at the end of each semester.

title: Entertaining Night, Vege
description: Learn to create imaginative appetizers that you won't believe are prepared vegetarian and dairy free. In this interactive class you will prepare finger foods for entertaining including stuffed mushrooms, tasty roll-ups, and more, capped off with a Chocolate Mole Mousse. Your new culinary mastery will be the talk of the party.

title: Legal Research
description: Examines the structure of the court systems and sources of law in the context of conducting legal research; covers how to locate and understand statutory, regulatory, and common law; examines how to identify real or hypothetical legal issues and then develop a research plan. Covers how to perform legal research using Westlaw and other Internet-based research databases.

title: Poetry & Prose: Call the Muse
description: Let the muse guide you as you write wonderful poetry inspired by your life and that of other writers. Cheryl Vatcher-Martin, a columnist, editor, award-winning poet and professor, will teach you how to turn an everyday experience or emotion into a powerful poem. Explore writing techniques that will help fill your notebook with poetry and prose. In this workshop you will practice your poetry and get feedback from your classmates. $5 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Easy MS Word for Beginners
description: Use your computer to create letters, memos, reports and other documents for business or personal use. See how easy it is to type in your information and have it look great. Learn to edit text, move things around, and even have the computer check your spelling and grammar! Seasoned instructor Audrey Spezia will show you how to save your work and how to find it again. Make text look fancy with simple formatting features. Print out perfect pages without whiteout and erasures! Discover shortcuts for making repetitive tasks easy. Prerequisites: intro PC or equivalent knowledge required. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: intro PC or equivalent knowledge required. Text provided.

title: Identity Theft:prevent & Aware
description: Provides an overview of the identity theft problem in America. Includes current statistics from around the nation, profiles of specific cases, various prevention techniques, and steps to take if victimized.

title: Honors Direct Study Serv Learn
description: Challenges sophomore students to apply knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic anchored in community service. Coursework includes presenting a research summary before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty at the end of each semester.

title: Contemporary Guitar 1
description: Learn the basics of playing guitar. While strumming your favorite pop, rock, or folk music, you will acquire tools to use for any style of guitar playing. Minimum age is 18; no experience with guitar is necessary. Bring your own guitar.

title: Help for Snrs on the Internet
description: See how easy it is to search the web in this fun and non-threatening class environment. Acquire information on agencies that assist seniors with issues that relate to them. Do research on enior topics from "A" to "Z". Find out about things like medicine interactions and generic equivalents. Learn toolbar features of Internet Explorer. Get senior-related questions answered and acquire so much more.

title: Wld Joint (Mtrl Cut,Grinding)
description: Explores the set-up and use of the Oxy/Fuel cutting torch, the Oxy/Fuel line cutter, Plasma Arc cutting, safety protocols, and proper use of power tools in the welding lab. Also explores how to assemble various weld joints. Laboratory activities.

title: Glass Flowers
description: Experience the fun of twisting, pulling and shaping hot glass into flower creations. Unlike nature's creations, your unique single-stemmed flower design can be displayed and enjoyed day after day for years to come. No experience necessary. Please wear loose-fitting cotton clothing. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Creating Inclusive Classroom
description: In this professional development course for teachers, you'll get the training you need to reach the diverse mix of students you face every day-learning proven strategies that turn diversity into opportunity. With students who have learning disabilities, neurobiological disorders, and physical challenges, the modern classroom requires an efficient and effective teacher who can prioritize under tight deadlines and be creative on demand. Learn how to be the kind of super teacher who can guide every student toward academic success.

title: Darkroom Photography
description: A beginning college level art class designed to teach the use of an manual 35 mm SLR film camera, development of black & white negatives and the making of black and white prints in the darkroom. If the student does not own a suitable camera prior to taking this class, he/she may wait until the first class meeting prior to purchasing one. Creativity is emphasized. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Mouth-Watering Holiday Dessert
description: Dazzle your family and friends with delicious desserts. Join professional caterer Andrea Taylor while she teaches you how to make such delicacies as cream puffs, tiramisu, fruit strudel, and cream-filled eclairs. Be ready to treat your taste buds. Bring an apron and a desire to try new deserts. Class limited to 16.

title: Criminal Investigation
description: Introduces criminal investigation and the techniques of forensic science. Topics include information accumulation, specific crimes, and preparation for purposes of prosecution.

title: Understanding Adolescents
description: To know your kids is to love them. In this course, an experienced social worker will help you gain a deep understanding and appreciation of your adolescent's development and behavior. You'll uncover the secrets of the adolescent mind and gain valuable information on how they think, how they feel, how their identities develop, and what steps you can take to ensure that you are well prepared to meet the needs of the teens in your life.

title: Auto Tech Serv Exp Lab II
description: Explores diagnosis and repair of major engine service, electrical/electronic systems, fuel/emission systems, ignition/engine control systems, ABS/traction control systems, active suspension systems, manual transmission, automatic transmission, driveline, and HVAC systems.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories as you work your upper body, lower body and abdominals. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Intermediate Modern Dance I
description: Provides students with continued applied study of modern dance technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in modern dance history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and the principles of time, space, and energy. The course provides students the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of modern dance. Learning activities physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate a kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of modern dance.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, awareness of the body in space, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.The modern dance work for Intermediate Modern Dance I is suitable for students starting to work on their intermediate modern dance knowledge and skill set. Intermediate Modern Dance II and III provides an opportunity for students to continue the development of their modern dance movement and expression skills. Student's will continue their academic growth through immersion in history, choreography, terminology and dance principles.

title: Photoshop CS Essentials
description: Learn to use Photoshop CS, the premier software for image processing and photo retouching. Become acquainted with its powerful tools to select, crop, copy, rotate and resize images and photos. Employ layers to professionally manage your edits and the history palette to successfully undo changes. Use a variety of options to adjust tone, brightness and color; create shading and special effects; and save images in formats for print or Internet. Learn to touch up images with the pain brush and cloning tools, create fast-loading Internet graphics, and combine several photos to create a collage. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent experience. Workbook provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent experience. Workbook provided.

title: Food and Nutrition
description: Examines basic concepts of nutrition, food composition, food technology, controversies in nutrition, and marketing nutrition in the food service business. Covers carbohydrates, fats, protein, vitamins, RDA, food labeling, menu planning, weight management, cardiovascular disease, nutrition and cancer, and modifying recipes for health and lower calorie content.

title: Vocal Jazz Ensemble 1
description: First of four sequential semesters of vocal jazz ensemble, covering preparation and performance of a variety of jazz and popular music in an ensemble of 8-20 voices. Group performs for various HFC and community functions. New singers may audition before or during the first week of class. No preparation is necessary, but the demonstration of the ability to sing in tune and hold a challenging part (soprano, alto, tenor, or bass) is required.

title: Bus Planning Tools Effec Oper
description: Now that you have decided to start a business or possibly re-evaluate a current one-it's time to take the critical step of developing a business plan. Learn how to put together an effective business plan that includes an industry analysis, marketing strategies, financial planning, and management techniques. In addition, learn about low-cost and no-cost community resources. An alarmingly high percentage of all business failures result from not having a business plan. Plan to make your new venture a success! Textbook included.

title: Winterize for Winning Roses
description: Before winter winds blow, gardeners in colder climates need to protect their roses to ensure lush blooms next year. Nancy Lindley, author of Roses for Michigan and owner of a popular Michigan rose store, will teach you how to get roses ready for Michigan's winters, including tips on spring and summer horticultural practices that increase winter hardiness. She'll also share photos of some new, hardy roses you may want to plant next spring. Learn her favoriate sources of rose information for winter garden reading and projects. You may buy an autographed copy of Roses for Michigan after class for $20.

title: Explore Mortgage Banking
description: Would you like to explore a new career opportunity? Would you like to acquire a basic understanding of the rapidly expanding mortgage banking industry and the exciting opportunities it offers? If so, join Brigitte Jaward-Hachem, President of American Center for Mortgage Banking, for this six-hour introduction to the lending field and its various activities. You will leave this seminar with an awarenes of the different job opportunities within the field, and an understanding of the history of mortgage lending, types of financing, loan origination process, sales essentials, completion of mortgage applications, credit reports and analysis, prequalification of a borrower, including credit report analysis and mortgage math.

title: Intermediate Hip Hop Dance II
description: This is the second of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of Hip Hop dance technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in Hip Hop dance history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and the principles of time, space, and energy. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of Hip Hop dance. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and/or writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of Hip Hop dance.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, awareness of the body in space, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Email for Seniors & Beginners
description: Finally - a class to help you really understand the features of email. Don't have an account? Anita will show you how to create a free gmail account. Learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, send photos, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. Discover how to protect yourself against spam and frauds. Prerequisite: Getting Started with Computers or equivalent experience.
prerequisite_text: Getting Started with Computers or equivalent experience.

title: Statistical Proc Ctrl in Manf
description: Covers the use of Statistical Process Control (SPC) in manufacturing to achieve optimum product quality at lowest cost. Development of statistical charts and their interpretation related to process improvement are integral parts of the course. Also covers formulas pertaining to various charts, gage R & R, Cp, and Cpk.

title: Tai Chi Mini Session
description: Tai Chi, sometimes referred to as "meditation in motion," is an ancient Chinese martial art consisting of a sequence of slow, graceful motions that relax and strengthen the mind, body and spirit. An excellent form of stress management suitable for people of all ages, Tai Chi has been shown to reduce blood pressure and strengthen bones. Join an experienced Tai Chi instructor to discover the benefits yourself. No experience needed. Wear loose, comfortable, modest clothing and shoes with no or low heels.

title: Anatomy and Physiology II
description: Lectures cover the principles and underlying concepts of special senses, the endocrine system, the circulatory system, the lymphatic system, immunity, the respiratory system, and the digestive system, metabolism and energetics, the urinary system, fluid, electrolyte and acid/base balance, and the reproductive systems. Lab reinforces understanding of the associated anatomic structures. BIO-233 and BIO-234 are a two-semester sequence designed for the student who plans to pursue a career in a health field. Three hours of lecture and two hours of lab per week.

title: Dsgn Proj Adobe Ill CS2
description: Practice while you learn the fundamentals of Adobe Illustrator CS2 with useful design projects. Master Illustrator's drawing tools, including the Pen tool. Learn how to use Illustrator's many image-editing features. Reinforce your Illustrator skills as you explore the elements of composition, color, and typography, along with secrets of good graphic design.

title: Glass Working Sampler Series
description: This FREE event showcase our glass work courses: neon, stained glass, hot glass, bead making, glass fusing, and glass ornament workshops. Teachers will be on hand to answer your questions and give short demonstrations. Come to 25331 Trowbridge in Dearborn to see what all the buzz is about! Registration is optional and everyone is welcome.

title: Special Topics Skilled Trades
description: TProvides specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics offered is identified by the Skilled Trades Apprenticeship Division faculty, STAD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the STAD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Course may be repeated for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Explore Watercolor
description: Free and flowing watercolor effects combined with a multitude of techniques will free the inner artist and journal enthusiast in you. Join artist Saundra Weed for this six-week class and learn how easy it is to create paintings and papers that can be combined with bookmaking, journal embellishment and creative self-expression. $5 due to instructor for first night's materials. Supply list will be provided in the first class. Appropriate for all levels.

title: Forever Fit
description: Take care of yourself, make new friends, and experience the benefits of fitness and activity by developing strength, endurance and flexibility in this 11-week course. Exercises are designed with older adults in mind and are adaptable to individual fitness levels.

title: Intermediate Knitting
description: This course will focus on skills for the knitter who wants to advance beyond a scarf or two needle hat project. You will learn more gauge, measurement and shaping. Learn the construction of a basic knitted sweater and the techniques commonly used in knitting of it. Become familiar with intarsia knitting, how to make cables, short rows and pick up stitches. Alternate methods of casting on and binding off will also be taught. Students will also be introduced to stitch patterns and techniques that will enliven the edges and borders of their projects. Supplies needed for class include worsted weight yarn, size 8 needles, a tape measure and stitch markers.

title: Introduction Public Speaking
description: This course explores how to develop public speaking skills through practice in preparing and delivering speeches for various audiences and situations.

title: Create a Website-Dreamweaver
description: This course will cover advanced Adobe Dreamweaver CS3 topics including cascading style sheets, forms, navigation bars, spry widgets, flash buttons and AP elements. Upon course completion, you will have the skills necessary to create a working website with mnay advanced features. There will be lots of hands-on practice at your own workstation. Prerequisite: Create a Great Website with Dreamweaver CS3 or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Create a Great Website with Dreamweaver CS3 or equivalent knowledge.

title: Operations & Supply Chain Mgmt
description: Explores the essential principles of Operations Management in Supply Chain Management (SCM). Emphasizes designing, planning, sourcing, processing, and distributing goods and services in SCM. Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able to demonstrate competency in the following SCM areas: operations and supply chain management, supply chain strategy and design, global supply chain procurement, demand forecasting methods, inventory management, scheduling and production design, lean systems, quality management, and project management.

title: Basic Life Support Healthcare
description: Designed for individuals working in healthcare or for health career and nursing students preparing for their clinical assignments. Course introduces the theory and skills of CPR for victims of all ages. Features ventilation with a barrier device, a bag-mask device, supplemental oxygen, use of automated external defibrillator (AED), and relief of foreign-body airway obstruction. Upon successful completion of the course, including both written and skills testing, the student receives an American Heart Association healthcare provider card (valid for two years).

title: College Dance Co IV
description: This is the fourth of four semesters of College Dance Company, providing opportunities to perform in a wide variety of genres and venues with different choreographers. Dance forms performed include, but are not limited to, classical, modern, jazz, tap, ballroom and Latin. In addition, HFCC Full Circle Dance Company students have an opportunity to attend dance festivals, master classes, professional dance company concerts, and to have their choreography presented. Two or more performances of a final concert are scheduled on campus as well as other performances, both on and off campus. This course is open to college students and high school juniors and seniors by audition and interview. New dance students may audition in one of several scheduled group auditions or may schedule an individual audition with the director. A strong dance background is required.

title: Industrial Appl of Basic Math
description: Utilizes shop problems to help students relate math to various job situations. Topics include addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of whole and mixed numbers and common and decimal fractions; percents, averages, and estimates; graphs, tables, and statistical measure; powers and roots; linear, angular, and circular measure; surface area, volume, and cubic measure; ratios and proportion; and metrics and metric conversion.

title: Introduction to Mediumship
description: Once again, Kristy Robinett offers a truly unique class experience. Introduction to Mediumship helps build the bridge between worlds. Learn the basics of developing your own mediumship skills. In addition to learning what mediumship is, explore the differences between a psychic and a medium, and different ways a spirit communicates with us. Topics covered include: mental vs. physical mediumship; the six intuitive senses; blocks/fears people have in communicating with a spirit; meeting your guides; giving messages; conducting a reading. Classes are limited to 24 people and fill very quickly.

title: Publish Your Own Cookbook
description: Do you want to publish a treasury of your family recipes or create a proven fund-raising tool for your organization? Then enroll in this class with Len Charla, author of Never Cooked Before/Gotta Cook Now. He will give you the inside scoop about several ways to get your cookbook designed and published, the best methods for testing and organizing your recipes, and a bonanza of marketing tips for successful sales. Learn how to copyright your cookbook and get it into stores. Ideal for club fundraisers and family reunion projects. After all, Mom's pie recipe may be your ticket to extra income!

title: ELL Reading & Listening 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college-preparatory reading and listening skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-102R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Intermediate Spanish II
description: Follows SPN-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level Spanish courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Spanish, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written Spanish. Presents Hispanic culture.

title: Blues Guitar
description: Come explore all aspects of "The Blues" as it relates to its performance on the guitar, and jam with renowned Detroit area musician Robert Penn. You will learn the importance of the guitar as well as theoretical principles that are characteristic of this particular genre. Examine various styles of different players as well as their approaches to improvising and techniques. The instructor will include the work of B.B. King, Jimi Hendricks, T-Bone Walker and artists of like importance. Another area of focus will be the pentatonic blues scales and its application to the I, IV, V Blues progression. Bring your acoustic or electric guitars and a music stand if you have one. Prerequisite: at least one year of basic guitar.
prerequisite_text: at least one year of basic guitar.

title: Condo Living
description: Do the benefits of condominium living entice you? From first-time homeowners to empty-nesters to retirees, condo ownership is becoming more and more popular. If you are considering purchasing a condo, learn from an individual who has served for 4 years as President of a large Detroit suburban complex tips on what to look for before closing the sale. If you are currently living in a condo or serve on an association board of directors, this is your opportunity to learn about management companies, legal issues, financial futures of your association, and more.

title: Theatre Experience I
description: Provides students with the opportunity to earn credit while actively participating in multiple areas of theatre production. This is the first of three experience-based one-credit courses where students work onstage, backstage, or in front-of-house operations for theatre productions during the semester. The course includes instructor presentation of specific elements of application though lecture, lecture/demonstration and/or laboratory experience. The production work for Theatre Experience I is suitable for a student's initial exposure to theatre production work. Theatre Experience II, III, IV, V and VI builds on the production work completed in Theatre Experience I. These courses will enable students to be engaged in up to six (6) public performances.

title: Weld Joint Design(code,tools)
description: Introduces welding codes and standards, identification of welding flaws, and the tools used to measure aspects of the weld. Emphasizes safety protocols and proper usage of hand tools in a welding lab.

title: Get Organized for Yr End & Hol
description: Are you wasting so much time looking for items that you give up and re-buy them? Are you overwhelmed by too many papers and often pay bills late because your system of paying them is not working? Join professional organizer Theresa Brune as she teaches pain-free techniques for organizing and parting with items. Theresa will also provide tips for getting organized for the holidays so you can enjoy them with less stress.

title: Ballet Fundamentals - Ages 4-7
description: Give your child an opportunity to develop grace, coordination and flexibility that will be a part of his or her life forever. In this class Stephanie Fullerton will teach the terminology, technique and precision associated with ballet.

title: The Bible as Literature
description: Focuses on reading, discussion, and written analysis of major literary selections from the Hebrew and Christian bibles. The Bible will be studied not as a religious document but as literary text and a source of ideas and styles reflected in various works of world literature.

title: Belly Dancing "basic Tech"
description: Get in shape as you experience the exotic, beautifully expressive art of belly dance--the Middle Eastern dance women have performed for ages. Under the direction of Cedena, one of Michigan's leading instructors, you'll learn the basic techniques of hip and arm work, turns and traveling, combinations, and more. Join this energetic, fun class and discover the many physical and mental benefits associated with the ancient movements of belly dance. Adults of all ages and body types, and youth 12 years and older, are welcome. No prior experience is needed.

title: Self Defense Kung Fu Workshop
description: Heads up! Learn to be aware of your surroundings and potential threats and use practical Kung Fu techniques to defend yourself. Explore and practice scenarios for precautions against assault. Develop a plan and be prepared to help keep yourself safe. Wear comfortable clothing.

title: ELL Communication 4
description: Develops low-intermediate-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-105C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Pen & Ink Drawing
description: Join artist Saundra Weed for this one-day workshop, and learn how to combine the strength and definition of pen and ink drawings with color. Using the simplest of tools, you will learn easy techniques for sketching and adding watercolor washes. These techniques are perfect for creating journals, sketch books, illustrations, note cards, scrapbooks and more. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Advanced Ballroom Boot Camp!
description: For those who have ballroom experience, have taken basic ballroom boot camp, or simply would like a challenge, this four-week intensive dance program is for you. We will dive into more advanced steps from East Coast Swing, Salsa, Tango, and Merengue. After participating, you should feel comfortable enough to test your moves at weddings, clubs, and dance parties. Singles or couples may join, though many feel more comfortable bringing a partner with them. See you on the dance floor!

title: Maps of Our Karmic Destiny
description: The ancients determined that there are various methods of divination or karmic road maps to understand our life lessons and destinies. Robert will discuss such maps as astrology, palmistry and various mystic oracles, with personal demonstrations for you.

title: Co-op in Science
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major in STEM to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Solid State Devices
description: Introduces the basic concepts of solid state devices and applications: semiconductor theory, the operational characteristics of devices such as the diode, bipolar junction transistors (BJT) and field effect transistors (FET), polarity, biasing, rectification and amplification. Introduces the basic DC power supply. Includes a discussion of camera-type vision systems, barcode readers and laser etchers as common Solid State devices found in industry. Pre-requisite: CIMEL 102 or Instructor Permission.

title: Lower Blood Pressure (nurses)
description: Do you wish you could lower blood pressure without expensive medications? Pharmacist Debbie Danto will discuss simple lifestyle changes and nutritional supplements to help you protect your heart and arteries and control blood pressure. She will also review the common blood pressure-lowering medications, their benefits, precautions and side effects. You will be given guidelines for safe blood pressure levels to help you understand your personal risk for heart attack, stroke and Alzheimer's disease. 2 nursing contact hours.

title: Dev Across Lifespan for PTAs
description: Examines the normal gross motor development of humans from infancy on. Relates early developmental sequence and reflexive maturation to rehabilitation techniques utilized with adult patients. Discusses the development of adults, the aging process, and death and dying. Psychosocial and cognitive issues throughout the life span are integrated in human development.

title: Belly Dancing - Personal Best
description: Reinforce your basic skills and learn new exotic movements in this intermediate-level course. Instructor Heidi Sapikowski will work with you to organize combinations into full-length performance routines. If you have prior dance experience (belly dance or other), you may prefer this class. All ages and body types welcome.

title: Intro to Sociology
description: Introduces the sociological approach to understanding culture and society. Examines human behavior in a group context, focusing on the prominent influence of culture and social structure on individuals. Connections between individuals and a variety of social institutions, such as the economy, government, and mass media, are examined and compared in U.S. society and on a global scale. Focuses on cultural diversity and social inequalities, such as those related to class, race, ethnicity and gender.

title: Carve a Duck Decoy
description: Discover the joys of woodcarving under the patient eye of Dave Oakley, who has won blue ribbons for his work. Use tools and patterns to carve a chickadee from basswood; then discover techniques for painting and finishing your bird. Dave will also talk the history of woodcarving, about the kinds of wood, carving shows and the history of this art. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: The Bark Covered House
description: Mary MacDonald, recently retired curator of the Dearborn Historical Museum and consummate story teller, will describe life in the early pioneer times when settlers came westward to settle the Dearborn area. She will reference the book The Bark Covered House Or Back In The Woods Again; being a graphic and thrilling description of real pioneer life in the wilderness of Michigan, written by William Nowlin, Esq., 1876. Learn about this land of trees where a father and son, walking up what is now Michigan Avenue, couldn't see daylight. View slides of rare old photos, a fascinating glimpse of life as it was for the early residents of Dearborn.

title: How Pub Best Sell
description: Do you have an idea, concept, or story that would make an excellent book? If so, spend three hours with Mr. Jerrold R. Jenkins, a guru of independent publishing, exploring the business aspects of publishing a best-selling book. Hear the pros and cons of traditional and non-traditional publishing methods, learn about the various markets, and discuss the steps involved in publishing a book. In short, acquire the knowledge and networking skills needed for survival in the competitive $25 billion book industry.

title: Co-op in CIS
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5

title: Woven Beaded Bracelet 2
description: Continue to develop your weaving techniques learned in Woven Beaded Bracelet 1. You will use traditional basketry techniques to weave non-traditional materials into a stunning woven bracelet incorporating handmade beads, waxed linen, perle cotton and metal charms. Project will be completed in class. $28 materials fee due to instructor.

title: Video Production
description: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and skills of film and video production, covering the entire production process from planning to editing. Emphasis is placed on key equipment and essential techniques, with an exploration of the unique aspects of different media formats. Students will complete individual and group projects, working both in the studio and on location to gain hands-on experience.

title: Trigonometry
description: For students in a technical or science program that requires knowledge of trigonometry. Topics include angles, right triangle trigonometry, circular functions and their graphs, trigonometric identities and equations, the Laws of Sines and Cosines, vectors, and a variety of physical applications. Course does not satisfy the MATH-175 or MATH-180 prerequisite. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for formal assessments and access to an online homework management system.

title: Cooking With Fresh Herbs
description: Executive Chef Peter Veach of the University of Michigan will show you what to do with all those abundant herbs from your garden. What and how to save and preserve those wonderful flavors of fresh herbs from your garden.

title: Instant Piano 1
description: You don't need years of weekly lessons to learn piano. In just three hours, learn enough to give yourself and your family years of musical enjoy- ment and entertainment. The traditional approach teaches note reading, while this workshop teaches all the chords needed to play any song, in any key, in any style. Then perfect your technique at home by practicing with an exclusive cassette provided by the instructor. Join us if you know your way around the keyboard a little bit and can read the treble clef. It's easy, fun and musically correct! $25 materials fee paid to instructor in class.

title: Resp Therapy Pharmacology
description: This course focuses on the appropriate and effective administration of respiratory care medications, including an overview of pharmacology, terminology, routes, techniques of administration, and calculation of dosages. The National Asthma Education and Prevention Program (NAEPP) Guidelines for Asthma Management are emphasized.

title: Beginning Folk Dance
description: Learn to dance the dances of your cultural heritage and that of others. Experience dances from Greece, Croatia, Philippines, Armenia, France, Bulgaria, Israel, Mexico, Serbia, Russia, Hungary, Japan, Turkey, USA and many more countries! Gain a better understanding of dance and music from people around the world, all while learning through a dance method that WILL make you successful. Folk Dancing requires no partners. Wear comfortable clothing and shoes. No sandals please.

title: Get Solutions for Reactive Gut
description: A "gut reaction" is more than a catchy phrase. Your gut, otherwise known as intestines, often absorbs negative emotions, unhealthy food, or a toxic environment. It may respond with gas, bad breath, heartburn, ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, allergies, stomachache, gluten intolerance, skin rashes, depression, ADD, or lowered resistance to illness. Let naturopath Mary Born teach you basic principles to restore health and activity to your gut and rid your body of toxins. Discover important scientific research often overlooked by mainstream medicine. Plan to leave with a "belly laugh" on your journey to better health.

title: Relieve Arthritis
description: If you have been diagnosed with arthritis, you not only aren't alone--you're in very good company. Arthritis is the most common joint disorder affecting millions of Americans. The good news, however, is that techniques are available to help you relieve and control your symptoms. Sandy Baumann, a biochemist, will present lifestyle and mental imagery techniques that can enhance relaxation, decrease stress, and ultimately reduce pain. In addition, she will share information about nutritional and herbal approaches to pain.

title: A Winter's Visit to Old Russia
description: Even without the snow and a troika, you can spend an evening 'traveling' to some of the historic sites of Mother Russia. A slide show, a display of Russian artifacts, and a Slavic dessert will make your experience a realistic one. The follow- ing Saturday you'll enjoy an authentic lunch served at a local Russian monastery. The cost of the meal is included.

title: Protect Yourself-Scams, Frauds
description: Celebrate Older Americans Month with a special presentation by Wayne County Sheriff's Deputy Ki Sobol about protecting yourself from crime. discover how criminals pick their targets for identity theft, home burglary, purse snatching, mugging and carjacking. Learn red flags that signal danger when you are walking, driving, or traveling, including special precautions for evening activities. Persons who are not seniors are welcome to attend. Refreshments are included. Sign up today to keep yourself and your loved ones safe in your home, at stores, in your vehicle, and in your neighborhood.

title: Fund. of Surgical Technology
description: The course introduces the fundamentals of operating room techniques, emphasizing principles and practices of asepsis and sterilization. Physical and psychosocial aspects of the patient are explored. Included are the roles and responsibilities of the surgical technologist and other surgical team members; identification of common equipment, instruments, and supplies; the perioperative phases and environment; students practice scrubbing, gowning, and gloving, and preparation of the sterile surgical fields.

title: Experience Pastels
description: Take a creative journey in the world of pastels. Pastels are pure powdered pigment that straddle the line between painting and drawing. This easy-to-use medium will allow you to create with rich colors and textures as you explore color, light effects, and composition. No experience necessary. Call us or visit the Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for a supply list.

title: Tie Flies for Trout & Salmon
description: Have fun tying your own fishing flies with David Oakley from Trout Unlimited. You will use a tying vise supplied by the instructor to tie dry flies, wet flies, streamers, nymphs and terrestrials. Learn to "Match the Hatch" for the correct fly ALL year long. Ideal for beginners. Optional: bring your own tying kit. $10 materials fee (hooks, feathers, thread, glue) due to instructor in class.

title: Basic Health Assessment
description: Focuses on theory and techniques involved in basic patient assessment using vital signs. Addresses the areas of medical asepsis, standard precautions, proper body mechanics, and patient transportation. Laboratory activities.

title: Introduction to PLCs
description: Introduces various elements of basic PLCs including the identification of programmable logic control systems as well as overview of PLC system architectures. Provides instruction in basic numbering systems, computer terminology, PLC functions, program structures, and point addressing basics. Pre-requisites: CIMEL 107 or Instructor Permission.

title: Intro Creative Writ & Poetry
description: If you are eager to express yourself through prose or poetry, learn from Cheryl Vatcher-Martin, published poet and writer, a variety of writing techniques that will enhance your creativity. You'll create characters, scenes, poems and more. Also learn about markets and contests for your work. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Special Topics Skilled Trades
description: Provides specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics offered is identified by the Skilled Trades Apprenticeship Division faculty, STAD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the STAD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Course may be repeated for credit when different topics are offered.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 4
description: Develops low-intermediate-level college-preparatory reading and listening skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELI-105R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Explore Hamtramck
description: Tour of Hamtramck for Henry Ford Village residents only.

title: Vocal Power
description: Sing, Sing, Sing!! Perform and record group and solo vocals in all styles, including Classical, Pop, and Jazz. Rehearsals, studio recording sessions, and live public performance with Kevin Dewey. This will be a fun, energetic, and rewarding experience! A special arrangement of a pop hit will be crafted specially for this group. Each participant will receive CD of their performance.

title: Using Ancestry.Com
description: Learn effective ways to search the main databases, as well as thousands of other ones, on the popular website Ancestry.com. Genealogist MichaelNeill also will teach you to view and print census records, passenger lists and newspaper images. Each student will have a computer and use of Ancestry.com during the workshop.

title: Health PE for Elem Teachers
description: This course provides the theoretical framework of elementary and middle school health, physical activity, and safety curriculum. Students will gain knowledge and skills for the elementary classroom teacher on methods used to integrate health, nutrition, safety, and physical education into their daily curriculum. The course includes teaching strategies, projects, movement exercises, and games designed to engage the Pre-Elementary Education major. Developmental sequences and the health needs of children from elementary through middle school is included. Course emphasis is on providing learning experiences for children's development of lifelong positive health and fitness behaviors. Particular attention is given to the national health standards as well as the Michigan Model Modules.

title: Intro to Sports Management
description: This course provides a comprehensive overview of the field of sport management, exploring its key concepts, principles, and applications. Students will gain a solid understanding of the historical development of sport management, the various segments within the industry (youth, community, scholastic, professional, and international sports), trends, and the essential managerial and leadership skills required for success in this dynamic field.

title: Ocular Measurements I
description: Discusses basic ophthalmic equipment and measurement protocol, instrument calibration, maintenance, and infection control. Students apply concepts to specific clinical situations related to vision care.

title: Make Money With eBay
description: Step up a notch in the world of eBay and learn the secrets of the pros. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer who has made hundreds of transactions himself, will teach you about the ins and outs of using design and bid services, setting up an "eBay store," using pictures effectively in ads, shipping and payment options and even avoiding the pitfalls.

title: Dining Room Service & Operati
description: Applies basic principles of table service in the production dining room. Emphasizes effective serving procedures and techniques, including cordial and prompt attention to customers, proper dress and grooming practices, and in-depth knowledge of menu items.

title: Human Potential Seminar
description: Examines how to identify and apply positive life skills in order for each individual to discover and any use their strengths, talents and abilities. Covers empathy, peak experiences, internal and external motivational factors, goal setting and values clarification.

title: Continuing Italian Language
description: Come experience a fun approach to speaking Italian and improving fluency. Beginning on day one, at least 90% of the class will be conducted in Italian, as you are encouraged to think in Italian, like an Italian. Gone are the extensive grammar and conjugation drills, replaced with activities such as inventing and acting out skits, drafting emails, watching Italian videos without subtitles, and planning a trip. Also learn to use many of the great resources available online, such as flashcards with an audio component. All skill levels welcome; if you know a little, you'll learn a lot.

title: MS Word: Tips Beyond Basics
description: Class will focus on how to use many less-known time saving feasures in Microsoft Word. Learn to use format styles, drop caps and auto text. Give new meaning to words and phrases by using the thesaurus, synonym and translation features. Find out how to use auto correct and password protect your document. Learn to convert text to tables, tables to text and so much more!

title: Weave an Amulet Bag
description: Just in time for Easter, design and weave your own amulet bag using the brick stitch (off loom). In Session 1, you'll chart your own neckpiece design, select your colors, and start weaving the body of the bag. Session 2, you'll attach the neckpiece and design the fringe components. In Session 3, you'll close the bottom of the bag and begin attaching fringe. Between sessions you'll need time to work on your bag. $15 supply fee payable to instructor.

title: Make Your Own Earring Wires
description: Learn to make three different earring wires using basic wire working techniques. Make them long or short, it's up to you... Techniques learned will include: cleaning and cutting wire; hammering wire; shaping and forming coils; making a bead connector; and artifically aging metal. Project will be completed in class. $28 materials fee due to instructor.

title: Teaching Students With Autism
description: Just 20 years ago, we didn't see students with autism in our classrooms. But today, we teach children with high-functioning autism and Asperger's Syndrome right alongside their neurotypical peers. Reaching and teaching these students requires a delicate balancing act: understanding how their brains are wired, helping them turn challenges into opportunities, and learning to enjoy the rich perspective they bring to the classroom.

title: Drains, Wastes, and Vents
description: Introduces the Michigan State Plumbing Code and the proper selection of materials for the installation and repair of sewer, soil, waste, and vents systems. Covers proper procedures for the design, sizing, and construction of residential plumbing systems. Introduces commercial systems and plumbing cross-connections. The use of blueprints and isometric diagrams are reviewed throughout the course. Also includes how to test plumbing systems in their various stages.

title: Heart Health
description: Here's a class for those with high cholesterol, heart conditions, high blood pressure or those seeking understanding and application of nutrition advice for a heart-healthy lifestyle. Dietician Lourdes Lore will help you find out about the diet that can help lower your blood pressure that does double duty to minimize PMS symptoms too.

title: Re-Intro & Integration:new Mfg
description: This class is designed to accomplish a number of goals and objectives to maximize a positive attitude in creating teamwork for implementing a new LEAN operation that will increase productivity and quality through employee participation and Input.

title: Conversation Spanish Intermed
description: If you have completed Conversational Spanish 1 or have a basic knowledge of the language, continue developing your skills in this fun and informative intermediate-level class. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $30).

title: Mystery of Nowlin Powder Horn
description: Mary MacDonald, Dearborn Historian, will share the story of a powder horn that belonged to the Nowlin family and was mentioned in the book The Bark Covered House. It was a hand carved horn that mysteriously disappeared from this area in 1955 and reappeared years later in an auction house in new York.

title: Introductory Psychology
description: Introduces elementary concepts and principles related to the scientific study of behavior and of the mental processes of cognition and affective states. Variables examined include the history of psychology, the scientific method, theory, biological foundations, psychological processes related to cognition and affective states, developmental changes over time, and applications related to healthy and unhealthy personalities.

title: Contemporary Guitar 2
description: Continue developing your guitar-playing skills as you learn more advanced techniques. Bring your own guitar, and strum your favorite pop, rock, and folk music under the instruction of an experienced guitarist.

title: Industrial Welding Advanced
description: In this three-week course, participants from Southeast Michigan Community Alliance (SEMCA) will learn how to set up a welding machine and use the gas metal arc welding (GMAW-MIG) process to permanently join parts. This class includes lectures, book instruction, and hands on welding practice with real world applications. Practice will include how to weld root, hot, fill, and cover passes; recognize welding discontinuities; and troubleshoot basic equipment issues. Safe work practices will be reinforced at all times. The decision to pursue welding certification is to be determined. Participants will receive personal protective equipment: safety glasses and shields, welding helmet, leather gloves, and flame retardant jacket.

title: Prod Mgt & Tech Prod for Theat
description: For intermediate students to learn by practicing personnel management, research and document preparation, house mangagement, and load in and technical rehearsal preparation.

title: Lebanese Cooking V
description: Arayes Lebanese kabab. Crush lintel soup. Lebanese cabbage salad.

title: Word Processing
description: Presents the essential features of Microsoft Word and word processing in creating and editing documents. Examines how to enhance and customize documents by applying formatting; creating headers and footers; working with columns; inserting page and section breaks; applying styles and themes; inserting hyperlinks; adding graphical elements; and creating footnotes, endnotes, citations, and bibliographies. Examines how to create tables and charts, merge documents, work with shared documents, and prepare and protect documents.

title: Composting Basics
description: Put off by composting because it sounds too bothersome or complicated? Fall is the perfect time to begin a composting project, so join us and learn how easy and rewarding it can be to recycle ordinary household and garden waste into "black gold." Learn from Master Composters, Allan and Jane Geisler, about constructing a compost pile, engaging in redworm composting, and using the finished compost in your garden.

title: C Programming
description: An intermediate-level course which familiarizes the student with the C programming language. Covers designing, coding, testing, and debugging programs. Emphasizes program and coding efficiency.

title: Energy Industry Fundamentals
description: Covers basic emerging principles and concepts that affect the energy industry, compliance with safety and health procedures, how electric power and natural gas generation, transmission, and distribution work, and entry-level energy careers. Students will gain an understanding of the energy industry as a prerequisite to occupation-specific knowledge and an understanding of the careers available in the energy industry.

title: Music Technology
description: This course covers computer literacy as relevant to the contemporary musician. In addition to computer basics like file management, word processing, and spreadsheets, students will be introduced to music notation software and digital recording techniques. A focus of the class is navigating though the complex menus of music software, understanding methods of collaboration, exploring current software options, and the creation of professional looking sheet music and professional sounding recordings. This course fulfills general education and institutional outcomes for computer technology. Students are required to read music notation for this class.

title: Save $5000/Yr & Get Out Debt
description: Having trouble getting ahead? This class will help! Instructor Patrick Shearer will teach you a series of simple steps to save up to $5,000 per year. Then he will teach you a technique to use that extra money to get out of debt without refinancing or getting a second job. Participants are asked to bring a calculator, a list of your current monthly expenses and a summary of your current debts and minimum payments (which will not be shared in class). Patrick will walk you through a worksheet of a debt reduction plan.

title: Authentic Mexican Enchiladas
description: Learn how to prepare the basic recipe to make authentic Mexican enchiladas with Alicia Bonilla-Arias. Make the rolled traditional Mexican type, the Sonora Style from Arizona, and the enchilada that is the flat pancake type. Alicia will introduce you to three different kinds of pepper that she puts in the sauce - mild, sweet and hot incorporated into one of her old family recipes. You will learn what to do with fresh and dry pepper and how to properly roll the enchiladas. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Camp Entrepreneurship
description: Curious about learning how to save, invest, build wealth, or start a business? Camp G.R.O.W. teaches students how to gain resources, and obtain wealth in a fun, exciting, and informative manner. Campers are immersed in the essentials of financial literacy, networking, goal setting, business planning and entrepreneurship. Using skill building games, visual aids and hands on activities, students will learn how to make, manage, and grow their money wisely. Participants will get experience making financial decisions they will face as adults, and finish the course with stronger business, financial leteracy, and communication skills.

title: Artisanal Cheese & Craft Beer
description: Covers introductory techniques used to bring cheese and craft beer together in a cohesive pairing. Examines the production, flavors, and origin of eight styles of cheese and four styles of beer.

title: RACH Light Commercial Systems
description: An advanced course continuing the study of light commercial heating, air conditioning/cooling systems. The focus is on the lash up, installation, and troubleshooting of all associated controls and control packages. Through lectures and laboratory experiences, students gain knowledge of the functions, operations and components in relation to pneumatic, electronic and DDC (Direct Digital Control), building control systems. Comparable to ENT 219.

title: Bridge to Healthcare Careers
description: The bridge courses will provided college readiness and preparedness while also exploring various healthcare programs offered at Henry Ford College.

title: HFV- Internet for Beginners
description: Around the world in 80 seconds! The internet helps you communicate instantly with family and friends, find travel bargains, shop for the best deals, and research any topic, including health questions. In four 2-hour sessions you will learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, surf the internet to find information, bookmark favorite sites, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. You will have time to practice on the internet in class, so bring your questions. THIS CLASS IS RESTRICTED TO HENRY FORD VILLAGE RESIDENTS AND IS BILLED BY SECTION.

title: Taste of Belly Dancing
description: Experience the art of belly dancing in this two-session sampler class. Under the instruction of Cedena, one of Michigan's leading instructors, you'll learn basic slow moves the first session and quick moves the second session. Who knows, you may decide you've found a new fun way to stay in shape!

title: Discover Hot New Wines
description: If you liked Bruce Bell's sell-out classes on ABC's of Wines, you'll enjoy his take on the new HOT wines for 2005. Amateurs and experts alike will enjoy sampling new domestic and international reds and whites to see how they compare by color, aroma and flavor to older varieties. The evening promises to be fun and educational. Participants must be at least 21 years old.

title: Creating Lasting Floral Design
description: We all love fresh flowers but they don't last long. Create a lovely expression of spring and summer in a floral basket centerpiece with beautifully crafted artificial flowers that will last season after season. Designer Barbara Simms will guide you through the process as you use your imagination and creativity to express the magic of spring and summer flowers in nature. You will need an apron or short sleeve shirt, glue gun, glue sticks and wire cutters. Flowers and basket are included in the price of the class. Deadline for registration will be Feb. 18 to allow time for ordering the flowers.

title: EV Battery Technician Cert
description: Participants in this certificate course will acquire the necessary skills for designing, operating, testing, maintaining, and replacing battery cells and packs for various applications. In addition, the certificate allows participants to choose the right technology for the right challenge and evaluate battery performance. Moreover, to be able to work out a solid total solution, the battery management systems, the charging and maintenance of batteries, and their recycling process are also discussed in more detail. Finally, we give sufficient attention to safely handling these electrical applications, ensuring the well-being of both individuals and the environment.This certification empowers learners to: . Evaluate what battery technology is best suited for which application based on its characteristics. . Examine the advantages and disadvantages of the most commonly used battery technologies. . Design and implement the best-fit battery solution for each application. . Safely handle, test, repair, and dispose of battery systems according to legal obligations.

title: Adult Tap Dance 1 (4 Week)
description: If you want to continue your tap dance routine through the semester break, this short course is for you. Continue to work on the basic skills learned at the start of the semester. Bring tap shoes. A four-week class.

title: Still Life: Focus on White
description: Learn to control color mixing in the white family.

title: DNA Camp for Girls
description: Fun hands-on activities and real lab work highlighting the structure of the cell and the structure and function of DNA. We will talk about the role of DNA in genetics, health and disease, biotechnology, and forensics. Lab activities will include extracting DNA from plant cells and analyzing DNA using forensic science techniques. We may even solve a mystery in the process! Women in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) fields will join the girls for informal discussions of career paths in these various areas.

title: Plumbing, Pipefitting Material
description: Introduces plumbing and pipe-fitting materials joining. Topics included are mechanical joints/pipe-cutting, brazing with turbo torches and oxyacetylene, soldering with LP and MAPP gases, plastic PVC pipe-joining, flared fittings, plastic fusion welding, and dielectric fittings. Students are required to study the theory behind these methods and perform hands-on labs.

title: Technical Mathematics
description: For students in technology programs who have had MATH 100 or one year of Beginning Algebra. Covers functions and graphs, properties of lines, the trigonometric functions, systems of linear equations, rational expressions, quadratic equations, solution of right triangles, vectors, integer exponents, linear regression, complex numbers, and an introduction to the non-graphing scientific calculator. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments.

title: Intro to Health and Wellness
description: Assesses current level of wellness and provides the tools necessary to improve and motivate the student to practice healthier lifestyles throughout adulthood. Covers environmental, reproductive, and psychological health; stress management; infectious disease; nutrition; chronic disease; aging; and physical fitness.

title: Construction Blueprint Reading
description: Explores mechanical (HVAC), architectural, and electrical drawings required for energy technology, plumbing and pipefitting, electrical, and construction related trades. Covers print reading, interpreting common symbols, working notes, views, and title blocks. Introduces project management and estimating. Note: Basic measurement and math skills required for print reading section.

title: Cardio Kickboxing
description: a fast, fun and energizing way to get or stay in shape while decreasing stress and fat! Using a combination of kickboxing techniques put to music, muscle toning and abdominal conditioning, you will burn calories and create a lean, sculpted appearance. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Return to Your Source
description: Live with clarity and passion. A spiritual journey - an inward voyage of discovery designed to bring balance to your outward behaviors and inner consciousness - can help you achieve this. Your journey is as unique as you are, and whether you are taking your first steps or you find yourself wandering along the way, here is a down-to-earth, non-denominational approach to guide you to your Source. Come learn some time-honored disciplines that can help you repel negativity, attract positive energy, and embrace a richer life experience. Instructor's book included.

title: Jazz for Kids
description: Swing, salsa, western, and lyrical are just a few of the dances your child will learn in this high-energy class. Under the direction of Stephanie Fullerton, kids will learn proper dance techniques while exercising and gaining flexibility.

title: Easy Delicious German Meal
description: If you'd like a hearty winter meal, you'll love "yummie, yummie" German cuisine. Join Ursula Markovits, who was born and raised in Germany, for a cultural delight. As she prepares a marinated beef roast, bread dumplings and chocolate nut bars, she'll share her first-hand knowledge of life in Germany. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Adv. Clinical Therapeutic Prac
description: Directed clinical experience that further develops the clinical skills and techniques used to manage patients requiring mechanical ventilator support in the hospital, sub-acute and home care settings. Twenty-four hours per week.

title: Simply Chakras & Auras
description: Join a discussion of chakras and auras. Kathy will provide exercises for participants to develop your own skills and enable you to discern auras and develop sensitivity to your own chakra system. Bring your imagination! This will definitely be entertaining and enlightening!

title: Inexpensive, Easy & Quick Meal
description: Time-saving meals that are easy on the budget? Learn how to stretch your food dollars and prepare quick and simple flavorful meals with Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estates.

title: Belly Dancing: Intermed & Adv
description: Even the most experienced students will be challenged in this accelerated-rate class as they reinvent the dance through artistic freedom. This is your opportunity to learn advanced techniques (including the cane), choreography and costume design. You may audition for dance roles in Cedena's annual show. Instructor permission required. For more information, e-mail the instructor at www.cedena-heidi@sbcglobal.net.

title: Pneumatic Power & Control
description: Explores the principles of fluid power pneumatics as applied to industrial systems. Examines various pneumatic components with respect to their functions within pneumatic power and control systems.

title: Stagecraft
description: Offers a basic survey of the major techniques and theories of scenography used in the modern theatre.

title: Access: Level 2
description: Take your database skills to the next level in this intermediate class. Learn how to enhance your database designs by using the principles of table relationships and referential integrity, integrate data with other applications, modify a table design, find data with filters and queries, customize form and report, and create data access pages. Access Level 1 or equivalent skills recommended.

title: Designer Stuffed Cupcakes
description: These simply-to-die-for cupcakes sell fo $4-$7 each in speciality stores! Make and take home an assortment of 12 unbelievably delicious cupcake creations stuffed with a variety of sweet fillings. You'll learn how to professionally decorate them too with rich buttercream frosting, and lots of yummy toppings! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $14-18).

title: Jazz Improvisation 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of jazz improvisation in which skills are developed through the study of jazz theory and the performances of a variety of jazz styles in a small group, jazz combo setting.

title: New L3c Legal Bus Structure
description: The L3C is a flexible LLC designed to further a socially-beneficial purpose while operating in a for-profit business structure. This provides an opportunity to invest in a typical "non-profit" venture while creating low-profit income. How could your company benefit?

title: All Skills Youth Baseball Camp
description: This camp is designed to sharpen baseball skills for Little League players at the elementary and middle-school levels. The camp will focus on throwing, hitting, pitching, base running and defensive (infield, outfield, and catching) drills and instructions. The camp is hosted directly by Coach Tim Leighton and Henry Ford College Baseball team, led by All-Conference Team players, will serve as camp assistants. Players will have fun while improving their skills playing the sport they love!

title: Investing for Income
description: Whether you are just starting your investing career or have already amassed a tidy nest egg, at some point you'll probably want some investments to generate income. For younger people, that income balances out the periodic dips in a stock-dominated asset mix; for retirees, it provides money to live on. Join Rick Bloom for an up-to-date analysis of the numerous investments that can provide that income. Rick was selected as one of the top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine and has written a daily column on financial advice for a Detroit newspaper. He currently hosts a daily radio show on a local station.

title: Basic Hydraulics
