description: Introduces basic hydraulic principles, laws, components, and symbols. Topics include: safety; the use of tools; and procedures in the design, building, testing, troubleshooting, and repair of industrial hydraulic systems. Includes demonstrations and extensive laboratory activities.

title: Improvisation for the Actor
description: Introduces the principles and practice of improvisational techniques as applied to performance.

title: DNA Camp for Boys
description: Fun hands-on activities and real lab work highlighting the structure of the cell and the structure and function of DNA. We will talk about the role of DNA in genetics, health and disease, biotechnology, and forensics. Lab activities will include extracting DNA from plant cells and analyzing DNA using forensic science techniques. We may even solve a mystery in the process! Women in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) fields will join the boys for informal discussions of career paths in these various areas.

title: Purchasing & Supply Chain Mgmt
description: Introduces the critical function in Supply Chain Management (SCM) known as purchasing. Covers purchasing decisions, strategies, procedures, supplier selections, and negotiations. Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able to demonstrate competency in the following areas of SCM: purchasing strategies, legal aspects of purchasing, material management, just-in-time (lean) purchasing, procurement, strategic outsourcing, global sourcing, total quality management, cost and price determination, and bargaining and negotiations.

title: Perennials: Perpetual Joy!
description: Plant perennials once and enjoy their beauty year after year! Discover how these plants can add excitement to any garden, offering dramatic and interesting ways to landscape your outdoor living room. Learn from a Master Gardener how to pick the right sun and shade perennials, amend and prepare the soil, and fertilize, divide, and prune perennial gardens. You'll also see how easy it is to grow them in containers for years of successful blooms.

title: Create Projects - MS Word
description: Learn to create a flyer, invitation, letter and calendar using this popular program. Design your own resume and find out how easy it is to make a table. Create other projects as time permits. Learn many features of Microsoft Word as you build different projects.

title: A Glimpse of Hungary
description: Hungary is known for its exotic, tasty and spicy food. Few people know more about preparing it than Elizabeth Krajcz, who was born in Hungary and has cooked at the Hungarian American Culture Center for 20 years. She'll teach you how to make a true Hungarian meal of beef goulash, cucumber salad, chicken paprikash and palacsinta (crepes filled with apricots). Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Theatre History
description: Offers a survey of theatrical production, including acting, directing, theatre architecture, artistic movements, and significant ideas that affect the development of theatre from the Greeks through the present. Emphasizes individual research and projects.

title: Programmable Controllers
description: Begins with a thorough discussion of the Allen Bradley Contrologix/Compactlogix programmable controller system characteristics, followed by a detailed presentation on how to use the programmable controller to solve the automated control problem.

title: SQL for Database Development
description: An intermediate-level course familiarizing the student with the SQL language used to retrieve and modify tables and data within a SQL Server database management system. Covers outer joins, summary queries, and subqueries. Also discusses using normalization techniques to design and create database structures, views, scripts, stored procedures, scalar functions, and triggers.

title: Theatre Experience I
description: Provides students with the opportunity to earn credit while actively participating in multiple areas of theatre production. This is the first of three experience-based one-credit courses where students work onstage, backstage, or in front-of-house operations for theatre productions during the semester. The course includes instructor presentation of specific elements of application though lecture, lecture/demonstration and/or laboratory experience. The production work for Theatre Experience I is suitable for a student's initial exposure to theatre production work. Theatre Experience II, III, IV, V and VI builds on the production work completed in Theatre Experience I. These courses will enable students to be engaged in up to six (6) public performances.

title: Illustration
description: Emphasizes digital drawing and painting techniques used by illustrators to create commercial illustrations for print and digital media. Presents effective techniques when using Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Intro to Energy Technology
description: Introduces traditional sources of energy and provides an overview of the various forms of energy and their applications. Covers alternative, renewable, and traditional non-renewable sources of energy with consideration for energy conservation and use and energy transmission systems.

title: Recording Studio Engineering 2
description: Emphasizes advanced digital recording techniques and independent problem solving in the recording process. Covers recording sessions, computer files, microphone usage and placement, digitizing principals, audio acquisition options, midi integration, and session management. Students work in an actual digital recording studio recording a wide variety of instrumental and vocal ensembles.

title: Radiographic Procedures IV
description: This lecture/laboratory course is the fourth in the four-course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This course introduces fundamental radiology positioning terminology and provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for the spinal column. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Quick Meals & Sides
description: Seem to be in a hurry all the time? Tired of eating the same ol' thing? Join Chef Marilyn Suter for a how-to session discovering tasty choices you can fix in a hurry. How about five-minute Zucchini and Cheese Soup, Squash Soup, Pesto Salmon, Italian Tortellini and more? Surprise the family with something different without spending hours in the kitchen.

title: Intermediate Modern Dance III
description: This is the third of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of modern dance technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in modern dance history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and the principles of time, space, and energy. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of modern dance. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of modern dance.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, awareness of the body in space, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Heal Digestive Disorder-Nurses
description: Does eating cause you pain? Have you been diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome, ulcers, heartburn, bloating, or esophageal burning? Learn how others have stopped pain and received relief during this workshop by popular naturopath and herbalist Mary Born. Discover the basis for digestive problems and learn methods to encourage good digestion and the importance of enzymes. Handouts will discuss specific nutrients matched to symptoms and foods that speed healing of the GI tract. 2.5 nursing contact hours.

title: More Camcorder Memories
description: Take your camcorder projects to a new level in this continuation class. Learn how to edit projects to create stories, videos and commercials using such editing software as Adobe Premiere, AVID, Final Cut and others. Prerequisite: Camcorder Memories or equivalent knowledge required.
prerequisite_text: Camcorder Memories or equivalent knowledge required.

title: Is Memory Loss Alzheimer's?
description: When you have a "senior moment," do you worry about Alzheimer's disease (AD)? Let Treena Horton of the Alzheimer's Association calm your fears. She will describe ways to lower your risk for this brain disease, 10 early warning signs of Alzheimer's, the difference between dementia and AD, genetic links for AD, as well as AD stages and common behaviors. Learn about physician evaluations for AD, treatment options, clinical trials, and community resources for family caregivers. Get valuable take-home fact sheets. Sign up today.

title: Buying a Computer for Those
description: Have you been thinking about buying your first computer? Well, in this relaxed environment, come learn the difference between a notebook and desktop computer. Get acquainted with terms like hard drive,memory, operating system, and others. Instructor will explain (in beginner's terms) how to be informed when faced with difficult issues of computer buying.

title: Starting My Own Business
description: Would you like to turn your hobby or talent into a source of income? Have you always wanted to be your own boss? Use the tools of this seminar to help you determine if you have the temperment and skill-sets needed to be in a small business. Learn to evaluate your business ideas and set realistic goals for success. Workbook included.

title: Sign Language 2
description: The key to becoming proficient in a new language is practice, practice and more practice. During this 10-week session, you will develop greater confidence in your signing ability as you continue communicating with a member of the deaf community.

title: Hollywood Screenplay Structure
description: Students will learn the "three act" screenplay structure and all the plot points contained within each act. The goal is to teach students how to arrange the plot points to create rising drama and tension as the story progresses. Class is ideal for beginning students who want to learn how to organize and construct a screenplay. It is also recommended for advanced students who want to ensure that their screenplays have a cohesive functional structure. The class will be conducted as a workshop. All student work will be read, discussed and evaluated by the other classmates. Student attendance, participations and completion of assignments are required.

title: Weld Metallurgy-Ferrous Metal
description: Covers the mechanical and physical properties and the crystalline structures of ferrous metals. Also explores heat treating of ferrous metals.

title: Mount Vernon
description: Celebrate George Washington's birthday, February 18, with Mary MacDonald, Dearborn Historian and consummate story teller, as she takes you back into an era that is remembered for its history. Mary will share what she knows about Mount Vernon.

title: Intermediate Arabic II
description: Follows ARA-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level Modern Standard Arabic courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Modern Standard Arabic, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written Arabic. Presents Arabic culture.

title: Explore You!
description: I am not discouraged because every wrong attempt discarded is another step forward - Thomas Edison. Positive thinking and self-confidence can effect your success in life as a parent, partner or employee. Discover the best you in this highly charged seminar that opens doors to your strengths, talents and abilities.

title: Auto Engine Dynamometer
description: An advanced-level course in the purpose, construction, and operation of the automotive engine dynamometer. Presents how to conduct engine tests of horsepower and torque, and covers test cell setup of various sensor types and adaptation.

title: Nutrition for the Professional
description: Introduces health care professionals to the basics of nutrition and its application to disease, growth, and development.

title: Paint W Watercolor (ages 9-11)
description: Let water make the colors flow as you unleash your creativity and paint works of art that only you can do. Make a splash and go under the sea - if that's where your imagination takes you. You will also have the opportunity to try your hand at landscapes, still lifes and a variety of abstract ideas. Art supplies are included. Wear clothes that you don't mind getting messy.

title: N American Tech Excellence Cer
description: Explores various types of basic work knowledge of the Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning and Refrigeration (HVACR) systems that are installed in the HVACR field. Topics covered include safety, trade tools, heat and matter, electrical fundamentals, transformers and motors, electrical diagrams, and proper use of required test instruments. As a result of this class, students will learn about the structure and HVACR communication skills expected of them in the technology and skilled trade industry. This course helps to prepare students to take the NATE Core Certification Exam based on ACCA, NATE, and RSES information.

title: Bpi Bldg Analyst
description: This training will prepare participants to take the Building Performance Institute Building Analyst Professional certification field and written exam. This is a nationally recognized Home Performance certification.

title: Underground Distribution
description: Designed as an introduction to operation and maintenance of underground electrical distribution systems. Electrical theory application for various distribution cables and underground cabinet terminations, cable splicing techniques and troubleshooting of underground circuits are explored. Distribution transformer applications are discussed. Students will be able to identify and demonstrate personal and electrical safety procedures utilized in switching, isolating, testing and diagnostic applications.

title: HFV-Introduction to Word
description: Wouldn't it be wonderful to compose letters, your life story, poetry, recipe files, a family newsletter, or even articles for your club right on the computer? In this class of four 2-hour sessions, you will discover how to use Microsoft Word features to write a document, check spelling and grammar, move and copy text, and edit mistakes - all before you print a final copy. Practice with other tools that help you jazz up your writing with bod and italic headlines, centered or indented text, special borders and more. Sign up now and amaze others with your new talents. THIS CASS IS RESTRICTED TO HENRY FORD VILLAGE RESIDENTS AND IS BILLED BY SECTION.

title: Mouth-Watering Mexican Suppers
description: Under the expert guidance of Chef Tim Dare, discover how easy it is to put together arroz con pollo (chicken and rice), beef enchiladas, quesadillas, and taco salad. Get his secret recipe for Mexican shrimp that will tantalize your taste buds. Match spices to your choice of mild, moderate or HOT! Then sample what you cook. Bring an apron and your favorite hot sauce. Get recipes you can make in 30 minutes!

title: Have a Dream Wedding
description: How can you afford the wedding of your dreams? Join Deborah Zahn-Simmons, a wedding planner with 17 years experience, for an eye-opening Saturday class to get the answers. Develop winning strategies to organize your wedding and stretch your budget from the engagement to the honeymoon. Get insider information about where to shop and how to negotiate the best prices for your reception, flowers, invitations, cake, photography, gowns and wedding party attire. Fiances of all ages (and parents) are welcome to enroll. Bring a notebook and a brown bag lunch so you can swap ideas with other attendees.

title: Adv Video Game Design & Dev
description: In the Advanced Video Game Design and Development online program students will move on to learn more advanced 3D graphics programming techniques. Students will explore mesh optimizations and hierarchical animation techniques, the use of level of detail (LOD) algorithms to improve application performance by reducing polygon counts in distant objects, skeletal animation systems for realistic character animation and more.

title: Adult Hip Hop
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? This high-energy, introductory class features the best of both! Work your body and soul to the hottest music craze this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Introductory Psychology
description: Introduces elementary concepts and principles related to the scientific study of behavior and of the mental processes of cognition and affective states. Variables examined include the history of psychology, the scientific method, theory, biological foundations, psychological processes related to cognition and affective states, developmental changes over time, and applications related to healthy and unhealthy personalities.

title: Beginning Tap
description: Introduces the basic rhythms, steps, and simple combinations of tap, a dance form derived from a combination of syncopated African rhythms, Irish clogging and folk dance. First class meeting discusses proper footwear.

title: Real Estate for Begin Investor
description: Buy Low-Sell High. 70% of the richest people in the world made their money in real estate. So where do you begin? This class will allow you to understand why this is the "best market" to invest in real estate and how to get "deals on foreclosures" and the advantages of being a landlord in today's market. $30 informational packet available from instructor.

title: Epoxy Resin Explorer
description: Unleash your creativity and problem-solving skills in this hands-on epoxy resin camp, where creativity and science meet! Over four fun-filled days, you will discover how to safely work with epoxy, explore its many uses in art and industry, and create one-of-a-kind, personalized projects. Whether you're an aspiring artist, designer, or maker, this hands-on camp is your chance to bring your out-of-the-box ideas to life while learning about the endless possibilities of epoxy.Session Highlights: - Safety First: Master the essentials of working with epoxy resin safely and confidently. -Epoxy Basics: Understand how to mix, pour, and cure epoxy safely while exploring its artistic possibilities. - Advanced Techniques: Experiment with vibrant pigments, metallic, flakes, and embedding objects to craft unique designs -Industry Insight: Explore how epoxy is used in trades from small to large scale, like interior design, flooring, manufacturing, woodworking, and other leading professions.Participants will take home all their creations, along with the skills to continue crafting with epoxy resin on their own. Don't miss this chance to explore the endless possibilities of resin art while making lasting memories and new friends!

title: Rental Real Est & Prop Managmt
description: Before you buy either residential or commercial rental property get the latest facts about actual profits and potential pitfalls in this illuminating class with successful property manager and investor Peter Pace. You will learn how to find and finance appropriate properties, set a market-driven rent, attract and screen tenants and generate cash flow. Get proven strategies for property management, timely maintenance, and tenant retention to maximize your income. Optional package of customized rental documents available from instructor after class for $25.

title: Vegetarian Feast
description: Learn to present an imginative Mexican dinner rivaling any you might find on the Mayan Riviera, prepared vegetarian and dairy free! In this interactive class we will start off he meal with a tortilla lime soup, prepare pumpkin and black bean enchiladas, and finish off the meal with a chocolate mole mousse. Your new culinary mastery will be the talk of the party. Bring an apron and an appetite. Your instructors Sherry Duquet and Sandy Boulton are local food bloggers at www.ExploitsofaVeganwannabe.com

title: Principles of Marketing
description: Focuses on marketing functions and institutions, the policies of marketing agencies, and current marketing channels. Also covers the retailing and wholesaling of consumer goods and raw materials, pricing policies and practices, anti-competitive practices, and recent governmental activities affecting marketing.

title: Tips for Buy, Rent, Sell Home
description: Attorney and CPA Jonathan Goldberg will discuss the many tax and legal aspects homeowners should know when buying, selling or renting out their homes. This is information ou can't afford to miss! Topics include purchase agreements, the legal and tax aspects of renting your home, and mortgage and real estate tax issues.

title: Pen & Ink Drawing
description: Increase your basic drawing skills and expand your knowledge of using pen and ink as a drawing medium. Join artist Saundra Weed for this four-week class and discover how to create artwork suitable for framing, journaling, illustrating books or making cards. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Supply list will be provided in class. Appropriate for all levels.

title: HTML/CSS Web Programming
description: Covers HyperText Markup Language (HTML) coding practices for both current and future browsers. Uses HTML, Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) extensively validating according to the guidelines of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C). Introduces JavaScript language. Includes the creation and publishing of a website using HTML and CSS.

title: Ambulatory Coding
description: Examines the theory and practice of coding techniques and coding systems used to capture billing data for care given to patients in settings other than acute care. Settings include but may not be limited to ambulatory surgery, emergency care, outpatient care, long term care, observation, and care rendered by health care practitioners especially physicians.

title: Psychic Phenomena
description: Make the body, mind, spirit connection! Learn about "Angels" and "Spirit Guides." Plenty of time for Q & A and spot readings for workshop participants. This workshop will be presented in a fun, lighthearted and entertaining way. Come join the fun and take charge of your life!

title: Polish Churches of Hamtramck
description: Visit the beautiful historic churches of Hamtramck, Detroit's Polish enclave, and then enjoy lunch at a restaurant in the area. Join your instructor, Don Samull, on a guided, self-driving and walking tour of the three major Roman Catholic churches of that city, as well as the quaint Polish National Catholic church. Maps showing where to meet will be sent to registered students prior to the tour. The cost of the meal is included.

title: Low-Cal, Tasty Appetizers
description: For your next event, treat your guests to yummy appetizers everyone can eat and enjoy. Sue Keskeny, author of the "Cooking with Sue" column in a local newspaper, will teach you how to make several low-calorie, diabetic-friendly recipes that are sure to be the hit of the party. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Basic Keyboarding Exploration
description: Learn how to layout and input recipients' names & address information for envelopes and labels. You will make sender's multiple return address labels. Create multiple letters and labels to mail to friends, using Microsoft Word's mail merge database feature. You will also create badges, DVD labels, stickers, tent cards, shipping labels, etc. Prequisite: Basic computer skills.

title: Excel for Beginners
description: Set up and manage your home or small business finances with Quicken's powerful, easy-to-use features. Instructor Tom McMahon will show you how to free yourself from time-consuming tasks, such as tracking your income and expenses, recording deposits, transferring funds, reconciling bank statements, budgeting and preparing for tax time. He's been doing it for years! This course is appropriate for new and intermediate-level Quicken users.

title: Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance and alignment in this twelve week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing and relaxation techniques learned in Hatha Yoga 1, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Photo Lighting I: Basic Camera
description: This comprehensive camera & flash workshop is designed especially for the photography enthusiast and hobbyist, wedding, event, indoor and outdoor photographer. Start with a mini version of Photography 101and walk through your camera settings in depth prior to flash exploration (Aperture, shutter speed, ISO, JPEG, CWB, RAW. Gain the confidence you need to stop guessing when you shoot and concentrate on the art of photography). The majority of the class will focus on using your flash--how to operate the settings and select the right mode for each situation. Learn how to blend natural light with camera flash. Explore flash modes such as TTL, Manual and Auto, wireless flash systems, which lenses work best with flash, and more. Only SLR cameras will be used for this class; film or digital. See a list of suggested equipment by visiting the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 or give us a call if you do not have web access.

title: Pediatric Nursing
description: Introduces the role of the professional nurse as the provider of care for children (from infancy to adolescence) within the context of the family environment. Discusses how to utilize critical thinking skills in a systematic, problem solving process as a framework for providing safe and effective care to restore and promote health in children and to foster growth and development. Focuses on family-centered nursing care related to the concepts of healthy children as well as children with common, acute, or chronic illness exemplars. Also covers how to research best practices and integrate theory in the care of children and families in a variety of clinical settings. This course includes supervised clinical agency/laboratory practicums. Course is comprised of a combination of theory and a clinical/lab experience per week.

title: Magic Close Up Workshop
description: This workshop will teach participants ages 9 through 99 to perform magic tricks with 2 feet of onlookers. You will explore the techniques of performing each trick as well as how to present the illusion to an audience of 1 or more. Students will learn a minimum of 10 tricks consisting of coin, rope, restoration and card illusions. Workshop fee includes the magic supplies for participants to take home.

title: Turn Recipes Into a Gold Mine
description: Famous Amos converted his cookie recipe into millions of dollars. Learn from professional caterer Andrea Taylor how you can make money using your kitchen and family recipes. She will cover calculating prices, finding the right audience, marketing specialty items, meeting sanitation guidelines, getting financing, and creating a hit. Take this class as your first step to more cash.

title: Fun & Easy Microsoft Off 2007
description: This class is an easy way for beginners to learn to use the new Microsoft Office 2007 Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Access). Learn the basics of word processing with MS Word, spreadsheet basics with MS Excel, and presentation techniques with MS Powerpoint. A demonstration of database concepts will also be shown using MS Access. The instructor has used these applications for 20+ years in the business world. Real world examples at a pace beginners can manage is the goal. Basic PC skills are a prerequisite. Text is included. A USB flash memory stick is recommended.

title: Baby Sign Language
description: Help your baby or toddler, ages 6-24 months, communicate what he or she sees, needs or feels, using nonverbal hand signs. Join speech and language pathologist Ruth Haberkorn-Halm to learn Baby Signs, a program featured on national TV, that helps children "talk" before they can speak. Discover how singing reduces tantrums, speeds intellectual growth, improves parent/child bonding, and helps parents of children with special needs. Ideal for moms, dads, grandparents and persons who work with infants and toddlers. $41 materials fee due to instructor in class for required Baby Signs Kit including 2 DVD's and a binder of resources.

title: Win the Weight Game
description: Don't give up the foods you love! Instead, give up dieting and find out how to lose weight and keep it off permanently. Through this class, you will find out why just willpower or deprivation never work. Instead change your thinking about food and learn how to develop a healthy approach to eating and exercise. You will acquire skills to stay motivated for a lifetime.

title: Get the Red Out
description: Holding anger and grudges can have physiological effects on the mind and body. Biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you these effects and discuss the many emotions often vented as anger. Learn to identify the steps of escalating anger in another person and receive a wealth of helpful tips, including nutrition, to help you deal with anger. discover how to let go of grudges and resentment and why it is important. $5 material fee due to instructor the first session.

title: MicroscaleOrganic Chem Lab II
description: Microscale glassware and analytical techniques are used in this follow-up course to CHEM-243. The primary focus of this course is multi-step organic syntheses and proper research techniques. Practical techniques from the previous course will be applied towards the synthesis of a novel polyaromatic hexaphenylbenzene, an antibacterial drug sufanilamide, triphenylmethanol, and cholesta-3,5-dien-3-ol acetate. Products will be analyzed (melting point/boiling point, infrared analysis, chromatographic properties (TLC, GC), solubility, refractive index) and compared to literature values to verify their veracity. Research will involve a critical analysis of competitive synthetic methods of a recent pharmaceutical agent. The sequence CHEM-241, CHEM-242 (Lecture I and II) and CHEM-243, CHEM-244 (Laboratory I and II) will transfer to all Michigan universities to meet their Organic Chemistry requirements for Science Majors. Approximately one hour of lecture and three hours of lab per week.

title: Intermediate Excel Workshop
description: Expand your Microsoft Excel 2003 capabilities. Learn to name cells and ranges, work with charts and graphs, use advanced functions and formatting features. Discover how to display, hide, edit and delete cell notes. Become skilled at keeping data secure by protecting cells and creating passwords. Learn how to work with multiple worksheets and workbooks. Streamline your work with templates and macros. Excel Workshop Intro or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: Assembly Detailing
description: Presents the concept of detailing of assembly drawings, including modeling of complex shapes and pictorial drawings of details. Through the use of traditional and computer-based drafting techniques, students explore drawing individual parts in their proper orientation. Modeling clay is required.

title: Exploring Popular Music
description: This class explores the history of popular music from the 1950's through today, with emphasis on the relationship between music and sociopolitical issues.

title: Camp Android
description: Have you ever wondered how mobile apps are developed? Interested in making some of your own Android programs? Join us for a one-week series of classes which will introduce you to the world of Android Development. During this class you will build your own apps from scratch, see how they get placed on the Google Play market, and will have a chance to work with teachers who have developed apps in the IT industry. You don't have to be an expert coder or even have any knowledge of code at all! We'll learn at a pace that you're comfortable with and we will encourage you to bring your great ideas to the table so we can create Android apps together.

title: Spiral Rope for Beginners
description: Spiral rope stitch is an easy, versatile form of basic stitch and some variations as you make an impressive bracelet. $15 material fee due to instructor. Class limited to 8.

title: MS Word 2007 Made So Easy
description: Explore features of Microsoft Word in a friendly environment. Find out how easy it is to create projects like a flyer, initation, letter and calendar using this poplar program. Create other projects as time permits. Learn many features of Microsoft Word as you build different projects. Bring a USB flash drive. Prerequisite: Basic computer skills.
prerequisite_text: Basic computer skills.

title: Gentle Yoga
description: Start your day by experiencing the health benefits includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance and alignment; and you will achieve mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Estate Planning
description: If you ask Rick Bloom why you need to be concerned about estate planning, he'll tell you it's because you love your family, and planning is essential if you want to protect your assets, control their disbursement, and generally make life easier for your heirs. In this seminar, Rick will discuss wills and trusts, the perils of probate, tax issues, powers of attorney, charitable gifts, and Family Limited Partnerships, Nevada Corporations, Charitable Trusts, and more. Rick was selected as one of the Top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine and currently writes a daily column on financial advice for a Detroit newspaper. For 16 years he hosted the popular "Money Talk" radio show, and he is a frequent spokesperson on financial matters on local TV and radio shows.

title: Digital Photography I
description: An introductory-level course on the basics of digital imaging where creativity is emphasized. Offers key concepts such as (manual) camera capture, basic file management, 'non-destructive' image processing, and printing which help foster the foundations for visual communications, new media disciplines, or a fine art practice. A student may wait until the first class meeting to make arrangements to buy/use a camera. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: A'la Carte and Buffet Cookery
description: Provides practical experience in all areas of quality food preparation by rotating throughout each station of the a'la carte kitchen. Students explore the proper techniques of broiling, sauteing, meat cutting, dessert presentation, buffet preparation, and cold food stations in the on campus, student-run restaurant.

title: Adult Tap Dance 2
description: If you have some experience with tap, join Emily Goward for this intermediate level class. This is a great opportunity to review the basic techniques and add another session of exercise to your life. Bring tap shoes.

title: Hybrid Cloud II
description: An advanced course covering Azure Hybrid Cloud Infrastructure and the configuration of advanced storage solutions such as Network Attached Storage (NAS) devices and Storage Area Networks (SAN). Emphasizes server and infrastructure security, fault-tolerant infrastructure in cloud computing, load balancing and fail-over clusters, disaster recovery, server and workload migration, and cloud and on-premise resource monitoring and troubleshooting. Course helps prepare students for Microsoft Windows Server Hybrid Administrator industry certification.

title: Algebra Skills
description: A supplemental course for students taking certain sections of MATH-110: Intermediate Algebra. Topics include simplifying algebraic expressions, solving linear equations, graphs of linear equations, simplifying expressions using laws of integer exponents, and simplifying polynomial expressions.

title: Fund of Plumbing & Pipefitting
description: Introduces plumbing principles. Topics include the proper selection of materials for installation and repair of gas, domestic water, sewer, soil, waste, vent systems, and various pipefitting systems. Utilizes blueprints and isometric diagrams throughout the course. Some laboratory activities.

title: Ease Pain & Chronic Disease
description: If you are tired of living with pain and swelling, join this class with naturopath Mary Born to find new solutions that don't involve prescription drugs. You will learn that often the root cause of pain and chronic disease is inflammation. Discover how many people have reduced pain and inflammation using food choices, supplements, antioxidants and lifestyle changes. See how reversing inflammation helps alleviate gum disease, arthritis pain, constipation, fatigue, constant hunger, breathing problems, risk for heart disease and more.

title: Principles of Finance
description: Introduces the fundamentals of financial management, financial statements, time value of money, interest rates, and stocks and bonds. Also examines capital budgeting models, cash flow analysis, working capital management, financial ratio analysis, and firm performance.

title: Academic Literacies
description: This course helps students develop their academic literacies in conjunction with college-level reading and writing in other courses. Course work will help students refine their reading and writing practices by asking them to think about how these skills work together in college and beyond. Course work will help students develop confidence as they learn how to meet the demands of reading and writing in diverse contexts.

title: ELL Communication 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-104C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: The Art of Bead Stringing
description: Tired of buying jewelry that doesn't quite fit? Frustrated with jewelry breaking after wearing it only a few times? Make your own quality jewelry in less than three hours! In this class you will learn the basics of using tools, stringing materials and selecting beads. Then make a fabulous pair of crystal earrings and matching crystal bracelet. $38 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Procurement 101: Step-By-Step
description: Make your venture into contracting with the government easier than you ever thought possible. Instructor will provide you with insights and a manual (included at no extra cost) that will help you understand the policies, processes, and opportunities available. It also includes multiple web resources to help ensure your success.

title: Hand and Power Tools
description: Introduces safe and effective use of hand and power tools. Emphasizes the application of tools most commonly used by industrial technicians.

title: Creat Glass Bead & Crystal Jew
description: Learn how to create jewelry to match your wardrobe! Designed for beginners, this class will teach you all the fundamentals of custom jewelry making. Create a beautiful bracelet with colorful, shimmering glass beads and Austrian crystals, along with a matching pair of dazzling earrings. Make wonderful gifts for family and friends! You can earn money too by selling your creations! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list ($10.00 kit fee collected in class).

title: Plumbing Pipefitting Code
description: Explores how to utilize the State of Michigan Plumbing Code to solve real-life problems (International Plumbing Code). Covers study habits and processes necessary to interpret and apply the code. Also helps prepare students for entry- level positions in the plumbing, multi-skilled maintenance, repair, and apprenticeship fields.

title: Machine Tool Processes I
description: An entry-level course in manufacturing processes involving turning, threading, drilling, reaming, tapping, and milling. Covers part processing, machines, and tooling used along with speeds and feeds and inspection techniques in relation to operations. Safety is an integral part of the course. Laboratory activities.

title: Found of Educ With Pract Exp
description: An educational foundations and policy studies approach to the field of education. Course topics will include but not be limited to the history of U.S. education, philosophies of education from Plato to the current testing philosophy, multi-culturalism/diversity, and historical records of educational reform movements. Other material covered within the course includes social issues such as socioeconomic status (SES), race, gender, and culture affecting educational achievement, teacher certification process, professional organizations (such as unions) and their purpose, differentiated teaching methods from a diversity perspective, classroom management, and historical changes of school financing. Students will be introduced to lesson planning, instructional techniques, and classroom instruction through their placement in a classroom setting at the grade level/special education area of interest at which the student wishes to teach. They will incorporate integrated essays into their e-portfolio.This course integrates the social foundations approach to the field of education with a 40-hour pre-student teaching practicum. Course reflective topics will include but will not be limited to the history of U.S. education, philosophies of education, multi-culturalism/diversity, educational reform movements, social issues affecting educational achievement, teacher certification process, professional organizations (such as unions) and their purpose, differentiated teaching methods, classroom management, and school financing. Standardized state standards and high-stakes testing will be addressed as well. Students will be introduced to lesson planning, instructional techniques, and classroom instruction during this practicum experience.This course requires a 40-hour off-campus field placement.

title: Glass Work
description: Come create a bit of early spring that will last all year long as you explore the world of fusing glass. Let nature's season of emerging color and fresh landscape guide you as you stack and arrange shapes and colors of glass to create your own spring-inspired glass tile. Each tile will be fired at the studio and available the following week for pickup. Please wear loose-fitting cotton clothing. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Downsizing 101
description: Do you have family members that moved out but still have items in the home? Do you want to downsize and reclaim the space or move? Let professional organizer Theresa Brune teach you pain-free techniques for sorting and sifting through years of "Stuff" and offer suggestions on what to do with unwanted items. Once you get the space back from the family member that moved out, you can start the re-decorating!

title: Holiday Gift Making Class
description: Make functional gifts for family, friends or even just for you! Using wire working techniques and simple knots, you will make your choice of two elegant bookmarks, key fobs, purse charms or zipper pulls. The key fob and purse charm/zipper pull can also double as a necklace pendant. Techniques learned will include basic design principles and gallery wrapping your dangles. Project will be completed in class. $30 materials fee due to instructor.

title: Organize Garage & Basement
description: Overstuffed closets? Paperwork in piles? Missed appointments? Is stuff taking over your life? If so, this class is just for you! Instructor Debi Weinstein is a professional organizer who has appeared on local television and radio stations, written for leading magazines, and been featured in local newspapers. Come explore the reasons you have been disorganized in the past, and learn techniques for getting and staying organized. Proper organizational skills will change your life!

title: History of Modern Philosophy
description: A continuation of the history of Western philosophy focusing on the major philosophers and major developments through the twentieth century from Descartes through Wittgenstein. Examines influential currents such as rationalism, empiricism, idealism, romanticism, Marxism, Darwinism, positivism, pragmatism, phenomenology, existentialism and analysis.

title: Nc Elementary Spanish 2
description: Builds upon the reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills introduced in SPN 131. Focuses on expanding pronunciation and grammatical principles. Topics of Hispanic culture will also be presented.

title: Organizing Pic., Files, Photos
description: Are your files, digital pictures, emails, and information scattered everywhere on your computer? Learn to create folders and get organized. Instructor and class will create an orderly system for locating pictures and files. Discover how to download information from the Internet. Find out how to save files and projects on your computer, CDs, and flashdrives, and easily find them. Class will also copy, move, and relocate files and folders. See a demonstration on arranging and deleting emails. Also search for misplaced files and locate them.

title: Comfort Food Wth Healthy Twist
description: Join Marilyn Suter as she shares some of her secrets for changing up ingredients to make some of the family favorites a bit healthier. How about sauteing (accented) a fish with flax seed or a scrumptious fall harvest flax seed salad? Take those favorite meatballs and add some oatmeal. Serve up a delicious apple cake with some of Marilyn's secret substitutions. Bring an apron and dress in layers as the kitchen tends to get a bit warm.

title: Brake Clinic
description: Reviews braking systems, including introduction to anti-lock braking systems (ABS), as well as diagnosis and troubleshooting experiences. Recommend prerequisite of AUTO 160.

title: QuickBooks Pro 10, Level 1.
description: Keeping records for your business can be a confusing and demanding aspect of handling your company. In the first four sessions learn the concepts and jargon of bookkeeping. In the next four sessions use the acclaimed accounting software program QuickBooks Pro. With QuickBooks you can manage customers, vendors and employees. You can quickly create estimates, invoices, reports and checks. Recording banking and credit card information is easy as you enter your sales and expenses and track inventory. Get organized and stay in the know.

title: Gourmet Cupcake Workshop
description: Transform 12 ordinary cupcakes into decadent, sinfully sweet gourmet treats! First you'll create 6 Double Stuffed Cupcakes, over-filled with a variety of luscious fillings, and covered with lots of sweet and crunchy toppings. Then you'll make 6 Candy Cup Cakes with multiple layers of moist cake, assorted candies (including chocolate), and decadent fillings, all displayed in cute serving cups! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12 - $15).

title: Juvenile Justice
description: Covers the legal and philosophical basis of the juvenile justice process along with a review of the juvenile court procedures in Michigan. Discusses problems related to delinquency and its control.

title: Soup Basics
description: Nothing beats soup as a basis for today's healthful home cooking. In this one-session class, you will learn four of the standbys of the home cook: old fashioned chicken noodle, French onion, creamed soup, and ox-tail. Once you have mastered these the effort needed to learn others will be minimal. Our chef instructor, Marilyn Szymanski Suter, is the Executive Chef at Eastern Michigan University where her soup class is always a sell-out! Come learn these soups in preparation for your Super-bowl Sunday and/or the long winter ahead. Bring an apron. This is a hands-on class.

title: Clinical Externship II
description: A full-time supervised clinical practicum at affiliated physical therapy departments. Students participate in patient treatment under the delegation and supervision of a licensed physical therapist. Students integrate classroom knowledge and laboratory experience to achieve the competencies of an entry-level physical therapist assistant as outlined by the American Physical Therapy Association (APTA).

title: Basic Economics
description: Offers an overview of macroeconomics and microeconomics. Discusses markets, supply and demand equilibrium, firm decision making, aggregate output, monetary and fiscal policy, and the principles of international trade.

title: Yoga - Hatha Yoga 1
description: Hatha Yoga is an ancient system of exercise that includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through yoga, your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment. You will achieve mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: DESC Robotics Cert Training
description: This non-credit accelerated course focuses on an operator level of skill and knowledge. Students with the level have a basic understanding of robot operations and programming, material handling and its components and introduction to Roboguide simulation software. This certificate is focused on the core Robot Operator Skills needed by entry level or incumbent workers. Registration is restricted to those students who are sponsored by DESC.

title: Zine Zone-Graphic Design
description: Develop and improve your graphic design skills while learning professional design tools, such as Adobe Photoshop. During this four day camp, students will be introduced to a new way of creatively expressing themselves! The camp will focus on ZINES, but also friendship, communication, and documenting the world around us. After learning about zines, campers will us photos, sketches, words, paint, stamps and other tools to create a personal zine that will be copied and traded with students. Daily activities include drawing from nature, observation and imagination. Campers will explore the natural settings around the campus to find inspiration. A gallery will showcase individual student works at the end of the event and provide a view into the Zine Zone.

title: Radio Station Wrkshp/WHFR-FM
description: Interested in how a first-rate radio station works? HFCC's award-winning radio station WHFR-FM is just the place. Join us in this intensive orientation for our non-commercial, educational radio station. Learn essential station rules and procedures, operation of equipment, and basic performance skills. You just might find yourself on the airwaves some day!

title: Chinese Medicine in Your Life
description: Gain additional insight into traditional Chinese medicine as you explore the pathways and relationships of the organ meridian system. Practice simple effective methods to address everyday ailments using the subtle energetics of acupressure therapeutics. Wear loose comfortable clothing.

title: Power of Access and Excel
description: Learn how Access and Excel can work together to improve your reporting and data analysis. Features of the class include utilization of the built-in features of Access and Excel to access data; learning to build connection strings using ADO and DAO; discovering how to automate Excel reports; including formatting, functions and page setup; using Excel charts and pivot tables with Access data. Prerequisites: basic knowledge of Access & Excel. You may want to bring a flash drive to take home the same exercises and cases you will work on in class.
prerequisite_text: basic knowledge of Access & Excel. You may want to bring a flash drive to take home the same exercises and cases you will work on in class.

title: Healthy Greek Vegetarian Dish
description: The Eastern Mediterranean diet is hailed by medical and nutrition experts as one of the most healthful in the world. Gather with Zoe to experience the Greek way where eating and socializing go hand in hand. Zoe will share her favorite vegetarian dishes such as zucchini fritters (Kolokithokefteoles), mixed green salad with Feta cheese and olives, fresh artichokes in the Constantinople style, spinach with rcie and a tasty dessert.

title: Continuing Italian Language
description: Come experience a fun approach to speaking Italian and improving fluency. Beginning on day one, at least 90% of the class will be conducted in Italian, as you are encouraged to think in Italian, like an Italian. Gone are the extensive grammar and conjugation drills, replaced with activities such as inventing and acting out skits, drafting emails, watching Italian videos without subtitles, and planning a trip. Also learn to use many of the great resources available online, such as flashcards with an audio component. All skill levels welcome; if you know a little, you'll learn a lot.

title: Return to Your Source
description: Live with clarity and passion. A spiritual journey - an inward voyage of discovery designed to bring balance to your outward behaviors and inner consciousness - can help you achieve this. Your journey is as unique as you are, and whether you are taking your first steps or you find yourself wandering along the way, here is a down-to-earth, non-denominational approach to guide you to your Source. Come learn some time-honored disciplines that can help you repel negativity, attract positive energy, and embrace a richer life experience. Instructor's book included.

title: Get Solutions for Reactive Gut
description: A "gut reaction" is more than a catchy phrase. Your gut, otherwise known as intestines, often absorbs negative emotions, unhealthy food, or a toxic environment. It may respond with gas, bad breath, heartburn, ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, allergies, stomachache, gluten intolerance, skin rashes, depression, ADD, or lowered resistance to illness. Let naturopath Mary Born teach you basic principles to restore health and activity to your gut and rid your body of toxins. Discover important scientific research often overlooked by mainstream medicine. Plan to leave with a "belly laugh" on your journey to better health.

title: English Language Prep
description: Get the necessary language skills to work in U.S. society and to prepare for further education at the college level. In this intermediate-level class you will improve your ability to speak, listen, read and write English, and gain practice for "real life" American situations. Ability to speak,read and write in English required. An interpreter will not be available. Required textbooks may be purchased in class for approximately $30.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: Plants not only add beauty to your home; they also improve air quality. Join Master Gardener Nancy Fedon and learn everything you ever wanted to know about indoor plants and unique indoor landscaping techniques. In addition to gardening basics, you'll leave with loads of information about flowering houseplants, foliage plants, the various succulents, and the effective management of common pests and diseases.

title: AutoCAD 2005
description: Auto CAD is an essential tool in many industries, including mechanical and civil engineering and architecture. Employers seek workers skilled in Auto CAD more than any other CAD system. This online program will teach you the skills needed to create and edit simple drawings and gradually introduces more advanced Auto CAD skills. Auto CAD 2005 student software and textbook included! Students must use PCs; Macs not compatible.

title: Lactation Consult Adv Pract
description: Focuses on the clinical application of lactation concepts to breastfeeding mothers. Select experiences in health care facilities enable students to assess, develop, implement and evaluate the care of the breastfeeding dyad. The course requires 200 hours in the health care setting. This course is the second part of the practicum, following AH-190, consistent with Pathway 2 according to International Board of Lactation Consultant Examiners (IBLCE).

title: Film History and Criticism
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-132 to register for this course. Introduces the film medium and the film experience as entertainment, artistic expression, and social communication, through examination of production techniques, critical theories, and historic & modern examples.

title: Descriptive Astronomy
description: Consists of a conceptual introduction to the fundamentals of astronomy. Describes the motion, structure, and evolution of celestial bodies as well as the Universe itself.

title: Singapore Math Strategies
description: In this professional development course for teachers, you'll get the training you need to start teaching model drawing, the powerful Singapore Math strategy that gives word problems a visual context. As a teacher, you know that many students groan when it's time to solve word problems. Why is that? Are the problems too difficult? Do students get lost trying to decipher the wording or figure out the computation? Do they simply not know which strategy to use? Actually, it's a combination of all these issues. Luckily, model drawing, a Singapore Math strategy for working word problems, will help your students start to enjoy math in a way they may never have before.

title: Swing & Salsa
description: No more standing on the sidelines at your favorite clubs. Get ready to hit the dance floors with the Swing and Salsa! Under the direction of an experienced dance instruction, you will learn the basics of these popular dances. Wear leather-soled shoes.

title: Shielded Mtl Arc Vertical Wld
description: Explores the theory and operation of shielded metal arc welding in a vertical welding position.

title: Acad Writing & Reading for Ell
description: Intended for English Language Learners (ELLs) whose placement scores indicate the need for instruction or review in order for them to write acceptable college compositions in ENG-131, or for ELLs who have successfully completed ELI-106 R, W, and C courses. Various methods such as lectures, group discussions, textbook exercises, peer review and conferences will be used to help students improve their expository writing skills. This course also emphasizes reading strategies and skills that will prepare English Language Learners for success in college level content-area and English composition courses. Students will learn how to make connections with a variety of authentic texts, practice strategies needed for critical reading, and expand their academic English vocabulary. Students must earn a grade of C or higher in ELL-130 before enrolling in ENG-131. This course is non-transferable.

title: PowerPoint Basics & Beyond
description: Why limit yourself to dull text-based slide presentations if you can enhance them with graphics? Discover the tools for designing exciting slides with drawings, clipart and photographs. Learn creative techniques to reposition, group and align content to give your slides that extra pizzazz. Identify the value of using the master slide feature. Create a slideshow. The instructor is a Microsoft certified and known as a PowerPoint "guru." Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.

title: Stress Makes Me Gain Weight
description: As we draw nearer to the holidays, do you find yourself reaching for cookies, ice cream, chips or a cocktail? Is the scale creeping up? Then this class is for you. Biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you how such simple measures as getting more light, drinking more water, turning off the TV, and increaisng pleasure can help weight loss. After a self-assessment to determine if you are a fast-burner or a slow-burner, you will discover what foods give you energy, which mineral controls chocolate cravings, and which minerals stop sugar binges. Give yourself this class as a gift to gain control of your food intake, your weight, your brain chemistry and your mood.

title: Introduction to PLCs
description: Introduces various elements of basic PLCs including the identification of programmable logic control systems as well as overview of PLC system architectures. Provides instruction in basic numbering systems, computer terminology, PLC functions, program structures, and point addressing basics. Pre-requisites: CIMEL 107 or Instructor Permission.

title: Residential Wiring
description: Explores the fundamentals of residential wiring, especially the aspects of the National Electrical Code specifically applying to residential construction and repairs. Covers electrical supplies and hardware appropriate for residential applications, and examines residential wiring techniques.

title: Child Knitting & Crochet
description: This is an introduction to knitting and crochet for children. To take knitting, the child would need to be 8 years of age. To take crochet, the child would need to be 11 years of age. Bring a tape measure, scissors, needles and a crochet hook.

title: Explore Watercolor
description: Free and flowing watercolor effects combined with a multitude of techniques will free the inner artist and journal enthusiast in you. Join artist Saundra Weed for this six-week class and learn how easy it is to create paintings and papers that can be combined with bookmaking, journal embellishment and creative self-expression. $5 due to instructor for first night's materials. Supply list will be provided in the first class. Appropriate for all levels.

title: Traditional Lebanese Cooking
description: With the recent emphasis on the health benefits of Mediterranean cuisine, many Americans are discovering and embracing Lebanese food. Learn how to prepare a delicious traditional meal, consisting of veg/burgol with tomato sauce and Lebanese cabbage salad. Chef Kamal Hachem will teach you his secrets for cooking up a fast, easy, authentic Lebanese meal. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Computers in Health Care
description: Presents the fundamentals of computer technology and specific applications that are important to the delivery of health care. Also examines legal and ethical considerations in the processing of confidential patient and medical information. Lab activities.

title: Art Appreciation
description: A global survey of the development of visual arts through studying major historic civilizations and cultures from the stone age to the present. Explains technical information on the materials, tools, and techniques used to create visual arts in different branches. Discusses how to interpret works of art by examining the contextual characteristics, the content, functionality and the culture(s) that produced it. Field work at a major museum/art gallery is required to strengthen student's knowledge.

title: Explor of Mechan Transmission
description: Introduces basic principles, skills, and terminology of mechanical transmissions. Uses LABVOLT simulator to demonstrate hands-on methods through a project-based approach. The purpose of this course is to explore various fundamental techniques of mechanical transmissions that are similar to industry method of operations.

title: Screenwriting Act 1
description: Learn to write visually in this writing-intensive course. Through assignments, class critiques, and screenings of films and music videos which demonstrate storytelling techniques, effective characters, and convincing dialogue, instructor Frank Jamal will guide you in composing your first act. You will leave with your own complete Act 1 for a music video, commercial or 5-minute movie.

title: Floral Design & Care of Flower
description: Professional floral designer Susan Muldowney will show you proper care of cut flowers and several designs with fresh flowers. You will learn how to arrange a dozen roses and make a simple fresh flower arrangement to take home. A materials fee of $15 is due to the instructor in class.

title: Aerobic Dance
description: This is an exercise class which covers all of the health related components of physical fitness, with an emphasis on cardiovascular endurance. The benefits are similar to those experience in jogging, cycling, swimming and other endurance activities. The course also covers how to make choices for a healthy diet; weight and stress management; and disease prevention. No dance background is required.

title: Energy Auditor
description: This training will prepare participants to take Certification Field and Written Exam. This is a nationally recognied Home Energy Professional Certification.

title: Technical Mathematics
description: For students in technology programs who have had MATH 100 or one year of Beginning Algebra. Covers functions and graphs, properties of lines, the trigonometric functions, systems of linear equations, rational expressions, quadratic equations, solution of right triangles, vectors, integer exponents, linear regression, complex numbers, and an introduction to the non-graphing scientific calculator. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments.

title: Downsize, Declutter
description: One of the reasons home are not selling in today's market is that they are too cluttered. If you have years of accumulated belongings, a garage so full that you can't park your car in it, this class is for you. Theresa Brune, a Professional Organizer, will teach you pain-free techniques for sorting and sifting through items, reclaiming and maximizing storage space, parting with objects you no longer want, and making the house clutter free and attractive to a potential buyer.

title: Belly Dancing "basic Tech"
description: Get in shape as you experience the exotic, beautifully expressive art of belly dance--the Middle Eastern dance women have performed for ages. Under the direction of Cedena, one of Michigan's leading instructors, you'll learn the basic techniques of hip and arm work, turns and traveling, combinations, and more. Join this energetic, fun class and discover the many physical and mental benefits associated with the ancient movements of belly dance. Adults of all ages and body types, and youth 12 years and older, are welcome. No prior experience is needed.

title: Fundamentals of Baking
description: Covers the basic concepts, standards, and practices involved in professional quantity baking production. Examines the preparation and techniques used in bakery operations. Instructor rotates students through various production areas, which include but are not limited to: quick breads, cookies, yeast products, layered dough, pies, basic cakes, cheesecakes, simple pastries, and doughnuts. NOTE: Requires chef jacket, chef hat, and chef apron by the second full week of classes.

title: Intro to Industrial Hydraulics
description: Introduces the principles of fluid power as applied to industrial systems. Presents various hydraulic components and their functions within fluid power systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Introduction College Writing
description: ENG-131 is the first college-level composition course in a two-semester sequence. Students will be introduced to writing, reading, critical thinking, and using information at the college level. In particular, the course will help students develop writing that reveals rhetorical and academic flexibility.

title: Create Your Own Spa Experience
description: Don't pay high prices at fancy department stores for luxurious sugar scrubs, skin cleansers, toners, bath salts, and facial masques - create them in our class for a fraction of the price! Fragrant, moisturizing, natural beauty products that will replenish your body and relax your mind - a true spa experience. Make wonderful gifts for your family and friends (these items are hot sellers online too - sell them and earn money!) Call us or visit the Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-$18.)

title: Exercise Techniques Lab
description: Provides the opportunity for application of the techniques learned in PTA 118. Focuses on therapeutic exercise procedures utilized by physical therapist assistants as well as assessment of joint motion and muscle strength, implications of range of motion (ROM) and muscle testing for exercise prescription, types of exercise, basic exercise programs, and the use of exercise equipment. Guided lab setting.

title: Exercise Techniques I
description: Provides instruction in therapeutic exercise procedures utilized by physical therapist assistants. Topics include assessment of joint motion and muscle strength, implications of range of motion (ROM) and muscle testing for exercise prescription, types of exercise, basic exercise programs, and the use of exercise equipment. Theory only-no lab activities.

title: Civil War Series
description: Come and enjoy mesmerizing stories of the life and times of the American Civil War. Our three presenters are all Civil War buffs who have walked the battlefields and read thousands of letters and diaries, many in private family collections. They will enthusiastically share with you firsthand accounts, fascinating human interest stories, amusing anecdotes and rare photos that bring these people back to life in a way no history book can do. If you enroll in this section you will be enrolled in all 3 classes: (CEART 9004, Tales of the CSS Hunley; CEART 9005, Battery M First Michigan Light Artillery; CEART 9006 George Custer: His Life and Legacy) for a special discounted rate.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-104C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Abs, Glutes & Thighs
description: Burn, fat... tighten your abs... slim and tone your inner and outer thighs... lift your derriere! This challenging but fun workout sculpts and tightens your lower body. Increase your energy level and feel better in an amazingly short time. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. However, beginners may need to adapt some of the exercises. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Second-Year French IV
description: Follows FRE-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level French courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in French, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written French. Presents francophone culture.

title: How Pub Best Sell
description: Do you have an idea, concept, or story that would make an excellent book? If so, spend three hours with Mr. Jerrold R. Jenkins, a guru of independent publishing, exploring the business aspects of publishing a best-selling book. Hear the pros and cons of traditional and non-traditional publishing methods, learn about the various markets, and discuss the steps involved in publishing a book. In short, acquire the knowledge and networking skills needed for survival in the competitive $25 billion book industry.

title: 6+YOU: Mking 6+1 Trait Wrtng
description: makes student writing good writing, whether the student is five or thirteen or anywhere in between. You will take charge of the traits, developing writing prompts and classroom activities to teach and reinforce good writing practices. Youll assess your own writing samples to discover your strengths as a writer, and then translate those strengths to your teaching. Some time will also be dedicated to aligning the 6+1 Traits with the Common Core State Standards.

title: Dreams
description: We all dream, but what does it mean? Enjoy an evening of learning about different types of dreams, universal dream symbols and sharing your own dreams. Pamela will help you interpret them and will give helpful suggestions.

title: Internet Marketing
description: Maximize the effectiveness of your website by creative Internet marketing. This course will cover the importance of web content, usability and "stickness." Stay in touch with your customers using email and other tools. Learn about search engine placement and the importance of links.

title: Financial Crossroads: What Now
description: Are you retiring? Receiving a lump sum distribution? Have you had a recent job change? Were you laid off or fear you may be? Get some valuable information that can help you make sound financial decisions. Understand tax consequences, explore the pros and cons of IRAs, and evaluate your financial goals. Please bring pen and paper to class.

title: Pro Assess Level 2
description: Increase your knowledge of property assessment as you prepare for the SAB Level II Certification. Topics will include property descriptions; parcel numbering; tax mapping; equalization and appeals; aerial photographic interpretation; local government finance; cost approach; market data approach; income approach; architectural types and construction; and residential, commercial, and industrial appraisals. Approved by the Board of Real Estate Appraisers for 58 hours of pre- licensure or continuing education credits. Textbook included.

title: Indust Appl Trigonomic Princip
description: Focuses on trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Covers trigonometric functions, solution of right triangles, solution of oblique triangles, and problem solving techniques.

title: Accupressure Weight Mgmt
description: A continuation of CEHFS 2137.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Tone your abs, legs and arms in a fun, low-impact aerobics class based on the delightful styles of Hawaiian and Tahitian dancing. Get a full body workout while enhancing your balance and grace. Your instructor Janeen Bodary, who has trained in Hawaii, will teach you traditional Hula and Tahitian steps that will be woven into your workout. Because this is a fitness class, no dances are taught. The class is ideal for beginners, for those looking for a novel way to get in shape, and for dancers who need to enhance their cardiovascular endurance.

title: Fun & Easy Microsoft Office 07
description: This class is an easy way for beginners to learn to use the Microsoft Office 2007 Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Access). Learn the basics of word processing with MS Word, spreadsheet basics with MS Excel, and presentation techniques with MS Powerpoint. A demonstration of database concepts will also be shown using MS Access. The instructor has used these applications for 20+ years in the business world. Real world examples at a pace beginners can manage is the goal. Basic PC skills are a prerequisite. Text is included. A USB flash memory stick is recommended.

title: Methods for Teaching Exercise
description: Provides a hands-on approach to demonstrating the skills that are necessary to lead group exercise instruction as well as personal training. Presents a variety of methods to lead exercise. NOTE: Also helps prepare students interested in taking industry certification exams (e.g. ACSM, ACE, and NSCA) in personal training, group exercise instruction, and weight management, but certification exams are not included in this course.

title: Secure Operations Management
description: This course presents the general concepts of threat detection, risk identification and analysis, and incident response for companies seeking guidance on establishing practical and systematic protection against cyber threats. It provides a comprehensive set of best practices and implementation guidance to help companies design, build, and operate effective vehicle cybersecurity threat detection, risk management, and incident response activities in their operating environment. This module examines the critical topics of threat detection and analysis, risk identification, prioritization, mitigation, and sustainment, as well as subsequent incident management within a pragmatic automotive cybersecurity ecosystem. The focus will be on standard operational best practices generally recognized by the industry. Students will learn how to plan, implement, and maintain a comprehensive, strategic threat detection and associated risk management and incident response posture and a systematic response to incidents.

title: Essentials Anatomy&Physiology
description: A comprehensive study of all eleven body systems in a one-semester course. Lecture emphasizes how anatomy, chemistry, and cell biology permit the specific functioning of organs and systems. Lab reinforces understanding of the associated anatomic structures. Please note: BIO-134 is a 5 credit, content-heavy course, which requires a substantial time commitment and focused study beyond the conventional expectations in a standard 100 level science course. Four hours of lecture and two hours of lab per week.BIO-134 does not substitute for the BIO-233/BIO-234 sequence required by HFC's Nursing and Physical Therapist Assistant programs in the School of Health and Human Services (HHS). BIO-134 may serve as refresher/remedial course for the 233/234 sequence if taken within 5 years of original courses and permission is granted from the School of HHS.

title: Adobe Illustrator Cs5
description: Adobe Illustrator CS5 is a sophisticated graphics application that helps you to create complex and attractive designs with type effects. Create logos, advertisements, and other illustrations with ease using this software. Learn to use Adobe Illustrator CS5 by drawing and manipulating simple shapes to create logos. You will also combine text and graphics to create advertisements. Illustrator is a great companion for Flash and integrates throughout the Adobe Suite. Text provided. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.

title: Zumba Toning
description: Complement your regular Zumba Fitness routine with this muscle toning and strength training option. Based on the same great music and dance moves of Zumba Fitness, you'll add additional fitness moves and use light weight toning sticks to target major muscle groups. This class is open to everyone, no previous dance or Zumba experience necessary. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Weave a Fabulous Bracelet
description: Express your personal style by designing and making a "uniquely you" woven bracelet. Under the direction of professional beader Kathleen Robinson you will learn to use traditional basketry techniques to weave handmade beads and metal charms into a stunning bracelet. $28 materials fee due to instructor.

title: Assembly Detailing
