description: Presents the concept of detailing of assembly drawings, including modeling of complex shapes and pictorial drawings of details. Through the use of traditional and computer-based drafting techniques, students explore drawing individual parts in their proper orientation. Modeling clay is required.

title: Practical Accounting
description: Offers practical knowledge of bookkeeping principles, including small-business accounting practices. Introduces the accounting cycle, the specialized journals employed by merchandising firms, and payroll accounting.

title: Paramedic III
description: Introduces the pathophysiology of respiratory disease and neurological emergencies, then integrates this knowledge with assessment findings to develop a field impression and deliver appropriate medical care, including an in-depth discussion of endocrine emergencies and the associated treatments. Significant course time details the anatomy, physiology, and pathophysiology of various types of trauma. The emphasis is on treating the "whole patient," avoiding treatment distractions of any singular injury.

title: Second-Year German IV
description: Follows GER-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level German courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written German. Presents the cultures of German-speaking countries.

title: Spec Topics Indust Draf Techno
description: Designed to explore selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current drafting technology trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Students can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Clinical Education II
description: This is second in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and ASRT competencies. Course offers experiences in chart recognition, basic patient assessment, patient communication, and basic departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, abdomen, upper extremity, lower extremity and shoulder girdle radiographic positioning are emphasized.

title: Intermediate Knitting
description: This course will focus on skills for the knitter who wants to advance beyond a scarf or two needle hat project. You will learn more gauge, measurement and shaping. Learn the construction of a basic knitted sweater and the techniques commonly used in knitting of it. Become familiar with intarsia knitting, how to make cables, short rows and pick up stitches. Alternate methods of casting on and binding off will also be taught. Students will also be introduced to stitch patterns and techniques that will enliven the edges and borders of their projects. Supplies needed for class include worsted weight yarn, size 8 needles, a tape measure and stitch markers.

title: Ice Carving and Design
description: Explores techniques used to shape, round, and sculpt ice displays with the use of hand and power tools. Also covers safety procedures related to ice handling; tools and equipment used in carving; qualities of the ice; and proper care and sharpening of tools. Final project involves carving an ice sculpture from a single block of ice.

title: Return to Your Source II
description: For those who need additional guidance to continue their journey. Continue your spiritual journey by adding depth of understanding and fulfillment to your experiences. Return to your Source II is a natural progression for those who desire renewed meaning in their lives.

title: Intermediate Feng Shui
description: Experience immediate positive changes in your life as you apply the principles of feng shui more specifically to your personal environment. Susan Haigfleigh, the instructor, will combine her practical, logical training as an Architect and Interior Designer and her intuitive/metaphysical training as she assists you in creating a balance of the best of Eastern and Western thinking, practice, and philosophy. Prerequisite: CLNC 7031
prerequisite_text: CLNC 7031

title: Issues in Personal Growth
description: Examines how to identify barriers to personal growth and how to overcome those barriers to find friendship, love and happiness.

title: Programming PLCs
description: Introduces various elements of programming PLCs. Addresses the basic elements of PLC programming and routines. Requires student to program using ladder logic, structured text, sequential function chart, and function block languages. Pre-requisites: CIMEL 109 or Instructor Permission.

title: Legal Writing
description: Explores how to identify legal issues and perform advanced-level research using Westlaw and other Internet-based research databases. Covers application of the law to a hypothetical fact scenarios and presenting results in an objective memorandum, persuasive brief, or oral argument, using appropriate legal terminology and citation.

title: Keep Records With Excel
description: Excel is a computer program for keeping track of numbers and lists. How much did I spend? How much will it cost? What if the interest rate goes up or my stocks go down? Calculations are easy in Excel when you know how to create the formulas (you don't need to be a math whiz!) and use the built-in functions that allow you to find a sum, compute an average, or count entries and much more with just a few clicks of the mouse. Whether you are tracking the family finances, involved in business or keeping your club or league records, Excel can be a useful tool for you. Prerequisites: Intro to Computers or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.

title: Art of Pearl Necklaces
description: Both trendy and classic, the pearl is the perfect piece of jewelry for almost any occasion. In this class, you will learn two techniques for making pearl jewelry. Kathleen Robinson-Young will teach you how to knot pearls and beads on silk thread and how to string pearls on flexible wire to make a stunning bracelet. You'll leave with your own handmade bracelet and matching pearl earrings. $38 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Exploration of Auto Tech
description: Introduces basic automotive technology principles, skills, and terminology with the assistance of an online course element (blended learning environment). Topics include career exploration in automotive technology, safety, tools and equipment, shop procedures, basic automotive maintenance, vehicle testing, and competitive automobiles.

title: Say It With Sign
description: Learn to listen with your eyes in this fun, easy, interactive class. Anne Bolander, long-time instructor and member of the deaf community, will teach you the basics of conversational sign language using the Pidgin Method (a combination of English sentence format and ASL shortcuts). Author of "I Was #87, "Anne will also share some of her moving experiences growing up as a deaf child. Come alone or enroll a friend or family member (children welcome too).

title: Secr Outstand Cust Serv
description: One of the biggest business challenges today is providing superior customer service--the kind of service that will make your customers stop, take notice, and tell their friends. Great products attract first-time customers, but it is the service they receive that brings repeat business and referrals. Explore the secrets to outstanding customer service and discover what it takes to keep today's customers satisfied--and loyal. Learn how to deal with customers who are disappointed, rushed, confused, argumentative and demanding as well as the average, good-natured ones. Come alone, send individual employees, or, better yet, send your entire customer service team.

title: Camp RN
description: At Camp RN students are engaged in anatomy and physiology and nutritional science, while learning nursing fundamentals including CPR and first aid. Participants also tour the HFC M-TEC Center, interact with nurses in a hospital setting (shadowing), and utilize the physical assessment laboratory to learn about the human body.

title: Plumbing Code
description: Introduces the use and application of the Michigan Mechanical Plumbing Code. Covers how to review each article of the code for content and application, and how to interpret plans and drawings as related to plumbing and pipefitting.

title: Conversation Spanish Intermed
description: If you have completed Conversational Spanish 1 or have a basic knowledge of the language, continue developing your skills in this fun and informative intermediate-level class. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $30).

title: Media Writing
description: This course explores techniques and critical analysis involved in media writing across traditional and digital platforms, including scriptwriting for commercials, public service announcements, broadcast and digital content, as well as screenwriting for film and television. Students will gain hands-on experience with industry-standard practices, focusing on impactful and ethical storytelling across cinematic, broadcast, internet, and social media.

title: Appeal Your Property Tax
description: Tired of paying high property taxes when your home value continues to decline? Whether you are a homeowner or an investor, you can save money by reducing your taxes. Learn the legalities of assessing property values and the process to effectively appeal at the local Board of Review and the Michigan State Tax Tribunal. You can not only lower your current taxes, but you also may be able to retroactively collect overpaid taxes from previous years. There is an informational packet availble from the instructor for $5.

title: Digital Camera for Thumb-Ones
description: Do you know the difference between auto setting and scene mode? How about the 10 most basic universal camera icons? Instructor and class will cover these and other basic camera features. Also, learn to edit photos using highlight, contrast, red-eye reduction and special effects. Get free online photo-editing software for your computer. Finally, visit photo-processing websites and create a free account to save, email, share, edit and buy pictures you have taken. Buy prints online and pick them up next day at your local store or have them delivered to your door! We will explore other helpful websites. You do not need a camera to be in this class. On second thought, please do not bring your camera to class.

title: Basic Sheet Metal Layout Fab
description: Covers layout, forming, and fabrication of basic sheet metal ductwork fittings and use of hand/power tools and equipment needed to accomplish various tasks. Explains how to fabricate square/round sheet metal ductwork, tapers, transitions, offsets, and how to fasten ductwork together.

title: Bldg Science Capstone
description: This studio class focuses on both residential and commercial building structural systems, techniques, materials and methods; including soils, foundations, substructure, and superstructure. Studio work will be a mix of hands on construction, physical modeling, digital modeling, building detailing, and analysis.

title: Wealth Wth Rental Real Estate
description: Is rental property your ticket to financial security? Get the latest facts about actual profits and potential pitfalls with rental real estate from successful property manager/investor Peter Pace. First, learn how to find property, evaluate its worth, negotiate price, and obtain financing. Then get Peter's advice to set a market-driven rent, write leases, attract and screen tenants, and generate cash flow. Discover his proven strategies for property management, timely maintenance, and tenant retention to maximize your income. Optional $25 package of rental documents available after class.

title: Windows Version 7 for Upgrader
description: This course is designed for persons who are familiar with and comfortable working in Windows XP and want to upgrade to the new and enhanced features of Windows 7. This course is not for students who are first-time Windows users.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 2
description: Teaches beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-103W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: MAP Leadership Skills
description: This course provides salaried employees tools and knowledge to improve the Leadership capability of the Michigan Assembly Plant.

title: Wyandotte Polish Churches
description: The unique downriver community of Wyandotte has long-time Polish roots. Join your instructor, Don Samull, on a guided, self-driving and walking tour of three of its charming Polish churches, including the jewel, Our Lady of Mt. Carmel. If there is time, you will also visit the small chapel of the Polish Mariavites. The tour includes a Polish lunch at the city's Polish restaurant. Maps showing where to meet will be sent to registered students prior to the tour.

title: Traditional Greek Spring Dish
description: Experience a culinary and cultural delight with instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and rasied in Greece's second largest city and is passionate about sharing her knowledge of food. In this class Zoe will thrill you with the delights of some of the traditional dishes of spring from the countryside of Greece. Start with triangles of cheese or spinach wrapped in a phylo dough and a colorful Greek salad. The main course will be Moussaka (a combination of eggplant, meat sauce and Bechamel sauce). A light dessert will complete the meal. Bring your apron and your appetite.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: As you watch the prices for produce soar at the market do you wish you knew how to grow your own vegetables? Let Laurie Jeffery, certified Master Gardener, help get you started! Learn how to pick the site for your garden, what to plant, how to plant, how to care for your garden and how to harvest your crop.

title: CNC Training Program
description: This non-credit CNC Training program has been designed to cover the essentials of operation and programming as it applied to CNC Milling and Turning Centers. The program is divided into three segments. The first segment on Manual Machines builds fundamental skills in print reading, shop math, and manual machining. Segment two consists of CNC Orientation and Operator Training with topics such as CNC/Shop safety, loading tools and altering overrides. The third segment completes the course with CNC Set-up/Basic Programming and encompasses CNC systems, CNC Program structure, canned cycles, sub-routines and sub-pograms, cutter compensation, and cutting holes.

title: The Flash & Color Workshop
description: Are you having camera flash and color problems? Well, you are not alone. Don't miss this opportunity to learn from two of the most experienced photography experts in the country. Andreas Browne and SPECIAL GUEST world-renowned color expert Gary Poole as they help you color correct and capture images like the pros do. Learn the everyday techniques they use that have been helping professional photographers across the United States. (Lunch included.)

title: Free Health Info on Internet
description: If you seek to lose weight, find the latest reports about your health concerns, or research prescription prices and drug side effects, come to this class. With Internet pro Anita Denard, you will use various websites to check out weight-loss strategies, discover up-to-date, reliable health information, or learn about alternative therapies, vitamins, minerals, and other supplements. Search for answers in class to your questions about heart disease, arthritis, high blood pressure, osteoporosis, men's health concerns, etc. Designed for beginners and seniors with basic Internet and computer skills

title: Automation Controls & Robotics
description: Utilizes control specifications written by the student for laboratory automation machines and industrial robots to implement controls for non-synchronous and synchronous operation of the machine. Covers techniques, terminology, and documentation currently used in automated manufacturing.

title: Growing Great Roses
description: Like people, roses need food, water, sun, air and love to grow and thrive. Join Nancy Lindley, author of Roses for Michigan and owner of a popular Michigan rose store, to learn how to give your garden the perfect blend of these nutrients. She'll overview a year in the life of a rose garden, with special emphasis on insect and disease problem. You may buy an autographed copy of Roses for Michigan after class for $20.

title: Comp Control Ignit Sys & Engin
description: An intermediate-level course on computer-controlled ignition systems and engine performance. Studies systems currently in use as well as newer systems available in the future.

title: Baseball Prospect Camp
description: This camp is designed for high school juniors and seniors to be evaluated by Henry Ford College coaches to be considered to join the Hawks. Eight-player form the last year's Prospect Camp were offered a roster spot and/or an athletic scholarship. Henry Ford College based players have been drafted by Major League Baseball teams and are often recruited to play at 4 year institutions around the country. Coach Tim Leighton host the Henry Ford Prospect Camp. Coach Leighton has completed three years as the Head Coach of the baseball team, and also serves as Associate Scout with Major League Baseball's San Diego Padres.

title: Energy Control Power Lockout
description: The goal of this course is to ensure that affected employees are provided with the knowledge necessary to ensure their safety when working in or around permit-required and non-permit confined spaces.

title: Prin of Programming Logic
description: An introductory-level course presenting programming logic concepts and skills. Emphasizes structured methods of programming. Covers input, output, selection/decisions, loops, arrays, data validation, functions, and modules. A computer lab is used for debugging, compilation and testing.

title: Bldg Analyst Cert - Field Exam
description: Take the Building Performance Institute (BPI) Building Analyst field exam to complete your certification process. Admission restricted. Contact Diane McCarthy.

title: CyberPatriot Camp
description: CyberPatroit is the National Youth Cyber Education Program. At the cent of CyberPatriot is the National Cyber Defense Competition. The completion puts teams of high school and middle school students in the position of newly hired IF professionals tasked with managing the network of a small company. In the rounds of competition, team are given a set of virtual images that represent operating systems and are tasked with finding cybersecurity vulnerabilities within the images and hardening the system while maintaining critical services in a six hour period.

title: About Boating Safely
description: This recently revised US Coast Guard-approved course is designed for students 12 years and older. In an entertaining and educational environment, you will learn how to boat safely and enjoyably avoid problems on the water, and handle an emergency. Boat trailering, safe PWC (Personal Water Craft) operation, personal safety equipment and procedures, and more will also be covered. Upon successful completion you will receive a course certificate that may qualify you for reduced boat insurance rates. The State of Michgan requires teens 14 and older to have a Boating Safety Certificate to operate a PWC (jet ski). An adult must accompany students under 17 years old. Bring a brown bag lunch.

title: Adobe Acrobat: Intermediate
description: This class is designed for people who know how to create PDF documents and are interested in enhancing existing PDF files using Acrobat's more advanced features. Learn to insert one PDF file into another PDF file, add digital signatures, passwords, security settings and restrictions. You will create and modify PDF forms; add radio buttons, check boxes, drop down lists; preview and export forms. Enhance document navigation and create bookmarks. Learn to use Acrobat in the document review cycle, create comment markups, cloud callouts and more.

title: Printmaking Without a Press
description: Create a one-of-a-kind print as well as multiple images that can be used as frameable artwork or in combination with other projects such as collages, journal covers, covered treasure boxes, photo albums, scrapbooking, cards and more. A rolling pin, plus easy-to-use tools found around your home, will be used to create printing plates. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Supply list will be provided in the first class.

title: Pilates
description: Flatten abs, strengthen and tone muscles and improve your flexibility, endurance and posture using the Pilates method, one of today's hottest fitness trends. Developed over 90 years ago, Pilates is a tried-and-true, low-impact exercise system. It is popular with athletes, dancers and anyone who wants to develop a more streamlined shape using an all-encompassing mind, body and spirit exercise. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Adv Cardiac Life Support
description: Students will be able to analyze life threatening cardiac emergencies and apply advanced principles of cardiac and respiratory anatomy and physiology. This course is designed for paramedical, medical, nursing or allied health personnel who daily occupation demands proficiency and certification in the knowledge of ACLS skills. Health care/nursing students must be in their final semester. The curriculum adheres to American Heart Association 2001 Guidelines. Prerequisite: successful completion of Basic Life Support (BLS) Healthcare Provider course within the past two years. Bring BLS card to first class session. Textbooks ($50) purchased from instructor.
prerequisite_text: successful completion of Basic Life Support (BLS) Healthcare Provider course within the past two years. Bring BLS card to first class session. Textbooks ($50) purchased from instructor.

title: Applied Food Serv Sanitation
description: Explores food contaminants, bacterial growth, safe food storage, and safe food handling procedures, as well as procedures for scheduling, cleaning, sanitizing, and pest control for facilities and equipment. NOTE: As part of this course, the National Restaurant Association Educational Foundation exam is included. Students who successfully pass the exam earn the ServSafe Food Protection Manager Certificate. This certificate is recognized by the state health department.

title: Internet-Beginners & Tinkerers
description: Find out how easy it is to search the internet for a potpourri of websites in this non-threatening atmosphere. See how to shop online and order airline reservations. Students will explore search engines such as Yahoo and Google to obtain information related to their interest. Also, learn toolbar features such as history, refresh, favorites, home and others.

title: Ballroom Boot Camp
description: You've always wanted to try it! It looks like fun; it is fun! Come alone or with a partner and learn the fundamentals of leading and following. You will be introduced to a variety of dances for social events such as weddings, parties, and nightclubs. This is your chance to learn the swing, disco, Foxtrot, Cha Cha, Rumba, Waltz, and more. No experience required--only enthusiasm. Wear leather-soled shoes.

title: Introduction to Telecommunicat
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-131 to register for this course. A survey course investigating the various electronic communication media, as well as print media, from historical, economic, ethical and social viewpoints.

title: Intuition Development 1
description: In this workshop Professional Intuitive Kristy Robinett will provide proven ways to tap into your own innate intuitive ability, which will help increase your effectiveness in all areas of your life. Participants will be able to: 1)use proven methods of centering to gain better clarity of the issues impacting their clients or themselves; 2) trust their intuition by removing their fear of tapping into this keen insight; and 3) be able to identify which of the three main types of intuition is their strongest. Gather up your friends and make a night of it!

title: Food and Beverage Controls
description: Emphasizes cost calculations of food, wine, spirits, supplies, and labor in order to understand a profit and loss statement. Presents the forecasting, production planning, inventory, and ordering cycle with the aid of MS Excel and web-based ordering systems. Also examines how buying decisions are made by utilizing calculations of yield and best value, along with government regulations and ethics.

title: Metallic Jewelry - Mommy & Me
description: Make and take home several unique and colorful earrings, pins and rings, all handmade using "Friendly Plastic" jewelry-making sticks. Lots of fun and easy projects to do, an adult and child will create beautiful looking designs for themselves and each other too! Children age six or older. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list. Registration fee for adult - register children separately at discount rate - see below. Instructor will collect $5 per person materials fee first day of class.

title: Media Performance
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-243 to register for this course. Offers study and practice in the skills required for successful communication from behind microphones and in front of cameras, with special attention on announcing, interviewing, and acting.

title: Reiki Master Level Cert
description: Achieve master level certification through instruction by Reiki Master Lisa Gutowski. In this class you will learn how to teach and become a mentor. Master certification requires apprenticeship, as well as classroom training. Prerequisite: Reiki Level 3 Certification. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.
prerequisite_text: Reiki Level 3 Certification. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Sew Intermediate
description: This course is designed for students who have mastered basic sewing skills and are ready to advance their technique and confidence. Sew Intermediate expands upon foundational knowledge, introducing more complex construction methods, precision techniques, and the use of specialty fabrics and tools. Participants will learn to follow multi-piece patterns, refine finishing skills, and explore creative design choices through a series of structured projects. Emphasis is placed on problem-solving, craftsmanship, and building independence at the sewing machine. By the end of the course, students will complete intermedicate-level garments or home decor projects that demonstrate improved technical ability and attention to detail.

title: Adobe Photoshop Cs2 for Photo
description: Photographers and studio owners, this workshop is specifically for you! Learn what you need to know about digital retouching and workflow. Get a clear view of what Photoshop can do and learn how to make your images remarkable. Designed by our photography guru, life and style photographer Andreas Browne, and taught by Adrian Herbst. This is an opportunity to work with concrete Photoshop concepts such as retouching, using actions for repetitive jobs, using layers, masks, blending tool secrets and more. Some computer experience is a must for this hands-on workshop. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.

title: Turn Recipes Into a Gold Mine
description: Famous Amos converted his cookie recipe into millions of dollars. Learn from professional caterer Andrea Taylor how you can make money using your kitchen and family recipes. She will cover calculating prices, finding the right audience, marketing specialty items, meeting sanitation guidelines, getting financing, and creating a hit. Take this class as your first step to more cash.

title: Medieval Art
description: A comprehensive overview of medieval art and architecture from the late antiquity to the late Gothic period in Europe and the Mediterranean region. Discusses the visual art and cultures of about 1000 years (around 400-1400 CE) including Late Roman, Early Christian, Byzantine, Romanesque, Islamic, and Gothic art. Offers diverse perspectives on the development, exchange, interaction, and influence between all these cultures through their visual arts. A field trip to a major museum is required.

title: Mathematics for the Technician
description: Covers basic mathematics skills specific to the technical field as well as examining measurement aspects beginning with angle and linear measurement, ratios and proportions, basic algebraic expressions, geometry as applied to area and volume calculations - as well as suspension geometry for vehicle alignment, and vibration analyzer data analysis.

title: Three Dimensional Design
description: Explores design fundamentals, analog and digital techniques, materials, and principles of organization as applied to three-dimensional art. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Computerized Acct - QB
description: Introduces the QuickBooks computerized-accounting software package. Covers how to design a company's accounting system for financial record keeping and how to enter data for the general journal, special journals, accounts receivable, accounts payable, payroll, and inventory.

title: Condominium - Buy Right!
description: Discover how to take advantage of the current buyer's market to purchase the home or condo of your dreams at an affordable cost. Realtor Rich Kaylor will teach you how to evaluate communities and condos for appreciation potential, negotiate the best price, make counter offers and steer clear of common traps and flaws in new construction. A certified home inspector will describe how to negotiate repair costs after an inspection. A mortgage specialist will describe ways to lower closing costs, estimate new tax bills, and uncover the best mortgages, such as those for first-time buyers.

title: Telecommunication Camp
description: Interested in all things media? The HFC Telecommunication Camp is the place for you! Our expert instructors will give you a front row seat to learn about the magic of film and television, how a real radio station works, the art of telling a story or delivering news through the written script, and a number of performance techniques including character voices, newscasts and interviews . Students will get hands-on opportunities to experience what their favorite actors, writers, editors, directors, newscasters and DJs do in the media field, from writing scripts, shooting footage, lighting, and editing to performing commercials, news and improv on camera and the radio. Your friends and family might even get to hear you on our award-winning radio station, WHFR. Make your mark on the world of media and have loads of fun in the process!

title: Travel Photography
description: If you're like most amateur photographers, you want to bring back vacation pictures that you'll enjoy looking at again and again. Whether you use a point-and-shoot camera, a single lens reflex, an APS, or a digital camera, this course will teach you photojournalistic techniques that will help you capture the spirit of the people and places you visit.

title: Exercise Physiology
description: Explores the physiological principles of exercise with emphasis on the circulatory, respiratory, and muscular systems. Discusses what changes occur to the body during exercise and as a result of regular exercise. Establishes a scientific basis for how to prescribe exercise appropriately. Laboratory activities include measurement of oxygen uptake, fat and muscle composition, anaerobic ability, and muscular strength.

title: Co-op in CIS
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: SolidWorks for MI Cataylst
description: This non-credit accelerated course combines DRAF 122, DRAF 124, and DRAF 126. Some topics to be covered are SolidWorks functionality, exploration of setting up drawing units, modifying dimensions on a sketch, 3D molding, creating, editing, and porting bills of materials, assembly-modeling procedures, utilizing Xpress tool, motion analysis, applying orthographic principles to mechanical drawings, and using the SolidWorks program. Registration is restricted to those students who are participants in the Michigan Catalyst program.

title: An Olde English Christmas
description: Enjoy a traditional English Christmas with Don Samull as he takes you on a slide tour of London and the surrounding area. In addition to "visiting" the sites, you'll enjoy an English holiday dessert and view a display of English holiday items. On Saturday you'll meet at an area restaurant to share an English holiday meal. Cost of the meal included in course fee.

title: Creating Web Pages II
description: Are you using HTML to its fullest potential? In this hands-on course, you'll become adept at using color, tables and frames to give your Web pages a more polished and professional look, improved navigation and more organization. Beef up the fun factor with audio and interactive forms, and learn how to save hours of time and effort with Cascading Style sheets.

title: Energy Industry Fundamentals
description: Covers basic emerging principles and concepts that affect the energy industry, compliance with safety and health procedures, how electric power and natural gas generation, transmission, and distribution work, and entry-level energy careers. Students will gain an understanding of the energy industry as a prerequisite to occupation-specific knowledge and an understanding of the careers available in the energy industry.

title: Jazzercise:Fitness
description: Welcome to Jazzercise, a total-body conditioning program designed to make you feel great and look terrific. Combining the art of jazz dance and the beat of the best new pop music, Colleen Gruse, a certified Jazzercise instructor, will lead you through this 60-minute aerobic program. Each class will include a warm-up segment, an aerobic segment, an aerobic cool-down, a muscle-toning segment, and a final cool-down. Join the fun, and leave feeling relaxed, refreshed, and revitalized! No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Nutrition
description: Emphasizes the importance of proper nutrition

title: Energy Systems Management
description: Presents the technical, economic, and regulatory aspects of energy efficient building energy management. Explores the latest energy and cost-reduction strategies. Focuses on laboratory exercises designed to simulate residential and commercial energy systems.

title: Intermediate Ballet III
description: This is the third of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of ballet technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in ballet history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and classical dance principles. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of this traditional and foundational dance art. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of ballet.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, placement, and turn-out, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Microbiology for AHS
description: For health service personnel not needing the extensive laboratory experience and breadth of material of BIO 251. Emphasizes the biology of medically important microbes, epidemiology and disease transmission, sterile technique, basics of immunity, the microbiology of wounds, and current regulations regarding blood-borne pathogens and bio-hazardous wastes. Three hours of lecture and two hours of lab per week.

title: Dearbornville Doctors
description: Discover the early physicians who cared for the people of Dearborn in pioneer days. Learn about doctors who lived in the community and practiced medicine - one of whom served as commandant of the old Detroit Arsenal in Dearbornville.

title: Dementia Caregiving
description: Are you caring for someone with dementia and find yourself at a loss as to how to keep them active and involved? Learn how one family has cared for their mother/wife at home for 16 years. In this workshop, Pricilla Davis, MAE, will share a variety of ways she has found successful in working with her own mother. Meet other care- givers and find new methods of creating a better environment for all.

title: Basic Video Production I
description: Many videos have similar themes (e.g. love, revenge, etc.) but the way they are structured should differentiate them from any other. Mr. Frank Jamal will teach you a logical and imaginative approach to visual story development. Get an introduction to the basic, essential elements that make up a story. Learn to structure your stories more effectively as you study concepts such as drama, character, act and scene. You will need a screenplay narrative, format, plot/story concept, and plot treatment.

title: Ask a Master Gardener
description: What can I grow in my shady areas? Is my soil a problem? How can I get rid of slugs? What kind of shrub is that in my front yard? For answers to these and your own specific questions, join Master Gardener Jane Geisler for an informative, fun evening of questions and answers. This is your chance to learn the basics of gardening from an expert while you interact with other gardeners. If you would like, bring along some "bragging photos" of favorite plants or designs.

title: Peyote Bead Bracelets
description: Learn peyote bead weaving, the popular single-needle handweaving technique, and create a distinctive, colorful bracelet using Delica seed beads and Swarovski crystal accents. $15 material fee payable to instructor in class.

title: Children's Theater Production
description: Beginning children's theatrical production class.

title: Goodbye to Pms, Mood Swings
description: If you are a woman coping with depression, hot flashes, migraines, thinning hair, memory loss, fibroids, fatigue, painful periods, or unexplained weight gain, hurry to this seminar. Dr. Mary Born will teach you how excess estrogen, deminished progesterone, environmental toxins, and nutritional deficiencies may be causing these symptoms. Learn proven strategies to restore balance to your body using healthy foods, herbal supplements, good fats, vitamins, minerals and non-prescription hormone creams. $5 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Weatherization Installer
description: This course will train students to install energy-efficiency measures to single family or 2-4 unit-homes using a variety of building science best practices to improve, safety, comfort, durability, indoor air quality, and energy efficiency. Course topics include job safety, identifying electrical, plumbing and roofing needs, preparing weatherization materials and maintaining tools, air sealing measures, loose fill and dense pack insulation installation, identification of mechanical systems, combustion safety hazards, and implementing weatherization scopes of work. OSHA, First-Aid/CPR, and EPA lead Safe RRP certifications as offered and math and communications courses.

title: Genealogy II
description: Are you encountering a seemingly insurmountable research obstacle? Do you want to know more about secondary genealogy sources and techniques? Patricia Gee, past president of the Dearborn Genealogical Society, will combine the latest internet technology with tried-and-true research strategies to help you find your ancestors. An optional research trip to the Library of Allen County in Fort Wayne (2nd largest genealogical library in the U.S.) will be discussed.

title: Main Print Read: Plant Layout
description: Introduces the techniques and procedures of plant layout and material handling. Discusses how to analyze and develop information in order to produce a plant layout. Covers print reading skills with emphasis on industrial equipment drawings and how to make simple plant layout drawings for the production of a part using basic drafting techniques.

title: Poetry Writing
description: Celebrate life's joys and clarify life's mysteries. Experiment with the tools of the craft: figurative language, imagery, sound devices and voice. Join us as we study ideas, devices and forms. Explore the creative process as you progress toward publication.

title: Capstone
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-281 to register for this course. Provides required assessment of knowledge and skill for students in their last semester of course work before completing their Associate of Arts degree in Telecommunication. Should be taken during the student's last term of required TCM classes.

title: Parent Care
description: If your kids have attention difficulties, you need a break. Take two hours a week to be gentle with yourself and nurture yourself the way you nurture your children. Make some time for yourself, so you come back to them refreshed and with more to give. Each week you will learn to give and receive a Serenity Ear Acupressure Treatment to help you relax and center. By the end of the series you will learn many different ways to nurture and treat yourself and your loved ones safely, without drugs. You will network with parents, educators and therapists. Bring tennis balls and socks. Wear comfortable soft clothes.

title: Advanced Clinical Education
description: This is fifth in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) competencies. Course emphasizes headword including skull, facial bones, paranasal sinuses, and increasing technical proficiency in all previously covered radiographic procedures. Students will continue to develop trauma radiography skills and advanced mobile radiography skills.

title: Light & Environ Sys for Interi
description: Introduces the fundamentals of lighting design and basic understanding of acoustical, electrical, plumbing, and climate control systems and their impact on environmental, energy and economic issues. Emphasizes lighting design process, principles and theories, light sources, lamps, and energy conservation.

title: Modern Wrld History
description: Studies world civilizations from the seventeenth century to the present. Emphasizes the Scientific, Political, and Industrial Revolutions and their global impact. Also explores cultural and political events that have affected national or international politics, such as the Enlightenment, nationalism, imperialism, and communism.

title: Microsoft Off Ste 2007 - Beg
description: Designed for the student who is relatively new to computing, this class will help you learn about Word, Excel, Access and PowerPoint. Whether you are looking to upgrade your job skills or plan to use Office at home, this class will help you get started. Lots of fun and practical exercises help you understand what the Office Suite is all about, and help you determine what direction you might like to take for future classes. A must for people who need to show proficiency as part of the job search. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.

title: Bldg/Construction Apprentice
description: This nine week, 315 hour course prepares participants to qualify for entry into the building trades apprenticeships. The course covers introduction to the trades, blueprint reading, First Aid/CPR, OSHA 30 certification, building science principles, power actuated tool certification, construction math, reading and locating information. This program is a collaborative program offering of the Michigan State AFL-CIO Human Resources Development (Michigan HRDI), Access for All and the Workforce Development Division of Henry Ford College.

title: Enviro As Clssrm Teach Ecology
description: This course is designed to provide teachers with examples of activities that will encourage their students to learn about the environment by using the Inquiry process. We will take on the role of elementary or middle school students so that you can learn how to best present these outdoor activities to your class. These activities correlate with the Science Standards and Benchmarks for elementary and middle school levels. Students that participate in these activities come to realize that science is all around them and they discover that first-hand while carrying out simulations", recording their findings, discussing their findings in small groups and working cooperatively to attempt to explain observed happenings-all within the framework of Scientific Inquiry.

title: Romance Writing Secrets
description: Learn how to write and sell romance novels. Facilitated by a bestselling, multi-published romance author, this course will guide you on your road to writing your first romance novel and getting it published. Topics to be covered include: character chemistry, the power of emotions, the art of romance, and handling love scenes. Plus you'll get the inside scoop on what it takes to get published to in today's romance market. This step-by-step course will put you well on the road to writing for love and money.

title: Art History Survey II
description: A survey of the development of visual arts from the early Renaissance to the present time. Offers broad background for understanding the visual art as a vehicle of communication in its functional context. Provides a concise appreciation to the development of socioeconomic, religious and political characteristics of the covered periods. Enables students to acquire a broad scope of cross-cultural understanding through continuous critical thinking perspective. Discusses how to comprehend and interpret works of art by examining the contextual characteristics of the work, its subject, functionality and the culture(s) that produced it. A field trip to a major museum and other appropriate sites is required to strengthen student knowledge.NOTE: It is recommended that a student who plans to take both ART-121 and ART-122 start with ART-121 first.

title: Ceramics III
description: An intermediate level course stressing refinement of ceramic construction techniques and design, primarily through utilitarian objects. This class also offers the opportunity for individual exploration of ceramic glaze and surface possibilities. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Operations & Supply Chain Mgmt
description: Explores the essential principles of Operations Management in Supply Chain Management (SCM). Emphasizes designing, planning, sourcing, processing, and distributing goods and services in SCM. Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able to demonstrate competency in the following SCM areas: operations and supply chain management, supply chain strategy and design, global supply chain procurement, demand forecasting methods, inventory management, scheduling and production design, lean systems, quality management, and project management.

title: PowerPoint in the Classroom
description: If you're an educator, this course will help you discover the exciting possibilities of using Power Point for and with your students. You'll master a variety of uses for PowerPoint in the classroom, and you'll be able to create compelling lessons and presentations filled with text, graphics, sound and video. You'll be able to use PowerPoint templates or create your own dynamic preentations that will increase interest in any subject you teach.

title: Wireless LANs
description: An intermediate-level course covering Wireless Networking. Discusses the IEEE 802.11 standards and the relationship to the OSI model. Also covers the planning, installation, configuration, troubleshooting, and securing of Wireless Networking products.

title: Monitor Child's Internet Use
description: Do you know about MySpace, Facebook and Friendster? Chances are they may be an essential part of your teens social networking. Learn what MySpace and its contemporaries are all about, how to navigate them, create profiles, find and monitor your child's activities, and more. Students must have a working knowledge in computers and the internet.

title: Industrial App. of Basic Math
description: Utilizes industrial shop problems to help students relate math to job situations. Topics include addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of whole and mixed numbers and common and decimal fractions; percentages, averages, and estimates; graphs, tables, and statistical measure; powers and roots; linear, angular, and circular measure; surface area, volume, and cubic measure; ratios and proportion; and metrics and metric conversion.Note: Summer semester for Skilled Trades and Apprenticeship starts in early May and ends inAugust. Students should consult the Class Schedule for specific course times anddates. 25 Jun 2014 2:41 PM Arlicia Summerville

title: Photoshop CS Essentials
description: Learn to use Photoshop CS, the premier software for image processing and photo retouching. Become acquainted with its powerful tools to select, crop, copy, rotate and resize images and photos. Employ layers to professionally manage your edits and the history palette to successfully undo changes. Use a variety of options to adjust tone, brightness and color; create shading and special effects; and save images in formats for print or Internet. Learn to touch up images with the pain brush and cloning tools, create fast-loading Internet graphics, and combine several photos to create a collage. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent experience. Workbook provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent experience. Workbook provided.

title: Math for Health Career
description: Explores the mathematical skills required for various careers in the health professions. Includes computational skills, essential algebraic concepts, problem-solving strategies, ratio and proportion, dimensional analysis, measurement and geometry, and an introduction to data analysis with problems chosen to represent those commonly encountered in health careers. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator.

title: Sheet Metal Radial & Triangula
description: Covers the development of geometrical elements of structures, their intersections by the radial line, triangulation methods of sheet metal layout, the drawing of development layouts, and the forming of cardboard or sheet metal models.

title: Drains, Wastes, and Vents
description: Introduces the Michigan State Plumbing Code and the proper selection of materials for the installation and repair of sewer, soil, waste, and vents systems. Covers proper procedures for the design, sizing, and construction of residential plumbing systems. Introduces commercial systems and plumbing cross-connections. The use of blueprints and isometric diagrams are reviewed throughout the course. Also includes how to test plumbing systems in their various stages.

title: Small Bus Bookkeeping
description: Continue learning the vast features of Quickbooks to learn how Quickbooks can be practically applied to your small business. Topics include inventory tracking, dealing with sales tax, creating purchase orders, receiving items, using QB classes, creating estimates, job costing and time tracking, working with customer & vendor profile lists, processing sales discounts & electronic payments, using Quickbooks for payroll, working with balance sheets and more. Prerequisite: Small Business Bookkeeping Using Quickbooks I.
prerequisite_text: Small Business Bookkeeping Using Quickbooks I.

title: Paint W Acrylics (ages 9-11)
description: The sky is the limit for opportunities for creativity! Come explore new worlds of color that include animals, flowers, abstracts and landscapes - perhaps even a blast into outer space. Art supplies are included. Wear clothes that you don't mind getting messy.

title: Media Writing
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-241 to register for this course. Offers an analysis of and practice with the forms and formats of mass media script writing: commercial, promotional, public service, instructional, and dramatic.

title: Ics 300: Ics Expand Incident
description: Building on your knowledge from the ICS 100 and ICS 200 courses, you will develop supervisory skills for use in expanding incidents. The course is designed to provide overall incident management skills rather than tactical expertise. You will implement the incident management process on a simulated Type 3 incident and develop an Incident Action Plan for a simulated incident. Pre-requisite: ICS 200: Single Resources & Initial Action Incidents.

title: Winter Birding in Michigan
description: This course introduces novices with an interest in birds to the basics of identification, observation, and recording their sightings. The course will begin with a classroom session to discuss some birding basics before taking to the field for the next four sessions. Sites to be visited will be announced during the class, all within about 50 miles of the HFCC campus, and will include opportunities to observe a good cross section of birds that spend late winter in Michigan. The course will provide participants with an opportunity to practice their identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor.

title: ALP for ELL:Reading & Writing
description: Intended for English language learners (ELLs) who need additional instructional support in order to be successful in ENG-131. ENG-095E is a co-requisite of ENG-131E as part of the Accelerated Learning Program (ALP) and provides writing, reading, and study skills instructional support of the ENG-131 curriculum. Course work will help ELL students develop as college writers and readers through discussion, workshops, homework, and reading and writing assignments that complement ENG-131 work.

title: Design Entrepreneur
description: This course is designed to help the individual navigate the business of design. Explores the fundamentals of design management, creative problem-solving techniques, and interpersonal skills for a beginning design entrepreneur who is building a client base.

title: Fantastic Gravies and Sauces
description: Make chicken and beef gravy the way Grandma did - without lumps! See the "Sauce King" Tim Dare demonstrate his secrets for turning a basic Bechamel mother sauce into Mornay, Chantilly, and cheese sauces. Also stir up a luscious lemon sauce and a 15-minute tomato sauce. Learn to pair sauces and gravies with meat and vegetable choices. Taste what you make and then dazzle your family with your favorite new creations. Bring your apron and your questions.

title: Kid's Keyboard 1
description: A beginning piano keyboard class for ages 7-11. This class will teach students to count, read music, and play simple songs. A student recital will be held at the end of class. Text included.

title: Theatre Appreciation
description: Explores the components, methods, and history of theatrical production. Subject areas may include but are not limited to acting, directing, and technical theatre.

title: Heating Technology
description: Prepares students to work on residential and light commercial gas, oil, and electric forced-air and hot-water heating systems and related equipment. Explains standard equipment and systems, how to provide service checks, and the preliminary skills needed to installation, repair, and replace heating equipment using appropriate service tools and instruments. Laboratory activities included.

title: Peyote Stitch for Beginners
description: The peyote stitch is an easy, basic stitch that may date back to Old Kingdom Egypt. It produces a flat piece of beadwork that can be fashioned into unique pieces of jewelry. Join Liz Thompson to create your own peyote bracelet by reading and beading from a pattern. $15 supply fee to instructor in class. Limited to 8.

title: Savor the Flavors of Hungary
description: According to Elizabeth Krajcz, who was born in Hungary and has cooked at the Hungarian American Culture Center for 20 years, hearty Dinebean goulash is a delicious favorite. In this class, learn how to make it the authentic Hungarian way. Then discover her secrets for making a torte topped with mocha frosting. Yum! Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Sterling Byzantine Bracelet
description: Discover the secrets of putting sterling silver links together to make a nifty Byzantine bracelet. You'll leave with a ready-to-wear bracelet that could be worn by both men and women. You might later use it as a charm bracelet. $28 materials fees due to instructor in class.

title: Pen & Ink Watercolor Washes
description: Increase your basic drawing skills and expand your knowledge of using pen and ink as a drawing medium. Join artist Saundra Weed for this six-week class and discover how to create artwork suitable for framing, journaling, illustrating books or making cards. $5 due to instructor for first night's materials. Supply list will be provided in class. Appropriate for all levels.

title: Drawing II
description: This is and intermediate college level drawing class that stresses drawing fundamentals through working with a variety of subject matter observed firsthand. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Polish Churches of Hamtramck
description: Visit the beautiful historic churches of Hamtramck, Detroit's Polish enclave, and then enjoy lunch at a restaurant in the area. Join your instructor, Don Samull, on a guided, self-driving and walking tour of the three major Roman Catholic churches of that city, as well as the quaint Polish National Catholic church. Maps showing where to meet will be sent to registered students prior to the tour. The cost of the meal is included.

title: Coordinate Jewelry Like a Pro
description: Design jewelry in "living color", by selecting the perfect mix of colors and shades. In this class you will learn how to complement and contrast with colors. You will receive a color wheel and swatches to that you may take home for future reference. $15 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Ballroom Boot Camp
description: You've always wanted to try it! It looks like fun; it is fun! Come alone or with a partner to learn the fundamentals of leading and following for such contemporary ballroom dances as the swing, foxtrot, waltz, and other familiar dances. Learn the moves that will get you noticed at your next wedding, party, or in a nightclub.

title: Topics in Theatre
description: Provides special study in the area of theatre, organized by style, production approach, historical period, or other criteria. Specific topics and any prerequisites are listed in the current semester's class schedule or may be obtained through the departmental office.

title: Intro to Industrial Hydraulics
description: Introduces the principles of fluid power as applied to industrial systems. Presents various hydraulic components and their functions within fluid power systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Conversational French 2
description: If you have completed Conversational French or have a basic knowledge of the language, continue developing your skills in this intermediate-level class.

title: Historic Dearborn: Arsenal
description: If you look carefully around downtown West Dearborn you will still find parts of the Federal Arsenal Grounds, which was moved from Detroit to Dearbornville in the 1830's. Still standing is the Commandant's Quarters at Michigan and Monroe. The old powder magazine, now the McFadden Ross House, is on Brady Street just north of Michigan Avenue. Mary MacDonald, our presenter, has been a long time student of the Arsenal, studying old records, journals, diaries and letters. She has even constructed a scale model. She will share rare old photos, stories and anecdotes of the people who lived and worked there.

title: Aromatherapy
description: For over 5000 years healers have recognized the therapeutic benefits of oils from fragrant plants such as lavender, eucalyptus, peppermint, jasmine, and chamomile. In this workshop you will learn about the theory and chemical properties of the essential oils and which ones are reputed to reduce stress, stimulate and energize, relieve minor ailments, and more. Rose Beres, an aromatherapy practitioner, will help you select and create your own personal blend to take home. $3 supply fee payable to the instructor.

title: Principles Radiation Biology
description: This lecture course provides an overview of the principles of the interaction of radiation with living systems. Radiation effects on molecules, cells, tissues, and the body as a whole are presented. Factors affecting biological response to radiation exposure are presented, including acute and chronic effects.

title: Basic Printmaking
description: 03 Aug 2012 11:13 AM Pauline Barbas So easy that even a child could do it! Learn a simple way to create block prints useful for small framed wall art, greeting or note cards, or decorative repeat patterns. Materials are inexpensive and will be available for use the first night of class for a fee of $6.00 paid to the instructor. Bring a #2 pencil, ballpoint pen, and an eraser to class.

title: Save $5000/Yr & Get Out Debt
description: Having trouble getting ahead? This class will help! Instructor Patrick Shearer will teach you a series of simple steps to save up to $5,000 per year. Then he will teach you a technique to use that extra money to get out of debt without refinancing or getting a second job. Participants are asked to bring a calculator, a list of your current monthly expenses and a summary of your current debts and minimum payments (which will not be shared in class). Patrick will walk you through a worksheet of a debt reduction plan.

title: More Orchids for Beginners
description: If you are passionate about orchids and want to expand your knowledge, join Suzanne Bucki of the Michigan Orchid Society for a dazzling slide show of Cattleya, Laelia, Oncidium, Angracoid and Vanda orchids. You will see how the shape and structure of these orchids differ, discover how to propagate them, and learn why certain species grow only in a specific region. When you fall in love with one of these exotic beauties, you will also find out how to meet its growth requirements so it will bloom in your home or greenhouse. Bring your questions.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Electrical diagnosis includes computer-controlled charging systems, battery, and starting systems, including one-touch and remote-start systems. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile.

title: Indust Appl Trigonomic Princip
description: Focuses on trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Covers trigonometric functions, solution of right triangles, solution of oblique triangles, and problem solving techniques.

title: Sheet Metal Appl Triangulation
description: Covers the development of geometrical elements of structures by the triangulation method of sheet metal layout. Includes the development of stretchouts and making cardboard or sheet metal models of transition pieces.

title: Jazz Improvisation 1
description: First of four semesters of jazz improvisation in which skills are developed through the study of jazz theory and the performances of a variety of jazz styles in a small group, jazz combo setting.

title: Strength Train & Phys Cond II
description: Explores the principles of intermediate and advanced strength and cardiovascular conditioning including evaluation of all components of physical fitness. Emphasizes flexibility, strength, cardiovascular conditioning, muscular endurance, and body composition. Also covers nutrition and exercise as it relates to weight management and disease prevention, and risk factors.

title: Photoshop Essentials & More
description: Continue developing your Photoshop CS skills for printing photos, creating illustrations, or enhancing websites. Discover some of the tricks professionals use to erase the background of a photo and replace it with another, solve brightness and contrast issues, add text to a photographic layout, restore damaged vintage photos, and use filters and various artistic enhancements. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, Photoshop Essentials or equivalent experience. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, Photoshop Essentials or equivalent experience. Text provided.

title: Spec Topics in Comp Info Syst
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current Computer Information System trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Machine Tool Blueprint-PAPP
description: An introduction to blueprint reading; interpretation of various kinds of lines, position of views, symbols conventions, dimensioning practices, sectioning, auxiliary views and symmetry with emphasis on techniques employed to show details in relation to assembly drawings.

title: Internal Combustion Engines
description: Introduces four-stroke engines by exploring principles of operation, compression ratio, piston displacement, operating tolerances, valve timing, horsepower and torque development along with adjustments, inspection, and troubleshooting procedures. Course topics help students prepare for ASE certification exams and State of Michigan licensure, but these exams/licensure are not included in this course. Laboratory activities.

title: Zumba Fitness
description: Eliminate the "work" in your workout. Zumba is an amazing combination of irrestible Latin music with dynamic, yet simple, exercise moves that is so much fun you're barely aware your getting an excellent, low impact, cardio workout. The beat of the music inspires you and keeps you going. Whether you're in great shape or a couch potato, or if you're seeking a change as much as a challenge, Zumba is for you! All abilities and fitness levels welcome.

title: Breakthrough for Health
description: A conference for the community and nurses. Price includes lunch & breaks. 5 nursing contact hours.

title: Customer Service
description: Examines the principal functions of a customer service representative, the skills needed to succeed in the workplace, and the knowledge to advance into a leadership role within the service industry. Emphasizes effective communication skills, positive attitudes, problem solving, strategies for coping with challenging and dissatisfied customers, retention of customers, methods of measuring customer satisfaction, technology and customer service, and motivational techniques. NOTE: Upon successful completion of this course, students may choose to take an exam for a National Professional Certification in Customer Service from the National Retail Federation (NRF) Foundation. This exam is not included in this course.

title: Exercise Techniques Lab
description: Provides the opportunity for application of the techniques learned in PTA 118. Focuses on therapeutic exercise procedures utilized by physical therapist assistants as well as assessment of joint motion and muscle strength, implications of range of motion (ROM) and muscle testing for exercise prescription, types of exercise, basic exercise programs, and the use of exercise equipment. Guided lab setting.

title: Audio Recording Technology 2
description: Provides hands-on experience and guided practice in digital recording techniques, advanced digital audio mixing and mastering, and independent problem solving in the recording process. Covers recording sessions, computer files, microphone usage and placement, digitizing principals, audio acquisition options, midi integration, preparing complex digital audio files for duplication, advanced techniques in mixing room acoustics, preparing audio for mixing, software plug-ins, troubleshooting audio problems, equalization, stereo imagery, noise limitation, bit conversion, and session management. Students work in an actual digital recording studio recording a wide variety of instrumental and vocal ensembles. Builds upon the basic competency developed during MUS-191 and MUS-192. Applies advanced skills, workflows, and concepts of recording, editing and mixing on an Avid Pro Tools system learned in MUS-292: Pro Tools 110.

title: Fancy Cookie Art Workshop
description: Instructor will collect $5.00 materials fee first day of class.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: As you watch the prices for produce soar at the market, do you wish you knew how to grow your own vegetables? Let Laurie Jeffery, certified Master Gardener, help get you started! Learn how to pick the site for your garden, what to plant, how to plant, how to care for your garden and how to harvest your crop.

title: Chorus 1
description: First of four semesters of Concert Choir including preparation and performance of a broad spectrum of choral music from classical to popular, show tunes, and jazz. At least two performances are scheduled each semester, sometimes accompanied by band or orchestra. These courses are open to college students and adult members of the community. New singers may audition before or during the first week of class. No preparation is necessary, but the demonstration of the ability to sing in tune and hold a part (soprano, alto, tenor, or bass) is required.

title: Zumba Fitness
description: Zumba is an amazing combination of irresistible Latin music with dynamic, yet simple, exercise moves that is so much fun youre barely aware youre getting an excellent, variable impact, cardio workout. The beat of the music inspires you and keeps you going. Whether youre in great shape or a couch potato, or if youre seeking a change as much as a challenge, Zumba is for you! All abilities and fitness levels welcome. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Indust Appl of Geometric Prin
description: Covers the fundamental principles of plane geometry with emphasis on application to the industrial environment. Explores angular, circular, linear, area, and volume measurement in relation to the types of geometric figures and configurations found in industry.

title: Land Your Dream Job
description: Blow away your competition with a resume that generates more interviews and interviews that result in higher salary offers. Learn from a professional resume writer the latest techniques for developing and marketing a winning resume in today's technological environment. You'll also discover strategies that will help you successfully interview, get the job you want, and then negotiate the very best job offer! Feel free to bring your current resume and information about any job of interest to you.

title: Paramedic IV
description: Introduces pathophysiology of cardiovascular disease and discusses how to recognize and treat the associated dysrhythmias. Covers the following topics in a broad manner: anatomy and physiology (review), general pathophysiology, geriatric considerations, assessment and management of emergencies in the areas of hematology, gastroenterology, toxicology, substance abuse, urology and nephrology, behavioral and psychiatric disorders, and environmental emergencies.

title: Elementary Spanish Conversatio
description: Focuses on expanding students' ability to understand and speak in Spanish. Emphasizes everyday topics of conversation, including current events. Is transferable but not a substitute for a basic language requirement. May be taken concurrently with Spanish 132, 231 or 232.

title: Interpersonal Communication
description: This course explores the process of communication between individuals in formal and informal, face-to-face and digital settings across family, social, and work groups. Examines the social, linguistic, cultural, and psychological factors impacting interpersonal communication.

title: Process Industry Health, Env
description: Offers exposure to the various safety, health, environmental, and safety issues directly associated with the process industries. Includes hazard recognition, types of hazards, cyber security, engineering controls, administrative controls, personal protective equipment, safety-related equipment, first aid, and governmental regulations. Emphasizes extensive hands-on experiences.

title: Co-op in Science
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major in STEM to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Am Gov:Democrat Participation
description: Examines principles and problems of American political institutions, including the role of government and politics in society, the balancing of liberty with authority, and the theories of the state. Probes mechanisms of citizen empowerment and control (public opinion, pressure groups, political parties, elections), and formal structure. Public policy as a means to meet societal needs is analyzed and evaluated along with the tools that citizens can use to influence the public policy process. Also examines foreign and domestic public policy and contemporary events. Includes how to write effective arguments as well as how to formally present political arguments.

title: Microsoft Excel 2003
description: Discover how Excel can help you improve your effectiveness as a teacher.

title: Fantasy Character Design
description: Develop the characters of your wildest imagination. Students will be introduced to anatomy basics and create a fantasy character based on facial expression, character development, and lighting. The class will discuss and add visual cues that enhance the narrative of the character. Campers will be encouraged to draw inspiration from contemporary characters in movies, video games and/or animee, as well as incorporate science fiction or fantasy elements within their work. A gallery exhibition will show of the work of class participants at the end of the final class session.

title: ELL Communication 5
description: Develops intermediate-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-106C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: German Soup & Potato Pancakes
description: Come join us in a small village in the County of Hessen in Germany. A hearty peasant wurst (smoked sausage) soup and authentic home made kartoffel puffer (potato pan cakes) are on the menu.

title: Discover Ham Radio
description: Ever thought about becoming an amateur radio operator or "ham" as they're often called? Licensed amateur radio operators communicate with each other in nearby places, across the country, around the world, or even with astronauts in outer space via wireless technology. Join more than 600,000 hams in the US by preparing to pass the FCC Amateur Radio Exam. Sponsored by the Motor City Radio Club this 11-week class teaches you all you need to know to pass the test. For more information log onto www.w8mrm.org.

title: Camp Journalism
description: Youth will receive a crash course in writing for print, during this week long class. Youth will interview subjects and produce news, feature and editorial ariticles that capture their perspective on local, regional and national topics.

title: To Journey the Medicine Wheel
description: Native Americans define "medicine" as anything that improves one's connection to the Great Creator Spirit and to all life. The stores that take you around to the Great Creator Spirit and to all of life. The stories that take you around the wheel are dedicated to keeping a healthy balance between the body, mind and Spirit. Your "medicine" brings out personal power, strength and a deep understanding of the interconnectedness of all of life. This walk around the wheel will introduce you to yourself at every turning point of your life. Whether you are in the middle of a wilderness or a bustling city, the Medicine Wheel and its" teachings will help you find the gentle balance that comes from walking the path of the "good red road."

title: Still Life: Focus on White
description: Learn to control color mixing in the white family.

title: Kitchen Design Studio
description: A studio course integrating the interior design process into a large-scale kitchen design project focusing on functional and aesthetic elements and the integral relationship to user needs. Incorporates human factors and Universal Design principles.

title: Microsoft Off Ste 2007 - Beg
description: Designed for the student who is relatively new to computing, this class will help you learn about Word, Excel, Access and PowerPoint. Whether you are looking to upgrade your job skills or plan to use Office at home, this class will help you get started. Lots of fun and practical exercises help you understand what the Office Suite is all about, and help you determine what direction you might like to take for future classes. A must for people who need to show proficiency as part of the job search. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.

title: Tai Chi Chu'an
