description: Tai Chi, sometimes referred to as "meditation in motion," is an ancient Chinese martial art consisting of a sequence of slow, graceful motions that relax and strengthen the mind, body, and spirit. An excellent form of stress management suitable for people of all ages, Tai Chi has been shown to reduce blood pressure and strengthen bones. Join an experienced Tai Chi instructor and discover the benefits yourself. No experience needed. Wear loose, comfortable, modest clothing and shoes with no or low heels. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Accupressure Weight Mgmt
description: A continuation of CEHFS 2137.

title: Seated Exercise
description: Safely strengthen and tone your muscles while sitting in a chair! Low impact exercises, done while sitting in or holding onto a chair, will strengthen muscle groups and improve muscular endurance. See how quickly daily activities become easier! Benefits can include: improved posture, stronger bones, and decreased risk of injuries to the body. It's never too late to start rebuilding muscle tissue. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights.

title: Coordinated Edu Exp Auto Svc 4
description: Designed to offer practical work experience within the Automotive Technology and Service field through participation in a supervised coordinated education program. This course integrates work experience with classroom and Lab instruction.

title: Intro to Digital Scrapbooking
description: Digital Scrapbooking is the hottest new way to capture moments and create layouts that will captivate your family and loved ones for generations. Learn the fundamentals of digital photography, equipment and scrapbook elements. Even if you've never put together a scrapbook page, you'll get tips you need to start organizing your photographs into exciting scrapbook pages right on your computer. Bring your pictures to class. Intro to PCs: Part 2 or the equivalent knowledge.

title: Modern American History
description: Covers the United States since the Civil War Period.

title: All About Annuals
description: Enhance your garden with the consistent, vivid, diverse colors only annuals can provide. Learn how to choose the right plants for your garden site, including some that will bloom right up to Thanksgiving. A Master Gardener will discuss preparing and amending your soil, proper watering methods, and precise fertilization techniques. She'll also tell you about typical problems you may encounter and how to fix them.

title: Auto Fuel Management Systems
description: Introduces the types of fuels and air/fuel ratio requirements of the various fuels used in current automotive engines, along with the construction and operation of fuel delivery systems. Discusses fuel pumps, fuel lines, fuel filters, and storage tanks. Fuel system types include returnable electronic fuel injection, returnless electronic fuel injection, and gasoline direct fuel injection. Also discusses elements of mechanical and electronic automotive emissions. This course also covers the use of diagnostic scan tools and covers vehicle programming principals.

title: Self-Publish Your Own Book
description: Do you have an idea, concept or story that would make an excellent book? If so, learn practical, realistic information to achieve your dream. Find out where to get books printed inexpensively and quickly, how to get a bar code, ISBN, and Library of Congress number, and how to get your book sold in major bookstores. $10 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Power Point for Beginners
description: This hands-on class is designed for beginning PowerPoint users. Learn to identify the different PowerPoint screens. Use features in the Drawing Toolbar and Picture Toolbar to create, modify, group and reposition images. Insert clipart. Reposition slides in the Slide Sorter view. Add text to speaker notes. Select different print options. Identify and use features in the master slide. Explore design layouts and templates.

title: Sign for Fun
description: Learn to listen with your eyes in this fun, easy, interactive class. Anne Bolander, long-time instructor and member of the deaf community, will teach you the basics of conversational sign language using the Pidgin Method (a combination of English sentence format and ASL shortcuts). Play games, eat snacks and tell stories in an informal class designed to get you "talking." Author of I Was #87, Anne will also share some of her moving experiences growing up as a deaf child. Come alone or enroll a friend or family member (children welcome) too.

title: Gourmet Candied Bacon Treats
description: Bacon and chocolate? It's the latest (and very popular) Gourmet treat! Create Sweet n' Salty Chocolate Bacon Kabobs, Chocolate Bacon Bark, Rocky Road Bacon Bites, and Chocolate Enrobed Apple Slices with Candied Bacon Bits! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12 - $15).

title: Conversational Chinese for Fun
description: Whether for business or fun, learning Chinese will be much more interesting if you know the basics of the beautiful Mandarin Chinese language and culture. Instructor Liping Prus will cover survival phrases, how to get directions, ordering in a restaurant, what to say when shopping and purchasing, how to get transportation, basic language differences between Asian and Western ways of thinking, unique cultural issues, and more. Plan to learn and have fun at the same time!

title: ICS 300 & 400
description: This course provides training on and resources for personnel who require advanced application of the Incident Command System (ICS). You will gain insight into the incident/event management process of ICS, learn how to implement the incident management process on a simulated Type 3 incident, and develop an Incident Action Plan. Special management challenges of an Area Command and multi-agency coordination systems are also covered.

title: Life Work Planning
description: Examines and analyzes one's own resources, strengths and constraints to determine what is realistic for future change. Covers decision-making tools which can be utilized for effective planning resulting in alignment between one's life and work goals. Potential challenges are examined through problem-solving techniques.

title: Sign for Fun 2
description: Increase your signing skills and understanding of the deaf culture in this unique intermediate-level class. Working with Anne Bolander, author and hearing-impaired instructor, you will become fully immersed in communicating with the deaf community. If you attended Sign for Fun or have a basic knowledge of signing, you won't want to miss this opportunity!

title: Theatre Experience III
description: This is the third of six experience-based one-credit courses where students work onstage, backstage, or in front-of-house operations for theatre productions during the semester. The course includes instructor presentation of specific elements of application though lecture, lecture/demonstration and/or laboratory experience.The production work for Theatre Experience III builds on the production work completed in Theatre Experience II. In this course, the student will practice and refine techniques learned in the previous Theatre Experience course in addition to learning new parts, backstage practices, etc.

title: African and Afro American Art
description: Provides a broad overview of the history of visual culture in different regions of continental Africa. Discusses different civilizations, which flourished in different parts of Africa from pre-historic time to the present. Offers study to native spiritual and ritualistic beliefs along with objects associated with and used in these various rituals and involved visual materials such as masks, helmets, costumes, weapons, body art, and other symbolic objects. Discusses African art in the Diaspora with focus on African artists in the Americas and the emerging African American art in North America. A field trip to a major museum is required.

title: Prin Macro Economics
description: Examines the theoretical operation of the economy as a whole. Covers the elements of a free market system, the measurement of macroeconomic performance, the creation and control of money, alternative models of government intervention to impact business cycles, economic growth, and international trade.

title: Personal Injury Litigation
description: Explores civil litigation in the context of a personal injury or product liability action. Introduces the substantive laws of negligence, intentional tort, personal-injury, and product liability.

title: CCNA: Networking II
description: The second in a series of three courses designed to help prepare students for entry-level networking jobs and the CCNA (Cisco Certified Network Associate) certification. Focuses on switching technologies and router operations that support small-to-medium business networks and includes wireless local area networks (WLANs) and security concepts. Students learn key switching and routing concepts. Students perform basic network configuration and troubleshooting, identify and mitigate LAN security threats, and configure and secure a basic WLAN. Note: course topics help students prepare for the CCNA certification exam, but the exam is not included in this course. Industry certification exams are administered by an independent testing agency.

title: Business Financial Tools
description: Designed especially for the business owner whose talents are not necessarily in finance, this workshop will teach you topics including cash flow, income statement, balance sheet, and ratios. An accurate accounting of your company's finances is basic to the success of your small business.sign up today! Textbook included.

title: Make Your Own Pet Treats
description: Has the recent pet food scare made you more cautious about what you feed your pets? In class you will create your own cat and dog friendly treats and know exactly what goes into them. These recipes are all veterinarian approved and have short preparation times. The instructor, Acyneith Swain-Grant will provide you with a list of foods that might be harmful for pets. She also provides references on pet nuitrition. Be a hero for your pet when you arrive home with freshly made treats.

title: The Healing Power of Crystals
description: As children we loved to pick up rocks and play with them, little did we know that rocks, crystals and gemstones have unique powers to heal on 4 levels: Physical, Mental, Emotional and Spiritual. In this class, we will explore several of the popular gemstones and their healing power, learn the different ways to cleanse the crystal, and see methods of programming and cleaning the crystal. We will also talk about Aura Fields and how to heal yourself and others using different crystals. Kristy will provide 3 different crystals to students for $10 payable in class.

title: Energy Fusion
description: Sculpt your muscles, boost your energy level, and burn calories in this class designed for fun and fitness. You'll start the class with an invigorating warm-up, followed by simple low-impact footwork for the hips and bootie, abdominal exercises to strengthen core muscles and light hand weights training for additional sculpting. All will be infused with yoga to add stretch and flexibility. Bring a mat, water and towel to class. All levels welcome.

title: Basic eBay
description: Thinking about getting started with eBay? Come learn the fundamentals of buying and selling on eBay including how to get started, research your item, pricing, shipping, feedback and more. If you are ready for a great overview of the buying and selling process - plus specific tips and strategies - sign up now! Basic computer and internet skills are required.

title: Chocolate Gourmet Confections
description: Make and take home melt-in-your-mouth chocolate enrobed marshmallows, buttery English toffee clusters, crunchy pretzels drenched in chocolate and covered in sinfully sweet toppings, chewy, chunky Rocky Road, decadent bark, chocolate covered Oreo and chocolate chip cookies, and much, much more! Learn how to professionally package and sell your culinary treats! all or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list. (approx. $12-15)

title: Ear Wires for Beginners
description: Make your own pierced ear wires for a fraction of what you'd pay for them in retail stores. Bring your tools to class and learn several techniques for making ear wires. $4 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Intermediate Excel Workshop
description: Expand your Microsoft Excel 2003 capabilities. Learn to name cells and ranges, work with charts and graphs, use advanced functions and formatting features. Discover how to display, hide, edit and delete cell notes. Become skilled at keeping data secure by protecting cells and creating passwords. Learn how to work with multiple worksheets and workbooks. Streamline your work with templates and macros. Excel Workshop Intro or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: Public Policy: Topics & Analys
description: A special topics course that allows students to explore issues of public policy. Focuses on the basics of public policy including agenda setting, policy formulation, policy implementation, and policy evaluation. Themes may vary. Themes may be specific such as healthcare policy or they might be more general such as state and local economic policy. Guest speakers.

title: Coordinated Edu Exp Auto Svc 3
description: Designed to offer practical work experience within the Automotive Technology and Service field through participation in a supervised coordinated education program. This course integrates work experience with classroom and Lab instruction.

title: Pcs for Seniors & Beginners- 2
description: Continue building on the skills introduced in Part I and learn to create a folder on a diskette; save a WordPad file to a folder on a diskette; move a file from one folder to another on a diskette; and create, move, and restore a folder on the Desktop. In addition, you will touch on the basics of using the Internet, including browsing to locate a web site, using common search engines, and sending and receiving e-mail messages. Textbook included. Prerequisite: CLNC 2061.
prerequisite_text: CLNC 2061.

title: Gourmet Cupcake Workshop
description: Transform 12 ordinary cupcakes into decadent, sinfully sweet gourmet treats! First you'll create 6 Double Stuffed Cupcakes, over-filled with a variety of luscious fillings, and covered with lots of sweet and crunchy toppings. Then you'll make 6 Candy Cup Cakes with multiple layers of moist cake, assorted candies (including chocolate), and decadent fillings, all displayed in cute serving cups! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12 - $15).

title: Coupon-Ology
description: You will learn the basics of couponing - where to find them and how to use them at your local grocery stores. We will look at current grocery store sale ads and apply what we learn to the sale prices so we can see how we can maximize our savings. Join Elvira Rivera, the "Coupon Connoisseur", and learn the do's and don'ts of the coupon connection.

title: Music Fundamentals
description: This course explains how to read music, including notes, clefs, rhythms, time signatures, scales, intervals, and basic chords. This course is recommended for the student who has a minimal background in music.

title: Culinary Camp 5101
description: This introductory course teaches students the basic aspects of cooking through a wide variety of hands-on activities. Students will prepare dishes in a professional kitchen and will be introduced to kitchen fundamentals, such as safety, sanitation, and different cooking methods. Initiatives include farm to fork, nutrition and healthful eating, a day in the bake shop, and kids taking over the kitchen.

title: Digital Photography I
description: An introductory-level course on the basics of digital imaging where creativity is emphasized. Offers key concepts such as (manual) camera capture, basic file management, 'non-destructive' image processing, and printing which help foster the foundations for visual communications, new media disciplines, or a fine art practice. A student may wait until the first class meeting to make arrangements to buy/use a camera. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Fancy Sterling Findings
description: No more searching for that special clasp for your jewelry or trendy ear wires! Learn how to hand form sterling silver into earring wires, free-form charms, and bracelet or necklace clasps. $20 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: CCNA Routing & Switching
description: This course is an intensive coverage of the skills needed to install, operate, verify a basic IPv4 & IPv6 network. Students will focus on the fundamentals and how to build simple LAN's, establish internet connectivity, troubleshoot VLAN issues, characteristics, theory based platform & virtual labs to simulate real world experience. Upon completion of this course, students will be eligible to sit for the CCNA certification exam, specifically the CCNA Routing & Switching, and earn their industry certification. The course curriculum stisfies the requirements needed to obtain employment and/or career advancement. The college cannot guarantee that a student will become certified. Certification costs are included in the cost of training. Eligibility requirements are subject to change by the certification testing body without prior knowledge given to the College.

title: WHFR Staff Training
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-189 to register for this course. Provides an orientation to the non-commercial, educational radio station licensed to the college. Covers essential station rules and procedures, operation of equipment, and basic performance skills. Course is open to all students but is required of those who wish to join the staff and assist in the operation of WHFR.

title: Mountain Dulcimer Workshop
description: Isn't it time you did something just for the fun of it? Indulge yourself by experiencing the rich musical tradition of Appalachia in this out-of-the-ordinary, hands-on mountain dulcimer workshop. Join Joe Zsigray to discover how easy it is to play this unforgettable, three-stringed American instrument. Included is your own mountain dulcimer. No musical training or experience necessary. All ages welcome.

title: Intro to Basic Electricity
description: This course is an introduction to various elements of basic electricity including the identification of electrical symbols as well as interpretation of schematics, cross referencing prints, interpreting sequential function charts, line drawings and time charts.

title: Meditation
description: Begin exploring ways to reduce stress, improve mental clarity, and create deep states of relaxation. Learn simple, effective breathing, visualization, and relaxation techniques that can help you develop a new sense of peace and tranquility. You may even lower your blood pressure!

title: Chinese Cooking
description: Chinese food need never again be a once-a-week restaurant treat. Learn to prepare your own authentic Chinese meals. Ping Bai, a graduate of Beijing Teaching College, will teach you how to prepare an authentic Chinese meal of pot stickers, peal meatballs and fried noodles. You'll also learn a bit about the culture, including the use of chopsticks. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Fast Meals Kids Will Eat - New
description: Beat the clock with time-saver meals your hungry kids will enjoy. Chef Tim Dare, father of two young boys, will share his insider tips for preparing healthy meals that his sons love - even the picky eater. Explore simple pasta recipes, versatile potato pancakes, quesadillas, easy cheesy vegetables and more. Create fast family meals without opening a box!

title: Wines of Michigan
description: Come savor the taste of a number of Michigan wines, including many available in limited quantities, and learn how and why they have received national recognition. David Creighton, your instructor, has spent more than 25 years studying, tasting, collecting and judging wines. You will find his enthusiasm infectious as he introduces you to the basics of wine production and compares and contrasts Michigan wines with those of other regions in terms of grape varieties, soil and climate. Cheese and bread will be served with the wine. What a pleasant way to end the week! (Note: This course focuses specifically on recent Michigan wine vintages, not on wine tasting in general.)

title: Real Estate Law
description: Learn about the relevant issues in today's real estate market. In this 30-hour course, you will cover contract law, real estate property law, tenant/landlord issues, civil rights and much more. This course may be applied to the hours required for a real estate broker's license. Textbook included.

title: Fuss-Free Party Foods
description: Appetizers...hors d'oeuvres...finger foods. Whatever you call them, they're not just a before-dinner treat any more. They can carry a party all by themselves. Whether you're planning a graduation party, shower or other get together, let Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estate, show you some of his favoite easy, but elegant party foods. Come hungry.

title: Facility Billing Concepts
description: Focuses on the billing process unique to health care facilities. Examines the hospital billing environment, coding, payment methods, and Uniform Billing Forms for Hospitals claims along with billing simulations.

title: Surgical Techniques I
description: Laboratory course designed to develop basic aseptic and sterile techniques used in the perioperative care of the surgical patient. This course focuses on the surgical scrub, gowning and gloving, setting up a sterile field, draping, handling of surgical instruments, and surgical counts. Students practice techniques in a guided lab setting.

title: Compound Angles/Adv Projection
description: Focuses on the mastery of solid trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Includes the solution of solid trigonometric problems using graphic and analytical solutions and problem solving techniques. Requires modeling clay.

title: Holiday Sweets & Tiny Treats
description: Get ready for a one-of-a-kind holiday cooking extravaganza. Just in time for the holidays, Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will amaze you with this part cookie exchange, part hors d'doeuvres delight class. You'll learn to bake and display a variety of cookies, including angel wings, birds next, a Polish treat and more. Then take some home to enjoy. You'll also make a unique Christmas tree stand suitable for displaying various hors d'oeuvres. Don't miss this one! Bring an apron. All supplies included.

title: Pump It Up!
description: Health and fitness experts tell us that those who regularly 'pump it up' increase lean muscle mass and improve their overall health. If you are ready for a leaner, fitter body, join this 8-week program designed to help tone and strengthen the major muscle groups. Learn from an American Council on Exercise certified Personal Trainer the proper techniques for fitness evaluation, Nautilus and hand weight training, and aerobic conditioning.

title: Theatre Capstone
description: Provides the required assessment of knowledge and skills for students in their last semester of course work for the Theatre Pathway of the Associates of Arts Degree in Liberal Arts.

title: PowerPoint: "Make Nice"
description: So you already know how to use the tools in PowerPoint - but somehow your work still looks simple or awkward. The instructor in an award-winning editor/writer. She will share strategies to grab attention with graphics, photos and text that are modified and placed effectively in your slide presentation. These techniques can be applied to printed material, such as newsletters and flyers as well.

title: Press Working Fundamentals
description: Discusses basic metal deformation theory, presses and ancillary equipment, die construction, and die component identification. Covers various die types and draws the various detail components using both traditional and computer-aided drafting.

title: Basic Lawn Care
description: You don't have to become a lawn expert to have a healthy lawn. Just follow the tips of Advanced Certified Master Gardener Nancy Fedon and you'll be well on your way. In this seminar you will learn how to effectively control weeds, pick and apply fertilizers, manicure the lawn, maintain the lawn mower, properly water a lawn and more.

title: Intro to Managerial Accounting
description: Builds on content presented in BAC-131. Covers in-depth financial statement analysis as well as managerial accounting. Also examines cost behavior, cost-volume profit analysis, business planning and accounting controls, and how accounting information is used in managerial decision-making.

title: Introduction to Sociology
description: Introduces the sociological approach to understanding culture and society. Examines human behavior in a group context, focusing on the prominent influence of culture and social structure on individuals. Connections between individuals and a variety of social institutions, such as the economy, government, and mass media, are examined and compared in U.S. society and on a global scale. Focuses on cultural diversity and social inequalities, such as those related to class, race, ethnicity and gender.

title: Business Math
description: Presents basic math problems in business scenarios. Coursework applies mathematical concepts in various personal and business situations such as payroll, taxes, bank reconciliations, profit/loss, commissions, discounts, depreciation, interest, annuities, and loans.

title: Underground Distribution
description: Designed as an introduction to operation and maintenance of underground electrical distribution systems. Electrical theory application for various distribution cables and underground cabinet terminations, cable splicing techniques and troubleshooting of underground circuits are explored. Distribution transformer applications are discussed. Students will be able to identify and demonstrate personal and electrical safety procedures utilized in switching, isolating, testing and diagnostic applications.

title: CATIA V5 Level II
description: Builds on the CATIA V5 concepts introduced in DRAF 123. Expands upon some workbenches demonstrated in the previous course and others will be introduced. Topics include: advanced part design, advanced assemblies, kinematics simulations, the use of the specification tree, creating design tables, and using component catalogs.

title: Filing Your 2010 Tax Return
description: Do you know what new tax laws and tax credits may effect your income tax return? What do you do if you need to file an extension? Jonathan Goldberg will provide some important tax tips to help you file your taxes properly and get all the deductions and credits you deserve.

title: Continuing Pilates
description: Continue sculpting your body in this class design- ed for those who have taken the beginning pilates class or already are familiar with the method. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfort- able, stretchable clothing.

title: A Glimpse of Hungary
description: Hungary is known for its exotic, tasty and spicy food. Few people know more about preparing it than Elizabeth Krajcz, who was born in Hungary and has cooked at the Hungarian American Culture Center for 20 years. She'll teach you how to make a true Hungarian meal of beef goulash, cucumber salad, chicken paprikash and palacsinta (crepes filled with apricots). Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Golf: Adv Group Indoor Lessons
description: Want to follow up from the first group class? Or do you need to brush up your skills and get comfortable with the game? Tom Rinna will focus on correct setup, backswing (coiling) and downswing (uncoiling). He has lots of drills and exercises to help you perfect your short game. Bring your clubs.

title: Introduction to CNC
description: An entry-level course covering the basic concepts of computer numerical control (CNC). Also explores set-up, operation, and programming of a CNC mill and CNC lathe. Laboratory activities.

title: Family Sys: Theories & Practic
description: This course further explores mental health theories used when working with clients. The focus of the course is on family systems theory. The course will focus on assessment procedures, including genograms and ecomaps, family sculpturing, art therapy, and treatment planning.

title: Real Estate for Begin Investor
description: Buy Low-Sell High. 70% of the richest people in the world made their money in real estate. So where do you begin? This class will allow you to understand why this is the "best market" to invest in real estate and how to get "deals on foreclosures" and the advantages of being a landlord in today's market. $30 informational packet available from instructor.

title: ELL American Culture 4
description: Develops low-intermediate-level knowledge of American culture, history, and geography for non-native English speakers. Focuses on both content (American culture) and format (academic lecture and note-taking). Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-105A. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Lighthouses of the Great Lakes
description: Enjoy an unforgettable afternoon as you explore the beauty and history of some of the 350 lighthouses that are found on the Great Lakes. Wayne Sapulski, author of Lighthouses of Lake Michigan, Past and Present, will show slides of these fascinating sites and discuss their critical role in Michigan maritime history. You will view successful lighthouse restorations, as well as lighthouses that are doomed to ruin. Held in the Edison Room of Henry Ford Village, 15101 Ford Road, Dearborn. Instructor's lighthouse book ($30) available for purchase and autographs after class. Register by Oct. 8.

title: Spanish in the Classroom
description: Learn the essential Spanish for teachers so you can bridge the communication gap with your Spanish-speaking students and parents! Rest assured that that this course won't be your typical high school Spanish course. We've tossed out long vocabulary lists and complex grammar rules and replaced them with games and stories to make learning easy, painless, and even fun. In addition to basic greetings and general Spanish vocabulary, you'll master words and phrases that directly relate to the classroom, such as classroom objects, front-office receptions, action verbs and commands, study skills, registration, course names, school rules and discipline, and parent-teacher conference vocabulary. By the end of this course, you'll be well on your way to being a Spanish speaker and communicating effectively with the Spanish-speakers all around you. ¡Vamos! (Let's go!)

title: Ms Office 2007 for Begin & Sen
description: This class is a fun, easy way for beginners to learn how to use the new Office 2007. The class presents a gentle introduction tgo the Office suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Access). Learning is reinforced with plenty of illustrations and practical, hands-on projects and exercises. Text included.

title: Paramedic Clinical I
description: Provides a structured clinical experience in hospital and field environments where designated tasks in specific topic areas must be completed. This set of clinical rotations emphasizes intravenous access and global patient management. Also covers a variety of cardiac diseases and dysrhythmias.

title: Spec Topic Mach Tool Technolog
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current Machine Tool Technology. Specific special topics are be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic. Course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree or certificate.

title: Radiographic Positioning II
description: This lecture/laboratory course is one of a four course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This course provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for shoulder girdle, lower extremity, and pelvic girdle. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Chinese Medicine: Seasons
description: Explore the dynamic fluctuation of life as it is expressed in nature and in your life. Using concepts from traditional Chinese medicine, determine your five-phase type and learn to live wisely according to life's daily, weekly and yearly cycles. Discover wonderful oriental foods and learn how to choose ordinary Western foods to manage weight and build a healthy lifestyle. Wear loose, comfortable clothing. Prerequisite: "Chinese Medicine in Your Life: an Introduction" is strongly recommended.
prerequisite_text: "Chinese Medicine in Your Life: an Introduction" is strongly recommended.

title: Directed Study
description: Allows individualized, advanced study under faculty direction in various subfields of theatre content. A Directed Study may be used to delve further into a subject than offered within the program, to individually explore a topic not otherwise accessible to a student, or to pursue an individual "practice-as-research" project. Students must obtain permission from a full-time theatre faculty member and have an instructor who has agreed to serve as mentor before the directed study will be approved. May be taken twice for credit, six hours maximum, and course subject must be different each time.

title: Food Safety in the Marketplace
description: Look at food from a viewpoint that you might not have considered before with dietician Lourdes Lore. Not in terms of calories or grams of carbs, but how what we eat not only affects our health in surprising ways, but the world as we now know it and future generations. Do you know that many of our food choices unwittingly support certain food production practices and multibillion (yes billion) dollar corporations? Find out a better way to eat that sustains our bodies, our earth and our world.

title: Adult Tap 3
description: Ready to learn more advanced routines? Put your knowledge and your muscles to work in this advanced class. Bring tap shoes. Prerequisite: Adult Tap Dance 1 & 2, or permission of instructor.
prerequisite_text: Adult Tap Dance 1 & 2, or permission of instructor.

title: Adv Video Game Design & Dev
description: In the Advanced Video Game Design and Development online program students will move on to learn more advanced 3D graphics programming techniques. Students will explore mesh optimizations and hierarchical animation techniques, the use of level of detail (LOD) algorithms to improve application performance by reducing polygon counts in distant objects, skeletal animation systems for realistic character animation and more.

title: Hips & Sciatica Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols are used to relieve hip pain and sciatica. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve stiffness in hips and pelvis as well as sciatica - without taking drugs.

title: Dream Worlds
description: Image a place where time is not linear. A lovely place wherein line and form and design rearrange themselves in the blink of an eye. Now, see yourself as a traveler in this mystic place. This is the land of your dreams and you journey there each and every night. Yet many of us are unaware of how to tap the potential of this inner vision. This study of sleep and dream mechanics is based on current dream studies as well as volumes of dream journals. Included will be lucid dreaming, common symbols and techniques for dream recall and interpretation. Learn how to create your own dream journal.

title: Strengthen Bones Naturally
description: Osteoporosis affects both men and women and produces 1.5 million fractures annually. To reduce your risk for fractures and osteoporosis, join biochemist Sandy Baumann to discover which common beverages, drugs and medical conditions rob bones. Find out which mineral is more important than calcium for bone density. Sign up now to learn natural strategies, including vitamins, minerals, hormones, supplements and lifestyle changes that strengthen bones at any age. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. 5 nursing contact hours. Bring your bone density report.

title: Relieve Pain Without Drugs
description: If you have stiff, achy joints from any type of arthritis, learn to relieve the pain using your fingertips. Enroll in this soothing Saturday class with Esther McCormick, a Shenjing acupressure specialist who practiced in Sweden for 13 years. Find out how to gently press specific spots on your body to activate pain relief processes and nourish your tendons, joints, bones and cartilage. Urge a friend or spouse also to register for this class so you can practice together. Wear loose, comfortable clothing. Bring 2 tennis balls, a towel and a clean sock.

title: Butterflies & Hummingbirds
description: Imagine your garden alive with beautiful butterflies and entertaining hummingbirds! Join Master Gardener Jane Geisler and learn about their intriguing life cycle and how you can create an environment that will attract them.

title: Holiday Centerpieces
description: Fresh blossoms can make any celebration more festive. Enliven your graduation parties, First Communion celebrations, showers, Mother's Day gathering, or other spring events with your own simple, but dramatic, floral arrangements. Internationally known floral designer and educator, Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will teach you basic floral-handling techniques and design elements as you create an arrangement to take home each week. Bring a notepaid, pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No experience necessary.

title: Create a Floorcloth
description: Turn a plain canvas rug into a work of art by stenciling, stamping or painting it to match your home decor. Learn all the steps necessary to complete your own project. Supply list will be mailed to participants.

title: Easy Classroom Podcasting
description: Have you ever felt like you just can't reach your students? They speak the language of technology while you speak the language of, well, teachers. But what if there was a bridge between the two? In this professional training course for teachers, you'll learn how to make a podcast that creates a memorable learning experience for your students. You'll find their interest reignited when you start using their favorite formats: audio, images and video. You won't even need an iPod or a big budget to harness the power of podcasting in your classroom.

title: Perform Base-Motorcycle Safety
description: This course is designed for the experienced but unlicensed rider to become licensed and legal. Many experienced, licensed riders use this course as a refresher and skills tune-up. Topics include: braking, counter steering, turning, speed judgment, and other techniques necessary for the experienced rider to operate a motorcycle skillfully and safely. All students will take a skills riding test covering the riding exercises used in the Secretary of State Motorcycle Skills Test. Students must furnish their own cycle with a clutch (no scooters), helmet, boots, gloves, long pants, long-sleeved jacket or shirt, eye protection and rain gear, if needed. All students must provide proof of insurance for their bike. The bike must pass a safety inspection prior to taking the riding skills test

title: Chakras & Auras - Part 1
description: Explore your "inner-energy"! Learn to tap into your inner-self with ease. Simply with guided meditation you will "see" how to discern your own chakra system and aura. This will help you to be more insightful and have a more fulfilling life. You will also learn how to "see" other people auras. Come join the fun and excitement of learning about the "inner-you".

title: Intro to Mobile App Developmen
description: An introductory-level course covering application development for mobile and handheld devices. Emphasizes application programming principles, user experience and functionality considerations, and software architecture. Students will build and deploy basic mobile apps for a major mobile platform.

title: Life Drawing
description: Draw figures from life! Learn the way the masters did and discover for yourself that you no longer have to be afraid of people in your pictures. Whatever your level of experience, take this opportunity to explore the structure, balance and movement of the figure in a welcomng environment. Bring any material you wish to use - from the simplest charcoal or pencil to crayon, pastel, wash or watercolor.

title: Indust Appl Trigonomic Princip
description: Focuses on trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Covers trigonometric functions, solution of right triangles, solution of oblique triangles, and problem solving techniques.

title: Stained Glass Sampler
description: Explore the craft of stained glass in this hands on sampler course. You will try cutting glass, soldering, grinding and looking at glass patterns to de-mystify the process and gain a greater understanding of the variety of projects that can be done in this medium. Make a small suncatcher to take with you. Please wear loose fitting cotton clothing. A materials fee of $15 is payable to the instructor in class. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Spiral Rope for Beginners
description: Spiral rope stitch is an easy, versatile form of basic stitch and some variations as you make an impressive bracelet. $15 material fee due to instructor. Class limited to 8.

title: Co-Op Engineering Technology
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major in STEM to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Sew Simple
description: Sewing is a wonderful way to express your artistic flair, to achieve a custom look, and to save money. If you've never sewn before or want to begin again, join Dianne Zanley for a fun, hands-on sewing class. Learn the basics of sewing machines, fabrics, cutting and assmebling while making a simple rod-pocket window treatment. You'll leave class with a custom curtain personally designed to fit your decor! Bring your own sewing machine. Supply list will be provided in the first class.

title: Aerobic Dance
description: This is an exercise class which covers all of the health related components of physical fitness, with an emphasis on cardiovascular endurance. The benefits are similar to those experience in jogging, cycling, swimming and other endurance activities. The course also covers how to make choices for a healthy diet; weight and stress management; and disease prevention. No dance background is required.

title: Plumbing Code
description: Introduces the use and application of the Michigan Mechanical Plumbing Code. Covers how to review each article of the code for content and application, and how to interpret plans and drawings as related to plumbing and pipefitting.

title: Adobe Acrobat: Beginner
description: Learn the skills you need to work efficiently with Adobe Acrobat software (Standard or Professional). This class focuses on creating and working within PDF documents. You will learn Adobe Acrobat basics, how to work with Acrobat tools and toolbars, task buttons and the navigation pane. You will convert Microsoft Office files to Adobe PDF files, create PDF portfolios, modify and edit PDF files, compare documents, search within PDF files, reduce file size, print and customize output quality.

title: Machu Picchu & the Incas
description: Join our favorite traveler, Doug Denard, as he takes you through Cusco, Peru (earth's navel", in the native language); the ancient city of Peru, which is the gateway to the Sacred Valley; and the ancient runs of Machu Picchu, the breath-taking stone fortress of the Andes. Study the architecture of Machu Picchu and the ceremonial sites as you learn of the ancient history and see how it relates to the spirtual aspects of their culture.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 5
description: Develops intermediate-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-106W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Honors Directed Study Abroad
description: In this sophomore-level directed study, students are challenged to apply the knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with the faculty member leading the Directed Study Abroad class on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Students present summaries of their research before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty held at the end of each semester or at another public forum. This course will be held abroad in a pre-selected country.NOTE: Additional special fees exist for travel and study abroad administration. Please see the Associate Dean/Program Administrator for specific information.

title: Wedding Floral Basics
description: Set the scene for your next celebration with beautiful homemade bouquets, stunning centerpieces and striking corsages. Learn basic floral handling techniques, design elements and principles and creative tips from an internationally known floral designer and educator. Using his extensive background in floral artistry, horticulture and retail/wholesale floristry, Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will demonstrate and guide you as you create a centerpiece, bouquet or corsage each week to take home. Bring a notepad, pen/pencil and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No previous experience necessary.

title: Applied Tech
description: An introductory course, appropriate for all trades, which uses practical concepts and examples to examine fluid power, electricity, mechanical power transfer, and rigging. Emphasizes appropriate safety procedures and protocols.

title: Musical Theater Production
description: Musical Theater Production.

title: Intermediate Feng Shui
description: Experience immediate positive changes in your life as you apply the principles of feng shui more specifically to your personal environment. Susan Haigfleigh, the instructor, will combine her practical, logical training as an Architect and Interior Designer and her intuitive/metaphysical training as she assists you in creating a balance of the best of Eastern and Western thinking, practice, and philosophy. Prerequisite: CLNC 7031
prerequisite_text: CLNC 7031

title: Advanced Media Production
description: This course is designed to build on foundational knowledge and equip students with advanced tools to further develop their skills in multimedia production. Through hands-on projects such as drone, gimbal, and glide camera operation, students will explore advanced techniques in shooting, lighting, editing, and special effects across specialized formats for news, entertainment, and education, working both on location and in studio settings.

title: Earth Science for Educators
description: An inquiry-based course covering topics in the earth sciences - geology, meteorology, and astronomy. Course content is designed to give students a thorough familiarity with concepts critical to teaching earth science at the K-6 level and how the topics covered in the course fulfill National Science Education Standards. Learning is primarily through student inquiry and discovery of core concepts, with guidance and facilitation provided by the instructor. Four hours of integrated lecture and lab per week.

title: Engineering Physics II
description: Designed to meet the requirements of engineering students and physics majors. Covers electrostatics, including electric fields, forces, and potential; electrical circuits, including resistors, capacitors, and inductors; magnetic fields and forces; induction; electromagnetic waves; and optical systems, including diffraction, refraction, and image formation. Four hours of lecture and three hours of laboratory activities per week.

title: Rebuild Your Bones Naturally
description: Osteoporosis affects both men and women and produces 1.5 million fractures annually. To reduce your risk for fractures and osteoporosis, join biochemist Sandy Baumann to discover which common beverages, drugs and medical conditions rob bones. Find out which mineral is more important than calcium for bone density. Sign up now to learn natural strategies, including vitamins, minerals, hormones, supplements and lifestyle changes to strengthen bones at any age. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. Bring your bone density report.

title: Cookin' for Mama
description: Here's your chance to learn how to put together a spectacular meal in the "down home" tradition that would be a perfect treat for your Mama for Mother's Day or anyone else that you want to honor with these savory dishes. Join Chef Saniyyah as she prepares Butterflied Jerk Chicken, Shrimp Satay Skewers, Five Seasoning Salmon, Mama's Shrimp and Tata Salad, Grilled Vegetables, Sweet Potato Cornbread, Banana Pudding, and Mississippi Spice Tea. Bring and apron and an appetite for some amazing food!

title: Fabulous Food Garnishes
description: Entertaining thoughts of throwing a sensational soiree, a fabulous fete, or a perfect party? Learn easy ways to dress up your dishes for most any occasion with stunning garnishes. You'll sculpt a strawberry fan, apply bird, tomato rose, melon swan, veggie centerpiece and more. Wear an apron and bring a cutting board and paring knife.

title: Sign Lang Cert II, Part A
description: Are you looking for a sign language class that will prepare you to take the certification test? Join long-time instructor and member of the deaf community, Anne Bolander, in the first half of the second course of a series designed to prepare you for certification. Look for more classes in future semesters. Includes 200-page workbook and DVD.

title: The Abc's of Digital Camera
description: This quick workshop is designed to help you solve digital photo issues such as saving your photos to the computer. Where do you store them and how do you keep them organized? Using the Microsoft Picture Manager that comes with Windows XP you will learn how to print, retouch and resize your pictures - even fix red eye! Your savy instructor, Noel, will also talk about taking your pictures to the local store or using the internet to get high quality photos in special sizes or placing them on a CD for easy archiving or sharing with friends and family. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers or similar experience.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers or similar experience.

title: Pizzas & Pasta With Pizzazz
description: Surprise your guests this year with creative twists on familiar traditional recipes. Marilyn Szymanski-Suter, Executive Chef at Eastern University, will share some of her favorite quick and easy pasta dishes, including roasted vegetable lasagna. She'll also teach you how to make not-so-ordinary homemade pizzas, including buffalo chicken and BLT. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Children's Theatre Production
description: Offers lecture and practice in the organization of specific skills necessary for the public performance of children's plays such as acting, assistant directing, stage management, publicity, and house management. Requires students to participate in HFC's annual Children's Theatre presentation.

title: Shower Singing in Public
description: Have you ever wanted to learn to use your voice more effectively? In just five hours, this workshop will improve your vocal performance by teaching you how to sing in a healthy and confident way. Also discover how to use a microphone effectively. Whether you are interested in Karaoke singing or simply want to work on fundamental voice techniques, you will benefit from the coaching of HFCC's vocal director, Kevin Dewey.

title: More Pilates
description: Continue sculpting your body in this intermediate-level class Appropriate for anyone who has completed Pilates for Beginners or has a basic knowledge of the fitness method. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable stretchable clothing.

title: Lower Cholesterol Naturally
description: Do you wish you could lower cholesterol and triglycerides without expensive medications? Pharmacist Debby Danto will discuss simple lifestyle changes and nutritional supplements to help you decrease harmful cholesterol levels, protect your heart, and control blood pressure. She will also review the common cholesterol-lowering medications, their benefits, precautions and side effects. You will be given guidelines for safe cholesterol and triglyceride levels to help you understand your personal risk for heart disease.

title: Web Server Administration
description: Introduces the installation, configuration, and management of web server software. Offers hands-on experience in installing and administering server software platforms such as Apache Web Server. Covers web server security, performance monitoring and tuning, virtual hosting, and supporting database and dynamic content. Intended for network, server, or web administration; web development; or computer information system students.

title: Principles of Image Production
description: This course provides an in-depth study of the equipment and principles involved in the production of diagnostic radiographic images. Emphasis is placed on the design, construction, and operational characteristics of the diagnostic X-ray tube and digital imaging systems. The course explores the physical interactions within the X-ray tube that result in X-ray production, the properties and quality of the X-ray beam, and the parameters influencing radiographic image formation and evaluation.

title: Advanced Surgical Techniques
description: Laboratory course designed to prepare students for clinical externship. Students will continue to practice aseptic and sterile techniques used in the perioperative care of the surgical patient. This course focuses on skills developed in SRG-140 and incorporates "mock surgery" and advanced clinical skills in a lab setting. Students practice techniques in a guided lab setting.

title: Early American History
description: Covers Colonial America and the United States through the Civil War Period.

title: Adv Grant Proposal Writing
description: Do you know the best way to present information in a grant proposal? In this course, an experienced grant writer will show you how to research and write winning proposals. Learn what to do--and, more importantly what not to do--on every part of your proposal. Understand the criteria funders use to determine whether your grant proposal receives money. You'll become expert at ferreting out corporate, foundation, and government grant makers, and you'll know how to tailor your responses to information found in the peer review criteria. You'll also discover a number of significant finishing touches that can give your project the edge over others. You'll know the best type of paper to use, which buzzwords to include, which fonts work best, and which types of graphics and formatting techniques will make your proposal more competitive.

title: Hybrid & Electric Vehicle Syst
description: An intermediate-level course covering hybrid and electric vehicle systems. Includes environmental impact of internal combustion engines (ICE). Also, includes theory of operation and service of electric propulsion systems, with emphasis on safety issues and concerns regarding the servicing of these systems. Also, covers hybrid system safety, hybrid systems in general (both series and parallel), plug-in hybrids, electric vehicles including battery types and fuel cell vehicles. High- voltage battery types, environmental impact, construction, and diagnosis.

title: Ldrshp Diverse Comm & Organiza
description: The purpose of Leadership in Diverse Communities and Organizations is to examine and assess the intricacies of traditional leadership praxis and the complexity of leading non-traditional organizations. While the course addresses traditional leadership theories, it also identifies innovative theories and approaches that acknowledge and value the multiplicity of social capital all shareholders bring to an organization. Additionally, the sociological perspective is employed to enable students to critically view their own lived experiences as framework from which to critically assess issues of equity and privilege.

title: Telecommunication Camp
description: Interested in all things media? The HFC Telecommunication Camp is the place for you! Our expert instructors will give you a front row seat to learn about the magic of film and television, how a real radio station works, the art of telling a story or delivering news through the written script, and a number of performance techniques including character voices, newscasts and interviews . Students will get hands-on opportunities to experience what their favorite actors, writers, editors, directors, newscasters and DJs do in the media field, from writing scripts, shooting footage, lighting, and editing to performing commercials, news and improv on camera and the radio. Your friends and family might even get to hear you on our award-winning radio station, WHFR. Make your mark on the world of media and have loads of fun in the process!

title: Self Defense for Women
description: Join other mothers, daughters, sisters, aunts and grandmothers in learning how to prevent victimization. Increase your awareness of self-protection strategies under the instruction of Jaye Spiro, a fifth-degree black belt. This fun and informative seminar is designed to increase confidence and reduce fear for you and your female loved ones. Come examine and practice simple, effective techniques--some physical, some not--which can be adapted into your life experience. Women of all ages, sizes, and fitness levels are welcome.

title: Hfv - Gentle Yoga
description: Experience the health benefits of yoga in this calming, therapeutic class. Designed and presented by professional yoga instructor Dianna Kinaschuk for beginners, returning practitioners, seniors, persons with limited mobility and flexibility, and those recovering from illness or injury. Gentle Yoga offers the postures and breathing techniques of Hatha Yoga in a format that can be modified to fit your needs and abilities, and at a gentle, slow pace. Open to persons of all ages and levels. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Getting Down & Dirty With Clay
description: Let's get down and dirty with some clay! Campers will be using clay as the medium to explore both functional and sculptural ideas. We will be making imaginary creatures, busts based on the human head and a set of mugs to take home. Campers' masterpieces will be displayed at a gallery exhibition on the last day of class.

title: Pharmacology for NSG Practice
description: This course is designed to provide a foundation in the basic principles of pharmacology that utilize clinical judgment and the nursing process for safe medication administration. Legal and ethical nursing responsibilities and rationale for understanding current drug therapy involved in the common disorders of the major body systems are also included. Routes of medication administration, client education, and dosage calculations are components of this course.

title: Therapeutic Clinical Mgt
description: This course provides the student an opportunity to apply in simulated settings concepts learned throughout the respiratory therapist program. Students spend time working on computerized clinical case study simulations, selecting appropriate patient information, and managing patients.

title: Intro Digital Photograph Slr
description: Have a digital SLR camera and are ready to start getting the most out of it? In this course learn about the exposure triangle: ISO, Aperture and Shutter speed. Learn how to control your camera's settings and get amazing photographs. Learn the digital camera terminology and learn how to navigate your DSL camera! Class includes hands-on exercises and photo critiques. Required: Digital SLR camera or a camera with program modes such as Av (Apperture Priority), Tv (shutterspeed Priority) and M (full manual modes). Text book available, approximately $20.

title: Weather and Climate
description: A non-mathematical introduction to the behavior of the atmosphere and its causes. Topics include atmospheric structure, earth-sun relationships, heat, humidity, clouds, wind, storms, forecasting, pollution, and climate. Offers students without scientific background an interesting look into the atmosphere and weather that surround them.

title: ICS 400: Adv Incident Command
description: In this final course of the ICS series you will explore how major and/or complex incidents engender special management challenges. You will discuss circumstances in which an Area Command is established and learn about circumstances in which multi-agency coordination systems are established. This course is designed to provide overall incident management skills rather than tactical expertise. Pre-requisite: ICS 300: Intermediate ICS for Expanding Incidents.

title: Ballet for Pre-Teens
description: Give your child an opportunity to develop grace, coordination, and flexibility that will be a part of his or her life forever. In this class, Stephanie Fullerton will teach the terminology, technique, and precision associated with ballet.

title: Paramedic V
description: Covers specialty topics such as OB/GYN, newborn resuscitation, and pediatrics. Also covers 12-Lead ECG including application and interpretation.

title: Com. Food Equip. Repair Basic
description: This entry level training for individual seeking employment in the commercial food equipment repair industry. This is a 24 hour course. Upon completion student will have a basic certification from the college.

title: Radio Station Wrksp/WHFR-FM
description: Interested in how a first-rate radio station works? HFCC's award-winning radio station WHFR-FM is just the place. Join us in this intensive orientation for our non-commercial, educational radio station. Learn essential station rules and procedures, operation of equipment and basic performance skills. You just might find yourself on the airwaves some day!

title: Greek for Travelers
description: Whether you are planning a trip to Greece or simply want to learn conversational Greek skills, this course will introduce you to the appropriate use of Greek in various social situations and will provide you cultural information about everyday life in modern Greece. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Thessaloniki, Greece, will teach you basic expressions that will help you when making accommodations, eating out, traveling, sightseeing, shopping and more. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $15).

title: Belly Dancing: Novice
description: If you are attending an Absolute Beginner class and would like to learn performance skills, join this optional half-hour extension session. Techniques covered in Absolute Beginner will be organized into a short choreography.

title: Thanksgiving the Soul Food Way
description: Discover the traditional Thanksgiving foods of the Deep South as Chef Saniyyah will show you how to cook a turkey three ways - baked, fried and smoked. You'll feel like one of the family as you make the dressing, gravy, yams and turnip/mustard greens and then all sit down to sample!

title: Dining Room Captain
description: Covers advanced principles of table service and managing the production dining room. Emphasizes effective management procedures and techniques including scheduling, table assignments, side work, reservations, expediting, and training of the HOSP-150 students.

title: Sprout a Healthier Life
description: What is packed full of vitamins, minerals and enzymes, can take less than a week to grow in less than three square feet? Sprouts! Join Health Coach George Avedisian as he opens your eyes to the magical world of sprouts. You will be planting sprouts in a planter the first class and bring the planter back the next week to harvest the sprouts and discuss any problems or issues that you may have had. Students may purchase a sprouting box and seeds for approx. $20 from the instructor on the first night of class.

title: Media Comm Arts Directed Study
description: This course offers students the opportunity to design and execute a self-directed project within the Media Communication Arts field under the mentorship of a faculty advisor. Students will conceptualize, plan, and develop a project that may be scholarly, creative, technical, or a combination of these approaches. Projects should reflect the student's academic and/or professional interests and must receive prior approval from the instructor. Regular progress reviews and critical reflection will support the creation of a high-quality final outcome.

title: Principles of Marketing
description: Focuses on marketing functions and institutions, the policies of marketing agencies, and current marketing channels. Also covers the retailing and wholesaling of consumer goods and raw materials, pricing policies and practices, anti-competitive practices, and recent governmental activities affecting marketing.

title: A Healthier Choice Yet Still
description: Executive Chef Peter Veach of the University of Michigan will deomonstrate some great flavorful recipes such as Spicy Mango, Chicken and Rice Salad, Chicken Puttanesca with whole wheat pasta. May of these recipes are University of Michigan "Good Choice" approved and are sure to become some of your favorite recipes. Learn that eating healthy does not have to be bland and boring.

title: Economics for Entrepreneurs
description: This course invites students to take a deeper look at some of the principles of microeconomics while introducing new material often found in an intermediate microeconomics course or a managerial economics course. Central to the course is the application of economic theory to business decisions that an entrepreneur or manager will face. Topics include optimization, elasticity of demand, estimation of demand and of costs, production theory, concentration and competition, pricing strategies, game theory, and behavioral economics. The impact of macroeconomic conditions on businesses is also covered.

title: Aerobics
description: Get in shape quick with this high energy class! Aerobics is a fast-paced, dance-based class which will slim you down, tone you up, and better your cardio vascular system. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Wear comfortable clothing. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lbs. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Prevent Identity Theft
description: Don't be a victim! With over 27 million identity thefts reported in the last five years, this crime has reached epidemic proportions. Let security expert and author Johnny May, CPP, CPO, show you how to avoid the nightmare. In this short but comprehensive class, you will learn what identity theft is, who is at risk, how the internet and other technology increase risk, how thieves steal your personal information, and what you can do to prevent it. Get step-by-step instructions for what to do if YOU become a victim. Includes May's Guide to Preventing Identity Theft.

title: Growing Tender Bulbs
description: If you can sprout a dandelion, you can grow great flowering tender bulbs. In just two hours, discover how easy it is to grow dahlias, gladiolus, tuberous begonias, and other tender bulbs, as well as elephant ears and certain exotic bulbs in your own back yard or container. You'll learn how, when, and where to plant these bulbs; how to divide, store, fertilize, and care for them; and how to get the right soil recipe.

title: Lightouses Great Lakes
description: Did you know the Great Lakes are home to 350 lighthouses--the highest concentration in North America? To see some of these landmarks, join Wayne Sapulski, author of Lighthouses of Lake Michigan, Past and Present, for an armchair slide show. Wyane has personally visited and photographed all 350 lighthouses. He will discuss the crucial role of lighthouses in maritime history and describe preservation efforts that are currently underway. You will learn where to find these historic treasures and how to get involved in preserving them. Price includes a locator map of Great Lakes lighthouses, so you can personally visit them.

title: Create Projects - MS Word
description: Learn to create a flyer, invitation, letter and calendar using this popular program. Design your own resume and find out how easy it is to make a table. Create other projects as time permits. Learn many features of Microsoft Word as you build different projects.

title: Tai Chi Chu'an (2-Wk Session)
description: Tai Chi, sometimes referred to as "meditation in motion," is an ancient Chinese martial art consisting of a sequence of slow, graceful motions that relax and strengthen the mind, body, and spirit. An excellent form of stress management suitable for people of all ages, Tai Chi has been shown to reduce blood pressure and strengthen bones. Join an experienced Tai Chi instructor and discover the benefits yourself. No experience needed. Wear loose, comfortable, modest clothing and shoes with no or low heels. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Microsoft Excel 2007
description: Get a hands-on overview of the new enhanced features of Microsoft Office Excel 2007. This latest release of the software is designed to improve the management, presentation and distribution of your spreadsheets. You will work with the most commonly used processed and functions to experience the changes and updates. In-depth exploration of advanced features will not be covered. Prerequisite: You must have a working knowledge of Microsoft Office Excel 2003. Text included.
prerequisite_text: You must have a working knowledge of Microsoft Office Excel 2003. Text included.

title: Sign for Fun 2
description: Increase your signing skills and understanding of the deaf culture in this unique intermediate-level class. Working with Anne Bolander, author and hearing-impaired instructor, you will become fully immersed in communicating with the deaf community. If you attended Sign for Fun or have a basic knowledge of signing, you won't want to miss this opportunity!

title: Co-op in Communications
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Filling the Gap for Men
description: Did you know that men undergo midlife hormone declines that affect muscle strength, the ability to play sports, memory, mood, sleep, love life, and energy? A male pharmacist will describe the symptoms of hormone declines, name the hormones important to men's health, and discuss how natural hormone replacement can benefit man.

title: Excel Workshop for Beginners
description: Excel 2007 spreadsheets can make personal and business record keeping a snap. See how simple it is to enter, edit, and move your information by mouse or keyboard. Discover the easy way to manipulate dollar signs, decimal points and other formatting. Calculations are streamlined with easy formulas and functions. Give your reports a polished look with formatting and printing techniques. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories as you work your upper body, lowe body, and abdominals. Come for a more challenging class - moderate to advanced fitness levels. Beginners welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Fall Flower Arranging
description: Fresh flower arrangements can have a positive effect on the mood in your home. This is your chance to create stunning fall floral arrangements. Learn basic floral handling techniques, design elements and principles, and creative tips from an internationally known floral designer and educator. Using his extensive background in floral artistry, horticulture, and retail/wholesale floristry. Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will demonstrate and guide you as you create a unique fall arrangement each week to take home. Bring a notepaid, pen/pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No previous experience necessary.

title: Word & Excel for Genealogists
description: Discover the many ways Microsoft Word and Excel can help you do genealogy research and keep your family tree organized. In this hands-on workshop, Michael Neill, weekly columnist for Ancestry Daily News, will show you various tables and forms that can be created to organize data and conduct research. You'll also learn how to export reports and forms from most genealogical software programs (i.e. Personal Ancestral File or Family Tree Maker) into Word, use Word to create computerized research logs, and download graphics from the Internet for integration into software applications. Each student will have a computer to use. Class limited to 14.

title: Spring Floral Arrangements
description: Fresh flower arrangements can have a positive effect on the mood in your home. This is your chance to create stunning spring floral arrangements. Learn basic floral handling techniques, design elements and principles, and creative tips from an internationally known floral designer and educator. Using his extensive background in floral artistry, horticulture, and retail/wholesale floristry, Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will demonstrate and guide you as you create a floral arrangement each week to take home. Bring a notepad, pen/pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No previous experience necessary.

title: DESC Robotics Cert Training
description: This non-credit accelerated course focuses on an operator level of skill and knowledge. Students with the level have a basic understanding of robot operations and programming, material handling and its components and introduction to Roboguide simulation software. This certificate is focused on the core Robot Operator Skills needed by entry level or incumbent workers. Registration is restricted to those students who are sponsored by DESC.

title: Jazz Dancing
description: Express yourself in the syncopated beat of jazz. Some basic moves include: ball change, twist, pivot step, touch step, Pique' passe', stag leap, jete', pencil spin, barrel turn, fan kick, jazz walk, jazz run, hip walk, chasses, cat walk and the catch step. Jazz dancing incorporates a wide variety of basic ballet based steps and modern dance. Join the fun with dance master Emily

title: Grill It
description: Discover delicious dishes served with an innovative vegetarian flair. Join instructors - and local food bloggers - Sherry Duquet and Sandy Boulton and re-imagine your grill in time for the summer patio season. Learn to explore the produce section with us, as we masterfully grill: cactus fajitas (you have to try'em to believe'em), with all the fixings, BBQ tempeh sandwiches made with a homemade rub, then lathered in sauce, and a grilled decadent dessert. Class includes interactive instruction, recipes, shopping suggestions and tips for finding new veggie options for your friends and family. Basic understanding of using cooking utensils and prepping food is helpful. All food prepared vegan. Class is not gluten free.

title: Culinary Exploration
description: This introductory exploratory camp is truly a culinary adventure for young enthusiasts eager to explore the art of cooking and baking. With a well-balanced schedule combining hands-on cooking sessions and essential kitchen fundamentals, participants will gain a comprehensive understanding of the culinary world.By incorporating initiatives like farm-to-fork, nutrition, and healthy eating, the camp goes beyond simply teaching cooking techniques. It instills in participants a deeper appreciation for the origins of food and its impact on our well-being, fostering lifelong healthy eating habits. Empowering kids to take charge of the kitchen sparks their creativity and builds confidence as they experiment with flavors and ingredients. Meanwhile, the focus on baking not only teaches essential skills but also lays a solid foundation for those with a passion for pastry arts.The culmination of the camp with a parent luncheon on the final day provides participants with a platform to showcase their newfound skills and share their culinary creations with loved ones. Overall, this camp offers an immersive experience that goes beyond teaching cooking and baking skills. It nurtures a deeper understanding and passion for the culinary arts, setting participants on a path of culinary exploration and discovery.

title: Help for ADHD
description: If you are a parent, teacher, or counselor who works with children and teens challenged by ADHD, come to learn practical strategies from an expert. Educator Geri Markel, Ph.D., will discuss self-management approaches for use at home and at school, ways to enhance reading, tips for improving educational planning for ADHD, and concerns about medication. Optional book available at class: Helping Adolescents with ADHD & Learning Disabilities.

title: Elementary Italian II
description: Further builds reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Italian, focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops knowledge of vocabulary, pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express everyday ideas in both spoken and written Italian. Presents Italian culture.

title: Graphic Design Studio 1
description: An introduction to visual communication using the Adobe Creative Software Suite. Provides a working knowledge of InDesign and Illustrator. Introduction to the fundamentals and principles of design. Explores the design and development of a variety of media, including multi-page print documents and digital output.
