This class meets for six (6) hours a week.Students are expected to have access to the Adobe Creative Cloud applications including Photoshop, Indesign, Illustrator, Acrobat, AfterEffects and XD.

title: Special Topics Mach Tool Techn
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current machine tool technology. Specific special topics are announced together with the prerequisites each term. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic. Course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree or certificate.

title: National Electrical Code
description: This course covers the pertinent aspects of Chapters 1 through 4 of the 2017 National Electrical Code (NEC) as they apply to electrical examinations and installations in all types of buildings and structures covered by the Code. Also covered are essential rules of the Skilled Trades Regulation Act 407 of 2016 and the 2017 Michigan Electrical Code Rules Part 8. The course content is disseminated by lecture utilizing PowerPoint presentations.

title: Photoshop CS5, Level 2
description: Move on in your exploration of Photoshop features as you learn how to use color fills and overlays as well as gradients. Explore various ways to extract an object from an image using a mask. Experiment with vector paths by creating vector masks and converting type into editable shapes. Learn to apply custom brushes, warping, and Smart Filters along with other creative image effects. After producing images for videos end the course by automating tasks and customizing Photoshop. Text provided. Prerequiste: Photoshop CS5 Level 1 or similar experience.

title: Create a Free E-Mail Account
description: You will create free e-mail accounts and start e-mailing during the first session! Continue to use your free e-mail address at any library, at home, or anywhere that has Internet access. Also learn how to create folders and move e-mails into them; send pictures and attachments; reply to, and forward e-mails; search for and find lost e-mails; and more as time permits.

title: Mid East People and Cultures
description: Introduces the various ethnic, linguistic, and religious groups as well as cultures of the Middle East with an emphasis on both the Arabs and Islam as two of the most powerful social forces to have shaped the history and culture of the region. Explores Orientalism and western perceptions of the Middle East, colonialism. globalization and identity, nationalism, and other popular ideological currents that continue to influence the region and its peoples. Also examines the history and experiences of Arabs in the United States with a special focus on the large Arab-American community in Dearborn, Michigan.

title: Adv Acupressure Face Lift
description: Treat your face to advanced facelift exercises and techniques in this fascinating class with acupressure specialist Esther McCormick. Learn what wrinkles say about your emotions and everyday reactions to stress. Then use gentle acupressure massage to relax your neck, shoulder and facial muscles into a more youthful appearance. Also create safe natural cleaners, masks, toners and moisturizers based on skin type to nourish your own face or use for holiday gifts. Bring a stand-up mirror, washcloth and 1-cup plastic bowl to class. $3 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Civil Technology Studio
description: Students will explore the materials and methods used in the development of both civil and commercial construction projects. Students will analyze existing civil and commercial projects with a focus on the development of project specific details centered on material use, performance, and adherence to industry specific design guidelines. Projects will include: bridges, roadways, commercial site development, and civil construction details.

title: Charm Bracelet Workshop 1
description: With a few inexpensive tools, copper sheet, a package of precious metal clay, and a variety of beads, yhou can make an incredible charm bracelet. This session will focus on making the link bracelet and stamping/embellishing the copper blanks. Bring a Phillips head screwdriver, one concrete nail, any comfortably sized hammer, and heavy duty scissors. Material kit $28.00 due to instructor in class.

title: Aerobics
description: Get in shape quick with this high energy class! Aerobics is a fast-paced, dance based class which will slim you down, tone you up and better your cardio vascular system. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Wear comfortable clothing. Please bring water and an exercise mat.

title: BBQ Ovenstyle
description: Discover how to prepare barbecue dishes without the backyard grill. Chef Sania shares her 37 years of "Soul Food" expertise with you while cooking barbeque in the oven and on top of the stove. Learn her cooking techniques, seasoning tips and top of the stove grill method. The menu includes BBQ ribs, BBQ chicken, BBQ fish and grilled vegetables.

title: Electrical Journeyman Prep
description: Course Description: This course provides an overview of electrical theory, the essentials of 2017 the Michigan Electrical Code Rules Part 8 and the 2017 National Electrical Code (NEC) to help prepare the student to take the electrical journeyman examination. Course content will be disseminated by lecture utilizing PowerPoint presentations. The method of instruction for the course is either classroom lecture or Zoom lecture utilizing PowerPoint. At the instructor's discretion, printed copies of the PowerPoint presentations will be handed out to the students, and/or they will be posted on the Henry Ford College Learning Management System (LMS).

title: Selling Home in Today's Market
description: Learn how to receive the most for your house and sell quickly in this hard buyer's market. Don't let the market beat you up. Learn how you can still come ahead selling your house. $15 material packet available from instructor.

title: Intuition Development 2
description: For those that took Intuition Development 1 and are looking to advance their intuition into a deeper realm. Professional Intuitive Kristy Robinett will help to fine tune your identified intuitive strengths through more fun exercises.

title: Salad & Sandwich Entertaining
description: Tired of serving the same old deli tray to guests? Get some unique salad and sandwich ideas that will upscale your next casual entertaining event. Chef Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will share her recipes for mango chutney cheese, tuna papaya and creamy pesto turkey sandwiches. She'll also whip up three salads: wilted bacom, roasted root vegetable and a not-so-orindary ambrosia. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Digital Audio Editing
description: An intensive workshop introduction to digital audio editing, using an industry standard software editing program on computers in the Telecommunication audio production labs. Both hardware and software issues will be covered, and several short editing exercises will be completed.

title: Stroll Hamtramck
description: Rain or shine, enjoy a spring Saturday strolling Jos Campau Avenue, the main street of this colorful ethnic city, and visiting its stores and landmarks. As you walk, tour guide Don Samull will talk about Hamtramck's fascinating history as well as it's current situation. You'll have plenty of time to make purchases before you stop at a local Polish restaurant for lunch. Wear comfortable shoes. Lunch included in course fee.

title: Music Notation W/Finale 1
description: This course provides basic instruction in the use of "Finale," by Coda. "Finale" is the industry standard for music software. Students will use the music technology lab to gain hands-on experience with "Finale." Topics to be covered include basic document setup, using templates, tool palettes, importing and saving files, and file playback. To register for this course, students must have a basic knowledge of how to use a computer and file playback.

title: Automotive Fundamentals
description: Provides an introduction to the major automotive systems covered in greater detail in other automotive technology specific system classes. Explores how to perform preventive maintenance routines, such as oil changes, tire rotations on standard systems as well as on vehicles equipped with TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System); brake and suspension system inspections; and how to service the cooling system. This is the only course allowed for Secondary Partnership transfer credit (4 credit hours) from approved high school programs.

title: Con't Conversational Italian
description: This class is for those who completed Conversational Italian 1 or for persons with a basic knowledge of the language. Books available for purchase the first night of class.

title: The Weekend Navigator
description: The U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary's Weekend Navigator course is a comprehensive course designed for both experienced and novice powerboat and sailboat operators. The course is designed to educate the boating enthusiast in skills required for a safe voyage on a variety of waters and boating conditions. Topics will include: fundamentals of waypoint navigation, how to read and use nautical charts and digital charts, cruise and chart planning software, chart plotters and navigating with radar and much more. Class includes The Weekend Navigator course book, charts, and handouts and a course completion certificate. The student needs to bring a parallel ruler or rolling chart plotter, dividers and a nautical slide rule for speed and distance calculations. There is no final examination. Visit the Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for a supply list, where to purchase supplies, and cost.

title: Shop Arithmetic PAPP
description: Review of basic arithmetic; whole numbers, fractions, decimals, singed numbers, grouping symbols, square root, ratio and proportion flat and round tapers, simple and complex gear ratios; practical industrial shop problems are employed.

title: Elementary ASL II
description: Further builds American Sign Language (ASL) skills, focusing on communication in a cultural context. Expands students' knowledge of fingerspelling, vocabulary and grammatical structures, and the development of visual receptive and gestural expressive skills in order to comprehend and express everyday ideas in ASL.

title: Develop Wills & Estate Plans
description: Even if you have a will it may not help you avoid transferred directly to your heirs. Jonathan Goldberg will discuss common mistakes many people make when developing a will or an estate plan and how to avoid these pitfalls.

title: Celebrate the Russian New Year
description: According to the old Julian calendar, Russian New Year is observed in mid-January. On the first evening of class, without flying half-way around the world, visit Moscow and some of the cities of the Golden Ring via a slide presentation. That evening view Russian artificats as you sample a Russian treat. Then on the following Sat. the class will gather for a short tour of a local orthodox monastery and a Slavic meal.

title: All Skills Youth Softball Camp
description: This camp is designed to sharpen baseball skills for Little League players at the elementary and middle-school levels. The camp will focus on throwing, hitting, pitching, base running and defensive (infield, outfield, and catching) drills and instructions. The camp is hosted directly by Henry Ford College Steve Brown. Area youth softball coaches and Henry Ford College student athletes, led by MCCAA All Conference players will serve as camp assistants.

title: Breakthroughs for Health
description: A conference for the community and nurses. Includes lunch and breaks. 5 hours instruction time.

title: Math for Food Service Careers
description: Explores the mathematical skills required for various careers in the food service profession. Topics include calculator usage, computational skills, ratio and proportion, percentages, measurement, dimensional analysis, and an introduction to data analysis with problems chosen to represent those commonly encountered in the food service profession. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator.

title: AK Steel Hydraulics Training
description: Hydraulics training for incumbent workers for AK Steel.

title: Blues Guitar
description: Come explore all aspects of "The Blues" as it relates to its performance on the guitar, and jam with renowned Detroit area musician Robert Penn. You will learn the importance of the guitar as well as theoretical principles that are characteristic of this particular genre. Examine various styles of different players as well as their approaches to improvising and techniques. The instructor will include the work of B.B. King, Jimi Hendricks, T-Bone Walker and artists of like importance. Another area of focus will be the pentatonic blues scales and its application to the I, IV, V Blues progression. Bring your acoustic or electric guitars and a music stand if you have one. Prerequisite: at least one year of basic guitar.
prerequisite_text: at least one year of basic guitar.

title: Introduction to Birding
description: This course introduces novices with an interest in birds to the basics of bird identification, observation, recording their sightings and selection of proper equipment. The course will begin with a classroom session to discuss some birding basics before taking to the field (locally in Dearborn) for the next session to begin putting into practice birding skills. (The Sibley Guide to Birds, required for class, can be purchased during the first class session. Binoculars required for second class session).

title: Medical-Surgical Nursing II
description: Continues to explore how to utilize critical thinking skills in a systematic, problem solving process, as a framework for providing safe and effective care to restore and promote health in adult clients. Focuses on nursing care related to concepts of healthy adult clients as well as adult clients with common, acute, or chronic illness exemplars, and explains how to research best practices and integrate theory in the care of adult clients and families in a variety of clinical settings. This course includes supervised clinical agency/laboratory practicums. Course comprised of four hours of theory and a clinical/lab day per week.

title: Plumbing Pipefitting Code
description: Explores how to utilize the State of Michigan Plumbing Code to solve real-life problems (International Plumbing Code). Covers study habits and processes necessary to interpret and apply the code. Also helps prepare students for entry- level positions in the plumbing, multi-skilled maintenance, repair, and apprenticeship fields.

title: Basic Print Reading
description: Discusses print reading fundamentals needed for presenting concepts and techniques to various segments of today's industry. Includes the alphabet (use) of lines; projection of various shapes and surfaces; presentation of hidden details; and methods, units of measurement, and techniques used for locating details in a variety of drawings.

title: Downsizing 101
description: Do you have family members that moved out but still have items in the home? Do you want to downsize and reclaim the space or move? Let professional organizer Theresa Brune teach you pain-free techniques for sorting and sifting through years of "Stuff" and offer suggestions on what to do with unwanted items. Once you get the space back from the family member that moved out, you can start the re-decorating!

title: One Pot Cooking
description: One pot meals are a time saver and there's nothing quite like the mouth watering aromas given off by a simmering pot of food. Simplify your life by learning techniques for making some easy but delicious meals. Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estates, will teach you how to make some of his favorite one pot meals, stews and other comfort foods. Bring an apron and your appetite. Class limited to 16.

title: History of Modern Philosophy
description: A continuation of the history of Western philosophy focusing on the major philosophers and major developments through the twentieth century from Descartes through Wittgenstein. Examines influential currents such as rationalism, empiricism, idealism, romanticism, Marxism, Darwinism, positivism, pragmatism, phenomenology, existentialism and analysis.

title: Clinical Education II
description: This is second in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and ASRT competencies. Course offers experiences in chart recognition, basic patient assessment, patient communication, and basic departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, abdomen, upper extremity, lower extremity and shoulder girdle radiographic positioning are emphasized.

title: Lebanese Cooking
description: Baba ghanouj, Lebanese tabouli salad, tomatoes kibee.

title: Paramedic Procedures Lab II
description: This laboratory course develops overall patient management skills. Covers equipment, assessment, evaluation, treatment, documentation, communication and more. Lab activities focus on the management of the cardiac patient, mega-code scenarios, spinal immobilization, traumatic injuries, and traumatic resuscitation.

title: Adult Tap Dance 2
description: If you have some experience with tap, join Emily Goward for this intermediate level class. This is a great opportunity to review the basic techniques and add another session of exercise to your life. Bring tap shoes.

title: Probation and Parole
description: Covers treatment of convicted law violators by correctional field services before and after prison, analysis of the role of probation and parole officers, and evaluation of community resources applied to the correctional task.

title: Ease Pain & Chronic Disease
description: If you are tired of living with pain and swelling, join this class with naturopath Mary Born to find new solutions that don't involve prescription drugs. You will learn that often the root cause of pain and chronic disease is inflammation. Discover how many people have reduced pain and inflammation using food choices, supplements, antioxidants and lifestyle changes. See how reversing inflammation helps alleviate gum disease, arthritis pain, constipation, fatigue, constant hunger, breathing problems, risk for heart disease and more.

title: Retirement Planning Today
description: In this course you'll learn how to: manage investment risks, use new tax laws to your advantage, make informed decisions about your company retirement plan, and adjust your estate plan to function properly under the new laws. However, money is only one aspect of retirement planning. This course blends financial education with life planning to help you build wealth, align your money with your values, and achieve your retirement lifestyle goals. This course includes a 225-page illustrated textbook. Class sizes are limited so register today.

title: Adobe Acrobat: Intermediate
description: This class is designed for people who know how to create PDF documents and are interested in enhancing existing PDF files using Acrobat's more advanced features. Learn to insert one PDF file into another PDF file, add digital signatures, passwords, security settings and restrictions. You will create and modify PDF forms; add radio buttons, check boxes, drop down lists; preview and export forms. Enhance document navigation and create bookmarks. Learn to use Acrobat in the document review cycle, create comment markups, cloud callouts and more.

title: Plmb Capstone Lab
description: PLMB 190 serves as the culminating capstone experience for students in the residential plumbing program. Students apply the knowledge and skills gained from prior coursework in a supervised residential plumbing lab or approved industry worksite. Emphasis is placed on demonstrating competency in system installation, troubleshooting, repair practices, safety protocols, and code compliance. Students complete hands-on projects, real-world service simulations, and employer-aligned tasks that reflect current industry standards. The course reinforces professional behavior, communication, technical accuracy, and job readiness for entry-level plumbing employment or apprenticeship placement.

title: The Fall Garden
description: Autumn is more than a garden clean-up season; it is also the beginning of your spring garden and an opportunity to create a winter landscape. Maintenance tasks such as pruning, transplanting, mulching and preparing the soil for spring plants will be covered. In addition, find out how you can enjoy fall blooms and a beautiful winter scene in your own backyard. Jane Geisler, Advanced Master Gardener, will take you through the steps to a healthy, well-maintained seasonal landscape.

title: Intro Integrated Manufacturing
description: Introduces integrated manufacturing systems. Presents the technology associated with a complex integrated manufacturing system through working with the SMC-FMS-200. Discusses manufacturing processes of feeding, handling, verification, and loading operations.

title: Apple Support
description: An introductory-level course focusing on using and supporting Apple products with primary emphasis on the Apple Operating Systems (OS), including installing, configuring, using, maintaining, troubleshooting, and securing. Working with applications, file management, network connections, peripherals, mobile products, and cross-platform compatibility are also covered. Suitable for end-users seeking an in-depth knowledge of Apple products or help desk specialists/computer technicians who will be supporting Apple products within an organization.

title: Abs, Glutes & Thighs
description: Burn, fat... tighten your abs... slim and tone your inner and outer thighs... lift your derriere! This challenging but fun workout sculpts and tightens your lower body. Increase your energy level and feel better in an amazingly short time. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. However, beginners may need to adapt some of the exercises. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Indust Appl of Algebraic Princ
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas.

title: Easy Entertain- Arm Chair Ath
description: Having the gang over to watch the game? Len Charla, cookbook author and sports fan, has held many game parties for friends and family over the past three decades. Learn how to wow your guests with party food that is easy to prepare, fun to serve and that makes for great eating - and an unforgettable game experience. Len provides hand-out receipes and techniques that are easy to follow.

title: Internet-Beginners & Tinkerers
description: Find out how easy it is to search the internet for a potpourri of websites in this non-threatening atmosphere. See how to shop online and order airline reservations. Students will explore search engines such as Yahoo and Google to obtain information related to their interest. Also, learn toolbar features such as history, refresh, favorites, home and others.

title: Get Organized for Yr End & Hol
description: Are you wasting so much time looking for items that you give up and re-buy them? Are you overwhelmed by too many papers and often pay bills late because your system of paying them is not working? Join professional organizer Theresa Brune as she teaches pain-free techniques for organizing and parting with items. Theresa will also provide tips for getting organized for the holidays so you can enjoy them with less stress.

title: Hearty Vegetarian Dishes
description: Even avid meat-eaters are adding vegetarian meals to their diets more frequently. Come learn about the value of meatless eating, and experience the pleasure of preparing tasty vegetarian and vegan meals. Instructor Joy Callan will once again surprise you with a variety of new recipes. You're in for a treat! Bring an apron. Class limited to 14. Enroll early.

title: You're on the Air!
description: Voice-overs are hot today! With such notable talent as Morgan Freeman, Glenn Close, and Alec Baldwin lending their voices to commercials, films, and videos, one would think that it would be next to impossible to break into this field. Not so! In this class you'll learn about an exciting new way to get around the competition and actually turn voice-overs into a thriving full or part-time business! The instructor will discuss the voice-over business as a whole, the numerous opportunities, the incredible income potential, and will discuss the all-important "demo" and how to have it produced. You'll step up to the mic and do some practice recording, and best of all, you'll hear the results!

title: Scenic Painting & Construction
description: This course explores beginning and intermediate techniques in traditional scenic contruction and painting, with direct application to live production, in a hands on environment.

title: Polish Soups 2
description: Trying to get back to your Polish roots? Join Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suterto make a Wild Mushroom Soup with Pasta. She will also show you how to make Sorrel (green leaf herb similar to spinich)with hard boiled eggs. You will also learn to make the Polish Zurrek, a sour soup made with fermented rye along with some village specialities. Wear an apron and bring a container.

title: Electronic Auto Chassis
description: This course is a study of electronic braking and chassis systems. Examines controller networks as they apply to steering, suspension, and braking systems. Explores computerized vehicle stability systems and components.

title: Ceramics I
description: A beginning level college course in ceramic materials and processes. Covers various hand-building techniques, basic surface and glazing techniques, and the differences between gas and electric firing. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Exploring Popular Music
description: This class explores the history of popular music from the 1950's through today, with emphasis on the relationship between music and sociopolitical issues.

title: Master Pivot Tables in Excel
description: Pivot tables are one of the hidden Excel gems that intimidate most users. It is a surprisingly easy way to quickly summarize and analyze large amounts of data organized in rows or columns. Mastering this tool will help you stand out from approximately 85% of all other Excel users who never take the time to learn to use this powerful feature. Overcome your fears with this course and become the de-facto Excel guru in your office. Previous Excel experience is a must! Students should bring a flash drive to save class work.

title: Gentle Yoga
description: Start your day by experiencing the health benefits includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance and alignment; and you will achieve mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Tour Capitals- Eastern Europe
description: Spend a crisp fall Tuesday evening traveling via a slide program to the fascinating capitals of east central Europe. You'll feel as if you're at a charming old world cafe as you enjoy pastries and experience the highlights of Warsaw, Prague, Budapest, Vienna, Bucharest, and Sophia. On Saturday, you will join instructor Don Samull for a delicious eastern European meal at one of the finest Hungarian restaurants in the area. Cost of meal included in course fee.

title: Polish Churches of Hamtramck
description: Visit the beautiful historic churches of Hamtramck, Detroit's Polish enclave, and then enjoy lunch at a restaurant in the area. Join your instructor, Don Samull, on a guided, self-driving and walking tour of the three major Roman Catholic churches of that city, as well as the quaint Polish National Catholic church. Maps showing where to meet will be sent to registered students prior to the tour. The cost of the meal is included.

title: Discover the World of Media
description: Got a passion for film, TV, audio, and other media? Here's your ticket to the action! Hosted by the Henry Ford College Media Communication Arts Department, we're rolling out the red carpet for you to explore everything from the magic of film and TV to the beats of a live radio station, and the craft behind creative storytelling through scripts and podcasts. Ever dreamt of voicing animated characters, announcing the latest news or sports, or mastering the art of the interview? We've got you covered. Dive into the roles of your favorite actors, directors, writers, and DJs. You'll get your hands on the real work: scriptwriting, shooting, lighting, editing, and even stepping in front of the camera or behind the mic. And there's a bonus! Your creativity might just hit the airwaves on our campus station, WHFR-FM, or light up the screen for friends and family to see. Get ready for four days filled with creativity, learning, and fun. It's your time to shine as an HFC Film and Media (FAM) Explorer... Lights, camera, action!

title: Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance and alignment in this twelve week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing and relaxation techniques learned in Hatha Yoga 1, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Tour Hamtramck!
description: You will explore the diverse cultural world of Hamtramck--up close and personal. This walking tour will introduce you to some of the city's important sites and institutions, from the library to the churches to the main shopping street. (There are those who say if you can't find it in Hamtramck you might as well stop looking!) We will emphasize the strong influence of its early Polish immigrants without overlooking the diverse array of newcomers and their influence. Lunch will be on your own at the nationally known St. Florian's Strawberry Festival. Wear your walking shoes and bring an umbrella if need be. (But we're told it never rains in Hamtramck.)

title: Turn Recipes Into a Gold Mine
description: Famous Amos converted his cookie recipe into millions of dollars. Learn from professional caterer Andrea Taylor how you can make money using your kitchen and family recipes. She will cover calculating prices, finding the right audience, marketing specialty items, meeting sanitation guidelines, getting financing, and creating a hit. Take this class as your first step to more cash.

title: Weld Joint Design(code,tools)
description: Introduces welding codes and standards, identification of welding flaws, and the tools used to measure aspects of the weld. Emphasizes safety protocols and proper usage of hand tools in a welding lab.

title: Get the Red Out
description: Holding anger and grudges can have physiological effects on the mind and body. Biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you these effects and discuss the many emotions often vented as anger. Learn to identify the steps of escalating anger in another person and receive a wealth of helpful tips, including nutrition, to help you deal with anger. discover how to let go of grudges and resentment and why it is important. $5 material fee due to instructor the first session.

title: Photoshop 1
description: Photoshop is the premier software package for image processing and photo retouching. Become acquainted with this powerful and versatile program as you learn to work with the selection tools, filters, layers, scanning, and more. This class is recommended before taking some of the other graphics design classes. There will be a computer for every student in this class.

title: Jazz Improvisation 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of jazz improvisation in which skills are developed through the study of jazz theory and the performances of a variety of jazz styles in a small group, jazz combo setting.

title: Hearty Soups & Stews, Veg Styl
description: Our chefs Sherry Duquet and Sandy Boulton will be instructing an introductory cooking class preparing three comfort food favorites, with a healthy twist, as vegetarian (vegan) cuisine. You will learn to prepare Cajun Jambalaya, Chik'n noodle soup and Old fashioned baked potato soup with all the trimmings. Class includes interactive instruction, recipes, shopping suggestions and tips for converting meaty favorites into vegetarian gourmet cuisine. Bring an apron and an appetite. Basic understanding of using cooking utensils and prepping food is helpful. Wheat and soy products are used during cooking.

title: Basic Hydraulics
description: Introduces basic hydraulic principles, laws, components, and symbols. Topics include: safety; the use of tools; and procedures in the design, building, testing, troubleshooting, and repair of industrial hydraulic systems. Includes demonstrations and extensive laboratory activities.

title: Make Over Life With Feng Shui
description: Improve your chances for good luck in many aspects of your life from money to relationships through Feng Shui, the ancient Chinese art of placement. Using a thoroughly modern approach you'll learn how to make simple, yet powerful, changes to your home and environment. Find your way through this fascinating subject as well as a better way through life. Madge Lawson, your instructor, is a graduate of New York's Sheffield School of Design and is a practicing Feng Shui consultant.

title: Perennials: Perpetual Joy!
description: Plant perennials once and enjoy their beauty year after year! Discover how these plants can add excitement to any garden, offering dramatic and interesting ways to landscape your outdoor living room. Learn from a Master Gardener how to pick the right sun and shade perennials, amend and prepare the soil, and fertilize, divide, and prune perennial gardens. You'll also see how easy it is to grow them in containers for years of successful blooms.

title: Intermediate Word
description: Make full use of Word 2000 by learning to use some of its advanced features. Learn how to use existing and create new templates; create letters, envelopes, and labels; use Word 2000's style features; create and modify outlines; create and modify headers and footers; insert and format tables; create and modify columns; and insert graphic elements. Microsoft Office User Specialist approved courseware (MOUS) materials included. Introduction to Word 2000 or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: Predict Maint-Vibration Analys
description: Provides specialized instruction in diagnosing and resolving machinery vibration in rotating equipment, the most cost-effective methods to reduce maintenance costs, and how to extend machinery life.

title: Adv Cardiac Life Support
description: Students will be able to analyze life threatening cardiac emergencies and apply advanced principles of cardiac and respiratory anatomy and physiology. This course is designed for paramedical, medical, nursing or allied health personnel who daily occupation demands proficiency and certification in the knowledge of ACLS skills. Health care/nursing students must be in their final semester. The curriculum adheres to American Heart Association 2001 Guidelines. Prerequisite: successful completion of Basic Life Support (BLS) Healthcare Provider course within the past two years. Bring BLS card to first class session. Textbooks ($50) purchased from instructor.
prerequisite_text: successful completion of Basic Life Support (BLS) Healthcare Provider course within the past two years. Bring BLS card to first class session. Textbooks ($50) purchased from instructor.

title: Digital Theory
description: Covers digital theory. Introduces basic gates and logic circuits, counters, registers, timers, LED and LCD displays, arithmetic circuits, and number systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Ebay Photo Workshop
description: Do you eBay? Do you have problems getting your photos that attract more bidders to your eBay auctions? Here's a one day workshop to learn some techniques from a veteran eBay seller. Learn camera features to look for, lighting tips, photo composition and types of photos you should or should not include. Find out how to get photos from your camera to your PC and then to eBay. Learn about useful software that you might wish to investigate.

title: Psychic Detectives
description: Join Kristy Robinett, a professional Psychic Medium, as she shares stories of her work with law enforcement. Hear about times when the tables were turned, and Kristy became the one chased - by the unlawful party! [There were even some cases where Kristy required police protection as a result of her involvement! ] Kristy will explain how psychics can help, and hurt, police cases. You won't want to miss this fascinating presentation.

title: Faux the Fun of It
description: Make any surface or wall come to life with creative painting techniques. In this hands-on workshop you'll work with a professional decorative artist to learn about paints, surface preparation and the tools needed to complete various faux finishes and painting techniques. Even novices can master these techniques. It's great fun and a fairly inexpensive way to redecorate. Water-based products will be used. Wear old clothes. $15 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Weld Metallurgy-Ferrous Metal
description: Covers the mechanical and physical properties and the crystalline structures of ferrous metals. Also explores heat treating of ferrous metals.

title: Byzantine Bracelet
description: Create a gorgeous sterling silver chain bracelet using the process of hand crafting chain. Monica Larson will take you through this surprisingly easy technique. Some homework will be necessary between sessions. $35 material fee (including 2 sets of pliers) payable to instructor in class.

title: Auto Engine Dynamometer
description: An advanced-level course in the purpose, construction, and operation of the automotive engine dynamometer. Presents how to conduct engine tests of horsepower and torque, and covers test cell setup of various sensor types and adaptation.

title: Italian for Beginners
description: Acquire basic Italian language skils and learn the cultural nuances necessary to communicate for business or pleasure. Emphasis will be on useful vocabulary, basic sentences, and grammar that will assist you in greetings, directions, money, foods and restaurants, shopping, time, days, colors and much more. Books are available for purchase the first night of class.

title: N American Tech Excellence Cer
description: Explores various types of basic work knowledge of the Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning and Refrigeration (HVACR) systems that are installed in the HVACR field. Topics covered include safety, trade tools, heat and matter, electrical fundamentals, transformers and motors, electrical diagrams, and proper use of required test instruments. As a result of this class, students will learn about the structure and HVACR communication skills expected of them in the technology and skilled trade industry. This course helps to prepare students to take the NATE Core Certification Exam based on ACCA, NATE, and RSES information.

title: Special Topics in Energy Techn
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current energy technology trends. Specific special topics are announced together with the prerequisites each term. The student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic. Course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree or certificate.

title: Accounting Fundamentals
description: Develop financial awareness and increase the odds of your business succeeding. You'll learn to do double-entry bookkeeping, analyze and record financial transactions, prepare various financial reports, and more.

title: Vocal Jazz Ensemble 1
description: First of four sequential semesters of vocal jazz ensemble, covering preparation and performance of a variety of jazz and popular music in an ensemble of 8-20 voices. Group performs for various HFC and community functions. New singers may audition before or during the first week of class. No preparation is necessary, but the demonstration of the ability to sing in tune and hold a challenging part (soprano, alto, tenor, or bass) is required.

title: Intro to Energy Technology
description: Introduces traditional sources of energy and provides an overview of the various forms of energy and their applications. Covers alternative, renewable, and traditional non-renewable sources of energy with consideration for energy conservation and use and energy transmission systems.

title: Differentiated Instruction
description: Differentiated instruction (DI) is becoming a mainstay in classrooms across the country as educators are starting to see the ways that the traditional classroom setting limits their ability to reach diverse learners. Join us on this journey through 10 practical DI integration strategies! Count on at least three sample integration lessons on each strategy, and just think of all the ways that you can apply them to improve learning outcomes for your students.

title: Plumbing Fixture Installation
description: Covers the operation and availability of common plumbing fixtures and appliances. Focuses on the selection and installation of fixtures in residential, commercial, and industrial applications. The control and accessories used in fuel burning appliances, the differences in vent capacity and vent type, and the steps necessary to place an appliance in service are covered. Explores how electricity is incorporated into a piping system including concepts of energy savings and safety.

title: Intro Process Instrumentation
description: Studies physical and chemical variables and the various instruments used to sense, measure, transmit, and control these variables. Introduces control loops and their components including controllers, regulators, sensors, and final control elements. Also discusses how to create instrumentation drawings and diagrams when developing and analyzing control loops.

title: Auto Diesel Principles
description: Explains the operating principles of the four-stroke diesel engine used in the automobile, including the construction, service, and diagnosis of the various engine systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Success With Less Stress (nur)
description: Do you dream of a day where you are well focused, completed your projects, and still have energy left for fun or family? Live your dream by joining Sandy Baumann, popular author of Feed Your Brain for Success, for an inspiring and lighthearted class. Find secrets for improving your productivity and mood with food, beverages, recreation, lifestyle choices, and simple supplements. Enjoy some laughter and a smorgasbord of easy ideas to fuel your brain and put the brakes on your stress! $12 materials fee due to instructor in class. RNs & LPNs who complete this class earn 4 nursing contact hours.

title: Heart Health
description: Here's a class for those with high cholesterol, heart conditions, high blood pressure or those seeking understanding and application of nutrition advice for a heart-healthy lifestyle. Dietician Lourdes Lore will help you find out about the diet that can help lower your blood pressure that does double duty to minimize PMS symptoms too.

title: First Aid for Dogs
description: Just like other family members, your pet can become ill or injured. To learn how to care for your pet in an emergency, join Jacqueline Renaud in this class approved by the American Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals. In addition to basic first aid care, you'll learn about medical and environment emergencies, common illnesses and conditions, administration of medicine and more.

title: EV Battery Technician Cert
description: Participants in this certificate course will acquire the necessary skills for designing, operating, testing, maintaining, and replacing battery cells and packs for various applications. In addition, the certificate allows participants to choose the right technology for the right challenge and evaluate battery performance. Moreover, to be able to work out a solid total solution, the battery management systems, the charging and maintenance of batteries, and their recycling process are also discussed in more detail. Finally, we give sufficient attention to safely handling these electrical applications, ensuring the well-being of both individuals and the environment.This certification empowers learners to: . Evaluate what battery technology is best suited for which application based on its characteristics. . Examine the advantages and disadvantages of the most commonly used battery technologies. . Design and implement the best-fit battery solution for each application. . Safely handle, test, repair, and dispose of battery systems according to legal obligations.

title: Adv Comp Assisted N/C Program
description: A study in advanced methods of part programming using CAD/CAM software (Mastercam, Catia, and UG NX) on a personal computer. Emphasizes programming three-dimensional (3-D) parts. Class assignments include multiple-part programming.

title: ELL American Culture I
description: Introduces low-beginning-level knowledge of American culture for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-102A. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Wall Makeovers With Paint
description: Give your home a fresh new look with a simple coat of paint. Instead of hiring someone, attend this workshop and learn to do it yourself like a "pro." A professional painter will explain how to properly plan and organize a project; discuss different paint types, finishes, tools and supplies; demonstrate proper wall preparation (including drywall repairs); and share some "tricks of the trade" that will simplify your life. Bring your questions. $10 material fee payable to instructor.

title: Kinesiology for PTA's
description: Reviews surface anatomy and functional anatomy, muscles and muscle function, proper posture and analysis of posture, and gait analysis and deviations. Medical Terminology is reinforced. A lab component familiarizes the student with functional aspects of human motion.

title: A Taste of Italy: Alla Carta
description: One evening a month, Italian couple Robert Cascardo and Cristina Cotronei take you on a journey to the heart of Italy as they prepare, with your help, an incredible, regionally authentic, Italian four-course meal. Experience the almost mythic approach to dining that makes Italy famous - the Meal as an Occasion. The menu is planned only one week in advance to take advantage of fresh seasonal foods, and is paired with appropriate wine. Traditionally, every town, every rural area in Italy has its own unique culinary twists and variations, so the menu possibilities are seeemingly endless.

title: Ophthalmic Surgical Assisting
description: Introduces ophthalmic surgical procedures, including the indications, goals, and steps of select ophthalmic procedures. Emphasizes principles of asepsis, and care and maintenance of ophthalmic surgical instruments.

title: Heart, Cancer Prev Cook Class
description: Dietitian Lourdes Reyes Lore will show you how to prepare easy, healthful yet delicious dishes and fun, healthful snacks your whole family can enjoy. Bring an apron.

title: ELL Reading & Study Skills 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college-preparatory reading and study skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELL-182. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Color Theory
description: An introduction to the basic principles and theories of color. The course examines color properties, color schemes and harmonies, as well as the illusionary values and emotive qualities of color, and how these are used in art. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Advanced Modern Dance
description: Explores the finer points of Modern Dance. Primary emphasis is on performing advanced movement phrases along with the study and practice of specific Modern Dance techniques. Course also supports the development of choreographic skill and stage presence.

title: Aroma Therapy-Health, Beauty
description: Indulge your senses by making your own perfume that smells good, is all natural and enlivens your spirit. In this sweet-smelling class with aromatherapist Mary Born, learn to blend essential oils into aromas that energize, relax or entice. The course fee includes one misting spray. You may blend your fragrance into an additional purse size, roll on or a body lotion the same night for a fee of $10 to $15 (based on oils chosen) paid to the instructor. Seats are limited. Enroll today!

title: Earn & Learn Read & Math Refre
description: Program is a partnership between HFCC and Southwest Solutions designed to aid Earn and Learn Program participants who wish to improve their reading and math skills in order to pursue manufacturing-based employment and/or pre-apprenticeship training.

title: Professional Issues in PT
description: This is intended to integrate classroom and clinical experiences. The course facilitates the transition of the PTA student to the PTA professional. Elements of professionalism are discussed including integrity in practice, advocacy, core and value-based behaviors, ethical principles and reasoning, and professional relationships with other health care professionals, the public, supervisors, PT's and clinical instructors. The course prepares the student PTA for a career in physical therapy including resume and cover letter writing, interviewing skills, educational opportunities, and continuing education. The course also offers preparation for the NPTE.

title: Camp Entrepreneurship
description: Curious about learning how to save, invest, build wealth, or start a business? Camp G.R.O.W. teaches students how to gain resources, and obtain wealth in a fun, exciting, and informative manner. Campers are immersed in the essentials of financial literacy, networking, goal setting, business planning and entrepreneurship. Using skill building games, visual aids and hands on activities, students will learn how to make, manage, and grow their money wisely. Participants will get experience making financial decisions they will face as adults, and finish the course with stronger business, financial leteracy, and communication skills.

title: Easy Holiday Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Whether you're planning the ultimate holiday party or want to make impressive first courses for a special dinner, this class is all you need for the big event. If you love to entertain but don't want to fuss for days over the food, have an appetizer party. Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estates for the past 14 years, will show you how to prepare some of his favorite easy - but elegant - hors d'oeuvres. Bring an apron and come ready to cook and eat.

title: HFV- Internet for Beginners
description: Around the world in 80 seconds! The internet helps you communicate instantly with family and friends, find travel bargains, shop for the best deals, and research any topic, including health questions. In four 2-hour sessions you will learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, surf the internet to find information, bookmark favorite sites, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. You will have time to practice on the internet in class, so bring your questions. THIS CLASS IS RESTRICTED TO HENRY FORD VILLAGE RESIDENTS AND IS BILLED BY SECTION.

title: Psychiatric Mental Health Nurs
description: Introduces the second semester nursing student to the role of the nurse as a provider of care for clients with mental health disorders with emphasis on therapeutic communication techniques. Course presents the mental-health/mental-illness continuum as a framework for differentiating between healthy and unhealthy mental health behaviors. Discusses how to utilizes critical thinking skills, in a systematic, problem solving process, to provide safe and effective care to clients in the psychiatric mental health setting. This course includes supervised clinical agency/laboratory practicums. Course comprised of two hours of lecture and six hours of lab/clinical per week.

title: Process Technology Equipment
description: Introduces students to the purpose, components, operation, and troubleshooting of the equipment used within the process industry.

title: Spec Topics in Electrical Tech
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current electrical technology trends. Specific special topics and the prerequisites will be announced each term. Course may be repeated when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Intro to Healthcare Profession
description: This course is designed to introduce students to various health care professions. It includes skills and topics common to all healthcare providers such as ethical and legal issues, professionalism, cultural considerations, vital signs, body mechanics, communication, infection control, and evidence based practice. This course will also introduce key elements in the formation and modification of medical terms. This course has a lab hands on component.

title: System Technology
description: This course is an overview of the technology found in the integrated systems of today's manufacturing facilities. The course examines the symbols, functions and circuits associated with Fluid Power, Electrical, Mechanical, Control and Communications found in modern integrated systems. Pre-requisites: None.

title: Stage Costuming
description: Introduces contemporary aesthetic and creative design principles for theatrical costuming, with an emphasis on line, form, meeting production requirements, budgeting, and thematic unity.

title: Winterize for Winning Roses
description: Before winter winds blow, gardeners in colder climates need to protect their roses to ensure lush blooms next year. Nancy Lindley, author of Roses for Michigan and owner of a popular Michigan rose store, will teach you how to get roses ready for Michigan's winters, including tips on spring and summer horticultural practices that increase winter hardiness. She'll also share photos of some new, hardy roses you may want to plant next spring. Learn her favoriate sources of rose information for winter garden reading and projects. You may buy an autographed copy of Roses for Michigan after class for $20.

title: Intro to Sociology
description: Introduces the sociological approach to understanding culture and society. Examines human behavior in a group context, focusing on the prominent influence of culture and social structure on individuals. Connections between individuals and a variety of social institutions, such as the economy, government, and mass media, are examined and compared in U.S. society and on a global scale. Focuses on cultural diversity and social inequalities, such as those related to class, race, ethnicity and gender.

title: Solidworks Level III - Applica
description: An advanced industrial applications course utilizing 3-dimensional (3D) advanced construction tools. Focuses on how to apply key concepts from prior SolidWorks courses to real-world industrial projects. Also examines how to create, edit, and export bills of materials; apply assembly modeling procedures; create exploded views; edit components in an assembly; add and constrain mates; utilize simulation Xpress tool; and perform basic motion analysis.

title: Birding Field Trips
description: After attending the Introduction to Bird Watching class (or equivalent experience), participants may choose from a variety of one-day field trips designed to focus on specific types of birds present. February - Feeder Birds; March - Gullds; April - Ducks; May - Warblers. These field trips will provide participants with an opportunity to practice their identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are planned from late winter through spring. All field trips will meet at the HFCC parking lot where we will carpool to the sites, generally within 50 miles, to look for our target birds.

title: DC and AC Electricity
description: Introduces DC and AC electrical theory, including electrical definitions, units of electrical measure, series and parallel resistive circuits, capacitance, inductance, AC voltage measurements, and AC calculations. Explores usage of meters and/or oscilloscopes.

title: Literacy Foundations
description: Explores the principles, methods, and materials for teaching literacy through a research-based, integrated approach with children PreK-5. Emphasis is placed on the influence of a child's linguistic, cultural, and social background, fostering engaging literacy development in diverse and multicultural communities. Instructional methods include lectures, discussions, videos, small group work, hands-on experiences, and individual and group projects. The primary components of reading - phonemic awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, and comprehension - will be studied through assessment strategies and applied to teaching methods. The NAEYC (student learning outcomes) and MDE Quality Standards are addressed in this course.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-102W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Making Sense of Your Retiremen
description: If you don't prepare for your retirement, no one else will. That's why it's important to take time now to discuss your retirement goals. An experienced financial advisor will help you learn more about the five basic questions you should ask yourself to prepare for retirement. Discover options you can use now to save for retirement, and how time can help or hurt your retirement goals. Take the first steps toward a successful retirement. Call today to reserve space for yourself and a spouse or friend.

title: Bmra Decon. Worker Training
description: This training prepares participants to be workforce ready for entry into the deconstruction, building materials reuse and related industries. Course delivery focuses on 10 industry-recognized core competencies required of deconstruction workers: intro. to deconstruction, evaluating the site, safety, hazardous materials, tools, plans, salvage, full deconstruction, materials management and job closeout. The course also prepares participants for the credentialing exams, a step toward achieving the International Building, Materials Reuse Association (BMRA) credential of deconstruction worker.

title: Knee, Ankle & Feet Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols to relieve pain and stiffness in knees, ankles and feet. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve stiffness and pain - without taking drugs.

title: Do It Yourself Floral
description: Do you love DIY projects? This course will teach you how to take a simple bunch of flowers and turn them into a breathtaking centerpiece. Using flowers like roses and hydrangea you'll learn how to make a table centerpiece perfect for a wedding, special event or holiday dinner that you can take home and enjoy. No experience required! All tools and supplies will be provided. Registration for this class closes on November 5 so supplies can be ordered.

title: Creating Web Pages II
description: Are you using HTML to its fullest potential? In this hands-on course, you'll become adept at using color, tables and frames to give your Web pages a more polished and professional look, improved navigation and more organization. Beef up the fun factor with audio and interactive forms, and learn how to save hours of time and effort with Cascading Style sheets.

title: Be Safe From Crime
description: Can you become a crime victim because you talk on a cell phone outside a store? Learn from Wayne County Sheriff's Deptuy Ki Sobol how criminals pick their targets for identity theft, home burglary, purse snatching and carjacking. Discover red flags that signal danger for walkers, joggers and travelers, including special precautions for rape prevention. Sign up today to keep yourself and your children safe at work, at school, in your home and in your vehicle.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Introduces matrix and linear algebra. Covers systems of linear equations, matrix operations and properties of matrices, determinants, the n-dimensional real vector space, general vector spaces, inner product spaces, linear transformations, and eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Presents various applications. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Intro to Homeland Security
description: Provides students with a comprehensive overview of Homeland Security policies, practices, and programs. Also discusses local, state, and federal initiatives, as well as the restructuring of the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Recession-Proof Your Wallet
description: Are you tired of living check to check every month? Did you know that up to 40% of all Americans spend more money than they actually earn? Learn the secrets of wealth management and how the rich are able to create money-making opportunities with little or none of their own money. Additionally, you will learn how to work less, increase earnings, pay less in taxes and much more. Don't miss out on this opportunity. Financial freedom is a choice. Make yours today. Join us!

title: Mechanical Power Transmission
description: Presents installation and maintenance of mechanical power transmission systems. Covers bearings, couplings, belts, chains, shafts, pulleys, and speed reducers used in the modern factory by skilled trades.

title: AC Theory & Equipment
description: Examines alternating current theory. Includes right triangle trigonometry and complex numbers to facilitate the AC calculations. Discusses concepts of reactance, impedance, and power factor. Compares single phase and three phase power. Introduces single phase and three phase alternators, motors, transformers, and both heating and lighting loads.

title: Getting Started With Email
description: Do you have an email account but feel that you really don't understand the features? Or maybe you'd like to open a free gmail account, the new electronic mail system everyone is talking about. Come and have Noel help you build your comfort level with creating email letters, attaching files (pictures), working with your contacts or address books, and creating your own signatures. Learn how to deal with unwanted emails and protect yourself against scams and frauds. Prequisite: Into to Computers or equivalent experience a must!

title: ALP: Reading and Writing
description: This course is intended for students whose placement review indicates the need for additional instructional support in order for them to be successful in ENG-131. ENG-094 is a co-requisite of ENG-131A as part of the Accelerated Learning Program and provides writing, reading, study skills, and non-cognitive instructional support of the ENG-131 curriculum. Course work will help students develop as college writers and readers through discussion, workshop, homework, and occasional reading and writing assignments that complement ENG-131 work.

title: Integrating Tech in Classroom
description: Join us for an exciting and fun-filled tour of the technology-friendly classroom. Educational technology is advancing at an astounding rate, offering today's busy teacher quick and easy solutions for more interactive lesson plans, exciting WebQuests, and challenging assignments. Discover the power and creativity that technology can bring to your classroom as we look at basic integration, subject-specific activities, and smart ways to make your integration seamless. Take your teaching to another level with these fun new ways to incorporate technology into your lesson plans.

title: Introd to Machine Tool Probing
description: Covers the elementary functions and application of the electronic probe on a Vertical Machining Center. Topics include set-up and calibration of the probe and use and application of the following cycles - Bore/Boss measuring cycle, Web/Pocket measuring cycle, and internal and external cycles. Laboratory activities.

title: Cont Exp Watercolors-Intermed
description: Gain more freedom in your compositions while reviewing and practicing your skills. Learn new techniques and helpful tips. This class is a great opportunity to apply your painting skills to create note cards, book covers, scrapbook pages and more. Bring your painting supplies the first night of class. (See supply list for Exploring Watercolors). $5 due to instructor for additional materials to be used in class. Supplies to be discussed in class.

title: Radiographic Procedures III
description: This lecture/laboratory course is the third in a four-course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This 8-week course reinforces fundamental radiology positioning terminology and provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for skull and headwork. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: eBay Photo Workshop
description: Do you eBay? Do you have problems getting your photos into your auctions? Would you like to have photos that attract more bidders to your eBay auctions? Here's a one-day workshop to learn some techniques from a veteran eBay seller. Learn camera features to look for, lighting tips, photo composition, and types of photos you should or should not include. Find out how to get photos from your camera to your PC and then to eBay. Learn about useful software that you might wish to investigate.

title: Specialties of Kenya
description: Enjoy popular dishes, cooking methods and food culture with Chef Marilyn Suter just returning from a recent visit to Kenya. We will make ugali, a Kenyan staple, goat, curried bananas, a great vegetarian bean dish, and more. Bring an apron and a sense of adventure!

title: Comp Angles & Adv Projection
description: Focuses on the mastery of solid trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Includes the solution of solid trigonometric problems using graphic and analytical solutions and problem solving techniques. Requires modeling clay.

title: Get Comfortable With Internet
description: Here's your chance to have a knowledgeable instructor walk you through the internet world. Topics include choosing how to gain access to the internet, using search tools with confidence, preventing pop-ups, guarding against identity theft and scams, and setting up bookmarks to your favorite sites. Learn about "cookies", Map Quest, Google, Mozilla, Microsoft Internet Explorer, Abode Reader and more.

title: Make Trad Pierogis
description: If you are not of Polish descent, you may never have tasted authentic, homemade Pierogis. Now is your chance to join instructor Marilyn Suter to make and eat the real thing. You will mix and handle the dough, use a variety of fillings, and stuff your pierogis. Bring an apron. You will eat what you create, and take home any leftovers. Class limited to 14. Enroll early!

title: Intermediate Ballet II
description: This is the second of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of ballet technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in ballet history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and classical dance principles. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of this traditional and foundational dance art. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of ballet.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, placement, and turn-out, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Java Programming
description: An intermediate-level course which examines Java programming language and its various components. Emphasizes designing, coding, testing, and debugging programs using the Java language through laboratory exercises and detailed lectures.

title: Creating a Classroom Web Site
description: Learn how to create a classroom Web site in this fun, easy-to-understand course for teachers. In no time, you'll build a site with text, images, animations, tables, links, and more. Then you'll create a WebQuest and a blog to add to your site. You'll do all this and more as you discover how simple creating a classroom Web site can be and how having one can make you a more effective and dynamic teacher.

title: Cyber Elec Crime Seminar
description: This workshop is designed for law enforcement officers. Topics covered include identify theft, credit card fraud and credit card skimming and protection of computer infrastructure.

title: Revealing Your Dharma
description: Dharma is the law of our existence, the path of our destiny. Some people know exactly who they are and why they are here, some have a partial understanding, and some don't have a clue, but they would like to learn more about it. Through lectures, discussions, group activities, videos, and written exercises this class will assist participants in a quest for Dharma. The aim of this class is to let the group work together to increase each person's understanding of his own Dharma and then suggest ways to enhance your ongoing pursuit of it. The group will also explore ways to create a legacy.

title: Birding: Focus on Ducks
description: We have heard that ducks fly south for the winter but do they all go? When is a duck not a duck? Spend the morning with Allen seeking out our feathered friends.

title: Heal Brain After Accidents
description: Did you know that simple falls and low-speed car accidents can produce hearing and vision changes, insomnia, panic attacks, persistent muscle pain, joint stiffness and depression? Let brain-stem chiropractor Dr. Barry Hobbs show you how these symptoms relate to certain blockages in nerve energy and can be treated with specific manipulation therapies. Then hear biochemist Sandy Baumann describe the foods, supplements and lifestyle strategies she used after a card accident to heal her brain from anxiety, memory loss and insomnia. Come find hope for your unresolved health problems. $12 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Quick Spanish for Travelers
description: Tune up your artistic signing abilities. Join Anne Bolander, an author and member of the deaf community, for this unique opportunity to learn how to incorporate facial expression, body movement and rhythm into your signing. Whatever your reason, join this fun, lively class and learn to sign a variety of song lyrics.

title: Honors Directed Study
description: In this sophomore-level directed study, students are challenged to apply the knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Students present summaries of their research before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty held at the end of each semester.

title: Digital Image Acquisition Mgt.
description: This course addresses all technical aspects of digital image acquisition, manipulation, and storage. Course includes digital imaging characteristics, types of imaging systems and system components, digital image acquisition and manipulation, and digital image archiving systems. Also included are principles of imaging system quality assurance and maintenance.

title: Birding: Focus on Hawks
description: We will visit a world-class hawk migration site to practice identification of hawk species in flight overhead. If weather conditions are favorable, we could see hundreds, or even thousands of hawks.

title: Spec Topics Indust Draf Techno
description: Designed to explore selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current drafting technology trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Students can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Journey Toward Peace
description: Take a step toward peace by joining four local religious leaders for an illuminating discussion. Speakers include Imam Hassan Al-Qazwini from the Islamic Center of American in Dearborn, Rabbi Jonathan Berkun from Congregation Shaarey Zedek in Southfield, Rev. Sharon Buttry, Director of REACH (an American Baptist mission in Hamtramck), and Michael Hovey, Assistant Advisor on Ecumenical and Interfaith Affairs for the Catholic Archdiocese of Detroit. Learn how three holy books, Bible, Koran and Torah challenge Christian, Muslim and Jewish people, respectively, to work toward peace. Since these three faiths trace their roots to the patriarch Abraham, you will discover the peace-making beliefs they share. Held in the Rosenau Rooms of the ASCC Building. You must pre-register.

title: Rehab Neuro Conditions Lab
description: Applies techniques learned in PTA-262 with an emphasis on the functional approach to patient treatment. Includes the use of Neurodevelopmental Treatment (NDT) and Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF) for adults with neurological conditions such as traumatic brain injury (TBI) and cerebrovascular accidents (CVAs). Guided laboratory setting.

title: Essential Paralegal Skills
description: Explores on-the-job realities of the paralegal profession with emphasis on the daily operations of a law office and the role of the paralegal in the office. Covers time management, billing, writing appropriate legal communiques, and communicating with clients.

title: Complete Stretch & Tone
description: Low impact exercise designed to improve flexibility and core strength while stretching and lengthening your muscles. Common side effects could include increased ability to concentrate, improved posture and reduced stress! Get out of your head and into your body in this yoga-inspired stretching and core conditioning class. Designed for adults of all fitness levels. Wear comfortable clothing and bring a mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Employment Skill Health Career
description: Explores the transition from campus to the workplace. Discusses career planning, workplace basics, and employer expectations. Also covers job search skills, networking, communication skills, interview techniques, organization systems, relationships, and management/employee issues. Emphasizes how to develop marketing strategies for health care positions and how to complete a resume package.

title: Success With Less Stress - New
description: Do you dream of a day where you are well focused, complete your work or projects, and still have energy left for fun or family? Live your dream by joining Sandy Baumann, popular author of Feed Your Brain for Success, for an inspiring and lighthearted class. Find secrets for improving your productivity and mood with food, beverages, recreation, lifestyle choices, and simple supplements. Enjoy some laughter and a smorgasbord of easy ideas to fuel your brain and put the brakes on your stress! $12 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Co-op in Industrial Technolog
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.

title: CCNA: Networking IV
description: This course is the last in a series of four courses in the Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA) Certificate of Achievement at HFC and focuses on Network Security. This course equips students with the knowledge and skills needed to prepare for entry-level security specialist careers. This course is a hands-on, career-oriented e-learning solution that emphasizes practical experience. It is a blended curriculum with both online and classroom learning. This course aims to develop an in-depth understanding of network security principles as well as the tools and configurations required to secure a network.

title: Stagecraft
description: Offers a basic survey of the major techniques and theories of scenography used in the modern theatre.

title: Generate Income From Portfolio
description: Investigate ways to increase current income from your portfolio using mutual funds, dividend-producing stocks, fixed annuities, and other investments in order to balance periodic dips in the stock market. Investment advisor Ken Bloom will give specific recommendations to help you preserve your principal while generating more cash flow.

title: Inter Bead Making: Gardens
description: Embellish your glass beads with florals, leaves and vines as you learn about design, color and stringer control. Learn the floral and cased cane techniques for making leaves and petals, surface decoration and clear casing.

title: Poetry & Prose: Call the Muse
description: Let the muse guide you as you write wonderful poetry inspired by your life and that of other writers. Cheryl Vatcher-Martin, a columnist, editor, award-winning poet and professor, will teach you how to turn an everyday experience or emotion into a powerful poem. Explore writing techniques that will help fill your notebook with poetry and prose. In this workshop you will practice your poetry and get feedback from your classmates. $5 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Desktop Publish- InDesign CS3
description: Use the brand new Adobe InDesign CS3 software to create your catalogs, brochures, flyers and other items. Lay out your text and grapahics like the pros and avoid having to send these items out to an expensive designer. Easily coordinate with professional printers, publish in your own office, or output to multiple media. Create rich full featured documents with easy integration with Photoshop and other programs. The instructor will get you started and help to demystify the many features incorporate in this professional page layout program. Text provided. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!
