prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!

title: Traditional Lebanese Lunch
description: With the recent emphasis on the health benefits of Mediterranean cuisine, many Americans are discovering and embracing Lebanese food. Learn how to prepare a delicious traditional lunch consisting of lentil soup, fattoush, beef shawarma and hommos. Sana Hamade will teach you her secrets for cooking up a fast, easy, authentic Lebanese meal. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Life Drawing II
description: An intermediate college level exploration Life Drawing through direct observation of a model. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Spring Birding
description: For a unique spring birding adventure, join Allen Chartier, coauthor of Birds of Detroit and a very active birder. Through both classroom instruction and field work with Allen you will improve your observation skills, develop ways to record your sightings, and learn to use field marks and songs to identify birds. After an initial classroom session, plan to meet your instructor at local sites (including a Michigan Field Ornithologist event) to practice identifying and observing a variety of birds in their native habitats. Directions to the sites will be provided the first day of class. Bring binoculars to enrich your experience.

title: Lightarian Reiki Levels 1 & 2
description: Lightarian Reiki is the next higher band of energy Lightarian Reiki is a very masculine, yang energy, designed to really get your life moving! Included is the Lightarian Levels 1 and 2 with the Buddhic Boost. Manuals will be available for ordering after your attunement. Master Level required.

title: Film History and Criticism
description: This course explores the medium of film as an art form, an industry, and a source of entertainment, examining its historical, cultural, and technical evolution. Students will analyze cinematic techniques, genres, and influential movements to assess films' visual and narrative elements, understand cinema's personal, social, and global impact, and appreciate its role in shaping our world-from early movies to contemporary films.

title: Construction Blueprint Reading
description: Explores mechanical (HVAC), architectural, and electrical drawings required for energy technology, plumbing and pipefitting, electrical, and construction related trades. Covers print reading, interpreting common symbols, working notes, views, and title blocks. Introduces project management and estimating. Note: Basic measurement and math skills required for print reading section.

title: Traditional Lebanese Cooking
description: With the recent emphasis on the health benefits of Mediterranean cuisine, many Americans are discovering and embracing Lebanese food. Learn how to prepare a delicious traditional meal, consisting of veg/burgol with tomato sauce and Lebanese cabbage salad. Chef Kamal Hachem will teach you his secrets for cooking up a fast, easy, authentic Lebanese meal. Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Recession-Proof Your Wallet
description: Are you tired of living check to check every month? Did you know that up to 40% of all Americans spend more money than they actually earn? Learn the secrets of wealth management and how the rich are able to create money-making opportunities with little or none of their own money. Additionally, you will learn how to work less, increase earnings, pay less in taxes and much more. Don't miss out on this opportunity. Financial freedom is a choice. Make yours today. Join us!

title: UG NX Level I - Introduction
description: Introduces the basics of UG NX and its functionality. Utilizes modeling, sketch creation, drafting, and assembly task environments to create and edit models that are properly constructed and constrained.

title: Max Retire Fund When Leave Job
description: What to do with your 401(k) or Qualified Plan rollover at retirement is one of the biggest decisions you'll ever make! Afterall, it's what you spent your entire life accumulating. In this workshop, you will learn about the advantages and disadvantages of the various options available to you and, under the guidance of a professional financial advisor, develop a customized investment strategy to meet your individual needs.

title: Intro to Sculpture: Human Head
description: This introduction course will familiarize you with a variety of materials and techniques for working with oil-based clay as well as introducing the form and anatomy of the human head. After learning to construct a proper armature, you will complete a 1/3 scale sculpture of the human head, and present your piece to peers on the final day of class. To complete sculptures in time for the final critique, you will be expected to work outside of regular class time. Wear comfortable, washable clothing. No prior experience necessary. Clay and foam included in the class cost. Students may wish to purchase other materials as the class proceeds but they are not required.

title: AC Theory & Equipment
description: Examines alternating current theory. Includes right triangle trigonometry and complex numbers to facilitate the AC calculations. Discusses concepts of reactance, impedance, and power factor. Compares single phase and three phase power. Introduces single phase and three phase alternators, motors, transformers, and both heating and lighting loads.

title: American History 2
description: Covers the United States since the Civil War period. This is a mirror class for HIST 152.

title: Return to Your Source
description: Live with clarity and passion. A spiritual journey - an inward voyage of discovery designed to bring balance to your outward behaviors and inner consciousness - can help you achieve this. Your journey is as unique as you are, and whether you are taking your first steps or you find yourself wandering along the way, here is a down-to-earth, non-denominational approach to guide you to your Source. Come learn some time-honored disciplines that can help you repel negativity, attract positive energy, and embrace a richer life experience. Instructor's book included.

title: Basic Elements of Grantwriting
description: Novice grant writers will explore basic grant writing questions with expert Mena Davis. Class will provide hands-on demonstration on how to write your own grant as you explore the grant writing process. Learn to search and locate grants for your ministry, new business or organization.

title: Conversational Spanish 1
description: Are you tired of language tapes that have you endlessly repeating phrases? Are you ready to learn Spanish for business or pleasure? This workshop covers the fundamental grammar points and functional vocabulary that will enable you to engage in basic conversations. Through interactive activities and exercises, gain a basic understanding of Spanish and its cultures. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $30).

title: More Women & Investing
description: Expand your knowledge of key financial concepts as they relate to the unique needs of women. Vera Johnson, a registered financial planner, will discuss financial planning, investing, retirement and more, and provide practical tools to empower you to create wealth and success through business and investing. Completion of Women & Investing or basic understanding of finance recommended.

title: Seated Exercise
description: Safely strengthen and tone your muscles while sitting in a chair! Low impact exercises, done while sitting in or holding onto a chair, will strengthen muscle groups and improve muscular endurance. See how quickly daily activities become easier! Benefits can include: improved posture, stronger bones, and decreased risk of injuries to the body. It's never too late to start rebuilding muscle tissue. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights.

title: Windows Version 7 for Upgrader
description: This course is designed for persons who are familiar with and comfortable working in Windows XP and want to upgrade to the new and enhanced features of Windows 7. This course is not for students who are first-time Windows users.

title: Introd to Machine Tool Probing
description: Covers the elementary functions and application of the electronic probe on a Vertical Machining Center. Topics include set-up and calibration of the probe and use and application of the following cycles - Bore/Boss measuring cycle, Web/Pocket measuring cycle, and internal and external cycles. Laboratory activities.

title: Theatrical Production
description: This course is designed as a continuation of performance activities for theater students who have completed performance for college credit. Admission by administrative permission only.

title: Energy Control Power Lockout
description: The goal of this course is to ensure that affected employees are provided with the knowledge necessary to ensure their safety when working in or around permit-required and non-permit confined spaces.

title: Applied Pathology for PTAs
description: Examines the signs, symptoms, etiology, course, prognosis, medical intervention, and treatment of diseases with an emphasis on diseases commonly encountered in physical therapy. Medical Terminology will be reinforced. Also highlights patient care with emphasis on physical therapy interventions relevant to the body system involved in various pathologies. Topics include aseptic technique and wound care, cardiac rehabilitation and chest physiotherapy (CPT), and selected manual therapy techniques. Guided lab setting.

title: Radiographic Procedures III
description: This lecture/laboratory course is the third in a four-course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This 8-week course reinforces fundamental radiology positioning terminology and provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for skull and headwork. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Computer Basics
description: Familiarizes students with the fundamentals of operating a computer and using software. Provides interactive, hands-on experience to help students become comfortable using computers. Topics include use of a mouse, familiarizing students with the keyboard, proper keyboarding technique, familiarizing students with features of Windows, introduction to files and folders, email basics, and use internet browsers and search engines.

title: Mandarin Chinese: Intro
description: Whether for business or for pleasure, your trip to China will be much more interesting if you know the basics of the beautiful Mandarin Chinese language and culture. Learn from Wen Chi Swadling (a Chinese/English translator, teacher, and licensed international tour guide) basic survival language skills and unique cultural issues.

title: Police Admin/Staff
description: Focuses on uniformed police operations, both patrol and traffic, as well as principles of organization, management, planning, and crime prevention.

title: Specialties of Kenya
description: Enjoy popular dishes, cooking methods and food culture with Chef Marilyn Suter just returning from a recent visit to Kenya. We will make ugali, a Kenyan staple, goat, curried bananas, a great vegetarian bean dish, and more. Bring an apron and a sense of adventure!

title: Advanced Techniques
description: Examines the use of Computer-aided design (CAD) in advanced operations of making three-dimensional models. Solids analysis and the manipulation of geometry is explored utilizing current CAD software. Concepts of parametric modeling, drafting principles are reviewed and developed as they relate to solid model geometry.

title: Adult Tap Dance 1 (4 Week)
description: If you want to continue your tap dance routine through the semester break, this short course is for you. Continue to work on the basic skills learned at the start of the semester. Bring tap shoes. A four-week class.

title: Get Comfortable With Internet
description: Here's your chance to have a knowledgeable instructor walk you through the internet world. Topics include choosing how to gain access to the internet, using search tools with confidence, preventing pop-ups, guarding against identity theft and scams, and setting up bookmarks to your favorite sites. Learn about "cookies", Map Quest, Google, Mozilla, Microsoft Internet Explorer, Abode Reader and more.

title: Adult Hip Hop
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? This high-energy, introductory class features the best of both! Work your body and soul to the hottest music craze this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Protect Your Independence
description: For senior citizens, handicapped individuals, and family caregivers, finding services to maintain your independence is time-consuming and frustrating. Let resource specialist Phyllis Slater ease your stress. Discover multiple options for home care, live-in care, and assisted living. Find out what questions to ask care providers, how to arrange financial and legal affairs to protect assets, and where to look for such services as reverse mortgages, Meals on Wheels, emergency help, home maintenance, property tax appeals, chore providers, home companions and more. Includes presenter's guide "Calm the Chaos of Aging."

title: Fund. of Surgical Tech Lab
description: The course introduces the fundamentals of operating room techniques, emphasizing principles and practices of asepsis and sterilization. Physical and psychosocial aspects of the patient are explored. Included are the roles and responsibilities of the surgical technologist and other surgical team members; identification of common equipment, instruments, and supplies; the perioperative phases and environment; students practice scrubbing, gowning, and gloving, and preparation of the sterile surgical fields. This is a laboratory course.

title: Intermediate Ballet II
description: This is the second of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of ballet technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in ballet history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and classical dance principles. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of this traditional and foundational dance art. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of ballet.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, placement, and turn-out, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Energy Audit & Certification
description: For students seeking to become BPI certified residential energy auditors or to understand the principles of energy auditing. Includes training in the principles of energy transfer, building envelopes, energy auditing, air leakage, insulation, windows and doors, heating systems, cooling systems, indoor air quality, lighting, appliances, and water heating. Students will gain the skills required to perform building inspections and make cost effective recommendations for improving energy efficiency. At the conclusion of this class students will be able to sit for the Building Performance Institute (BPI) theory written test for Building Analyst certification.

title: DNA Camp for Girls
description: Fun hands-on activities and real lab work highlighting the structure of the cell and the structure and function of DNA. We will talk about the role of DNA in genetics, health and disease, biotechnology, and forensics. Lab activities will include extracting DNA from plant cells and analyzing DNA using forensic science techniques. We may even solve a mystery in the process! Women in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) fields will join the girls for informal discussions of career paths in these various areas.

title: Intro to Crochet
description: Learn the basics of crochet in this comprehensive class. As you practice the five stitches associated with crochet, you will learn to read patterns and be able to make the ever-favorite "Granny square" motif. Tips and tricks will be shared to make your projects true hand-crafted treasures. Upon finishing the course you will have the skills and confidence necessary to complete a beginner-to-easy-level project of your choosing. Bring a tape measure, scissors, yarn and crochet hook.

title: Creating a Classroom Web Site
description: Learn how to create a classroom Web site in this fun, easy-to-understand course for teachers. In no time, you'll build a site with text, images, animations, tables, links, and more. Then you'll create a WebQuest and a blog to add to your site. You'll do all this and more as you discover how simple creating a classroom Web site can be and how having one can make you a more effective and dynamic teacher.

title: About Boating Safely
description: This recently revised US Coast Guard-approved course is designed for students 12 years and older. In an entertaining and educational environment, you will learn how to boat safely and enjoyably avoid problems on the water, and handle an emergency. Boat trailering, safe PWC (Personal Water Craft) operation, personal safety equipment and procedures, and more will also be covered. Upon successful completion you will receive a course certificate that may qualify you for reduced boat insurance rates. The State of Michgan requires teens 14 and older to have a Boating Safety Certificate to operate a PWC (jet ski). An adult must accompany students under 17 years old. Bring a brown bag lunch.

title: Differentiating K-12 Assessmen
description: Differentiated assessment is the key to helping every student succeed-and no matter what grade you're teaching, this course will give you the tools you need to gather your assessment data quickly and easily. You'll begin by exploring strategies for performing pre-assessments, formative (ongoing) assessments, and summative assessments. Next, you'll discover how to identify and share learning goals, gather assessment information, adjust your instruction, and provide feedback to your students. Whether you're a new or experienced teacher, this course will provide you with a wealth of practical ideas you can start using in your classroom right away. This is a 6 week online course.

title: Nc Elementary Spanish 2
description: Builds upon the reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills introduced in SPN 131. Focuses on expanding pronunciation and grammatical principles. Topics of Hispanic culture will also be presented.

title: Revealing Your Dharma
description: Dharma is the law of our existence, the path of our destiny. Some people know exactly who they are and why they are here, some have a partial understanding, and some don't have a clue, but they would like to learn more about it. Through lectures, discussions, group activities, videos, and written exercises this class will assist participants in a quest for Dharma. The aim of this class is to let the group work together to increase each person's understanding of his own Dharma and then suggest ways to enhance your ongoing pursuit of it. The group will also explore ways to create a legacy.

title: Common App for College Adm
description: Prepare for the college application process by learning the "ins and outs" of successfully completing the current common Application. In this course you'll review the application cycle and standardized testing options, learn to prepare the common Application with supporting documentation, become familiar with the FAFSA and CSS Profiles, and discuss the selection of a major, minor and college. You will also learn about your choices and options after college admission's decisions are announced. This course is writing intensive and requires a considerable amount of work outside the classroom.

title: Intro to Sports Management
description: This course provides a comprehensive overview of the field of sport management, exploring its key concepts, principles, and applications. Students will gain a solid understanding of the historical development of sport management, the various segments within the industry (youth, community, scholastic, professional, and international sports), trends, and the essential managerial and leadership skills required for success in this dynamic field.

title: Strength Train & Phys Cond I
description: Introduces beginning and intermediate training techniques in strength and cardiovascular conditioning including evaluation of all components of physical fitness. Emphasizes flexibility, strength, cardiovascular conditioning, muscular endurance, and body composition. Course also includes information on nutrition and exercise as it relates to weight management and disease prevention, and discusses common risk factors.

title: Heal Brain After Accidents
description: Did you know that simple falls and low-speed car accidents can produce hearing and vision changes, insomnia, panic attacks, persistent muscle pain, joint stiffness and depression? Let brain-stem chiropractor Dr. Barry Hobbs show you how these symptoms relate to certain blockages in nerve energy and can be treated with specific manipulation therapies. Then hear biochemist Sandy Baumann describe the foods, supplements and lifestyle strategies she used after a card accident to heal her brain from anxiety, memory loss and insomnia. Come find hope for your unresolved health problems. $12 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Sacred Geometry
description: Sacred Geometry is in the beauty and elegance of ancient architecture and art, the principles of musical scales, ancient alphabets and more. Did the mysteries of "The Da Vinci Code" intrigue you? Come learn more about the mysteries behind Leonardo da Vinci's art as well as the Fibonacci sequence that was hidden in the numerical code that was referenced in the novel/movie. Satisfy your curiosity and experience clarity and amediative calm as we explore some of the ancient wisdom embedded within this language of creation.

title: Co-op in Science
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major in STEM to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Industrial Safety Awareness
description: Presents a comprehensive approach to safety, designed to give the skilled tradesperson the knowledge of safety fundamentals and practices, from the causes of accidents to the study of safety hazards and rules associated with equipment and tools utilized in modern industrial facilities. Successful completers will be issued the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) 30-Hour card for General Industry.

title: Quick Spanish for Travelers
description: Tune up your artistic signing abilities. Join Anne Bolander, an author and member of the deaf community, for this unique opportunity to learn how to incorporate facial expression, body movement and rhythm into your signing. Whatever your reason, join this fun, lively class and learn to sign a variety of song lyrics.

title: PowerPoint Basics & Beyond
description: Why limit yourself to dull text-based slide presentations if you can enhance them with graphics? Now you can design exciting slides and computer presentations using clipart, photos, drawings and charts. Learn to create slideshows, speaker's notes and handouts. The instructor is an instructional designer in the auto industry and is known as a PowerPoint "guru" among her coworkers. Ideal for all speakers, including managers. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge.

title: Make Money With eBay
description: Step up a notch in the world of eBay and learn the secrets of the pros. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer who has made hundreds of transactions himself, will teach you about the ins and outs of using design and bid services, setting up an "eBay store," using pictures effectively in ads, shipping and payment options and even avoiding the pitfalls.

title: Radiographic Procedures III
description: This lecture/laboratory course is the third in a four-course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This 8-week course reinforces fundamental radiology positioning terminology and provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for skull and headwork. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Digital Image Acquisition Mgt.
description: This course addresses all technical aspects of digital image acquisition, manipulation, and storage. Course includes digital imaging characteristics, types of imaging systems and system components, digital image acquisition and manipulation, and digital image archiving systems. Also included are principles of imaging system quality assurance and maintenance.

title: Police Academy
description: Covers the proper techniques of investigation, crime scene process, patrol procedures, operations and techniques. Emphasizes conflict mediation, report writing, and detention and prosecution of prisoners. First aid, investigations, evidence collection, disaster control, civil disorders and tactical operations will also be covered.

title: Metallic Jewelry - Child
description: Instructor will collect $5 materials fee day of class.

title: Chinese Spring Festival Cookin
description: February 14, 2010 marks the start of the Year of the Tiger on the Chinese Lunar Calendar. It's a time when Asian chefs bring out their specialty dishes as the family gathers for the big celebration. Bring your appetite and learn how some of the favorites are cooked. Also learn about ancient and mystical Chinese traditions and customs with instructor Liping Prus.

title: Spec Topics Indust Draf Techno
description: Designed to explore selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current drafting technology trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Students can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Intro to Children's Literature
description: Introduces the forms, themes, history, and uses of literature written for children ages three to twelve. Students learn to evaluate and select literature critically and understand its use in preschool, elementary, and middle school classrooms. Genres to be studied include traditional fiction/folktales, contemporary realistic fiction, picture books, fantasy/science fiction, historical fiction, biography, nonfiction, and poetry/verse.

title: Human Potential Seminar
description: Examines how to identify and apply positive life skills in order for each individual to discover and any use their strengths, talents and abilities. Covers empathy, peak experiences, internal and external motivational factors, goal setting and values clarification.

title: Introduction Public Speaking
description: This course explores how to develop public speaking skills through practice in preparing and delivering speeches for various audiences and situations.

title: Rehab Neuro Conditions Lab
description: Applies techniques learned in PTA-262 with an emphasis on the functional approach to patient treatment. Includes the use of Neurodevelopmental Treatment (NDT) and Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF) for adults with neurological conditions such as traumatic brain injury (TBI) and cerebrovascular accidents (CVAs). Guided laboratory setting.

title: Publish Your Own Cookbook
description: Do you want to publish a treasury of your family recipes or create a proven fund-raising tool for your organization? Then enroll in this class with Len Charla, author of Never Cooked Before/Gotta Cook Now. He will give you the inside scoop about several ways to get your cookbook designed and published, the best methods for testing and organizing your recipes, and a bonanza of marketing tips for successful sales. Learn how to copyright your cookbook and get it into stores. Ideal for club fundraisers and family reunion projects. After all, Mom's pie recipe may be your ticket to extra income!

title: Create Graphics in Power Point
description: Not an artist? Don't fret! Create graphics or modify existing clipart in this hands-on training. Use the PowerPoint drawing tools, AutoShap, and WordArt to create your own graphics. Resize, crop and recolor existing clipart. Group and reposition images. Create wall paper backgrounds. Download images from Microsoft.com. Preserve WordArt and graphics in image files with Picture Manager for convenient reuse and stability. Skills learned in this class can be applied to slides, flyers, cards and the Web.

title: Darkroom Photography
description: A beginning college level art class designed to teach the use of an manual 35 mm SLR film camera, development of black & white negatives and the making of black and white prints in the darkroom. If the student does not own a suitable camera prior to taking this class, he/she may wait until the first class meeting prior to purchasing one. Creativity is emphasized. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Complete Stretch & Tone
description: Low impact exercise designed to improve flexibility and core strength while stretching and lengthening your muscles. Common side effects could include increased ability to concentrate, improved posture and reduced stress! Get out of your head and into your body in this yoga-inspired stretching and core conditioning class. Designed for adults of all fitness levels. Wear comfortable clothing and bring a mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Food and Nutrition
description: Examines basic concepts of nutrition, food composition, food technology, controversies in nutrition, and marketing nutrition in the food service business. Covers carbohydrates, fats, protein, vitamins, RDA, food labeling, menu planning, weight management, cardiovascular disease, nutrition and cancer, and modifying recipes for health and lower calorie content.

title: MS Word: Tips Beyond Basics
description: Class will focus on how to use many less-known time saving feasures in Microsoft Word. Learn to use format styles, drop caps and auto text. Give new meaning to words and phrases by using the thesaurus, synonym and translation features. Find out how to use auto correct and password protect your document. Learn to convert text to tables, tables to text and so much more!

title: Fingerspelling and Numbering
description: Provides an overview of American Sign Language (ASL) fingerspelling techniques and numbering skills, focusing on improving students' receptive and expressive skills so that students will be able to communicate with speed, dexterity and clarity.

title: Success With Less Stress - New
description: Do you dream of a day where you are well focused, complete your work or projects, and still have energy left for fun or family? Live your dream by joining Sandy Baumann, popular author of Feed Your Brain for Success, for an inspiring and lighthearted class. Find secrets for improving your productivity and mood with food, beverages, recreation, lifestyle choices, and simple supplements. Enjoy some laughter and a smorgasbord of easy ideas to fuel your brain and put the brakes on your stress! $12 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Personal Finance
description: Examines common financial concerns such as budgeting, obtaining and managing credit, buying insurance, planning for taxes, purchasing a car and/or home, and saving for retirement. Discusses stocks, bonds, and mutual funds. Also provides information on career planning and employee benefits.

title: Voice and Movement
description: Musical TheaterTechniques: Voice and Movement.

title: Film History and Criticism
description: This course explores the medium of film as an art form, an industry, and a source of entertainment, examining its historical, cultural, and technical evolution. Students will analyze cinematic techniques, genres, and influential movements to assess films' visual and narrative elements, understand cinema's personal, social, and global impact, and appreciate its role in shaping our world-from early movies to contemporary films.

title: Desktop Publish with InDesign
description: Use the brand new Adobe InDesign CS3 software to create your catalogs, brochures, flyers and other items. Lay out your text and graphics like the pros and avoid having to send these items out to an expensive designer. Easily coordinate with professional printers, publish in your own office, or output to multiple media. Create rich full featured documents with easy integration with Photoshop and other programs. The instructor will get you started and help to demystify the many features incorporated in this professional page layout program. Prerequisite: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!
prerequisite_text: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!

title: Advd. Graphic Interpretation
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of oblique surfaced objects, sectioning, processes used in the manufacturing environment, and geometric and traditional tolerancing. Requires knowledge of traditional and computer-based drafting techniques. Non- credit equivilent to TAGD 120

title: DIMS Excel 2010 Lvl 1
description: Teaches the fundamental skills needed to work with Excel 2010, starting with basics like the Excel environment, entering and editing numbers and text, then moving on to writing formulas and using Excel's built-in functions. Students will also learn about formatting spreadsheets, sorting, filtering, and charts.

title: Intermediate Inline Skating
description: Take what you learned in Beginner Inline Skating to the next level! Learn how to get more power out of your push and do it in style. Skate backwards and do transitions, long turns and crossovers with ease. Students must provide skates equipped with a heel brake, helmet, wrist guards, elbow and kneepads.

title: Tastes of Italy
description: Prepare for a treat in this introductory cooking series that will teach you not only the basics of preparing authentic Italian regional cuisine but also the almost mythic approach to dining that makes Italy famous. With an emphasis on fresh seasonal foods, each week Robert Cascardo and Cristina Cotronei will teach you how to prepare a three-course meal, pair it with an appropriate wine and eat it the way they do in Italy. Cristina was born near Milan, and Robert has lived in various Italian cities including Florence, Rome and Turin. Naturally, they both love to cook. Join them for fun, educational evenings of preparing, eating and, of course, cleaning up after memorable meals. This class sells out, so register early.

title: Gage, Cam Layout, and Fixture
description: Focuses on the mastery of gage design principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Discusses gage design theory, computer-aided manufacturing, cam layout procedures, and fixturing. Emphasizes the team approach to projects.

title: Relax Tech for Stress Mgmt
description: Covers stress and its effects on the body. Emphasizes ways to reduce stress such as Yogic breathing, meditation, and other relaxation techniques. Discusses time and money management; conflict resolution and effective communication; resolving anxiety; a healthy diet, and how to incorporate these into one's lifestyle.

title: Foundations of Investing
description: Foundations of Investing is an educational program geared to people who are either new to investing or need a refresher. Robynne Henry will cover the basic features of bonds, stocks and mutual funds and the importance of asset allocation, as well as the impact of inflation on your long term goals. If you want an overview of investing, including key terms and investment types this class is for you.

title: Piano Class 2
description: This course builds upon skills developed in MUS 117 (Piano Class 1). Instruction takes place in the HFC Music Technology Lab.

title: Collecting Antique Dolls
description: Whether it's grandma's doll from the attic or a garage sale find, that special doll may become the centerpiece of a fascinating collection of various types of dolls made of wood, wax, cloth, porcelain, tin, plastic or perhaps celluloid. Learn of the great doll makers of Germany and France and our own American artists.

title: Exploration of Welding
description: Introduces basic welding principles, skills, and terminology. Uses welding simulator to demonstrate hands-on methods through a project-based approach. The purpose of this course is to explore various basic welding techniques that are similar to industry method of operations.

title: Auto Alignment Clinic
description: Designed to cover the types of front and rear suspension systems used on today¿s vehicles. In addition, there is in-depth coverage of alignment factors. The majority of the class is devoted to inspection, repair, and alignment of a variety of suspension systems.

title: Residential Wiring Multifamily
description: This course provides an in-depth study of electrical systems and code requirements for multifamily dwellings. Students will explore wiring methods, switching layouts, and safety systems unique to multifamily residential structures. Emphasis is placed on electrical load calculations, firestopping standards, and compliance with the National Electrical Code (NEC) for installations, permits, and inspections. Students will also look at how to use special equipment and where to correctly install smoke alarms, carbon monoxide detectors, and safety devices like GFCI and AFCI circuits to make sure the electrical design and operation are safe and effective.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-104W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Spice & Herb for Better Health
description: Beyond their flavorful aromas and delicate flavors, did you know that spices and herbs have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, anticancer and other health properties? If you haven't tried cooking with them, now's the time to start. Bring your apron and together we will create a scrumptious meal: Curry - Honey Chicken, Escabeche (fish with ginger), rosemary and garlic potatoes, and cucumber salad.

title: Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, bala includes stretching, breathing, posturing and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga, your body will evelop flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment; and you will achieve mental concentration relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Studio Art Capstone
description: A capstone class for graduating Studio Art majors to be taken during their final semester that includes portfolio and Exit Exhibition preparation and implementation.

title: Women Crave Chocolate (nurses)
description: Do you respond to stress with cravings for chocolate, steak or potato chips? This class with biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you the physiological reasons for such cravings and help you reduce them. You will discover why stress makes you gain weight and why a high-carbohydrate diet increases appetite and cravings. You will learn to lessen cravings and reduce weight by decreasing stress and increasing pleasure. You can gain control of your brain chemistry, your food intake and your mood! $5 material fee due to instructor the first session. 6 nursing contact hours.

title: Steam and Hot Water Systems
description: For technical construction apprentices and those interested in seeking basic pipefitting -plumbing skills. Introduces principles of steam and hydronic systems, converter trap sizing, steam traps, and skimming boilers. Also examines the proper selection, sizing, and installation of pipe and fittings related to hydronic and steam systems. Application exercises allow students the opportunity to design and lay out typical systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Measurement & Rx of Fitness
description: Examines various tests used for evaluating the health-related and skill-related components of physical fitness. Includes tests for body composition, flexibility, cardiovascular endurance, muscular endurance, muscular strength, speed, agility, and power. Students will learn how to interpret test results and accurately recommend exercise programs for improvement.

title: Taming Excel Charts
description: Create Excel charts and learn which charts to make to improve your skills of visualizing your data sets. Relevant class examples derived from real world scenarios. Excel charts improve your presentations and will help you sell your audience. Topics include: Column Charts, Bar Charts, Line Charts, Pie Charts, Scatter Charts, Doughnut Charts, Pivot Charts, 2-D vs 3-D Charts, and printing options for charts. Concepts discussed will include, but are not limited to: Axis (primary and secondary), data points, values, series, gridlines, legend, data sheet, chart area, plot area, chart scale, etc. USB stick (flash drive) is required for this class. Prerequisite: Intermediate Level Excel skills.
prerequisite_text: Intermediate Level Excel skills.

title: Golf: Indoor Group Lessons
description: If you are brand new to the game, or you need to revisit the basics and break down the old habits, this is the class for you. Bring your clubs for lots of hands-on experience using your short irons, long irons, and woods. Learn how to grip, setup and swing as you focus on your short game including pitching, chipping, sand play and putting. Instructor Tom Rinna is a certified teaching professional who received his education from Jimmy Ballard, Jim Colbert and Sam Byrd (who helped Ben Hogan).

title: EV Battery Technician Cert
description: Participants in this certificate course will acquire the necessary skills for designing, operating, testing, maintaining, and replacing battery cells and packs for various applications. In addition, the certificate allows participants to choose the right technology for the right challenge and evaluate battery performance. Moreover, to be able to work out a solid total solution, the battery management systems, the charging and maintenance of batteries, and their recycling process are also discussed in more detail. Finally, we give sufficient attention to safely handling these electrical applications, ensuring the well-being of both individuals and the environment.This certification empowers learners to: . Evaluate what battery technology is best suited for which application based on its characteristics. . Examine the advantages and disadvantages of the most commonly used battery technologies. . Design and implement the best-fit battery solution for each application. . Safely handle, test, repair, and dispose of battery systems according to legal obligations.

title: Abc's of Red Wine
description: Tired of always ordering the "safe" choice of Cabernet Sauvignon? Whether you're a rookie or a blooming connoisseur, join us as we explore red wines beyond Cabernet. Bruce Bell will take you on an educational tour that will teach you how to identify some common and some new red wines by color, aroma and flavor; how to taste them; and how to store, pour, and serve them like a pro. Participants must be at least 21 years old.

title: Cool Stuff for Sizzling Days
description: Forget the "same ole same ole." This class will help you to add some pizzazz to your summer entertaining. The salads and side dishes that will be prepared will be perfect for summer: quick and easy and able to be made ahead to leave you free to enjoy your company. Chef Marilyn Szymanski-Suter is an executive chef. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Business Organizations
description: Examines forms of business organizations including corporations, partnerships, and limited liability companies. Covers the formation, structure, rights, and liabilities associated with each entity, along with the legal consequences of dissolving them. Covers the drafting of documents necessary to create and maintain business entities.

title: ELL American Culture I
description: Introduces low-beginning-level knowledge of American culture for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-102A. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Unique Gifts Made With Money
description: Long stem roses with petals made from real money? An adorable mini men's shirt made almost entirely from real money? In this hands-on class you'll learn both of these very popular crafts! Great for party favors, gift giving, centerpieces, etc. The money is not damages in any way and can be easily removed for spending. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $16-$18).

title: Gentle Yoga
description: Start your day by experiencing the health benefits of yoga in this calming, therapeutic class. Designed for beginners, seniors, pregnant women, persons with limited mobility and flexibility, and those recovering from illness or injury, Gentle Yoga offers the postures and breathing techniques of Hatha yoga in a modified format and at a gentle, slow pace. Professional yoga instructor Dianna Kinaschuk will work with you to adapt them to your own individual needs. Open to persons of all ages.

title: Lebanese Cooking 1
description: Falafel. Hommous. Stuffed rice with chicken. Lebanese Mediterranean salad.

title: Kinesiology for PTA's
description: Reviews surface anatomy and functional anatomy, muscles and muscle function, proper posture and analysis of posture, and gait analysis and deviations. Medical Terminology is reinforced. A lab component familiarizes the student with functional aspects of human motion.

title: Update Prop. Lease Agrmnt
description: This class will cover all the basics, line for line, review security deposit documentation, inventory checklist, how to fill in the blanks and answer all questions concerning the lease. Students should bring in their existing lease to compare.

title: Festive Holiday Meals
description: The holidays are a time to get together and celebrate with friends and family. What better way could there be than to share a homemade meal with those you love. Amaze your guests this year with delicious new dishes. Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estates, will pick up where he left off last week by preparing two festive holiday meals - both full of easy delightful recipes you can add to your collection. Come hungry and bring an apron.

title: Co-Op in Graphic Design
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible. To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5. Contact the HFC Career Services Office for more information at careers@hfcc.edu.

title: Ophthalmic Surgical Assisting
description: Introduces ophthalmic surgical procedures, including the indications, goals, and steps of select ophthalmic procedures. Emphasizes principles of asepsis, and care and maintenance of ophthalmic surgical instruments.

title: Energy Auditor
description: This training will prepare participants to take Certification Field and Written Exam. This is a nationally recognied Home Energy Professional Certification.

title: Microsoft Excel 2003
description: Discover how Excel can help you improve your effectiveness as a teacher.

title: Rehab Neurological Cond 1
description: Reviews basic neuroanatomy and neurophysiology followed by instruction in the theory and rationale of the treatment approach for the patient with neurological involvement. Emphasizes the functional approach to patient treatment including use of Neurodevelopmental Treatment (NDT) and Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF) for adults with neurological conditions such as traumatic brain injury and cerebrovascular accidents.

title: ELL Reading & Study Skills 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college-preparatory reading and study skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELL-182. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Basic Writing: Sentences to
description: WFPD-ENG088 is a developmental writing course required of students whose scores on the placement test indicate that skills need to be learned before they enroll in ENG-093. Students will learn several sentence patterns, some grammatical and mechanical skills, and various methods for developing and organizing paragraphs. Writing a short essay, supplemental laboratory work and conferences will be required. Students who do not place into ENG-093 with a minimum placement Writing Test score must take WFPD-ENG088. Students must successfully meet all objectives (receive a grade of OJM) in WFPD-ENG088 before enrolling in English 093.

title: Chinese Medicine in Your Life
description: Traditional Chinese medicine is used more and more throughout the world. Are your curious why? Come explore the history, principles and self-care philosophies of Taoist philosophy, Ci Gung, acupuncture, acupressure massage, eating with the five seasons and herbal tonics.

title: Help! I've Pushed a Button
description: Fix some of those annoying computer problems! Be part of our supportive class, where you will learn to: identify what a secure shopping website looks like; close a "stuck" program; send an e-mail attachment; move hidden windows. Learn what to safely click when faced with pop-up ads and boxes. Find out if your computer is protected with virus software, and more!

title: Introductory Psychology
description: Introduces elementary concepts and principles related to the scientific study of behavior and of the mental processes of cognition and affective states. Variables examined include the history of psychology, the scientific method, theory, biological foundations, psychological processes related to cognition and affective states, developmental changes over time, and applications related to healthy and unhealthy personalities.

title: Art of Pearl Necklaces
description: Both trendy and classic, the pearl is the perfect piece of jewelry for almost any occasion. In this class, you will learn two techniques for making pearl jewelry. Kathleen Robinson-Young will teach you how to knot pearls and beads on silk thread and how to string pearls on flexible wire to make a stunning bracelet. You'll leave with your own handmade bracelet and matching pearl earrings. $38 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Exploration of Pneumatics
description: Introduces basic pneumatic principles, skills, and terminology. Students will demonstrate hands-on methods through a project-based approach. The purpose of this course is to explore the fundamentals of pneumatics that are similar to industry method of operations.

title: Operation Next ITM Mechanical
description: Component of Operations Next performance-based training to enable students to earn the NIMS credentials in Mechanical Maintenance Technician.

title: Earn & Learn Read & Math Refre
description: Program is a partnership between HFCC and Southwest Solutions designed to aid Earn and Learn Program participants who wish to improve their reading and math skills in order to pursue manufacturing-based employment and/or pre-apprenticeship training.

title: Hearty Stews & Casseroles -New
description: Join Chef Tim Dare to stir up some wonderful suppers for winter. Prepare and taste Angus beef stew, homemade chicken or beef potpie, spicy Jambalaya (seafood and sausage), and easy chicken ala king. Gather ingredient lists and cooking tips to get these meals to the table in record time. Get ideas for salads to round out your meals. Bring an apron and your appetite. Men and women of all ages are welcome to enroll, including beginners!

title: General Physics I
description: Introduces the principles of physics, including units on mechanics, heat, and sound. Partially fulfills the physics requirement in pre-medicine, pre-dentistry, teaching, and law. Three hours of lecture and three hours of laboratory per week.

title: Humanities/Perform Art Educat
description: This course studies the aspects of human society and culture through an education lens. This course prepares the student majoring in education to incorporate the various visual and performing arts from multiple humanities into everyday education curricula. The course will cover the fundamental and formal elements, the major periods, styles and philosophies, as well as the functions and processes of the humanities and visual and performing arts. This course provides the educator with knowledge on how to effectively employ those creative processes through collaboration, communication, cooperation, and interaction within the classroom. This is an interactive course where the instructor, guest speakers, and students present topics. Knowledge will be applied through small and large group discussions and projects. Students participation requires completion of projects/assignments both during and outside of class. At least one humanities/fine arts field trip will be planned as an integral part of the course.

title: Camp Entrepreneurship
description: Curious about learning how to save, invest, build wealth, or start a business? Camp G.R.O.W. teaches students how to gain resources, and obtain wealth in a fun, exciting, and informative manner. Campers are immersed in the essentials of financial literacy, networking, goal setting, business planning and entrepreneurship. Using skill building games, visual aids and hands on activities, students will learn how to make, manage, and grow their money wisely. Participants will get experience making financial decisions they will face as adults, and finish the course with stronger business, financial leteracy, and communication skills.

title: Ready, Set, Go!
description: This is a 9-week long full-day skill enrichment and work readiness program available to qualified individuals who are interested in improving their basic understanding of essential mathematics, reading comprehension, computer literacy and professional work skills. The purpose of the program is to help individuals perform at a 9th grade level or higher on the TABE tests in reading and math.

title: Theatre Capstone
description: Provides the required assessment of knowledge and skills for students in their last semester of course work for the Theatre Pathway of the Associates of Arts Degree in Liberal Arts.

title: Mexican Fiesta: Cinco De Mayo
description: Celebrate "Cinco de Mayo" and to learn how to prepare one of Alicia Bonilla-Arias authentic Mexican recipes she has learned from her mother and grandmother. Alicia has owned several Mexican restaurants and now wants to share her heritage with you. The class will focus on her Mole recipe. Mole (pronounced mo-lay) is a very traditional dish served on very special occasions, like a 15th birthday celebration called "Fiesta de Quinceanera", kind of a sweet sixteen party, and especially at weddings. Mole is made of a very special chocolate combined with many spices and peppers served with chicken, turkey, or pork. Bring your apron and an appetite.

title: HFV- Internet for Beginners
description: Around the world in 80 seconds! The internet helps you communicate instantly with family and friends, find travel bargains, shop for the best deals, and research any topic, including health questions. In four 2-hour sessions you will learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, surf the internet to find information, bookmark favorite sites, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. You will have time to practice on the internet in class, so bring your questions. THIS CLASS IS RESTRICTED TO HENRY FORD VILLAGE RESIDENTS AND IS BILLED BY SECTION.

title: Plumbing Pipefitting Code
description: Explores how to utilize the State of Michigan Plumbing Code to solve real-life problems (International Plumbing Code). Covers study habits and processes necessary to interpret and apply the code. Also helps prepare students for entry- level positions in the plumbing, multi-skilled maintenance, repair, and apprenticeship fields.

title: Decadent Choc Cake Truffles
description: In this hands-on class you will learn this new, very popular (and expensive) culinary art! Delicate, oh-so-creamy "cakebites" surrounded by chocolate candy shells flavored in lemon, raspberry, orange and fudge, decorated with several sweet toppings and drizzled with even more chocolate. These indulgent truffles are perfect for unique dessert presentations and make wonderful gifts too! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12-$14).

title: Lebanese Cooking I
description: Chicken swarma, fatoush salad.

title: Process Technology Equipment
description: Introduces students to the purpose, components, operation, and troubleshooting of the equipment used within the process industry.

title: Create Greeting Cards, Flyers
description: Join popular instructor Anita Denard to discover the joy of creating greeting cards, address labels, flyers, business cards, brochures, signs and photo album pages with user-friendly Print Shop software. This class is ideal for retirees, teachers, small business owners, hobbyists, club officers and more. Bring a recent photo to scan into a greeting card. Prerequisite: ability to use a mouse and to create, open, save and copy files. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Limited to 10 students.
prerequisite_text: ability to use a mouse and to create, open, save and copy files. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Limited to 10 students.

title: Abs, Glutes & Thighs
description: Burn fat... tighten your abs... slim and tone your inner and outer thighs... lift your derriere! This challenging but fun workout sculpts and tightens your lower body. Increase your energy level and feel better in an amazingly short time. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Basic Coastal Navigation
description: This course is a must for big water boaters. Keep your family and boat safety by learning how to safely pilot a boat. Course content includes: understanding nautical charts, navigator's tools, dead reckoning and piloting skills. The course requires solving navigational problems on a chart using the skils learned and concludes with a general knowledge exam. Class space is limited to 24 students. Minimum age 18 years. No prerequisites. Bring brown bag lunch. Call (313)317-1500 for supply list or go to "Supplies and Maps" on our webpage.

title: Intro to Healthcare Profession
description: This course is designed to introduce students to various health care professions. It includes skills and topics common to all healthcare providers such as ethical and legal issues, professionalism, cultural considerations, vital signs, body mechanics, communication, infection control, and evidence based practice. This course will also introduce key elements in the formation and modification of medical terms. This course has a lab hands on component.

title: Complete Retirement Planning
description: Whether you're 10 days, 10 months or 10 years from retiring, you need to explore many issues before making that move. In this class you will examine the financial issues of retirement as well as such psychological aspects as personal and spousal adjustment, changes in family dynamics, and how you will spend your time. A licensed clinical psychologist, an estate-planning attorney, and a CPA will discuss how to make this much-anticipated transition successfully.

title: Applied Food Serv Sanitation
description: Explores food contaminants, bacterial growth, safe food storage, and safe food handling procedures, as well as procedures for scheduling, cleaning, sanitizing, and pest control for facilities and equipment. NOTE: As part of this course, the National Restaurant Association Educational Foundation exam is included. Students who successfully pass the exam earn the ServSafe Food Protection Manager Certificate. This certificate is recognized by the state health department.

title: Golden Age of Toys
description: Explore the world of antique toys from the mid-1800's to pre WWI with our favorite historian, Mary MacDonald. See how toys mirror the times as Mary talks about cast iron, tin and other materials used to manufacture these childhood treasures. See a range of mechanical toys from the simple gravity and pull toys to wind-up, steam-operated, and early electrical toys. See beautiful dolls produced in Europe. It is a delightful evening for the child in each of us.

title: Stage Costuming
description: Introduces contemporary aesthetic and creative design principles for theatrical costuming, with an emphasis on line, form, meeting production requirements, budgeting, and thematic unity.

title: Return Rider Basic Rider Crse
description: A one day course designed for the experienced but unlicensed rider to become licensed and legal. Many experienced, licensed riders use this course as a refresher and skills tune up. Topics include: braking, counter steering, turning, speed judgment, and other techniques necessary for the experienced rider to operate a motorcycle skillfully and safely. To participate in this class, you must be at least 18 years old. If you are 16 or 17, you may take this class if you have a valid license and a current motorcycle endorsement. Each student must provide their own motorcycle. Sharing is not allowed. The cycle must be titled, properly registered, and display a valid license plate. Each student must provide proof of insurance and the bike must pass a safety inspection. Successful completion allows a student to waive the Secretary of State riding skills test.

title: Portrait Drawing (ages 12-15)
description: If you love art and drawing, accelerate your skills with this class. Every aspect of portrait drawing will be covered, from working on the small details to large, finished works of art. You will be drawing from pictures, mirrors and real life. Supplies included.

title: Buffet Entertaining
description: Whether you're planning a graduation party, a shower or another gathering of 50-100 people, make it a day to remember. Professional caterer Joy Callan will teach you how to save time, money and effort with hassle-free buffet entertaining. You will learn everything from menu planning to grocery shopping, food preparation and serving the meal. Bring your appetite, an apron and entertaining questions. Joy's a pro! Enroll early! Class limited to 14.

title: Intro to Sociology
description: Introduces the sociological approach to understanding culture and society. Examines human behavior in a group context, focusing on the prominent influence of culture and social structure on individuals. Connections between individuals and a variety of social institutions, such as the economy, government, and mass media, are examined and compared in U.S. society and on a global scale. Focuses on cultural diversity and social inequalities, such as those related to class, race, ethnicity and gender.

title: Relax, Replenish, Revive! Yoga
description: A yoga class can be a slow gentle stretch or a fast paced cardio workout. The basic poses provide an endless variety of sequences for a fun, challenging and stress busting class. Students will master the foundation yoga poses that are necessary to progress to more challenging styles of yoga. This class is for all level.

title: Maintenan Trades Print Reading
description: Covers shape description; conventions and symbols; size description; notes and instruction associated with manufacturer's and maintenance-related drawings, including castings, weldments, and machined parts; electrical/electronic schematics and ladder diagrams; piping and fluid power-related drawings and schematics; structural and architectural drawings; and sheet-metal and plant layout. For apprentices and trainees in industrial plant maintenance trades.

title: ELL Communication 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-102C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Birding Field Trips
description: After attending the Introduction to Bird Watching class (or equivalent experience), participants may choose from a variety of one-day field trips designed to focus on specific types of birds present. February - Feeder Birds; March - Gullds; April - Ducks; May - Warblers. These field trips will provide participants with an opportunity to practice their identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are identification and observation skills, under the guidance of the instructor. Four field trips are planned from late winter through spring. All field trips will meet at the HFCC parking lot where we will carpool to the sites, generally within 50 miles, to look for our target birds.

title: Introduction to Nutrition
description: An introductory focused on the basics of nutrition. Topics covered include digestion, planning nutritious diets, evaluating nutrition information, energy nutrients, food safety, nutrition at different life stages, energy balance and weight control. This class provides you with the skills necessary to follow a nutritious diet that will positively impact health throughout life.

title: Press Working Fund
description: Discusses basic metal deformation theory, presses and ancillary equipment, die construction, and die component identification. Covers various die types and draws the various detail components using both traditional and computer-aided drafting.

title: Line Dancing
description: Step away from your daily routine and join us as we learn some popular line dances and get a great workout at the same time. This class is designed for beginners, but all levels are welcome. And, one of the best features of this type of dance is partners are optional. Wear comfortable shoes, come with energy, and be ready for fun!

title: Literacy Foundations
description: Explores the principles, methods, and materials for teaching literacy through a research-based, integrated approach with children PreK-5. Emphasis is placed on the influence of a child's linguistic, cultural, and social background, fostering engaging literacy development in diverse and multicultural communities. Instructional methods include lectures, discussions, videos, small group work, hands-on experiences, and individual and group projects. The primary components of reading - phonemic awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, and comprehension - will be studied through assessment strategies and applied to teaching methods. The NAEYC (student learning outcomes) and MDE Quality Standards are addressed in this course.

title: Adv Topics in Skilled Trades
description: Provide the skilled trades community the opportunity to receive advanced specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics will be offered as identified by the Skilled Trades & Apprenticeship Department faculty, ST&AD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the ST&AD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Learners may repeat the course for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Soul Searching
description: Are you on the right path? This will be a unique opportunity to explore your spiritual journey. You will become more aware of your personal spirtual path and this will help you discover your life's purpose. You will be introduced to your own spirituality and purpose. Some of the fun techniques will include, guided meditation, inspiration and intuition. Come discover your path and purpose!

title: Intro to Stage Makeup
description: Provides an analysis of the basic functions of stage makeup and demonstrates the art of makeup application and design, including such potential topics as age, animal, corrective, contouring, fantasy/horror, cosplay, high fashion, and foam latex prosthetic makeup techniques. Students design and apply their own makeup as well as design and apply makeup for classmates and/or actors in HFC mainstage productions.

title: Making Sense of Your Retiremen
description: If you don't prepare for your retirement, no one else will. That's why it's important to take time now to discuss your retirement goals. An experienced financial advisor will help you learn more about the five basic questions you should ask yourself to prepare for retirement. Discover options you can use now to save for retirement, and how time can help or hurt your retirement goals. Take the first steps toward a successful retirement. Call today to reserve space for yourself and a spouse or friend.

title: Clinical Education III
description: This is the third in a five-course sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) competencies. This course offers continued exposure to chart recognition, patient assessment, patient communication, and departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, abdomen, upper extremity, shoulder girdle, lower extremity, and pelvic girdle are emphasized. Skull radiographic positioning is introduced.

title: Therapeutic Massage Techiques
description: Are you living with ongoing pain? Do you have pain on a daily basis in your low back, neck, shoulders or hands? Is regular massage to get rid of your pain not in your budget? Combining 9 years of experience and 8 modalities of bodywork, Deborah will teach you the best way to get rid of that ongoing pain wherever you feel it. Sign up a partner and learn easy, effective and money saving approaches to feel great, improve circulation and eliminate your pain!

title: Knee, Ankle & Feet Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols to relieve pain and stiffness in knees, ankles and feet. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve stiffness and pain - without taking drugs.

title: Step Aerobics - Walk-In Option
description: Using both high and low impact moves, this high energy aerobics class will help slim you down, tone you up, and improve your cardio vascular system. Want the workout without the steps? No problem! Instructor will work with you to create a routine that gets results. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Wear comfortable clothing. Please bring water and a towel.

title: Industrial Appl of Basic Math
description: Utilizes shop problems to help students relate math to various job situations. Topics include addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of whole and mixed numbers and common and decimal fractions; percents, averages, and estimates; graphs, tables, and statistical measure; powers and roots; linear, angular, and circular measure; surface area, volume, and cubic measure; ratios and proportion; and metrics and metric conversion.

title: Creating Web Pages II
description: Are you using HTML to its fullest potential? In this hands-on course, you'll become adept at using color, tables and frames to give your Web pages a more polished and professional look, improved navigation and more organization. Beef up the fun factor with audio and interactive forms, and learn how to save hours of time and effort with Cascading Style sheets.

title: Arabic for Business & Pleasure
description: With one of the largest concentrations of Arabic speaking people in the US right here in southeast Michigan, why not learn a bit of the language to make shopping, dining, business transactions, and communicating with co-workers and neighbors an enriching experience. Take advantage of this opportunity to learn about the Arab culture and how it relates to everyday life and business. Text included.

title: Standup Comedy 101
description: Do you enjoy telling funny stories and making people laugh? Ever wondered what it would be like to develop your own material and stand up on the stage and deliver it? Build your confidence in performing and overcome stage fright with proven techniques used in comedy. Instructor will guide you through developing your own routine, and the course will culminate with an opportunity for you to deliver an "invitation only" stand-up comedy performance for family and friends.

title: Condo Living
description: Do the benefits of condominium living entice you? From first-time homeowners to empty-nesters to retirees, condo ownership is becoming more and more popular. If you are considering purchasing a condo, learn from an individual who has served for 4 years as President of a large Detroit suburban complex tips on what to look for before closing the sale. If you are currently living in a condo or serve on an association board of directors, this is your opportunity to learn about management companies, legal issues, financial futures of your association, and more.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Introduces matrix and linear algebra. Covers systems of linear equations, matrix operations and properties of matrices, determinants, the n-dimensional real vector space, general vector spaces, inner product spaces, linear transformations, and eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Presents various applications. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments, access to a free graphing utility application to support learning and during informal assessments, and access to an online homework system.

title: Computers for Seniors/Beginner
description: So you took Getting Started with Computers for Seniors & Beginners and now you want more! In this new class you will learn concepts and skills needed for working with the web, how to utilize your email and use the MS Office tools and accessories. Take a look at how to manage files and use keyboard shortcuts. Prerequisite: Getting Started with Computers for Seniors and Beginners/Part I or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Getting Started with Computers for Seniors and Beginners/Part I or equivalent knowledge.

title: CATIA V5 Level III
description: Explores the use of the drafting workbench and surfacing. Covers the application of dimensions and tolerances, and the creation of advanced views. Instruction in surfacing includes extruding and the creation of simple and complex surfaces in the generative shape design workbench.

title: Media Camp
description: Make your mark on the world of media and have loads of fun in the process. Campers get a front row seat to learn about the magic of film and television, how a real radio station works, the art of telling a story or delivering news through the written script, and a number of performance techniques including character voices, newscasts, and interviews. Students will get hands-on opportunities to experience what their favorite actors, writers, editors, directors, newscasters and DJs do in the media field, from writing scripts, shooting footage, lighting, and editing to performing commercials, news and improve on camera and the radio.

title: Control Cholesterol (nurses)
description: If you have high cholesterol and are tired of the side effects or expense of cholesterol-lowering medications, sign up for this exciting class with naturopath Mary Born. Get amazing facts about natural ways to lower your cholesterol and triglyceride levels, discover the role of good fats in reducing bad fats, evaluate your risk for heart attack, and learn simple diet changes that dramatically improve heart health. Learn about three new risk factors for heart disease: homocysteine, pH, and C-reactive protein. Take an in-class quiz to see if you have Syndrome X - a known risk for heart disease. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. 2.5 nursing contact hours.

title: Legal Research
description: Examines the structure of the court systems and sources of law in the context of conducting legal research; covers how to locate and understand statutory, regulatory, and common law; examines how to identify real or hypothetical legal issues and then develop a research plan. Covers how to perform legal research using Westlaw and other Internet-based research databases.

title: Intro to Latin Dance: Salsa
description: Do you like to dance to Latin music and wish you could really move with the beat? Take this opportunity to learn the basics of Latin expression by exploring the difference dance styles of the salsa, merengue, cha-cha, and rumba.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: As you watch the prices for produce soar at the market do you wish you knew how to grow your own vegetables? Let Laurie Jeffery, certified Master Gardener, help get you started! Learn how to pick the site for your garden, what to plant, how to plant, how to care for your garden and how to harvest your crop.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-104W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Ext - Raw - Ordinary Health
description: Join health coach George Avedisian as he helps you lay the knowledge foundation for a raw foods lifestyle while making raw dishes such as smoothies, soups, super healthy chips and desserts. Raw food is full of vitamins, minerals, nutrients and enzymes. A raw diet has been the prescription for exceptional health for many people - come see how this prescription could work for you! Bring an apron, an appetite, and enjoy!

title: Pneumatic Hoist Repair
description: This course consists of 100% hands-on-time using on the job coaching for technicians. Time will be in properly equipped, designated training areas of maintenance shop, using actual Air Hoists and shop tools provided by Ford at each technicians work site and on the plant floor.

title: Glass Flowers
