description: Experience the fun of twisting, pulling and shaping hot glass into flower creations. Unlike nature's creations, your unique single-stemmed flower design can be displayed and enjoyed day after day for years to come. No experience necessary. Please wear loose-fitting cotton clothing. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Topics in Social Science
description: Provides special study on a topic in the Social Sciences, organized by subject, field of inquiry, or other criteria. This course may be taken twice for credit, six hours maximum, but the two topics must be different. Specific topics and any prerequisites are listed in the current semester's class schedule or may be obtained through the Social Science Division office.

title: Photoshop Elements
description: Learn to use Photoshop Elements 4, the home-user version of the popular Photoshop photo-editing software. Get step-by-step instructions about how to share, organize, edit, crop and size your digital images. Correct red-eye, adjust skin tones, adjust skin tones and remove scars, wrinkles and other blemishes like a pro! Use new tools to lift images and place them into other photos for amazing collages and scrapbook effects. Discover techniques for combining photos into panoramic views and for restoring old photos. Includes a CD with practice files for home use. If you prefer to enroll in Photoshop CS, see CL2 schedule.

title: Aerobics
description: Roll your way into shape! Get an aerobic workout while using skills you learned in beginner skating. Have fun on your skates and learn how to create your own exercise program all at the same time. An International Inline Skating Association (IISA) certified instructor will guide you through a circuit of moves that will get your heart rate going, and help to improve your skating skills. Students must provide skates equipped with a heel brake, helmet, wrist guards, elbow and kneepads.

title: Online Census Research
description: Census records are a staple of genealogical research. If you have had some experience researching your family history and would like to learn how to access free and fee-based 1790-1930 census information via the internet join Michael J. Neill, a weekly columnist for Ancestry Daily News, for this informative one-day workshop. You'll lean the most effective techniques for locating various online indexes, including the free indexes to the 1880 census at the Mormon genealogy site and at Ancestry.com. Basic knowledge of computers and the internet necessary. Each participant will have a computer to use. Enroll early. Class limited to 14.

title: Basic Sheet Metal Layout Fab
description: Covers layout, forming, and fabrication of basic sheet metal ductwork fittings and use of hand/power tools and equipment needed to accomplish various tasks. Explains how to fabricate square/round sheet metal ductwork, tapers, transitions, offsets, and how to fasten ductwork together.

title: Romance Writing Secrets
description: Learn how to write and sell romance novels. Facilitated by a bestselling, multi-published romance author, this course will guide you on your road to writing your first romance novel and getting it published. Topics to be covered include: character chemistry, the power of emotions, the art of romance, and handling love scenes. Plus you'll get the inside scoop on what it takes to get published to in today's romance market. This step-by-step course will put you well on the road to writing for love and money.

title: Gourmet Fresh Fruit Bouquet
description: Gourmet fresh fruit bouquets make elegant accents for all your entertaining needs. Why pay top dollar for these popular culinary art edible centerpieces when you can make your own? In this fun, step-by-step class you'll create a sweet and colorful bouquet bursting with fresh pineapple hearts, juicy grape spirals, mouthwatering strawberries, and succulent honeydew or cantaloupe crescents all nestled in a lovely container. Once you've learned how they're made you'll be able to recreate any other design you see, big or small. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-20).

title: Email for Seniors & Beginners
description: Finally - a class to help you really understand the features of email. Don't have an account? Anita will show you how to create a free gmail account. Learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, send photos, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. Discover how to protect yourself against spam and frauds. Prerequisite: Getting Started with Computers or equivalent experience.
prerequisite_text: Getting Started with Computers or equivalent experience.

title: Fluoroscopy Studies
description: This course is one in a series of four courses in radiographic positioning and procedures. The course provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for contrast studies of the digestive and the genitourinary tracts. Consideration is given to special radiographic contrast studies. Consideration is also given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Dsgn Proj Adobe Ill CS2
description: Practice while you learn the fundamentals of Adobe Illustrator CS2 with useful design projects. Master Illustrator's drawing tools, including the Pen tool. Learn how to use Illustrator's many image-editing features. Reinforce your Illustrator skills as you explore the elements of composition, color, and typography, along with secrets of good graphic design.

title: History of Jewelry
description: Mary MacDonald, Dearborn historian, will present an exciting evening describing the history of jewelry through time. She will show a slide presentation and bring some jewelry samples. Mary will cover some famous jewels like the Hope Diamond, and precious stones like diamonds, emeralds and rubies. She will also discuss prehistoric jewelry, nobility jewelry of kings and queens, costume jewelry, patriotic jewelry, and WWII jewelry. Join Mary for a dazzling evening of jewelry history.

title: Digital Photography (11-14)
description: Discover old uses for new technology! Utilizing today's digital cameras, you will create pieces with a taste of yesterday. Flip books, stereoviews, and other time-based and motion-based projects are the focus of this class. Supplies included. Digital cameras will be available for in-class use.

title: Intro to Industrial Technology
description: Introduces students to the fundamentals of being a successful college student on the path to a profession in industrial technology. Subjects are divided into three categories: Student Self Awareness, Manufacturing Work and Culture, and Test Taking (technical), and include topics such as self-directed learning, critical thinking and problem solving, industrial safety policies and procedures, job outlook, and elements of standardized testing including graphic arithmetic, assemblies, mechanical comprehension, and troubleshooting. The class includes guest speakers from manufacturing and the service industry. The course utilizes digital learning materials, hands-on labs, and a student self assessment of their learning and study skills.

title: Metaphysics & Psychic Phen II
description: This workshop will explore "The sleeping prophet Edgar Cayce", "The Secret", the "Akashic Records", auras, intuition, spirits, and much more. Bring your imagination and an open mind. You'll be glad you did!

title: Basic Electricity
description: Covers the fundamentals of electricity as applied to the electrical field. Topics include the electron theory, Ohm's Law, circuits, magnetism, inductance, capacitance, and alternating current circuits. Utilizes voltmeters, ammeters, power supplies, signal generators, and oscilloscopes to construct circuits during lab activities. Also utilizes computer simulations. Introduction to writing reports. Labs involve breadboards and the above-mentioned equipment and components. Extensive laboratory activities.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Electrical diagnosis includes computer-controlled charging systems, battery, and starting systems, including one-touch and remote-start systems. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile.

title: Asian Art: India & SE Asia
description: Offers a global view on Asian art, culture, and history by exploring the major monuments, traditions, and civilizations of India, China, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. Covers important Asian artists and historical figures, artistic terms, monuments, sites, and time periods. Discusses art and architecture in terms of historical, social, and religious context. Analyzes and distinguishes various styles of art, and examines how and why certain styles are characteristic of specific times and places.

title: Intro to ASP.NET
description: Learn how to create exciting, interactive and professional web sites with ASP.NET. In this hands-on course you'll begin by adding the power of interactive controls to your web pages, and you'll discover how you can use style sheets to give every web page you create the same clean and consistent look and feel. You'll find out how to connect to a database and put the information it contains on public display. Then you'll develop a true community-based web site that allows users to register, log in, create and update profiles, and post messages to a forum. You'll also explore the issue of web security and learn some simple things you can do to secure your site against some common attacks.

title: Fancy Cookie Art Workshop
description: Instructor will collect $5.00 materials fee first day of class.

title: Holiday Crafts for Kids
description: Got a young budding artist at home? Here's an opportunity to encourage their talent this holiday season. Bring them to this craft class designed especially for young people. Experienced artists will work with them to make two gifts - a whimsical picture frame and a jewelry box - suitable for parents or other special people. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class. Match your child to an age appropriate section.

title: Monitor Your Child's Net Use
description: Do you know about MySpace, Facebook and Friendstar? Chances are they may be an essential part of your teen's social networking. Learn what MySpace and its contemporaries are all about, how to navigate them, create profiles, find and monitor your child's activities and more. Students must have a working knowledge of computers and the internet.

title: Greek for Travelers
description: Whether you are planning a trip to Greece or simply want to learn conversational Greek skills, this course will introduce you to the appropriate use of Greek in various social situations and will provide you cultural information about everyday life in modern Greece. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Thessaloniki, Greece, will teach you basic expressions that will help you when making accommodations, eating out, traveling, sightseeing, shopping and more. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $15).

title: Webmaster
description: This online program prepares students for a dynamic internet-based career as a Webmaster. Webmasters design, develop and maintain web sites. This Webmaster program begins by teaching simple web page development and progresses by introducing new concepts by involving you in active web page implementation using HTML and Dynamic HTML. Textbook included.

title: The Art of Couponing
description: Looking for a way to save money on your monthly budget? Couponer Elvira Rivera will teach you how to utilize your weekly sale flyers and coupons to your advantage. Learn how to find coupons, pitfalls to avoid, how to organize, how to use websites and how to track your savings. Things have changed in the world of coupons--learn the newest methods to save your hard-earned dollars!

title: Substation Operation & Mainten
description: Introduces the operation and maintenance of substations and switchyards, and discusses commonly used equipment and and the roles of various field personnel. Topics include safety practices, power transformers, circuit breakers, system performance, and substation batteries.

title: Should You Incorp Business
description: If you have a small business, are a sole proprietor, or are thinkin about starting a business, you need to discover the best legal entity to protect your assets and avoid problems with the IRS. Ken Bloom, an attorney and tax specialist, will teach you the benefits, record keeping requirements and tax consequences of choosing a limited liability corporation (LLC), S corporation, partnership, and C corporation. Bring your questions to discuss.

title: Continuing Pilates
description: Continue sculpting your body in this class design- ed for those who have taken the beginning pilates class or already are familiar with the method. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfort- able, stretchable clothing.

title: Adobe Flash Cs3
description: Create Flash-based movies that contain text and animation that will display correctly in almost every browser on most computers. This hands-on class is a must for anyone who wants to develop multimedia and distribute it on the web for electronic communications. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Text provided.

title: Art of Precious Metal Clay
description: Learn the basics of working with this revolutionary new potter's clay that converts into pure precious metal. Kathleen Robinson-Young, certified through the Precious Metal Clay (PMC) Guild, will introduce you to the different types of PMC and lead you through the process of creating your own necklace pendant and a pair of earrings. $37 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: CPR, AED & First Aid
description: Gain the knowledge and skills needed to effectively identify and respond to emergencies caused by injury or sudden illness. Basic first aid, cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) and the automatic external defibrillator (AED) will be covered. Students will be expected to participate in all class activities. Upon successful completion you will receive an American Red Cross certificate. Course is appropriate for lay rescuers, adult or child caregivers, and anyone wanting to know how to help someone in an emergency. Wear comfortable clothing and bring a notebook and pen. Materials fee of $35 is payable to the instructor in class.

title: IRC Ford-Mach Tool BP Reading
description: An introduction of blueprint reading; interpretation of various kinds of lines, position of views, symbols, conventions, dimensioning practices, sectioning, auxiliary views and symmetry with emphasis on techniques employed to show details in relation to assembly drawings. This class is only for UAW-Ford employees who will be participating in Industrial Readiness Certificate Program.

title: Plumbing Design
description: Topics include health and safety, water supply, water wastes, piping materials, and building plans and drawings. The proper use of plumbing tools and equipment selection emphasizing overall job safety is integrated into the course material. Also covers the components of plumbing systems and installations.

title: Polish Sides
description: Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suter will show you how to make summer seasonal salads, to include a summer egg salad, potato salad, beet salad, potato pancakes, Kasha & others. Please bring an apron and an appetite and enjoy.

title: Applied Music (2 Credits) 2
description: Second of four semesters of weekly one-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: More Tradition German Cuisine
description: Guten Appetit: If you love German cuisine, you are going to love this class. If you are not sure and just want to try it, you are also in the right place. Join Ursula Markovits, who was born and raised in Germany, for a bit of the country's food and culture. Learn how to whip up a traditional German dinner of rouladen (stuffed beef rolls), noodles, and cherry bread pudding. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Line Dancing (3 Sessions)
description: Abbreviated version of CEART 1133.

title: Dreamweaver CS3 - Level 2
description: This course will cover advanced Adobe Dreamweaver CS3 topics including cascading style sheets, forms, navigation bars, spry widgets, Flash buttons and AP elements. Upon course completion, you will have the skills necessary to create a working website with many advanced features. There will be lots of hands-on practice at your own workstation. Prerequisite: Create a Great Website with Dreamweaver CS3 or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Create a Great Website with Dreamweaver CS3 or equivalent knowledge.

title: Decorate Like a Designer
description: Would you love to have your home interior draw raves from your friends. Learn how from Madge Lawson, a graduate of the Sheffield School of Interior Design. She will teach you how designers place furniture, choose colors, and create harmony with accessories. In class you will sketch your room's measurements on graph paper, so you can plan pleasing furniture layouts. Then get help choosing companion colors for walls, furniture and flooring to achieve your dream look. Take home many handouts and helpful tips. Bring a small pair of scissors.

title: Desc Geom: Planes, Solid & Dev
description: Presents basic descriptive geometry theory and practice. Discusses how to use orthographic principles to find true views of planes and solids and their intersections.

title: Heat Pumps & Hydronic Systems
description: This course introduces technician-level students to the principles, operation, and application of heat pump and hydronic heating/cooling systems. Students learn how energy is transferred using water and refrigerants, how to safely install and service equipment, and how system components work together to provide efficient thermal comfort. Laboratory sessions reinforce classroom theory through hands-on exercises in system assembly, measurement, troubleshooting, and performance evaluation.

title: Heal Arthritis Naturally
description: From pharmacist Debbie Danto find out whether glucosamine, chondroitin, MSM, SAM-e, Vitamin C, fish oil, and other supplements alleviate pain and swelling. Also learn about prescription and over-the-counter medications commonly used for arthritis, includin their benefits and side effects.

title: Digital Media
description: An introduction to the usage of digital media as it applies within a studio art discipline. Various types of imaging software, the use of time-based media as well as cross platform construction will be covered. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Surgical Techniques I
description: Laboratory course designed to develop basic aseptic and sterile techniques used in the perioperative care of the surgical patient. This course focuses on the surgical scrub, gowning and gloving, setting up a sterile field, draping, handling of surgical instruments, and surgical counts. Students practice techniques in a guided lab setting.

title: Teaching Science: Grades 4-6
description: Ready to maximize your abilities in teaching science? You'll learn about foundational content in physical, life, and earth science, and ways to teach that content to your students. Discover specific teaching methods and science process skills, and learn how to improve the emotional climate in your classroom. Lots of examples and worksheets you can use in your own classroom are included. There's a big emphasis on the scientific method, and there's even an entire lesson devoted to science fairs!

title: World Regional Geography
description: An overview examining the spatial distribution of people and places around the world, with emphasis on the cultural, political, social, and economic aspects of regions. Topics are explored by examining ways that humans interact with the environment and one another. Emphasis is placed on the spatial variation of human activities at all scales from local to global.

title: Shop Tools & Techniques
description: Introduces the basic tools, safety protocols, and technical information required by the skilled trades. Includes non-precision and precision measurement tools and methods, layout tools and techniques, hand and bench tools, drills and drilling machines, principles of metal cutting and metal cutting saws, pedestal grinders, and various manufacturing processes.

title: Computer Security/Maintenance
description: Learn about computer security and maintenance in both Windows Vista and XP. You will work with user accounts, passwords and desktop security. Take the mystery out of installing & updating hardware & software. Learn to maintain and back up your hard drive and data. Class work includes discussion of the latest security threats and defenses. Basic computer knowledge required. Text book provided.

title: Wrking W/Fractions: Fund Ideas
description: This course is designed to provide teachers with fundamental understanding of fractions used for teaching children at a variety of grades. It is designed as a workshop so that each session will include examination of one key element of fractions to study the mathematical elements, childrens work with this key element to study childrens understandings of this element, and curricula to study teachers resources to teach this element. Consistent with the Common Core State Standards, participants will explore mathematical problems in which fundamental ideas about fractions are embedded. They will examine cases of childrens work that has been used for professional development and cases from their teaching to see alternative ways to think about fractions. Additionally, teachers will work with the curricula from which they teach to design lessons about fractions.

title: Fall Birding in Michigan
description: If you are at least 16 years old, join Allen Chartier, co-author of Birds of Detroit and a very active birder, for a unique fall birding adventure. Through classroom instruction and field work you will improve your observation skills, develop ways to record your sightings and learn to use field marks to identify birds. After an initial classroom session, plan to meet your instructor at local sites to practice identifying and observing a variety of birds in their native habitats. Directions to the sites will be provided in the first class. Bring binoculars to enrich your experience. The required textbook, The Sibley Guide to Birds, will be available in class for approximately $25.

title: Chinese Cooking
description: Come savor the flavors, aromas and textures of authentic Chinese cuisine as you help Liping Prus, a certified cook from Shanghai, prepare some popular dishes. Learn to make favorites like Won-Ton Soup and Egg Rolls, and other delights chosen especially for this session. Bring an apron and an appetite - you'll definitely want to sample everything!

title: Cartooning-Humor to Your World
description: Have you always wanted to draw cartoons, either for fn or profit? Attend this class and enter the wonderful world of 'tooning! Using a variety of methods your instructor will teach you the basic terms and techniques of the cartoon trade. You'll learn how to sketch and compose using various styles and techniques, create your own cartoons, and market and sell your work in the industry.

title: NC History of Rock & Roll
description: This course covers Rock & Roll from its origins to the present. A field trip to The Rock & Roll Hall of Fame is offered. This course is primarily for the non-music major and fulfills Humanities requirements. Comparable to MUS 133.

title: How to Find the Right Tenants
description: Have a spare bedroom or rental property you are leasing? Worried about how to find the right tenant? This class will show you proven tenant screening methods, review lease documentation, liability concerns, insurance and how to protect yourself from the uncertainty. Our proven methods will relieve you from these concerns.

title: Glass Beadmaking 1
description: Take your jewelry-making skills to a whole new level in this basic lampworking course. Under the guidance of professional glass bead and jewelry maker Kathleen Robinson-Young, you will learn to use a Minor Bench Burner torch to design, shape and decorate eads using such techniques as dots raking, stripes, silver leaf, millefiori and twisties. You'll leave the class with a piece of jewelry made from your own beads. $35 supply fee due to instructor. Please bring a notebook,pen or pencil and drinking water (to prevent dehydration from torch) to the class. For directions to the studio, go to "Supplies and Maps". Class limited to 9.

title: Intuition Development 1
description: In this workshop Professional Intuitive Kristy Robinett will provide proven ways to tap into your own innate intuitive ability, which will help increase your effectiveness in all areas of your life. Participants will be able to: 1)use proven methods of centering to gain better clarity of the issues impacting their clients or themselves; 2) trust their intuition by removing their fear of tapping into this keen insight; and 3) be able to identify which of the three main types of intuition is their strongest. Gather up your friends and make a night of it!

title: Birding: Focus on Feeder Birds
description: Do you have a bird feeder? What types of birds visit in the winter? Do you know their names? Travel with veteran birder and author Allen Chartier for this first birding trip of the year to several locations to observe the birds that spend the winter with us.

title: Leadership, Organizing & Publi
description: This course provides the leadership tools and theories to help students create change through hands-on work in community organizations. The course will use a project-based learning to examine the strategic nature of coalition building, networking, and goal/power mapping. This will be done within diverse communities to provide help students advocate with the experience for and effect change within an organization. The course is designed for both those students already engaged in community-based organizations and partnerships as well as students who are interested in working in/and or developing such organizations.

title: NC Descriptive Astronomy
description: Designed for general education students, this course consists of a non-mathematical introduction to elements of the astronomical universe by means of lectures and planetarium demonstrations. Organized to interest the individual who is without scientific background but who desires to understand the major units of the universe and their interrelation. Three hours of lecture per week. Comparable to ASTR 131.

title: Holiday Sweets & Tiny Treats
description: Get ready for a one-of-a-kind holiday cooking extravaganza. Just in time for the holidays, Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will amaze you with this part cookie exchange, part hors d'doeuvres delight class. You'll learn to bake and display a variety of cookies, including angel wings, birds next, a Polish treat and more. Then take some home to enjoy. You'll also make a unique Christmas tree stand suitable for displaying various hors d'oeuvres. Don't miss this one! Bring an apron. All supplies included.

title: Mexican Tamales
description: Chef Alicia Bonilla-Arias will show you how to make authentic tamales to spice up your holiday fare. You will make the dough and use corn husks, parchment or foil. She makes vegetarian tamales with spinach or sweet tamales with pecans, pine-nuts, raisins, pineapples or cranberries. She will show you how to make the meat sauce. You will be able to sample your tamales. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Indust App of Phys Science
description: Offers the apprentice lecture exposure to the applications of physics and chemistry found in today's industrial workplace. Covers the six elemental machines; applications of forces, motion, and work; and the chemistry of industrial materials and chemical interactions in the environment.

title: Cultures of North America
description: Introduces the diverse cultures, histories, and traditions of the indigenous peoples, nations, and civilizations of North America from their origins through the era of European colonization. Examines contemporary social, economic, and political currents as well as challenges faced by Native American communities today. The course employs an interdisciplinary approach that draws from historical archaeology, ethnography, history, studies in colonialism and material culture, literature, and film.

title: Intro Microsoft Project 2003
description: Discover how to effectively plan, implement and control projects using Microsoft Project 2003. In this hands-on course, you will find out how to apply the world's most popular project management software to your needs. You'll convert lists and schedules into an integrated plan you can be proud of, and you'll see how Microsoft Project can walk you through any project by helping you organize details, sequence tasks, produce a baseline, assign resources and costs, track progress, identify and anlyze variances, revise your project plan, create schedules, and share everything with your customers and coworkers.

title: Civil War, Class 1: Css Hunley
description: In 1864 the CSS H. L. Hunley was the first submarine to sink an enemy ship in combat That same night it was sunk and lost for more than 130 years. Its sailors remained at their battle stations preserved in a unique time capsule. Local resident and presenter, Larry Jackson, took a private tour of the submarine and talked extensively with the divers who made the discovery. He will regale you little known stories about the sailors, the politics and rivalries of the day. Larry Jackson belongs to many Civil War groups, has published numerous articles, and is an accomplished and entertaining speaker with a fascinating story to tell.

title: Advanced Refrigeration
description: This course expands upon foundational refrigeration principles by introducing advanced system configurations, components, and control strategies used in commercial, industrial, and energy network applications. Students will study complex refrigeration cycles, multi-evaporator systems, cascade and secondary loop designs, and the integration of refrigerant systems with hydronic and district energy systems. Emphasis is placed on energy efficiency, environmental considerations, and modern control technologies.

title: Knitting 101
description: It's not just for grannies anymore! Whether you're new to knitting or trying to develop more sills, let instructor Carol Bacon, a long-time knitter and an event coordinator at a major arts and crafts store, show you the basics of the fine art of knitting. In this fun, introductory stress-reducing class, she will teach you to knit, purl, cast off, sew seams and more. Bring any size needles and 4-ply yarn the first night. Supplies for projects will be discussed in the first class.

title: Your Beautiful Cakes: Wedding
description: A wedding cake is the most important piece of elegant yet edible artwork that a bride will purchase in her lifetime. As a wedding cake designer it is your job to create the cake of her dreams! Join Chef Rachel to learn how to construct magnificent tiered cakes that will make the wedding reception most memorable. Discover various high level decorating techniques as well as the building, sculpting, transporting and even contract writing for this specialized cake.

title: Cont Cake Decorating
description: Step ahead with your decorating skills as you continue your work in developing more complex designs with our professional cake designer. Learn to dabble in gum paste and fondant to create more complex flowers and other decorative items.

title: Stage Your Home for Quick Sale
description: If you plan to sell your home soon and it has the "lived-in" look, resembles the set for "That 70's Show," or needs a general sprucing up, attend this timely seminar. Darlene Rothman, a professional home stager, will show you how to make your home more attractive to buyers using inexpensive tips from a new trend called "home staging." She'll discuss furniture placement, uncluttering, paint colors, traffic flow and more. Feel free to bring pictures of your home.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 2
description: Teaches beginning-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-103W. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Special Topics in Energy Techn
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current energy technology trends. Specific special topics are announced together with the prerequisites each term. The student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic. Course may be used toward fulfilling the specific degree requirements for an associate degree or certificate.

title: Reiki "try It" Session
description: Here's an opportunity to come and have a Reiki chair treatment as part of our open Reiki share group. In a safe and serene environment you will be invited to relax in a chair as a trained Reiki practitioner brings Reiki energy (chi) to your body through various gentle hand positions. (Or hands off if you prefer.) It is also referred to by some as energy balancing of the chakras (energy centers of the body.) Most find a treatment to be relaxing and refreshing. Reiki philosophy teaches that the chi or energy brought from the Universe goes to the "highest healing good." For some that may be physical, emotional, or spiritually oriented. You are welcome to make two visits for free without signing up to take a Reiki class. Please register so we'll know you are planning to attend.

title: Fit Ball Fusion
description: Sculpt a long, strong body in an amazing class that fusses strength training, yoga, pilates, and core conditioning with the unique functions of the fit ball. Add a new dimension to your workouts, and reap the extra benefits possible by using an exercise ball. Have a ball with Instructor Dena Raptis-Evans. Born and raised in Detroit, Dena moved to Boston, where she owned a popular aerobic and martial arts academy and won the "Best of Boston" award for top fitness instructor. Bring your own mat and ball (buy 55 cc ball if you are 5' to 5'8" or 65 cc if you are over 5'8").

title: Software Engineering
description: A capstone course involving the study and application of software engineering principles to design and develop software. Emphasizes written and oral communication skills necessary for the information technology professional through participation in a software project designed to reinforce course material.

title: Intro to Science for Elem Educ
description: An inquiry based course that provides students a broad overview of both science instruction and the process of science. Students will investigate, discuss and design experiments using the group inquiry method of instruction. This is the first science course in a sequence intended for pre-service elementary school education majors. Two combined classroom/lab sessions per week.

title: Industrial Applications of Geo
description: Covers the fundamental principles of plane geometry with emphasis on application to the industrial environment. Explores angular, circular, linear, area, and volume measurement in relation to the types of geometric figures and configurations found in industry.

title: The Flash & Color Workshop
description: Are you having camera flash and color problems? Well, you are not alone. Don't miss this opportunity to learn from two of the most experienced photography experts in the country. Andreas Browne and SPECIAL GUEST world-renowned color expert Gary Poole as they help you color correct and capture images like the pros do. Learn the everyday techniques they use that have been helping professional photographers across the United States. (Lunch included.)

title: Screenwriting Act II
description: The screenplay is the foundation of a visual story and is usually divided into three acts, where each successive act builds on the previous towards a masterful conclusion. Using your completed Act I, apply additional techniques that will move your story along to the next level.

title: ELL Writing & Grammar 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college-preparatory writing and grammar skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELL-192. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Creative Gift for One You Love
description: Flowers, candy and a card...nice, but ho-hum. It's time to step out of the box and give from the heart. Learn to personalize a gift for that special someone and present it in unique and thoughtful style in a basket or other unusual presentation. Do you need a small gift for a co-worker, shower or housewarming? In class learn how to create personalized gift baskets, how to become a creative gift giver, and how to make small gifts that look special for any occasion. $30 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Electrician Skills Upgrade
description: This course provides an entry level student with an overview of the technology used in automated integrated systems found in manufacturing. Included is also an overview of the typical plant networks and their associated responsibility. Distributed and local control is examined. Analysis of sequencing machines is completed along with an introduction of how to troubleshoot these systems. Topics include: fluid power components, electrical components, conveyers, part sensing components and an overview of PLC control principles.

title: Read Face-Assess Health & Pers
description: What can your lips tell about your GI tract, the lines between your eyebrows about your liver health, and wrinkles around the eyes about your kidneys? Get the answers by attending this enlightening class with John Jo M., a naturopathic physician who has practiced the art of facial diagnosis in India. Learn to read specific facial signs that may predict current or future health issues without expensive lab tests or x-rays. Practice this art in class by bringing a standing mirror and a picture of someone's face. Ideal for parents, caregivers, seniors, etc.

title: Spec Topics in Electrical Tech
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current electrical technology trends. The specific special topics and the prerequisites will be announced each term. Course may be repeated when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Bridge to Success in Math
description: Utilizes adaptive and/or modular software to assist students in learning or refreshing mathematics skills. Topics vary by individual student. Course supports satisfying mathematics prerequisites across disciplines. Requires regular on campus attendance and access to an online homework management system.

title: Mechanics of Materials
description: An introduction to stress analyses with emphasis on both mechanics fundamentals and design applications. Elastic relationships between external forces acting on deformable bodies and the associated stresses and deformations; structural members subjected to axial load, torsion, and bending; and combined stresses.

title: Bus & Tech Writing & Research
description: Designed for students interested in developing professional workplace communication and critical thinking skills, ENG-135 focuses on designing documents such as resumes, letters, memos, instructions, proposals, and an extensive, research-based documented report for the kinds of readers they will address as professionals. This course should be especially valuable for students pursuing careers in engineering business, computer science, automotive technology, allied health, and other technical fields.

title: Yoga
description: This yoga class is an invigorating mix between vinyasa (or flow) and power yoga. The focus is on postures and breathing as you move smoothly from one pose to another. Improve your health, fitness level and mental acuity. Bring a yoga mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Insure Retirement Investments
description: Your chance of living 20, 30 or more years after retirement are significantly greater than that for previous generations. Thus, your retirement income must last longer. Certified Financial Planner Vera Johnson will present various options (including one that is new since 9/11) for protecting the value of your savings, assuring continued income during retirement, and minimizing tax consequences.

title: Fantasy Character Design
description: Develop the characters of your wildest imagination. Students will be introduced to anatomy basics and create a fantasy character based on facial expression, character development, and lighting. The class will discuss and add visual cues that enhance the narrative of the character. Campers will be encouraged to draw inspiration from contemporary characters in movies, video games and/or animee, as well as incorporate science fiction or fantasy elements within their work. A gallery exhibition will show of the work of class participants at the end of the final class session.

title: Indust App of Phys Science
description: Offers the apprentice lecture exposure to the applications of physics and chemistry found in today's industrial workplace. Covers the six elemental machines; applications of forces, motion, and work; and the chemistry of industrial materials and chemical interactions in the environment.

title: What STEMS from Here?
description: Join us this summer to find out "What STEMS from Here? This hands-on camp highlights robotics, design, and manufacturing in a way that you have never imagined. Learn about opportunities in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) as well as entrpreneurship.

title: Beginning Japanese
description: Instructor Scott Corp with 10 years experience of living and teaching in Japan will give you insight about the culture and the basics of the language. Whether you want to learn for fun or for travel, he will give you an easy guided course to learning a great Asian language. The book, Beginning Japanese, will be available for purchase the first night of the class from the instructor for approx. $35. You will also need to bring a pen and a notebook to each class.

title: Land Your Dream Job
description: Blow away your competition with a resume that generates more interviews and interviews that result in higher salary offers. Learn from a professional resume writer the latest techniques for developing and marketing a winning resume in today's technological environment. You'll also discover strategies that will help you successfully interview, get the job you want, and then negotiate the very best job offer! Feel free to bring your current resume and information about any job of interest to you.

title: Technical Descriptive Geometr
description: Covers advanced projection techniques. Utilizes auxiliary views to manipulate geometry and define relationships between points, lines, surfaces, and solids. Also covers revolution, intersections, solids, and developments.

title: Polish Village Wedding
description: Come join Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suter as she shares with you the pictures and recipes of her trip this past summer to Poland where she enjoyed the 2 day feast and celebration of a traditional Polish village wedding. She will talk about and prepare with the students some of the local dishes and explain the customs that took place. We will prepare several salads and delights that will make any party a delight.

title: Proces Tech Instrumentation II
description: Provides a review of microprocesser control components and control systems, power supplies, emergency shutdown procedures, and malfunctions. Examines switches, relays, and annunciator systems. Also covers covers signal conversion and transmission; controllers; and control schemes that maintain environmental regulations, energy efficiency, and quality of the process.

title: Online Genealogy
description: Discover ways to save hours of valuable reseach time using online maps and library card catalogs. In this hands-on workshop, Michael Neill, an experienced genealogist, will teach you how to search both modern and historical online map and geographical sites. You will also learn how to use online library card catalogs, including World Cat, a worldwide online card catalog of 9,000+ libraries.

title: Automotive Fundamentals
description: Provides an introduction to the major automotive systems covered in greater detail in other automotive technology specific system classes. Explores how to perform preventive maintenance routines, such as oil changes, tire rotations on standard systems as well as on vehicles equipped with TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System); brake and suspension system inspections; and how to service the cooling system. This is the only course allowed for Secondary Partnership transfer credit (4 credit hours) from approved high school programs.

title: Desktop Pub Adobe Indesign Cs5
description: Use the brand new Adobe InDesign CS5 software to create your catalogs, brochures, flyers and other items. Lay out your text and graphics like the pros and avoid having to send these items out to an expensive designer. Easily coordinate with professional printers, publish in your own office, or output to multiple media. Create rich full-featured documents with easy integration with Photoshop and other programs. The instructor will get you started and help to demystify the many features incorporated in this professional page layout program. Text provided. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Sign up today!
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Sign up today!

title: Mouth Watering Desserts
description: Candy canes are great for hanging on a tree, but if you really want to satisfy your sweet tooth this season, try a luscious homemade holiday dessert. Spend an evening with Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estate. Learn to prepare some of his favorite desserts, including creme brulee, chocolate mousse, cranberry cheesecake, and an assortment of miniature pastries. Limited to 16.

title: Get Started With Email
description: Do you have an email account but feel that you really don't understand the features? Or maybe you'd like to open a free gmail account, the new electronic mail system everyone is talking about. Come and have Noel help you build your comfort level with creating email letters, attaching files (pictures!), working with your contacts or address books, and creating your own signatures. Learn how to deal with unwanted emails and protect yourself against scams and frauds. Prerequisite: Into to Computers or equivalent experience a must!
prerequisite_text: Into to Computers or equivalent experience a must!

title: Indst. App of Algebraic Prin
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas. Non-credit equivilent to TAMA 120

title: How to Buy Hud/Foreclosed Prop
description: Learn about buying homes for less than $20,000! The time has never been better! Class will address the benefits and advantages of HUD (government owned) properties and foreclosures. An informational packet is available from the instructor for $5 collected at class.

title: Buy & Sell on Ebay - Beginners
description: eBay has become the world's online auction king-an international marketplace where people meet to buy and sell everything! Here's your chance to learn to make money by selling things like unused gifts or vintage items from an estate. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer, will teach you how to locate excellent deals on hard-to-find items, reach millions of potential buyers for your items, maximize your selling price, minimize your costs as a buyer, and more. Basic computer skills needed.

title: Microsoft Off Ste 2007 - Beg
description: Designed for the student who is relatively new to computing, this class will help you learn about Word, Excel, Access and PowerPoint. Whether you are looking to upgrade your job skills or plan to use Office at home, this class will help you get started. Lots of fun and practical exercises help you understand what the Office Suite is all about, and help you determine what direction you might like to take for future classes. A must for people who need to show proficiency as part of the job search. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.

title: Hospitality & Travel Marketing
description: Explores the need and value of a cooperative marketing effort among hotels, airlines, restaurants, travel agents, and other important industry groups. Discusses research and analysis, the development and implementation of marketing plans and strategies, advertising, promotions, public relations, and pricing structures.

title: Chinese Cooking
description: Chinese food need never again be a once-a-week restaurant treat. Learn to prepare your own authentic Chinese meals. Ping Bai, a graduate of Beijing Teaching College, will teach you how to prepare an authentic Chinese meal of pot stickers, peal meatballs and fried noodles. You'll also learn a bit about the culture, including the use of chopsticks. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Jazz Band 1
description: First of four semesters of jazz band, including preparation and performance of Big Band music in a variety of jazz styles from swing to contemporary. This group, which performs several concerts each semester, is open to college students and adult members of the community who play saxophone, trumpet, trombone, piano, guitar, bass, or drums. New instrumentalists should contact the director to arrange for an audition prior to the first rehearsal.

title: Siemens PLC/HMI Programming
description: A discussion of the Siemens PLC hardware and the Siemens HMI device is given. Step by step programming instructions are then explained to use the Siemens hardware and Siemens HMI device as a system. The student builds on the information discussed and learns both programming skills as well as troubleshooting skills through both lecture and lab situations. An extensive hands-on approach to programming and troubleshooting of the Siemens PLC/HMI system.

title: Musical Theater Techniques
description: Production of Oliver.

title: JavaScript
description: An intermediate-level course which explores the JavaScript programming language and its various components. Emphasizes designing, coding, testing, and debugging programs using the JavaScript language through laboratory exercises and detailed lectures.

title: Beginning Latin & Ballrm Dance
description: Provides the basics of ballroom in the American and International styles in a group setting. Students will be able to recognize and execute basics of American Smooth and International Standard styles of Waltz, Foxtrot, Cha Cha, Rumba and East Coast Swing. Technique, rhythms and leading and following are studied and practiced.

title: SPC in Manufacturing
description: Covers the use of Statistical Process Control (SPC) in manufacturing to achieve optimum product quality at lowest cost. Development of statistical charts and their interpretation related to process improvement are integral parts of the course. Also covers formulas pertaining to various charts, gage R & R, Cp, and Cpk.

title: Get the "Red" Out
description: Explore the effects of anger on your mind and body in this two-hour workshop by biochemist Sandy Baumann. Contrary to what is often taught, venting anger is not always the best approach. Come learn anger-reducing techniques that will not damage your health or relationships.

title: Honors Directed Study
description: Challenges sophomore students to apply knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Coursework includes presenting a research summary before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty at the end of each semester.

title: Easy Holiday Entertaining
description: Amaze your friends and family at Thanksgiving, Christmas, or New Year's with terrific parties and buffet dinners featuring foods you actually prepare. Let cookbook author Leonard Charla help you master the art of fun, easy entertaining, including flavorful appetizers and canapes, roast turkey breast, make-ahead salads, and easy oven dishes. Gain realistic, down-to-earth, practical tips and recipes while you prepare and eat in this class designed for eager novices. Instructor's cookbook (optional) available for $11 in class. Bring your questions and an apron. Men and women of all ages are welcome.

title: Basic Rider Course-Traditional
description: Basic Rider Course - Traditional This class consists of approximately 20 hours of instruction - 10 hours of classroom teaching and 10 hours of hands-on training. Designed for someone who has minimal experience operating a motorcycle, this course focuses on the development of basic riding skills: clutch and throttle coordination, straight-line riding, braking, turning, and shifting. Successful completion of this course and its knowledge and skills tests will result in a riding test waiver by the Secretary of State. Prerequisite: Ability to ride a bicycle. For more information, contact 313.317-6600.
prerequisite_text: Ability to ride a bicycle. For more information, contact 313.317-6600.

title: Create Neat & Sweet Holiday
description: Make and take home a big tray full of goodies for your holiday table including a Mini "Snow" Covered Cream Puff Tree, Cheery Chocolate Mice and adorable White Chocolate Marshmallow Family, Sweet and Crunchy Peppermint Bark, Chocolate Covered Spoons (to flavor your coffee) and a festive Holiday mini Yule log. Call or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list. (approx $14-18)

title: Medical Transcription Editor
description: This online program helps you develop the knowledge and skills of quality medical transcriptionists in addition to the specialized skill set of medical transcription editors. The demand for well-trained medical transcription editors is rapidly increasing, and this program prepares you for both career paths, making you more flexible after graduation. Within the program, you focus on keyboarding, medical terminology, language and grammar, and the editing skills required to work with speech recognition technology. This program's online format provides you the flexibility to study around your schedule and at your own pace. You will have one year to complete this course; earlier completion is typical. (Course fee includes foot pedal, BenchMark KB 15-month subscription, AHDA 15-month membership, student support, placement assistance, and career guidance - all through our partner provider.)

title: Do It Yourself Wedding Bouquet
description: Do you love DIY projects? This course will teach you how to take a simple bunch of flowers and turn them into a breathtaking wedding bouquet and bridesmaids bouquet that you get to take home and enjoy. No experience required! All tools and supplies will be provided. Registration for this class closes on November 5 so supplies can be ordered.

title: A'la Carte and Buffet Cookery
description: Provides practical experience in all areas of quality food preparation by rotating throughout each station of the a'la carte kitchen. Students explore the proper techniques of broiling, sauteing, meat cutting, dessert presentation, buffet preparation, and cold food stations in the on campus, student-run restaurant.

title: Beginning Modern Dance
description: Explores Modern Dance as an art form that has a limitless way to express oneself in human movement. Primary emphasis is on developing technique of the basic Modern movement vocabulary. Course also covers skeletal muscular anatomy and function as it applies to dance movement, and an introduction to improvisation and composition.

title: History of Collecting
description: When did accumulating "stuff" transform into acquiring collectibles? Why is collecting so popular? Join Mary McDonald, Dearborn historian, for a fun and fact-filled discussion on the evolution of this world-wide pastime. Learn about markings and what collections will likely be of value in the future. This class is a must for all collectors. Bring pen and paper to take notes.

title: Industrial Detail
description: Explores the making of working drawings using proper dimensioning techniques. Showing necessary views, placement of dimensions, use of different dimensioning styles, and calculation of tolerances are the basis of instruction. The function and relationship of mating parts in an assembly are considered when dimensioning to insure proper fit and function. Threads, fasteners, and common manufacturing operations are applied and dimensioned. Standard and commercial parts are selected from catalogs. Setup and application of CAD dimensioning styles and tolerances are utilized.

title: Internal Combustion Engines
description: Introduces four-stroke engines by exploring principles of operation, compression ratio, piston displacement, operating tolerances, valve timing, horsepower and torque development along with adjustments, inspection, and troubleshooting procedures. Course topics help students prepare for ASE certification exams and State of Michigan licensure, but these exams/licensure are not included in this course. Laboratory activities.

title: Old Time Polish Sweets & Baked
description: Marilyn is bringing in a new class for your cooking pleasure. Join her as she makes Babka, a traditional Polish dessert that is a cross between a cake and a bread. Make Chrusick (deep-fried pastry sprinkled with icing sugar) also known as Angel Wings. Learn her techniques as she puts together a sweet yeast bread that will warm both heart and soul! Bring an apron and dress in layers as the kitchen tends to get a bit warm.

title: Media Comm Arts Internship
description: This course provides hands-on experience supporting media-related work in a variety of setting options, such as a radio or TV station, local community access facility, social media agency, advertising firm, public relations office, non-profit, or other organization that integrates media as part of their operations or outreach efforts. Students will complete 125-150 hours of work, either volunteer or paid, as determined by the host organization. Internship opportunities vary each semester, and students must collaborate with the instructor to secure placement at least ONE MONTH before the semester begins. This experience offers valuable industry insights, skill development, and professional networking in the dynamic field of media communication.

title: Adv Children's Theater Prod
description: Advanced children's theatrical production class.

title: C# Programming
description: An intermediate programming course using C# for Windows application development. Covers coding and debugging techniques for an object oriented environment. Different Microsoft graphical user interface projects will be used such as Windows Forms, Windows Presentation Foundation (WPF), and ASP Web Forms.

title: Spend Smart & Reduce Debt
description: Whether you have student loans to pay off, are overwhelmed by increased expenses, or are facing present or future unemployment, you can rein in your budget. Join Connie Costner from MSU Extension Wayne County to gain powerful but simple tools and techniques that have helped many people have money left over at the end of each month. Profit from Connie's money-stretching tips gained from financial research about credit cards, mortgages, loan reduction, spending limits and much more. Many free handouts included. Open to anyone. Invest in this class today to ensure a more financially secure future.

title: Interfaith Tour
description: To explore the beauty and architecture at three houses of faith and learn about Christian, Jewish and Muslim worship styles, register for an exciting Sunday afternoon motorcoach tour originating from the Center of Lifelong Learning. The first stop is Gesu Catholic Church in Detroit built in 1937, which features modified Spanish Mission architecture with Moorish interior decor. Then travel to Southfield to tour the striking synagogue of Congregation Shaarey Zedek, the first Conservative Jewish congregation in the Detroit area. Your final visit is the Islamic Center of America in Dearborn, the largest mosque in North America; it features golden domes, tall minarets and exquisite chandeliers. After touring the mosque, enjoy a Middle Eastern pastry and beverage before departing for home. You must register by February 16, 2007.

title: Recording Arts Capstone
description: As the capstone for the Recording Arts Certificate program, course provides special study in audio recording and is intended for students completing the Certificate program. Students choose the topic for study and design an instructional plan under the guidance and approval of instructor. Must complete a major project that demonstrates skills and knowledge learned in the program, and project is presented at the end of the semester.

title: Diabetic Desserts
description: Who says you can't enjoy tasty desserts just because you're diabetic? Not Sue Keskeny, author of the "Cooking with Sue" column in a local newspaper. In fact, she cooks them all the time for her own family. In this class, she'll demonstrate four diabetic-safe desserts: blueberry lemon bunt cake, sugar-free strudel, chocolate parfaits and apple trifle. Bring an apron and come prepared to sample the goodies.

title: Contemporary Social Problems
description: Focuses on understanding current social problems in U.S. society, such as economic, racial, and gender inequality, from a sociological perspective. Because the course relates to social issues, the exact set of problems covered can change from semester-to-semester. Other issues covered in recent semesters include climate change, crime, and social movements intended to solve social problems. The primary goals of this course are increasing student awareness of social problems and their causes, as well as possible solutions.

title: Elec Fuel & Emissions Control
description: Introduces electronic computer-controlled fuel delivery and metering systems. Discusses electronic emission controls and environmental impact of tailpipe emissions. Comparison of mechanical historical fuel and emissions control systems to modern electronic systems. Discussion of onboard diagnostic system monitors. Provides exposure to electronic diagnostic equipment. Also, discusses connected vehicle monitoring and autonomous over-the-air controller updates.

title: Cake Decorating-Beginners
description: Have you always wanted to learn this beautiful culinary art? In this fun class you'll create beautiful icing shell and star borders, flowers, leaves, lacework and more! You'll also discover "secret" methods to properly ice, fill and assemble tiered cakes. Popular products and tools cake decorators use will be discussed as well. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx $12-15).

title: Cybersecurity Basics
description: This course presents the concepts of cybersecurity for individuals or organizations seeking guidance on establishing a comprehensive vehicle cybersecurity effort. It offers best practices and implementation guidance to help individuals or organizations design, build, and institute an effective, organization-wide vehicle cybersecurity function. Topics include terminology, basic principles and paradigms, attacks, threats and vulnerabilities, security of operations, confidentiality, PII, management of risks, security analytics, ISO/SAE 21434, and a short intro to reverse engineering.

title: Financial Crossroads: What Now
description: Are you retiring? Receiving a lump sum distribution? Have you had a recent job change? Were you laid off or fear you may be? Get some valuable information that can help you make sound financial decisions. Understand tax consequences, explore the pros and cons of IRAs, and evaluate your financial goals. Please bring pen and paper to class.

title: Pre-Apprenticeship Pharm Tech
description: Provides non-credit training as a pre-apprenticeship entry-level pharmacy technician. Pharmacy technicians are skilled health specialists who, under the direction and supervision of a pharmacist, assist in the varied activities of a pharmacy department. The students are provided with both classroom and laboratory skills practice in the area of pharmacy technician. Registration is restricted to those students who are participants in the Going Pro program.

title: How Talk With Terminally Ill
description: In this compassionate and insightful class with Karen Ludwig, a family support social worker from Arbor Hospice and Home Care, you will gain skills to help a terminally ill friend, relative or client deal with his or her impending death. Gain clues about mysterious words and actions of dying persons. Find out about the physical signs that death is near, and learn how to support and comfort dying persons and their families. Get helpful strategies to cope with your own emotions as well.

title: Visual Communications
description: This course introduces students to a project based, hands on approach to visual communication in the fields of architecture and design. Traditional and digital methods and techniques will be used to explore basic principles of design, color theory, image production, composition, 3D modeling, lighting, and scene creation. Students will develop and present design projects using both digital media, and print media.

title: Quality Cont Gaging & Inspecti
description: An entry-level course covering the following manufacturing inspection methods: layout, surface plate techniques, tool and instrument reading and uses, and floor and receiving inspection. Also discusses surface finish measurement, introduction to SPC techniques, and GDT. Laboratory activities.

title: Yoga
description: This yoga class is an invigorating mix between vinyasa (or flow) and power yoga. The focus is on postures and breathing as you move smoothly from one pose to another. Improve your health, fitness level and mental acuity. Bring a yoga mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Join the Frame
description: This class will teach you the techniques necessary to matt, mount, and frame those pictures and mementoes that you have been meaning to get into frames--for display and protection. In the first session the teacher will demonstrate the whole process and discuss the requirements of framing a variety of objects. The second night students will work on their own projects. Students will provide their own matt board, frames, glass, and supplies (a supply list will be given out in the first session.) The hardest part of the process is cutting the matts; thus, that will be the focus of the second evening.

title: Prop Appraisal Prin
description: Overview the appraisal profession and the business and process of appraising and meet one of the SAB requirements for Level III Certification. You will learn how to evaluate, how to collect and analyze data relevant to different types of property, and how to value property using the three basic approaches to value. Approved by the Board of Real Estate Appraisers for 30 hours of prelicensure or continuing education. Textbook included.

title: Child Knitting & Crochet
description: This is an introduction to knitting and crochet for children. To take knitting, the child would need to be 8 years of age. To take crochet, the child would need to be 11 years of age. Bring a tape measure, scissors, needles and a crochet hook.

title: Chinese Medicine in Your Life
description: Gain additional insight into traditional Chinese medicine as you explore the pathways and relationships of the organ meridian system. Practice simple effective methods to address everyday ailments using the subtle energetics of acupressure therapeutics. Wear loose comfortable clothing.

title: Hand & Foot Reflexology
description: If you have always wondered about what Reflexology is and how it benefits the body, this is the class that will answer your questions! Class includes 2 color-coded charts that will help you identify organs, muscular & skeletal zones that correlate with specific areas on your hands & feet. Sign up with a partner and learn the techniques that will help you and those you love!

title: Introduction to WHFR Radio
description: This course offers an introduction to WHFR-FM, the college's non-commercial, educational broadcast and internet radio station. Students will learn essential station rules, operational procedures, and equipment usage, while developing foundational skills in broadcast writing and on-air announcing, including music, public service, promotional, and/or sports/news. Open to all students, this course is required for those interested in joining the WHFR staff and contributing to station operations and programming. Upon successful completion, students will be eligible to volunteer as WHFR staff members.

title: Professional Transitions
description: Continues to examine the role of the professional nurse within today's health care environment. Presents how to use previously developed critical thinking skills, in a systematic, problem solving way, as a framework for providing safe and effective care within this environment. Focuses on how to effectively and efficiently transition into the role of entry-level Associate Degree Registered Nurse and the development of professional identity.

title: Acting I
description: Introduces basic acting techniques, theories of acting, and rehearsal procedures. Emphasizes modern and contemporary comedies, and dramas. Students perform scenes from contemporary plays.

title: Industrial Safety Awareness
description: Presents a comprehensive approach to safety, designed to give the skilled tradesperson the knowledge of safety fundamentals and practices, from the causes of accidents to the study of safety hazards and rules associated with equipment and tools utilized in modern industrial facilities. Successful completers will be issued the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) 30-Hour card for General Industry.

title: Not All Carb Are Created Equal
description: Glycemic Index? Syndrome X/Hyperinsulinemia/Insulin Resistance? Learn aobut the good, the bad, and the ugly of carbs with dietitian Lourdes Reyes Lore. You may be surprised to learn that you may have been missing out on some of the best natural choices to help prevent cancer, diabetes, heart problems and common gastric ailments.

title: Party Planning Made Simple
description: Whether you're planning a graduation party, a shower, or another gathering, make it a day to remember. Executive Chef Marilyn Suter will teach you how to create a stunning table or buffet display, fold napkins into unique shapes and plan the appropriate amount of food for your guests. Bring a variety of tablecloths, cloth napkins, assorted display items (bottles, jars, candles, baskets, etc.) and your entertaining ideas, recipes and questions. Marilyn will show you how to use common items to decorate for your next event. Class limited to 14.

title: EIF Prep Course
description: Designed to prepare students for the CEWD Energy Industry Fundamentals exam. The core competencies of the exam are presented. They include: reading comprehension, mathematics, mechanical and spatial reasoning, and test-taking strategies.

title: Boating Skills & Seamanship
description: This US Coast Guard course is designed to provide valuable boating skills for the power boater, experienced and novice alike. It covers nautical language, boating laws, safety equipment, rules of the road, aids to navigation (waterway signs), piloting and navigation, marlinspike (knots and line handling), marine engines, marine weather, marine radio and much more. Successful completion may qualify you for a discount on your marine insurance.

title: Tour Capitals of Scandinavia
description: Spend a winter's evening visiting the beautiful cities of Scandinavia. Sit back, relax and sample a Scandinavian dessert while Don Samull takes you on a slide show tour of Copenhagen Helsinki, Stockholm and Oslo. The following Sunday you will be a guest at a wonderful Swedish smorgasbord in Farmington.

title: Polish Special
description: This is one of our series of special Saturday workshops in Advanced Home Cooking. These classes are designed for students who know how to cook but wish to learn new techniques and to make specialty dishes. In this class students will create a range of Polish specialties from stuffed cabbage to pierogi (small dumplings with a variety of fillings) to city chicken. Students will cook their own lunch and divide up the day's creations. Your instructor, Marilyn Szymanski, is the Executive Chef at Eastern Michigan University. Bring an apron. This is a hands-on class.

title: The American Civil War
description: Examines the American Civil War, including the causes, personalities, soldiers, social groups, significant battles, and legacy of America's defining conflict from 1820-1877.

title: Music Appreciation
description: This basic appreciation course includes a study of the elements of music and emphasizes listening skills, music vocabulary, and an open-mindedness toward all music. Fulfills humanities requirements.

title: Fun & Easy Microsoft Office 07
description: This class is an easy way for beginners to learn to use the Microsoft Office 2007 Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Access). Learn the basics of word processing with MS Word, spreadsheet basics with MS Excel, and presentation techniques with MS Powerpoint. A demonstration of database concepts will also be shown using MS Access. The instructor has used these applications for 20+ years in the business world. Real world examples at a pace beginners can manage is the goal. Basic PC skills are a prerequisite. Text is included. A USB flash memory stick is recommended.

title: Math for Computer Professional
description: Covers basic mathematics for the computer and information technology field. Focuses on how computers process and store numbers and applications of mathematics in practical computer programs involving arithmetic, algebra, statistics, geometry, and physics. Course will utilize a modern programming language in a lab environment.

title: Micro Word: Tables to Org Wrk
description: Discover how simple it is to make a table, a grid of rows and columns in Microsoft Word. By typing information in the various cells of the grid you can quickly organize your information into a professional looking document. Did you know you can sort a table or perform calculations? It's a marvelous way to keep track of mailing lists and other information.

title: Ceramics III
description: An intermediate level course stressing refinement of ceramic construction techniques and design, primarily through utilitarian objects. This class also offers the opportunity for individual exploration of ceramic glaze and surface possibilities. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Introduction to CNC
description: An entry-level course covering the basic concepts of computer numerical control (CNC). Also explores set-up, operation, and programming of a CNC mill and CNC lathe. Laboratory activities.

title: Beginning Writer's Workshop
description: Get an in-depth writing experience filled with challenging hands-on exercises, expert advice, and plenty of direct support and encouragement.

title: Simply Chakras & Auras- Part 1
description: Explore your inner energy! Learn to tap into your inner self with ease. With guided meditation you will "see" how to discern your own chakra system and aura which will help you to be more insightful and have a more fulfilling life. You will also learn how to see other people's auras. Come join the fun and excitement of learning about the inner you.

title: Introduction to Nutrition
description: Introduces how to evaluate nutrition information, plan nutritious diets, digestion issues, macro and micro nutrients, energy balance and weight control, food safety, and proper nutrition for a lifetime. Also examines how to assess nutritive content, spread of calories, and the calorie balance of various diets.

title: Goodbye to Pms, Mood Swings
description: If you are a woman coping with depression, hot flashes, migraines, thinning hair, memory loss, fibroids, fatigue, painful periods, or unexplained weight gain, hurry to this seminar. Dr. Mary Born will teach you how excess estrogen, deminished progesterone, environmental toxins, and nutritional deficiencies may be causing these symptoms. Learn proven strategies to restore balance to your body using healthy foods, herbal supplements, good fats, vitamins, minerals and non-prescription hormone creams. $5 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Latin Hip Hop Groove
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? With a splash of salsa, merengue, mambo and cha-cha, followed by straight up, off the hook hip-hop moves, it's the best of both! Under the guidance of Dena Raptis-Evans, former owner of a Boston fitness center, you'll work your body and soul to the hottest music this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Wines of Michigan
description: Come savor the taste of a number of Michigan wines, including many available in limited quantities, and learn how and why they have received national recognition. David Creighton, your instructor, has spent more than 25 years studying, tasting, collecting and judging wines. You will find his enthusiasm infectious as he introduces you to the basics of wine production and compares and contrasts Michigan wines with those of other regions in terms of grape varieties, soil and climate.
