Cheese and bread will be served with the wine. What a pleasant way to end the week! (Note: This course focuses specifically on recent Michigan wine vintages, not on wine tasting in general.)

title: College Reconnect
description: This course is specifically designed to help Adult students transition to college and achieve academic and career success. As an Adult Learner you will learn how to navigate coming back to a college environment, familiarize yourself with online education, manage time, maximize your learning, discover ways to stay motivated, think critically, take tests and develop a career/academic plan. Being successful in these areas is the goal of this course.

title: Mathematics for Calculus
description: Topics include algebraic, graphical and numerical representations of functions, including transformations, composition and inverses of functions. Focuses on the study of polynomial, rational, root, exponential and logarithmic functions, and trigonometric and inverse trigonometric functions of real numbers. Also includes equation solving and trigonometric identities. Techniques of problem solving and applications are included throughout the course requiring the frequent usage of graphing utilities. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments and access to an online homework system.

title: Maj Wine Grape Var
description: Examines varietal wines from many broad geographical areas, including vintage and specialty wines. Covers how to identify alcohol, acid, sugar, and tannin in wines. Also discusses the challenges of wine service in today's hospitality industry.

title: Ocular Measurements
description: Discusses basic ophthalmic equipment and measurement protocol, instrument calibration, maintenance, and infection control. Students apply concepts to specific clinical situations related to vision care.

title: Culinary Skills & Nutritional
description: Introduces basic concepts in food preparation and techniques in food service operation. Explores proper use of kitchen procedures with hands-on food production methods, and discusses how to utilize the principles, standards, and practices involved in professional quantity food production. Instructor rotates students in the following production areas: pantry, soups, stocks, sauces, vegetables, and the entree department.

title: Intro Med Asst Clinical Proced
description: An introductory level clinical course for students in the medical assistant program. The student will wear program clinical attire, (HFC scrubs, lab coat), and will be introduced to OSHA, CDC, emergency preparedness, along with universal and standard precautions. The student will be able to apply safety knowledge learned in the course while mastering content in the class such as a complete physical exam, specialty exams, patient transfer and vital signs.

title: Explor Arch & Construction
description: Introduces basic methods and materials used in the design and construction Industry. Students will learn basic manual and digital drawing techniques used to read and create basic design and construction documents, and basic construction skills and material applications.

title: Speed Spanish
description: Imagine yourself reading and writing Spanish. Learn six easy recipes for putting Spanish words together to form sentences. In no time, you'll be able to converse in Spanish. Ideal for teachers, travelers, and health care workers. You may continue learning the language with Speed Spanish II and III after you complete this class.

title: Chorus 3
description: Third of four semesters of Concert Choir including preparation and performance of a broad spectrum of choral music from classical to popular, show tunes, and jazz. At least two performances are scheduled each semester, sometimes accompanied by band or orchestra. These courses are open to college students and adult members of the community.

title: Second-Year French III
description: Follows FRE-132 and is the first of two intermediate-level French courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in French, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express essential ideas in both spoken and written French. Presents francophone culture.

title: Pen & Ink Watercolor Washes
description: Increase your basic drawing skills and expand your knowledge of using pen and ink as a drawing medium. Join artist Saundra Weed for this six-week class and discover how to create artwork suitable for framing, journaling, illustrating books or making cards. $5 due to instructor for first night's materials. Supply list will be provided in class. Appropriate for all levels.

title: Logistics & Distribution Strat
description: Focuses on the complexities of logistics and transportation in the field of Supply Chain Management (SCM). Covers the critical link of transportation in SCM, various modes of transportation (air, water, rail, pipeline, road), global transportation planning, execution, and management. Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able demonstrate competency in the following logistical areas: importance of logistics in SCM, information management systems, demand and order management, customer service, inventory control, facility location, warehouse management, packing and materials handling, transportation modes, transportation management, and international logics.

title: Holiday Centerpieces
description: Fresh blossoms can make any celebration more festive. Enliven your graduation parties, First Communion celebrations, showers, Mother's Day gathering, or other spring events with your own simple, but dramatic, floral arrangements. Internationally known floral designer and educator, Robbin Yelverton, AIFD, PFCI, will teach you basic floral-handling techniques and design elements as you create an arrangement to take home each week. Bring a notepaid, pencil, knife, scissors and wire cutters to each session. Other supplies included. No experience necessary.

title: Prin Organ & Bio Chem
description: This is the second course in a traditional two-semester General, Organic, and Biochemistry (GOB) sequence. Emphasizes nomenclature and functional group reactivity. Addresses the structure and function of lipids, carbohydrates, proteins, and nucleic acids at an introductory level. There are three hours of lecture and three hours of laboratory each week.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 2
description: Teaches beginning-level college-preparatory reading and listening skills to non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for NCELI-103R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Ceramics (ages 8 -12)
description: Discover how much fun you can have playing in mud! Using handbuilding techniques, explore the versatility of clay as you create a variety of fun and interesting projects. Supplies are included. Come dressed to mess!

title: Compound Angles/Adv Projection
description: Focuses on the mastery of solid trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Includes the solution of solid trigonometric problems using graphic and analytical solutions and problem solving techniques. Requires modeling clay.

title: AC/DC Circuit Analysis
description: Focuses on D.C. single- and multi-source circuitry with the application of loop, node, and Thevenin's theorem. Also covers A.C. reactive circuits using both phasors and complex numbers for determining reactance, impedance, and power factor. Laboratory activities.

title: Monthly Unlimited $5 Fitness
description: Fitness Club Members - sign up for a calendar month of unlimited $5 Fitness Classes. Includes any or all of the following offerings: CEHFS-1166 Cardio Kick; CEHFS-1176 Cardio Pump; CEHFS-1177 Pilates; CEHFS-1181 Zumba Fit; CEHFS-1186 Zumba Gold; CEHFS-1198 Low Impact Aerobics; CEHFS-1199 Step Aerobics. See course schedule for specific course descriptions and offering details.

title: Power Market for Photographers
description: Are you a wedding, portrait or senior photographer or a photography studio manager who would like to increase your cash flow? Are you having too many slow days and not enough cash? Well then, join photographer Andreas as he shares his simple 10-5-1 steps of completing the sale and increasing your cash flow. This three session class will focus on re-activating old clients, using the telephone to sell, and other useful topics.

title: CCNA Routing & Switching
description: This course is an intensive coverage of the skills needed to install, operate, verify a basic IPv4 & IPv6 network. Students will focus on the fundamentals and how to build simple LAN's, establish internet connectivity, troubleshoot VLAN issues, characteristics, theory based platform & virtual labs to simulate real world experience. Upon completion of this course, students will be eligible to sit for the CCNA certification exam, specifically the CCNA Routing & Switching, and earn their industry certification. The course curriculum stisfies the requirements needed to obtain employment and/or career advancement. The college cannot guarantee that a student will become certified. Certification costs are included in the cost of training. Eligibility requirements are subject to change by the certification testing body without prior knowledge given to the College.

title: Advanced Graphic Interpretatio
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of oblique surfaced objects, sectioning, processes used in the manufacturing environment, and geometric and traditional tolerancing. Requires knowledge of traditional and computer-based drafting techniques.

title: Taste of Italy: the Series
description: Prepare for a treat in this introductory cooking series that will not only teach you the basics of preparing authentic Italian regional cuisine but also the almost mythic approach to dining that makes Italy famous. With an emphasis on fresh seasonal foods, each week Robert Cascardo and Cristina Cotronei will teach you how to prepare a three-course meal, pair it with an appropriate wine, and eat it the way they do in Italy. Cristina was born near Milan and Robert has lived in various Italian cities, including Florence, Rome and Turin. Naturally, they both love to cook. Join them for three fun, educational evenings of preparing, eating, and of course cleaning-up after memorable meals.

title: Intro College Writing (ALP)
description: ENG-131A is the first college-level composition course in a two-semester sequence. Students will be introduced to writing, reading, critical thinking, and using information at the college level. In particular, the course will help students develop writing that reveals rhetorical and academic flexibility.

title: Meditation
description: Begin exploring ways to reduce stress, improve mental clarity, and create deep states of relaxation. Learn simple, effective breathing, visualization, and relaxation techniques that can help you develop a new sense of peace and tranquility. You may even lower your blood pressure!

title: Introduction to Birding
description: Explore the basics of bird identification, observation, equipment selection and use, and recording bird sightings. In a classroom setting, prepare for upcoming field trips as you discuss some birding basics and review the use of the Field Guide. The Sibley Guide to Birds is a required text, and will be available for purchase at the beginning of the class.

title: Advanced CNC Operations
description: Emphasizes the total aspects of computer numerical control (CNC) as applied in manufacturing today. Covers estimating for CNC manufacturing, DNC, tool selection, cutting-tool material selection, and small-batch production.

title: Reiki Level 3 Certification
description: Deepen your understanding of Reiki by learning more advanced techniques, the last 2 symbols, and how to chart and assess. Homework is required. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. Prerequisite: Reiki Level 2 Certification.
prerequisite_text: Reiki Level 2 Certification.

title: Medicolegal Prin for Health
description: Provides a core foundation of the basic legal issues specific to the health care practitioner. Focuses upon laws related to the delivery of care as well as health care employment issues.

title: Wyandotte Polish Churches
description: The unique downriver community of Wyandotte has long-time Polish roots. Join your instructor, Don Samull, on a guided, self-driving and walking tour of three of its charming Polish churches, including the jewel, Our Lady of Mt. Carmel. If there is time, you will also visit the small chapel of the Polish Mariavites. The tour includes a Polish lunch at the city's Polish restaurant. Maps showing where to meet will be sent to registered students prior to the tour.

title: Public Policy: Topics & Analys
description: A special topics course that allows students to explore issues of public policy. Focuses on the basics of public policy including agenda setting, policy formulation, policy implementation, and policy evaluation. Themes may vary. Themes may be specific such as healthcare policy or they might be more general such as state and local economic policy. Guest speakers.

title: Downsize, Declutter
description: One of the reasons home are not selling in today's market is that they are too cluttered. If you have years of accumulated belongings, a garage so full that you can't park your car in it, this class is for you. Theresa Brune, a Professional Organizer, will teach you pain-free techniques for sorting and sifting through items, reclaiming and maximizing storage space, parting with objects you no longer want, and making the house clutter free and attractive to a potential buyer.

title: Intro to Your New (pc) Laptop
description: Learn the components, attachments and accessories of your new laptop computer. Understand how to connect with Wi-Fi, networks and other Internet connections. Class includes: setting up, power modes, hardware, components, security, games, digital photos, music CD / DVD movies, and more. Prerequisites: each student must bring his or her laptop (PC) computer to class.
prerequisite_text: each student must bring his or her laptop (PC) computer to class.

title: Vitamins, Minerals & Health
description: Did you know magnesium helps you sleep better, and fish oil reduces depression? Learn why as biochemist Sandy Baumann teaches you the importance of and food sources for common vitamins and minerals, ways to choose the best supplements, and the role of nutritional deficiencies in insomnia, arthritis, irritability, anxiety, bone loss, diabetes, and depression. You will also learn to read food labels to increase your vitamin and mineral intake. Bring your own supplements to evaluate, using reference guides and body responses. $5 materials fee payable to insructor on first night.

title: Create Silver & Copper Pendant
description: Create a hand-stamped sterling silver pendant with accompanying drop. In this class you will learn how to stamp words and designs into sterling and copper. Complete your very own one of a kind designed sterling pendant that you will take home from class. Sample photos above indicate only a few possibilities. The options are unlimited based on your imagination. Great gift idea for someone special.

title: Exercise Facility, Equip&Safe
description: Training for how to build exercise facilities, purchase exercise equipment, and prevent and treat injuries associated with exercise.

title: Real Estate Law
description: Learn about the relevant issues in today's real estate market. In this 30-hour course, you will cover contract law, real estate property law, tenant/landlord issues, civil rights and much more. This course may be applied to the hours required for a real estate broker's license. Textbook included.

title: Advanced Tap
description: Ready to learn more advanced routines? Put your knowledge and your muscles to work in this advanced class. Bring tap shoes. Prerequisite: Permission of instructor.
prerequisite_text: Permission of instructor.

title: Birding in Michigan
description: Bird watching or birding as it is often called is one of the nation's fastest growing pastimes. Join Allen Chartier, co-author of Birds of Detroit and an active birder, for an introduction to the world of birding. The meet at selected area sites (all within 50 miles of our campus) to practice identifying and observing a cross section of late-winter Michigan birds. Directions to sites will be provided the first day of class. Textbook ($35) available in class. Binoculars desirable, if you have them.

title: Make Your Own Pet Treats
description: Has the recent pet food scare made you more cautious about what you feed your pets? In class you will create your own cat and dog friendly treats and know exactly what goes into them. These recipes are all veterinarian approved and have short preparation times. The instructor, Acyneith Swain-Grant will provide you with a list of foods that might be harmful for pets. She also provides references on pet nuitrition. Be a hero for your pet when you arrive home with freshly made treats.

title: Intro to Industrial Technology
description: Introduces students to the fundamentals of being a successful college student on the path to a profession in industrial technology. Subjects are divided into three categories: Student Self Awareness, Manufacturing Work and Culture, and Test Taking (technical), and include topics such as self-directed learning, critical thinking and problem solving, industrial safety policies and procedures, job outlook, and elements of standardized testing including graphic arithmetic, assemblies, mechanical comprehension, and troubleshooting. The class includes guest speakers from manufacturing and the service industry. The course utilizes digital learning materials, hands-on labs, and a student self assessment of their learning and study skills.

title: Metaphysics & Psychic Phen II
description: This workshop will explore "The sleeping prophet Edgar Cayce", "The Secret", the "Akashic Records", auras, intuition, spirits, and much more. Bring your imagination and an open mind. You'll be glad you did!

title: Basic Electricity
description: Covers the fundamentals of electricity as applied to the electrical field. Topics include the electron theory, Ohm's Law, circuits, magnetism, inductance, capacitance, and alternating current circuits. Utilizes voltmeters, ammeters, power supplies, signal generators, and oscilloscopes to construct circuits during lab activities. Also utilizes computer simulations. Introduction to writing reports. Labs involve breadboards and the above-mentioned equipment and components. Extensive laboratory activities.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Electrical diagnosis includes computer-controlled charging systems, battery, and starting systems, including one-touch and remote-start systems. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile.

title: Asian Art: India & SE Asia
description: Offers a global view on Asian art, culture, and history by exploring the major monuments, traditions, and civilizations of India, China, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. Covers important Asian artists and historical figures, artistic terms, monuments, sites, and time periods. Discusses art and architecture in terms of historical, social, and religious context. Analyzes and distinguishes various styles of art, and examines how and why certain styles are characteristic of specific times and places.

title: Intro to ASP.NET
description: Learn how to create exciting, interactive and professional web sites with ASP.NET. In this hands-on course you'll begin by adding the power of interactive controls to your web pages, and you'll discover how you can use style sheets to give every web page you create the same clean and consistent look and feel. You'll find out how to connect to a database and put the information it contains on public display. Then you'll develop a true community-based web site that allows users to register, log in, create and update profiles, and post messages to a forum. You'll also explore the issue of web security and learn some simple things you can do to secure your site against some common attacks.

title: Fancy Cookie Art Workshop
description: Instructor will collect $5.00 materials fee first day of class.

title: Holiday Crafts for Kids
description: Got a young budding artist at home? Here's an opportunity to encourage their talent this holiday season. Bring them to this craft class designed especially for young people. Experienced artists will work with them to make two gifts - a whimsical picture frame and a jewelry box - suitable for parents or other special people. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class. Match your child to an age appropriate section.

title: Monitor Your Child's Net Use
description: Do you know about MySpace, Facebook and Friendstar? Chances are they may be an essential part of your teen's social networking. Learn what MySpace and its contemporaries are all about, how to navigate them, create profiles, find and monitor your child's activities and more. Students must have a working knowledge of computers and the internet.

title: Greek for Travelers
description: Whether you are planning a trip to Greece or simply want to learn conversational Greek skills, this course will introduce you to the appropriate use of Greek in various social situations and will provide you cultural information about everyday life in modern Greece. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Thessaloniki, Greece, will teach you basic expressions that will help you when making accommodations, eating out, traveling, sightseeing, shopping and more. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $15).

title: Pre-Apprenticeship Pharm Tech
description: Provides non-credit training as a pre-apprenticeship entry-level pharmacy technician. Pharmacy technicians are skilled health specialists who, under the direction and supervision of a pharmacist, assist in the varied activities of a pharmacy department. The students are provided with both classroom and laboratory skills practice in the area of pharmacy technician. Registration is restricted to those students who are participants in the Going Pro program.

title: Color Theory Drawing (11-14)
description: The effect of color on a drawing can really make a difference on a finished work of art. Come have fun exploring the world of color through abstracts, landscapes and perspective drawings.

title: Ballroom Boot Camp
description: You've always wanted to try it! It looks like fun; it is fun! Come alone or with a partner to learn the fundamentals of leading and following for such contemporary ballroom dances as the swing, foxtrot, waltz, and other familiar dances. Learn the moves that will get you noticed at your next wedding, party, or in a nightclub.

title: Start Licensed Day Care Home
description: Would you love to be home with your children and yet earn money providing services to other parents? Then meet Doris Dickhudt, who ran a licensed home day care for over 20 years. Gain expert coaching and find out how to apply for a home day care license. Learn about daily routines, liability issues, marketing, insurance, business planning and educational resources. Discover the rewards of teaching and training the next generation. Bring your questions!

title: Honors Directed Study
description: In this sophomore-level directed study, students are challenged to apply the knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Students present summaries of their research before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty held at the end of each semester.

title: Cartooning-Humor to Your World
description: Have you always wanted to draw cartoons, either for fn or profit? Attend this class and enter the wonderful world of 'tooning! Using a variety of methods your instructor will teach you the basic terms and techniques of the cartoon trade. You'll learn how to sketch and compose using various styles and techniques, create your own cartoons, and market and sell your work in the industry.

title: Jigs, Fixtures, and Tools
description: Covers advanced techniques in design, layout, and detail of production tooling. Consideration is given to locating, clamping, and tolerancing a jig and fixture for the manufacture of an industrial part. Standard parts catalogs and library reference materials are used to provide guidance in solving design problems. Drawing assignments are done extensively on CAD.

title: Continuing Hatha Yoga
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment in this eight-week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing and relaxation techniques learned in Beginning Yoga, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Internal Combustion Engines
description: Introduces four-stroke engines by exploring principles of operation, compression ratio, piston displacement, operating tolerances, valve timing, horsepower and torque development along with adjustments, inspection, and troubleshooting procedures. Course topics help students prepare for ASE certification exams and State of Michigan licensure, but these exams/licensure are not included in this course. Laboratory activities.

title: Foreman Grill
description: Grab your lean, mean, fat-reducing grilling machine and learn how to prepare some of your favorite dishes in no time at all! In this hands-on class Marilyn Suter will show you how to prepare vegetables, fish, pork chops, sandwiches, and things that can be made ahead of time and eaten cold. Bring an apron, your appetite and a George Foreman grill (if you own one). Enroll early. Class limited to 14.

title: Dementia Caregiving
description: Are you caring for someone with dementia and find yourself at a loss as to how to keep them active and involved? Learn how one family has cared for their mother/wife at home for 16 years. In this workshop, Pricilla Davis, MAE, will share a variety of ways she has found successful in working with her own mother. Meet other care- givers and find new methods of creating a better environment for all.

title: Medical-Surgical Nursing I
description: Introduces the first semester nursing student to the role of the professional nurse as the provider of care for adult clients and a member of the profession. Explains how to utilize critical thinking skills in a systematic, problem solving process as a framework for providing care. Provides a framework for best practices, to think critically, assess factors that influence safe and effective care delivery, and integrate theory with care of clients. This course includes supervised clinical agency/laboratory practicums. Course comprised of four hours of theory and one day of clinical/lab per week.

title: Surgical Procedures II
description: Designed to further develop the student's knowledge of advanced surgical procedures. It features cardiovascular, thoracic, neurologic, oral, ophthalmic, plastic and reconstruction, pediatric, and geriatric surgical procedures.

title: Introduction to Birding
description: Explore the basics of bird identification, observation, equipment selection and use, and recording bird sightings. In a classroom setting, prepare for upcoming field trips as you discuss some birding basics and review the use of the Field Guide. The Sibley Guide to Birds is a required text, and will be available for purchase at the beginning of the class.

title: Second-Year German IV
description: Follows GER-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level German courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written German. Presents the cultures of German-speaking countries.

title: Informat Assurance Methodology
description: A capstone course providing hands-on experiences in the planning, implementation, and maintenance of Information Security systems. Covers security technologies and tools, footprinting, scanning and enumeration, web browser security, access control, data management and recovery, log security issues, network intrusion detection systems, virtual private networks, encryption and malware prevention, and defense. Analyzes and implements information security techniques through laboratory exercises.

title: Medical Transcription - Online
description: This online program helps you develop the knowledge and skills of quality medical transcriptionists, specifically focusing on keyboarding, language and grammar, and medical terminology. The program also emphasizes practical experience, and by graduation, you will have transcribed hundreds of authentic dictations, preparing for a quick and successful transition to the workplace. In addition, the program's online format provides you the flexibility to study around your schedule and at your own pace. You will have one year to complete this course; earlier completion is typical. (Course fee includes foot pedal, BenchMark KB 15-month subscription, AHDA 15-month membership, student support, placement assistance, and career guidance - all through our partner provider.)

title: Industrial Appl of Basic Math
description: Utilizes shop problems to help students relate math to various job situations. Topics include addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of whole and mixed numbers and common and decimal fractions; percents, averages, and estimates; graphs, tables, and statistical measure; powers and roots; linear, angular, and circular measure; surface area, volume, and cubic measure; ratios and proportion; and metrics and metric conversion.

title: Applied Music (one Credit) 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of weekly half-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester to confirm teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Color Your Garden With Annuals
description: Enhance your garden with the consistent, vivid, diverse colors only annuals can provide. Learn about hot new annuals and how to choose the right plants for your garden site, including some that will bloom right up to Thanksgiving. A Master Gardener will discuss preparing and amending your soil, proper watering methods, and precise fertilization techniques. She'll also tell you what typical problems you may encounter and how to fix them.

title: Intermediate Word Workshop
description: Explore Microsoft Word 2003 by exploring some of the more advanced features. Create and use templates to expedite letters, envelopes and labels. Design and use styles for easy formatting of documents. Discover the power of tables and columns. Learn to use headers & footers and the secrets of page numbering. Place pictures and graphics like the pros! Microsoft Office User Specialist (MOUS) approved text provided. Intro Word Workshop or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: MS windows 7 made so easy
description: Windows 7 is the newest operating system. Learn to use features found on your desktop and under the start menu. Change desktop backgrounds and use screensavers with ease. Other features include using free software found on your computer, working with computer options, undertanding the notification area, saving files, creating folders and more.

title: Theatre Capstone
description: Provides the required assessment of knowledge and skills for students in their last semester of course work for the Theatre Pathway of the Associates of Arts Degree in Liberal Arts.

title: Stress Mgmnt-Personal Approach
description: Offers a supportive group setting for students to examine their own personal sources of stress, how these stress factors are affecting them and what they can do to develop more effective coping strategies.

title: Introduction to Public Health
description: Provides a broad overview of the various aspects of public health in the United States. Presents applications, stakeholders, and methods of public health in a way that allows for the identification of strengths and limitations of the current model of care delivery. Also offers an overview of careers available in the area of public health.

title: Heat Pumps & Hydronic Systems
description: This course introduces technician-level students to the principles, operation, and application of heat pump and hydronic heating/cooling systems. Students learn how energy is transferred using water and refrigerants, how to safely install and service equipment, and how system components work together to provide efficient thermal comfort. Laboratory sessions reinforce classroom theory through hands-on exercises in system assembly, measurement, troubleshooting, and performance evaluation.

title: Savory Soulfully Baked Chicken
description: Grab your apron and join Chef Saniyyah to prepare savory baked chicken, smothered cabbage, red beans/rice and cornbread for some "down home" good eatin'.

title: Coordinated Edu Exp Auto Svc 4
description: Designed to offer practical work experience within the Automotive Technology and Service field through participation in a supervised coordinated education program. This course integrates work experience with classroom and Lab instruction.

title: Baby Sign Language
description: Help your baby or toddler, ages 6-24 months, communicate what he or she sees, needs or feels, using nonverbal hand signs. Join speech and language pathologist Ruth Haberkorn-Halm to learn Baby Signs, a program featured on national TV, that helps children "talk" before they can speak. Discover how singing reduces tantrums, speeds intellectual growth, improves parent/child bonding, and helps parents of children with special needs. Ideal for moms, dads, grandparents and persons who work with infants and toddlers. $41 materials fee due to instructor in class for required Baby Signs Kit including 2 DVD's and a binder of resources.

title: Start Emailing Family & Friend
description: Find out how easy it is to email family and friends. Learn to use email features to enhance your email. Instructor and students will also send, reply, and forward email right in the class! Understand how to attach a file or picture to your email. Get rid of annoying multiple names in the header of your email before forwarding your message.

title: Charm Bracelet
description: Design and make a stunning charm bracelet using a technique that will virtually guarantee that your charms will never fall off. You'll use a combination of semi-precious stones, crystal glass, sterling silver and other fun items to embellish the bracelet. Additional charms can be added later to enhance its beauty. Such a bracelet might sell for over $100 in a fine jewelry store. $35 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Basic Print Reading
description: Discusses print reading fundamentals needed for presenting concepts and techniques to various segments of today's industry. Includes the alphabet (use) of lines; projection of various shapes and surfaces; presentation of hidden details; and methods, units of measurement, and techniques used for locating details in a variety of drawings.

title: Low Impact Aerobics - Walk-In
description: Burn body fat, build leaner muscle and improve circulation in this energetic, upbeat aerobics class that is sure to get your body moving. A great workout for fitness enthusiasts of all levels, with less risk of injury to ankle, knee and hip joints. Wear comfortable clothing. Please bring water and a towel.

title: Perfect Party-Planning Skills
description: Whether you love throwing dinner parties or it's simply your turn to host a holiday meal, join professional event planner Julie Gheen and learn the secrets of throwing a successful party! You'll explore such basic elements as site selection, party layout and flow, decorations, guest list invitations, budget, and more, and leave being able to develop an action plan for a party of your own. Also benefits those who want to work in events planning or start a business in producing parties.

title: PaintTool Oper & Programming
description: The course covers the tasks and procedures that an operator, technician, engineer or programmer needs to set up and program a FANUC Robotics PaintTool Software Package.

title: Lightouses Great Lakes
description: Did you know the Great Lakes are home to 350 lighthouses--the highest concentration in North America? To see some of these landmarks, join Wayne Sapulski, author of Lighthouses of Lake Michigan, Past and Present, for an armchair slide show. Wyane has personally visited and photographed all 350 lighthouses. He will discuss the crucial role of lighthouses in maritime history and describe preservation efforts that are currently underway. You will learn where to find these historic treasures and how to get involved in preserving them. Price includes a locator map of Great Lakes lighthouses, so you can personally visit them.

title: Organize Garage & Basement
description: Overstuffed closets? Paperwork in piles? Missed appointments? Is stuff taking over your life? If so, this class is just for you! Instructor Debi Weinstein is a professional organizer who has appeared on local television and radio stations, written for leading magazines, and been featured in local newspapers. Come explore the reasons you have been disorganized in the past, and learn techniques for getting and staying organized. Proper organizational skills will change your life!

title: Gage, Cam Layout, and Fixture
description: Focuses on the mastery of gage design principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Discusses gage design theory, computer-aided manufacturing, cam layout procedures, and fixturing. Emphasizes the team approach to projects.

title: Golf: Adv Group Indoor Lessons
description: Want to follow up from the first group class? Or do you need to brush up your skills and get comfortable with the game? Tom Rinna will focus on correct setup, backswing (coiling) and downswing (uncoiling). He has lots of drills and exercises to help you perfect your short game. Bring your clubs.

title: Introductory Astronomy Lab
description: Emphasizes experimental work with the scientific concepts important in astronomy. Includes planetarium-based observation and/or computer simulations, as well as hands-on experimentation of astronomical phenomena. This course combined with ASTR-131 provides the student with a four-hour laboratory science credit. Two hours of laboratory per week.

title: 2 Day Mech Power Press
description: This course, offered by the Michigan Bureau of Safety&Regulatin, provides information about Power Press Safeguards; Control Reliability and Brake Monitors; Die Setting and Fastening; Injury Statistics; Most Frequently Cited Safety Rules - Part 24; Press Room Health Issues; Lockout Program and Procedures; and Job Safety Analysis & Operator Safety Training.

title: Applied Music (2 Credits) 2
description: Second of four semesters of weekly one-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Programmable Controllers
description: Begins with a thorough discussion of the Allen Bradley Contrologix/Compactlogix programmable controller system characteristics, followed by a detailed presentation on how to use the programmable controller to solve the automated control problem.

title: Creating Lasting Floral Design
description: We all love fresh flowers but they don't last long. Create a lovely expression of spring and summer in a floral basket centerpiece with beautifully crafted artificial flowers that will last season after season. Designer Barbara Simms will guide you through the process as you use your imagination and creativity to express the magic of spring and summer flowers in nature. You will need an apron or short sleeve shirt, glue gun, glue sticks and wire cutters. Flowers and basket are included in the price of the class. Deadline for registration will be Feb. 18 to allow time for ordering the flowers.

title: Rehab Neurological Cond 1
description: Reviews basic neuroanatomy and neurophysiology followed by instruction in the theory and rationale of the treatment approach for the patient with neurological involvement. Emphasizes the functional approach to patient treatment including use of Neurodevelopmental Treatment (NDT) and Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF) for adults with neurological conditions such as traumatic brain injury and cerebrovascular accidents.

title: Zumba Toning-Walk in Option
description: Complement your regular Zumba Fitness routine with this muscle toning and strength training option. Based on the same great music and dance moves of Zumba Fitness, you'll add additional fitness moves and use lightweight toning sticks to target major muscle groups. This class is open to everyone, no previous dance or Zumba experience necessary. If you would like to try it out or participate in single sessions for other reasons, you may walk in to any session listed above (CEHFS-1183) and enroll and pay at the door.

title: Ballet Fundamentals - Ages 4-7
description: Give your child an opportunity to develop grace, coordination and flexibility that will be a part of his or her life forever. In this class Stephanie Fullerton will teach the terminology, technique and precision associated with ballet.

title: Control & Instrumentation Fund
description: Explores soldering techniques and Electromagnetic/Static considerations when handling, replacing, and repairing of electronic components. Also discusses various troubleshooting methodologies.

title: Advanced Powerpoint
description: Are you familiar with PowerPoint's tools and now want to give your slides presentations and printed materials a more professional look? In this course, Althea Lockridge, an award-winning editor & writer, will share techniques for balancing space, text, graphics, photos, animation, and color. PowerPoint: Basics and Beyond or strong basic knowledge of PowerPoint recommended.

title: Design Wedding Glass Goblets
description: Design your own unique glass goblets for that demonstrate hand crafting Venetian styled stemware with traditional glass blowing techniques. You will learn about stemware styles, designs and colors before drafting your special occasion goblet design. You will leave with a complete design plan that can be commissioned to make at a later date if you so choose. Note: commissioning your goblets to be made is not required, and cost of commissioned pieces is not included; if commissioned, allow 5 weeks for completion. class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: How Pub Best Sell
description: Do you have an idea, concept, or story that would make an excellent book? If so, spend three hours with Mr. Jerrold R. Jenkins, a guru of independent publishing, exploring the business aspects of publishing a best-selling book. Hear the pros and cons of traditional and non-traditional publishing methods, learn about the various markets, and discuss the steps involved in publishing a book. In short, acquire the knowledge and networking skills needed for survival in the competitive $25 billion book industry.

title: Published Freelance Writer
description: If you have a magazine article, poem, novel or short story to sell, enroll in this comprehensive workshop with published writer LeeAnne Krusemark. Find out how to submit manuscripts correctly, locate the right publisher, and write irresistible query letters. Get your work to the appropriate editor, so you can see it in print! Learn one hundred paths to profit as a freelance writer. This course is only offered in the Fall term. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Polish Baking Class
description: Chef Marilyn Szymanski Suter shares more of her Polish heritage with this baking class. Paczki and Easter Babka are two of the dishes covered this day. Her authentic paczki truly convey the tradition of a decadent treat before the rigors of Lent. This is a hands-on class. Bring an apron and a lunch.

title: Voice Techniques 1
description: This course covers basic singing techniques including breath control, diction, posture, and tone quality; instruction takes place in a classroom setting. Techniques are applied to a variety of songs. This course is for singers of all ages and interests.

title: Acupressure Weight Management
description: It is now generally recognized by health professionals that diets don't work. Eating well and eating wisely does. This class is a training and support program presenting a unique and effective collection of resources from all over the world. These resources act to increase the connection between mind, body and emotions by which real, lasting changes in habits can be realized. Learn to use acupressure techniques, a Five Element nutritional approach, and Esther's Emotional Freedom Techniques to clear old traumas and mental/emotional programming and entanglements that are often impediments to healthy/eating habits.

title: Let's Act
description: Learn how to act by doing! At this acting summer camps for teens, we aim to connect that excitement and energy for the stage. Whether a student has theatre acting experience, performed in a few short films with friends, or simply is taken by the dream of becoming an actor, our acting summer camps offers many possibilities to deepen your training and ultimately, to help guide you towards your dreams! Campers will display their new skills in a performance for family and friends on the last day of camp.

title: Creating Collage
description: Take your artwork and creative expression into a world without limits in this one-of-a-kind workshop. Under the guidance of Saundra Weed you will learn to cut, paste, paint, layer paper and other objects to make original collage art. In addition, you'll be able to add stamping and writing to your collage. Personalize your projects by bringing personal photos and memorabilia, newspaper clippings, buttons, jewelry, beads, etc. $10 material fee due to instructor in class. Bring a bag lunch.

title: Cake Decorating-Beginners
description: Have you always wanted to learn this beautiful culinary art? In this fun class you'll create beautiful icing shell and star borders, flowers, leaves, lacework and more! You'll also discover "secret" methods to properly ice, fill and assemble tiered cakes. Popular products and tools cake decorators use will be discussed as well. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx $12-15).

title: Photoshop
description: Provides an introductory-level study of digital imaging with Photoshop. Including print, web, illustration, image enhancement/correction and animation techniques. This is the first digital course for the Digital and Graphic Arts programs.

title: Intro to Surgical Tech Lab
description: The course introduces the fundamentals of operating room techniques, emphasizing principles and practices of asepsis and sterilization. Physical and psychosocial aspects of the patient are considered throughout the various procedures in the operating process. Included are the roles and responsibilities of the surgical technologist and other surgical team members; identification and handling of common equipment, instruments, and supplies; students are introduced to and practice the sterile and unsterile rules in the operating room. This is a laboratory course.

title: Math for Elem Teachers I
description: For students who are involved in a curriculum for elementary teachers. Includes problem solving, set theory, number theory, numeration systems, whole numbers, and fractions. Addresses two aspects of teaching children mathematics - content and pedagogy - focused on Michigan Department of Education Standards for the Preparation of Teachers of Lower Elementary (Pre-K - 3) and Upper Elementary (Grades 3 - 6). Also addresses concept development, algorithms, children's mathematical work, and communication skills--both oral and written--important for teaching children.

title: Basic First Aid
description: Properly applied first aid can save lives, reduce recovery time, and lower the risk of lifelong disabilities. Attend this American Safety and Health Institute course and learn crucial information about caring for life-threatening emergencies; injuries to muscles, bones, joints, and skin and deeper tissues such as burns, scrapes and cuts; sudden illness such as seizure activity and shock; controlling bleeding; and handling heat and cold emergencies. Meets the requirements of group home and child care workers and is appropriate for any lay rescuer. Results in a three-year certificate. Bring a pillow or blanket and a pen or pencil.

title: Fitness Club
description: Join our Fitness Club and be eligible to attend unlimited $5 Fitness classes each month. Sign up once each semester.

title: Mircrosoft Project - I
description: This course focuses upon the creation of a simple project schedule and exploring the basic tools available in Microsoft Progject.

title: Fantasy Character Design
description: Develop the characters of your wildest imagination. Students will be introduced to anatomy basics and create a fantasy character based on facial expression, character development, and lighting. The class will discuss and add visual cues that enhance the narrative of the character. Campers will be encouraged to draw inspiration from contemporary characters in movies, video games and/or animee, as well as incorporate science fiction or fantasy elements within their work. A gallery exhibition will show of the work of class participants at the end of the final class session.

title: Elec Power Distribution & Tran
description: Covers the fundamentals of electrical power distribution, including substation and transmission line protection as well as construction. Also examines transmission lines, operation of distribution transformers, instrument transformers, circuit breakers, reclosers, sectionalizers, fuses, relays, and disconnects.

title: Adv. Clinical Therapeutic Prac
description: Directed clinical experience that further develops the clinical skills and techniques used to manage patients requiring mechanical ventilator support in the hospital, sub-acute and home care settings. Twenty-four hours per week.

title: Basic Shape Size Interpretatio
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of normal surfaced, inclined (single-angle) surfaced, and cylindrical objects. Presents traditional and computer-based drafting techniques. Covers projection of three-dimensional objects into two-dimensional representations, the reverse process, and sketching and modeling of objects.

title: WHFR Staff Training
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-189 to register for this course. Provides an orientation to the non-commercial, educational radio station licensed to the college. Covers essential station rules and procedures, operation of equipment, and basic performance skills. Course is open to all students but is required of those who wish to join the staff and assist in the operation of WHFR.

title: Personal Property Valuation
description: Obtain a Personal Property Examiner's Card from the State Tax Commission by successfully completing this tested course. Approved by Michigan SAB for level III and Level IV certification credit. Textbook included.

title: Exploration of MTT
description: Introduces safe operation of basic machine tools, specifically the horizontal band saw and engine lathe. Precision measurement and lay out techniques are also covered giving students an exploratory experience in early stage manufacturing processes.

title: Basic Shape & Size Interpret
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of normal surfaced, inclined (single-angle) surfaced, and cylindrical objects. Presents traditional and computer-based drafting techniques. Covers projection of three-dimensional objects into two-dimensional representations, the reverse process, and sketching and modeling of objects.

title: Typical Everyday German Meal
description: If you love German cuisine, you are going to love this class. If you are not sure and just want to try it, you are also in the right place. Join Ursula Markovits, who was born and raised in Germany, for a bit of the country's food and culture. Learn how to whip up a traditional German dinner of rouladen (stuffed beef rolls), noodles, and cherry bread pudding. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: A Taste of Italy: Alla Carta
description: One evening a month, Italian couple Robert Cascardo and Cristina Cotronei take you on a journey to the heart of Italy as they prepare, with your help, an incredible, regionally authentic, Italian four-course meal. Experience the almost mythic approach to dining that makes Italy famous - the Meal as an Occasion. The menu is planned only one week in advance to take advantage of fresh seasonal foods, and is paired with appropriate wine. Traditionally, every town, every rural area in Italy has its own unique culinary twists and variations, so the menu possibilities are seeemingly endless.

title: Musical Production 2
description: This course combines the study of musical skills and techniques involved in a staged musical production with performance activity in a major production and/or the classroom.

title: Intro to Your New (pc) Laptop
description: Learn the components, attachments and accessories of your new laptop computer. Understand how to connect with Wi-Fi, networks and other Internet connections. Class includes: setting up, power modes, hardware, components, security, games, digital photos, music CD / DVD movies, and more. Prerequisites: each student must bring his or her laptop (PC) computer to class.
prerequisite_text: each student must bring his or her laptop (PC) computer to class.

title: Easy Movement With Feldenkrais
description: Discover the way that small, gentle movements can help you to think about how you move and force your brain to focus on these movements through the Feldenkrais method. Marj Levine, who taught our Effortless Gardening class featured in a recent article in the Detroit Free Press, is a certified Feldenkrais instructor who can use simple movements (or sometimes just imagining the movements) to ease tension, boost coordination and relieve muscles. "With Feldenkrais, we don't ever try and fix anything," she says. "We attempt to improve the whole system." The Feldenkrais Method is about learning, not about doing exercises. Bring a floor mat and an open mind.

title: Advanced Study in Ceramics
description: An individualized advanced-level course study of a topic or project under the direction of a faculty member who, together with the student, designs the format of the study or project. Must have completed the appropriate sequence of courses offered by the department. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.

title: Art History Survey II
description: A survey of the development of visual arts from the early Renaissance to the present time. Offers broad background for understanding the visual art as a vehicle of communication in its functional context. Provides a concise appreciation to the development of socioeconomic, religious and political characteristics of the covered periods. Enables students to acquire a broad scope of cross-cultural understanding through continuous critical thinking perspective. Discusses how to comprehend and interpret works of art by examining the contextual characteristics of the work, its subject, functionality and the culture(s) that produced it. A field trip to a major museum and other appropriate sites is required to strengthen student knowledge.NOTE: It is recommended that a student who plans to take both ART-121 and ART-122 start with ART-121 first.

title: Recording Studio Engineering 2
description: Emphasizes advanced digital recording techniques and independent problem solving in the recording process. Covers recording sessions, computer files, microphone usage and placement, digitizing principals, audio acquisition options, midi integration, and session management. Students work in an actual digital recording studio recording a wide variety of instrumental and vocal ensembles.

title: Enhancing Language Development
description: Follow your child's lead and have fun while enhancing language development! In this fun and user-friendly course for parents, teachers, and caregivers, you will discover how children learn to process language and how they become proficient speakers and thinkers. This course will help you enrich your child's life by stimulating his or her continued speech, brain, and language development in an enjoyable, age-appropriate, and natural way.

title: Computer Security/Maintenance
description: Learn about computer security and maintenance in both Windows Vista and XP. You will work with user accounts, passwords and desktop security. Take the mystery out of installing & updating hardware & software. Learn to maintain and back up your hard drive and data. Class work includes discussion of the latest security threats and defenses. Basic computer knowledge required. Text book provided.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Compression analysis including cylinder compression testing and cylinder leak down testing. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Cooling system evaluation and testing, including identification of cooling system components. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile. Ignition system diagnostics that includes the use of secondary ignition scope and electronic system testing.

title: More Pilates
description: Continue sculpting your body in this intermediate-level class Appropriate for anyone who has completed Pilates for Beginners or has a basic knowledge of the fitness method. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable stretchable clothing.

title: Heal Digestive Disorder-Nurses
description: Does eating cause you pain? Have you been diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome, ulcers, heartburn, bloating, or esophageal burning? Learn how others have stopped pain and received relief during this workshop by popular naturopath and herbalist Mary Born. Discover the basis for digestive problems and learn methods to encourage good digestion and the importance of enzymes. Handouts will discuss specific nutrients matched to symptoms and foods that speed healing of the GI tract. 2.5 nursing contact hours.

title: Scrapbooking for Beginners
description: Discover scrapbooking - the new hobby that is sweeping the nation. Bring 6-8 photos or souvenirs to class, and create your very first scrapbook page. This class could begin a lifelong hobby to celebrate the milestones, vacations and memories of life. Lorraine Paffenroth, your instructor, will provide cutting tools, markers, and adhesives. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class. Class limited to 12 attendees.

title: Parent Care
description: If your kids have attention difficulties, you need a break. Take two hours a week to be gentle with yourself and nurture yourself the way you nurture your children. Make some time for yourself, so you come back to them refreshed and with more to give. Each week you will learn to give and receive a Serenity Ear Acupressure Treatment to help you relax and center. By the end of the series you will learn many different ways to nurture and treat yourself and your loved ones safely, without drugs. You will network with parents, educators and therapists. Bring tennis balls and socks. Wear comfortable soft clothes.

title: Industrial Computer Applicatio
description: Introduces computer systems and associated components typically found in the industrial environment. Presents basic skills in word processing, database, spreadsheet, email, specialized vendor software, and communication software. Also covers Internet and Intranet environments, including searching for industry manuals and parts, downloading software and drivers, and other related tasks. Utilizes the Windows operating system in conjunction with proprietary software. Discusses the HFC learning management systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Industrial Computer Applicatio
description: Introduces computer systems and associated components typically found in the industrial environment. Presents basic skills in word processing, database, spreadsheet, email, specialized vendor software, and communication software. Also covers Internet and Intranet environments, including searching for industry manuals and parts, downloading software and drivers, and other related tasks. Utilizes the Windows operating system in conjunction with proprietary software. Discusses learning management systems including AMTEC curriculum and Moodle Rooms. Laboratory activities.

title: Singing on the Stage
description: Offers both theoretical and practical study of basic and intermediate-level vocal technique singing in live, theatrical performance. Explores proper breath and alignment, sound production and projection, articulation, genre, and interpretation based on story, composition, lyrics, subtext, and character.

title: Create Gourmet Caramel Apples
description: You've seen these exquisite (and expensive!) applies for sale in upscale department stores and on cable TV shopping shows. Sinfully sweet caramel enrobed apples covered with layer upon layer of assorted flavors of luxurious chocolate, heavily sprinkled with loads of crunchy, chewy toppings, and drizzled with even more chocolate! In this class you'll create five different gourmet caramel applies using four different kinds of chocolate and several assorted toppings. Gourmet applies make wonderful gifts for people of all ages. They're great party favors too. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-$18).

title: Michigan Works! Workshop
description: Workshop designed to help Michigan Works! customers prepare for employment opportunities.

title: Beginning Modern Dance
description: Explores Modern Dance as an art form that has a limitless way to express oneself in human movement. Primary emphasis is on developing technique of the basic Modern movement vocabulary. Course also covers skeletal muscular anatomy and function as it applies to dance movement, and an introduction to improvisation and composition.

title: Office Ready!
description: Office ready is a non-credit certificate program designed to train students in Microsoft Office Word 2010 and customer service skills that will help students handle specific customer service chanllenges. Students will be trained in on day workshops in Microsoft Office Word 2010 Levels 1, 2, and 3; Microsoft Office Excel 2010 Levels 1, 2, and 3, and Microsoft Office PowerPoint Levels 1 &2. the customer service portion included in this program is a two day workshop that will focus on learning timps and techniques that help best meet the customer's needs. The skills incluce deternining what to do and how to manage the customer interchange. The use of practical self-management skills, excercises and simulations will help each participant learn to solve their customer service chanllenges.15 May 2014 2:33 PM Arlicia Summerville

title: Web Database Dev with PHP
description: An intermediate-level course in which a database-backed web site using PHP and SQL is developed. Also covers HTML, CSS, PHP, database design, JSON, Git, and security.

title: Principles of Marketing
description: Focuses on marketing functions and institutions, the policies of marketing agencies, and current marketing channels. Also covers the retailing and wholesaling of consumer goods and raw materials, pricing policies and practices, anti-competitive practices, and recent governmental activities affecting marketing.

title: Co-op in Electrical Technolog
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Indust App of Phys Science
description: Offers the apprentice lecture exposure to the applications of physics and chemistry found in today's industrial workplace. Covers the six elemental machines; applications of forces, motion, and work; and the chemistry of industrial materials and chemical interactions in the environment.

title: Be Your Own Bodyguard
description: Feel safer in any environment. Using techniques from a combination of several martial art forms, quickly develop survival skills and strategies that will help you defend yourself as well as stay in shape. Javarri Hill, a master in Chinese kenpo, a black belt in tae kwon do and an experienced instructor, will teach you how to use the martial arts for self defense. Appropriate for women and men of all ages. No prior experience required. Uniform included in cost and is yours to keep.

title: Whole Grains & a Hill of Beans
description: Healthful, economic, filling and satisfying - from soup to dessert - whole grains and beans can keep you fit and trim. Join Lourdes Lore, Dietitian/college instructor in creating rib-sticking meals and flavor to savor with mixed bean stew and brown rice, quinoa black bean salad, and for dessert multi-grain guinataan. Yum! Lourdes will also explain how fiber from grains and beans can help prevent common gastric ailments. Bring an apron and a taste for gastronomic adventure.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories and boost your metabolism as you work your upper body, lower body, and abdominals. You'll love the results! Great class for men and women of all fitness levels, however beginners may need to adapt some of the routines. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Steam and Hot Water Systems
description: For technical construction apprentices and those interested in seeking basic pipefitting -plumbing skills. Introduces principles of steam and hydronic systems, converter trap sizing, steam traps, and skimming boilers. Also examines the proper selection, sizing, and installation of pipe and fittings related to hydronic and steam systems. Application exercises allow students the opportunity to design and lay out typical systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Ophthalmic Externship III
description: Continues structured clinical experiences with emphasis on gaining autonomy in performing basic procedures, while adding new experiences in digital imaging and surgical assisting. Students must document 168 clinical hours.

title: Advanced Respiratory Concepts
description: A study of the current theory and techniques encountered by the respiratory therapist in a variety of clinical settings. Emphasis is placed on advanced ventilator applications, advanced cardiopulmonary monitoring, pulmonary rehabilitation, smoking cessation and the respiratory care professional's dynamic role in the health care setting.

title: Metal Formabililty & Die Impr
description: This 40 hour class will provide Tool & Die personnel with instruction on the skills needed to understand and perform metal formbility and die improvement. Participant will receive instruction on techniques and procedures that enable them to improve quality and reduce down time in the manufacturing system. This instruction will contribute to the plant's implementaion of the Reference Panel System, a key component to attaining quality.

title: Windowsill Herb Gardening
description: You don't need to live in the country or even have a backyard to grow an abundant supply of fresh herbs. Liven up family meals year round with fresh herbs that are less expensive, fresher and grown right in your kitchen. Learn how to grow and care for herbs in this hands-on class. You'll plant three small pots of herbs and take them home ready to place in your kitchen. All materials included.

title: Hs All Skills Volleyball Camp
description: At the All Skills camp players will learn/improve specific skills - emphasizing on serving, passing, setting, blocking, attacking, and defense - through fun drills and team play.

title: Ultimate German Feast
description: If you like a good substantial meal, you'll love German cooking. For a cultural experience, join Ursula Markovits, who was born and rasied in Germany. She'll teach you how to make an authenic German meal of kasseler rippchen (smoked pork chops in sauerkraut) and pan-fried potatoes wth onions. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Co-op in Science
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major in STEM to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Access: Part 1
description: Simplify customer records, personnel files, sales records, membership directories and more using the most popular database software. You will learn and practice the critical skills necessary to create a database and enter, find, edit and report on the data it contains. The course is designed for the newer user of Access and assumes no experience with databases. Intro to PC: Part 2 or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: Construction Blueprint Reading
description: Explores mechanical (HVAC), architectural, and electrical drawings required for energy technology, plumbing and pipefitting, electrical, and construction related trades. Covers print reading, interpreting common symbols, working notes, views, and title blocks. Introduces project management and estimating. Note: Basic measurement and math skills required for print reading section.

title: Recording Arts Capstone
description: As the capstone for the Recording Arts Certificate program, course provides special study in audio recording and is intended for students completing the Certificate program. Students choose the topic for study and design an instructional plan under the guidance and approval of instructor. Must complete a major project that demonstrates skills and knowledge learned in the program, and project is presented at the end of the semester.

title: Predictive Maint Shaft Align
description: Provides specialized instruction in the practices and equipment used in shaft alignment, and the end-to-end and parallel alignments of machines. Explores machine failures due to rotating shaft misalignment and vibration created from shaft center lines not being in the same plane. Covers inefficiencies and increased wear due to misalignment, shaft alignment methods, soft foot, thermal growth, rim, face, reverse dial indicator, and MPT couplings.

title: Industrial Wiring
description: Introduces the fundamentals and techniques of industrial wiring. Emphasizes aspects of the National Electrical Code specifically applying to industrial applications and repairs. Includes electrical supplies and hardware appropriate for industrial applications.

title: Indust Appl Trigonomic Princip
description: Focuses on trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Covers trigonometric functions, solution of right triangles, solution of oblique triangles, and problem solving techniques.

title: Spanish in the Classroom
description: Learn the essential Spanish for teachers so you can bridge the communication gap with your Spanish-speaking students and parents! Rest assured that that this course won't be your typical high school Spanish course. We've tossed out long vocabulary lists and complex grammar rules and replaced them with games and stories to make learning easy, painless, and even fun. In addition to basic greetings and general Spanish vocabulary, you'll master words and phrases that directly relate to the classroom, such as classroom objects, front-office receptions, action verbs and commands, study skills, registration, course names, school rules and discipline, and parent-teacher conference vocabulary. By the end of this course, you'll be well on your way to being a Spanish speaker and communicating effectively with the Spanish-speakers all around you. ¡Vamos! (Let's go!)

title: Join the Frame
description: This class will teach you the techniques necessary to matt, mount, and frame those pictures and mementoes that you have been meaning to get into frames--for display and protection. In the first session the teacher will demonstrate the whole process and discuss the requirements of framing a variety of objects. The second night students will work on their own projects. Students will provide their own matt board, frames, glass, and supplies (a supply list will be given out in the first session.) The hardest part of the process is cutting the matts; thus, that will be the focus of the second evening.

title: Sheet Metal Appl Triangulation
description: Covers the development of geometrical elements of structures by the triangulation method of sheet metal layout. Includes the development of stretchouts and making cardboard or sheet metal models of transition pieces.

title: Soup Cooking 101
description: Chef Peter Veach will be preparing four types of simple and easy soups.

title: College Writing and Research
description: ENG-132 is the second college-level composition course in a two-semester sequence. Students will continue to write, read, think critically, and use information at the college level. In particular, students will be exposed to a range of literary texts and will be instructed in ethical, effective research writing.

title: Elementary Arabic I
description: Introduces the basic skills of reading, writing, speaking, and listening in Modern Standard Arabic within communicative contexts. Covers the elementary pronunciation and grammatical principles necessary for comprehending and expressing simple ideas in both spoken and written Modern Standard Arabic. Presents Arabic culture. NOTE:Requires basic proficiency in recognizing, reading, and writing simple sentences in Modern Standard Arabic.

title: Create Wealth Globally
description: Because of increased US outsourcing, rapid industrialization in China and India, and rising energy prices, allocating some of your portfolio to international investments can help you grow wealth. Let financial planner Ken Bloom review for you the opportunities and risks in global investing. Get inside information about why you need an emerging market fund in your account and how to use international funds to lower your investment risk. Take home many investment strategies and a packet of handouts.

title: Mathematics for Calculus
description: Topics include algebraic, graphical and numerical representations of functions, including transformations, composition and inverses of functions. Focuses on the study of polynomial, rational, root, exponential and logarithmic functions, and trigonometric and inverse trigonometric functions of real numbers. Also includes equation solving and trigonometric identities. Techniques of problem solving and applications are included throughout the course requiring the frequent usage of graphing utilities. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments and access to an online homework system.

title: Intermediate Arabic II
description: Follows ARA-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level Modern Standard Arabic courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Modern Standard Arabic, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in both spoken and written Arabic. Presents Arabic culture.

title: Co-Op Automotive Technology
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Fund Plumbing & Pipefitting
description: Introduces plumbing principles. Topics include the proper selection of materials for installation and repair of gas, domestic water, sewer, soil, waste, vent systems, and various pipefitting systems. Utilizes blueprints and isometric diagrams through the corse some laboratory activities.

title: Wireless LANs
description: An intermediate-level course covering Wireless Networking. Discusses the IEEE 802.11 standards and the relationship to the OSI model. Also covers the planning, installation, configuration, troubleshooting, and securing of Wireless Networking products.

title: Watercolor Sampler
description: Ever thought you might like to paint with watercolors? Here's your opportunity to learn the eight basic techniques while painting a small landscape picture.
