Join Saundra Weed for a one-day introduction to this art medium. Then decide if you'd like to continue exploring watercolor in her six-week class. $5 material fee due to instructor. Bring a bag lunch.

title: Holiday Centerpiece
description: Learn how to create a professional looking holiday centerpiece to brighten your home this holiday season. Professional designer Susan Muldowney of Black Eyed Susans will show you how in this hands-on class. Bring $13.00 for supplies and a pair of garden clippers.

title: Contemp Guitar
description: Learn the basics of playing guitar. No experience with guitar is necessary. While strumming your favorite pop, rock, or folk music, you will acquire the tools to continue on to any style of guitar playing. Bring your own guitar.

title: German for Business & Pleasure
description: If your business or travels take you to Germany, this introductory course will make your trip smoother. Emphasis will be on German customs and useful phrases with a guide to pronunciation. You will learn about the cultural differences related to the environment, political developments, the economic system, holidays, and more. At the same time, you will learn about greetings, directions, money, foods & restaurants, shopping, time, days, and colors. All materials and texts included.

title: Extremity Orthopedics
description: Presents the etiology, course, prognosis, medical intervention, and treatment of orthopedic conditions of the extremities. Includes physical therapy intervention. Guided lab setting.

title: Carve Ice!
description: This lecture and hands-on ice carving class is certified by the American Culinary Federation Educational Institute and is designed to benefit both beginning and advanced students. Learn how to carve single-block and multi-block ice sculp- tures under the direction of Chef Richard Teeple, an HFCC instructor whose ice carving teams are consistent local and national prize winners. Additional ice-carving experience will be available with the Ice Carving Club. All necessary tools will be provided.

title: Coninuing Piano Basics
description: For those who have already taken Piano Basics for Adults, this class offers an opportunity to go on with the piano lessons with Leslie Melville.

title: Legal Research
description: Examines the structure of the court systems and sources of law in the context of conducting legal research; covers how to locate and understand statutory, regulatory, and common law; examines how to identify real or hypothetical legal issues and then develop a research plan. Covers how to perform legal research using Westlaw and other Internet-based research databases.

title: Intro Microsoft Frontpage
description: Learn the essentials of Microsoft's popular web site building software. You'll begin with an introduction to basic web design principles, and you'll explore the interface and features of the FrontPage program. Then you'll learn how to manipulate words and images to create a site worthy of publishing to the internet. By the end of the course you'll be armed with all the knowledge you need to create an attractive, well-designed and functional web site.

title: Trad Polish Perogies
description: If you are not of Polish descent, you may never have actually tasted authentic, homemade peirogies. Here is your chance to roll up your sleeves and learn about making and handling the dough, and stuffing them with a variety of fillings that will appeal to all tastes. Plan to eat what you create and take home the leftovers. Bring an apron.

title: Lifespan Development
description: Covers the processes of change and stability in human development from conception through death. Course begins with the study of prenatal concerns and progresses through the years of infancy, childhood, adolescence, adulthood and aging. Discusses theory, research, and application associated with the biological, cognitive, affective, and social domains of development.

title: Managing Finances With Excel
description: Master the basics of your Palm Pilot and become more organized and productive. Tom McMahon, a long-time Palm enthusiast, will teach you how to use the built-in features (to do list, address, date book, memo pad, calculator, graffiti), add new programs and features, and synchronize your Palm with your PC. This course is designed for new and intermediate-level users and is applicable to any Palm operating system (Palm, Sony Clie, Handspring Visor, Symbol, etc). Bring your Palm Pilot to class.

title: Intro to Health and Wellness
description: Assesses current level of wellness and provides the tools necessary to improve and motivate the student to practice healthier lifestyles throughout adulthood. Covers environmental, reproductive, and psychological health; stress management; infectious disease; nutrition; chronic disease; aging; and physical fitness.

title: Windows Vista: Part 1
description: If you can operate a car you can operate a computer. Don't let confusing computer jargon stop you! Attend this hands-on workshop designed for the person with little or no experience; and see how easy it is to use the basic features of the latest operating system, Windows Vista. You'll learn how to work with the mouse, keyboard, desktop, start menu and become familiar with the components of a working window. By the end of the class you'll also understand the difference between a hard drive, a disk and a CD-ROM.

title: Line Dancing (mini-Session)
description: Step away from your daily routine and join us as we learn some popular line dances and get a great workout at the same time. This class is designed for beginners, but all levels are welcome. And, one of the best features of this type of dance is partners are optional. Wear comfortable shoes, come with energy, and be ready for fun!

title: Honors Directed Study
description: Challenges sophomore students to apply knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Coursework includes presenting a research summary before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty at the end of each semester.

title: Polish Paczki
description: You don't have to be Polish to enjoy our paczki party! Come see Don Samull present slides of Warsaw, Poland; and enjoy Polish hors d'oeuvres and, of course, delicious jelly-filled paczkis. In addition, to complete the evening, the noted men's choir from Detroit's St. Hyacinth Polish Church will perform a mini concert.

title: Introduction to Nutrition
description: Introduces how to evaluate nutrition information, plan nutritious diets, digestion issues, macro and micro nutrients, energy balance and weight control, food safety, and proper nutrition for a lifetime. Also examines how to assess nutritive content, spread of calories, and the calorie balance of various diets.

title: MS Word Fundamentals
description: A two hour hands-on computer lab with practical instruction.

title: Acad Writing & Reading for Ell
description: Intended for English Language Learners (ELLs) whose placement scores indicate the need for instruction or review in order for them to write acceptable college compositions in ENG-131, or for ELLs who have successfully completed ELI-106 R, W, and C courses. Various methods such as lectures, group discussions, textbook exercises, peer review and conferences will be used to help students improve their expository writing skills. This course also emphasizes reading strategies and skills that will prepare English Language Learners for success in college level content-area and English composition courses. Students will learn how to make connections with a variety of authentic texts, practice strategies needed for critical reading, and expand their academic English vocabulary. Students must earn a grade of C or higher in ELL-130 before enrolling in ENG-131. This course is non-transferable.

title: Adult Tap Dance 1
description: Get in shape and learn to tap! Its fun and it will improve your memory and coordination too! Learn basic steps skills such as shuffles, flaps, slaps, ball changes and cramp rolls. By the end of this session you should be able to perform short combinations with these basic steps. You can take this class over and over until you feel you can perform longer and faster combinations. Tap shoes mandatory or you will not be able to participate.

title: Gas Distribution Apprentice
description: This course was designed to combat the shortage of a skilled workforce in the gas pipeline industry. Students will cover areas such as various OSHA and safety training, how to operate heavy equipment such as mini excavators and backhoe loaders, and pipe fusion. The opportunity to obtain a CDL-A license is provided, along with training on how to drive a 2x5 yard dump truck with a 16' trailer.

title: Intro to Media Communication
description: This course explores the role of media and communication in shaping society, culture, and public opinion across digital, social, and print platforms such as books, radio, TV, film, video games, PR, advertising, social media, global media, and more. Through historical, economic, ethical, and cultural perspectives, students will examine how media affects our daily lives and influences our interactions, values, and understanding of the world.

title: Paint W Acrylics (ages 9-11)
description: The sky is the limit for opportunities for creativity! Come explore new worlds of color that include animals, flowers, abstracts and landscapes - perhaps even a blast into outer space. Art supplies are included. Wear clothes that you don't mind getting messy.

title: Learn to Belly Dance
description: Join seasoned pro Abida for six exciting sessions of belly dancing. Learn the classic movements and experience the exhilaration of moving your body with the music in this ancient dance tradition. Have fun and dance your cares away! All fitness levels welcome. Wear comfortable clothing.

title: Live Well Be Well- Memca
description: Student lead conference surounding health, wellness, & community leadership.

title: Organic Chemistry II
description: Builds on the concepts introduced in CHEM-241. Emphasizes acid-base chemistry, spectroscopy, and retrosynthetic analysis. Reaction mechanisms and physical properties of the following functional groups will be explored: carbonyl chemistry (aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acid derivatives); carboxylic acids; amines; carbohydrates; lipids; amino acids, proteins; and nucleotides. This course is designed to meet the requirements for science majors, pre-professional biology, pre-professional chemistry, as well as premedical, pre-dental, pre-pharmacy, veterinary, and chemical engineering students. Four hours of lecture per week without lab.

title: Make Money With eBay
description: Step up a notch in the world of eBay and learn the secrets of the pros. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer who has made hundreds of transactions himself, will teach you about the ins and outs of using design and bid services, setting up an "eBay store," using pictures effectively in ads, shipping and payment options and even avoiding the pitfalls.

title: Zigzag Jewelry
description: Are you looking for something different for your next jewelry project? A lightweight, elegant, zigzag necklace and matching earrings might be the perfect jewelry to wear to a social event or a business meeting. Choose your own colors. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: General Physics II
description: Builds on the concepts introduced in PHYS-131. Topics include electricity, magnetism, light, and modern physics. Three hours of lecture and three hours of laboratory per week.

title: Advanced CNC Operations
description: Emphasizes the total aspects of computer numerical control (CNC) as applied in manufacturing today. Covers estimating for CNC manufacturing, DNC, tool selection, cutting-tool material selection, and small-batch production.

title: Blues Guitar
description: Come explore all aspects of "The Blues" as it relates to its performance on the guitar, and jam with renowned Detroit area musician Robert Penn. You will learn the importance of the guitar as well as theoretical principles that are characteristic of this particular genre. Examine various styles of different players as well as their approaches to improvising and techniques. The instructor will include the work of B.B. King, Jimi Hendricks, T-Bone Walker and artists of like importance. Another area of focus will be the pentatonic blues scales and its application to the I, IV, V Blues progression. Bring your acoustic or electric guitars and a music stand if you have one. Prerequisite: at least one year of basic guitar.
prerequisite_text: at least one year of basic guitar.

title: Adv Children's Theater Prod
description: Advanced children's theatrical production class.

title: Patient Care in Radiography
description: This course is designed to introduce the basic concepts of patient care including patient interactions, body mechanics, patient transfer techniques, vital signs, infection control, emergency situations, age specific care, oxygen administration, basic pharmacology and venipuncture.

title: Summer Container Gardening
description: Create attractive flowering planters and exquisite container displays that will bloom all season long. Hear a Master Gardener discuss everything from choosing the right plants for your porch or deck conditions to making successful soil mix- tures, to providing care and maintenance. You'll even find out how to plant perennials in containers and winterize them for years of bloom- ing success.

title: Swing Dance Academy!
description: Always watched people swing dance and wonder "how do they do that?" Now you too can learn the art of East Coast Swing Dance! This high energy, fast and fun dance has been popular in the United States for decades and now you can be a part of the craze! This class will allow those who have taken beginning swing lessons or classes to go to the next level, or those who are just beginning a chance to see what its all about. Singles or couples may join, though many feel more comfortable bringing a partner with them. See you on the dance floor!

title: Intro to Stage Makeup
description: Provides an analysis of the basic functions of stage makeup and demonstrates the art of makeup application and design, including such potential topics as age, animal, corrective, contouring, fantasy/horror, cosplay, high fashion, and foam latex prosthetic makeup techniques. Students design and apply their own makeup as well as design and apply makeup for classmates and/or actors in HFC mainstage productions.

title: Property Mgmt
description: If you are a landlord or are considering becoming one, this course is for you. Learn the basics of property management including how to determine rental rates, screen tenants, legally handle security deposits, and more. This course is approved for 30 hours toward the 90 hours required education needed to obtain a real estate brokers license for licensed salespeople. Cost includes text and materials.

title: Make Money on eBay
description: Become one of the 725,000 people in the U.S. who makes some or all of their income using eBay. Step up a notch in the world of eBayers, and learn the secrets of the pros. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer who has made hundreds of transactions himself, will teach you about the ins and outs of using design and bid services, setting up an "eBay store", using pictures effectively in ads, developing shipping and payment options, and even avoiding the pitfalls. Prerequisite: Buy & Sell on eBay or similar eBay experience. An optional $5 CD of helpful class notes and resources available from the instructor in class.
prerequisite_text: Buy & Sell on eBay or similar eBay experience. An optional $5 CD of helpful class notes and resources available from the instructor in class.

title: Do It Yourself Floral
description: Do you love DIY projects? This course will teach you how to take a simple bunch of flowers and turn them into a breathtaking centerpiece. Using flowers like roses and hydrangea you'll learn how to make a table centerpiece perfect for a wedding, special event or holiday dinner that you can take home and enjoy. No experience required! All tools and supplies will be provided. Registration for this class closes on November 5 so supplies can be ordered.

title: Easy MS Word for Beginners
description: Use your computer to create letters, memos, reports and other documents for business or personal use. See how easy it is to type in your information and have it look great. Learn to edit text, move things around, and even have the computer check your spelling and grammar! Seasoned instructor Audrey Spezia will show you how to save your work and how to find it again. Make text look fancy with simple formatting features. Print out perfect pages without whiteout and erasures! Discover shortcuts for making repetitive tasks easy. Prerequisites: intro PC or equivalent knowledge required. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: intro PC or equivalent knowledge required. Text provided.

title: Basic Lawn Care
description: You don't have to become a lawn expert to have a healthy lawn. Just follow the tips of Advanced Certified Master Gardener Nancy Fedon and you'll be well on your way. In this seminar you will learn how to effectively control weeds, pick and apply fertilizers, manicure the lawn, maintain the lawn mower, properly water a lawn and more.

title: N American Tech Excellence Cer
description: Explores various types of basic work knowledge of the Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning and Refrigeration (HVACR) systems that are installed in the HVACR field. Topics covered include safety, trade tools, heat and matter, electrical fundamentals, transformers and motors, electrical diagrams, and proper use of required test instruments. As a result of this class, students will learn about the structure and HVACR communication skills expected of them in the technology and skilled trade industry. This course helps to prepare students to take the NATE Core Certification Exam based on ACCA, NATE, and RSES information.

title: Crochet a Winter Hat
description: Kick off a crochet craze by making your own jaunty new hat. Crocheter Carol Bacon will show you how quick and easy it is to begin this new craft. Please bring a size 1 hook and 4 oz. of 4-ply yarn in your favorite color.

title: Residential Troubleshooting
description: PLMB 245 provides advanced knowledge and hands-on skills for diagnosing, servicing, and repairing residential plumbing systems. Students apply systematic troubleshooting procedures to potable water distribution, drainage, waste and vent (DWV) systems, fixtures, pumps, water heaters, and residential appliances. Emphasis is placed on interpreting symptoms, identifying root causes, correcting system deficiencies, and performing safe, code-compliant repairs. Students practice field-ready service skills including leak detection, pressure and flow testing, drain diagnostics, fixture repair, soldering and joining, and system restoration. The course integrates Michigan Residential Code requirements, manufacturer specifications, and industry best practices for residential plumbing service work.

title: Internetw 4 Cisco
description: CISCO Local Academy teachers will learn WAN theory, design and technology, PPP, Frame Relay, ISDN, and network troubleshooting. CLCE 9952 must be completed prior to taking this class.

title: Inexpensive, Easy & Quick Meal
description: Time-saving meals that are easy on the budget? Learn how to stretch your food dollars and prepare quick and simple flavorful meals with Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estates.

title: Basic Video Production I
description: Many videos have similar themes (e.g. love, revenge, etc.) but the way they are structured should differentiate them from any other. Mr. Frank Jamal will teach you a logical and imaginative approach to visual story development. Get an introduction to the basic, essential elements that make up a story. Learn to structure your stories more effectively as you study concepts such as drama, character, act and scene. You will need a screenplay narrative, format, plot/story concept, and plot treatment.

title: Authentic Mexican Enchiladas
description: Learn how to prepare the basic recipe to make authentic Mexican enchiladas with Alicia Bonilla-Arias. Make the rolled traditional Mexican type, the Sonora Style from Arizona, and the enchilada that is the flat pancake type. Alicia will introduce you to three different kinds of pepper that she puts in the sauce - mild, sweet and hot incorporated into one of her old family recipes. You will learn what to do with fresh and dry pepper and how to properly roll the enchiladas. Bring an apron and an appetite.

title: Topics in Skilled Trades
description: Provides specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics offered is identified by the Skilled Trades Apprenticeship Division faculty, STAD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the STAD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Course may be repeated for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Website With Adobe Dreamweaver
description: Let experienced instructor walk you through the accessible website with the brand new version of Dreamweaver. (Macromedia version users also welcome). Learn the Dreamweaver interface basics, work with text and images, create links and anchors, create successful navigation and maintain the site. Lots of hands-on practice at your own workstation and plenty of time for questions. Prerequisite: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Text provided.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: Plants not only add beauty to your home; they also improve air quality. Join Master Gardener Nancy Fedon and learn everything you ever wanted to know about indoor plants and unique indoor landscaping techniques. In addition to gardening basics, you'll leave with loads of information about flowering houseplants, foliage plants, the various succulents, and the effective management of common pests and diseases.

title: Industrial Safety Awareness
description: Presents a comprehensive approach to safety, designed to give the skilled tradesperson the knowledge of safety fundamentals and practices, from the causes of accidents to the study of safety hazards and rules associated with equipment and tools utilized in modern industrial facilities. Successful completers will be issued the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) 30-Hour card for General Industry.

title: Intro to Business
description: Presents an integrated examination of the American business system and the operations of business organizations. Covers theories of management and their application, production, operations, marketing, and financial management of firms.

title: Christianity - First 1000 Year
description: Introduces the origins of Christianity beginning with the figure of Jesus and his portrayal in early Christian writings. Studies the early teachings and their influence on later Christian theological understanding of the nature of Jesus through readings in the New Testament. Also explores the historical development of Christianity from its origins in Palestine beginning 1 B.C.E. through its expansion into the Roman Empire and ending at the schism between the Eastern Orthodox Church and the Western Catholic Church in the 1054 C.E.

title: Belly Dance Workshop
description: Spend an evening with Cedena and learn a sensual, but simple, routine that includes basic veil technique. Some experience is recommended. If you've completed the first session of Absolute Beginners you will find the techniques familiar. Veil required.

title: Reiki Level 1 Certification
description: Reiki (pronounced Ray-Key) is a form of energy healing used to treat the body, mind, and spirit. Learn from a Reiki Master instructor the history, principles, hand positions, chakras, and treatment used in this Japanese system. Upon completion, you will be certified and given an attunement as a Reiki Level 1 practitioner. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Hips & Sciatica Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols are used to relieve hip pain and sciatica. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve stiffness in hips and pelvis as well as sciatica - without taking drugs.

title: Intro to Industrial Technology
description: Introduces students to the fundamentals of being a successful college student on the path to a profession in industrial technology. Subjects are divided into three categories: Student Self Awareness, Manufacturing Work and Culture, and Test Taking (technical), and include topics such as self-directed learning, critical thinking and problem solving, industrial safety policies and procedures, job outlook, and elements of standardized testing including graphic arithmetic, assemblies, mechanical comprehension, and troubleshooting. The class includes guest speakers from manufacturing and the service industry. The course utilizes digital learning materials, hands-on labs, and a student self assessment of their learning and study skills.

title: Orig Fast Track Rdg & Math Crs
description: This is an 8-week course available to qualified individuals who are interested in improving their basic understanding of reading comprehension and essential mathematics. The reading topics include indentifying the author's purpose, summarizing infromation, critical reading, context clues and projecting, math includes integers, decimals, fractions, percents, algebra and geometry. Note taking, test-taking skills, time management and study skills will also be included.

title: Spec Topics in Comp Info Sys
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current Computer Information System trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites when offered. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Intro to Healthcare Profession
description: This course is designed to introduce students to various health care professions. It includes skills and topics common to all healthcare providers such as ethical and legal issues, professionalism, cultural considerations, vital signs, body mechanics, communication, infection control, and evidence based practice. This course will also introduce key elements in the formation and modification of medical terms. This course has a lab hands on component.

title: Auto Alignment Clinic
description: Designed to cover the types of front and rear suspension systems used on today¿s vehicles. In addition, there is in-depth coverage of alignment factors. The majority of the class is devoted to inspection, repair, and alignment of a variety of suspension systems.

title: Making Facebook Work for You
description: Learn to take advantage of the many features available through Facebook to promote your small business. See how Facebook works and discover the difference between a personal and business account. Learn how to set up a business page and/or a group page, how to use the best marketing practices on Facebook and much more!

title: ELL Reading & Listening 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college-preparatory reading and listening skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELI-102R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Digital Video Editing
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-157 to register for this course. An intensive introduction to digital video editing, using an industry standard software editing program in the Telecommunication computer video lab. Covers both hardware and software issues, and explores how to complete short editing exercises.

title: Intro to Windows 7 - Part 2
description: Continue building on the skills introduced in Part 1 and become more comfortable with the basic features of Windows. After a quick review of what you learned in Part 1, you will discover how easy it is to create, move, copy, delete, and restore information. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers Part 1, or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers Part 1, or equivalent knowledge.

title: Abs, Glutes & Thighs
description: Burn, fat... tighten your abs... slim and tone your inner and outer thighs... lift your derriere! This challenging but fun workout sculpts and tightens your lower body. Increase your energy level and feel better in an amazingly short time. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. However, beginners may need to adapt some of the exercises. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Web Design 1
description: Design and create your own web site. Learn to prepare graphics for the web, use HTML and set up pages using a web-authoring program, establish links, and save and organize your files efficiently. By the end of the session, you should be able to take a working web site home with you on disk. Some experience with image editing programs (i.e., Photoshop) helpful. Dreamweaver 3.0 will be covered. Each student will have his or her own computer.

title: Start Ebay Drop Off Shore
description: Turn other people's unwanted items into a lucrative source of income by starting an eBay drop off store. Master the art of setting up a business, writing a marketing plan, taking super photos, creating dynamite listings, and offering great customer service. Thousands of people have transformed their eBay knowledge into profitable second income sources or even full time jobs. Take your skills to the next level and avoid the common pitfalls that keep amateur sellers from reaching their full potential.

title: Illusion Necklace
description: Design and make an elegant necklace using your choice of illusion cord or stretch magic. Cindy Taylor, the instructor, will bring a wonderful selection of beads, including Swarovski crystals, from which to choose. You'll leave with a necklace suitable for a special event. Bring a crimping tool (or you may purchase one in class for $12). $10 material fee payable in class.

title: Hvac Installation and Startup
description: This course provides students with hands-on experience and theoretical knowledge in the proper installation, setup, and commissioning of residential and light commercial HVAC systems. Students will learn to safely and efficiently install furnaces, condensers, evaporator coils, and air handlers, as well as connect refrigerant lines, electrical components, and ductwork. Emphasis will be placed on system startup procedures, performance testing, documentation, and adherence to manufacturer specifications, codes, and industry standards. Students will perform complete installations and startups on operating HVAC systems, simulating real-world job conditions. Safety practices, quality workmanship, and customer satisfaction are emphasized throughout the course.

title: Laugh 'til Your Sides Hurt
description: Hoot, holler, snicker, and giggle with Mike Millington, Michigan's only certified laughter therapist, and regain your ability to be playful. You will learn the difference between humor and laughter, trace the history of laughter, and discover the herapeutic advantages of hearty laughter. Meanwhile, you will leave this upbeat workshop refreshed with both new energy and a renewed ability to laugh heartily, like you did as a child.

title: Web Desgin 2
description: A continuation of CLCE 2068--Creating a Web Site, you will learn to create simple graphics and add functionality to your web site using Dreamweaver 3.0. By the end of class you should be ready to FTP your web site to the Internet. Your instructor, Shelly Andrews is a web designer in the auto industry. Check out her site at http://home.earthlink.net/..sheland. Prerequisite: CLCE 2068 or a working knowledge of Dreamweaver and web design. 3 sessions.
prerequisite_text: CLCE 2068 or a working knowledge of Dreamweaver and web design. 3 sessions.

title: Honors Coll Writing & Research
description: ENG-132 is the second college-level composition course in a two-semester sequence. Students will continue to write, read, think critically, and use information at the college level. In particular, students will be exposed to a range of literary texts related to the Honors Colloquium topics, and will be instructed in ethical, effective research writing.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Compression analysis including cylinder compression testing and cylinder leak down testing. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Cooling system evaluation and testing, including identification of cooling system components. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile. Ignition system diagnostics that includes the use of secondary ignition scope and electronic system testing.

title: Estate Planning and Probate
description: Examines laws relating to the control and disposition of property in anticipation of death and post-death under Michigan's Estate and Protected Individuals Code (EPIC). Covers wills, trusts, laws of intestate succession, and powers of attorney. Also covers the substantive, procedural, and practical aspects of probate practice under EPIC from the time of the notification of death through final distribution.

title: Clinical Education III
description: This is the third in a five-course sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologist (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) competencies. This course offers continued exposure to chart recognition, patient assessment, patient communication, and departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, abdomen, upper extremity, shoulder girdle, lower extremity, and pelvic girdle are emphasized. Skull radiographic positioning is introduced.

title: Chinese Surv Guid for Trav
description: Whether for business or for pleasure, your trip to China will be much more interesting if you know the basics of the beautiful Mandarin Chinese language and culture. Learn from Wen Chi Swadling, a Chinese/English translator, teacher, and licensed international tour guide, basic survival language skills and unique cultural issues. The final session (which is optional) will be held at a Chinese restaurant chosen by the instructor. Cost of meal not included in course fee.

title: Art of Precious Metal Clay 2
description: Take your PMC skills to another level as you create three unique projects - a picture frame pendant, cufflinks and set of buttons. You'll also learn how to make your own tools and use different finishing methods. Prerequisite: Art of Previous Metal Clay 1 or prior experience with PMC. #35 materials fee due to instructor in class.
prerequisite_text: Art of Previous Metal Clay 1 or prior experience with PMC. #35 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Machine Tool Blueprint-PAPP
description: An introduction to blueprint reading; interpretation of various kinds of lines, position of views, symbols conventions, dimensioning practices, sectioning, auxiliary views and symmetry with emphasis on techniques employed to show details in relation to assembly drawings.

title: Registry Review
description: Provides a comprehensive review designed to prepare radiologic technology students for the American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) certification examination. An orderly review based on the ARRT radiography content specifications is presented. Test preparation and test-taking strategies are also discussed.

title: Chorus 1
description: First of four semesters of Concert Choir including preparation and performance of a broad spectrum of choral music from classical to popular, show tunes, and jazz. At least two performances are scheduled each semester, sometimes accompanied by band or orchestra. These courses are open to college students and adult members of the community. New singers may audition before or during the first week of class. No preparation is necessary, but the demonstration of the ability to sing in tune and hold a part (soprano, alto, tenor, or bass) is required.

title: Current Issues in Vision Care
description: Explores issues and trends in healthcare delivery, including certification requirements and systematic review for the national certification examination for ophthalmic technicians.

title: Indust Appl of Geometric Prin
description: Covers the fundamental principles of plane geometry with emphasis on application to the industrial environment. Explores angular, circular, linear, area, and volume measurement in relation to the types of geometric figures and configurations found in industry.

title: Adv Topics in Skilled Trades
description: Provide the skilled trades community the opportunity to receive advanced specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics will be offered as identified by the Skilled Trades & Apprenticeship Department faculty, ST&AD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the ST&AD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Learners may repeat the course for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Paramedic IV
description: Introduces pathophysiology of cardiovascular disease and discusses how to recognize and treat the associated dysrhythmias. Covers the following topics in a broad manner: anatomy and physiology (review), general pathophysiology, geriatric considerations, assessment and management of emergencies in the areas of hematology, gastroenterology, toxicology, substance abuse, urology and nephrology, behavioral and psychiatric disorders, and environmental emergencies.

title: Mechanical Power Transmission
description: Presents installation and maintenance of mechanical power transmission systems. Covers bearings, couplings, belts, chains, shafts, pulleys, and speed reducers used in the modern factory by skilled trades.

title: Customer Service
description: Examines the principal functions of a customer service representative, the skills needed to succeed in the workplace, and the knowledge to advance into a leadership role within the service industry. Emphasizes effective communication skills, positive attitudes, problem solving, strategies for coping with challenging and dissatisfied customers, retention of customers, methods of measuring customer satisfaction, technology and customer service, and motivational techniques. NOTE: Upon successful completion of this course, students may choose to take an exam for a National Professional Certification in Customer Service from the National Retail Federation (NRF) Foundation. This exam is not included in this course.

title: Introduction to Nutrition
description: An introductory focused on the basics of nutrition. Topics covered include digestion, planning nutritious diets, evaluating nutrition information, energy nutrients, food safety, nutrition at different life stages, energy balance and weight control. This class provides you with the skills necessary to follow a nutritious diet that will positively impact health throughout life.

title: Exercise Techniques Lab
description: Provides the opportunity for application of the techniques learned in PTA 118. Focuses on therapeutic exercise procedures utilized by physical therapist assistants as well as assessment of joint motion and muscle strength, implications of range of motion (ROM) and muscle testing for exercise prescription, types of exercise, basic exercise programs, and the use of exercise equipment. Guided lab setting.

title: Silkscreen T-Shirt
description: The screening class will teach you how to make professional looking silkscreened items. Learn to apply the photo emulsion, process the screen and apply the ink. This is a very time consuming process but you will be able to go through the phases and the instructor will have a silk screen set up so that you can squeegee on a design. She will tell you what to buy to set up your own screening operation. Bring a t-shirt, tote bag or similar item for printing. $10 materials fee will be collected by the instructor.

title: Fancy Cookie Art Workshop
description: Instructor will collect $5.00 materials fee first day of class.

title: Line Dancing
description: Step away from your daily routine and join us as we learn some popular line dances and get a great workout at the same time. This class is designed for beginners, but all levels are welcome. And, one of the best features of this type of dance is partners are optional. Wear comfortable shoes, come with energy, and be ready for fun!

title: Dearbornville Doctors
description: Discover the early physicians who cared for the people of Dearborn in pioneer days. Learn about doctors who lived in the community and practiced medicine - one of whom served as commandant of the old Detroit Arsenal in Dearbornville.

title: Predict Main-Vibration Analysi
description: Provides specialized instruction in diagnosing and resolving machinery vibration in rotating equipment, the most cost-effective methods to reduce maintenance costs, and how to extend machinery life.

title: Inspect Real Estate Property
description: This class will enable an individual to investigate a property before making an offer to purchase. Peter Pace will give you the tools and tested techniques to save money and time, to teach you what concerns to look at before you make your purchase. You do not need a home inspector, be that person yourself, learn what they know and profit from these techniques.

title: Relax, Replenish, Revive! Yoga
description: A yoga class can be a slow gentle stretch or a fast paced cardio workout. The basic poses provide an endless variety of sequences for a fun, challenging and stress busting class. Students will master the foundation yoga poses that are necessary to progress to more challenging styles of yoga. This class is for all level.

title: Topics in Philosophy
description: A non-historical approach to philosophy for serious students interested in the professions or for professionals interested in focusing on topics of professional or personal significance. Section offerings are sufficiently diverse to acquaint serious students or professionals with the extensive scope of philosophy. Diverse areas such as philosophy of religion, philosophy of science, aesthetics, philosophy of law, business ethics, biomedical ethics, philosophy of language, etc. may be the current topic offered that semester. Topics vary each semester and with each instructor.

title: Piano Basics for Adults
description: Don't let that keyboard sit in the corner collecting dust anymore! Have some fun this winter learning how to play well-known classic and contemporary pieces. Under the direction of pianist Lesley Melville, you will become familiar with the keyboard, basic notes, hand positions, and music theory concepts. By the end of the course, you will be able to play popular songs using your left hand for chords and your right hand for melody. $16 textbook fee due to instructor in class. Please bring correct change.

title: Competency Evaluated NA(CENA)
description: Prepares individuals to become a Nurse Aide. This course serves as the educational foundation for the Nursing Care Skills certificate. Aides render care to clients in hospitals, long-term care facilities, and in home-care. This course, and the Nursing Care Skills certificate, consists of 35 hours of course work in theory, 45 hours of laboratory activities, and 20 hours in a clinical agency. The course requires consistent attendance from 9:00 a.m. - 2:30 p.m. four days a week.

title: ELL Communication 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for NCELI-102C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: More Cake Decorating
description: Take your cake decorating skills to a new level in this intermediate class. Kristin Jablonski, an award winning pastry chef, will teach you how to stack a cake and other more advanced techniques in finishing work. Supplies list will be discussed in first class. Beginning Cake Decorating required.

title: Principles of Respiratory Care
description: An in-depth study of the cardiopulmonary system from fetal development through old age in health and disease. Topics include Respiratory Care history, cardiopulmonary anatomy and physiology, the effects of aging on the pulmonary system, basic pulmonary assessment and an introduction to basic pulmonary diseases

title: Recording Studio Engineering 1
description: This course provides hands-on experience in digital audio engineering. Students will learn to engineer recording sessions by working in an actual recording studio with live musicians. The course covers basic techniques in setting up recording sessions, preparing computer files, microphone usage and placement, digitizing principals, audio acquisition options, midi integration and session management.

title: Media Camp
description: Make your mark on the world of media and have loads of fun in the process. Campers get a front row seat to learn about the magic of film and television, how a real radio station works, the art of telling a story or delivering news through the written script, and a number of performance techniques including character voices, newscasts, and interviews. Students will get hands-on opportunities to experience what their favorite actors, writers, editors, directors, newscasters and DJs do in the media field, from writing scripts, shooting footage, lighting, and editing to performing commercials, news and improve on camera and the radio.

title: DC and AC Motors
description: Covers DC and AC motor fundamentals. Includes DC generators; series, shunt, and compound DC motors; single-phase and three-phase AC induction motors; and synchronous, wound rotor, and universal motors. Compares AC alternators to DC generators.

title: Estas Al Aire
description: Voice Over hoy en día es una profesión de gran demanda!!! No tienes que ser actor para realizar trabajos como Voice Over, aunque es una ventaja si lo eres. Grandes talentos notables como James Earl Jones, Linda Hunt, y Donal Sutehrland utilizan sus voces para comerciales, películas y videos, cualquiera pensaría que sería imposible entrar en esta lucrativa profesión. No es asi!!! En nuestra clase aprenderas todo lo relacionado a la verdadera forma de entrar en el fascinante mundo de Voice Over, vencer a la competencia y convertir tu carrera de voice over en un excitoso trabajo de medio tiempo ¡!

title: Relief for Headaches (nurses)
description: If you are one of the millions of people who suffer regularly with headaches and/or migraines, relief is available. Come to hear Dr. Barry Hobbs discuss new research about the causes of headaches and migraines. He will also discuss the symptoms, treatments, and alternative therapies for these painful challenges. 1.5 nursing contact hours.

title: Condo Living
description: Do the benefits of condominium living entice you? From first-time homeowners to empty-nesters to retirees, condo ownership is becoming more and more popular. If you are considering purchasing a condo, learn from an individual who has served for 4 years as President of a large Detroit suburban complex tips on what to look for before closing the sale. If you are currently living in a condo or serve on an association board of directors, this is your opportunity to learn about management companies, legal issues, financial futures of your association, and more.

title: Indust Appl Trigonomic Princip
description: Focuses on trigonometric principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Covers trigonometric functions, solution of right triangles, solution of oblique triangles, and problem solving techniques.

title: Continuing Digital Photography
description: This 4 week course is based on student interaction inside and outside the classroom. Students will be required to complete assignments to be discussed in detail in class. Learn how to shoot on manual mode, basic differences of lenses, more detailed composition, how to use your on-camera flash, photo critique, and other details not covered in the first class. Please bring your manual, fully charged camera, memory card, and a small collection of photographs taken since the first completed course for review, critique and questions! Prerequisite: Basic Digital Photography for SLR or permission of instructor.
prerequisite_text: Basic Digital Photography for SLR or permission of instructor.

title: Jazz Improvisation 1
description: First of four semesters of jazz improvisation in which skills are developed through the study of jazz theory and the performances of a variety of jazz styles in a small group, jazz combo setting.

title: Computers for Seniors/Beginner
description: So you took Getting Started with Computers for Seniors & Beginners and now you want more! In this new class you will learn concepts and skills needed for working with the web, how to utilize your email and use the MS Office tools and accessories. Take a look at how to manage files and use keyboard shortcuts. Prerequisite: Getting Started with Computers for Seniors and Beginners/Part I or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Getting Started with Computers for Seniors and Beginners/Part I or equivalent knowledge.

title: Photoshop Essentials & More
description: Continue developing your Photoshop CS skills for printing photos, creating illustrations, or enhancing websites. Discover some of the tricks professionals use to erase the background of a photo and replace it with another, solve brightness and contrast issues, add text to a photographic layout, restore damaged vintage photos, and use filters and various artistic enhancements. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, Photoshop Essentials or equivalent experience. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, Photoshop Essentials or equivalent experience. Text provided.

title: Creative Writing Workshop
description: If you are eager to express yourself through writing, this workshop is for you. As you practice a variety of writing strategies, you will develop confidence, enhance observation skills and gain fluency. Collaborate with other participants as you exchange ideas and respond to each other's work. You will also learn to shape your writing in imaginative and inventive ways. Through expressive, well-developed ideas, you will discover the meaning of your life even as you learn to hone language into a lively and precise style that is yours alone.

title: Intermediate Windows Vista
description: Master the skills you need to work with files, folders, disks, programs, and shortcuts in Windows Vista.

title: Beginning Lead Glass
description: Have you long admired the timeless, elegant look of leaded glass doors and windows? Karen Firosz, a certified stained glass instructor, will teach you how to design and make a leaded glass project using the lead-came method. Basic tools will be available for use in class. Supply list (approximately $25) will be discussed in the first class. If you hae a chronic health condition, please consult your physician about lead-exposure risks. Women who are pregnant or nursing should not attend. Limited to 10 students.

title: Intro Industrial Drafting
description: Introduces the use of drafting instruments, lettering technique, geometric construction, orthographic projection, pictorial drawing, basic dimensioning for manufacturing, sectioning, auxiliaries, an introduction to assembly drawings, print reading, and interpretation of drawings.

title: Access 3
description: The Level 3 course is for the individual whose job responsibilities include working with heavily related tables; creating advanced queries, forms, and reports; writing macros to automate common tasks; and performing general database maintenance. Prerequisite: Access 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text included.
prerequisite_text: Access 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text included.

title: Continuing Drawing
description: 03 Aug 2012 11:14 AM Pauline Barbas Continue developing your drawing skills, knowledge, and aptitude as you explore self portraits, composition, collage, color theory, and more. Some drawing experience helpful. Supplies purchased by student ($40 - $50); list provided in the first class.

title: Arabic for Business & Pleasure
description: With one of the largest concentrations of Arabic speaking people in the US right here in southeast Michigan, why not learn a bit of the language to make shopping, dining, business transactions, and communicating with co-workers and neighbors an enriching experience. Take advantage of this opportunity to learn about the Arab culture and how it relates to everyday life and business. Text included.

title: Abs, Glutes & Thighs
description: Burn fat... tighten your abs... slim and tone your inner and outer thighs... lift your derriere! This challenging but fun workout sculpts and tightens your lower body. Increase your energy level and feel better in an amazingly short time. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Introduction to Telecommunicat
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-131 to register for this course. A survey course investigating the various electronic communication media, as well as print media, from historical, economic, ethical and social viewpoints.

title: Employment Testing Preparation
description: DTE preparation workshops are designed to provide exposure and preparation for the employment battery testing process. Workshop will offer academic instruction, exposure to daily tasks, and STAR interview test preparation.

title: Voice and Movement
description: Musical TheaterTechniques: Voice and Movement.

title: Advanced Topics Skilled Trades
description: Provide the skilled trades community the opportunity to receive advanced specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics will be offered as identified by the Skilled Trades & Apprenticeship Division faculty, ST&AD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the ST&AD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Learners may repeat the course for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Differentiating K-12 Assessmen
description: Differentiated assessment is the key to helping every student succeed-and no matter what grade you're teaching, this course will give you the tools you need to gather your assessment data quickly and easily. You'll begin by exploring strategies for performing pre-assessments, formative (ongoing) assessments, and summative assessments. Next, you'll discover how to identify and share learning goals, gather assessment information, adjust your instruction, and provide feedback to your students. Whether you're a new or experienced teacher, this course will provide you with a wealth of practical ideas you can start using in your classroom right away. This is a 6 week online course.

title: Wines of the World
description: Examines the major grape varieties, the effect of soil and climate, classification system, and the unique methods of various wine makers. Also focuses on the major wine producing areas, giving complete guidelines for reading a wine label as well as purchasing, storing, and serving.

title: International Cooking
description: Presents a comprehensive overview of cuisines throughout the world. Explores how demographic changes and the accessibility of travel have altered America's cultural and culinary perspectives of the world at large.

title: Elementary Spanish II
description: Further builds reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Spanish, focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops students' knowledge of vocabulary, pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express everyday ideas in both spoken and written Spanish. Presents Hispanic culture.

title: Desc Geometry: Lines & Planes
description: Covers basic descriptive geometry theory, and explores how to use orthographic principles to find true views of lines and planes.

title: Principles of Design
description: An introduction to the principles and elements of design that provide the foundation for aesthetic concepts in the interior design profession. Development of professional visual presentation techniques through the creation of interior design boards and models. Introduction to the design process and aesthetic conceptualization techniques.

title: Office Ready!
description: Office ready is a non-credit certificate program designed to train students in Microsoft Office Word 2010 and customer service skills that will help students handle specific customer service chanllenges. Students will be trained in on day workshops in Microsoft Office Word 2010 Levels 1, 2, and 3; Microsoft Office Excel 2010 Levels 1, 2, and 3, and Microsoft Office PowerPoint Levels 1 &2. the customer service portion included in this program is a two day workshop that will focus on learning timps and techniques that help best meet the customer's needs. The skills incluce deternining what to do and how to manage the customer interchange. The use of practical self-management skills, excercises and simulations will help each participant learn to solve their customer service chanllenges.15 May 2014 2:33 PM Arlicia Summerville

title: Visionary Art
description: Through Esther McCormick's classes such as Pain Relief at Your Fingertips many people have learned the basics of acupressure in pain management. As she has talked with students they have asked for a chance to meet and discuss their experiences in a support group atmosphere. It is an opportunity to learn from each other as well as refine techniques that might provide additional pain relief. Fees will be charged on a walk-in/per class basis.

title: Intro to JSP Programming
description: JSP is the language of choice for developers looking to create dynamic Web pages. JSP applications power many of today's top Web sites. In this course, you'll learn how to create JSP code and use it to access data stored in a Web application or in a database. You'll be able to create and manage cookies, handle forms, display custom data and more!

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories as you work your upper body, lower body, lower body, and abdominals. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat.

title: Stress Management
description: This course integrates research and theories on the nature of normal and abnormal stress, their impact on physical and mental health, and effective management strategies. It examines intrapersonal and interpersonal stressors, in family, work, and society settings. It will guide students to identify specific attitudes, behaviors, and coping skills as well as target areas for improvement.

title: Fit for Life
description: Experience the value of fitness and the benefits of activity by developing strength, endurance, and flexibility at a comfortable pace. Exercises are designed with seniors in mind and are adaptable to individual fitness levels.

title: Medical Office Externship
description: Offers a capstone clinical externship for the medical assistant student, including 165 hours of clinical experience at an assigned ambulatory care facility. The course Includes a structured class component.

title: Honors Directed Study
description: Challenges sophomore students to apply knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Coursework includes presenting a research summary before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty at the end of each semester.

title: EMT-Basic Fundamentals
description: Presents the technical knowledge and skills necessary for certification as a Basic Emergency Medical Technician (EMT-B). Focuses on Basic Life Support (CPR), airway management, medical emergencies, trauma, disaster management, shock and resuscitation, emergency childbirth, psychological emergencies, as well as extrications and more. Meets the recommendations of the United States Department of Transportation, National Traffic Safety Administration, National Emergency Medical Services Core Content, Scope of Practice Model, National Education Standards, and the Committee on Accreditation of Educational Programs for the Emergency Medical Services Professions Curriculum Supplement. NOTE: Successful completion of this course is required for individuals to be eligible to take the National Registry Certifying Exam for EMT-Basics; this exam is not included in this course.

title: Radiographic Positioning
description: This lecture/laboratory course is one of a four course curriculum in radiographic positioning and procedures. This course introduces fundamental radiology positioning terminology and provides instruction in standard imaging procedures for chest, abdomen, and upper extremity. Positioning skills will be practiced and reinforced through laboratory assignments. Consideration is given to the evaluation of optimal diagnostic images.

title: Get the "Red" Out
description: Explore the effects of anger on your mind and body in this two-hour workshop by biochemist Sandy Baumann. Contrary to what is often taught, venting anger is not always the best approach. Come learn anger-reducing techniques that will not damage your health or relationships.

title: Spec Topics in Electrical Tech
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current electrical technology trends. The specific special topics and the prerequisites will be announced each term. Course may be repeated when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Career Exploration
description: Examines how to perform a career assessment, create a genogram, and research potential career opportunities.

title: Chinese Medicine in Your Life
description: Gain additional insight into traditional Chinese medicine as you explore the pathways and relationships of the organ meridian system. Practice simple effective methods to address everyday ailments using the subtle energetics of acupressure therapeutics. Wear loose comfortable clothing.

title: Musical Theater Techniques
description: Production of Oliver.

title: Cartooning-Humor to Your World
description: Have you always wanted to draw cartoons, either for fn or profit? Attend this class and enter the wonderful world of 'tooning! Using a variety of methods your instructor will teach you the basic terms and techniques of the cartoon trade. You'll learn how to sketch and compose using various styles and techniques, create your own cartoons, and market and sell your work in the industry.

title: Easy Appetizers for Entertain
description: For the beginning cook. Learn some easy appetizers for your spring and summer entertaining. Leonard Charla will be teaching you how to make ten tantalizing treats per session.

title: Holiday Centerpiece
description: Learn how to create a professional looking holiday centerpiece to brighten your home this holiday season. Professional designer Susan Muldowney of Black Eyed Susans will show you how in this hands-on class. Bring $13.00 for supplies and a pair of garden clippers.

title: Hospitality & Travel Marketing
description: Explores the need and value of a cooperative marketing effort among hotels, airlines, restaurants, travel agents, and other important industry groups. Discusses research and analysis, the development and implementation of marketing plans and strategies, advertising, promotions, public relations, and pricing structures.

title: Revolutionize Your Retirement
description: A new breed of retirees is redefining retirement as a period for fulling life dreams by exploring new careers, enjoying the rewards of volunteering, taking up new adventures or hobbies, and creating home businesses. In this exciting class, psychologist Sue-Anne Sweeney will assist you in discovering new options and finding resources to help you plan for this new chapter of your life. She will also help you explore the social, emotional and health issues that may affect retirees. This is not a financial-planning seminar. Retirees and those soon to retire are invited. You may also enjoy Discover Your Passion.

title: Italian Christmas Traditions
description: No need to travel to Italy to experience an Italian Christmas! Spend an evening with Don Samull viewing slides taken during a holiday spent in Rome and the surrounding area. View a display of Christmas artifacts as you sample a delicious panetonne coffee cake. The following Saturday enjoy an Italian Christmas meal of a seafood appetizer, tortellini, soup, & salad at a well-know Dearborn Italian restaurant. Buon natale!

title: Basic Glass Fusing
description: Want to do something different with your jewelry? Then this is the class for you! In Basic Glass Fusing you will learn about compatibility, devitrification and annealing. Learn the basics of forming glass in a kiln. Melt layers of glass together to form gorgeous pendants and cabochons. Each student will complete at least one fused pendant and one pair of earrings. $20 materials fee due to instructor in class. Limited to 12.

title: Buy & Sell on Ebay - Beginners
description: eBay has become the world's online auction king-an international marketplace where people meet to buy and sell everything! Here's your chance to learn to make money by selling things like unused gifts or vintage items from an estate. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer, will teach you how to locate excellent deals on hard-to-find items, reach millions of potential buyers for your items, maximize your selling price, minimize your costs as a buyer, and more. Basic computer skills needed.

title: Fuss-Free Party Foods
description: Appetizers...hors d'oeuvres...finger foods. Whatever you call them, they're not just a before-dinner treat any more. They can carry a party all by themselves. Whether you're planning a graduation party, shower or other get together, let Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estate, show you some of his favoite easy, but elegant party foods. Come hungry.

title: History of Arab Art & Architec
description: Presents an overview of the art, architecture, culture, and history of the Arab world. Discusses the history and development of Arab visual art and architecture from around 1000 BCE to the present time. Explains the relationship between the artistic forms of Arab art with different religious and political factors as well as the impact of different cultures on the identity of Arab art. A field trip to a major museum is required.

title: Surgical Techniques I
description: Laboratory course designed to develop basic aseptic and sterile techniques used in the perioperative care of the surgical patient. This course focuses on the surgical scrub, gowning and gloving, setting up a sterile field, draping, handling of surgical instruments, and surgical counts. Students practice techniques in a guided lab setting.

title: Indst. App of Algebraic Prin
description: Emphasizes mastery of basic algebraic principles as related to the industrial environment. Covers symbols, positive and negative numbers, equations, exponents, roots, and formulas. Non-credit equivilent to TAMA 120

title: Arms, Elbows, Wrists & Hands
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols are used to relieve pain in arms, elbows, wrists and hands (carpal tunnel syndrome). Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve aches and pains - without taking drugs.

title: Intro Digital Photo-Slr Users
description: Have a digital SLR cmera and are ready to start getting the most out of it? In this course learn about the exposure triange: ISO, Aperture and Shutterspeed. Learn how to control your camera's settings and get amazing photographs. Learn the digital camera terminology and learn how to navigate your DSL camera! Class includes hands-on exercises and photo critiques. Required: Digital SLR camera or a camera with program modes such as Av (Apperture Priority), Tv (shutterspeed Priority) and M (full manual modes).

title: History of Jewelry
description: Mary MacDonald, Dearborn historian, will present an exciting evening describing the history of jewelry through time. She will show a slide presentation and bring some jewelry samples. Mary will cover some famous jewels like the Hope Diamond, and precious stones like diamonds, emeralds and rubies. She will also discuss prehistoric jewelry, nobility jewelry of kings and queens, costume jewelry, patriotic jewelry, and WWII jewelry. Join Mary for a dazzling evening of jewelry history.

title: Mouth Watering Desserts
description: Candy canes are great for hanging on a tree, but if you really want to satisfy your sweet tooth this season, try a luscious homemade holiday dessert. Spend an evening with Peter Veach, Executive Chef at the Henry Ford Estate. Learn to prepare some of his favorite desserts, including creme brulee, chocolate mousse, cranberry cheesecake, and an assortment of miniature pastries. Limited to 16.

title: Holiday Sweets & Tiny Treats
description: Get ready for a one-of-a-kind holiday cooking extravaganza. Just in time for the holidays, Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will amaze you with this part cookie exchange, part hors d'doeuvres delight class. You'll learn to bake and display a variety of cookies, including angel wings, birds next, a Polish treat and more. Then take some home to enjoy. You'll also make a unique Christmas tree stand suitable for displaying various hors d'oeuvres. Don't miss this one! Bring an apron. All supplies included.

title: Hear Rick Bloom
description: Because developing nations are gobbling resources and commodities, successful investors think globally. For a crash course about opportunities in precious metals, natural resources like oil and gas, mutual funds that feature commodities, and real estate investment trusts, come to hear Rick Bloom, host of a popular radio show on investing. Selected as one of the top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine, Rick will give you timely investment strategies, a list of his latest "Rick's Picks," and a wealth of handouts.

title: Therapeutic Massage Techiques
description: Are you living with ongoing pain? Do you have pain on a daily basis in your low back, neck, shoulders or hands? Is regular massage to get rid of your pain not in your budget? Combining 9 years of experience and 8 modalities of bodywork, Deborah will teach you the best way to get rid of that ongoing pain wherever you feel it. Sign up a partner and learn easy, effective and money saving approaches to feel great, improve circulation and eliminate your pain!

title: How Talk With Terminally Ill
description: In this compassionate and insightful class with Karen Ludwig, a family support social worker from Arbor Hospice and Home Care, you will gain skills to help a terminally ill friend, relative or client deal with his or her impending death. Gain clues about mysterious words and actions of dying persons. Find out about the physical signs that death is near, and learn how to support and comfort dying persons and their families. Get helpful strategies to cope with your own emotions as well.

title: Continuing Pilates
description: Continue sculpting your body in this class design- ed for those who have taken the beginning pilates class or already are familiar with the method. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfort- able, stretchable clothing.

title: Get Start Wth Comp- Sen & Begi
description: Around the world in 80 seconds! The internet helps you communicate instantly with family and friends, find travel bargains, shop for the best deals, and research any topic, including health questions. In this class you will learn to surf the internet to find information, bookmark favorite sites, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. You will have time to practice on the internet in class, so bring your questions. Open to persons of any age. Prerequisite: Getting Started with Computers or Intro to Computers with Windows.
prerequisite_text: Getting Started with Computers or Intro to Computers with Windows.

title: Powerpoint Basics & Beyond
description: Why limit yourself to dull text-based slide presentations if you can enhance them with graphics? Discover the tools for designed exciting slides with drawings, clipart and photographs. Learn creative techniques to reposition, group and align content to give your slides that extra pizzazz. Identify the value of using the master slide feature. Create a slideshow. The instructor is Microsoft certified and known as a PowerPoint "guru." Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge.

title: Portrait Drawing (ages 12-15)
description: If you love art and drawing, accelerate your skills with this class. Every aspect of portrait drawing will be covered, from working on the small details to large, finished works of art. You will be drawing from pictures, mirrors and real life. Supplies included.

title: Brew Beer At Home
description: Interested in the creatively or economy of making your own beer? If you've been considering this as a hobby, learn the basics of beer making in this three-hour introductory workshop. Home brewer Allan Geisler will describe the process, alert you to likely mistakes, share helpful tips and, of course, some samples. He will also discuss the ingredients and equipment needed, sanitation procedures and resources. Students must be at leat 21 years old to enroll.

title: Public Policy: Topics & Analys
description: A special topics course that allows students to explore issues of public policy. Focuses on the basics of public policy including agenda setting, policy formulation, policy implementation, and policy evaluation. Themes may vary. Themes may be specific such as healthcare policy or they might be more general such as state and local economic policy. Guest speakers.

title: Cake Decorating-Beginners
description: Have you always wanted to learn this beautiful culinary art? In this fun class you'll create beautiful icing shell and star borders, flowers, leaves, lacework and more! You'll also discover "secret" methods to properly ice, fill and assemble tiered cakes. Popular products and tools cake decorators use will be discussed as well. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx $12-15).

title: Pcs for Seniors & Beginners- 2
description: Continue building on the skills introduced in Part I and learn to create a folder on a diskette; save a WordPad file to a folder on a diskette; move a file from one folder to another on a diskette; and create, move, and restore a folder on the Desktop. In addition, you will touch on the basics of using the Internet, including browsing to locate a web site, using common search engines, and sending and receiving e-mail messages. Textbook included. Prerequisite: CLNC 2061.
prerequisite_text: CLNC 2061.

title: NC Heathing Technology
description: Prepares students as service technicians on residential and light commercial gas, oil, and electric forced-air and hot-water heating systems and related equipment. Lab experiences duplicate workplace skills accounting for 40-60% of the course including familiarization with equipment and systems; planned service checking; troubleshooting; and consideration for installation, repair, and replacement of components using appropriate service tools and instruments. Comparable to ENT 104.

title: Coupon-Ology
description: You will learn the basics of couponing - where to find them and how to use them at your local grocery stores. We will look at current grocery store sale ads and apply what we learn to the sale prices so we can see how we can maximize our savings. Join Elvira Rivera, the "Coupon Connoisseur", and learn the do's and don'ts of the coupon connection.

title: New Earth Reiki
description: New Earth Reiki is a new and powerful energy sent to assist those who are ready to shift into a higher frequency of being! The 2012 vibrations are here, and with New Earth Reiki the process of merging with them can be integrated more easily and efficiently. Come experience the upgrade! Manual, certificate and attunement included. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. Prerequisite: Reiki Level 2. For more info, go to myspace.com/newearththerapy.
prerequisite_text: Reiki Level 2. For more info, go to myspace.com/newearththerapy.

title: EIF Prep Course
description: Designed to prepare students for the CEWD Energy Industry Fundamentals exam. The core competencies of the exam are presented. They include: reading comprehension, mathematics, mechanical and spatial reasoning, and test-taking strategies.

title: Intro Digital Photo Output!
description: Do you love your digital camera, but can't seem to get the photos to display or print correctly? In this course, you'll learn how to edit and crop unwanted portions of your photos, resize them, adjust their colors, add special effects, print at the highest possible quality, e-mail digital photos to friends and family, and more.

title: History of Collecting
description: When did accumulating "stuff" transform into acquiring collectibles? Why is collecting so popular? Join Mary McDonald, Dearborn historian, for a fun and fact-filled discussion on the evolution of this world-wide pastime. Learn about markings and what collections will likely be of value in the future. This class is a must for all collectors. Bring pen and paper to take notes.

title: Contemporary Social Problems
description: Focuses on understanding current social problems in U.S. society, such as economic, racial, and gender inequality, from a sociological perspective.
