Because the course relates to social issues, the exact set of problems covered can change from semester-to-semester. Other issues covered in recent semesters include climate change, crime, and social movements intended to solve social problems. The primary goals of this course are increasing student awareness of social problems and their causes, as well as possible solutions.

title: Pre-Apprenticeship Pharm Tech
description: Provides non-credit training as a pre-apprenticeship entry-level pharmacy technician. Pharmacy technicians are skilled health specialists who, under the direction and supervision of a pharmacist, assist in the varied activities of a pharmacy department. The students are provided with both classroom and laboratory skills practice in the area of pharmacy technician. Registration is restricted to those students who are participants in the Going Pro program.

title: Vocal Jazz Ensemble 4
description: Fourth of four sequential semesters of vocal jazz ensemble, covering preparation and performance of a variety of jazz and popular music in an ensemble of 8-20 voices. Group performs for various HFC and community functions.

title: What STEMS from Here?
description: Join us this summer to find out "What STEMS from Here? This hands-on camp highlights robotics, design, and manufacturing in a way that you have never imagined. Learn about opportunities in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) as well as entrpreneurship.

title: Circuit Training - Beginners
description: Develop a good all-round level of fitness in minimal time! Circuit training combines various exercise activities to build your strength and stamina, promoting heart health as you move from one activity to the next. Its a great time saver, as well as a refreshing and fun change from the more monotonous types of exercise. Bring an exercise mat, towel and water. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Mich 2008 Real Estate Cont Ed
description: This course is approved by the Board of Real Estate Brokers and Salespeople for the required six hours of continuing education (Approval #100-J012-65, Topic #566 A, B, C & D). Topics include real estate mortgage fraud, real estate law cases, national industry updates, and the required two-hour core curriculum legal update. Participants may show up and register the day of the class.

title: Pastels & Other Color Media
description: Whether you are beginning your creative journey or returning to it, learn to draw or paint with pastels to create richly colored, textured works of art. In this eight-week course, Kathy Boettcher, an experienced art instructor, will help you "see like an artist" and understand color, light effects, composition and more. Bring charcoal paper in various values (especially mid-value grey) and pastels. Individuals using other color media are also welcome.

title: Charm Bracelet
description: Design and make a stunning charm bracelet using a technique that will virtually guarantee that your charms will never fall off. You'll use a combination of semi-precious stones, crystal glass, sterling silver and other fun items to embellish the bracelet. Additional charms can be added later to enhance its beauty. Such a bracelet might sell for over $100 in a fine jewelry store. $35 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Bike Repair/Maint
description: Does your bicycle need a tune-up? If so, meet the Bicycle Doctor and learn the basics of doing it yourself. Session one will cover tube and tire repair and bearing adjustment. In session two, learn brake adjustment and minor truing. In session three, learn how to adjust gears. Bring your bicycle and any appropriate tools (if you have them) to class.

title: Ask a Foot & Ankle Specialist
description: In this exciting new class, join Dr. Lori Duncan, podiatrist, to learn how to ease the pain in your feet, ankles and calves. She will talk about the latest treatments for corns, bunions, fungal infections of the feet, hammertoes, athletic injuries to the feet, plantar warts, etc. Special attention will be given to the cause of heel pain, foot care for persons with diabetes, arch supports and much more! After her presentation Dr. Duncan will answer your questions about foot care.

title: Machu Picchu & Land of Incas
description: Join our favorite traveler, Doug Denard, as he takes you through Cusco, Peru ("earth's navel", in the native language). The ancient city of Peru is the gateway to the Sacred Valley and the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu, the breath-taking stone fortress of the Andes. Study the architecture of Machu Picchu and the ceremonial sites as you learn of the ancient history and see how it relates to the spiritual aspects of their culture.

title: Paint With Acrylics (ages 6-8)
description: Welcome to the world of color! Come create your own painting masterpieces using the colors of the rainbow. Let nature inspire you to create pictures of animals, plants, flowers, trees, rivers, lakes - anything you find in nature. All supplies are included. Also, remember to come dressed to mess!

title: Start Emailing Family & Friend
description: Find out how easy it is to email family and friends. Learn to use email features to enhance your email. Instructor and students will also send, reply and forward email right in the class! Understand how to attach a file or picture to your email. Get rid of annoying multiple names in the header of your email before forwarding your message.

title: Ethnic & Racial Diversity
description: Introduces students to the sociological study of ethnic and racial groups. Students explore key concepts and issues in connection with definitions of race and ethnicity, immigration, patterns of group inequality and domination, discrimination and prejudice. Focuses on the diversity ofU.S.society and explores the experiences of Native Americans and immigrant groups, including those from Africa, Asia, andEurope. Emphasis on current patterns and consequences of immigration will be studied. Ethnic and racial diversity in other societies will also be explored.

title: Film History and Criticism
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-132 to register for this course. Introduces the film medium and the film experience as entertainment, artistic expression, and social communication, through examination of production techniques, critical theories, and historic & modern examples.

title: Industrial Detail
description: Explores the making of working drawings using proper dimensioning techniques. Showing necessary views, placement of dimensions, use of different dimensioning styles, and calculation of tolerances are the basis of instruction. The function and relationship of mating parts in an assembly are considered when dimensioning to insure proper fit and function. Threads, fasteners, and common manufacturing operations are applied and dimensioned. Standard and commercial parts are selected from catalogs. Setup and application of CAD dimensioning styles and tolerances are utilized.

title: Trade Related Prep-PAPP
description: Preparation for a career in the trades is assessed through an overview of industrial processes. A review of math, blueprint reading and measuring tools relative to shop application are major components of the course. Students receive diagnostic evaluation through testing in these major areas.

title: Introduction to Knitting
description: The basics of knitting - to knit, purl, create stitch pattern and read patterns - are covered in this comprehensive course. Tips and tricks will be shared to make your projects true hand-crafted treasures. Upon finishing the course you will have the skills and confidence necessary to complete a beginner-to- easy- level project of your choosing. Bring a tape measure, scissors, yarn, and needles.

title: Basic eBay
description: Thinking about getting started with eBay? Come learn the fundamentals of buying and selling on eBay including how to get started, research your item, pricing, shipping, feedback and more. If you are ready for a great overview of the buying and selling process - plus specific tips and strategies - sign up now! Basic computer and internet skills are required.

title: Old Time Polish Sweets & Baked
description: Marilyn is bringing in a new class for your cooking pleasure. Join her as she makes Babka, a traditional Polish dessert that is a cross between a cake and a bread. Make Chrusick (deep-fried pastry sprinkled with icing sugar) also known as Angel Wings. Learn her techniques as she puts together a sweet yeast bread that will warm both heart and soul! Bring an apron and dress in layers as the kitchen tends to get a bit warm.

title: Ear Wires for Beginners
description: Make your own pierced ear wires for a fraction of what you'd pay for them in retail stores. Bring your tools to class and learn several techniques for making ear wires. $4 supply fee due to instructor in class.

title: Separat/Divor: Woman View
description: This eight-week seminar, led by Pauline Furman, Ph.D., is for women who are divorced, separated, or thinking about either process. This time of transition can be frightening and overwhelming. Support of other women can be crucial to the healing process and can be of tremendous help o the journey of self-discovery and personal growth. This discussion and support group will help women understand what they are experiencing and discover what options are available.

title: Mach Guard/JSA&Oper Train
description: This MIOSHA program involves a full day of safety training to address one of MIOSHA's Strategic Plan Goals: Reduce Amputations. Severed fingers, hands, and arms are a result of horrifying machinery-related injuries. This seminar will increase employer and worker awareness of these dangers and how to prevent them. Safeguards are essential for protecting workers from injury. Three areas have been identified as leading causes of these injuries: machine guarding, operator safety training, and lockout. Topics to be covered include hazard recognition (point of operation and power transmission), machine guards and safeguards, writing and using a JSA to conduct job-specific safety training, and requirements for eliminating hazards by locking out power while maintaining equipment.

title: Beginning Modern Dance
description: Explores Modern Dance as an art form that has a limitless way to express oneself in human movement. Primary emphasis is on developing technique of the basic Modern movement vocabulary. Course also covers skeletal muscular anatomy and function as it applies to dance movement, and an introduction to improvisation and composition.

title: A Hunger for Drawing
description: Food is a common human experience: we all must eat to survive and food ia a major theme in many of our lives. Aside from that, food also has the benefit of serving as subject matter in a way that can help one to develop their skills in drawing. By using food as inspiration we can develop our skills in fundamentals of drawing, such as line, space and an unconventional approach. It is recommended that you don't come to class hungry... unless you are hungry for drawing!

title: Refrig Recov Cert Exam
description: Sit for the EPA Refrigerant Recovery Examination during this two-hour course.

title: The Healing Power of Crystals
description: As children we loved to pick up rocks and play with them, little did we know that rocks, crystals and gemstones have unique powers to heal on 4 levels: Physical, Mental, Emotional and Spiritual. In this class, we will explore several of the popular gemstones and their healing power, learn the different ways to cleanse the crystal, and see methods of programming and cleaning the crystal. We will also talk about Aura Fields and how to heal yourself and others using different crystals. Kristy will provide 3 different crystals to students for $10 payable in class.

title: Spend Smart & Reduce Debt
description: Whether you have student loans to pay off, are overwhelmed by increased expenses, or are facing present or future unemployment, you can rein in your budget. Join Connie Costner from MSU Extension Wayne County to gain powerful but simple tools and techniques that have helped many people have money left over at the end of each month. Profit from Connie's money-stretching tips gained from financial research about credit cards, mortgages, loan reduction, spending limits and much more. Many free handouts included. Open to anyone. Invest in this class today to ensure a more financially secure future.

title: Microsoft Excel 2003
description: Discover how Excel can help you improve your effectiveness as a teacher.

title: Stage Your Home for Sale
description: If you are planning to put your home on the market this spring and it has the "lived-in" look, resembles the television set for "That '70s Show," or needs a general sprucing up, attend this timely seminar. Darlene Rothman, a professional home stager, will show you how to enhance the salability of your home using inexpensive tips from a new trend called "home staging." She'll discuss furniture arrangement, decluttering, paint colors, traffic flow and more. Feel free to bring pictures of your home.

title: ELL Communication 5
description: Develops intermediate-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-106C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Create Neat & Sweet Holiday
description: Make and take home a big tray full of goodies for your holiday table including a Mini "Snow" Covered Cream Puff Tree, Cheery Chocolate Mice and adorable White Chocolate Marshmallow Family, Sweet and Crunchy Peppermint Bark, Chocolate Covered Spoons (to flavor your coffee) and a festive Holiday mini Yule log. Call or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list. (approx $14-18)

title: Discover Ham Radio
description: Ever thought about becoming an amateur radio operator or "ham" as they're often called? Licensed amateur radio operators communicate with each other in nearby places, across the country, around the world, or even with astronauts in outer space via wireless technology. Join more than 600,000 hams in the US by preparing to pass the FCC Amateur Radio Exam. Sponsored by the Motor City Radio Club this 11-week class teaches you all you need to know to pass the test. For more information log onto www.w8mrm.org.

title: Best Srategies for Saving
description: Investing in the stock market might seem like a wild,roller-coaster ride, but it doesn't have to be if you learn the ins and outs of saving and investing. Join Rick Bloom for an up-to-date analysis of the best strategies for saving and investing. Rick was selected as one of the Top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine and has written a daily column on financial advice for a Detroit newspaper. He currently hosts a daily radio show on a local station.

title: Interpersonal Communication
description: This course explores the process of communication between individuals in formal and informal, face-to-face and digital settings across family, social, and work groups. Examines the social, linguistic, cultural, and psychological factors impacting interpersonal communication.

title: Do It Yourself Wedding Bouquet
description: Do you love DIY projects? This course will teach you how to take a simple bunch of flowers and turn them into a breathtaking wedding bouquet and bridesmaids bouquet that you get to take home and enjoy. No experience required! All tools and supplies will be provided. Registration for this class closes on November 5 so supplies can be ordered.

title: Co-op in Science
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major in STEM to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Find Your Way on the Internt
description: Still wondering what is the big deal about that Internet that everyone is talking about? Do you have fears and reservations about what might be lurking out there? Learn to conduct effective searches and find almost everything you need to know. Address issues of Internet security and privacy. Learn to use and understand your own email. Investigate web browsers and see what works best for you. What is Social Networking and is it a good idea foryou? Why are financial institutions requiring that you have "direct deposit" and how do you keep it safe? Prerequisites: basic computer skills. Students will also need an active email account and the ability to access the email account. (You must know the email address and the password.)
prerequisite_text: basic computer skills. Students will also need an active email account and the ability to access the email account. (You must know the email address and the password.)

title: Seated Exercise
description: Safely strengthen and tone your muscles while sitting in a chair! Low impact exercises, done while sitting in or holding onto a chair, will strengthen muscle groups and improve muscular endurance. See how quickly daily activities become easier! Benefits can include: improved posture, stronger bones, and decreased risk of injuries to the body. It's never too late to start rebuilding muscle tissue. Adults of all fitness levels welcome. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights.

title: Painting a Chickadee Bird
description: Do you enjoy painting or want to learn on a colorful bird? Enjoy learning dry brush techniques on a Black-capped Chickadee. Once done you can use this bird as an ornament, gift, or even a shade pull. All patterns, instructions and tips from a blue-ribbon woodcarver. Please bring to the first class a cup for water, paper towels, note book, painting apron or old shirt and $20 for supplies (bird kit & paint) payable to the instructor.

title: Neon Basics
description: Come visit with a traditional neon sign bender as he demonstrates how neon signs are made. Instructor Al Bodrie will take you through a series of steps with some hands on exposure to show you how each tube is hand manipulated to create that glowing sign. At the end of the session you will leave with a completed sample of neon work. Please wear cotton clothing, no hand jewelry (other than wedding bands). A materials fee of $10 is payable to the instructor in class. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Piano Basics for Adults
description: Don't let that keyboard sit in the corner collecting dust anymore! Have some fun learning how to play well-known classic and contemporary pieces. Under the direction of our instructor, you will become familiar with the keyboard, basic notes, hand positions, and music theory concepts. By the end of the course, you will be able to play popular songs using your left hand for chords and your right hand for melody. Text book included.

title: Intro Anthropology
description: Introduces the holistic study of humanity using a multi-faceted approach that draws from the discipline's four field: archaeology, physical, cultural, and linguistic anthropology. Course topics include human evolution and variability, cultural diversity and change, material culture and language. other themes explored include religion, kinship, social and political organization, the neolithic revolution, as well as the intellectual contributions of anthropology to the social sciences.

title: Glass Fusing
description: Explore the endless possibilities of creations that can be made by stacking, arranging and then fusing glass into patterns using squares, circles, stringers, dots and more. You will use layers of clear and colored sheet glass to produce a one of a kind fused glass tile. Each tile will be fired at the studio and available the following week for pickup. Please wear loose fitting cotton clothing. A materials fee of $15 is payable to the instructor in class. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Beginning Folk Dance
description: Experience the joy of dance; gain a better understanding of dance and music from people around the world. Folk dancing requires no partners, and dances will be presented in an easy-to-learn style. Wear comfortable clothing and shoes. No sandals please.

title: Cooking for One
description: Anyone who has ever cooked for just one person knows how challenging it can be. If you find yourself regularly buying frozen dinners or fast food, you need to learn some quick and easy ways to cook a variety of healthful meals for one. Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will share shopping tips and ideas for preparing ahead as she shows you how to marinate meats and make a simple stir fry, pasta dishes, and items you can freeze for later. Bring an apron, and come ready to cook and eat. Class limited to 14. Enroll early.

title: To Journey the Medicine Wheel
description: Native Americans define "medicine" as anything that improves one's connection to the Great Creator Spirit and to all life. The stores that take you around to the Great Creator Spirit and to all of life. The stories that take you around the wheel are dedicated to keeping a healthy balance between the body, mind and Spirit. Your "medicine" brings out personal power, strength and a deep understanding of the interconnectedness of all of life. This walk around the wheel will introduce you to yourself at every turning point of your life. Whether you are in the middle of a wilderness or a bustling city, the Medicine Wheel and its" teachings will help you find the gentle balance that comes from walking the path of the "good red road."

title: How to Recession-Proof Wallet
description: Are you tired of living check to check every month? Did you know that up to 40 percent of all Americans spend more money than they actually earn? Learn the secrets of wealth management and how the rich are able to create money-making opportunities with little or none of their own money. Additionally, you will learn how to work less, increase earnings, pay less in taxes, and much more. Don't miss out on this opportunity. Financial freedom is a choice... make it yours today. Join us!

title: ELL Reading & Listening 1
description: Introduces low-beginning-level college-preparatory reading and listening skills for non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course qualifies students for ELI-102R. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Mexican Party Food
description: Learn about Antojitos (appetizers) such as Botana, Guacamole, Salsas: grilled, raw, & stewed; and all the goodies that accompany them. For dessert, consider Mexican wedding cookies. You will enjoy making and tasting these delicious authentic Mexican dishes, under the supervision of Chef Alicia Bonilla-Arias. Please bring your apron and an appetite.

title: Soup's Up!
description: Functional Foods? Antioxidants? Phytochemicals???? From the earth to the table: old-fashioned home-cooking with modern nutrition sensibility. Prepare and enjoy three good-for-you, heart-warming soups that taste great: winterblast chickpea, leek-butternut squash and lentil-meatball. Dietitian/college instructor Lourdes Lore will discuss how the Slow Food Movement can transform your meals into nourishing, soulful dining rather than dwelling on calories, carbs and marketing claims on food labels. Bring an apron and an adventurous palate.

title: AC/DC Electricity
description: Covers the fundamentals of DC and AC circuits and circuit calculations. Examines electrical definitions, units of electrical measure, use of meters, series and parallel resistive circuits, capacitance, and inductance. Also explores basic wiring techniques and how to troubleshoot circuit faults.

title: Co-op in Hospitality
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible. To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5

title: Second-Year German III
description: Follows GER-132 and is the first of two intermediate-level German courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops students' reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German, expands their vocabulary, and deepens their knowledge of pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express essential ideas in both spoken and written German. Presents the cultures of German-speaking countries.

title: Six Paths to Best Time of Life
description: Midlife is a time to reassess your career, your life satisfaction, and your plans for retirement. Join June Strieby, career and midlife transition coach, for an eye-opening workshop on six positive, proactive ways to get the most enjoyment from the third age of your life. Learn about research to help you create a vibrant and satisfying life as you begin a new season of exciting possibilities. Share in lively discussions about finding and expressing your passions, exploring six principles for growth and renewal in this time of personal choices, and dispelling myths about aging and retirement.

title: Birding: Focus on Feeder Birds
description: Do you have a bird feeder? What types of birds visit in the winter? Do you know their names? Travel with veteran birder and author Allen Chartier for this first birding trip of the year to several locations to observe the birds that spend the winter with us.

title: Myths & Symbols:Deciphering
description: Introduces the academic study of religious myths. Examines spiritual and religious perspectives of cultures as sources of myths. Analyzes symbols, themes, and plots, enabling the student to identify common characteristics and patterns in myths originating in various cultures and religions throughout human history.

title: RT Clinical Sciences
description: This course focuses on the clinical application of science to the practice of respiratory care. Topics include oxygenation, acid-base balance, ventilation in health and disease, basic microbiology, common microbial pathogens seen with pulmonary infection and the physical principles of spontaneous and mechanical ventilation. Emphasis is placed on blood gas interpretation, capnography, arterial puncture techniques, and disinfection and sterilization of respiratory care equipment.

title: Surgical Techniques II
description: Laboratory course designed to prepare students for clinical externship. Students will continue to practice aseptic and sterile techniques used in the perioperative care of the surgical patient. This course focuses on skills developed in SRG-140 and incorporates "mock surgery" and advanced clinical skills in a lab setting. Students practice techniques in a guided lab setting.

title: Intro to UNIX Shell Script
description: An intermediate-level course covering the fundamentals of the UNIX Operating System, including the file system, editor, and standard UNIX utilities. Emphasizes how the UNIX shell operates, and also presents advanced forms of utilities, regular expressions, and shell scripts.

title: Intro to Industrial Hydraulics
description: Introduces the principles of fluid power as applied to industrial systems. Presents various hydraulic components and their functions within fluid power systems. Laboratory activities.

title: Get the Red Out (for Nurses)
description: Holding anger and grudges can have physiological effects on the mind and body. Biochemist Sandy Baumann will teach you these effects and discuss the many emotions often vented as anger. Learn to identify the steps of escalating anger in another person, and receive a wealth of helpful tips, including nutrition, to help you deal with anger. Discover how to let go of grudges and resentment and why it is important. $5 material fee due to instrucor the first session. 6 nursing contact hours.

title: Co-op in Business
description: Catalog Course Description Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Civil War, Class 1: Css Hunley
description: In 1864 the CSS H. L. Hunley was the first submarine to sink an enemy ship in combat That same night it was sunk and lost for more than 130 years. Its sailors remained at their battle stations preserved in a unique time capsule. Local resident and presenter, Larry Jackson, took a private tour of the submarine and talked extensively with the divers who made the discovery. He will regale you little known stories about the sailors, the politics and rivalries of the day. Larry Jackson belongs to many Civil War groups, has published numerous articles, and is an accomplished and entertaining speaker with a fascinating story to tell.

title: Adult Hip Hop (4 Weeks)
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise?

title: Explore Classics
description: If you would like to know more about the Baroque, Classical, and Romantic periods in music, here is your opportunity. Learn how to listen and trust your own response to the music. The elements of the music will be identified and considered. Composers to be heard will include Bach and Handel for the Baroque period; Mozart and Haydn for the Classical; and Beethoven, Schubert, and Wagner for the Romantic. No previous knowledge or experience needed! If there is interest, a field trip to a Detroit Symphony or Michigan Opera Theater performance could be arranged. The teacher, Jan VanDenburg, has degrees in music education and fine arts and is retired from Edsel Ford High School.

title: Construction Systems 1
description: This class focuses on both residential and commercial construction framing systems, techniques, materials and methods. Course work will be a mix of hands on construction, physical modeling, digital modeling, and building detailing.

title: Adult Ballet (4 Week)
description: Enter the magical world of ballet, one of the most beautiful forms of dance ever devised. Under the instruction of Stephanie Fullerton you will learn the basic steps, techniques and dance routines. Join other beginning ballet students and develop elegance, correct posture and flexibility in a fun, adult environment. Ballet shoes are required. Four week class.

title: Living- Glow of Posit.Thought
description: Our daily news is filled with high unemployment, foreclosures, budget deficits, school closings and bankruptcies. The negative thought and depressed mood of this recession creates a vortex of self-fulfilling gloom. We think what we hear, we hear what we think, our lives are our thoughts. To turn our economy, our state and our lives towards a brighter future we must harness the power of positive thought. Join us to learn how to live in the glow of positive thought. Experience through practical deomonstration how positive thinking really does create abundance and happiness. Learn to wield positive power through speech patterns, visualization, color and spatial manipulation as well as positive energy transfer.

title: Principles of Image Production
description: This course provides an in-depth study of the equipment and principles involved in the production of diagnostic radiographic images. Emphasis is placed on the design, construction, and operational characteristics of the diagnostic X-ray tube and digital imaging systems. The course explores the physical interactions within the X-ray tube that result in X-ray production, the properties and quality of the X-ray beam, and the parameters influencing radiographic image formation and evaluation.

title: Wrking W/Fractions: Fund Ideas
description: This course is designed to provide teachers with fundamental understanding of fractions used for teaching children at a variety of grades. It is designed as a workshop so that each session will include examination of one key element of fractions to study the mathematical elements, childrens work with this key element to study childrens understandings of this element, and curricula to study teachers resources to teach this element. Consistent with the Common Core State Standards, participants will explore mathematical problems in which fundamental ideas about fractions are embedded. They will examine cases of childrens work that has been used for professional development and cases from their teaching to see alternative ways to think about fractions. Additionally, teachers will work with the curricula from which they teach to design lessons about fractions.

title: Early American History
description: Covers Colonial America and the United States through the Civil War Period.

title: Make Over Life With Feng Shui
description: Improve your chances for good luck in many aspects of your life from money to relationships through Feng Shui, the ancient Chinese art of placement. Using a thoroughly modern approach you'll learn how to make simple, yet powerful, changes to your home and environment. Find your way through this fascinating subject as well as a better way through life. Madge Lawson, your instructor, is a graduate of New York's Sheffield School of Design and is a practicing Feng Shui consultant.

title: Social Security & Medicare Pla
description: This valuable workshop is designed especially for baby boomers. Tony LaJeunesse will instruct you on the 5 factors to consider when deciding when to apply for benefits. When it makes sense to delay benefits - and when it does not, and how to estimate your benefits. Why you should always accurately check your earnings. Two innovative strategies for coordinating benefits with your spouse. How to minimize taxes on Social Security benefits, and how to coordinate Social Security with your other sources of retirement income. How Medicare enrollment periods work and what you need to do to avoid late enrollment penalties. How much you can expect to pay in health care costs after going onto Medicare. How Medicare works with private insurance to provide comprehensive coverage. Why most people pay too much for private insurance and how to you can avoid excess costs. Why you must plan for higher health care costs in retirement, including the possibility of needing long-term care. The decisions you make today can have a tremendous bearing on the total amount of benefits you stand to receive over your lifetime. Presentation workbook is provided with class.

title: Start Ebay Drop Off Shore
description: Turn other people's unwanted items into a lucrative source of income by starting an eBay drop off store. Master the art of setting up a business, writing a marketing plan, taking super photos, creating dynamite listings, and offering great customer service. Thousands of people have transformed their eBay knowledge into profitable second income sources or even full time jobs. Take your skills to the next level and avoid the common pitfalls that keep amateur sellers from reaching their full potential.

title: Theatrical Production
description: This course is designed as a continuation of performance activities for theater students who have completed performance classes for college credit. Admission by administrative permission only. contact Mr. Gerard Dzuiblinski at 313-845-9634 for details.

title: Poetry & Prose: Call the Muse
description: Let the muse guide you as you write wonderful poetry inspired by your life and that of other writers. Cheryl Vatcher-Martin, a columnist, editor, award-winning poet and professor, will teach you how to turn an everyday experience or emotion into a powerful poem. Explore writing techniques that will help fill your notebook with poetry and prose. In this workshop you will practice your poetry and get feedback from your classmates. $5 material fee due to instructor in class.

title: Airbrushing
description: Explore the world of airbrushing and discover how versatile and fun this art form can be for pro- fessional artists as well as beginners. In just 6 weeks you will learn the basic techniques to crete great effects on surfaces such as t-shirts, motorcycles, hockey masks, ceramics, fingernails, paper, cars, and many more. Students supply their own airbrush and materials. A supply list will be mailed to registrants. Limited to 15.

title: Party Like the Stars
description: Average budget? Celebrity taste? Personalize your party for that special occasion - birthdays, anniversaries, or maybe just a girl's night out - and have fun doing it! Learn to plan a party that is stress free, create a "theme" party for children or adults for any occasion, and be able to give it "high style" on a reasonable budget. For over 10 years, instructor Tanya Hughes has been a successful event planner, decorating for many corporate and private events. She will share her secrets with you and challenge you to get creative. Come on, let's party!

title: MAP Leadership Foundations
description: This course provides salaried employees with foundation knowledge of the processes and systems that together create a capable MAP Leadership Team.

title: Introduction to Archaeology
description: Introduces the branch of anthropology that relates to the study of past societies, cultures, and civilizations. Explores the role of excavation, survey, material culture analysis, dating methods, and interpretation of artifacts, features, inscriptions, and monumental architecture in the ancient world. Examines developments and archaeological discoveries related to various historical periods ranging from the era of pre-human history to the emergence of state-level societies in places such as ancient Mesopotamia, Egypt, and China. Part of the course is also dedicated to familiarizing students with studies and local projects related to the archaeology of Detroit.

title: Hfv - Gentle Yoga
description: Experience the health benefits of yoga in this calming, therapeutic class. Designed and presented by professional yoga instructor Dianna Kinaschuk for beginners, returning practitioners, seniors, persons with limited mobility and flexibility, and those recovering from illness or injury. Gentle Yoga offers the postures and breathing techniques of Hatha Yoga in a format that can be modified to fit your needs and abilities, and at a gentle, slow pace. Open to persons of all ages and levels. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Hatha Yoga 2
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, bala includes stretching, breathing, posturing and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga, your body will evelop flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment; and you will achieve mental concentration relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Pract Plumbing Lab-State Lic P
description: For the apprentice and journeyman. Covers the subject of pipes and tubes, their uses, the materials of which they are made, and some of the related general specifications. Also covers existing and new process skills related to plumbing-pipefitting. Course is 25% lecture related to safety, tools, equipment, and demonstrations prepping; the rest of the course emphasizes laboratory activities.

title: Intro to Financial Accounting
description: Introduces basic financial accounting principles including the accounting cycle; merchandise accounting; income, asset, and liability measurements; and preparation and evaluation of financial statements.

title: Rebuild Your Bones Naturally
description: Osteoporosis affects both men and women and produces 1.5 million fractures annually. To reduce your risk for fractures and osteoporosis, join biochemist Sandy Baumann to discover which common beverages, drugs and medical conditions rob bones. Find out which mineral is more important than calcium for bone density. Sign up now to learn natural strategies, including vitamins, minerals, hormones, supplements and lifestyle changes to strengthen bones at any age. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. Bring your bone density report.

title: Teach Water Qual Monitor Wkshp
description: One-day workshop to provide teachers from the Rouge Education Workshop with the knowledge and skill to lead students in safely conducting water quality monitoring of the Rouge River.

title: Blues Guitar
description: Come explore all aspects of "The Blues" as it relates to its performance on the guitar, and jam with renowned Detroit area musician Robert Penn. You will learn the importance of the guitar as well as theoretical principles that are characteristic of this particular genre. Examine various styles of different players as well as their approaches to improvising and techniques. The instructor will include the work of B.B. King, Jimi Hendricks, T-Bone Walker and artists of like importance. Another area of focus will be the pentatonic blues scales and its application to the I, IV, V Blues progression. Bring your acoustic or electric guitars and a music stand if you have one. Prerequisite: at least one year of basic guitar.
prerequisite_text: at least one year of basic guitar.

title: Create Gourmet Caramel Apples
description: You've seen these exquisite (and expensive!) applies for sale in upscale department stores and on cable TV shopping shows. Sinfully sweet caramel enrobed apples covered with layer upon layer of assorted flavors of luxurious chocolate, heavily sprinkled with loads of crunchy, chewy toppings, and drizzled with even more chocolate! In this class you'll create five different gourmet caramel applies using four different kinds of chocolate and several assorted toppings. Gourmet applies make wonderful gifts for people of all ages. They're great party favors too. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-$18).

title: Video Game Design & Developmt
description: The Video Game Design and Development online program is the perfect starting place for students who are seeking to break in to the exciting gaming industry. This online program lays the groundwork for students who wish to learn the high-end techniques and engineering principles behind modern video game technology. Using a comprehensive and analytical approach to game development principles and techniques, students will begin to develop the skills that are necessary to implement their own game ideas.

title: Basic Reading
description: WFPD-ENG079 is a developmental course to prepare native English speakers to read at an acceptable level for English 081. Students will improve their reading comprehension skills by engaging with a variety of fiction and non-fiction texts and focusing on vocabulary improvement. Learning lab and/or online work is required. Students who do not place into ENG-081 with a minimum placement Reading Test score must take WFPD-ENG079. Students must successfully meet all objectives (receive a grade of OJM) in WFPD-ENG079 before enrolling in English 081.

title: PowerPoint: Get Photos
description: Don't have Photoshop? This hands-on training is great for PowerPoint users and amateur photographers. Learn easy, yet powerful, photo editing features in PowerPoint. Use the Picture Tools to create several new pictures from a single photograph. Modify photos - resize, crop, reformat and group them. Create wallpaper backgrounds. Use the Photo Album feature. Download images from Microsoft.com. Change color photos into black & white or grayscale images. Convert pictures into image files with MS Picture Manager. Reduce the size of image files for faster uploading speed to the Web.

title: Technical Automotive Welding
description: An entry-level welding course in electric welding processes and cutting processes relating to the repair and maintenance of the automobile.

title: Audio Production
description: This course introduces students to professional audio recording and production techniques, including microphone setup, digital multi-track editing, and mixing. Using industry-standard software, students will complete hands-on projects to develop foundational skills in audio programming and production.

title: Strengthen Bones Naturally
description: Osteoporosis affects both men and women and produces 1.5 million fractures annually. To reduce your risk for fractures and osteoporosis, join biochemist Sandy Baumann to discover which common beverages, drugs and medical conditions rob bones. Find out which mineral is more important than calcium for bone density. Sign up now to learn natural strategies, including vitamins, minerals, hormones, supplements and lifestyle changes that strengthen bones at any age. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class. 5 nursing contact hours. Bring your bone density report.

title: MS windows 7 made so easy
description: Windows 7 is the newest operating system. Learn to use features found on your desktop and under the start menu. Change desktop backgrounds and use screensavers with ease. Other features include using free software found on your computer, working with computer options, undertanding the notification area, saving files, creating folders and more.

title: Intro to Exercise Science
description: This course provides a foundation that students will need to be successful in future classes in exercise science, sports medicine, and fitness leadership. Sample topics covered in the class include sports nutrition, sports psychology, exercise physiology, exercise prescription, motor behavior, biomechanics, as well as an exploration into the career possibilities in the field.

title: Adult Tap Dance 2
description: If you have some experience with tap, join Emily Goward for this intermediate-level class. This is a great opportunity to review the basic techniques and add another night of exercise to your life. Bring tap shoes.

title: Head-To-Toe Body Toning
description: Target, tone and reshape every muscle group in this head-to-toe body toning class. You will burn mega calories and boost your metabolism as you work your upper body, lower body, and abdominals. You'll love the results! Great class for men and women of all fitness levels, however beginners may need to adapt some of the routines. Please bring a pair of 3 or 5 lb. hand weights, water and exercise mat. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Basic Troubleshooting 101
description: Introduces the fundamentals of troubleshooting including analog and digital circuits. Topics include printed circuit board repair, power supply repairs and troubleshooting, transducers repair and troubleshooting, amplifier repair and troubleshooting, silicon controlled rectifiers (SCR's) repair and troubleshooting. The class will also include various digital electronic circuit repair and troubleshooting. Circuits are constructed by students with faults so troubleshooting and testing can be employed to find and repair the faulty circuits.

title: Gourmet Cupcake Workshop
description: Transform 12 ordinary cupcakes into decadent, sinfully sweet gourmet treats! First you'll create 6 Double Stuffed Cupcakes, over-filled with a variety of luscious fillings, and covered with lots of sweet and crunchy toppings. Then you'll make 6 Candy Cup Cakes with multiple layers of moist cake, assorted candies (including chocolate), and decadent fillings, all displayed in cute serving cups! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12 - $15).

title: Tai Chi Chu'an (2-Wk Session)
description: Tai Chi, sometimes referred to as "meditation in motion," is an ancient Chinese martial art consisting of a sequence of slow, graceful motions that relax and strengthen the mind, body, and spirit. An excellent form of stress management suitable for people of all ages, Tai Chi has been shown to reduce blood pressure and strengthen bones. Join an experienced Tai Chi instructor and discover the benefits yourself. No experience needed. Wear loose, comfortable, modest clothing and shoes with no or low heels. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Honors Coll Writing & Research
description: ENG-132 is the second college-level composition course in a two-semester sequence. Students will continue to write, read, think critically, and use information at the college level. In particular, students will be exposed to a range of literary texts related to the Honors Colloquium topics, and will be instructed in ethical, effective research writing.

title: Word & Excel for Genealogists
description: Discover the many ways Microsoft Word and Excel can help you do genealogy research and keep your family tree organized. In this hands-on workshop, Michael Neill, weekly columnist for Ancestry Daily News, will show you various tables and forms that can be created to organize data and conduct research. You'll also learn how to export reports and forms from most genealogical software programs (i.e. Personal Ancestral File or Family Tree Maker) into Word, use Word to create computerized research logs, and download graphics from the Internet for integration into software applications. Each student will have a computer to use. Class limited to 14.

title: Theatre Experience III
description: This is the third of six experience-based one-credit courses where students work onstage, backstage, or in front-of-house operations for theatre productions during the semester. The course includes instructor presentation of specific elements of application though lecture, lecture/demonstration and/or laboratory experience.The production work for Theatre Experience III builds on the production work completed in Theatre Experience II. In this course, the student will practice and refine techniques learned in the previous Theatre Experience course in addition to learning new parts, backstage practices, etc.

title: Vocal Jazz Ensemble 4
description: Fourth of four sequential semesters of vocal jazz ensemble, covering preparation and performance of a variety of jazz and popular music in an ensemble of 8-20 voices. Group performs for various HFC and community functions.

title: Introduction to Crochet
description: Learn the basics of crochet in this comprehensive four week class. You will learn how to execute the five stitches associated with crochet. Learn to read patterns and be able to make the ever favorite "Granny square" motif. Tips and tricks will be shared to make your projects true hand-crafted treasures. Upon finishing the course you will have the skills and confidence necessary to complete a beginner-to-easy level project of their choosing. Bring a tape measure, scissors, yarn and a crochet hook.

title: Introduction to Knitting
description: The basics of knitting - to knit, purl, create stitch pattern and read patterns - are covered in this comprehensive course. Tips and tricks will be shared to make your projects true hand-crafted treasures. Upon finishing the course you will have the skills and confidence necessary to complete a beginner-to- easy- level project of your choosing. Bring a tape measure, scissors, yarn, and needles.

title: A Glimpse of Hungary
description: Hungary is known for its exotic, tasty and spicy food. Few people know more about preparing it than Elizabeth Krajcz, who was born in Hungary and has cooked at the Hungarian American Culture Center for 20 years. She'll teach you how to make a true Hungarian meal of beef goulash, cucumber salad, chicken paprikash and palacsinta (crepes filled with apricots). Bring an apron and your appetite.

title: Pilates for Anyone
description: Flatten abs; strengthen and tone muscles; and improve your flexibility, endurance and posture using the Pilates method, one of today's hottest fitness trends. Developed over 90 years ago, Pilates is a tried-and-true low-impact exercise system. It is popular with athletes, dancers, and anyone who wants to develop a more streamlined shape using an all-encompassing mind, body, and spirit exercise. Please bring an exercise mat.

title: Best Srategies for Saving
description: Investing in the stock market might seem like a wild,roller-coaster ride, but it doesn't have to be if you learn the ins and outs of saving and investing. Join Rick Bloom for an up-to-date analysis of the best strategies for saving and investing. Rick was selected as one of the Top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine and has written a daily column on financial advice for a Detroit newspaper. He currently hosts a daily radio show on a local station.

title: Adv Video Game Design & Dev
description: In the Advanced Video Game Design and Development online program students will move on to learn more advanced 3D graphics programming techniques. Students will explore mesh optimizations and hierarchical animation techniques, the use of level of detail (LOD) algorithms to improve application performance by reducing polygon counts in distant objects, skeletal animation systems for realistic character animation and more.

title: Explore Classics
description: If you would like to know more about the Baroque, Classical, and Romantic periods in music, here is your opportunity. Learn how to listen and trust your own response to the music. The elements of the music will be identified and considered. Composers to be heard will include Bach and Handel for the Baroque period; Mozart and Haydn for the Classical; and Beethoven, Schubert, and Wagner for the Romantic. No previous knowledge or experience needed! If there is interest, a field trip to a Detroit Symphony or Michigan Opera Theater performance could be arranged. The teacher, Jan VanDenburg, has degrees in music education and fine arts and is retired from Edsel Ford High School.

title: Build Your Wealth
description: Back by popular demand is financial planner Rick Bloom with the newest "Rick's Picks" investments to help you increase your net worth. Rick currently hosts a Sunday radio show on a local station and has been selected as one of the top 250 financial planners by Worth magazine. He will discuss what factors to consider before purchasing a mutual fund, when to sell an investment, how to protect your principal, and what rating firms you can trust. This class will be held in the Chapel of Henry Ford Village, 15101 Ford Road in Dearborn. Interested persons of all ages are invited. Register for this popular event by October 6.

title: Broadcast Journalism
description: This course offers an immersive experience in broadcast journalism, covering news writing, production, and ethical considerations for radio, television, and digital media. Students will work on the news team of WHFR-FM 89.3, Henry Ford College's student-run station, contributing high-quality broadcast stories through written copy, audio actualities, and prerecorded interviews. In addition, students will produce TV news packages and stories in the studio, gaining hands-on experience with industry-standard equipment and techniques while upholding professional ethical standards in journalism.

title: Spiral Rope for Beginners
description: Spiral rope stitch is an easy, versatile form of basic stitch and some variations as you make an impressive bracelet. $15 material fee due to instructor. Class limited to 8.

title: Italian Christmas Traditions
description: No need to travel to Italy to experience an Italian Christmas! Spend an evening with Don Samull viewing slides taken during a holiday spent in Rome and the surrounding area. View a display of Christmas artifacts as you sample a delicious panetonne coffee cake. The following Saturday enjoy an Italian Christmas meal of a seafood appetizer, tortellini, soup, & salad at a well-know Dearborn Italian restaurant. Buon natale!

title: Stretch, Tone, & Strengthen
description: Would you like a regular program of exercise that offers total body conditioning and leaves you energized rather than exhausted at the end of a workout? Learn a program of safe stretching and strengthening exercises to help tone and firm your entire body. You'll look and feel better in an amazingly short time. Designed for adults of all fitness levels. Wear comfortable clothing and bring a mat or towel.

title: Jazz Band 3
description: Third of four semesters of jazz band, including preparation and performance of Big Band music in a variety of jazz styles from swing to contemporary. This group, which performs several concerts each semester, is open to college students and adult members of the community who play saxophone, trumpet, trombone, piano, guitar, bass, or drums.

title: Network Design & Implement
description: A capstone course covering the design, implementation, and support of a Local Area Network (LAN). Examines basic LAN design methods and tools, Network Operating Systems, LAN implementation considerations, LAN hardware, and network troubleshooting techniques. Includes the design and implementation of a LAN in a lab setting complete with file servers, system backups, wireless and wired clients, printing, Internet Access, configuration of switches, VLANs, routers, VPNs, and firewalls.

title: College Algebra
description: Covers analytic geometry, functions and their graphs, algebraic and graphical solutions of equations and inequalities, graphs and zeros of polynomial functions, conic sections, linear and polynomial modeling, systems of equations and inequalities, sequences and series, and the Binomial Theorem. Techniques of problem-solving and applications are included throughout the course requiring the frequent usage of graphing utilities. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for some formal assessments and access to an online homework system.

title: Make Money-Wordprocessing Bus
description: Be your own boss and set your own hours by starting a home business with your computer. Services you may offer include word processing, resumes, transcription and more. Learn 50 ways to market your services, methods to find clients, secrets to choosing equipment, tips for effective pricing of services, etc. This class is only offered in the fall term; you must take CESBU 1102 Successful Home Business to be admitted to this class. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Intro to Surgical Tech Lab
description: The course introduces the fundamentals of operating room techniques, emphasizing principles and practices of asepsis and sterilization. Physical and psychosocial aspects of the patient are considered throughout the various procedures in the operating process. Included are the roles and responsibilities of the surgical technologist and other surgical team members; identification and handling of common equipment, instruments, and supplies; students are introduced to and practice the sterile and unsterile rules in the operating room. This is a laboratory course.

title: Spec Topics in Comp Info Syst
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current Computer Information System trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites each term. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Art History Survey I
description: A survey of the development of visual arts from the pre-historic to the end of medieval period, covering major civilizations and cultures worldwide. Offers students strong background for understanding the visual art as a vehicle of communication in its functional context. Provides cross-cultural understanding through continuous critical thinking perspective. Enables students to comprehend and interpret works of art by examining the contextual characteristics of the work, its subject, functionality and the culture(s) that produced it. A field trip to a major museum and other appropriate sites is required to strengthen student knowledge.

title: Radio Station Wrksp/WHFR-FM
description: Interested in how a first-rate radio station works? HFCC's award-winning radio station WHFR-FM is just the place. Join us in this intensive orientation for our non-commercial, educational radio station. Learn essential station rules and procedures, operation of equipment and basic performance skills. You just might find yourself on the airwaves some day!

title: Int Mfg Sys Troubleshooting 1
description: This course provides an entry level student with an overview of the technology used in automated integrated systems found in manufacturing. Included is also an overview of the typical plant networks and their associated responsibility. Distributed and local control is examined. Analysis of sequencing machines is completed along with an introduction of how to troubleshoot these systems. Topics include: fluid power components, electrical components, conveyers, part sensing components and an overview of PLC control principles.

title: Sign Language Cert 1
description: Are you looking for a sign language class that will prepare you to take the certification test? Join long-time instructor and member of the deaf community, Anne Bolander, in the first course of a series designed to prepare you for certification. Look for more classes in future semesters. Includes 200 page workbook and DVD.

title: Create Website with Adobe CS3
description: Let an experienced instructor walk you through the process of creating an intuitive, attractive, and accessible website with the brand new version of Dreamweaver. (Macromedia version users also welcome.) Learn the Dreamweaver interface basics, work with text and images, create links and anchors, create successful navigation and maintain the site. Lots of hands-on practice at your own workstation and plenty of time for questions. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2, or equivalent knowledge is absolutely necessary to participate in this class. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work. Text provided.

title: Crocheting for Anyone
description: Did you know it's hip to crochet? The new crochet is anyhthing but square! Whether you're a first-time crocheter or looking to expand your skills, Carol Bacon, a long-time crocheter and an event coordinator at a major arts and crafts store, will help you go from hook and yarn to scarves, sweaters and afghans. You'll learn to perform the basic stitch techniques, select hooks and yarns, read patterns, change color and more. Bring any size needles and 4-ply yarn the first night. Supplies for projects will be discussed in class.

title: Refrigeration Technology
description: Prepares students as multi-level service technicians in the refrigeration field. Covers basic refrigeration system design and the components for various domestic, residential, and light commercial systems. Discusses refrigeration system tools, materials, and instruments. Also covers advanced electrical wiring, refrigeration characteristics, charging, evacuation, dehydration, and refrigerant recovery. Course work includes hands-on wiring and circuit troubleshooting related to both mechanical and electrical components. The EPA Section 608 Technician Certification test in refrigerant reclamation is given at the conclusion of the semester.

title: Own a Successful Wedding Busin
description: Have you always loved attending weddings or watching Whose Wedding Is It Anyway? Think you might have what it takes to own your own business? If so, this class is the perfect way to turn an idea into a moneymaking business! Certified Wedding Planner Lindsey Nartker will teach you how to build a successful wedding planning business, how to create an inspiring event design and a few tips to managing the wedding day.

title: Cardio Kick
description: Achieve endurance, strength, stability, balance and mental toughness in this cardio kickboxing class. A series of fast-paced jabs, punches, shuffles and kicks are all incorporated into this exhilarating class. Great for increasing strength and pliability of connective tissue, and for your cardiovascular fitness. All fitness levels welcome! Walk-in sessions only. Enroll in and pay for class at the door.

title: Applied Music (two Credits) 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of weekly one-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Ballroom Boot Camp
description: You've always wanted to try it! It looks like fun; it is fun! Come alone or with a partner to learn the fundamentals of leading and following for such contemporary ballroom dances as the swing, foxtrot, waltz, and other familiar dances. Learn the moves that will get you noticed at your next wedding, party, or in a nightclub.

title: Metal Formabililty & Die Impr
description: This 40 hour class will provide Tool & Die personnel with instruction on the skills needed to understand and perform metal formbility and die improvement. Participant will receive instruction on techniques and procedures that enable them to improve quality and reduce down time in the manufacturing system. This instruction will contribute to the plant's implementaion of the Reference Panel System, a key component to attaining quality.

title: Acrylic Painting 1
description: Among the most versatile media ever developed, acrylic colors offer an extraordinary variety of painting methods. Artist and instructor Kathy Boettcher provides a wealth of useful suggestions and practical information about acrylic painting methods and materials for beginning and intermediate level painters. You'll learn about colors and their characteristics, painting surfaces and their preparation, painting tools, and much more. $5 due to instructor for first night's materials. Supply list will be provided in class.

title: Mastery Business Applications
description: Learn the essence of business applications. Understand how marketing differs from sales, how the marketing concept helps a firm succeed and why consumer behavior is such a challenging topic. Increase your knowledge of the basics of international business and learn about the various options and requirements for success. Discover the nature of information technology and realize how operations management helps increase efficiency and effectiveness. Learn the definition of quality, discover the nature of Six Sigma and total quality management (TQM), and find out how to implement a quality culture. Understand the nature of project management and learn about the different phases of the project life cycle.

title: Auto Ignition Systems
description: An intermediate-level course covering the operating principles, construction, troubleshooting and maintenance of units of the automotive ignition system, including distributors, coils, electronic controls and advance mechanisms. Studies ignition timing, wiring, and ignition malfunctions as well as electronic ignition systems and ignition-related emission controls.

title: Sweet Deep-Fried Treats
description: Unbelievably rich and decadent, these all-time, long-time favorite treats have become even more popular when served deep-fried! Join us in this fun class where we'll make sweet, crispy & crunchy deep-fried Twinkies, Oreo Cookies, Snickers Candy Bars, and Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough Bites! Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12-$15).

title: Cost Accounting
description: Explores the process by which companies control and plan the costs of doing business.

title: Basic Income Approach
description: Prepare to become a commercial or industrial appraiser in this Level II class. Topics include real estate investments and finance, gross income estimates, income and expense statements, capitalization formulas and rates, and capitalization procedures. This course meets one of the Michigan SAB Level II requirements and is approved by the Board of Real Estate Appraisers for 30 hours and the International Association of Assessing Officers as a reciprocal for their Course 102 or for 30 hours recertification credit.

title: Mexican Party Food
description: Learn about Antojitos (appetizers) such as Botana, Guacamole, Salsas: grilled, raw, & stewed; and all the goodies that accompany them. For dessert, consider Mexican wedding cookies. You will enjoy making and tasting these delicious authentic Mexican dishes, under the supervision of Chef Alicia Bonilla-Arias. Please bring your apron and an appetite.

title: State Assessors Board Cert
description: Get those requirements out of the way in an easy one-day forum by Lisa Hobart. This is one of the three mandatory six-hour renewal programs assessing officers must complete by September 30, 2011. This program will focus on the following specific topics: Clarification of terminology (for new, loss, additions, losses and adjustment); Association of terms with values; Treatment of example situations and required report preparation.

title: Emergency Preparedness
description: Who can forget the images from 9/11 or hurricane Katrina? While you may not be able to prevent natural or man-made disasters from occuring, you can reduce some of the fear associated with them. Join Allan Geisler, an emergency-preparedness guru who is still using up his Y2K supplies, to learn the basic steps and supplies needed to prepare you and your loved ones for a variety of possible emergencies. View some of the items needed and leave with handouts for future reference.

title: Intro to Clinical Therapeutics
description: Selected experience in health care facilities for clinical practice with oxygen delivery systems and basic patient assessment techniques.

title: Wealth Wth Rental Real Estate
description: Is rental property your ticket to financial security? Get the latest facts about actual profits and potential pitfalls with rental real estate from successful property manager/investor Peter Pace. First, learn how to find property, evaluate its worth, negotiate price, and obtain financing. Then get Peter's advice to set a market-driven rent, write leases, attract and screen tenants, and generate cash flow. Discover his proven strategies for property management, timely maintenance, and tenant retention to maximize your income. Optional $25 package of rental documents available after class.

title: Foreman Grill
description: Grab your lean, mean, fat-reducing grilling machine and learn how to prepare some of your favorite dishes in no time at all! In this hands-on class Marilyn Suter will show you how to prepare vegetables, fish, pork chops, sandwiches, and things that can be made ahead of time and eaten cold. Bring an apron, your appetite and a George Foreman grill (if you own one). Enroll early. Class limited to 14.

title: Work Wth Learn Disable Student
description: Learn how to successfully meet the diverse needs of the learning disabled students in your classroom.

title: Competency Evaluated NA(CENA)
description: Prepares individuals to become a Nurse Aide. This course serves as the educational foundation for the Nursing Care Skills certificate. Aides render care to clients in hospitals, long-term care facilities, and in home-care. This course, and the Nursing Care Skills certificate, consists of 35 hours of course work in theory, 45 hours of laboratory activities, and 20 hours in a clinical agency. The course requires consistent attendance from 9:00 a.m. - 2:30 p.m. four days a week.

title: Camp RN
description: At Camp RN students are engaged in anatomy and physiology and nutritional science, while learning nursing fundamentals including CPR and first aid. Participants also tour the HFC M-TEC Center, interact with nurses in a hospital setting (shadowing), and utilize the physical assessment laboratory to learn about the human body.

title: Spider Bag
description: Using just a needle, waxed linen cord, and one simple stitch, make a beautiful bag necklace to hold a cherished item or keepsake. The first night you will work on the body of the necklace. On the second night bring accent beads to finish the necklace. Your instructor wil show you sample bags the first night. $7.50 supply fee due to instructor in class. Limited to 8.

title: Surgical Pharmacology
description: A study of medications used in the operating room with an emphasis on the common drugs used in the surgical area. This course examines the administration, actions, interactions, side effects, and terminology of perioperative medications. Included are legal responsibilities of the surgical technologist and common calculations performed in a sterile field. The student must present two reports as well as complete a term paper.

title: Hfv-Introduction to Computers
description: If you have operated a car, you can learn the basics of computers in just four 2-hour sessions! Don't let computer jargon stop you! Attend this hands-on workshop designated for beginners with little or no experience, and see how easy it is to use the basic features of Windows XP. Learn to start and shut down a computer safely, use a computer mouse, navigate the keyboard and the special keys, open and close software files and windows. By the end of this class, you will recognize computer hardware, know the difference between an operating system and software, and be able to navigate Windows XP. THIS CLASS IS RESTRICTED TO HENRY FORD VILLAGE RESIDENTS AND IS BILLED BY SECTION.

title: Fit for Life
description: Experience the value of fitness and the benefits of activity by developing strength, endurance and flexibility at a comfortable pace. This class, designed for the needs of the older body, is for those who believe you are never too old to exercise.

title: Keep Records With Excel
description: Excel is a computer program for keeping track of numbers and lists. How much did I spend? How much will it cost? What if the interest rate goes up or my stocks go down? Calculations are easy in Excel when you know how to create the formulas (you don't need to be a math whiz!) and use the built-in functions that allow you to find a sum, compute an average, or count entries and much more with just a few clicks of the mouse. Whether you are tracking the family finances, involved in business or keeping your club or league records, Excel can be a useful tool for you. Prerequisites: Intro to Computers or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.

title: ABC's of Cooking for 1 or 2
description: Starting out on your own, or downsizing from family-sized needs? Join instructor Len Charla and explore cooking for one (or two)! Learn how to set up and run a smaller kitchen for today's busy lifestyles, and how to prepare easy, economical, well-balanced, sensational menus for one or two. Len will also show you how to turn ho-hum leftovers into appetizing future meals. (Optional instructor's cookbook will be available for $11 after class). All you need for this class is an apron, pen and paper for notes.

title: Adv Human Potential Seminar
description: Use the group process and the tools developed in COUN-110 to further develop life skills that enable each individual to discover his or her potential, and to deal more effectively and efficiently with conflicts, blocks, failures, and life-style problems.

title: Mi Real Estate Cont Educ
description: This course is approved by the Board of Real Estate Brokers and Salespeople - Provider Approval #188 Course #1200-2/4, Subjects L5032 and 15034. Topics covered will include Michigan statutes and rules update, real estate law cases, fair housing update. Did you know? Department disciplinary action and federal laws and rules update. This course includes the required 2 hour law.

title: Insider's Guide Job Searches
description: Learn tips and techniques that will help you position yourself favorably with people who can hire you, open doors for you, or leverage you to be hired by friends and colleagues. Experts say that about 80 percent of jobs are found through networking, the informal channels of communication between employers and job seekers. Come discover and practice methods used by successful people for years. It can work for you too!

title: Adult Hip Hop
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? This high-energy, introductory class features the best of both! Under the guidance of dancer Emily Goward, you'll work your body and soul to the hottest music craze this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Beginning Jazz Dance
description: Explores theatre style jazz dance. Primary emphasis is on developing knowledge of and experience in basic jazz movement vocabulary and technique accompanied by jazz and popular music. Course also includes study of music rhythms, a brief history of jazz, and the body instrument from an anatomical perspective.

title: Home Staging Tips
description: Make your home stand out over the others in your neighborhood! Darlene Rothman, a professional stager (ASP), will show you how to present your home to buyers in the most appealing way. Let her practiced eye alert you to pitfalls and quick tips to help potential buyers visualize it as a place they'd like to call their own. In this economy, staging is an important component in successfully selling a home quickly and for optimum prices.

title: Camp Screenprinting 101
description: Learn the basics of screenprinting on fabric from your own designs. We will cover the basics of creating your own design, transferring it onto a screen, and printing it onto the final material.

title: Eastern Philosophy
description: An exploration of the metaphysical, epistemological, and ethical frameworks of select philosophical traditions rooted in East Asia.

title: Simple Plumbing Repairs
description: If you are tired of dripping faucets and toilets that keep running, don't call a plumber. Join contractor Peter Pace to get expert fix-it advice. In one evening, he will show you how to change a faucet and its seal and how to replace a sink trap in your kitchen or bathroom. He will also tell you reasons why a toilet keeps running and demonstrate how to replace a toilet handle, anti-siphon, and ballcock. Think of the money you'll save by doing it yourself. Bring your questions.

title: Money for Life!
description: Guarantee yourself an income for life!
