You CAN have the money you'll need in spite of the fact that Corporate America is gettin gout of the pension business, Social Security funding remains a major concern, and buyout and retirement issues are becoming more prominent. This is not your typical "how to get rich" course. Instead, you will learn how to keep from ever becoming poor. Instructor Lawrence Stack, a Chartered Financial Consultant with 25 years of experience, will show you techniques for calculating how much money you'll need through retirement, and how to manage risk and minimize taxation. $20 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Basic Auto Elec
description: A study of basic automotive electricity theory and principles. Covers voltage, amperage, Ohm's law, accessories, and lighting as well as series, parallel, and series-parallel circuits. Reading of electrical schematics/wiring diagrams is covered. Also discusses the various meters that are used in the service of automobiles. Emphasizes circuit diagnosis and troubleshooting using proper diagnostic techniques and tools, including flex-probes.

title: Lebanese Cooking IV
description: Fried kibbe balls stuffed with lamb. Lebanese green beans. Lebanese rice.

title: Prevent Identity Theft
description: Don't be a victim! With over 27 million identity thefts reported in the last five years this crime has reached epidemic proportions. Let security expert and author Johnny May, CPP, CPO, show you how to avoid the nightmare. In this short but comprehensive class, you will learn what identity theft is, who is at risk, how the Internet and other technology increase risk, how thieves steal your personal information, and what you can do to prevent it. Get step-by-step instructions for what to do if YOU become a victim. Includes May's Guide to Preventing Identity Theft.

title: Residential Wiring
description: Course explores the history and fundamentals of residential wiring including the inspectors perspective, and the aspects of the National Electrical Code (NEC) that apply to residential construction and repairs. Electrical supplies, hardware and their associated wiring techniques for residential applications are explained. The method of instruction is classroom lecture utilizing PowerPoint.

title: Desktop Publish Indesign Cs5
description: Use the brand new Adobe InDesign CS3 software to create your catalogs, brochures, flyers and other items. Lay out your text and graphics like the pros and avoid having to send these items out to an expensive designer. Easily coordinate with professional printers, publish in your own office, or output to multiple media. Create rich full featured documents with easy integration with Photoshop and other programs. The instructor will get you started and help to demystify the many features incorporated in this professional page layout program. Prerequisite: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!
prerequisite_text: Introduction to Computers 1 & 2 or similar experience is an absolute necessity for participation in this class. Sign up today!

title: Reiki Master Level Cert
description: Achieve master level certification through instruction by Reiki Master Lisa Gutowski. In this class you will learn how to teach and become a mentor. Master certification requires apprenticeship, as well as classroom training. Prerequisite: Reiki Level 3 Certification. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.
prerequisite_text: Reiki Level 3 Certification. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Hfv - Gentle Yoga
description: Experience the health benefits of yoga in this calming, therapeutic class. Designed and presented by professional yoga instructor Dianna Kinaschuk for beginners, returning practitioners, seniors, persons with limited mobility and flexibility, and those recovering from illness or injury. Gentle Yoga offers the postures and breathing techniques of Hatha Yoga in a format that can be modified to fit your needs and abilities, and at a gentle, slow pace. Open to persons of all ages and levels. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Advanced Mobile App Dev (iOS)
description: An advanced-level course expanding on mobile application development on the iOS platform. Covers data handling and connectivity to back-end services including those hosted in a cloud environment. Design approaches to efficiently reach a large segment in the mobile market will be covered.

title: Fire Alarm Sys for Electrician
description: Provides an overview of fire alarm systems. Reviews fire alarm system requirements from the International Building Code and the National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) 72 Standard for fire alarm and signaling systems. Discusses the basics of detection, notification, suppression, and cabling requirements for residential and commercial applications.

title: Metallic Jewelry - Mommy & Me
description: Make and take home several unique and colorful earrings, pins and rings, all handmade using "Friendly Plastic" jewelry-making sticks. Lots of fun and easy projects to do, an adult and child will create beautiful looking designs for themselves and each other too! Children age six or older. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list. Registration fee for adult - register children separately at discount rate - see below. Instructor will collect $5 per person materials fee first day of class.

title: Beginner Inline Skating
description: Safely learn the basics of inline skating from a certified instructor from the International Inline Skating Association (IISA). In beginner inline skating you will learn the primary skills of inline skating and how to blend them into functional skills that will get you out rolling in no time. Apply what you learn toward exercise programs and more advanced maneuvers. At the end of this 6-week class, you will be ready to skate outdoors with your friends. Students must provide skates equipped with a heel brake, helmet, wrist guards, elbow and kneepads.

title: Reinvent Your Career - New
description: To thrive in an economy that has lost 2.3 million jobs since 2001, you need to learn to sell your skills to a wide range of employers. Gain support to make a fulfilling career change by linking up with career transition coach June Strieby. June has changed careers seven times herself and coached many others to successful transitions. She will teach you to identify your transferable skills, evaluate career possibilities and write a resume that wins interviews with your targeted employers. Includes self-assessments and practical tips. Open to anyone.

title: Active Parenting
description: Explores how to apply the basic principles of active parenting to both children and teens. Also discusses topics such as, but not limited to: how to parent children at different developmental states, the development of a child's ethnic identity, effective punishment strategies, challenges faced by working parents, step-families and communication patterns.

title: Pro Tools 110
description: Expands upon the principles taught in MUS-192: Pro Tools 101 and introduces the core concepts and skills you will need to operate a Pro Tools system running mid-sized sessions with dozens of tracks. Teaches more advanced skills, and workflows involved in recording, editing, and mixing on an Avid Pro Tools system. Prepares for part two of the Avid Pro Tools online exam for certification. Part one of the certification test is offered in MUS-192: Pro Tools 101. Successful completion of both parts of the exam constitutes Avid Pro Tools certification.

title: More Cake Decorating
description: Take your cake decorating skills to a new level in this intermediate class. Kristin Jablonski, an award winning pastry chef, will teach you how to stack a cake and other more advanced techniques in finishing work. Supplies list will be discussed in first class. Beginning Cake Decorating required.

title: Polish Easter
description: Experience the beauty of Polish easter customs from Paczki Day to Dyngus Day via a narrated slide lecture. Polish Easter Babka wil be served and a display of relevant artifacts will be available for the evening program. Included is the traditional Polish easter meal called "Swieconka" which will take place at a Hamtramck restaurant on a following Saturday. "Wesolego Alleluia!"

title: PowerPoint in the Classroom
description: If you're an educator, this course will help you discover the exciting possibilities of using Power Point for and with your students. You'll master a variety of uses for PowerPoint in the classroom, and you'll be able to create compelling lessons and presentations filled with text, graphics, sound and video. You'll be able to use PowerPoint templates or create your own dynamic preentations that will increase interest in any subject you teach.

title: Creating Web Pages
description: Plan the content, structure, and layout of your website; create pages of neatly formatted text; build links between pages and to the outside world; and add color, backgrounds, graphics, tables, hot buttons, and animation. Get your best location in search engine listings and learn powerful no-cost or low-cost web marketing strategies.

title: Color Ther for Pain & Stress
description: Have you suffered with sleep disorers, allergies, fatigue, nervousness, anxiety, or closed head injury and not found relief with conventional medical treatment? Then come to hear Susanna Stoica, Ph.D., a holistic practitioner and author of Healing with a Loving Heart, discuss recent therapies including Healing Touch, Bach Flower Remedies, NAET allergy elimination technique, kinesiology, and more. She will describe how low-grade food and environmental allergies cause so much suffering and demonstrate therapies you can employ to heal yourself. You will learn how and when to combine these therapies with traditional medical care. $15 material fee due to instructor at first class.

title: Create a Gourmet Candy Bouquet
description: Discover the secrets to creating gourmet candy bouquets. Expensive to buy in stores or on the Internet, see how sparkling floral stems - made with yards of colorful cellophane, metallic foil and curling ribbon - and dozens of assorted mini candy bars and hard candies, all nestled into a lovely vase, become a unique and dazzling bouquet. Once learned you'll be able to create bouquets in endless colors and for any theme/occasion. Other items (dollar bills, gift cards, etc.) can be added as well. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $12-$15).

title: Introduction to Philosophy
description: An introduction to the perennial problems that have occupied philosophers primarily in the Western tradition, including appearance vs. reality, the mind/body problem, free will vs. determinism, the nature of morality, the existence of God, and the justification of political power.

title: Graphic Design Studio 1
description: An introduction to visual communication using the Adobe Creative Software Suite. Provides a working knowledge of InDesign and Illustrator. Introduction to the fundamentals and principles of design. Explores the design and development of a variety of media, including multi-page print documents and digital output. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.Students are expected to have access to the Adobe Creative Cloud applications including Photoshop, Indesign, Illustrator, Acrobat, AfterEffects and XD.

title: Telecommunication Camp
description: Interested in all things media? The HFC Telecommunication Camp is the place for you! Our expert instructors will give you a front row seat to learn about the magic of film and television, how a real radio station works, the art of telling a story or delivering news through the written script, and a number of performance techniques including character voices, newscasts and interviews . Students will get hands-on opportunities to experience what their favorite actors, writers, editors, directors, newscasters and DJs do in the media field, from writing scripts, shooting footage, lighting, and editing to performing commercials, news and improv on camera and the radio. Your friends and family might even get to hear you on our award-winning radio station, WHFR. Make your mark on the world of media and have loads of fun in the process!

title: Charm Bracelet Workshop 1
description: With a few inexpensive tools, copper sheet, a package of precious metal clay, and a variety of beads, yhou can make an incredible charm bracelet. This session will focus on making the link bracelet and stamping/embellishing the copper blanks. Bring a Phillips head screwdriver, one concrete nail, any comfortably sized hammer, and heavy duty scissors. Material kit $28.00 due to instructor in class.

title: Predictive Maint Shaft Align
description: Provides specialized instruction in the practices and equipment used in shaft alignment, and the end-to-end and parallel alignments of machines. Explores machine failures due to rotating shaft misalignment and vibration created from shaft center lines not being in the same plane. Covers inefficiencies and increased wear due to misalignment, shaft alignment methods, soft foot, thermal growth, rim, face, reverse dial indicator, and MPT couplings.

title: Digital Audio Editing
description: An intensive workshop introduction to digital audio editing, using an industry standard software editing program on computers in the Telecommunication audio production labs. Both hardware and software issues will be covered, and several short editing exercises will be completed.

title: Grow Your Own Vegetables
description: As you watch the prices for produce soar at the market do you think, "I wish I knew how to grow my own vegetables?" This course, taught by Laurie Jeffery, certified Master Gardener, will help get you started on that very rewarding project. Learn how to pick the site for your garden, what to plant, how to plant, how to care for your garden and how to harvest your crop.

title: Develop Wills & Estate Plans
description: Even if you have a will it may not help you avoid transferred directly to your heirs. Jonathan Goldberg will discuss common mistakes many people make when developing a will or an estate plan and how to avoid these pitfalls.

title: Basic Shape Size Interpretatio
description: Introduces the concepts of shape and size description of normal surfaced, inclined (single-angle) surfaced, and cylindrical objects. Presents traditional and computer-based drafting techniques. Covers projection of three-dimensional objects into two-dimensional representations, the reverse process, and sketching and modeling of objects.

title: Medical-Surgical Nursing II
description: Continues to explore how to utilize critical thinking skills in a systematic, problem solving process, as a framework for providing safe and effective care to restore and promote health in adult clients. Focuses on nursing care related to concepts of healthy adult clients as well as adult clients with common, acute, or chronic illness exemplars, and explains how to research best practices and integrate theory in the care of adult clients and families in a variety of clinical settings. This course includes supervised clinical agency/laboratory practicums. Course comprised of four hours of theory and a clinical/lab day per week.

title: Spec Topics in Comp Info Sys
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current Computer Information System trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites when offered. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Paramedic Procedures Lab II
description: This laboratory course develops overall patient management skills. Covers equipment, assessment, evaluation, treatment, documentation, communication and more. Lab activities focus on the management of the cardiac patient, mega-code scenarios, spinal immobilization, traumatic injuries, and traumatic resuscitation.

title: Renaissance Voices
description: This course is designed as a continuation of performance activities for vocal music students who have completed four vocal performance classes for college credit. Admission by administrative permission only.

title: Intro Integrated Manufacturing
description: Introduces integrated manufacturing systems. Presents the technology associated with a complex integrated manufacturing system through working with the SMC-FMS-200. Discusses manufacturing processes of feeding, handling, verification, and loading operations.

title: You're on the Air!
description: Voice-overs are hot today! With such notable talent as Morgan Freeman, Glenn Close, and Alec Baldwin lending their voices to commercials, films, and videos, one would think that it would be next to impossible to break into this field. Not so! In this class you'll learn about an exciting new way to get around the competition and actually turn voice-overs into a thriving full or part-time business! The instructor will discuss the voice-over business as a whole, the numerous opportunities, the incredible income potential, and will discuss the all-important "demo" and how to have it produced. You'll step up to the mic and do some practice recording, and best of all, you'll hear the results!

title: Mathematics for the Technician
description: Covers basic mathematics skills specific to the technical field as well as examining measurement aspects beginning with angle and linear measurement, ratios and proportions, basic algebraic expressions, geometry as applied to area and volume calculations - as well as suspension geometry for vehicle alignment, and vibration analyzer data analysis.

title: Tour Capitals- Eastern Europe
description: Spend a crisp fall Tuesday evening traveling via a slide program to the fascinating capitals of east central Europe. You'll feel as if you're at a charming old world cafe as you enjoy pastries and experience the highlights of Warsaw, Prague, Budapest, Vienna, Bucharest, and Sophia. On Saturday, you will join instructor Don Samull for a delicious eastern European meal at one of the finest Hungarian restaurants in the area. Cost of meal included in course fee.

title: Belly Dancing: Novice
description: If you are attending an Absolute Beginner class and would like to learn performance skills, join this optional half-hour extension session. Techniques covered in Absolute Beginner will be organized into a short choreography.

title: Photoshop 1
description: Photoshop is the premier software package for image processing and photo retouching. Become acquainted with this powerful and versatile program as you learn to work with the selection tools, filters, layers, scanning, and more. This class is recommended before taking some of the other graphics design classes. There will be a computer for every student in this class.

title: Yoga - Hatha Yoga 1
description: Hatha Yoga is an ancient system of exercise that includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through yoga, your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment. You will achieve mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Adv Cardiac Life Support
description: Students will be able to analyze life threatening cardiac emergencies and apply advanced principles of cardiac and respiratory anatomy and physiology. This course is designed for paramedical, medical, nursing or allied health personnel who daily occupation demands proficiency and certification in the knowledge of ACLS skills. Health care/nursing students must be in their final semester. The curriculum adheres to American Heart Association 2001 Guidelines. Prerequisite: successful completion of Basic Life Support (BLS) Healthcare Provider course within the past two years. Bring BLS card to first class session. Textbooks ($50) purchased from instructor.
prerequisite_text: successful completion of Basic Life Support (BLS) Healthcare Provider course within the past two years. Bring BLS card to first class session. Textbooks ($50) purchased from instructor.

title: Broadcast Journalism
description: TCM (Telecommunication) is now MCA (Media Communication Arts). Please search for MCA-261 to register for this course. A real life experience, guided by a Telecommunication instructor, in broadcast news writing for the mediums of radio and television. Working as a reporter on the news staff of WHFR-FM 89.3, the student-staffed station of Henry Ford College, the student is expected to weekly contribute quality broadcast news stories: written copy, copy with actualities, and prerecorded interviews.

title: Italian for Beginners
description: Acquire basic Italian language skils and learn the cultural nuances necessary to communicate for business or pleasure. Emphasis will be on useful vocabulary, basic sentences, and grammar that will assist you in greetings, directions, money, foods and restaurants, shopping, time, days, colors and much more. Books are available for purchase the first night of class.

title: Extraordinary German Desserts
description: If you've been to Germany, you know that northern Europeans love their desserts. Ursula Markovits, who was born and raised in Germany, not only loves to eat them, but also make them. Join her and learn how to make four of her favorites: coffeecake, almond chocolate log, apricot streusel sheet cake, and mousse with fruits. After all, an occasional decadent dessert indulgence is good for the soul.

title: Intro Process Instrumentation
description: Studies physical and chemical variables and the various instruments used to sense, measure, transmit, and control these variables. Introduces control loops and their components including controllers, regulators, sensors, and final control elements. Also discusses how to create instrumentation drawings and diagrams when developing and analyzing control loops.

title: Recording Studio Engineering 1
description: This course provides hands-on experience in digital audio engineering. Students will learn to engineer recording sessions by working in an actual recording studio with live musicians. The course covers basic techniques in setting up recording sessions, preparing computer files, microphone usage and placement, digitizing principals, audio acquisition options, midi integration and session management.

title: Wall Makeovers With Paint
description: Give your home a fresh new look with a simple coat of paint. Instead of hiring someone, attend this workshop and learn to do it yourself like a "pro." A professional painter will explain how to properly plan and organize a project; discuss different paint types, finishes, tools and supplies; demonstrate proper wall preparation (including drywall repairs); and share some "tricks of the trade" that will simplify your life. Bring your questions. $10 material fee payable to instructor.

title: Aroma Therapy-Health, Beauty
description: Indulge your senses by making your own perfume that smells good, is all natural and enlivens your spirit. In this sweet-smelling class with aromatherapist Mary Born, learn to blend essential oils into aromas that energize, relax or entice. The course fee includes one misting spray. You may blend your fragrance into an additional purse size, roll on or a body lotion the same night for a fee of $10 to $15 (based on oils chosen) paid to the instructor. Seats are limited. Enroll today!

title: Geography of Michigan
description: Examines the spatial distribution of the human and environmental landscapes of Michigan. Places particular attention on the historical geography of settlement and human use of the natural environment.

title: Spec Topics in Electrical Tech
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current electrical technology trends. Specific special topics and the prerequisites will be announced each term. Course may be repeated when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Workout With Pilates (4 Weeks)
description: Flatten abs, strengthen and tone, muscles, and improve your flexibility, endurance and posture using the Pilates method, one of today's hottest fitness trends. Developed over 90 years ago, Pilates is a tried-and-true, low-impact exercise system. It is popular with athletes, dancers and anyone who wants to develop a more streamlined shape using an all-encompassing mind, body and spirit exercise. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. NO CELL PHONES PERMITTED IN CLASS.

title: Adobe Acrobat 9 Pro
description: Adobe Acrobat is the most widely used tool for sharing formatted documents over the Web. This course will show you the many ways Acrobat is used to create and use PDF documents. You'll learn how to convert many popular file types into PDF documents, how to manipulate and format your documents, collaboration and document sharing techniques, how to create bookmarks and links, how to create interactive forms, how to keep your documents secure and more.

title: Paramedic V
description: Covers specialty topics such as OB/GYN, newborn resuscitation, and pediatrics. Also covers 12-Lead ECG including application and interpretation.

title: Be Safe From Crime
description: Can you become a crime victim because you talk on a cell phone outside a store? Learn from Wayne County Sheriff's Deptuy Ki Sobol how criminals pick their targets for identity theft, home burglary, purse snatching and carjacking. Discover red flags that signal danger for walkers, joggers and travelers, including special precautions for rape prevention. Sign up today to keep yourself and your children safe at work, at school, in your home and in your vehicle.

title: Elementary Italian I
description: Introduces the basic skills of reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in Italia within communicative contexts. Covers the elementary pronunciation and grammatical principles necessary for comprehending and expressing simple ideas in both spoken and written Italian. Presents Italian culture.

title: Integrating Tech in Classroom
description: Join us for an exciting and fun-filled tour of the technology-friendly classroom. Educational technology is advancing at an astounding rate, offering today's busy teacher quick and easy solutions for more interactive lesson plans, exciting WebQuests, and challenging assignments. Discover the power and creativity that technology can bring to your classroom as we look at basic integration, subject-specific activities, and smart ways to make your integration seamless. Take your teaching to another level with these fun new ways to incorporate technology into your lesson plans.

title: Criminal Justice Internship 1
description: This is an internship program that provides the student with field experience with municipal, county, state, and local criminal justice agencies and related agencies in the private sector. This includes but is not limited to: District, Circuit, and Juvenile Courts, halfway houses, detention centers, local, county and state police agencies, the FBI, U.S Marshall Service, Drug Enforcement Agency and Homeland Security. The internship is available to students in both the Probation/Corrections and Parole and the Criminal Justice programs. Students will perform all of the duties of professionals in the given area at the staff level. Students will not have arrest authority or be authorized to carry guns but may accompany a ride along, shadow criminal justice personnel, work in crime labs, interview probation clients, and perform other administrative work. Students will receive hands on experience in their chosen field working alongside with and performing the duties of criminal justice professionals.The internship requires a minimum of 9 hours of work each week off campus at a criminal justice site. Students are also required to meet on campus with the HFC faculty member on a weekly basis by arrangement. This class can only be taken once.

title: Make Trad Pierogis
description: If you are not of Polish descent, you may never have tasted authentic, homemade Pierogis. Now is your chance to join instructor Marilyn Suter to make and eat the real thing. You will mix and handle the dough, use a variety of fillings, and stuff your pierogis. Bring an apron. You will eat what you create, and take home any leftovers. Class limited to 14. Enroll early!

title: Intermediate ASL II
description: Follows ASL-231 and is the second of two intermediate-level American Sign Language (ASL) courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Further develops knowledge of fingerspelling, vocabulary and grammatical structures, and expands visual receptive and gestural expressive skills in order to comprehend and express a wide range of ideas in ASL.

title: Auto Alignment Clinic
description: Examines the types of front and rear suspension systems used on today's vehicles. Emphasizes the inspection, repair, and alignment of a variety of suspension systems.

title: Email for Seniors & Beginners
description: In this class you will learn to send and receive email, create and open attachments, send photos, and evaluate internet providers for your home computer. Discover how to protect yourself against spam and frauds.

title: Stunning Desserts, Dairy Free
description: Learn to create three delicious desserts that you won't believe are prepared dairy free. Chocolate lovers will want to be sure and attend this class. In this interactive class, Chefs Sherry Duquet and Sandy Boulton will prepare a Dark Chocolate Peanut Butter and Banana Pie, Choco-nut Truffles and Zesty Key Lime Pie. Nuts, wheat and soy products are used during cooking. Basic understanding of using cooking utensils and prepping food is helpful. Come ready to learn, cook and taste these delicious desserts.

title: Peyote Bead Bracelets
description: Learn peyote bead weaving, the popular single-needle handweaving technique, and create a distinctive, colorful bracelet using Delica seed beads and Swarovski crystal accents. $15 material fee payable to instructor in class.

title: Simple Plumb & Elec Repairs
description: Register for this section to take both Simple Electrical Repairs for Beginners and Simple Plumbing Repairs for Beginners at a reduced rate.

title: Purchasing & Supply Chain Mgmt
description: Introduces the critical function in Supply Chain Management (SCM) known as purchasing. Covers purchasing decisions, strategies, procedures, supplier selections, and negotiations. Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able to demonstrate competency in the following areas of SCM: purchasing strategies, legal aspects of purchasing, material management, just-in-time (lean) purchasing, procurement, strategic outsourcing, global sourcing, total quality management, cost and price determination, and bargaining and negotiations.

title: Coordinated Edu Exp Auto Svc 2
description: Designed to offer practical work experience within the Automotive Technology and Service field through participation in a supervised coordinated education program. This course integrates work experience with classroom and lab instruction.

title: Descriptive Astronomy
description: Consists of a conceptual introduction to the fundamentals of astronomy. Describes the motion, structure, and evolution of celestial bodies as well as the Universe itself.

title: Med Collection & Legal Issues
description: An advanced physician billing course focusing upon payment posting, fee schedules, HMO capitation reports, rejections, billable/non-billable services, claim status, and collections. Also discusses ethics in relation to billing and billing issues.

title: Pharmacology for NSG Practice
description: This course is designed to provide a foundation in the basic principles of pharmacology that utilize clinical judgment and the nursing process for safe medication administration. Legal and ethical nursing responsibilities and rationale for understanding current drug therapy involved in the common disorders of the major body systems are also included. Routes of medication administration, client education, and dosage calculations are components of this course.

title: Media Performance
description: This course offers experience in media performance, focusing on techniques for effective communication in front of the camera and microphone. Students will develop skills in announcing, interviewing, voiceover, and on-camera performance, building confidence in vocal delivery, body language, and character portrayal within a professional media studio environment. Emphasis is placed on industry standards, studio protocol, and adaptability across a variety of audio and visual media platforms, including independent and social media performance roles. Additionally, this class offers insights for those students interested in directing or producing to collaborate effectively with talent, and for media writers to explore the performance side of character development.

title: Solidworks Level II - Advanced
description: Focuses on applying orthographic principles to mechanical drawings, using the SolidWorks software program. Provides instruction on how to develop section views, auxiliary views, and isometric projections; how to lay out detailed drawings, including advanced assembly procedures and manipulation of assembly components; and how to apply dimensions for manufacturing to orthographic views.

title: Return to Your Source II
description: You asked for more and here it is. This class was designed for those who want additional guidance to continue their journey.

title: Photoshop CS5 Begin Course
description: Adobe Photoshop has been an indispensable image editing software application for many years. As an aspiring graphic designer you would like to familiarize yourself with image creation and editing using this software. Understanding the different tools and features available in Photoshop CS3 will help you to maximize your creative potential. In this course you'll use the several tools and features of Photoshop CS3 to work with images. You will learn to use layers and to apply layer effects and filters to create special effects. Additionally, you will use painting tools and blending modes to create shading effects. Finally, you will save images in print and web formats. Prerequisites: Students should be familiar with the basic funcitons of their computer's operating system, such as creating folders, launching programs, and working with windows. They should also have basic Windows application skills, such as copying and pasting objects, formatting text, saving files, etc.
prerequisite_text: Students should be familiar with the basic funcitons of their computer's operating system, such as creating folders, launching programs, and working with windows. They should also have basic Windows application skills, such as copying and pasting objects, formatting text, saving files, etc.

title: Pathophysiology for Nurses
description: Enhances basic knowledge and understanding of human diseases. Reviews the etiology and the pathogeneses of diseases using the system approach. Also reviews etiology, risk factors, long term effects, and prognosis of commonly occurring health issues.

title: Music Literature
description: This class explores the development of listening skills and traces the musical trends of different time periods of Western Music History. With a deep look at music through the lens of symphonic and chamber music, students can expect open discussions on the definition of art and a challenge to preconceived notions of what music is and how it can be experienced.

title: Creative Afford Rm MAKEOVERS
description: Break out of your room design dilemmas without breaking the bank! In this four-week fun, interactive workshop, interior designer Sheila Pettus wil teach you how to creatively and affordably redesign existing rooms. You'll learn how to measure a room, draw a floor plan, place furniture and accessories, use color and lighting, identify and select fabrics, choose flooring and more! You'll leave with a design board complete for a room of your choice. $15 material fee due to instructor.

title: Adventures in Australia
description: If you plan a trip to the "Land Down Under" or simply want to learn more about this awesome destination, join world traveler Doug Denard for an enthralling presentation about Australia. This vast land has tropical rainforests, unique wildlife, a world-famous opera house, exciting cities and beautiful beaches. Learn about Australia's people, history, culture, wildlife, coral reefs and even the famous Outback. Tips for travelers include the best time to go, airfare strategies, transportation options, solo traveling, currency and shopping questions, and must-see attractions. Bring your questions.

title: Chw Training W/ Indep. Study
description: Designed to train front-line public health workers to provide culturally responsive services in Michigan communities. The core competencies of the CHW are presented. They include: role, advocacy, and outreach; organization and resources; teaching and capacity building; legal and ethical responsibilities; coordination, documentation, and reporting; communication and cultural competence; healthy lifestyles; and metal health; 126 hours of training and 40 hours of intership hours. Internship consist of field experience in local agencies for those who are not employed as a CHW.

title: Start Clowning Around! NEW
description: If you want to bring laughter to the world and have dreamt of wearing big shoes, outlandish wigs, and a big nose, learn all about clowning in this Saturday class with professional clown Carey Ann. Under her skilled direction, discover how to apply clown makeup, create a fun balloon animal, dream up your unique costume, and practice some silly magic tricks. Wear comfortable clothing; bring any clown items you own. Held in a clown studio at 17841 Poplar Avenue in Riverview. Get ready to clown at parades, hospitals, festivals, parties and more. Open to persons ages 16 to 90. $7 material fee due to instructor in class. No other supplies required.

title: Hfv - Gentle Yoga
description: Experience the health benefits of yoga in this calming, therapeutic class. Designed and presented by professional yoga instructor Dianna Kinaschuk for beginners, returning practitioners, seniors, persons with limited mobility and flexibility, and those recovering from illness or injury. Gentle Yoga offers the postures and breathing techniques of Hatha Yoga in a format that can be modified to fit your needs and abilities, and at a gentle, slow pace. Open to persons of all ages and levels. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Create Projects - MS Word
description: Learn to create a flyer, invitation, letter and calendar using this popular program. Design your own resume and find out how easy it is to make a table. Create other projects as time permits. Learn many features of Microsoft Word as you build different projects.

title: Machine Tool Processes 1
description: An entry-level course in manufacturing processes involving turning, threading, drilling, reaming, tapping, and milling. Covers part processing, machines, and tooling used along with speeds and feeds and inspection techniques in relation to operations. Safety is an integral part of the course. Laboratory activities.

title: Herbs for Heart & Circulation
description: Almost 50% of the 1.5 million heart attacks in the U.S. affect persons under the age 65, and heart attack is the leading cause of death for U.S. women! Discover how to protect your heart with simple lifestyle changes, scientifically proven herbal remedies, and easy-to-learn techniques to reduce stress. Naturopath Mary Born will discuss simple tests to monitor your heart health, the role of aromatherapy in healing a "broken heart," and healthy nutrition for your circulatory system. Sign up now - the life you save may be your own! $5 materials fee payable to instructor in class.

title: ELL Reading & Listening 3
description: Develops high-beginning-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion qualifies students for ELI-104C. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Intro Digital Photograph Slr
description: Have a digital SLR camera and are ready to start getting the most out of it? In this course learn about the exposure triangle: ISO, Aperture and Shutter speed. Learn how to control your camera's settings and get amazing photographs. Learn the digital camera terminology and learn how to navigate your DSL camera! Class includes hands-on exercises and photo critiques. Required: Digital SLR camera or a camera with program modes such as Av (Apperture Priority), Tv (shutterspeed Priority) and M (full manual modes). Text book available, approximately $20.

title: New Year's Solution for Health
description: It's that time of year again when many people resolve to improve their health. Make this the year you slim down and keep the weight off for good with practical advice from Lorraine Stefano on how to achieve healthy eating and exercise goals, create healthy habits and stay motivated throughout the year.

title: CHW Training
description: Designed to train front-line public health workers to provide culturally responsive services in Michigan communities. Learning objectives include: role, advocacy, and outreach; organization and resources; teaching and capacity building; legal and ethical responsibilities; coordination, documentation, and reporting; communication and cultural competence; healthy lifestyles; and mental health. The program is 126 classroom hours with an additional 40 hours internship hours. Internship hours apply to those currently not working as a CHW. The internship consist of field experiences in local agencies where the focus is for the student to apply and integrate their course work into practice.

title: Lightarian Reiki Levels 1 & 2
description: Lightarian Reiki is the next higher band of energy Lightarian Reiki is a very masculine, yang energy, designed to really get your life moving! Included is the Lightarian Levels 1 and 2 with the Buddhic Boost. Manuals will be available for ordering after your attunement. Master Level required.

title: Orchids for Beginners
description: The exotic splendor of orchids has fascinated gardeners for centuries, and now beginners can grow these beauties at home. Suzanne Bucki of the Michigan Orchid Society will introduce you to common orchid species, teach you to recognize signs of disease, pest infestation, and watering problems, and show you how to pot and repot them. Register now to learn insider secrets about how to grow orchids and get them to bloom, including sunlight and temperature requirements.

title: Introduction to Social Media
description: Introduces students to different social media platforms and how they are used by people and organizations. Students will critically examine various social media forms in the context of society. Students will be exposed to the rules of ethical communication, create a basic social media communication plan, and gain basic understanding of social media analytics.

title: Maximize Your Financial Future
description: Do you fantasize about retiring at age 55 to follow your dreams? If so, join financial planner Ken Bloom to learn easy ways to save for retirement using a 401k, 403-b, Roth IRA or regular IRA plan. Get expert guidance to study the pros and cons of investment options, increase your chances for early retirement, and even protect your retirement investments if you change jobs. Discover ways to minimize taxes on withdrawals, as well as the hazards of borrowing from your retirment savings. Open to persons of all ages.

title: Introduction College Writing
description: ENG-131 is the first college-level composition course in a two-semester sequence. Students will be introduced to writing, reading, critical thinking, and using information at the college level. In particular, the course will help students develop writing that reveals rhetorical and academic flexibility.

title: Principle of Strength Training
description: Teaches the student how to establish comprehensive strength training programs for a variety of populations. Lessons focus on applied physiology of muscle contraction, proper and safe strength training exercise techniques, nutritional factors affecting performance, bioenergetics, program design, and periodization.

title: Advanced Powerpoint
description: Are you familiar with PowerPoint's tools and now want to give your slides presentations and printed materials a more professional look? In this course, Althea Lockridge, an award-winning editor & writer, will share techniques for balancing space, text, graphics, photos, animation, and color. PowerPoint: Basics and Beyond or strong basic knowledge of PowerPoint recommended.

title: Knotting Pearls for Beginners
description: Both trendy and classic, the pearl is the perfect piece of jewelry for almost any occasion. In this class you will learn two techniques for making pearl jewelry. Kathleen Robinson-Young will teach you how to knot pearls and beads on silk thread and how to string pearls into a stunning bracelet on flexible wire. You'll leave with your own handmade bracelet and matching pearl earrings. $25 supply fee due to instructor.

title: Det Westside Polish Churches
description: Many of Detroit's ethnic churches rival the beautiful houses of worship found in Europe. On this tour, visit three of these churches built by Detroit's large Polish community in its original settlement on the west side of the city: St. Hedwig, St. Francis D'Assisi (recently spotlighted in a local newspaper), and St. Cunegunda. The tour includes a Polish lunch (your choice of 4 entrees) at a restaurant in the area. Meet Don Samull, your instructor, at St. Hedwig's to begin the guided, self-driving and walking tour. Maps will be sent to registered students prior to the tour.

title: Start Home-Based Catering Bus
description: If you are passionate about parties and love to cook, you can start a home catering business with a small investment. Join professional caterer Andrea Taylor as she reveals the tricks of the trade for keeping food safe, equipping your kitchen for quantity cooking, calculating food costs so you make a profit, finding customers and pricing parties on a per-person basis. Learn to organize parties with ease and develop pleasing food presentations. To practice pricing, bring a calculator along with recipes for an entree, a vegetable and a potato. In one class you will cook and taste a full party menu.

title: Beginning Ballet
description: Introduces ballet movement and ballet movement vocabulary in French. Fundamental barre exercises are learned through the identification, execution, and understanding of their purpose. Students will learn technique at the barre, at the centre, and through the dance space. Classical dance is recognized, learned and performed throughout the world.

title: Special Topics
description: This course is designed as a special topics class, with its purpose and content tailored to meet the specific needs of an employer partner. The curriculum will be flexible and adaptable, evolving each term based on the current demands, projects, or skills required by the partnering organization. This approach allows the course to provide relevant, real-world learning experiences that align directly with industry requirements and expectations.

title: ICS 400: Adv Incident Command
description: In this final course of the ICS series you will explore how major and/or complex incidents engender special management challenges. You will discuss circumstances in which an Area Command is established and learn about circumstances in which multi-agency coordination systems are established. This course is designed to provide overall incident management skills rather than tactical expertise. Pre-requisite: ICS 300: Intermediate ICS for Expanding Incidents.

title: Adult Ballet 1
description: Enter the magical world of ballet, one of the most beautiful forms of dance ever devised. Under the instruction of Stephanie Fullerton, you will learn basic steps, techniques, and dance routines. Join other beginning ballet students and develop elegance, correct posture, and flexibility in a fun, adult environment. Ballet shoes are required.

title: Intro to Industrial Technology
description: Introduces students to the fundamentals of being a successful college student on the path to a profession in industrial technology. Subjects are divided into three categories: Student Self Awareness, Manufacturing Work and Culture, and Test Taking (technical), and include topics such as self-directed learning, critical thinking and problem solving, industrial safety policies and procedures, job outlook, and elements of standardized testing including graphic arithmetic, assemblies, mechanical comprehension, and troubleshooting. The class includes guest speakers from manufacturing and the service industry. The course utilizes digital learning materials, hands-on labs, and a student self assessment of their learning and study skills.

title: Metaphysics & Psychic Phen II
description: This workshop will explore "The sleeping prophet Edgar Cayce", "The Secret", the "Akashic Records", auras, intuition, spirits, and much more. Bring your imagination and an open mind. You'll be glad you did!

title: Basic Electricity
description: Covers the fundamentals of electricity as applied to the electrical field. Topics include the electron theory, Ohm's Law, circuits, magnetism, inductance, capacitance, and alternating current circuits. Utilizes voltmeters, ammeters, power supplies, signal generators, and oscilloscopes to construct circuits during lab activities. Also utilizes computer simulations. Introduction to writing reports. Labs involve breadboards and the above-mentioned equipment and components. Extensive laboratory activities.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Electrical diagnosis includes computer-controlled charging systems, battery, and starting systems, including one-touch and remote-start systems. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile.

title: Asian Art: India & SE Asia
description: Offers a global view on Asian art, culture, and history by exploring the major monuments, traditions, and civilizations of India, China, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. Covers important Asian artists and historical figures, artistic terms, monuments, sites, and time periods. Discusses art and architecture in terms of historical, social, and religious context. Analyzes and distinguishes various styles of art, and examines how and why certain styles are characteristic of specific times and places.

title: Intro to ASP.NET
description: Learn how to create exciting, interactive and professional web sites with ASP.NET. In this hands-on course you'll begin by adding the power of interactive controls to your web pages, and you'll discover how you can use style sheets to give every web page you create the same clean and consistent look and feel. You'll find out how to connect to a database and put the information it contains on public display. Then you'll develop a true community-based web site that allows users to register, log in, create and update profiles, and post messages to a forum. You'll also explore the issue of web security and learn some simple things you can do to secure your site against some common attacks.

title: Fancy Cookie Art Workshop
description: Instructor will collect $5.00 materials fee first day of class.

title: Holiday Crafts for Kids
description: Got a young budding artist at home? Here's an opportunity to encourage their talent this holiday season. Bring them to this craft class designed especially for young people. Experienced artists will work with them to make two gifts - a whimsical picture frame and a jewelry box - suitable for parents or other special people. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class. Match your child to an age appropriate section.

title: Monitor Your Child's Net Use
description: Do you know about MySpace, Facebook and Friendstar? Chances are they may be an essential part of your teen's social networking. Learn what MySpace and its contemporaries are all about, how to navigate them, create profiles, find and monitor your child's activities and more. Students must have a working knowledge of computers and the internet.

title: Greek for Travelers
description: Whether you are planning a trip to Greece or simply want to learn conversational Greek skills, this course will introduce you to the appropriate use of Greek in various social situations and will provide you cultural information about everyday life in modern Greece. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Thessaloniki, Greece, will teach you basic expressions that will help you when making accommodations, eating out, traveling, sightseeing, shopping and more. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $15).

title: Softball Prospect Camp
description: Are Interested in playing softball at Henry Ford College? The prospect camp gives high school sophomores, juniors and seniors an opportunity to be evaluated by Henry Ford College coaches to be considered to join the Hawks. Roster spots and/or an athletic scholarships may be offered during the Prospect Camp. Players will receive evaluations regarding their overall performance and skill level.

title: Lebanese Cooking VI
description: David Basha ground ball lamb mix spices and more. Stuffed lamb with rice. Stuffed lamb grape leaves.

title: Simplify Your Life - New
description: Imagine being able to find anything in your home within 15 minutes! Live this dream by learning the secrets professional organizer Theresa Brune uses to get control of paperwork piles, tidy cluttered closets and basements, and restore order to your home or office. You will find out how to set up paperwork systems, where to donate unused items, and how to get started with organizing. If you are tired of wasting time looking for things, want to stop paying for a storage unit, or are planning to move to a smaller residence, enroll today before you lose this catalog!

title: Stage Your Home for Sale
description: Making a home inviting to buyers is the key to staging your home. Darlene Rothman, a professional stager (ASP), will show you how to get your home ready for buyers to see so it will sell faster and for more money. Many recent TV shows and real estate articles are emphasizing staging as an important component in successfully selling a home. Make your home standout over the others in your neighborhood!

title: Pizzas & Pasta With Pizzazz
description: Surprise your guests this year with creative twists on familiar traditional recipes. Marilyn Szymanski-Suter, Executive Chef at Eastern University, will share some of her favorite quick and easy pasta dishes, including roasted vegetable lasagna. She'll also teach you how to make not-so-ordinary homemade pizzas, including buffalo chicken and BLT. Bring your appetite and an apron.

title: Fund: Appraising
description: This is a practical 30-hour course for anyone interested in real estate. It offers an ideal introduction to the fundamental concepts of appraisal and the everyday applications involved. Topics include residential construction, the data collection process, the direct market comparison approach, the cost approach, the income approach, reconciliation, and the appraisal report. This class can be applied toward the hours required for a Real Estate Brokers License, Limited Real Estate Appraiser, Certified Residential Real Estate Appraiser, or Certified General Real Estate Appraiser. Cost of text included.

title: Intermediate Modern Dance II
description: This is the second of three skill-based two-credit courses where students continue the applied study of modern dance technique based on skill level, supplemented by instruction in modern dance history, current choreographers and companies, terminology and the principles of time, space, and energy. The course provides students with the opportunity to explore multiple aspects of modern dance. Learning activities include physical practice, observation, discussion, reading and writing to cultivate kinesthetic, aesthetic, and intellectual understanding of modern dance.Classroom physical practice will focus on anatomically correct body alignment, awareness of the body in space, musicality, strength, stamina, and flexibility. Care of the body and conditioning for dance is also emphasized.

title: Basic Economics
description: Offers an overview of macroeconomics and microeconomics. Discusses markets, supply and demand equilibrium, firm decision making, aggregate output, monetary and fiscal policy, and the principles of international trade.

title: Heal Digestive Disorder-Nurses
description: Does eating cause you pain? Have you been diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome, ulcers, heartburn, bloating, or esophageal burning? Learn how others have stopped pain and received relief during this workshop by popular naturopath and herbalist Mary Born. Discover the basis for digestive problems and learn methods to encourage good digestion and the importance of enzymes. Handouts will discuss specific nutrients matched to symptoms and foods that speed healing of the GI tract. 2.5 nursing contact hours.

title: Learn to Knit Socks
description: Come and learn techniques necessary for basic sock needles, picking up stitches, grafting, working short rows - all useful in making other projects as well. Toddler size socks will be completed during this session. Students will need to bring a skein of worsted weight yarn, size 5 double point needles (bamboo recommended), a tape measure and one stitch marker. Some previous basic knitting experience required. Knitting homework will be necessary to advance and complete your socks.

title: Kickboxing
description: A fast, fun and energizing way to get or stay in shape while decreasing stress and fat! Learn realistic kickboxing moves and get an incredible workout designed to burn calories. This class provides great self-defense and mental discipline and will help improve self-esteem and self-confidence. No previous martial arts experience is necessary. No price reduction or refund for missed sessions.

title: Family Internet Safety
description: Get tips and tools for keeping both children and adults safe on the internet. Topics include: Protecting your kids from online risks and bullies, protecting your computer, protecting yourself from scams, protecting your personal information and creating stronger passwords. Prerequisite: Basic computer skills.
prerequisite_text: Basic computer skills.

title: Med Correspon-Elec Hlth Record
description: Focuses on the variety of medical correspondence found in ambulatory health care facilities. Introduces the procedures used for initiating and documenting patient care using an electronic health record system. Also covers medical office workflow, practice finance, medical billing and coding, clinical procedures, and administrative functions of the front office. Laboratory activities.

title: Intro to the Study of Religion
description: WR-130 is an introduction to the academic study of religion. In this context, "academic" refers both to an objective approach to course material and to the emphasis on scholars whose writings have helped shape the field of religious studies. Through the works of Kant, Otto, Feuerbach, Marx and others, students examine various ways that "religion," as a category of academic inquiry, has been defined. In addition, through the lens of critical theory, they explore the thought of contemporary scholars as it pertains to definitions of religion and the role of religion in promoting or reducing the power of given social groups.

title: Taste of Greece
description: Experience a culinary and cultural delight in a cooking series that will introduce you to the basics of authentic Greek cooking. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Greece's second largest city, is passionate about sharing her knowledge of food. With an emphasis on fresh herbs, spices and the health benefits of a Mediterranean diet, each session Zoe will teach you how to prepare a full meal, including main dishes of pastichio, spinach pie, mousaka, and some vegetarian ones as well. You'll then sit down and enjoy it in true Greek tradition.

title: Auto Tech Serv Exp Lab I
description: Provides real world work experiences and develops entry-level skills in diagnosis and repair of basic engine service, basic electrical, brake systems, suspension systems, and steering systems. Also covers general preventative maintenance procedures such as oil changes and tire rotation. Laboratory activities.

title: Theatre History
description: Offers a survey of theatrical production, including acting, directing, theatre architecture, artistic movements, and significant ideas that affect the development of theatre from the Greeks through the present. Emphasizes individual research and projects.

title: Spec Topics in Comp Info Sys
description: Explores selected topics as determined by the academic department and the instructor with emphasis on current Computer Information System trends. The specific special topic will be announced together with the prerequisites when offered. Student can repeat the course when different topics are offered, earning credit for each different topic.

title: Watercolor Sampler
description: Ever thought you might like to paint with watercolors? Here's your opportunity to learn the eight basic techniques while painting a small landscape picture. Join Saundra Weed for a one-day introduction to this art medium. Then decide if you'd like to continue exploring watercolor in her six-week class. $5 material fee due to instructor. Bring a bag lunch.

title: Surgical Procedures I
description: Designed to continue to develop the concepts from SRG-104. It features general, gynecology, genitourinary, orthopedic, otorhinolaryngology, and endoscopy surgical procedures. The course also emphasizes the scheme or steps as well as the instrumentation of these surgical interventions. One or two half day operating room (OR) observations are required.

title: Engineering Physics II
description: Designed to meet the requirements of engineering students and physics majors. Covers electrostatics, including electric fields, forces, and potential; electrical circuits, including resistors, capacitors, and inductors; magnetic fields and forces; induction; electromagnetic waves; and optical systems, including diffraction, refraction, and image formation. Four hours of lecture and three hours of laboratory activities per week.

title: Begin Comp- Seniors & Newbies
description: Instructor Mike Wilson is on the ground floor. He will show you how to properly turn your computer on and off, use the mouse, and understand print features. Step up to creating a letter, saving a file, and learn how to use your computer's calculator and address book. Explore and use your computer's free software programs. How about a quick game of Solitaire, and then from the Internet, let's play "Who Wants to be a Millionaire." Find out how to download software onto your computer. Let's look at your creative side by using the free and fun software "Paint." Class will explore other computer basic features as time permits.

title: MTWD NC Basic Shape & Size
description: Designed to introduce the concepts of shape & size description of normal surfaced, inclined (single-angle) surfaced, and cylindrical objects. Students use traditional and computer-based drafting techniques. This course covers projections of three- dimensional objects into two-dimentional representations and also the reverse process. Sketching and modeling of objects is covered. This class is for non-degree seeking students. Comparable to TAGD 110.

title: Nature Painting Wth Pen & Wash
description: Explore your interest in nature and landscape through pen and ink wash! This is an introductory level course. In each class students will learn about the tools and techniques used in pen and ink wash paintings and spend time creating a landscape of their own. A supply list will be discussed at the first class meeting.

title: Microbiology
description: Introduces microbes and their activities. Covers a wide range of material in lecture and laboratory exercises, including structure and function of diverse microbes, nutrition, growth, metabolism, microbial genetics, and the role of microbes in disease, immunity, and other selected applied areas. Emphasizes the biology of microbes and their interaction with humans and the environment, and in learning the techniques used to safely work with bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Recommended for students anticipating further study in biological or biomedical sciences. Three hours of lecture and five hours of lab each week.

title: Hearty Vegetarian Cuisine
description: Even avid meat eaters are adding vegetarian meals to their diets more frequently. Come learn about the value of meatless eating and experience the pleasure of preparing tasty vegetarian and vegan meals. Instructor Marilyn Szymanski-Suter will surprise you with a variety of new recipes. You're in for a treat! Bring an apron. Class limited to 14. Enroll early.

title: Music Theory 1
description: This course is intended for music majors as the first class in the music theory sequence. Students should be able to read music and have a basic understanding of key signatures and time signatures.

title: Indust Maintenance Externship
description: Provides field experience in an advanced manufacturing mechatronics environment with students working a minimum of four hours per week throughout the 15 week semester. Offers first-hand the activities, work-practices, and procedures of mechatronics maintenance personnel. Intended for those who have never worked in, or been exposed to, mechatronic manufacturing maintenance in an industrial production facility. Students will work with the divisional faculty/staff to identify field placements.

title: Tool, Jig, & Fixture Design
description: Focuses on the mastery of tool-design principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Covers tolerances, fits, principles of tool design, template jigs and fixtures, plate and channel jigs, and materials joining fixtures. Discusses economical design principles and the teamwork approach to various projects.

title: Capstone Clinical Education
description: This is sixth in a sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses emphasizing final mastery of all aspects of professional, entry level radiologic technology. Structured clinical experiences are offered in selected specialty rotations which may include Ultrasound, Computed Tomography, Magnetic Resonance Imaging, Radiation Therapy, Mammography, and Positron Emission Tomography.

title: Personal Property Valuation
description: Obtain a Personal Property Examiner's Card from the State Tax Commission by successfully completing this tested course. Approved by Michigan SAB for level III and Level IV certification credit. Textbook included.

title: Intro to Business
description: Presents an integrated examination of the American business system and the operations of business organizations. Covers theories of management and their application, production, operations, marketing, and financial management of firms.

title: Basic First Aid
description: Properly applied first aid can save lives, reduce recovery time, and lower the risk of lifelong disabilities. Attend this American Safety and Health Institute course and learn crucial information about caring for life-threatening emergencies; injuries to muscles, bones, joints, and skin and deeper tissues such as burns, scrapes and cuts; sudden illness such as seizure activity and shock; controlling bleeding; and handling heat and cold emergencies. Meets the requirements of group home and child care workers and is appropriate for any lay rescuer. Results in a three-year certificate. Bring a pillow or blanket and a pen or pencil.

title: SBCEU Rouge River
description: Rouge Educational Project teachers tour the Rouge Watershed while the workshop facilitator lectures on the history of the watershed and how human activities have impacted the river over time. Restricted to Rouge Education Project teachers.

title: Psychic Experiences of People
description: A "how to" on exploring your own psychic/intuitive ability and using it to improve your life! Kathy will provide participants with techniques to use in daily life to improve psychic/intuitive skills. Come join the fun.

title: Garde Manger and Menu Planning
description: Emphasizes the art of food preparation with a focus on cold foods. Covers the preparation and presentation of salads, sandwiches, hors d'oeuvres, cold sauces and dressing, pate/terrine, and sausage. Offers in-depth instruction in catering, menu planning, and American cuisine leading sauces and their respective small sauces.

title: Cookin' for Mama
description: Here's your chance to learn how to put together a spectacular meal in the "down home" tradition that would be a perfect treat for your Mama for Mother's Day or anyone else that you want to honor with these savory dishes. Join Chef Saniyyah as she prepares Butterflied Jerk Chicken, Shrimp Satay Skewers, Five Seasoning Salmon, Mama's Shrimp and Tata Salad, Grilled Vegetables, Sweet Potato Cornbread, Banana Pudding, and Mississippi Spice Tea. Bring and apron and an appetite for some amazing food!

title: Fa Flowering Ctnr Garden Deck
description: Learn to effectively assemble an attractive flowering planter using colorful, cold hardy annuals and accent plants. You will see what plants will bloom and survive through Michigan's Autumn (through Thanksgiving). Includes the recipe for successful soil mixture, choosing the right plants for your conditions, care and maintenance.

title: Contact Center/Help Desk Pract
description: Presents the components, job roles, and performance factors of today's customer contact center. Discusses techniques to handle incoming customer inquires regarding products, services, accounts, and equipment; how to respond to customer inquiries, requests, and complaints; and how to troubleshoot for the customer, with prompt and courteous service, and professionalism.

title: Evolutionary Astrology
description: On the day that you were born, at the time you were born, the planets created a unique pattern in the heavens. Through centuries of study Astrologers have discovered how your habit patterns, life lessons, talents and needs are shown in that heavenly display. Planets transitting (moving around) your Natal Chart will indicate cycles of life lessons you can expect, and how to best use them. The choices that you make determine how well you process those lessons. No one else is exactly like you. That's why you will meet neat freak Virgo types and messy Virgos, shy Cancers and "hostess with the mostest" Cancers. This unique course of study offers information to brand new Astrology students, as well as those with more experience. Because every class is based on your Natal Chart, you will receive updated Charts every week, as well as a personalized booklet explaining your unique Chart and a Forecast Report. From Compatibility to Career choices, to why and how some situations push your buttons, it's all there in your Chart. Participants are requested to send full Birth Data, Name, Date of Birth, Time of Birth, City, State & County of Birth by email to CL2 - 4Life@hfcc.edu to receive your chart at the first class. Join us and learn the symbols and signs of your true self. (no experience necessary).

title: Find Your Way on the Internt
description: Still wondering what is the big deal about that Internet that everyone is talking about? Do you have fears and reservations about what might be lurking out there? Learn to conduct effective searches and find almost everything you need to know. Address issues of Internet security and privacy. Learn to use and understand your own email. Investigate web browsers and see what works best for you. What is Social Networking and is it a good idea foryou? Why are financial institutions requiring that you have "direct deposit" and how do you keep it safe? Prerequisites: basic computer skills. Students will also need an active email account and the ability to access the email account. (You must know the email address and the password.)
prerequisite_text: basic computer skills. Students will also need an active email account and the ability to access the email account. (You must know the email address and the password.)

title: Performing Romantic Drama
description: An introduction to the performance style of romantic literature including: historical context, characterization and emotionalism. It will include public performance.

title: Pastels & Other Color Media
description: Whether you are beginning your creative journey or returning to it, learn to draw or paint with pastels to create richly colored, textured works of art. In this eight-week course, Kathy Boettcher, an experienced art instructor, will help you "see like an artist" and understand color, light effects, composition and more. Bring charcoal paper in various values (especially mid-value grey) and pastels. Individuals using other color media are also welcome.

title: Prepare Luscious Lebanese Food
description: Let Chef Kamal Hachem, owner of a local Lebanese restaurant, share with you his passion for authentic Lebanese cuisine. Get hands-on experience while you prepare such healthy delights as tabouli salad, baba ganoush (dip for pita bread), shish tawook (chicken with oregano), and falafel (vegetarian dish made from chickpeas or fava beans). You will also learn about the culture and famous hospitality of Lebanese people. After you finish cooking you will have time to treat your tastebuds to your creations. Bring an apron and your appetitite.

title: Ethnic & Racial Diversity
description: Introduces students to the sociological study of ethnic and racial groups. Students explore key concepts and issues in connection with definitions of race and ethnicity, immigration, patterns of group inequality and domination, discrimination and prejudice. Focuses on the diversity ofU.S.society and explores the experiences of Native Americans and immigrant groups, including those from Africa, Asia, andEurope. Emphasis on current patterns and consequences of immigration will be studied. Ethnic and racial diversity in other societies will also be explored.

title: Continuing Italian Language
description: Come experience a fun approach to speaking Italian and improving fluency. Beginning on day one, at least 90% of the class will be conducted in Italian, as you are encouraged to think in Italian, like an Italian. Gone are the extensive grammar and conjugation drills, replaced with activities such as inventing and acting out skits, drafting emails, watching Italian videos without subtitles, and planning a trip. Also learn to use many of the great resources available online, such as flashcards with an audio component. All skill levels welcome; if you know a little, you'll learn a lot.

title: Ready, Set, Read!
description: Learn what the newest research says about how children really learn to read and write and become a powerful guide to literacy development when you work with young children. Gain confidence in your ability to guide a child's literacy development, and take pleasure in seeing how even the littlest events can be really big steps in reading and writing success.

title: Introduction to CATIA V5
description: Introduces the basics of CATIA V5 and its functionality. Utilizes the part design, sketcher, drafting, and assembly design workbenches to make models that are properly constructed and constrained. Coursework explores how CATIA's tools and toolbars are used in these workbenches.

title: Hear Rick Bloom
description: Because developing nations are gobbling resources and commodities, successful investors think globally. For a crash course about opportunities in precious metals, natural resources like oil and gas, mutual funds that feature commodities, and real estate investment trusts, come to hear Rick Bloom, host of a popular radio show on investing. Selected as one of the top 250 Financial Planners by Worth magazine, Rick will give you timely investment strategies, a list of his latest "Rick's Picks," and a wealth of handouts.

title: Conversation Spanish Intermed
description: If you have completed Conversational Spanish 1 or have a basic knowledge of the language, continue developing your skills in this fun and informative intermediate-level class. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $30).

title: Empowering Stud W/Disabilities
description: Teaching students with disabilities is a rewarding challenge, and this course gives you the tools you'll need to succeed. No matter what grade you teach-from preschool through high school-you'll learn powerful strategies you can put to work immediately in your classroom. In addition, you'll gain the knowledge you need to understand and cope with the most common disabilities you'll encounter. This is a 6 week online course.

title: Principles of Image Production
description: This course provides an in-depth study of the equipment and principles involved in the production of diagnostic radiographic images. Emphasis is placed on the design, construction, and operational characteristics of the diagnostic X-ray tube and digital imaging systems. The course explores the physical interactions within the X-ray tube that result in X-ray production, the properties and quality of the X-ray beam, and the parameters influencing radiographic image formation and evaluation.

title: Family Genealogy on the Web
description: Been searching for your ancestors until your eyes are glazing over? Whether you're a beginning or experienced genealogist, you will add to your success by joining Michael Neill for this one-day workshop. You'll learn tips and techniques for conducting an online search, including making the most of various online search sites, using the Family History Library, and researching a "new" relative. Basic knowledge of computers and genealogy helpful. Each participant will have a computer to use.

title: Breakthrough for Health
description: A conference for the community and nurses. Price includes lunch & breaks. 5 nursing contact hours.

title: Applied Music (one Credit) 3
