description: Four sequential semesters of weekly half-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music lead teacher during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a fee to the private instructor.

title: Songwriting & Arranging
description: Well written songs have changed lives, made people fall in love, and changed the course of history. Explore what makes some of the greatest songs ever written work so well in this seminar-style class. The elements of form, melody, harmony, and arrangement that help make legendary songs stand out can then be applied to your own music and make your song the strongest it can be. The class is a combination of classroom discussions and small group instructor-led composition lessons. You should have a basic understanding of music and have a way of creating a song in fixed form, be it music notation, lyrics sheets, or recording software. Amateur and experienced songwriters welcome! Note: class meets for 1.5 hours each session, with small group lessons after class. Small group format will be explained by the instructor in first class.

title: Chorus 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of Concert Choir including preparation and performance of a broad spectrum of choral music from classical to popular, show tunes, and jazz. At least two performances are scheduled each semester, sometimes accompanied by band or orchestra.

title: Historic Dearborn: Arsenal
description: If you look carefully around downtown West Dearborn you will still find parts of the Federal Arsenal Grounds, which was moved from Detroit to Dearbornville in the 1830's. Still standing is the Commandant's Quarters at Michigan and Monroe. The old powder magazine, now the McFadden Ross House, is on Brady Street just north of Michigan Avenue. Mary MacDonald, our presenter, has been a long time student of the Arsenal, studying old records, journals, diaries and letters. She has even constructed a scale model. She will share rare old photos, stories and anecdotes of the people who lived and worked there.

title: Residential Design Studio
description: Introduces the interior design process and development of functional and aesthetically pleasing environments. Emphasizes programming, ideation, analysis, concept development, space planning, design development, documentation, and graphic communication culminating in the presentation of a residential project.

title: Relief for Headaches (nurses)
description: If you are one of the millions of people who suffer regularly with headaches and/or migraines, relief is available. Come to hear Dr. Barry Hobbs discuss new research about the causes of headaches and migraines. He will also discuss the symptoms, treatments, and alternative therapies for these painful challenges. 1.5 nursing contact hours.

title: Co-Op in Paralegal Studies
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: Sheet Metal Appl Triangulation
description: Covers the development of geometrical elements of structures by the triangulation method of sheet metal layout. Includes the development of stretchouts and making cardboard or sheet metal models of transition pieces.

title: Michigan Works! Workshop
description: Workshop designed to help Michigan Works! customers prepare for employment opportunities.

title: Still Life: Focus on Blue
description: Learn to control color mixing in the blue family.

title: Paralegal Internship
description: An unpaid internship which provides paralegal students with the opportunity to apply classroom knowledge to "real-world" situations by experiencing meaningful legal work in a professional setting. Students engage directly with lawyers and other legal professionals to gain work experience directly related to their paralegal studies major.

title: Jazz for Kids
description: Swing, salsa, western, and lyrical are just a few of the dances your child will learn in this high-energy class. Under the direction of Stephanie Fullerton, kids will learn proper dance techniques while exercising and gaining flexibility.

title: Drains, Wastes, and Vents
description: Introduces the Michigan State Plumbing Code and the proper selection of materials for the installation and repair of sewer, soil, waste, and vents systems. Covers proper procedures for the design, sizing, and construction of residential plumbing systems. Introduces commercial systems and plumbing cross-connections. The use of blueprints and isometric diagrams are reviewed throughout the course. Also includes how to test plumbing systems in their various stages.

title: Early American History
description: Covers Colonial America and the United States through the Civil War Period.

title: Tax-Free Retirement Plans
description: Small business owners and self-employed persons can shelter income by creating a tax-free retirement plan. Let taxation specialist and attorney Ken Bloom guide you through the maze of Keogh, Solo 401k, SEP, Simple 401K and other plans. He will discuss contribution limits, reporting requirements, and deadlines for establishing and contributing to each type of plan. Also find out what happens when you add employees. Get the unbiased information you need to create the best plan for you! If you act now, you may even save on your 2006 taxes.

title: Access: Level 2
description: Take your database skills to the next level in this intermediate class. Learn how to enhance your database designs by using the principles of table relationships and referential integrity, integrate data with other applications, modify a table design, find data with filters and queries, customize form and report, and create data access pages. Access Level 1 or equivalent skills recommended.

title: Weatherization Installer
description: This course will train students to install energy-efficiency measures to single family or 2-4 unit-homes using a variety of building science best practices to improve, safety, comfort, durability, indoor air quality, and energy efficiency. Course topics include job safety, identifying electrical, plumbing and roofing needs, preparing weatherization materials and maintaining tools, air sealing measures, loose fill and dense pack insulation installation, identification of mechanical systems, combustion safety hazards, and implementing weatherization scopes of work. OSHA, First-Aid/CPR, and EPA lead Safe RRP certifications as offered and math and communications courses.

title: Principles of Physics
description: Explores physical principles of motion, energy, fluids, electro-magnetism, waves, light, radiation, and the atom. Course is designed to meet the need for a one semester course in physics in many program areas including Allied Health, Teacher Education, Business, and Social Science. Three hours of lecture and two hours of laboratory per week.

title: Orig Fast Track Rdg & Math Crs
description: This is an 8-week course available to qualified individuals who are interested in improving their basic understanding of reading comprehension and essential mathematics. The reading topics include indentifying the author's purpose, summarizing infromation, critical reading, context clues and projecting, math includes integers, decimals, fractions, percents, algebra and geometry. Note taking, test-taking skills, time management and study skills will also be included.

title: Back Stiffness & Pain Relief
description: Acupressure stretches and point protocols are used to relieve chronic or acute back pain and stiffness. Turn your hands into healing tools to relieve backaches and pains - without taking drugs.

title: Adult Tap Dance 3 (4 Weeks)
description: Put your knowledge and your muscles to work in this advanced class.

title: Applied Music (1 Credit) 1
description: First of four semesters of weekly half-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester for teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Food, Culture, and Economy
description: Explores the interconnectedness of food, culture, and economy as an overlapping set of practices and approaches that transcend geographic boundaries. Examines the relationship between food and group identity, status, culture, gender, religion, politics, economics, class, and power using ethnographic, cross-cultural comparisons. Analyzes food traditions in the context of globalization, and looks at the political, economic, social, and cultural impact of global food system structures on the United States and the rest of the world.

title: Camp Baking Skills 5101
description: Cave in to your sweet tooth! This course is a continuation of the Culinary Kids Camp and dedicated to students with a passion for baking and pastry. Students learn the art of baking through a wide variety of hands-on activities. Students will prepare treats in a professional kitchen and will be introduced to baking fundamentals, such as safety, sanitation, measurements, cookies, quick breads, yeast breads, and various desserts.

title: Into Law Enf & Crim Just
description: Description: An overview of the criminal justice system in the U.S. Topics covered are: the history of law enforcement: the political, sociological, and philosophic, and background of police functions; and the criminal courts. Constitutional problems as they relate to the police function are surveyed, and the use of recent technology in criminal justice is discussed.

title: Heart, Cancer Prev Cook Class
description: Dietitian Lourdes Reyes Lore will show you how to prepare easy, healthful yet delicious dishes and fun, healthful snacks your whole family can enjoy. Bring an apron.

title: Fall Seasonal Vegetables
description: Not sure what to do with all those fall vegetables? Chef Marilyn Suter will give you some healthy alternatives to meat and potatoes. We will make a butternut squash soup, enjoy a root vegetable salad that could be eaten hot or cold, a cooked celery root side dish and more. Come prepared to cook and have a good time.

title: DTE Academic Readiness
description: DTE Academic Readiness- Math and English Remediation Course This course is intended to prepare DTE's summer interns who received low scores on the Accuplacer exam for TAMA 110 and BLDE 100 in the fall. The course will consist of English and Math remediation and college preparation. Students will leave the course ready to begin college in the fall. The course will take place at DTE Headquarters.

title: Get the Most Frm Family Search
description: Learn the ins and outs of the Family History Library, the world's largest family history library, in this all-day workshop. Drawing on over 20 years experience, Michael Neill will teach you how to effectively search its various databases, including the Card Catalog, Ancestral File, Pedigree Resource File, International Genealogical Index, and the 1880 census transcripts. You'll also learn how to use the Library's website to plan a trip to Salt Lake City or to your local family history library, as well as use the Library's many research aids and guides. Each student will have a computer to use. Class limited to 14.

title: Hybrid & Electric Vehicle Syst
description: An intermediate-level course covering hybrid and electric vehicle systems. Includes environmental impact of internal combustion engines (ICE). Also, includes theory of operation and service of electric propulsion systems, with emphasis on safety issues and concerns regarding the servicing of these systems. Also, covers hybrid system safety, hybrid systems in general (both series and parallel), plug-in hybrids, electric vehicles including battery types and fuel cell vehicles. High- voltage battery types, environmental impact, construction, and diagnosis.

title: Advanced Topics Skilled Trades
description: Provide the skilled trades community the opportunity to receive advanced specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics will be offered as identified by the Skilled Trades & Apprenticeship Division faculty, ST&AD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the ST&AD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Learners may repeat the course for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Intro to Microsoft Visio
description: In today's fast-paced, technology-driven workplace you need clear communication to get ahead. Learning to create professional looking diagrams in Microsoft Visio 2003 will take your skills set to the next level. Follow along as a seasoned industry professional leads you through easy-to-follow projects and exercises to design diagrams based on industry standards and best practices. You'll learn how to use Visio's many features and functions to make your diagrams pop.

title: Energy Systems Management
description: Presents the technical, economic, and regulatory aspects of energy efficient building energy management. Explores the latest energy and cost-reduction strategies. Focuses on laboratory exercises designed to simulate residential and commercial energy systems.

title: Adult Hip Hop
description: Is it dance or is it aerobic exercise? This high-energy, introductory class features the best of both! Work your body and soul to the hottest music craze this side of the border. You don't need to be a great dancer. Just come ready to cut loose and have some fun.

title: Drains, Wastes, & Vents
description: Discusses how to properly select materials for the installation and repair of sewer, soil, waste, and vent systems. Covers proper procedures for the design and layout of residential and commercial systems, and the use of blueprints and isometric diagrams.

title: Downsize Clutter for Sen/Begin
description: If you have years of accumulated belongings, clutter in the basement, a garage so full you can't park your car, or an estate to settle, this class is for you! Let professional organizer Theresa Brune teach you pain-free techniques for sorting and sifting through items, reclaiming and maximizing storage space, and parting with objects you no longer want. Get solutions that really work, discover how to recycle your discards for tax deductions, and find out which organizing tools are most helpful. Discover manageable steps to bring order to your personal environment.

title: Introductory Astronomy Lab
description: Emphasizes experimental work with the scientific concepts important in astronomy. Includes planetarium-based observation and/or computer simulations, as well as hands-on experimentation of astronomical phenomena. This course combined with ASTR-131 provides the student with a four-hour laboratory science credit. Two hours of laboratory per week.

title: The Healing Power of Crystals
description: As children we loved to pick up rocks and play with them, little did we know that rocks, crystals and gemstones have unique powers to heal on 4 levels: Physical, Mental, Emotional and Spiritual. In this class, we will explore several of the popular gemstones and their healing power, learn the different ways to cleanse the crystal, and see methods of programming and cleaning the crystal. We will also talk about Aura Fields and how to heal yourself and others using different crystals. Kristy will provide 3 different crystals to students for $10 payable in class.

title: Mini Candy Bar Cake Centrpiece
description: Create a colorful,dazzling two-layer centerpiece candy "bites", and yards of metallic curling ribbons. Once learned you'll be able to make "cakes" in different themes, sizes and shapes, using endless combinations of candies, gums and other confections. To see this very popular culinary craft do a Google search (Keywords: custom candy bar cake). Call us or vist the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for supply list (approx. $15-$18).

title: Sports Facilities
description: This course provides an understanding of the key principles, practices, and challenges involved in planning, designing, and managing of sport facilities. It also includes topics, including facility planning and design, staff management, marketing strategies, revenue generation, ancillary services, scheduling and operating.

title: Abc's of White Wine
description: Do you know the difference between a Chardonnay, Pinot Gringo and a Sauvignon Blanc? Are you intimidated by the vast number of white wines from which to choose? If you enjoy the taste of white wine, join Bruce Bell for a fun, educational evening designed to educate amateurs, as well as experts, about the parietals and the pleasures of drinking them. Participants must be at least 21 years old.

title: Beginning Tap
description: Introduces the basic rhythms, steps, and simple combinations of tap, a dance form derived from a combination of syncopated African rhythms, Irish clogging and folk dance. First class meeting discusses proper footwear.

title: Conversational French 2
description: If you have completed Conversational French or have a basic knowledge of the language, continue developing your skills in this intermediate-level class.

title: A+ Technical Support
description: Explores the hardware and software sections of the CompTIA A+ certification exam in detail. Covers installation, configuration, and troubleshooting of various hardware components. Also covers operating systems from a PC repair technician perspective and discusses how the operating system interacts with the PC's hardware, the boot process, troubleshooting, and interaction with application software. The student will engage in hands-on lab activities with actual hardware components, operating systems, and application installations.

title: Beautiful Brush Strokes
description: Explore the art of black ink painting (sometimes called Sumi-e or Oriental Brush Painting) in this one-day workshop. Artist Saundra Weed will teach you the four basic brush strokes for painting flowers, fish, butterflies, bamboo and more. Use these easy-to-learn techniques, rice paper, and bamboo brushes to create note cards and small paintings. You will also learn mounting techniques. $5 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Adult Ballet (4 Week)
description: Enter the magical world of ballet, one of the most beautiful forms of dance ever devised. Under the instruction of Stephanie Fullerton you will learn the basic steps, techniques and dance routines. Join other beginning ballet students and develop elegance, correct posture and flexibility in a fun, adult environment. Ballet shoes are required. Four week class.

title: Continuing Hatha Yoga
description: Continue building body flexibility, strength, balance, and alignment in this eight-week course. Using the postures, stretching, breathing and relaxation techniques learned in Beginning Yoga, you will increase mental concentration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat, and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Grab Dream Home-Buyer's Market
description: Find the house or condo of your dreams at a cost you can afford. Realtor Jim Arminiak will teach you how to negotiate the best price, how to comparison shop your future community, whether to get a buyer's agent, how to counter offer, and how to avoid common traps in home buying. A certified home inspector and a mortgage specialist will describe how to get a property inspected, how to negotiate repair costs, where to shop for a mortgage, how to lower closing costs, how to raise your credit score for a lower mortgage rate, and how to estimate your new mortgage payment and tax bills.

title: Dance Performance - Need Perm
description: This is a dance repertory class that provides opportunities to perform with the HFC Dance Company. Full dances will be taught by guest artists, student choreographers and teachers for performance. A dance technique background is required in any genre along with an audition and interview. Please call Diane Mancinelli at (313) 845-6314 for further information.

title: Have a Dream Wedding
description: How can you afford the wedding of your dreams? Join Deborah Zahn-Simmons, a wedding planner with 17 years experience, for an eye-opening Saturday class to get the answers. Develop winning strategies to organize your wedding and stretch your budget from the engagement to the honeymoon. Get insider information about where to shop and how to negotiate the best prices for your reception, flowers, invitations, cake, photography, gowns and wedding party attire. Fiances of all ages (and parents) are welcome to enroll. Bring a notebook and a brown bag lunch so you can swap ideas with other attendees.

title: Bldg Science Special Topic
description: This course provides students with an opportunity to explore emerging or specialized topics within the field of Building Science. Each semester, the focus of the course will vary based on current trends, faculty expertise, and industry developments-such as sustainable design practices, digital fabrication, adaptive reuse, building performance analysis, or community-based design. Students will engage in research, discussion, and hands-on projects that deepen their understanding of the built environment and expand their creative and technical skills. This course is designed to be flexible, encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration and critical thinking as students apply classroom knowledge to unique design and construction challenges.

title: Veggie Smoothies for Better He
description: Would you like to have more energy and clarity every day while increasing your consumption of healthy fruits and vegetables? Let Laurie Bigelow show you how to create quick and delicious smoothies, which are complete liquid meals containing fiber. Gain a new understanding of the importance of greens in your diet and the role of organic fruits and vegetables. Learn about the benefits of omega-3 oils and how fiber can reduce toxins in your body. She will share recipes and samples of several variations of a basic smoothie recipe. You won't believe how good they are until you taste them!

title: Secrets Puff Pastr & Fi Do
description: Delight your friends and family at your next event with elegant homemade puffed pastry and fillo dough appetizers. Learn the secrets to baking spinach pie, strudels with pastry dough, and other yummy pastry appetizers perfect to have on hand for the holidays or that special party. Instructor Marilyn Suter will have you turning out these tasty nibbles like a pro! Come prepared to bake and sample some fantastic appetizers! Bring an apron. Class limited to 14.

title: ELL Communication 6
description: Develops high-intermediate-level college communication skills and interactions of non-native English speakers. Satisfactory completion of this course, along with satisfactory completion of ELI-106R, qualifies students for ELL-182. This course does not transfer nor count toward degree graduation requirements.

title: Intermediate ASL I
description: Follows ASL-132 and is the first of two intermediate-level American Sign Language (ASL) courses focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops knowledge of fingerspelling, vocabulary and grammatical structures, and expands visual receptive and gestural expressive skills in order to comprehend and express essential ideas in ASL.

title: Reiki Level 1 Certification
description: Reiki (pronounced Ray-Key) is a form of energy healing used to treat the body, mind, and spirit. Learn from a Reiki Master instructor the history, principles, hand positions, chakras, and treatment used in this Japanese system. Upon completion, you will be certified and given an attunement as a Reiki Level 1 practitioner. $10 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Shakespeare
description: Focuses on reading, discussion, and written analysis of six to eight of William Shakespeare's comedies, histories, and tragedies. Readings can also include Shakespeare's non-dramatic poetry. Students also have the opportunity to observe, analyze, and evaluate his works in performance, either live or recorded.

title: Psychic Phenomena
description: Make the body, mind, spirit connection! Learn about "Angels" and "Spirit Guides." Plenty of time for Q & A and spot readings for workshop participants. This workshop will be presented in a fun, lighthearted and entertaining way. Come join the fun and take charge of your life!

title: Fitness Club
description: Join our Fitness Club and be eligible to attend unlimited $5 Fitness classes each month. Sign up once each semester.

title: Computer Security & Maint.
description: Take a deeper look at computer maintenance for laptops and desktops running Windows XP and VISTA. Learn routine procedures like cleaning the internals of the computer. Install memory, hard drives, fans, and video & sound cards. Also covered will be computer security subjects: Windows and IE 8 advanced settings, testing your computer's vulnerabilities, and an in-depth discussion of types and characteristics of various types of malware.

title: Final Control Elements
description: This course explores automation output devices including AC, DC, and servo motors, variable speed drives, relays, motor starters and sizing of components for various applications. Pre-requisites: CIMEL 106 or Instructor Permission.

title: Woven Beaded Bracelet 2
description: Continue to develop your weaving techniques learned in Woven Beaded Bracelet 1. You will use traditional basketry techniques to weave non-traditional materials into a stunning woven bracelet incorporating handmade beads, waxed linen, perle cotton and metal charms. Project will be completed in class. $28 materials fee due to instructor.

title: Ballroom Boot Camp
description: Let's get ready to Rumba.and more! Always wanted to see what ballroom and Latin dancing was all about? Now is your chance to experience many different dances all in one class. Get an introduction to Foxtrot, Waltz, Rumba, Salsa, Nightclub Slow, Swing and more! After taking this class, you should feel comfortable enough to do the basics at weddings, clubs, dance parties or other ballroom dance venues. Singles or couples may join, though many feel more comfortable bringing a partner with them. See you on the dance floor!

title: The Bark Covered House
description: Mary MacDonald, recently retired curator of the Dearborn Historical Museum and consummate story teller, will describe life in the early pioneer times when settlers came westward to settle the Dearborn area. She will reference the book The Bark Covered House Or Back In The Woods Again; being a graphic and thrilling description of real pioneer life in the wilderness of Michigan, written by William Nowlin, Esq., 1876. Learn about this land of trees where a father and son, walking up what is now Michigan Avenue, couldn't see daylight. View slides of rare old photos, a fascinating glimpse of life as it was for the early residents of Dearborn.

title: Introduction to Robotics
description: Introduces industrial robotic safety standards, historic timeline of industrial robots, industrial classification of robots, common industrial applications of robots, basic system components found in industrial robot applications, robotic motion concepts and common robot terms and definitions used in computer integrated manufacturing (CIM). Pre-requisites: CIMEL 111 or Instructor Permission.

title: Excel for Beginners
description: Set up and manage your home or small business finances with Quicken's powerful, easy-to-use features. Instructor Tom McMahon will show you how to free yourself from time-consuming tasks, such as tracking your income and expenses, recording deposits, transferring funds, reconciling bank statements, budgeting and preparing for tax time. He's been doing it for years! This course is appropriate for new and intermediate-level Quicken users.

title: Construction Blueprint Reading
description: Explores mechanical (HVAC), architectural, and electrical drawings required for energy technology, plumbing and pipefitting, electrical, and construction related trades. Covers print reading, interpreting common symbols, working notes, views, and title blocks. Introduces project management and estimating. Note: Basic measurement and math skills required for print reading section.

title: Com. Food Equip. Repair Basic
description: This entry level training for individual seeking employment in the commercial food equipment repair industry. This is a 24 hour course. Upon completion student will have a basic certification from the college.

title: Mechanical Power Transmission
description: Presents installation and maintenance of mechanical power transmission systems. Covers bearings, couplings, belts, chains, shafts, pulleys, and speed reducers used in the modern factory by skilled trades.

title: Garden Design
description: Spruce up your yard with ideas and tips for renovating or replacing your home landscape. Learn from Master Composter & Advanced Master Gardener Jane Geisler how to plan and develop pleasing designs through plant selection and placement, color, and garden hardscape.

title: NC Introd to Anthropology
description: cannibalism, hunger, race, and human intelligence are also explored. Various customs, myths, and beliefs, especially pertaining to women, are examined cross-culturally. Comparable to ANTH 131.

title: Applied Music (one Credit) 4
description: Fourth of four semesters of weekly half-hour private lessons intended to improve vocal or instrumental musicianship through study with a professional teacher. Students should contact the applied music director prior to or during the first week of the semester to confirm teacher assignments. A juried performance is required at the conclusion of each semester. In addition to tuition, the student must also pay a weekly fee to the private instructor.

title: Commercial Design Studio
description: Explores how to integrate the interior design process into a small-scale, non-residential design project focusing on functional and aesthetic elements, and the integral relationship to user needs. Also discusses how to incorporate human factors, Universal Design principles, and building codes into the design process. Emphasizes environmentally responsible product selection and specification.

title: Computer Litr for Online Train
description: A basic computer literacy course designed to provide participants with the foundation skills needed to effectively use a computer. Upon completion of the trainin, participants will have been introduced to such keyboarding, use of operation systems, file management, web browsers, searching, resources, and IP addresses. These skills will enable a succeful completer to use the computer for both personal and workplace needs.

title: Contemporary Guitar 1
description: Learn the basics of playing guitar. While strumming your favorite pop, rock, or folk music, you will acquire tools to use for any style of guitar playing. No experience with guitar is necessary. Bring your own guitar.

title: Alzheimer's Disease
description: Are you dealing with agitation, physical aggression, poor hygiene habits, wandering or hoarding in a person with Alzheimer's disease or dementia? Then preserve your sanity with helpful strategies given by Alzheimer's educator Kelley Fulkerson. She will describe the stages of Alzheimer's disease, reasons for and possible causes of difficult behaviors, warning signs that a problem behavior is about to occur, ways to communicate effectively with persons who suffer memory impairment and specific techniques that personal and professional caregivers can use to reduce such behaviors. You will receive very practical tips, since Kelley has served as a director of a residential care unit for Alzheimer's patients.

title: Handwriting At Its Best
description: Handwriting Examiner Karen Elly will focus on health problems and specific situations. She will show you how handwriting can give you clues to questions like: Doe she have realistic goals or is he a dreamer? Will she become defiant toward authority? Is your child really ADD or ADHD, or are they just being labeled? Is she likely to claim sexual harassment? Is he hiding a dangerously aggressive personality? My father had cancer, could I have it too? Find out all of these and more when Karen Elly teaches YOU how to look for these handwriting signals. You don't want to miss out on this one!

title: Trigonometry
description: For students in a technical or science program that requires knowledge of trigonometry. Topics include angles, right triangle trigonometry, circular functions and their graphs, trigonometric identities and equations, the Laws of Sines and Cosines, vectors, and a variety of physical applications. Course does not satisfy the MATH-175 or MATH-180 prerequisite. Requires a non-graphing scientific calculator for formal assessments and access to an online homework management system.

title: Art of Precious Metal Clay 2
description: Take your PMC skills to another level as you create three unique projects - a picture frame pendant, cufflinks and set of buttons. You'll also learn how to make your own tools and use different finishing methods. Prerequisite: Art of Previous Metal Clay 1 or prior experience with PMC. #35 materials fee due to instructor in class.
prerequisite_text: Art of Previous Metal Clay 1 or prior experience with PMC. #35 materials fee due to instructor in class.

title: Celebrate Paczki Day
description: Everyone's Polish on Paczki Day! Regardless of your heritage, come celebrate with Don Samull and learn almost everything there is to know about the history of this popular day. On Thursday you will enjoy delicious packzkis as you watch a slide presentation. The following Sunday afternoon you will meet at a Hamtramck area church for a unique, homemade Polish buffet.

title: Traditional Greek Spring Dish
description: Experience a culinary and cultural delight with instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and rasied in Greece's second largest city and is passionate about sharing her knowledge of food. In this class Zoe will thrill you with the delights of some of the traditional dishes of spring from the countryside of Greece. Start with triangles of cheese or spinach wrapped in a phylo dough and a colorful Greek salad. The main course will be Moussaka (a combination of eggplant, meat sauce and Bechamel sauce). A light dessert will complete the meal. Bring your apron and your appetite.

title: Epoxy Resin Explorer
description: Unleash your creativity and problem-solving skills in this hands-on epoxy resin camp, where creativity and science meet! Over four fun-filled days, you will discover how to safely work with epoxy, explore its many uses in art and industry, and create one-of-a-kind, personalized projects. Whether you're an aspiring artist, designer, or maker, this hands-on camp is your chance to bring your out-of-the-box ideas to life while learning about the endless possibilities of epoxy.Session Highlights: - Safety First: Master the essentials of working with epoxy resin safely and confidently. -Epoxy Basics: Understand how to mix, pour, and cure epoxy safely while exploring its artistic possibilities. - Advanced Techniques: Experiment with vibrant pigments, metallic, flakes, and embedding objects to craft unique designs -Industry Insight: Explore how epoxy is used in trades from small to large scale, like interior design, flooring, manufacturing, woodworking, and other leading professions.Participants will take home all their creations, along with the skills to continue crafting with epoxy resin on their own. Don't miss this chance to explore the endless possibilities of resin art while making lasting memories and new friends!

title: Organizational Behavior
description: Introduces psychological theory, research, and practice in the business environment. Examines selection criteria and predictors, training and development, performance appraisal, employee motivation and satisfaction, and occupational health. Analyzes and evaluates effective teamwork strategies, leadership theories, and union-management relationships.

title: Adv Studies in Audio Recording
description: This course serves as the capstone class for the Recording Arts Certificate program. It provides for special study in audio recording and is intended for students completing the Certificate program. Students choose the topic for study and design an instructional plan under the guidance and approval of the instructor. The students will complete a major project that demonstrates skills and knowledge learned in the program. The project will then be presented to the other students at the end of the semester.

title: Guided Read-Differentiat Class
description: Now more than ever, today's teachers are grappling with the question of how to reach struggling readers. While there are no quick fixes, thousands of teachers are combining the principles of differentiated instruction and guided reading with unparalleled success. Mixed in the right proportion, these popular strategies will help you build a balanced literary framework that gets results with even the most challenged learners.

title: Plmbg Materials & Components
description: Examines water distribution and pipe sizing. Topics include hot water systems, tanks, drains, wastes, vent systems, construction drawings and diagrams, and system sizing. Introduces material joining through soldering, and brazing, Includes the various plastic pipes and fittings being used in today's industry. Explores how to evaluate issues related to water pipe sizing in buildings, including concepts of water flow and water supply fixture units.

title: Fluid Pow Sys: Circuit Design
description: Explores the principles of fluid power design and troubleshooting as applied to industrial systems. Presents various hydraulic circuits and discusses functions, efficiencies, and troubleshooting guidelines within fluid power systems.

title: Lighting
description: Examines the processes, techniques, and principles involved in lighting the stage, studio, and location. Discusses the properties and uses of light, color media, and stage lighting equipment. Also offers the opportunity to participate in the stage lighting of an HFC theatre production.

title: Imaging Equipment
description: This course focuses upon the equipment routinely utilized to produce diagnostic images. The major emphasis will be on the construction and operation of the diagnostic x-ray tube, digital imaging systems, and fluoroscopic imaging systems. Basic principles of advanced imaging equipment such as Computed Tomography (CT) and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) are introduced.

title: Vegetarian Feast
description: Learn to present an imginative Mexican dinner rivaling any you might find on the Mayan Riviera, prepared vegetarian and dairy free! In this interactive class we will start off he meal with a tortilla lime soup, prepare pumpkin and black bean enchiladas, and finish off the meal with a chocolate mole mousse. Your new culinary mastery will be the talk of the party. Bring an apron and an appetite. Your instructors Sherry Duquet and Sandy Boulton are local food bloggers at www.ExploitsofaVeganwannabe.com

title: Survival Skills in the Kitchen
description: Are you tired of those peanut butter and jelly sandwiches and restaurant meals that have become the biggest part of your diet? There's no need to continue on that path any longer! You will be surprised at the meals you can prepare by learning a few simple techniques. Marilyn Szymanski-Suter, executive chef at Eastern Michigan University, will show you how to use convenient items from the store to create old fashioned pot roast, Russian orange chicken, quick stir fries, pastas, old-fashioned chicken soup, and more! Bring an appetite and an apron. Enroll early! Class limited to 14.

title: Legal Writing
description: Explores how to identify legal issues and perform advanced-level research using Westlaw and other Internet-based research databases. Covers application of the law to a hypothetical fact scenarios and presenting results in an objective memorandum, persuasive brief, or oral argument, using appropriate legal terminology and citation.

title: Group Dyna: Theories & Practic
description: Explores mental health theories used when working with clients and provides the students with the opportunity to practice these theories in a simulated environment. The course will focus on group dynamics as used in the treatment modalities of the mental health fields, education, and social work fields.

title: Lower Cholesterol Naturally
description: Do you wish you could lower cholesterol and triglycerides without expensive medications? Pharmacist Debby Danto will discuss simple lifestyle changes and nutritional supplements to help you decrease harmful cholesterol levels, protect your heart, and control blood pressure. She will also review the common cholesterol-lowering medications, their benefits, precautions and side effects. You will be given guidelines for safe cholesterol and triglyceride levels to help you understand your personal risk for heart disease.

title: Basic Electronics
description: Introduces the fundamentals of solid-state components found in electronic circuits. Topics include solid state diodes, field effect transistors (FET), silicon controlled rectifiers (SCRs), insulated gate bi-polar transistors (IGBTs), buck and boost converters, and bipolar transistors. Discusses characteristics of these components and some basic circuits in which they are commonly used. Utilizes a software program to simulate various electronic circuits. Circuits are constructed in lab using these components. Extensive laboratory activities.

title: Convert Room/Space to Rental P
description: Like to generate a little extra money by renting out a spare room and renovating a garage or basement as rental space? What are the concerns of sharing your home and property? Discuss how to find the right tenant and explore important topics such as liability and homeowners insurance. What about parking, utilities, access to laundry or cooking facilities, pets, noise, smoking concerns or visitors? Learn how to create a lease that will protect you and your property. Avoid problems by taking a good look at the pitfalls and concerns!

title: Modern American History
description: Covers the United States since the Civil War period.

title: Tips for Buy, Rent, Sell Home
description: Attorney and CPA Jonathan Goldberg will discuss the many tax and legal aspects homeowners should know when buying, selling or renting out their homes. This is information ou can't afford to miss! Topics include purchase agreements, the legal and tax aspects of renting your home, and mortgage and real estate tax issues.

title: PowerPoint Basics & Beyond
description: Why limit yourself to dull text-based slide presentations if you can enhance them with graphics? Discover the tools for designing exciting slides with drawings, clipart and photographs. Learn creative techniques to reposition, group and align content to give your slides that extra pizzazz. Identify the value of using the master slide feature. Create a slideshow. The instructor is a Microsoft certified and known as a PowerPoint "guru." Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Text provided.

title: Access 1
description: Simplify customer records, personnel files, sales records, membership directories, and more using this popular database software. You will learn and practice the critical skills necessary to create a database and enter, find, edit, and report on the data it contains. The course is designed for the new user of Access and assumes no experience with databases. Text provided. Prerequisite: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.
prerequisite_text: Intro to Computers 1 & 2 or equivalent knowledge. Students may wish to bring a USB flash drive to save class work.

title: Stage Your Home for Sale
description: If you are planning to put your home on the market this spring and it has the "lived-in" look, resembles the television set for "That '70s Show," or needs a general sprucing up, attend this timely seminar. Darlene Rothman, a professional home stager, will show you how to enhance the salability of your home using inexpensive tips from a new trend called "home staging." She'll discuss furniture arrangement, decluttering, paint colors, traffic flow and more. Feel free to bring pictures of your home.

title: A Hunger for Drawing
description: Food is a common human experience: we all must eat to survive and food ia a major theme in many of our lives. Aside from that, food also has the benefit of serving as subject matter in a way that can help one to develop their skills in drawing. By using food as inspiration we can develop our skills in fundamentals of drawing, such as line, space and an unconventional approach. It is recommended that you don't come to class hungry... unless you are hungry for drawing!

title: Tech Foundation SCM
description: Introduces students to the information necessary to be competent in the activities common across all facilities within the supply chain including safety, quality control, communications, teamwork, good workplace conduct, familiarity with the key computer systems that underpin supply chain operations, product receiving, product storage, order processing, packing and shipment, inventory control, safe handling of hazardous materials, evaluation of transportation modes, customs, and dispatch and tracking operations. Prepares students to successfully pass the Certified Logistics Associate assessment and the Certified Logistics Technician assessment that would allow Certified Logistics Associate (CLA) and a Certified Logistics Technician (CLT) recognition by the Manufacturing Skill Standards Council (MSSC). MSSC is a 501(c)3 non-profit, national, industry-led, training, assessment and certification organization. BSC-130 is the first course in the series of 5 BSC courses.

title: Jazzericse Walk in Program
description: If you would like to 'try out' jazzercise or participate in single sessions for other reasons, you may enroll in and pay for class at the door on a walk-in basis.

title: An Olde English Christmas
description: Enjoy a traditional English Christmas with Don Samull as he takes you on a slide tour of London and the surrounding area. In addition to "visiting" the sites, you'll enjoy an English holiday dessert and view a display of English holiday items. On Saturday you'll meet at an area restaurant to share an English holiday meal. Cost of the meal included in course fee.

title: HTML/CSS Web Programming
description: Covers HyperText Markup Language (HTML) coding practices for both current and future browsers. Uses HTML, Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) extensively validating according to the guidelines of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C). Introduces JavaScript language. Includes the creation and publishing of a website using HTML and CSS.

title: Hatha Yoga for Beginners
description: Hatha Yoga is an ancient system of exercise that includes stretching, breathing, posturing, and total relaxation techniques. Through Yoga your body will develop flexibility, strength, balance and alignment; and you will achieve mental con- centration, relaxation and self-awareness. Bring an exercise mat and wear comfortable, stretchable clothing.

title: Advanced Surgical Techniques
description: Laboratory course designed to prepare students for clinical externship. Students will continue to practice aseptic and sterile techniques used in the perioperative care of the surgical patient. This course focuses on skills developed in SRG-140 and incorporates "mock surgery" and advanced clinical skills in a lab setting. Students practice techniques in a guided lab setting.

title: Intro Feng Shui:Art of Placmt
description: Feng Shui (pronounced Fung Shway) is a 5,000 year old set of practices which creates the perfect balance of energy in an environment. Starting with the external site, working down to the building and then to each room in your house, office, store or business, learn how to apply the principles. Enhance your health, relationships, wealth, self-expression, creativity, prosperity and career by re-aligning the energy flow within your space.

title: DC and AC Motors
description: Covers DC and AC motor fundamentals. Includes DC generators; series, shunt, and compound DC motors; single-phase and three-phase AC induction motors; and synchronous, wound rotor, and universal motors. Compares AC alternators to DC generators.

title: Intro to Engineering
description: Introduces the engineering profession, problem solving fundamentals, applications, fields of study, career paths, teamwork skills, and ethics necessary in the engineering profession. Course work includes projects that deal with real world application of these aspects of engineering as related to engineering problems.

title: Elementary German II
description: Further builds reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German, focusing on communication in a cultural context. Develops students' knowledge of vocabulary, pronunciation and grammatical principles in order to comprehend and express everyday ideas in both spoken and written German. Presents the cultures of German-speaking countries.

title: UG NX Level II - Intermediate
description: Builds on UG NX concepts introduced in DRAF-131. Explores different task environments. Topics include part families, expressions, assembly simulation, animation, and functional application of dimensions in drafting techniques for manufacturing.

title: Mount Vernon
description: Celebrate George Washington's birthday, February 18, with Mary MacDonald, Dearborn Historian and consummate story teller, as she takes you back into an era that is remembered for its history. Mary will share what she knows about Mount Vernon.

title: Bio: Organisms, Genes, Ecology
description: A one-term course that, with BIOLOGY 152, collectively forms the majors-level introductory biology course unit designed to meet the needs of students interested in transferring to four-year institutions and majoring in biological sciences or related fields, as well as students interested in entering programs in pharmacy, medicine, veterinary medicine, dentistry, and related areas. Course focuses on the nature of science, the diversity and the unity of life, evolution, inheritance, ecology, plant function and reproduction, animal physiology, and animal development. Through laboratory investigations, students develop cognitive and laboratory skills and gain experience with model organisms used in many areas of biological research. Three hours of lecture and three hours of lab per week.

title: Build Wireless Home
description: Wireless networking is hot! And why not? No more bothersome wires. . .minimal setup. . . The ability to run many computers without buying a hub. . .and the freedom to move around with your laptop. Find out how easy it can be to build your own home or small office wireless network. You'll learn how to select, access, configure and test a wireless router using a windows platform, as well as how to use a browser to access the Internet.

title: ABC's of Intellect Property
description: This introductory overview of copyright, patent, and trademark rights will discuss the origin of these rights, why these rights are important, what these protections provide for their owners, and how to go about obtaining this protection for your own creative work, inventions, and proprietary marks. Also discussed will be some of the pitfalls to avoid when dealing in the intellectual property arena. Len Charla is the President of Countinghouse Press, Inc., and a former Chair of the Arts, Communications, Entertainment and Sports Section of the State Bar. Note: This course is not, and should not be considered to be, legal advice; students wanting legal advice should consult an attorney of their choice. A $4 handout fee will be collected in class by the instructor. Pre-requisite: Publish Your Book Yourself.

title: Clinical Education I
description: This is the introductory course in a five-course sequence of structured, competency based clinical courses designed in concert with American Society of Radiologic Technologists (ASRT) curriculum and American Registry of Radiologic Technologist (ARRT) competencies. The course offers exposure to chart recognition, basic patient assessment, patient communication, and basic departmental procedures and policies. Equipment manipulation, chest, bony thorax, abdomen, and upper extremity radiographic positioning are emphasized.

title: Musical Theater Techniques
description: Production of Oliver.

title: Special Topics Skilled Trades
description: TProvides specialized training and education in specific topics and concepts identified as relative to current maintenance issues. The precise topic or topics offered is identified by the Skilled Trades Apprenticeship Division faculty, STAD Steering Committee, employers with students enrolled in the STAD program, and/or other employers or individuals who have recognized a need for training. Course may be repeated for credit when different topics are offered.

title: Modern Dance
description: Modern dance is a language of the body in motion. Learn movements designed to develop grace, increase flexibility and coordination, enhance rhythmic sensitivity, and develop body awareness to allow for freedom of expression. In this fun, unique class,dancer Stephanie Fullerton, will teach the innovative Horton technique, which combines diverse elements from ballet, jazz, syncopation, and even Native American dances. Wear comfortable clothing. Dance shoes are optional.

title: Kickboxing Aerobics
description: Punch and kick your way to fitness with a popular new form of aerobic exercise. Dena Raptis, an experienced instructor, will combine music with aerobic, cardiovascular, body toning and self-defense tactics in this high-energy, fun class. You'll improve your strength, muscle tone and endurance while you reduce body fat and stress levels.

title: Handwriting Basics
description: Did you know that handwriting analysis can detect lying, jealously, fears, career strengths, intelligence, self-esteem states, irritability and many other personality traits? While handwriting analysis is fun and informational, it can be upheld in a court of law as evidence. Instructor Karen Elly-Trapani is a Certified Forensic Handwriting Examiner and has examined forged documents for police departments, appeared on local radio stations, and published articles for magazines. Bring handwriting samples. $10 materials fee, payable to the instructor in class. Class limit - 15 students.

title: Return to Your Source
description: Live with clarity and passion. A spiritual journey - an inward voyage of discovery designed to bring balance to your outward behaviors and inner consciousness - can help you achieve this. Your journey is as unique as your are, and whether you are taking your first steps or you find yourself wandering along the way, here is a down-to-earth, non-denominational approach to guide you to your Source. Come learn some time-honored disciplines that can help you repel negativity, attract positive energy, and embrace a richer life experience. Instructor's book included.

title: Learn to Belly Dance
description: Join seasoned pro Abida for six exciting sessions of belly dancing. Learn the classic movements and experience the exhilaration of moving your body with the music in this ancient dance tradition. Have fun and dance your cares away! All fitness levels welcome. Wear comfortable clothing.

title: Get Solutions for Reactive Gut
description: A "gut reaction" is more than a catchy phrase. Your gut, otherwise known as intestines, often absorbs negative emotions, unhealthy food, or a toxic environment. It may respond with gas, bad breath, heartburn, ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, allergies, stomachache, gluten intolerance, skin rashes, depression, ADD, or lowered resistance to illness. Let naturopath Mary Born teach you basic principles to restore health and activity to your gut and rid your body of toxins. Discover important scientific research often overlooked by mainstream medicine. Plan to leave with a "belly laugh" on your journey to better health.

title: Intro to MS Word: the Basics
description: Learn to create attractive documents for business or personal use with this powerful word processing program. Designed especially for the novice, this course will teach you how to create, edit, save, and print documents in Word 2000; move and copy text; use Word 2000's AutoText and AutoComplete features; format text, paragraphs, and documents; and use the Find and Replace and spelling and grammar checking features. Microsoft Office User Specialist approved courseware (MOUS) materials included. Intro to PCs with Windows or equivalent knowledge recommended.

title: Intermediate Feng Shui
description: Experience immediate positive changes in your life as you apply the principles of feng shui more specifically to your personal environment. Susan Haigfleigh, the instructor, will combine her practical, logical training as an Architect and Interior Designer and her intuitive/metaphysical training as she assists you in creating a balance of the best of Eastern and Western thinking, practice, and philosophy. Prerequisite: CLNC 7031
prerequisite_text: CLNC 7031

title: Auto Fuel Management Systems
description: Introduces the types of fuels and air/fuel ratio requirements of the various fuels used in current automotive engines, along with the construction and operation of fuel delivery systems. Discusses fuel pumps, fuel lines, fuel filters, and storage tanks. Fuel system types include returnable electronic fuel injection, returnless electronic fuel injection, and gasoline direct fuel injection. Also discusses elements of automotive emissions and their control, use of scan tools, PVT, 5-gas analyzer, and DSO and ASE performance tests.

title: Cardiopulmonary Diagnostics
description: This course discusses basic pulmonary function testing and electrocardiographic testing. Basic theory and techniques for testing are covered. Students are expected to perform and evaluate these tests for use by the physician. This course is offered as an eight-week course.

title: Real Estate Fund
description: If you are planning to invest in real estate, or preparing for the Michigan Real Estate Exam, this course is for you! Topics include real estate laws, rules and regulations, contracts, financing, ownership, brokerage, valuation, and fair housing. This program is state licensed for 40 hours of instruction and is open to the general public. Textbook included.

title: Chinese Medicine: Seasons
description: Explore the dynamic fluctuation of life as it is expressed in nature and in your life. Using concepts from traditional Chinese medicine, determine your five-phase type and learn to live wisely according to life's daily, weekly and yearly cycles. Discover wonderful oriental foods and learn how to choose ordinary Western foods to manage weight and build a healthy lifestyle. Wear loose, comfortable clothing. Prerequisite: "Chinese Medicine in Your Life: an Introduction" is strongly recommended.
prerequisite_text: "Chinese Medicine in Your Life: an Introduction" is strongly recommended.

title: Create Camcorder Memories
description: Want to produce impressive videos of weddings, anniversaries, reunions, parties, recitals and other celebrations? Let Frank Jamal, a 24-year veteran of media production, teach you how to shoot better videos, add special effects, edit goofs and unneeded footage, apply soundtracks and more. You'll be surprised at the professional look you can achieve. Bring your analog or digital video camera.

title: Basic eBay
description: Thinking about getting started with eBay? Come learn the fundamentals of buying and selling on eBay including how to get started, research your item, pricing, shipping, feedback and more. If you are ready for a great overview of the buying and selling process - plus specific tips and strategies - sign up now! Basic computer and internet skills are required.

title: Relieve Pain Without Drugs
description: If you have stiff, achy joints from any type of arthritis, learn to relieve the pain using your fingertips. Enroll in this soothing Saturday class with Esther McCormick, a Shenjing acupressure specialist who practiced in Sweden for 13 years. Find out how to gently press specific spots on your body to activate pain relief processes and nourish your tendons, joints, bones and cartilage. Urge a friend or spouse also to register for this class so you can practice together. Wear loose, comfortable clothing. Bring 2 tennis balls, a towel and a clean sock.

title: Intro to Industrial Technology
description: Introduces students to the fundamentals of being a successful college student on the path to a profession in industrial technology. Subjects are divided into three categories: Student Self Awareness, Manufacturing Work and Culture, and Test Taking (technical), and include topics such as self-directed learning, critical thinking and problem solving, industrial safety policies and procedures, job outlook, and elements of standardized testing including graphic arithmetic, assemblies, mechanical comprehension, and troubleshooting. The class includes guest speakers from manufacturing and the service industry. The course utilizes digital learning materials, hands-on labs, and a student self assessment of their learning and study skills.

title: Metaphysics & Psychic Phen II
description: This workshop will explore "The sleeping prophet Edgar Cayce", "The Secret", the "Akashic Records", auras, intuition, spirits, and much more. Bring your imagination and an open mind. You'll be glad you did!

title: Basic Electricity
description: Covers the fundamentals of electricity as applied to the electrical field. Topics include the electron theory, Ohm's Law, circuits, magnetism, inductance, capacitance, and alternating current circuits. Utilizes voltmeters, ammeters, power supplies, signal generators, and oscilloscopes to construct circuits during lab activities. Also utilizes computer simulations. Introduction to writing reports. Labs involve breadboards and the above-mentioned equipment and components. Extensive laboratory activities.

title: Automotive Diag & Engine Eval
description: An advanced-level course in the application of diagnosing, locating, and correcting trouble encountered in automotive service, using various types of testing equipment. Engine diagnosis includes variable cam timing and variable displacement engine systems. Electrical diagnosis includes computer-controlled charging systems, battery, and starting systems, including one-touch and remote-start systems. Fuel system diagnosis includes pressure and injector tests using active commands and relative flow analysis. Coursework also explores various common problems that occur in the servicing of the modern automobile.

title: Asian Art: India & SE Asia
description: Offers a global view on Asian art, culture, and history by exploring the major monuments, traditions, and civilizations of India, China, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. Covers important Asian artists and historical figures, artistic terms, monuments, sites, and time periods. Discusses art and architecture in terms of historical, social, and religious context. Analyzes and distinguishes various styles of art, and examines how and why certain styles are characteristic of specific times and places.

title: Intro to ASP.NET
description: Learn how to create exciting, interactive and professional web sites with ASP.NET. In this hands-on course you'll begin by adding the power of interactive controls to your web pages, and you'll discover how you can use style sheets to give every web page you create the same clean and consistent look and feel. You'll find out how to connect to a database and put the information it contains on public display. Then you'll develop a true community-based web site that allows users to register, log in, create and update profiles, and post messages to a forum. You'll also explore the issue of web security and learn some simple things you can do to secure your site against some common attacks.

title: Fancy Cookie Art Workshop
description: Instructor will collect $5.00 materials fee first day of class.

title: Holiday Crafts for Kids
description: Got a young budding artist at home? Here's an opportunity to encourage their talent this holiday season. Bring them to this craft class designed especially for young people. Experienced artists will work with them to make two gifts - a whimsical picture frame and a jewelry box - suitable for parents or other special people. $25 materials fee due to instructor in class. Match your child to an age appropriate section.

title: Monitor Your Child's Net Use
description: Do you know about MySpace, Facebook and Friendstar? Chances are they may be an essential part of your teen's social networking. Learn what MySpace and its contemporaries are all about, how to navigate them, create profiles, find and monitor your child's activities and more. Students must have a working knowledge of computers and the internet.

title: Greek for Travelers
description: Whether you are planning a trip to Greece or simply want to learn conversational Greek skills, this course will introduce you to the appropriate use of Greek in various social situations and will provide you cultural information about everyday life in modern Greece. Instructor Zoe Styloglou, who was born and raised in Thessaloniki, Greece, will teach you basic expressions that will help you when making accommodations, eating out, traveling, sightseeing, shopping and more. Purchase textbook in class (approximately $15).

title: Softball Prospect Camp
description: Are Interested in playing softball at Henry Ford College? The prospect camp gives high school sophomores, juniors and seniors an opportunity to be evaluated by Henry Ford College coaches to be considered to join the Hawks. Roster spots and/or an athletic scholarships may be offered during the Prospect Camp. Players will receive evaluations regarding their overall performance and skill level.

title: Neon Basics
description: Come visit with a traditional neon sign bender as he demonstrates how neon signs are made. Instructor Al Bodrie will take you through a series of steps with some hands on exposure to show you how each tube is hand manipulated to create that glowing sign. At the end of the session you will leave with a completed sample of neon work. Please wear cotton clothing, no hand jewelry (other than wedding bands). A materials fee of $10 is payable to the instructor in class. Class is held at 25331 Trowbridge, Dearborn.

title: Leadership, Organizing & Publi
description: This course provides the leadership tools and theories to help students create change through hands-on work in community organizations. The course will use a project-based learning to examine the strategic nature of coalition building, networking, and goal/power mapping. This will be done within diverse communities to provide help students advocate with the experience for and effect change within an organization. The course is designed for both those students already engaged in community-based organizations and partnerships as well as students who are interested in working in/and or developing such organizations.

title: Honors Directed Study
description: In this sophomore-level directed study, students are challenged to apply the knowledge and research skills acquired in their freshman year in the Honors Program. Students work individually with a faculty member of their choice on a mutually-agreed-upon study topic. Students present summaries of their research before a meeting of Honors Program students and faculty held at the end of each semester.

title: Tax Acct I
description: Examines the basic application and rationale of the federal income tax law and provides training through specific problem assignments. Emphasizes tax preparation for individuals.

title: Weld Metallurgy-Non-Ferrous
description: Examines the mechanical and physical properties, and the crystalline structures of non ferrous metals. Also covers alloying and phase diagrams associated with non-ferrous metals like aluminum and copper.

title: Fund. of Surgical Technology
description: The course introduces the fundamentals of operating room techniques, emphasizing principles and practices of asepsis and sterilization. Physical and psychosocial aspects of the patient are explored. Included are the roles and responsibilities of the surgical technologist and other surgical team members; identification of common equipment, instruments, and supplies; the perioperative phases and environment; students practice scrubbing, gowning, and gloving, and preparation of the sterile surgical fields.

title: Co-Op in Paralegal Studies
description: Cooperative education is a structured method of combining classroom-based education with practical work experience. A cooperative education experience, commonly known as a "co-op," provides academic credit for structured employment experience. Work experience must be directly related to the student's declared major to be eligible.To register for this course, a student must have completed 50% of core coursework, maintain an overall GPA of 2.0 and a program specific GPA of 2.5.

title: More Photoshop Elements
description: Learn to use some of the more advanced features of Photoshop Elements 4 as instructor Doug Denard teaches you to work with filters and distortions, building layered compositions, and working with text and shapes, special effects and much more. Prerequisite:Photoshop Elements 1. Text provided.
prerequisite_text: Photoshop Elements 1. Text provided.

title: Buy & Sell on Ebay - Beginners
description: eBay has become the world's online auction king-an international marketplace where people meet to buy and sell everything! Here's your chance to learn to make money by selling things like unused gifts or vintage items from an estate. Tom McMahon, a veteran eBayer, will teach you how to locate excellent deals on hard-to-find items, reach millions of potential buyers for your items, maximize your selling price, minimize your costs as a buyer, and more. Basic computer skills needed.

title: Smart Couples Finish Rich
description: If you are among the many couples trying to navigate the complex waters of joint finance, attend this fun, interactive seminar and discover how to plan your financial future as a team. You'll discuss the differences in how men and women approach financial decisions, be able to organize your finances at home in less than an hour, discover the five biggest mistakes couples make with money and more.

title: Quick & Easy Hand Quilting
description: Experience the warmth and friendship of an old-fashioned quilting bee. Sandra's new and easy hand quilting designs let you "sew as you go" - anywhere and anytime you have a few extra minutes. You will be surprised at how quickly quilt blocks can be made and turned into pillows, totes, wall hangings and full-size bedspreads as you join these squares together using a tried and true dressmaker's technique. Bring a fabric scissors, straight pins, and your favorite sewing needle. Project completed in class. $10 materials fee due to instructor. Appropriate for all levels.

title: Chinese Med: Waking the Dragon
description: Combat modern stress and burnout, gather and conserve your vital energy through traditional Chinese medicine practices. Explore and practice the ancient energy techniques of Chi Gung. Also practice stimulating acuponts to experience optimal personal energy and restoration of stamina.

title: Lebanese Cooking
description: Baba ghanouj, Lebanese tabouli salad, tomatoes kibee.

title: Principles of Management
description: Examines the functions, principles, history, environments, and philosophy of modern management as well as the nature and structure of organizations. Emphasizes recent developments in organizational decision making, styles of managerial leadership, and the role of effective communication in management. Also explores motivating, managing, recruiting, and terminating employees.

title: Prod Mgt & Tech Prod for Theat
description: For intermediate students to learn by practicing personnel management, research and document preparation, house mangagement, and load in and technical rehearsal preparation.

title: Basic Photo
description: Greatly improve the quality of your photos! Whether you are a beginner or an experienced photographer, you will gain a better understanding of your camera and its functions, photographic tools and materials, and basic photographic principles. This class is designed for users of 35 MM SLR cameras. An optional working field trip on Saturday, February 10, is included.

title: HTML/CSS Web Programming
description: Covers HyperText Markup Language (HTML) coding practices for both current and future browsers. Uses HTML, Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) extensively validating according to the guidelines of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C). Introduces JavaScript language. Includes the creation and publishing of a website using HTML and CSS.

title: Modern and European Pastry
description: Offers advanced study of commercial baking techniques and procedures. Stresses the fundamentals of baking along with the production and presentation of cakes and pastries. Course work provides in-depth instruction in designing, baking, and decorating wedding cakes, fondant cakes, European pastries, petit fours, and other specialty desserts.

title: The Healing Power of Crystals
description: As children we loved to pick up rocks and play with them, little did we know that rocks, crystals and gemstones have unique powers to heal on 4 levels: Physical, Mental, Emotional and Spiritual. In this class, we will explore several of the popular gemstones and their healing power, learn the different ways to cleanse the crystal, and see methods of programming and cleaning the crystal. We will also talk about Aura Fields and how to heal yourself and others using different crystals. Kristy will provide 3 different crystals to students for $10 payable in class.

title: Monthly Unlimited $5 Fitness
description: Fitness Club Members - sign up for a calendar month of unlimited $5 Fitness Classes. Includes any or all of the following offerings: CEHFS-1166 Cardio Kick; CEHFS-1176 Cardio Pump; CEHFS-1177 Pilates; CEHFS-1181 Zumba Fit; CEHFS-1186 Zumba Gold; CEHFS-1198 Low Impact Aerobics; CEHFS-1199 Step Aerobics. See course schedule for specific course descriptions and offering details.

title: Field Trip to Gee Farms
description: Gee Farms is Michigan's largest retail garden nursery, encompassing 20 acres of plants and trees, from the familiar to the exotic. Meet instructor Kevin Collins, Landscape Designer at Gee Farms, and explore options that would be suitable for your landscaping needs. Whether you're coming to shop or just look around, enjoy some time with a plant expert who will show you some of the wonders that this nursery has to offer. Call us or visit the Maps & Supplies link on our website www.hfcc.edu/CL2 for directions.

title: Update Prop. Lease Agrmnt
description: This class will cover all the basics, line for line, review security deposit documentation, inventory checklist, how to fill in the blanks and answer all questions concerning the lease. Students should bring in their existing lease to compare.

title: Specialties of Kenya
description: Enjoy popular dishes, cooking methods and food culture with Chef Marilyn Suter just returning from a recent visit to Kenya. We will make ugali, a Kenyan staple, goat, curried bananas, a great vegetarian bean dish, and more. Bring an apron and a sense of adventure!

title: Jazz Band 1
description: First of four semesters of jazz band, including preparation and performance of Big Band music in a variety of jazz styles from swing to contemporary. This group, which performs several concerts each semester, is open to college students and adult members of the community who play saxophone, trumpet, trombone, piano, guitar, bass, or drums. New instrumentalists should contact the director to arrange for an audition prior to the first rehearsal.

title: Gage, Cam Layout, and Fixture
description: Focuses on the mastery of gage design principles as applied to the industrial workplace. Discusses gage design theory, computer-aided manufacturing, cam layout procedures, and fixturing. Emphasizes the team approach to projects.

title: DC and AC Motors
description: Covers DC and AC motor fundamentals. Includes DC generators; series, shunt, and compound DC motors; single-phase and three-phase AC induction motors; and synchronous, wound rotor, and universal motors. Compares AC alternators to DC generators.

title: Organizing Pic., Files, Photos
description: Are your files, digital pictures, emails, and information scattered everywhere on your computer? Learn to create folders and get organized. Instructor and class will create an orderly system for locating pictures and files. Discover how to download information from the Internet. Find out how to save files and projects on your computer, CDs, and flashdrives, and easily find them. Class will also copy, move, and relocate files and folders. See a demonstration on arranging and deleting emails. Also search for misplaced files and locate them.

title: Tips for Buy, Rent, Sell Home
description: Attorney and CPA Jonathan Goldberg will discuss the many tax and legal aspects homeowners should know when buying, selling or renting out their homes. This is information ou can't afford to miss! Topics include purchase agreements, the legal and tax aspects of renting your home, and mortgage and real estate tax issues.

title: Hand & Foot Reflexology
description: If you have always wondered about what Reflexology is and how it benefits the body, this is the class that will answer your questions! Class includes 2 color-coded charts that will help you identify organs, muscular & skeletal zones that correlate with specific areas on your hands & feet. Sign up with a partner and learn the techniques that will help you and those you love!

title: World Civilization I
description: Introduces major themes and events in World History from the Ancient to the Early Modern Periods through the study of the arts (fine and performing), architecture, literature, religions, and/or philosophies of (selected) World Civilizations.

title: Applied Statics
description: Provides the necessary mechanics background for engineering technology programs. Main topics are forces, moments, equilibrium, free body diagrams, trusses, frames, internal forces, friction, and area moment of inertia.

title: Typography
description: Typography is the art of arranging type to create visually communicated messages. Covers typesetting, and layout design including history, anatomy, and and design fundamentals. Introduces Adobe Illustrator and InDesign. ART-107 is highly suggested prior to taking this course. This class meets for six (6) hours a week.Students are expected to have access to the Adobe Creative Cloud applications including Photoshop, Indesign, and Illustrator.

title: Photo Lighting Ii: Intermediat
description: This class expands from what was learned from Photo Lighting I is one of our most sought after classes. Gain knowledge about all the components that make a studio lighting set up - and how to use each one. Learn how to light different types of backgrounds (black, white, and muslin) to get the results you want (high key and low key.) Learn how to photograph portraits like the pros! Capture your subject using a single light or multiple lights, and explore how to use light meters, white balancing, & more!

title: Elementary French I
description: Introduces the basic skills of reading, writing, speaking, and listening in French within communicative contexts. Covers the elementary pronunciation and grammatical principles necessary for comprehending and expressing simple ideas in both spoken and written French. Presents francophone culture.

title: Cutting and Forming Dies
description: MTWD TAGD-165Presents basic die design criteria and methods. Introduces various die types and draws the various die assemblies using both traditional and computer-aided drafting.
